title: Prediction of the Breast Cancer in Histopathology Images using JAYA-Optimized Deep Network
authors: K V L Bhavya, Figlu Mohanty, K Pranathi, Ch. Harika, Suvendu Rup
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Breast cancer is still a dominant cause of cancer death in the world, thus emphasizing the need for effective and reliable diagnostic tools. Histopathological image diagnosis of breast cancer is a critical diagnostic method; however, conventional manual diagnosis is usually followed by excessive time consumption and inherent subjectivity. This paper presents an automated classification system using deep learning methods for feature extraction and machine learning classifiers to enhance diagnostic accuracy. A modified Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and VGG16 are utilized as feature extraction tools, while Random Forest (RF), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB) are utilized as classification tools. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is utilized for dimension reduction, while JAYA optimization is utilized to aid feature extraction and hyperparameter tuning. Empirical results on the BreaKHis dataset show that CNN-based models perform better than VGG16-based models, with the CNN+XGB configuration recording the best accuracy of 87.18% after JAYA optimization. These results underscore the importance of feature extraction methods, classification methods, and optimization methods in enhancing the classification of breast cancer. The current study presents an end-to-end framework to assist pathologists in the accurate and timely identification of cancer, eventually leading to better clinical outcomes.

title: Warm-stamping mechanical behavior and microstructural characterization of Al-Si metal sheets using water refrigerated tooling
authors: Estrada-Warn P, Garcia-Santibañez GJ, Jauregui-Pablos M, Vazquez-Gomez-Orduña S, Gonzalez-Ojeda R
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The use of water refrigerated tooling in a laboratory scale was used to simulate conditions of warm (500°C) stamping of aluminum plates with 0.8 mm in thickness. The work presents load-displacement curves of room temperature and heat treated samples during stamping at 120 and 240 mm/min, effect of the treatment in the anisotropic characteristics of the rolled sheet plate and electron microscopy analysis along with element detection, as this alloy contains small amounts of silicon. The processes resembles a solution treatment and, thus, also an aging treatment at 120°C for 12 h was carried out in order to understand the application possibilities of the manufacturing process in the automotive or aero-space industry.

title: Fake News in the Digital Age: The Role of Social Media and its Consequences
authors: Divyshikha, Rajender Singh Thakur, Pushpendra Sachan, Priyanka Singh, Adarsh Kumar Singh, Jayant Rathee
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In the current digitized world, digital media forms like blogs, online news media, social media, etc., have replaced conventional news transmission platforms like newspapers and magazines. This research aims to examine the format, structure, and themes of fake news on social media. This research paper employed a qualitative content analysis methodology. The study collected & analyzed data from fifty viral stories about fake news. The dataset only extends from January 2025 to June 2025. The goal of this study is to help individuals better understand fake news ecosystems by showing how stories are made and shared on social media. It also shows how important it is to teach people how to use technology responsibly, critically, and proactively in order to lessen the effects of fake information that is spreading in the public domain.

title: Depthwise Separable Convolution architectures for 3D Object Recognition *
authors: Lalitha Swamy Thayumanavar, Sukumar Nandi, Bunil Kumar Balabantaray
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Fast and efficient recognition of 3D objects remains vital in computer vision. View-based recognition has gained increasing attention because it mimics human perception by recognizing objects from multiple viewpoints. Convolutional neural network-based architectures have achieved remarkable success in this field; however, they often require extensive computational resources for training and inference, which poses a significant challenge. To develop lightweight architectures, replacing standard convolutions with depthwise separable and pointwise convolutions has been widely adopted in image processing and related domains. This work analyzes the performance of depthwise separable convolution-based architectures for object classification. The experiments were conducted using MobileNetV1 and Xception, two prominent depthwise separable convolution architectures. Their performance is compared with VGG19, a standard convolution-based architecture, on the benchmark datasets ModelNet10 and ModelNet40.The results demonstrate that depthwise separable convolution-based models outperform the VGG19 architecture on both datasets.

title: LightDiff-Shadow: A Lightweight Global Guided Diffusion Model for Real-Time Shadow Removal
authors: Stutee Mohanty, Rajiv Senapati
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Computer vision works to interpret the content of images and videos. Shadows often cause problems because they can obscure important details and confuse object recognition and understanding of a scene. Existing shadow removal methods are generally complex, require a lot of resources, and struggle to adapt easily to real-time use or smaller devices. This paper introduces LightDiff-Shadow, a simple diffusion model for removing shadows from images in real-time. The method uses a two-part design that combines local denoising with understanding the overall scene. It uses a Reweighted Cross Attention and a Global Guided Sampling strategy to be more efficient and accurate. The model is trained from scratch using three standard datasets ISTD, ISTD+, and SRD. Experimental results show that our method achieves better performance in both qualitative and quantitative metrics outperforming recent state-of-the-art models such as ShadowFormer, Diff-Shadow, and Mask-ShadowGAN. The approach keeps textures sharp, illumination consistent, and overall visual quality good while using much less computing power. The findings suggest that simple setups can remove shadows well on devices with limited resources. This improves the accuracy with which a system interprets scenes and observes outdoor areas. It is highly beneficial for devices that need to process information instantly. The model produces output that demonstrate good visual quality, shows no artificial shadows, and preserves detail clarity under natural lighting conditions. This shows that it works in the real world.

title: Application of ChatGPT in Today’s Medical System
authors: Madhu Lata Nirmal, Gaurav Kumar, Sambit Satpathy
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: ChatGPT is an innovative language model offered by OpenAI, which transforms various spheres, and the healthcare area is among the ones that are impacted the most. Its application in medical systems is totally transforming everything by enhancing medical education and training, simplifying the process of diagnosis, accelerating the process of clinical documentation and increasing patient involvement. The model may prove useful to the patients and doctors since it can react in a manner similar to how people react. The ChatGPT helps to receive information much faster, accelerate the work process, and minimize the number of tasks that an administrator has to perform in clinical conditions. It provides medical professionals and students who learn via simulations, content development, and other one-onone tutoring. It also assists in making diagnostic decisions by examining large amounts of clinical data in providing insights and proposing potential diagnosis. Although it does not go without its advantages, the use of ChatGPT in the healthcare sector is concerning in terms of data security, misinformation, discrimination, and responsibility. Very important are to reduce such risks through ethical use and control. This paper examines six primary uses of ChatGPT within the context of healthcare, its advantages and disadvantages, and ethical considerations. The complete overview is provided with the purpose of promotion of correct usage of this AI tool and ensuring that it becomes an efficient and reliable tool of creation of modern healthcare systems.

title: Computer Technology-Driven Dynamic Parameters Identification for Symmetrical Dual-Branched 4PPa-2PaR Parallel Manipulator Using Genetic Algorithm and Log-Cholesky Parameterization
authors: Weilong Yang, Silu Chen, Chi Zhang, Guilin Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Accurate dynamic modeling is crucial for achieving high-performance control in parallel manipulator (PM). However, conventional identification methods often struggle to ensure physical consistency due to the intrinsic structural complexity of these mechanisms. In this paper, we propose a computer-aided identification framework that integrates advanced computational techniques to enhance the accuracy and reliability of dynamic parameter estimation. Specifically, excitation trajectories are optimized using a Genetic Algorithm, with explicit consideration of joint constraints to guarantee persistent excitation. To enforce physical consistency among the identified parameters, a log-Cholesky parameterization is employed, transforming the inertia matrix into an unconstrained optimization space. The entire identification pipeline, from trajectory generation to parameter regression, is algorithmically automated, ensuring both reproducibility and computational efficiency. Experimental validation on a 4PPa-2PaR PM confirms that the proposed approach can accurately estimate dynamic parameters while preserving their physical consistency.

title: Offloading Reservoir Computing-Based Hand-Waving Action Recognition to FPGAs for Service Robots
authors: Sojiro Shimada, Akinobu Mizutani, Kanta Yoshioka, Kosei Isomoto, Hakaru Tamukoh
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: We propose a system that offloads hand-waving action recognition processing in service robots to a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) accelerator. The accelerator implements a LUTNet-based reservoir computing (LUTNet-RC) model. Conventionally, such processing was performed by laptops connected to robots. Nevertheless, the proposed system offloads some of these task processing to an FPGA. This improves the overall efficiency of the robot and optimizes the use of computational resources. The experimental results show that in hand-waving action recognition, the LUTNet-RC on FPGA achieves higher accuracy and precision than the conventional echo state network (ESN) model, while significantly reducing the power consumption. In addition, the system can be expanded to large-scale networks, which are challenging to implement on laptops.

title: The Role of Real-World Data in Evaluating Causal Bayesian Networks: Data Collection Guidelines and Case Study
authors: Zhitao Liang, Maximilian Diehl, Nanami Hashimoto, Anne Koepken, Daniel Leidner, Karinne Ramirez-Amaro, Emmanuel Dean
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Causal Bayesian Networks (CBNs) in robotics are often learned in simulation due to the considerable amount of data required for training. However, discrepancies between simulation and the physical world can cause the learned causal relations to fail in real-world scenarios. Thus, the sim-to-real evaluation is a critical step to deploy a simulation-learned CBN in the real-world. The main challenges in this process are the lack of real-robot evaluation datasets that capture the complexity, noise, and variability of physical environments, which are missing in simulation. In this paper, we propose a set of task-agnostic guidelines for real-robot data collection to evaluate Causal Bayesian Networks (CBNs). The guidelines are generalizable and can be applied to collect real-robot datasets across different robot tasks and platforms. To demonstrate this, we apply them to a robotic platform performing one concrete task, e.g., the robot TIAGo performing a two-cube stacking task, and we collect the real-robot dataset from 100 trials. As a case study, we demonstrate how the dataset can be used to evaluate a simulation-trained CBN on real-robot executions, reporting 10% accuracy drop from sim-to-real transfer. We present this as a first step towards standardized and quantifiable sim-to-real evaluation for CBNs.

title: GeetaVani: A Retrieval-Augmented LLM Framework for Contextual Dialogue from the Bhagavad Geeta
authors: Nutakki Namratha, Sai Sarat Spuran Naidu, Kshira Sagar Sahoo, Manthan Singla, Bhavana V S S S R Praneeth
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The Bhagavad Geeta, a sacred and treasured scripture in Hindu philosophy This work presents a novel Generative AI and LLM-based chatbot called GeetaVani, inspired by the teachings of the Bhagavad Geeta. It is designed to deliver contextually rich, spiritually grounded responses to user queries. By using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework, the system seamlessly provide precise and meaningful answers based on Geeta. The system employs Sentence-BERT to convert both Geeta verses and user queries into dense vector embeddings. These embeddings are indexed using FAISS. It enables fast and accurate retrieval of the most semantically relevant verses. The retrieved verses are then passed as contextual input to the Groq LLM API, which uses LLaMA 3 to yield response.Designed prompt ensures that the output remains faithful to the original teachings. The chatbot achieves 100% precision for the top$(k)$-6 results, with a Mean Average Precision of 0.89 and a gradual precision decline for broader queries beyond k is 6.

title: A Web-Based Multi-Robot Teleoperation Platform: Architecture, Implementation, and User Evaluation
authors: E. Rafael Hernandez-Rios, Christian Penaloza
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents the design and evaluation of a web-based multi-robot teleoperation platform that enables remote access to heterogeneous robots for education and research. In contrast to most existing remote laboratories, which are limited to single-robot control or simulation environments, the proposed system integrates real-time video streaming, bidirectional control, and user management within a modular client–server web architecture.The platform was deployed with three distinct robotic systems—an industrial manipulator, a humanoid, and a quadruped—each hosted on independent servers with public IP access. A cross-border user study with Technological University Dublin was conducted, where participants performed guided teleoperation tasks and completed structured surveys. The results demonstrated high usability, low-latency performance, and positive educational impact across diverse robot morphologies.These findings suggest that the proposed platform provides a scalable and accessible solution for remote robotics education and international collaboration, bridging the gap between simulation-based training and real-world robot interaction.

title: Lightweight Mask-Aware User Authentication Framework Using MobileFaceNet for Medical IoT Network
authors: Praveen R, Balaji S
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The adoption of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has transformed healthcare on continuous patient monitoring, remote diagnostics, and real-time medical analytics. The expanded seamless integration of medical devices has increased concerns with data privacy, user authentication, and computational limitations in resource-limited environments. The regular use of facial masks in medical environment affects the accuracy of traditional facial recognition systems due to partial facial occlusion. This paper propose a Lightweight Mask-Aware User Authentication Framework for IoMT edge medical devices to address the challenge. The proposed architecture enhances feature discrimination in face occluded situations while reducing computational expenses by integrating a Mask-Aware MobileFaceNet (MaskAwareMFN) model with Mask-Aware Feature Extraction (MAFE) and a Lightweight Attention Mechanism (LAM). The proposed model enhanced average accuracy from 95.1% to 98.0% and improved masked-face recognition accuracy from 93.0% to 96.1%, utilizing the Real-World Masked Face Dataset (RMFD). Performance evaluations of inference duration, model dimensions, and hardware utilization illustrate the proposed model's real-time capabilities and suitability for embedded medical devices. Consequently, the proposed framework provides a secure, efficient, and privacy-preserving user authentication solution for next-generation IoMT ecosystems.

title: Iterative development of a mechanical gripping system for civil counter-UAS applications
authors: Dmytro Zhukov
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In recent years, drones have become widely used. Consequently, their potential to cause harm or damage to people, property, or infrastructure has also increased. As drones have become more prevalent, so has the need to counter them and prevent potential misuse. Unlike net or projectile-based systems, a gripper enables precise midair capture and secure handling of the intruder drone without causing uncontrolled descent or collateral damage. This paper presents our iterative development process for such a mechanical gripping system for use in civil counter-UAS applications. After a brief overview of existing gripping systems, we will describe the initial development steps and progress to increasingly complex design solutions. During the development process, three prototype designs were built and evaluated, one after another. Finally, we present our final design, which satisfies all requirements for integration into the UAS and can be used to catch the intruders in mid-flight.

title: Co-Optimization of Design and Manufacturing Parameters for Low-Cost Robotic Actuation
authors: Gregory M. Campbell, Yi Cao, Hannah Escritor, Zihao Zhou, Mark Yim
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Additive and low-cost manufacturing techniques promise increased access to robotic actuation at the cost of mechanical precision. In this work, we employ principled Design of Experiments (DoE), including Taguchi orthogonal arrays, in parallel to sequential experimentation enabled by Bayesian Optimization (BO) for co-optimization of design and manufacturing parameters across two design case studies. We optimize for a combination of gear ratio and backdrivability in a 3D-printed compound Wolfrom bilateral gearbox. We also optimize for crack pressure and steady-state pressure differential of an injection-molded silicone check valve. Using BO, we find a 3D-printing compatible gear design with a gear ratio of 63.6 that backdrives without ever needing more than 0.35 Nm of input torque. This represents a 49% increase in ‘score’ over the Taguchi method. Similarly, we find a BO valve with lower combined crack and steady-state pressure errors than the Taguchi trials, decreasing cumulative error by 55%. Tracking model uncertainty throughout training, we conclude that further model training is necessary to reach optimal results in both cases. We further conclude that BO via the Ax platform is not yet as "plug-and-play" as Taguchi arrays.

title: Review on Low Noise Amplifier for High Frequency Applications
authors: Dheeraj Kalra, Manish Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Low Noise Amplifiers (LNAs) has a crucial part in communication systems, significantly influencing the overall performance of wireless transceivers. This paper presents a comprehensive review of various LNA design techniques employed for high-frequency applications, highlighting advancements in circuit topologies, noise reduction strategies, and power efficiency optimizations. Several state-of-the-art LNA architectures have been analyzed, including transformer feedback, cascode, cascade, resistive shunt feedback, noise cancellation, current reuse, subthreshold, and variable gain techniques. The study further explores the impact of CMOS scaling on LNA performance, covering designs from 180 nm down to 40 nm CMOS technology. A comparative analysis of LNAs based on key performance parameters such as noise figure (NF), gain, power dissipation, bandwidth, and figure of merit (FoM) is provided. The review identifies critical design trade-offs in achieving low NF, high gain, and broad bandwidth while maintaining low power consumption. Key challenges in high-frequency LNA design are also discussed, including biasing complexity, NF optimization, and FoM enhancement. Based on the literature survey, existing gaps in high-frequency LNA designs are highlighted, emphasizing the need for improved biasing techniques, enhanced noise performance, and optimized FoM. This review serves as a valuable reference for researchers and engineers working on next-generation LNA architectures for high-speed wireless communication and millimeter-wave applications.

title: Unified Transmission-Distribution Coordination for Secure and Economical Frequency Support From Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Rushuai Han, Qinran Hu, Mengtong Chen, Yuanshi Zhang, Tao Qian, Zaijun Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: Utilizing numerous distributed energy resources (DERs) for frequency regulation is a promising pathway to enhance the security and economy of future power systems. However, a fundamental challenge arises from the emergent collective behavior of these resources: their aggregated response, intended to support macro-level system frequency, can jeopardize micro-level local voltage security within distribution networks. To this end, this paper proposes a unified hierarchical framework based on the principle of synergistic co-design. The framework employs a stage-dependent architecture that systematically integrates distinct control strategies for different regulation stages. For security-critical response, it utilizes a novel stage-aware adaptive virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control method with stability formally proven by Lyapunov analysis, while for economic efficiency, it transitions to a distributed control method for cost-optimal restoration. The entire process is underpinned by a proactive voltage security management strategy that enforces micro-level safety. To enable efficient analysis of these complex dynamics, a dual time-scale information interaction mechanism is designed to accelerate the transmission-distribution (T&D) co-simulation process. Simulations on a hybrid transmission-distribution test system validate the framework's effectiveness, demonstrating that the proposed approach enables scalable, secure, and economical frequency support while effectively managing the emergent voltage challenges from collective DER control.

title: A Remaining Useful Life Prediction Method for Power Devices Based on Multi-Indicator Fusion and Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Yalei Yuan, Fengjiao Dai, Hao Sun, Zhaoyang Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid life prediction framework (MI-BLSTM-PSO) for power devices to overcome the limitations of single-feature information, non-monotonic degradation modeling, and local optima in traditional methods. Multiple features highly correlated with aging are selected via Pearson correlation coefficient to construct a fused health indicator. A Bidirectional LSTM (BLSTM) captures complex temporal dependencies in degradation data. Crucially, a dynamic Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) mechanism is triggered upon training stagnation to escape local minima. Experimental results on power cycling data show that our model significantly outperforms unidirectional LSTM and other benchmarks in prediction accuracy (RMSE%) and robustness, demonstrating its strong potential for predictive maintenance.

title: Road Extraction from Remote Sensing Images Using Efficient Channel Attention and Transformer
authors: Lele Hou, Yunhong Li, Weizhong Chen, Qianbo Yang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: A road extraction model named ETD-LinkNet is proposed to address issues in remote sensing images such as fragile road structures, complex background interference, and insufficient feature discriminability. Based on D-LinkNet, the model incorporates an Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module in the encoder to enhance road feature representation through channel recalibration, effectively suppressing background noise. A multi-scale dilated convolution pyramid captures features of roads with different widths. Finally, a Transformer module is introduced after Dblock to model global dependencies, maintaining connectivity of slender roads and reducing fractures. Experiments on DeepGlobe and CHN6-CUG datasets demonstrate the model's effectiveness, achieving mIoU values of 83.70% and 80.54% respectively, significantly outperforming comparative models and validating its advantages in segmentation accuracy and generalization.

title: A Floating Level Converter With A Negative Voltage Tolerance Of -35V
authors: Tiancheng Lu, Xing Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This brief introduces a novel level shifter for gate drive applications. This level shifter can extend the floating voltage domain to the negative voltage range without requiring coupling capacitors. With the development of AMOLED technology, the corresponding power supply circuits have also received extensive attention. For novel hybrid SIBO circuits, level shifters capable of operating in both positive and negative voltage ranges are required. Traditional active level shifters have limitations in negative voltage tolerance and fail to meet application requirements. To address this issue, this paper proposes a level shifter applicable to single-inductor bipolar output (SIBO) systems. This converter can be applied to a voltage range of ±37.5V, and its basic architecture can achieve a high common-mode transient immunity (CMTI) of 50V/ns with a delay of 1.18ns.

title: Estimating Wrist Joint Angle using Electromyography and Cepstral Coefficients
authors: Sougo Horimatsu, Kensuke Takenaka, Takayuki Mukaeda, Keisuke Shima
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: A method was developed for increasing the accuracy of the continuous wrist joint angle estimations for myoelectric prosthetic hands. The method considers cepstral coefficients, which efficiently represent the frequency spectrum characteristics of electromyography (EMG) signals as features. The root mean square myoelectric power (RMS) is conventionally used for myoelectric prosthetic control; however, the sensitivity of the RMS to angle changes is low, especially at the fine muscle contraction level. The proposed method incorporates low-order cepstral coefficients into the feature vector as well as the RMS myoelectric power. This approach increases the angle estimation accuracy through capturing the changes in the spectral shape related to the firing patterns of the motor units. The results of an angle estimation experiment involving wrist dorsiflexion and palmar flexion movements showed that adding a few cepstral coefficients substantially increased the estimation accuracy, particularly when estimating the small flexion angle range. The cepstral coefficient can be used to effectively estimate joint angles from EMG signals, contributing to the development of smoother and more intuitive myoelectric prosthetic control.

title: An Optimal Design Method for High-Frequency Transformers in High-Frequency Link AC-DC Matrix Converters
authors: Yang Mei, Xiang Liu, Ruijuan Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To meet the requirements of high efficiency and high power density for high-frequency link AC-DC matrix converters, this paper proposes an optimized design method for high-frequency transformers in such converters. Based on the improved NSGA-Ⅱ algorithm, the method aims to reduce high-frequency transformers losses and minimize volume, comprehensively considering factors such as leakage inductance, temperature rise, magnetic flux density, and spatial constraints. The optimized transformer parameters are calculated, and the high-frequency transformers is designed accordingly. Simulation and experimental results show that the transformer designed by this method achieves an efficiency of over 98.6% under wide-range operation, with the maximum efficiency reaching 99.3% at rated power. The leakage inductance is 49.2 μH, and the volume is 220 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. These results validate the correctness and effectiveness of the proposed design method.

title: Automatic Diabetic Retinopathy Identification and Grading in Retinal Fundus Pictures
authors: R. Shamli, P. Vasuki
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Given that diabetic retinopathy (DR) remains a primary cause of vision impairment worldwide, there is a pressing need for automated and precise diagnostic systems. To automatically detect and grade DR, this study presents MWRes-Ensemble (Mobile WideRes Forward Harmonic Ensemble), a novel deep learning framework. The suggested hybrid architecture outperforms previous single-CNN techniques in lesion detection by combining the EfficientNet, ResNet, and DenseNet models, particularly in low-quality retinal images. During preprocessing, extensive data augmentation techniques and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) were used to reduce class imbalance. The robustness of the proposed approach is demonstrated by experimental evaluation using the Kaggle DR and EyePACS datasets, which yielded an accuracy of 92.50%, a sensitivity of 90.80%, a specificity of 94.20%, and an AUC of 0.96. The severe DR cases showed an F1 score of 0.89, indicating significant progress in identifying moderate and severe DR stages. According to the results, the MWRes-Ensemble model may be a dependable automated tool for effective and timely DR screening in clinical settings.

title: EcoGrow: IoT-Based Smart Plant Monitoring System
authors: Bhavvya Mehta, Cheshta Khurana, Vasudev Grover, Khushboo Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Traditional plant monitoring systems are time-consuming and error-prone, causing wastage of resources and loss of plant health. EcoGrow is a smart Internet of Things plant monitoring system that solves the problem using a series of sensors, actuators, microcontrollers. It constantly monitors environment parameters like soil moisture, temperature, light intensity. As a result of low moisture, the system automatically changes to drip irrigation. A plastic sheet is rolled over the plant under direct sunlight to shade the plant. A PIR (Passive Infrared) motion sensor is also incorporated to detect the intrusion of pests or intrusions. Whenever it detects motion, an audible warning is produced to alert in time in order to save the plant. This system not only maximizes the use of resources and minimizes labor but also provides early detection of plant disease and pest infestation. With upcoming technologies like Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), the Internet of Things (IoT) as its power, EcoGrow maximizes predictive analysis, reduces wastage, facilitates energy-efficient, sustainable agriculture for home gardens and commercial agriculture.

title: Robotic System Architecture Design for Manipulation of 3D Deformable Objects
authors: Félix Nadon, Angel J. Valencia, Pierre Payeur
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents a system architecture for the robotic manipulation and reshaping of 3D deformable objects. The inherent complexity of manipulating objects with evolving shapes requires the construction and efficient integration of components that are reusable and remain functional under operational scenarios that involve high variability. Such original components are developed and integrated in this paper, notably including a mapping subsystem to aggregate and analyse sensor data for extracting general manipulation and sensing heuristics, as well as a deformation modelling component to predict the effect of candidate robot actions on the shape of an object of interest. A simulation interface is further implemented on top of robotic simulators, allowing a modular creation of environments to evaluate the performance of the aforementioned components and to generate synthetic datasets of 3D deformable object manipulation tasks. These general-use components are integrated with application-specific task and motion planning components in real and simulated environments.

title: AIREC-Basic: Consistent Demonstration Data Collection for Imitation Learning with Redundant Robot Arms
authors: Hiroshi Ito, Yoshiki Kanai, Akira Kanazawa, Hideyuki Ichiwara, Takahiro Yoshida, Naoaki Noguchi, Tetsuya Ogata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The performance of robotic imitation learning (IL) largely depends on the quality of human demonstrations. To address this challenge, we present AIREC-Basic, a leader–follower teleoperation system equipped with a dual-arm mobile manipulator that enables efficient data collection. The system employs 8-DoF redundant arms to realize diverse task postures, but such redundancy can reduce the consistency of demonstrations. To overcome this issue, we propose a novel control strategy, Soft Homing Control (SHC), which mitigates redundancy while preserving intuitive operator control, thereby improving dataset consistency. We validate our approach on three household tasks using state-of-the-art IL algorithms (ACT, Diffusion Policy, and HSARNN). Experimental results show that SHC significantly reduces joint trajectory variance and improves task success rates, particularly in scenarios with strong trajectory constraints and frequent contacts.

title: A self-supervised learning-based 3D model completion method for automatic reconstruction systems
authors: Jing Li, Yanshen Wang, Meng Du, Yanzi Kong
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: Practical limitations, such as constrained sensor trajectories and inevitable occlusions, often lead to incomplete and partial observations, resulting in deficient 3D models. This situation is particularly prominent when dealing with multi-scale complex objects. To address the problem of completing the full shape of an object based on its incomplete model, we proposed a novel self-supervised learning method. We employed three pseudo-label generation strategies to randomly produce training labels. A weighted function is utilized as the loss criterion. We conducted experiments on multi-scale objects. The results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms traditional completion methods, effectively generates complete 3D models under severe occlusion.

title: Virtual Domain-Guided Cross-Modal Distillation With Multiview Correlation Awareness for Domain-Specific Multimodal Neural Machine Translation_supp1-3656559.pdf
authors: Junjun Guo
publish: N/A
abstract: Domain-specific multimodal neural machine translation (DMNMT) aims to translate source language domain sentences into target language by incorporating images as additional contextual information. However, domain-specific multimodal scenarios frequently suffer from visual imbalance issues, such as one sentence corresponding to multiple images or even no images at all. Effectively integrating visual information into text to enhance domain sentence translation performance under visual imbalance issues is one of the critical challenges for DMNMT, especially for domain-related terms. To tackle these domain-specific visual imbalance problems, this article introduces a virtual domain distillation-enhanced multimodal fusion with the awareness of multiview correlations to enhance the robustness and performance of domain machine translation across various multimodal domain scenarios. We first adopt a multiview correlation-aware cross-modal distillation strategy to generate virtual domain visual scenes by extracting visual correlations among all images through multikernel representations. Subsequently, we integrate pseudo-domain visual scenes into text to improve the performance of domain-specific machine translation. Our proposed approach has the ability to capture domain visual representations across different scenarios, and contributing to more effective domain-specific translation. We conduct expensive experiments on three domain-specific and general-domain benchmark datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) machine translation scores on most test sets. The in-depth analysis demonstrates the effectiveness and robustness of our proposed approach for domain machine translation.

title: Next Generation of Brain-Computer Interfaces: A Proposal for Spikes Detection Based on CDMA and Power Domain Nonorthogonal Multiple Access Techniques
authors: Jesus Minguillon, Miguel Angel Lopez-Gordo, Pablo Martinez-Cañada
publish: IEEE Transactions on Human-Machine Systems
abstract: The first generation of invasive brain-computer interfaces was conceived as a centralized and monolithic technology composed of an array of microelectrodes implanted on the brain surface and wired to an external unit for telemetry and power supply. Although this technology exceeded the expectations and possibilities of the noninvasive one, risks associated with implantation of wired devices and procedures appeared. Advances in electronics miniaturization and wireless power transfer methods, among others, have favored the proposal of wireless submillimeter implantable devices with stimulation and telemetry capabilities. This constitutes the foundation stone of a next generation of submilliliter implanted neurosensors (NG-SINs) based on massive distributed networks of untethered neuroimplants deployed on the brain. This particular ecosystem, because of the inherent severe restrictions in size, power consumption, electromagnetic safety, and computational capacity, presents significant challenges in the field of communications and internetworking, such as media access control, addressing, data error rate, and others. In this study, we propose a combination of orthogonal multiple access (OMA) and nonorthogonal media access (NOMA) as the base for the telemetry of the NG-SINs, with a focus on the detection of neural spikes in real time. Specifically, we propose the use of asynchronous code division multiple access (aCDMA) and power domain non orthogonal media access (PD-NOMA) with successive interference cancelation (SIC) over backscattered communications. While aCDMA mimics the spontaneous nature of brain activity, and provides addressing and grant-free random-access mechanism, its inherent near-far issue can be conveniently used to improve the performance by means of PD-NOMA and SIC. Based on our simulations, we conclude that this combination of NOMA and OMA techniques is a viable and promising approach for the NG-SINs applied to neural spikes detection that enables scalability with improved performance.

title: PropMambaSR: Lightweight Image Super-Resolution with Propagation State Space Model
authors: Wushuai Jin, Jie Li, Xinbo Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: State Space Models (SSMs), particularly Mamba, have emerged as promising alternatives to Transformers for lightweight Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR) due to their linear complexity in modeling long-range dependencies. Advanced Mamba-based Super-Resolution models, such as MambaIR and VMambaIR, have demonstrated compelling performance by integrating bidirectional and multi-directional scanning mechanisms. However, these methods are still constrained by several critical challenges that limit their ultimate reconstruction capabilities. Firstly, the inherent requirement for 2D-to-1D serialization disrupts local spatial coherence, which is vital for preserving fine-grained texture details during reconstruction. Second, stacking multiple SSM blocks in deep networks leads to progressive decay of fine-grained information, as hidden states are typically confined within individual layers without cross-layer propagation. To address these challenges, we propose PropMambaSR, a novel hybrid architecture that synergistically combines global context modeling with local feature extraction. Our core contribution is the Propagation State Space Model (PropSSM), which establishes explicit cross-layer hidden state routing to enable direct propagation of fine-grained information from shallow to deep layers, effectively mitigating information decay while preserving reconstruction details. Additionally, we introduce an Enhanced Spatial Feature Block (ESFB) that employs multi-scale residual distillation to explicitly capture local texture information compromised by the serialization process. Finally, a dynamic Fusion Model (FM) adaptively integrates global contextual features from PropSSM with local spatial features from ESFB through learnable gating mechanisms. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PropMambaSR achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple benchmarks while maintaining computational efficiency. Notably, on the Urban100 dataset for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times 2$</tex-math></inline-formula> SR, our model surpasses the next-best method by 0.33 dB in PSNR, demonstrating superior capability in reconstructing high-frequency textures and regular structural patterns.

title: PO-GUISE+: Pose and Object Guided Transformer Token Selection for Efficient Driver Action Recognition
authors: Ricardo Pizarro, Roberto Valle, Rafael Barea, José M. Buenaposada, Luis Baumela, Luis M. Bergasa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: We address the task of identifying distracted driving by analyzing in-car videos using efficient transformers. Although transformer models have achieved outstanding performance in human action recognition tasks, their high computational costs limit their application onboard a vehicle. We introduce PO-GUISE+, a multi-task video transformer that, given an input clip, predicts the distracted driving action, the driver’s pose, and the interacting object. Our enhanced features for token selection are specifically adapted to driver actions by leveraging information about object interaction and the driver’s pose. With PO-GUISE+, we significantly reduce the model’s computational demands while maintaining or improving baseline accuracy across various computational budgets. Additionally, to evaluate our model’s performance in real-world scenarios, we have developed benchmarks on a Jetson computing platform, demonstrating its effectiveness across different configurations and computational budgets. Our model outperforms current state-of-the-art results on the Drive&Act, 100-Driver, and 3MDAD datasets, while having superior efficiency compared to existing video transformer-based methods.

title: Learning Optimal Robust Control for Nonlinear Mixed Traffic Under External Disturbance
authors: Jie Li, Jiawei Wang, Yangang Ren, Yujie Yang, Shen Li, Guofa Li, Shengbo Eben Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The integration of connected and automated vehicles (CAVs) into traffic systems holds potential to mitigate undesired disturbances. Nevertheless, coexisting human-driven vehicles (HDVs) introduce complex behavioral disturbances, which has imposed critical challenges for control robustness. This study develops a computational framework based on policy iteration to derive robust control policies with optimized attenuation performance for nonlinear mixed traffic flow. Specifically, robust <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> control problem is solved by applying the framework of zero-sum game, whose solution at the Nash equilibrium is transformed into a Hamilton–Jacobi (HJ) inequality with a Hamiltonian constraint. For achieving desired attenuation performance, the value function is updated by gradient descent based on counterexamples violating Hamiltonian and monotonicity constraints, where the positive definiteness of the value function is ensured by convex neural networks, facilitating the analysis of control stability via Lyapunov methods. By utilizing constraint gaps, the attenuation level is optimized through the analytical formulae derived from the HJ inequality. The stability and algorithm convergence are proved. Experimental results demonstrate the capability of the learned controller to effectively attenuate disturbance propagation and stabilize mixed traffic flow.

title: Modularity Maximization and Community Detection in Complex Networks Through Recursive and Hierarchical Annealing in the DWAVE Advantage Quantum Processing Units
authors: Joan Falcó-Roget, Kacper Jurek, Barbara Wojtarowicz, Karol Capała, Katarzyna Rycerz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Quantum adiabatic optimization has long been expected to outperform classical methods in solving NP -type problems. While this has been proven in certain experiments, its main applications still reside in academic problems where the size of the system to be solved would not represent an obstacle to any modern desktop computer. Here, we develop a systematic procedure to find the global optima of the modularity function to discover community structure in complex networks solely relying on pure annealers rather than hybrid solutions. We bypass the one-hot encoding constraints by hierarchically and recursively encoding binary instances of the problem that can be solved without the need to guess the exact penalties for the Lagrange multipliers. We study the variability and robustness of the annealing process as a function of network size, directness of connections, topology, and the resolution of the communities. We show how our approach produces meaningful and at least equally optimal solutions to state-of-the-art community detection algorithms while maintaining tractable and potentially scalable computing times. Lastly, due to its recursive nature, the annealing process returns intermediate subdivisions, thus offering interpretable rather than black-box solutions. These dendrograms can be used to unveil normal and pathological hidden hierarchies in brain networks, hence opening the door to clinical workflows. Overall, this represents a first step towards an applicable practice-oriented usage of pure quantum annealing, potentially bridging two segregated communities in modern science and engineering: that of network science and quantum computing.

title: DWSF-Net: A Dynamic Wavelet-based Spatial-frequency Fusion Network for Multispectral Object Detection
authors: Fan Yang, Wei Li, Lei Li, Min Yang, Jianwei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Multispectral object detection aims to identify tar gets under diverse illumination conditions by leveraging complementary information from multiple spectral modalities. A major challenge in this field lies in effectively fusing multispectral features while accounting for both spatial and frequency domain characteristics. Existing methods primarily focus on spatial fusion, often neglecting critical frequency domain cues and treating all spectral channels equally, despite their distinct properties. In particular, RGB images capture high-frequency texture and color, whereas infrared (IR) images focus on low frequency thermal signatures—rendering conventional spatial only fusion suboptimal. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Dynamic Wavelet-based Spatial-Frequency Fusion Net work (DWSF-Net) that integrates both spatial and frequency information for enhanced multispectral representation. DWSF Net introduces a learnable wavelet encoder to adaptively extract frequency-aware features, a wavelet modulation fusion module to selectively combine informative sub-bands across spectra, and a frequency-domain sub-band fusion scheme with adaptive weight learning to refine cross-spectral integration. Finally, modulated spatial features and adaptively fused frequency components are aggregated to form the final representation. Extensive experiments conducted on three public datasets demonstrate that the proposed DWSF-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance, highlighting its effectiveness and potential for improving the accuracy of multispectral object detection.

title: 3D-SceneQ: Empowering 3D LLM with Query-Guided Adaptive Pruning and Multi-modal Feature Enhancement
authors: Yongqi Shan, Lu Zhang, Jiazuo Yu, Yunzhi Zhuge, Huchuan Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Current 3D scene understanding pipelines typically concatenate vast numbers of 3D object tokens with text tokens and feed the resulting sequence to a Large Language Model (LLM). However, existing 3D-LLMs for scene understanding encounter two major limitations: (i) excessive inclusion of task-irrelevant object data, introducing noise that reduces reasoning accuracy and increases hallucinations; and (ii) reliance solely on point cloud data, which inherently lacks the rich semantic information available in complementary 2D modalities, such as color, material properties, texture, and high-level contextual relationships. To address these challenges, we introduce 3D-SceneQ, a 3D LLM that unifies adaptive token pruning with multimodal semantic enrichment, markedly advancing scene understanding, reasoning, and grounding. Specifically, we propose a Query-Guided Adaptive Pruning (QGAP) module that adaptively selects task-relevant objects based on the intended meaning of user instructions, leveraging a learnable latent query to retain critical information while effectively suppressing irrelevant noise. In addition, we introduce a Multi-modal Object-level Feature Enhancement (MOFE) module, which integrates 2D feature embeddings from pre-trained models into 3D representation to enrich object-level semantic information. Evaluations show that 3D-SceneQ outperforms current state-of-the-art methods across multiple benchmarks (e.g., +5.7 CIDEr on Scan2Cap and +17.0 EM@1 on SQA3D), demonstrating its remarkable capabilities in various 3D language reasoning tasks.

title: Dual-Layer Bumpless Transfer Control for Markovian Jump Systems and Its Applications: A Weighted Coefficient Mechanism_supp1-3666949.pdf
authors: Qingchuan Feng
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper addresses a dual-layer bumpless transfer control (DLBTC) problem for Markovian jump systems (MJSs), aiming to achieve smooth transitions during stochastic mode switchings. First, the design of a multi-controller structure necessitates a system mode-dependent Markov chain, which enriches system dynamics and enhances robustness. However, it design inevitably increases stochastic mode switchings and aggravates bump phenomena. Second, to suppress these bumps, a dual-layer bumpless transfer control strategy is developed that constrains both input smoothness and rate continuity. Meanwhile, the traditional reference matrix mechanism is replaced by a weighted coefficient mechanism to improve adaptability across operating scenarios. Then, sufficient conditions on stochastic stabilization and DLBTC performance of MJSs are established via linear matrix inequalities. Finally, the impact of multi-controller structure and the proposed DLBTC on stochastic stabilization and transient behavior is demonstrated through an RLC circuit and a 40K engine control system as case studies.

title: A Dual-Lunate-Ring Bulk RE-Ba-Cu-O Undulator
authors: Yimin Tong, Haolin Lu, Dabin Wei, Zhuangwei Chen, Chan Liu, Marco Calvi, Kai Zhang, Haixiao Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: RE-Ba-Cu-O (REBCO) bulk high temperature superconducting undulators have been experimentally demonstrated to generate undulator fields exceeding 2 T at a 10 mm period, outperforming alternative undulator technologies for short-period applications. This study aims to further enhance undulator performance by reducing the period length through optimization of staggered-array, half-moon-shaped REBCO bulks. We propose a novel dual-lunate-ring-based REBCO undulator geometry that enables control of magnetization currents within individual lunate rings. The coexistence of magnetization currents on both the upper and lower sides of the undulator axis contributes to a significant enhancement of the on-axis magnetic field. To evaluate the performance of this design, the undulator period and gap were fixed at 7 mm and 3.3 mm, respectively, while the dimensions of the bulk rings were optimized numerically using the H-formulation. When the background solenoidal field changes from 10T to zero (ΔBs = 10 T), the undulator is expected to achieve a field strength B0 of 1.65T with a 7 mm period, corresponding to a deflection parameter of K = 1.1.

title: Dynamics of the Electric-Driven Gas Compressor Station Under Electric and Gas Network Disturbances
authors: Amin Salehi, Mahdi Pourakbari-Kasmaei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article focuses on the interaction between the electricity and hydrogen networks. Increasing the share of hydrogen production in demand-supply can impact the dynamics of equipment involved in the hydrogen grid, which may affect the security and stability of both power and hydrogen networks. One of these components is the electric-driven compressor station (EDCS). This article, for the first time, evaluates the effects of potential disturbances from gas grid sides on the dynamics of the EDCS. Then, it further investigates the impacts of joint electric and gas network disturbances on the dynamics of EDCS. Two approaches, namely analytical and MATLAB Simulink, are developed to model the dynamics of the EDCS under various disturbances. This gives a better flexibility for researchers and different stakeholders to utilize the model in their software. The proposed framework is further developed for parallel configuration of the EDCSs, with the possibility of extending to unlimited parallel EDCSs setup. Test results indicate that the electricity and gas grid disturbances affect the EDCS’s electric and gas state variables. The electric state variable is the rotational speed of the EDCS, while the gas state variables include compressor flow, suction pressure, and discharge pressure. Outcomes demonstrate that the gas state variables significantly change whether the compressor faces the electric or gas network disturbances. On the other hand, the electric state variable dramatically changes only when the compressor faces the power network disturbances.

title: UniOrch: A Unified Mixed Framework for High-Efficiency LLM Training on Heterogeneous AI Chips
authors: Jingyi Wang, Jia Wang, Jun Gao, Yan Cao, Yang Zhai, Haojie Wang, Wanxin Song, Dezhi Kong, Liu Liu, Wei Li, Zhaofeng He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Efficient coordination of heterogeneous AI chips (GPU/NPU/DCU) in data centers is crucial for Large Language Model (LLM) training, but this process is hindered by architectural mismatches, protocol fragmentation, and network partitioning. Existing solutions fail to achieve unified resource management across different chips, resulting in severe resource fragmentation and reduced allreduce efficiency. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes the unified coordination framework UniOrch, which not only integrates three core functionalities, including hardware abstraction, software standardization, and communication coordination, but also enables the training and inference of large models across heterogeneous AI chips. UniOrch's hardware-agnostic bare-metal cloud eliminates virtualization overhead through Border Gateway Protocol Ethernet Virtual Private Network (BGP EVPN) overlay networks and gateway-based chip integration; its PyTorch-based adaptation layer masks hardware differences and reduces migration costs; the Transformer Collective Communication Library (TCCL) unifies NCCL, HCCL, and OpenMPIprotocols to support seamless hybrid parallel training. Furthermore, the framework ' score scheduling mechanism is the Heterogeneous Hybrid Estimation Model (HHEM), which employs a two stage cost model combining static analysis with dynamic runtime feedback to dynamically allocate computing power based on Transformer task loads, ensuring cross-chip task synchronization, resource pooling, and dynamic allocation. Deployment verification in real-world production environments (e.g., China Construction Bank) shows that UniOrch achieves significant improvements: resource utilization of heterogeneous AI infrastructure is increased by 35%, cross-chip latency is reduced by 42%, accuracy loss in heterogeneous environments is <0.8%.

title: Zero-Pose-Prior NeRF: Recursive Radiance Field Reconstruction from Unposed and Unordered Images
authors: Xinxin Liu, Qi Zhang, Xue Wang, Guoqing Zhou, Qing Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: The dependence of neural radiance fields (NeRF) on accurate camera poses has emerged as a critical obstacle to their widespread real-world applications. While recent advances have demonstrated the potential for simultaneously addressing camera registration and scene reconstruction, these methods inherently rely on reasonable initialization derived from pose or scene priors and struggle with complex scenes involving large camera motions, particularly in unordered 360-degree scenes. In this work, we propose Zero-Pose-Prior NeRF to recover radiance fields from unposed and unordered image collections without any prior knowledge. Our key insight is to decompose this complex problem into smaller sub-problems, wherein the sub-problems’ camera poses are initially estimated to provide self-bootstrapping priors for the global pose estimation, followed by a recursive registration and reconstruction. To achieve this, we first perform scene partitioning to establish a hierarchical structure that describes registration order from local to global. Thereafter, we devise a conditionally-decoupled positional encoding for NeRFs, which serves as the basic model for camera pose estimation and scene representation. Following this, we develop a recursive registration to recursively estimate the poses of local scenes and register them into a unified global pose space, ultimately enabling the reconstruction of the entire scene. Experiments on real-world scenes show that our approach outperforms the state-of-theart pose-free methods in terms of accurate camera poses and robust radiance field reconstruction, resulting in high-fidelity view synthesis.

title: IEEE Control Systems Society Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control

title: Winfree-Model-Type Synchronization Control for Swimming Modular Robot through Physical Interaction
authors: Kohei Nishikawa, Hayato Dan, Daisuke Kurabayashi
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper proposes a Winfree-model-type synchronization control method for a swimming modular robot. In addition to reconfigurability, fault tolerance, robustness, and adaptability are crucial characteristics for modular robots. Self-organization phenomena arising from physical interactions play an important role in achieving these capabilities. Building on the Winfree synchronization model, we develop a Winfree-model-type synchronization control approach and apply it to a swimming modular robot inspired by Volvocine algae. We experimentally verify that this method enables self-organized synchronization through physical interactions and contributes to stabilizing the posture of the robot colony. Furthermore, we demonstrate the fault tolerance of the system through experiments in which one module in the colony ceases operation. Extending the interaction-based philosophy demonstrated by TEGOTAE controllers to swimming modular robots, our proposed Winfree-type scheme functions independently of specific structural assumptions.

title: ISED 2025 Content Announcement Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)

title: Anticipating Human Behavior for Safe Navigation and Efficient Collaborative Manipulation with Mobile Service Robots
authors: Simon Bultmann, Raphael Memmesheimer, Jan Nogga, Julian Hau, Sven Behnke
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The anticipation of human behavior is a crucial capability for robots to interact with humans safely and efficiently. We employ a smart edge sensor network to provide global observations, future predictions, and goal information to integrate anticipatory behavior for the control of a mobile manipulation robot. We present approaches to anticipate human behavior in the context of safe navigation and collaborative mobile manipulation. First, we anticipate human motion by employing projections of predicted human trajectories from smart edge sensor observations into the planning map of a mobile robot. Second, we anticipate human intentions in a collaborative furniture-carrying task to achieve a given room layout. Our experiments indicate that anticipating human behavior allows for safer navigation and more efficient collaboration. Finally, we showcase an integrated robotic system that anticipates human behavior while collaborating with an operator to achieve a target room layout, including the placement of tables and chairs.

title: Improved Predator-based Point Cloud Registration Method for Inner Liners in Utility Tunnel
authors: Huijuan Zhang, Wangjiang Xie, Biying Dong, Zhechen Yang, Jie Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Prefabricated concrete inner liners are important components in the construction of utility tunnels by prefabricated assembly method. To solve the problem of pose estimation in the automatic installation of prefabricated concrete inner liners, an improved Predator-based point cloud registration method for large inner liners in utility tunnel is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the structured light camera is used to obtain the point cloud image of the inner liners, and the edge of the point cloud is extracted based on the edge index gradient feature to obtain the edge outline. Secondly, convolutional block attention module (CBAM) is introduced to make the features focus more on important local structures and points that may be located in overlapping areas. Thirdly, registration based on maximal clique (MAC) is used to improve the accuracy and efficiency of registration. Then, the loss function is improved to enhance the accuracy of point pair matching. Finally, the experiments are carried out on the public dataset 3DMatch/3DLoMatch and the real environment of the inner liners. The experimental results show that on the 3DMatch/3DLoMatch dataset, the registration recall rate of the proposed method is improved by 2.5%/11.7%, and the average time of single registration calculation efficiency is reduced by 77.6%. Compared with Predictor, the registration recall rate of the proposed method is increased by 3.23%, the relative rotation error is reduced by 0.268°, and the relative displacement error is reduced by 0.011m in the real environment. Code and models are available at: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/397898948_ImprovedPredator

title: Responsible AI Monetization: Ethical and Economic Implications of Algorithmic Business Models
authors: Durga Krishnamoorthy, Raghu Para
publish: 2025 International Conference on Responsible, Generative and Explainable AI (ResGenXAI)
abstract: As businesses push to monetize from AI, from powering social media feeds to driving financial decision tools, they're unlocking major economic opportunities but also surfacing complex ethical dilemmas. This paper explores how organizations can balance profit with principles of fairness, transparency, and accountability. It highlights real-world cases where AI-driven monetization has led to bias, misinformation, and loss of trust, underscoring the risks of a short-term, revenue-first approach. To address this, we introduce the Responsible AI Monetization (RAIM) framework, a practical guide designed to help businesses innovate while avoiding ethical pitfalls. The findings suggest that embedding ethics into AI strategy not only safeguards against regulatory and reputational risks but also builds consumer confidence and supports sustainable growth. Through case studies and actionable guidance, the paper outlines how enterprises can create profitable AI solutions while ensuring that long-term value and responsibility remain at the core.

title: Theme: Smart Technologies and Systems
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)

title: A Common Source Impedance Compensation Method to Achieve Balanced Dynamic Current Sharing of Paralleled SiC MOSFETs
authors: Yan Wang, Laili Wang, Ting Hou, C.C. Chan, K.T. Chau, Wei Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Parallel connection of SiC MOSFETs is widely employed to increase current handling capability in high-power equipment. However, in multichip parallel configurations, establishing symmetrical layouts poses significant challenges. These asymmetries can induce current imbalance that ultimately lead to device derating. This paper proposes a quantitative model based on common-source impedance compensation to suppress current imbalance in asymmetrically arranged multi-chip paralleling configurations. The effectiveness of the proposed methodology has been experimentally validated through 500V/319A double-pulse tests, which demonstrate the capability to achieve complete current sharing of this method.

title: Optimizing Stacking Ensemble Learning to Enhance Meta-Model Performance in Forest Fire Risk Level Detection
authors: Yuda Irawan, Sarjon Defit, Rini Sovia
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Indonesia is one of the countries most vulnerable to forest and land fires, especially in the Riau region, which is declared an emergency alert almost every year. These impacts damage the ecosystem, while endangering public health and disrupting economic stability. Therefore, an accurate and reliable fire risk detection system based on meteorological data is needed. This study proposes a detection model with a stacking approach that integrates six algorithms as a base model, namely C4.5, LightGBM, HistGradientBoosting, MLP, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest. The prediction results of the base model are used to form a new training set which is then processed by the Random Forest meta model, which is further boosted using XGBoost and hyperparameter optimization with Optuna. The preprocessing workflow improves data quality by applying StandardScaler for normalization, LASSO for feature selection, and SMOTE-ENN for class balancing. Model evaluation uses 10-Fold Cross Validation, classification reports, confusion matrices, and ROC curves. The experimental results show an average accuracy of 93.66%; Precision, recall, and F1 score each reached 94%. The best AUC was 0.979, outperforming the baseline stacking. Experimental evidence suggests that integrating stacking with XGBoost and Optuna hyperparameter tuning results in more accurate detection of forest fire risk levels.

title: Dialogue Generation for Robot Family Using ROS and Generative AI: Initial Implementation of Centralized and Distributed Systems
authors: Taichi Hirano, Fumihide Tanaka
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The problem of human isolation and loneliness is growing worldwide. Although there are ways to provide human support, there are sustainability issues, so support using conversational physical partners, such as robots, is attracting attention as an alternative solution. Most previous attempts have been based on a single robot, and there are few attempts that use multiple robots, especially those that form a family and try to save the user from isolation and loneliness. This study aims to develop a basic infrastructure system to realize such a family consisting of multiple robots and a single human. More specifically, in this paper we report on the implementation and preliminary verification of two types of dialogue generation for robots using ROS (Robot Operating System) and GPT-4o-mini: one is a centralized system (in which the command center generates conversation scenarios for each robot in batches) and the other is a distributed system (in which each robot generates conversations individually).

title: ICETISI 2025 List Reviewer Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)

title: Natural Language-Driven Automation for Network Device Management with Large Language Models
authors: Hasbian Alif, Yulius Denny Prabowo, Viga Laksa Hardjanto
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: An optimized network infrastructure is essential for ensuring the reliability, security, and growth of IT-based businesses. However, operating network devices often requires advanced technical knowledge, which can become a significant barrier for non-technical users, particularly in large-scale networks. This study presents the development of a web-based network automation application utilizing Natural Language Processing (NLP) to minimize dependency on deep technical expertise in network operations. Using Python, Netmiko, and the OpenAI API, the system translates natural language commands into executable network actions. Experimental evaluation consisting of 30 test cases shows that the system successfully executed the intended function calls in 27 cases (90% success rate). User interface latency was recorded between 2 and 10 seconds, while device connection times ranged from 3 to 5 seconds. All reported values are based on direct measurement during controlled functional testing. These results demonstrate that NLP-based automation can effectively simplify network operations, improve accessibility, and enhance efficiency in managing network infrastructure.

title: Evaluating the Out-of-Distribution Generalization of Robot Diffusion Policies under the DINOv2 Visual Encoder
authors: Ángel Montejo, Iñigo Iturrate
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The generalizability of visuomotor policy models is crucial for their real-world usefulness in settings such as industrial environments. This is heavily impacted by the choice of visual encoder. In this paper, we integrate the DINOv2 foundation visual encoder with Diffusion Policy by designing a spatially-aware projection head, that allows the policy to shape its visual representation while benefiting from DINOv2’s robust embeddings. We evaluate this in drastic out-of-distribution conditions. As success rate can be uninformative in these conditions, where failure rates are high, we present three evaluation criteria for goal-driven policies that remain informative despite task failure. Our result shows that our approach outperforms the baseline under color alterations and camera displacements. We observe promising emergent task-relevant feature tracking using the DINOv2 visual encoder for policy learning.

title: Single-Machine Equivalent Modelling of Wind Farms Based on Data-Driven Multiplier Parameter Correction
authors: Weihan Hao, Jinchuan Guo, Cheng Su, Jianwu Wang, Yiping Lu, Zehaon Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The rapid development of new energy power systems has raised higher demands for the accuracy and efficiency of wind farm equivalent modeling. To address the insufficient impedance parameter adaptation capability of the traditional single-machine multiplication model, this paper proposes a single-machine equivalent modeling method for wind farms based on data-driven multiplier parameter correction. By constructing a Backpropagation Neural Network (BP), a nonlinear mapping relationship is established between the number of units, operating conditions, and impedance parameters, enabling adaptive parameter adjustment. Additionally, a multi-machine equivalent model is built by clustering based on output distribution characteristics. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to traditional methods, the proposed approach significantly reduces active and reactive power errors under various operating conditions, effectively improves dynamic response accuracy while maintaining modeling efficiency, and provides a novel solution for efficient modeling of new energy power stations.

title: Integrating Automatic Force Assistance Configuration with Mixed Reality for Active Exoskeletons
authors: Olmo A. Moreno Franco, Gabriele Giurin, Yonas T. Tefera, Christian Di Natali, Luigi Monica, Darwin G. Caldwell, Jesus Ortiz
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Work-related musculoskeletal disorders are highly prevalent in physically demanding industries due to manual material handling tasks such as lifting, hauling, and carrying heavy loads. Occupational exoskeleton technology has emerged to help mitigate these injuries. Exoskeletons are wearable devices that replicate the structure of the human body to enable mechanical interaction between the user and the system. Active exoskeletons use powered actuators and sensors to provide versatile and adaptive assistance for demanding tasks. However, a gap remains in user interaction and intuitive control for these systems. Human-computer interaction technologies, including mixed, virtual and augmented reality, offer novel solutions to enhance user interaction and enable intuitive, customisable control strategies for active exoskeletons. This study integrates mixed-reality technologies with the XoTrunk active occupational exoskeleton to enable parameter tuning and system calibration through immersive interfaces. Two alternative mixed-reality interfaces were developed for this purpose: one that relies on manual user input and the other that incorporates computer vision for automatic adjustment. Experiments involving 15 participants were conducted to evaluate interfaces by performing setup and operational activities while wearing the XoTrunk exoskeleton. The results showed that the automatic interface achieved a higher System Usability Scale score (84.83/100) compared to the manual interface (78.5/100), indicating improved user acceptance and intuitiveness.

title: Accurate PQ-ωV Admittance Model for Parallel VSC Systems
authors: Ziwen Zhao, Ronghui An, Zhiheng Huang, Boyang Shen, Hongwei Zhou, Jinjun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes an accurate PQ-ωV admittance model for parallel voltage-source converters with grid connection, comprehensively covering the dynamics of the power control, voltage-current dual-loop control, and all circuit components. Based on a unified synchronous reference frame of the system, the proposed model provides a tool to precisely depict the system stability and the oscillation characteristics of active/reactive power, frequency and voltage amplitude. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed modeling approach.

title: VAE-Based Synthetic EMG Generation with Mix-Consistency Loss for Recognizing Unseen Motion Combinations
authors: Itsuki Yazawa, Akira Furui
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Electromyogram (EMG)-based motion classification using machine learning has been widely employed in applications such as prosthesis control. While previous studies have explored generating synthetic patterns of combined motions to reduce training data requirements, these methods assume that combined motions can be represented as linear combinations of basic motions. However, this assumption often fails due to complex neuromuscular phenomena such as muscle co-contraction, resulting in low-fidelity synthetic signals and degraded classification performance. To address this limitation, we propose a novel method that learns to synthesize combined motion patterns in a structured latent space. Specifically, we employ a variational autoencoder (VAE) to encode EMG signals into a low-dimensional representation and introduce a mix-consistency loss that structures the latent space such that combined motions are embedded between their constituent basic motions. Synthetic patterns are then generated within this structured latent space and used to train classifiers for recognizing unseen combined motions. We validated our approach through upper-limb motion classification experiments with eight healthy participants. The results demonstrate that our method outperforms input-space synthesis approaches, achieving approximately 30% improvement in accuracy.

title: Analysis and Design of a Novel 13.56 MHz Push-Pull Class E Inverter
authors: Jingbo Cui, Yueshi Guan, Yi Cheng, Yijie Wang, Dianguo Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The radio frequency (RF) power amplifier constitutes an indispensable component in numerous academic, medical, and industrial applications.
