The robotic hands incorporated into such systems must possess capabilities comparable to those of human operators to accomplish a wide range of tasks.In this study, we present the design and development of a tendon-driven robotic hand intended for integration into tele-operated systems. The target tasks encompass both dexterous manipulations and force-intensive operations typically performed by humans. To achieve these capabilities, we first defined the performance requirements necessary for task execution and analytically derived the link ratios that maximize gripping efficiency. From these, we further identified the link ratio that yields the largest operational workspace, thereby narrowing the range of suitable design parameters.Based on the analytical findings, we devised an optimized link structure and tendon routing configuration tailored for tendon-driven actuation. A corresponding control system was implemented to operate the robotic hand and realize precise tendon-driven motion. Finally, we evaluated the system’s ability to perform the designated tasks, demonstrating that the proposed robotic hand can effectively serve as a substitute for human operators in tele-operated applications.

title: MAEIE 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)

title: Energy-Efficient Adaptive Beamforming for 5G Massive Mimo Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: G. Kanagavalli, P. Dhinakar
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Massive MIMO is the backbone of 5 G network operation with tremendous improvement in spectral efficiency and network capacity. The conventional beamforming methods are hindered by excessive computational complexity and energy consumption, limiting their scalability in dynamic wireless networks. To address this challenge, introducing an adaptive energy-efficient beamforming system that employs Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) with hybrid analog-digital beamforming. The main goal is to realize real-time optimization of beam patterns that are optimized in accordance with user mobility, interference, and traffic dynamics. The proposed system dynamically adjusts beam configurations, thus saving energy while ensuring optimal signal quality, and also introduce a new low-overhead Channel State Information (CSI) feedback scheme with the goal of avoiding redundant updates in CSI feedback, consequently leading to a tremendous improvement in power efficiency. The novelty in this work is its DRL-based predictive beam adaptation and energy-efficient CSI management, combined to enhance spectral efficiency and decrease computational complexity. Simulation results validate the system's enhanced performance, making it an efficient and sustainable solution for future 5 G networks.

title: Performance Comparison of Machine Learning Models in Data-Driven Receiver Circuit Design
authors: Yichen Gu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and electronic design automation (EDA) technologies, data-driven automation methods are gradually becoming the core approach driving electronic system design, optimization, and verification. Traditional circuit design methods typically rely on engineers’ experience and repeated simulations, facing challenges such as high computational complexity, long debugging cycles, and large-dimensional design parameters. This paper systematically explores an automated design framework based on data-driven principles, comprehensively comparing the performance differences among several mainstream machine learning models (linear regression, ridge regression, decision tree, random forest, and XGBoost). Combined with the SHAP feature importance analysis method, the study further investigates the interpretability and optimization potential of these models in Receiver circuit design. Through empirical analysis on a Receiver circuit dataset, the study finds that XGBoost and random forest models outperform traditional linear algorithms in terms of Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) and the coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), exhibiting stronger generalization and stability. The results demonstrate that data-driven automated design methods offer significant advantages in shortening the design cycle, improving prediction accuracy, and enabling adaptive circuit optimization.

title: Leveraging Graph Databases for Real-Time Spam Detection on Social Media
authors: Sakshi Srivastava, Dhirendra Kumar Shukla
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Spam is a recurring problem on Twitter, a well-known microblogging site, impairing user experience and the integrity of information sharing. This paper explores an innovative approach to tackle this issue by employing Neo4j, a graph database, for Twitter spam detection. Our methodology involves modeling the Twitter ecosystem as a graph, representing users, tweets, and relationships as interconnected nodes and edges. We extract significant patterns and features suggestive of spam behavior by utilizing Neo4j’s graph processing capabilities. Finding unusual activity, like coordinated spam campaigns and dishonest user interactions, is made easier by the graph structure. We experimented with a variety of datasets that included both legitimate and spam-related Twitter activity. The outcomes show how well our method works to separate spam from authentic content. Additionally, the graph-based model demonstrates flexibility in response to changing spam strategies, offering a reliable real-time spam detection solution on Twitter. By utilizing the capabilities of graph databases, particularly Neo4j, in the context of social media platforms, this study advances spam detection techniques. The knowledge gathered from this study improves our comprehension of spam detection and has applications in protecting online communication channels from changing threats.

title: YOLO-Based Framework for Efficient Lung Cancer Detection from CT Imaging
authors: Sujal Khadgi, Priyanshu Gawate, Gaurav Machade, Rahul Agrawal, Nekita Chavan, Chetan Dhule
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Lung cancer is a dangerous disease that is causing many deaths worldwide. If it is detected early, the chances of being cured increase. Computed Tomography (CT) is a standard tool for lung cancer screening, but manually checking CT images is very time consuming and there can also be variance among radiologists. To automate this process, deep learning techniques will be used, which will provide faster and more reliable outcomes for lung cancer detection. The You Only Look Once (YOLO) object detection and segmentation framework was applied in this study to exactly identify and locate cancerous regions by creating boundary boxes around them. The model was trained and tested on the RIDER CT dataset, which contains scans reconstructed under various imaging conditions. YOLO's real-time detection capabilities were used in the system's architecture to improve diagnostic efficiency without affecting accuracy. The system is made to improve diagnostic effectiveness without sacrificing precision and strong performance in terms of precision, recall, and total detection accuracy.

title: An Improved Harmonic Detection Method Based on Multi-synchronous Rotating Frame with Mean Filtering
authors: Wenbo Zhu, Yao Li, Hao Qin, Yan Deng, Xiangning He, Ramesh Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the development of power system and the large-scale application of power electronic devices in power grid the harmonic problems have gradually become the focus of attention. Harmonic detection algorithm is very important in harmonic elimination device. The traditional harmonic detection method in multi-synchronous rotating coordinate system utilizes a low-pass filter(LPF), and the accuracy of detection results depend on the performance of the filter. Meanwhile, the detection of negative-sequence and zero-sequence components resulting from load asymmetry in power grid should also be taken into consideration. Therefore, this paper proposes a multi harmonic detection method based on the periodic mean method in the multi-synchronous rotating coordinate system. The corresponding treatment methods of positive sequence and negative sequence harmonics for unbalanced power grid are summarized. The simulation results show that the proposed periodic mean multi-synchronous rotating harmonic detection method can effectively extract the different harmonic components in the power grid and can achieve no static error detection.

title: QDM-RNN: Acquisition of High-speed and Robust Behavior from Low-speed Demonstrations
authors: Masaki Yoshikawa, Hiroshi Ito, Hyogo Hiruma, Tetsuya Ogata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Imitation learning has gained significant attention as a promising approach to enable flexible and generalizable robot motion generation across diverse tasks. However, existing models often suffer from long inference times, limiting their applicability to high-speed and fine-grained tasks. Moreover, while faster computation enables shorter inference cycles, it introduces new challenges such as overcontrol and motion instability when the inference frequency exceeds the sensor sampling rate. To address these issues, we propose QDM-RNN, a lightweight motion generation model that learns from slow but high-quality demonstrations and remains robust under high-frequency inference. Our method utilizes Softmax Transformation, which discretizes the robot end-effector pose into a high-resolution probability distribution, enabling accurate and smooth trajectory prediction. Furthermore, by incorporating multi-timestep prediction, which simultaneously predicts several future steps, our model mitigates instability arising from the mismatch between sensor and inference rates, ensuring consistent long-horizon motion generation. We validated the effectiveness of our approach through real-world robotic experiments on a Sport Stacking task, which requires both high speed and precision. QDM-RNN maintained high success rates and motion stability at speeds up to seven times faster than demonstrations, and showed robustness to external disturbances including background changes, lighting variations, dynamic object perturbations, and obstacles.

title: Scalable Vehicle Toll Model using Second Layer Blockchain
authors: Sujit Sangram Sahoo, Akash Kumar, Pravas Ranjan Bal, Vijay K. Chaurasiya
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Roadways worldwide are moving towards digitisation to enhance their operations; one of these operations is Toll tax collection, which can be digitised to reduce the time consumption in the traditional Toll tax collection System. One of the challenges for the digitisation of Toll tax collection is data integration and security. The blockchain technology used in this project ensures data integration and security. This paper proposed the design and implementation of the Automatic Vehicle Toll Collection System. The system enables the Toll tax collection authority to collect toll tax by tracking the travel journey of vehicles and eliminating the traditional method of managing tax in physical Toll booths, which is time-consuming and expensive to maintain. The system utilises Ethereum smart contracts during registration to ensure the smooth processing of the tax generation process. Every Vehicle integrates with an in-vehicle unit that uses a GPS device to acquire the Vehicle’s current position and final destination. Moreover, the proposed model incorporates micro-transactions through an off-chain payment system. The entire model is free from the scalability issues of on-chain blockchain transactions, Such as Flood and loot attacks. It provides the blockchain transaction finality time $\Delta$. The proposed theorems ensure security; the results demonstrate the correct functioning and reduced response time of smart contracts.

title: Design and Implementation of A DSP-Enhanced RISC-V Pipelined Processor Using Verilog HDL
authors: Aditya Goswami, Kumar Rishi, Vineet Kumar, Nikhat Anjum, Pragyan Parimita Padhan, Prashant Pranav, Pooja Kumari, Pratyush Pranjal, Vijay Nath
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a 5-stage pipelined RISC-V processor architecture augmented with DSP (Digital Signal Processing) capabilities using Verilog HDL. The processor architecture integrates essential pipeline stages-Instruction Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory Access, and Write Back-enhanced by advanced modules such as hazard detection and forwarding units to support efficient instructionlevel parallelism. A specialised ALU is developed to support both arithmetic/logical operations, as well as DSP-centric computations, optimising performance for signal processing applications. The system is verified using a custom testbench in Vivado 2025.1, and waveform analysis confirms accurate pipeline behaviour and DSP instruction execution. This processor is targeted for embedded and real-time systems requiring lowpower, high-performance computation with programmable logic integration.

title: Comparing a Store-less Password Manager with Traditional Password-Only Authentication
authors: Mohammed Jubur, Maliheh Shirvanian, Salahaldeen Duraibi, Nitesh Saxena
publish: IEEE Internet Computing
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive usability evaluation of HIPPO (Hidden-Password Online Password), a novel store-less password manager, compared to traditional password-only authentication methods. In the digital age, robust password management solutions are essential to mitigate cognitive burdens and enhance security. Unlike conventional store-based password managers, HIPPO dynamically generates passwords without storing them, significantly reducing the risk of data breaches. Our study involved 25 participants who performed tasks such as installation, configuration, password updating, and login using both HIPPO and traditional methods. The findings reveal that HIPPO offers significant security advantages and is perceived as more secure and trustworthy by users. While HIPPO introduces additional steps for password generation and entry, participants reported higher satisfaction and ease of use compared to traditional password-only authentication. This study highlights the trade-offs between security and usability in password management, providing valuable insights for developing more user-friendly and secure password management tools.

title: Analysis of Machine Learning Framework for Burnout Detection Using Social Media Sentiment
authors: Nur Syafiqah Mohd Nafis, Suryanti Awang, Nur Hafieza Ismail, Nureen Afiqah Mohd Zaini, Mohammed Ahmed Talab
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Mental illness issues comprise a broad spectrum of conditions that impact a person’s thoughts, emotions, behavior, and psychological health. Burnout is one of the mental illness issues caused by excessive work-related stress. It has become a major problem in several industries, including IT, healthcare, and education. The increasing burnout rate, especially in the aftermath of the Coronavirus disease (COVID-19) pandemic, highlights the urgent need for early detection. Preventive strategies can be ruled out to lessen their negative side effects on worker turnover and general well-being. This study aims to analyze the trend of sentiment classification as a predictive tool for predicting mental illness by utilizing the immense quantity of unstructured data from social media platforms. Through a review of the literature on sentiment classification frameworks and Machine Learning (ML) (e.g. Neural Network (NN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), and Random Forest (RF)) applications in mental illness disorder prediction, this study makes clear that using social media data to identify early indicators of burnout is feasible. Ultimately, a more resilient and healthier workforce can be achieved by using sentiment classification as a viable tool for employers to track employee well-being and swiftly intervene.

title: A Compact and High-Conversion-Gain W-Band Differential Distributed Frequency Doubler With Over-NeutralizingSecond-Harmonic Feedback
authors: Dongho Yoo, Byung-Wook Min
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a compact W-band distributed frequency doubler that achieves high-conversion gain (CG) by employing over-neutralized second-harmonic (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) feedback. The proposed two-stage distributed doubler consists of two push–push frequency doublers (PPFDs). A transmission line with a 90° electrical length is inserted between the two PPFD stages to generate in-phase and quadrature-phase signals. Compared to conventional quadrature-coupler-based topologies, this network achieves a smaller chip area and reduced insertion loss. Furthermore, cross-coupled capacitors (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C_{cc}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) are connected from the drain of one PPFD to the gate of the other. By choosing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C_{cc}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> larger than the parasitic gate-to-drain capacitor, over-neutralization is achieved. This converts the inherent negative feedback into positive feedback, enabling constructive summation at the drain. As a result, the doubler exhibits enhanced CG, total efficiency, saturated <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> output power, and fundamental (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) rejection. In addition, the matching network of the doubler is designed to operate as an input matching network at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and as a reflector at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, compensating the phase variation of the positive-feedback path to ensure perfect constructive summation. The measured peak CG of the proposed doubler is 0.1 dB. The saturated output power is 6.7 dBm, and the peak total efficiency is 6.2% at a DC power consumption of 49 mW. The measured 3-dB bandwidth is 13.9 GHz, which is from 74.8 to 88.7 GHz, and within this bandwidth, the fundamental rejection ratio (FRR) exceeds 36 dBc. The core area is only 0.05 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating the compactness of the proposed design.

title: Motion Simulation of the Human Upper Limb Based on Optimal Control
authors: Miki Eto, Masahiro Migita, Masashi Toda, Kazuaki Kondo, Qi An, Junichi Akita, Yuichi Nakamura
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Human upper limb movements are performed with smooth and coordinated control, reflecting complex neuromuscular mechanisms. Quantitative analysis of these movements is essential for understanding joint load distribution and preventing excessive stress during daily or occupational tasks. In recent years, simulation-based approaches that integrate musculoskeletal modeling and optimal control have become powerful tools for motion analysis. However, while lower limb movements have been extensively studied, fundamental analyses of simple upper limb motions remain limited. In this study, we employed a torque-driven upper limb model using OpenSim Moco to generate elbow flexion motion and evaluated its mechanical behavior under unloaded and loaded conditions. The results demonstrated that the model produced smooth and physiologically reasonable torque profiles, with the joint torque increasing appropriately in response to external loads. These findings indicate that even a simplified torque-driven formulation can stably reproduce fundamental motion characteristics, providing a useful basis for future extensions toward muscle-driven and fatigue-aware simulations.

title: Bibliometric Landscape of Text and Document Classification Research in Natural Language Processing
authors: Lorensius Anang Setiyo Waloyo, Mochamad Agung Wibowo, Ridwan Sanjaya
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study presents a bibliometric analysis of research developments in Natural Language Processing (NLP), with a particular focus on text and document classification. The analysis was conducted using a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) approach with the PRISMA framework on publications indexed in the Scopus database between 2010 and 2024. A total of 2,480 articles were collected, cleaned, and analyzed using VOSviewer to visualize bibliometric networks. The results show a significant growth of NLP-related publications, with an annual increase rate of 19.37%, especially after 2020. Keyword co-occurrence analysis identified five major research clusters: sentiment analysis with classical techniques, deep learning and neural models, modern language models and clinical applications, social media and network analysis, and multilingual artificial intelligence. These findings highlight a paradigm shift from traditional feature-based methods to large-scale representation learning with models such as BERT and GPT. The study concludes that NLP research is increasingly multidisciplinary, emphasizing accuracy, sustainability, security, and applications in complex social and clinical domains.

title: Design and Implementation of a Human Behavior Recognition Information System Based on RFID and AI
authors: Wei Huang, Yayan Xie, Shaoqin Shen, Jiaqian Jiang, Lvqing Yang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: As China’s population ages, the issue of elderly care has become an increasingly focal point of societal concern. This paper designs and implements a human behavior recognition information system based on radio frequency identification (RFID) technology and artificial intelligence (AI) to improve monitoring of daily activities, enable faster emergency responses, and enhance overall safety for older adults. The primary focus is on applications in nursing homes and rehabilitation healthcare institutions. It provides an integrated solution spanning RFID hardware to software. By integrating modern programming architectures with RFID technology, the system achieves effective recognition and localization of human behavior. The system employs high-performance asynchronous communication modules for data acquisition and constructs recognition and positioning models based on convolutional neural networks (CNN) and k-nearest neighbors (KNN) algorithms. This approach significantly enhances perception accuracy and system efficiency.Experimental results demonstrate that this method achieves a recognition accuracy of approximately 98.62% on a small dataset of 10,000 samples, which positively contributes to advancing intelligent development in rehabilitation healthcare and related fields.

title: Deep Learning-Based Meat Freshness Detection with Segmentation and OOD-Aware Classification
authors: Hutama Arif Bramantyo, Mukarram Ali Faridi, Rui Chen, Clarissa Harris, Yin Sun
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this study, we present a meat freshness classification framework from Red–Green–Blue (RGB) images that supports both packaged and unpackaged meat datasets. The system classifies four indistribution (ID) meat classes and uses an out-of-distribution (OOD)-aware abstention mechanism that flags low-confidence samples as No Result. The pipeline combines U-Net-based segmentation with deep feature classifiers. Segmentation is used as a preprocessing step to isolate the meat region and reduce background, producing more consistent inputs for classification. The segmentation module achieved an Intersection over Union (IoU) of 75% and a Dice coefficient of 82%, producing standardized inputs for the classification stage. For classification, we benchmark five backbones: Residual Network-50 (ResNet-50), Vision Transformer- Base/16 (ViT-B/16), Swin Transformer-Tiny (Swin-T), EfficientNet-B0, and MobileNetV3-Small. We use nested 5×3 cross-validation (CV) for model selection and hyperparameter tuning. On the held-out ID test set, EfficientNet-B0 achieves the highest accuracy (98.10%), followed by ResNet-50 and MobileNetV3-Small (both 97.63%) and Swin-T (97.51%), while ViT-B/16 is lower (94.42%). We additionally evaluate OOD scoring and thresholding using standard OOD metrics and sensitivity analysis over the abstention threshold. Finally, we report on-device latency using TensorFlow Lite (TFLite) on a smartphone, highlighting practical accuracy–latency trade-offs for future deployment.

title: Harnessing Attention Weight Tables for Computationally Efficient Multiple Object Tracking With Transformers
authors: Xing Hexu, Braun Torsten
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Transformer-based architectures have introduced end-to-end solutions for Multiple Object Tracking (MOT), seamlessly integrating object detection and association. However, their high computational demands—such as the need for feature map fusion across multiple frames—pose significant challenges to real-time deployment, limiting their practicality. In this paper, we present WT-MOT (Weight Table-based Multiple Object Tracking), a novel framework that addresses these limitations by leveraging the underutilized potential of attention weight tables for efficient object similarity evaluation. WT-MOT employs self- and cross-attention mechanisms to assess object similarity and directly assign identifications, integrating spatial, appearance, and temporal dimensions. By introducing the “frame embedding” concept, WT-MOT enhances the ability to distinguish objects across frames without relying on motion models or post-processing steps. Experimental results on the MOT17 and MOT20 benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of WT-MOT, achieving MOTA scores of 76.9% and 73.2%, respectively, setting new performance standards for Transformer-based MOT solutions. These findings highlight WT-MOT as a computationally efficient and robust tool for real-time MOT applications, paving the way for broader adoption of Transformer-based tracking methods in practical environments.

title: ESG-Net: Event-Aware Semantic Guided Network for Dense Audio-Visual Event Localization
authors: Huilai Li, Yonghao Dang, Ying Xing, Yiming Wang, Jianqin Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Dense audio-visual event localization (DAVE) aims to identify event categories and locate the temporal boundaries in untrimmed videos. For such challenging task settings, most studies only employ audio-visual event semantic constraints on the final outputs, lacking progressive cross-modal semantic bridging in intermediate layers. This causes semantic gaps that hinder alignment between representations in audio and visual features, making it difficult to distinguish between event-related and irrelevant background content. Moreover, they rarely consider the correlations between events, which limits the model to infer co-occurring events among complex scenarios. In this paper, we incorporate multi-stage semantic guidance and multi-event relationship modeling, which respectively enable progressive semantic understanding of audio-visual events and adaptive extraction of event dependencies, thereby better focusing on event-related information. Specifically, our event-aware semantic guided network (ESG-Net) includes a early semantic interaction (ESI) module and a mixture of dependency experts (MoDE) module. ESI applys multi-stage semantic guidance to explicitly constrain the model in learning semantic information through multi-stage feature fusion and several classification loss functions, ensuring multi-stage understanding of event-related content. MoDE promotes the extraction of multi-event dependencies through multiple serial mixture of experts with adaptive weight allocation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method significantly surpasses the state-of-the-art methods, while greatly reducing parameters and computational load. Our code will be released on https://github.com/uchiha99999/ESG-Net.

title: Dependence of Mo/Au Transition-Edge Sensor Properties on Normal Resistance and Critical Temperature
authors: N. A. Wakeham, J. S. Adams, C. V. Ambarish, S. R. Bandler, R. B. Borrelli, J. A. Chervenak, F. A. Colazo Petit, R. S. Cumbee, N. S. DeNigris, T. Farrahi, F. M. Finkbeiner, J. D. Fuhrman, S. V. Hull, R. L. Kelley, C. A. Kilbourne, H. Muramatsu, F. S. Porter, A. Rani, K. Sakai, S. J. Smith, E. J. Wassell, M. C. Witthoeft, S. H. Yoon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: We have investigated the dependence of superconducting tran sition parameters on the bilayer properties in Mo/Au transition edge sensors (TESs). We present measurements of the normalized partial derivatives of TES resistance with respect to temperature (α) and current (β), in several fabricated wafers with differing bilayer properties, but in a single TES design characterized at the same relative point in the transition. The results show α increases and β decreases approximately linearly with TES temperature T and normal state resistance Rn. To study these dependencies further, we measured the temperature dependence of the critical current Ic(T), and found it decreases approxi mately exponentially at high temperature. The slope of Ic(T) on a logarithmic scale correlates with Rn but is independent of the transition temperature. We examine to what extent these f indings can explain the observed T and Rn dependence of α and β. These results have implications for our understanding of the fundamental physics of the transition in these devices, and the reproducibility of TES performance resulting from small changes in bilayer fabrication.

title: Automated Detection and Recognition of Engraved Serial Numbers on Metallic Surfaces
authors: Thanh Dat Nguyen, Sachin Ranjan, Le-Anh Tran, Kichul Lee, Moonsik Kang, Hoon Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Automated reading of engraved or printed serial numbers on metallic components remains a challenging problem in industrial manufacturing due to low contrast, reflective surfaces, arbitrary orientations, and surface degradation. Conventional optical character recognition (OCR) systems often fail in such environments, particularly when text is embedded within geometrically structured objects and appears under uncontrolled rotations. This paper presents a robust end-to-end vision pipeline for accurately recognizing numeric series on industrial metallic surfaces. The proposed approach first employs an oriented object detection model to localize target objects and their associated text regions using oriented bounding boxes. The detected text areas are then rotation-normalized through geometry-aware cropping and square padding, enabling consistent downstream recognition. A YOLO-based character recognition model is subsequently applied, with dual-orientation inference used to resolve rotational ambiguity. To further improve reliability, a confidence-driven post-processing strategy suppresses duplicate detections, enforces valid text-length constraints, and selects the most consistent recognition results. Experiments on real-world industrial data demonstrate that the proposed system achieves high end-to-end recognition accuracy and robustness under challenging imaging conditions, outperforming baseline configurations without orientation handling or post-processing. The proposed pipeline offers a practical and deployable solution for automated traceability and quality control in manufacturing environments.

title: A Parametric-Adaptive Filter for Improving LiDAR Performance Under Snowfall Conditions
authors: Xiangmo Zhao, Chenghao Sun, Pengpeng Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: LiDAR is widely used in autonomous driving and intelligent roadside perception systems due to its high detection accuracy and light independence. However, snowfall has a significant impact on LiDAR, increasing noise and changing object resolution in point clouds, posing a significant challenge to the task of traffic object detection using LiDAR. This paper proposes a probabilistic model-driven adaptive filter for effectively removing snowfall noise from LiDAR point clouds and improving LiDAR detection capabilities under snowfall conditions. First, the proposed filter extracts snow noise from a point cloud, creates a spatial distribution model for the snow noise, and obtains the model’s key parameters. Then, the snow noise is extracted using a density filter based on the spatial model, which is iteratively optimized. Compared to existing methods, the filter is more scene-adaptable and less dependent on priori datasets, and it can effectively remove snow noise from various types of LiDAR sensors under varying snowfall intensities. Experimental evaluation on publicly available datasets, including CADC, BOREAS, and WADS, demonstrates that the proposed method achieves an F1-Score of more than 90 on all three datasets, indicating excellent accuracy and generalizability. Additionally, testing object detection algorithms under snowfall conditions reveal that the proposed filter improves the accuracy of three typical detection algorithms by 5.9, 9.7, and 6.4 percentage points, indicating that our filter improves the performance of downstream 3D target detection tasks under snowfall.

title: Geo-SelfSSC: Integrating Dense Geometric Priors for Enhanced Self-Supervised Semantic Scene Completion
authors: Hao Zhu, Pute Guo, Xiaotong Li, Longsheng Qu, Jinjian Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Accurate 3D scene understanding is vital for applications like autonomous driving and robotics. However, existing voxel-based methods struggle with the reliance on large-scale labeled data, inherent voxel-pixel misalignments, and high computational costs. Recent self-supervised Semantic Scene Completion (SSC) methods using Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) reduce 3D annotation needs but assume Lambertian surfaces and rely on photometric consistency, which fails in real-world scenes with non-Lambertian effects and sparse camera coverage. In this paper, we introduce Geo-SelfSSC, a self-supervised framework that leverages temporal information from consecutive frames and slight variations in camera poses for supervision, while integrating dense geometric cues to enhance the reconstruction quality and efficiency of neural implicit models. Specifically, Geo-SelfSSC leverages depth priors along with complementary semi-local and local geometric supervision to facilitate efficient sampling, while ensuring effective complementarity between photometric and geometric cues. Our method yields significant improvements in reconstructing reflective and under-observed regions where conventional photometric-based strategies struggle. Comprehensive experiments on challenging tasks demonstrate that Geo-SelfSSC not only achieves strong results in semantic scene completion but also establishes competitive performance in geometry-only 3D occupancy prediction and monocular depth estimation. Our code and models are available at: https://github.com/Xidian AIGroup190726/GeoSelfSSC.

title: A ROS-Based Multi-Modal Architecture for Fall Detection and Response with a Social Robot
authors: Kavyan Zoughalian, Imene Tarakli, Aung Htet, Joshua Bamforth, Alejandro Jiménez-Rodriguez, Jims Marchang, Alessandro Di Nuovo
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Falls are a leading cause of injury in older adults, requiring detection systems that are both sensitive and reliable. We present a multi-modal robotic framework that integrates wearable sensing, vision-based verification, and dialogue-driven assessment. A smartwatch streams inertial data, with thresholds tuned through pilot testing to maximise fall sensitivity. Vision verification is performed using a fine-tuned YOLOv11 model, while Whisper ASR and a lightweight GPT-based classifier enable simple verbal checks of user responsiveness. Our tuned thresholds outperformed published baselines (F1 = 0.857), and the vision module achieved strong accuracy (mAP@0.5 = 0.827). In integrated trials, the system reached a 90.6% success rate with a mean end-to-end response time of 43.5 seconds. These results show that combining complementary modalities enhances robustness and moves socially assistive robots toward interactive fall response in real-world care.

title: Marketing Technology and Innovation in Financial Services: A Bibliometric Mapping of Research Trends
authors: Neviana Neviana, Rano Kartono, Mohammad Hamsal, Willy Gunadi, Asnan Furinto
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Advancements in digital marketing have transformed how financial institutions engage customers, deliver value, and implement innovation. This bibliometric study analyzes 110 Scopus-indexed publications from 2004 to 2025 to uncover evolving themes and conceptual developments in financial marketing research. Using VOSviewer, keyword co-occurrence networks, citation analysis, and publication dynamics were employed to identify structural patterns in the literature. The results reveal three dominant thematic areas: customer-centric strategies, financial digitalization through fintech and e-commerce, and enabling technologies such as artificial intelligence, big data, and algorithmic ethics. Research output surged after 2018, peaking in 2024, reflecting the post-pandemic momentum in digital transformation across financial services. Highly cited studies emphasize marketing automation, intelligent personalization, cyber-security, and trust-based engagement models. However, gaps remain in areas such as blockchain integration, cross-sector benchmarking, and performance evaluation frameworks for digital banking platforms. This study offers a structured intellectual map of the financial marketing landscape, highlighting both mature and emerging research fronts. The findings support scholars, practitioners, and policymakers in aligning digital marketing strategies with customer trust, regulatory expectations, and technological innovation. By revealing both conceptual foundations and future directions, this review contributes to strengthening the strategic role of marketing in the evolving digital financial ecosystem.

title: Research on Harmonic Suppression Strategy of Grid-Forming Impedance-Adapter-Based Droop-Controlled Converters
authors: Haonan Teng, Lei Li, Weiqi Zhu, Zhejun Zhan, Wanjun Lei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a grid-forming impedance-adapter control that shapes the converter small-signal impedance to mitigate harmonics and damp node resonance in distribution networks. Building on a sequential impedance model of the grid-forming converter, the scheme embeds a harmonic-aware virtual admittance while preserving fundamental voltage and frequency regulation. Design guidelines are provided to ensure suppression of node resonance under practical grid-impedance variations. The approach is validated on an 8-node distribution network via Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) experiments, showing marked reductions in PCC voltage distortion and resonance amplitude, thereby enhancing harmonic suppression performance.

title: DoC-Informer: Automated Discrimination of Disorders of Consciousness under Adaptive EEG Settings
authors: Fengyuan Yang, Longlin Pi, Dan Chen, Zhenhu Liang, Yong Wang, Ye Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Disorders of consciousness (DoC) diagnosis critically depends on accurate state discrimination to guide treatment and prognosis. Current EEG-based techniques face challenges of incomplete electrode coverage and manual feature reliance, due to the complex nature of DoC conditions. Methods: This study proposes DoC-Informer, a CNN-Transformer framework for automated DoC discrimination under adaptive EEG settings. By integrating a channel-independent architecture-enabled by electrode position encoding and spatial transformers-with channel masking training, the framework employs: 1) Shallow Temporal Feature Encoding with parallel temporal convolutions to extract channel-independent temporal features; 2) Spatiotemporal Representation Modeling using a Spatial Transformer (with 3D electrode position encoding) to infer spatial dependencies and a Temporal Transformer for long-range dynamics. A Channel Masking Training Strategy enhances robustness to incomplete data. Results: Extensive experiments on two real-world DoC datasets (including UWS and MCS patients) demonstrate DoC-Informer's superiority over the cutting-edge deep learning counterparts and a machine learning baseline, with results showing: 1) State-of-the-art performance, 2) Robustness to channel loss, and 3) Validated module efficacy via ablation studies. Conclusion and Significance: DoC-Informer bridges brain science and clinical needs by integrating anatomical priors (electrode coordinates) with adaptive deep learning. Its resilience to variable EEG configurations offers a practical solution for real-world DoC diagnosis, particularly in settings with sparse or incomplete recordings. The source code of the framework is available at https://github.com/pilonglin/docinformer.

title: ZVS Three-Phase Interleaved Totem-Pole Bridgeless PFC Converter
authors: Zheyuan Li, LiPan He, Han Wei, Hao Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Bridgeless totem-pole PFC converter is an ideal input AC-DC converter for the bidirectional converter required for G2V/V2G. However, the circuit operates in hard switching mode in CCM, which produces significant switching losses and limits further improvement in switching frequency. To handle this problem, a three-phase interleaved totem-pole PFC topology with three symmetrical auxiliary inductors is proposed in this paper. The proposed converter utilizes energy storage and release from the auxiliary inductors to achieve zero voltage switching for all high-frequency switches, thereby reducing switching losses and enabling higher switching frequency in high power level. This approach minimizes device size and enhances power density. The feasibility and optimization of the proposed converter is tested and verified with a 50Hz, 220Vrms input and 8.6kW output prototype which with adjustable output voltage from 360V to 440V.

title: Securing Biometric Authentication System using Blockchain Technology
authors: Vandana Kumari, Poonguzhali N
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: In an era where users' digital privacy and security are continuously breached, the convergence of biometric systems and decentralized systems is a potential field for secure authentication technology. The suggested system is to show an effective fingerprint biometric authentication system based on blockchain technology and deep learning models for accomplishing the objective of overcoming the main flaws of forgery, spoofing, and centralization vulnerability in conventional biometric systems. The system employs a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to learn discriminative feature vectors of fingerprints, which are subsequently hashed by the SHA-256 cryptographic hash algorithm. Such hashed information is kept and regulated by a blockchain network using smart contracts to render the fingerprint information immutable, decentralized, and traceable while preserving the original biometric material. The system demonstrates how the intersection of machine learning and blockchain can securely bypass the inherent vulnerability of conventional biometric solutions by decentralizing control and offering cryptographic safekeeping of privacy-related information. The results affirm that the hybrid approach offers an effective privacy-preserving, scalable, and tamper-evident solution for future biometric authentication.

title: Configurable Pneumatic Soft Actuators for Multi-Directional Wrist Rehabilitation
authors: Ibrahim A. Seleem, Hiroshi Takemura
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Due to their inherent compliance and safe interaction with their environment, soft robots play a significant role in wrist rehabilitation. However, current designs lack bending in multiple directions and have limited payload capacity. This article aims to explore various configurations of pneumatic soft fingers, specifically targeting the different bending directions of the wrist joint. A pair of parallel-connected soft actuators is mounted on the index and ring fingers to achieve extension and flexion movements by simultaneously pressurizing both actuators. Pronation and supination are achieved by activating two diagonally positioned actuators, while radial and ulnar motions are accomplished through two side-connected actuators attached to the little finger and thumb. A nonlinear static analysis based on the Yeoh model is conducted to validate the design while concerning its bending and deflection. A series of experiments is carried out to verify the bending and payload capacity of the pneumatic soft finger. The results show that it achieves a bending angle of 270°, while carrying a payload of 200 g. Moreover, the effectiveness of soft design is validated by bending a 660 g metal frame, which is designed to mimic a human hand, in multiple directions. Finally, four soft fingers were employed to successfully perform flexion deviation on a real human hand. This paper represents the initial phase of utilizing pneumatic soft fingers to achieve multiple wrist-bending movements.

title: Advancing Multimodal Sentiment Analysis: Models, Datasets, and Future Directions
authors: Shamim Ahmad, Parul Agarwal, Bhavya Alankar, Sameena Naaz
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) is an important topic in affective computing and natural language processing (NLP). The main aim of MSA is to understand human emotions in a better way. It does this by using text, images, and audio together. MSA is useful in many areas like social media monitoring, human-computer interaction, healthcare, education, and business. Using only one type of data is often not enough in these areas. Even though there has been good progress, many problems are still there. It is difficult to align information from different types of data. Models do not work well when used in a new domain. They are not very strong when some data is noisy or missing. Performance drops in low-resource and multilingual settings. Also, the models are not easy to understand or explain. Future research will try to solve these problems. It will focus on creating multimodal large language models. It will also work on few-shot and zero-shot learning methods. Researchers will try to build explainable and trustworthy multimodal models. They will also design lightweight and real-time systems that can be used easily in real situations. All these improvements can make MSA strong, easy to understand, and scalable. They can also make it useful for many real-world applications.

title: 3D-Printed Sub-Terahertz High-Scanning-Rate Leaky-Wave Antenna Based on Metallic Spoof Surface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Zheliang Liu, Yilin Gao, Hongda Lu, Lu Qian, Yong Liu, Steven Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: This letter proposes a 3D-printed sub-terahertz (sub-THz) fully metallic frequency-scanning (FS) periodic leaky-wave antenna (LWA) based on a spoof surface plasmon polaritons (SSPPs) transmission line. Glide symmetry is introduced into the conventional domino-type SSPPs transmission line to increase dispersion, thereby enabling a higher scanning rate. An asymmetric periodic modulation structure is designed to excite leaky-wave radiation while suppressing the open stopband (OSB) effect without extra elements. A gap waveguide (GWG) is utilized as the feeding structure, which mitigates energy leakage from potential assembly gaps and significantly reduces fabrication difficulty and assembly complexity. The SSPPs and GWG structures are monolithically fabricated using high-precision 3D printing and surface-metallized via magnetron sputtering of gold. Experimental measurements of a prototype demonstrate continuous beam scanning from -29° to +49° within the frequency range from 180 to 220 GHz, achieving a scanning rate of 3.9°/%BW. The peak gain varies between 11.13 dBi and 16.53 dBi. This work provides a cost-effective, high-performance and feasible solution for metallic FS LWAs, with promising applications in terahertz imaging and radar systems.

title: Docking-Based Insights into Phyllanthus Amarus Bioactives as Potential Inhibitors of Human Papillomavirus (HPV) E6 Protein
authors: Santhanalakshmi K, Vinoth M, Gomathi Thandapani, Kalaiarasi K
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Human Papillomavirus (HPV) is a major cause of cervical and other anogenital malignancies, owing to the carcinogenic activity of viral proteins including E6 and E7. Current medicines only give partial relief and have drawbacks such as resistance and toxicity. Natural phytochemicals are rapidly being identified as possible sources of safe and effective antivirals. Molecular docking analysis is used in this work to examine the antiviral potential of Phyllanthus amarus bioactive compounds against the HPV E6 protein. Phyllanthin, hypophyllanthin, and niruriflavone were docked with the E6 protein using AutoDock. The findings demonstrated high binding affinities, with phyllanthin having the best binding score (-9.1 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{kcal} / \text{mol}$</tex>), generating interactions with residues required for E6p-53 binding. These results indicate Phyllanthus amarus as a possible source of anti-HPV compounds.

title:
Topological Indices of Melem Chain, Polymethyl Methacrylate Network and SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Nanostructures
authors: K. Pattabiraman, A. Anivarsha, P. Danesh
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Predicting molecular properties is a challenging aspect of chemical research. Topological descriptors, derived from molecular graphs, provide numerical insights into structural and physicochemical behavior. Among them, distance-based descriptors play a vital role in characterizing molecular topology. This study examines the distance-based polynomial associated with Szeged indices and its application to structures such as the Melem chain, poly(methyl methacrylate) network, and SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> nanostructures.

title: A Multi-phase to Multi-phase Direct AC/AC Converter
authors: Ziming Li, Yi Lu, Wenyao Ye, Yanxia Yao, Jiachun You, Fujin Deng, Fujun Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: High-voltage, high-capacity AC/AC converters are widely used in various applications. Power conversion imposes diverse requirements on voltage amplitude, frequency, phase angle, and phase number. This paper proposes a novel multi-phase to multi-phase direct AC/AC converter, evolved from the modular multilevel converter (MMC) used for AC/DC conversion. Compared to the traditional modular multilevel matrix converter (M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>C), the proposed topology significantly reduces submodule count, simplifies control strategies, and offers excellent scalability.

title: Research on Grid-connected and Off-grid Control Strategy of Bidirectional VSG with Energy Storage Coordination
authors: Yifan Shi, Zhe Zhang, Hengchang Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG) presynchronization control method based on dynamic coordination of the energy storage system's State of Charge (SoC). By real-time monitoring of the SoC status of energy storage units and the voltage/phase information at the grid side, the stability of the VSG grid-connected presynchronization process in the energy storage system is achieved. Simulation verification on the MATLAB/Simulink platform confirms that the introduction of dynamic virtual resistance realizes intelligent suppression of the charging/discharging power of energy storage units, and provides an effective solution for the safe operation of VSG grid-connected systems under the integration of high-proportion new energy.

title: Quantum Annealed Geometric Mean Optimization for Calibrated Optimization of KAN
authors: Ayush Mishra, Nibedan Panda
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Optimization is crucial to modern day computational intelligence providing effective approaches for convoluted, high dimensional and nonlinear challenges. Based on geometric means and statistical actions, the Geometric Mean Optimizer (GMO) is an intriguing metaheuristic that exhibits promise in forging an ideal equilibrium between exploration and exploitation while searching. However, in intricate environments, GMO may find it challenging to diverge from local optima and can often undergo premature convergence. With a goal to advance beyond these restrictions we propose a hybrid framework incorporating GMO with Quantum Annealing (QA), a quantum inspired approach that probabilistically overcomes local minima and guides the search to global optima employing quantum tunneling. This hybrid strategy known as QAGMO, accomplishes accurate and dependable optimization through the probabilistic capability of QA with the statistical acumen of GMO. To optimize the internal structure and parameters of the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN), a neural architecture founded on functional decomposition leveraging adaptive basis functions, the recommended QAGMO is employed as an application. Three datasets of different sizes-large, medium and small-are deployed to evaluate our strategy. Results through experiments indicate that QAGMO consistently outperforms traditional optimization strategies, validating its robustness and ability for generalization spanning a range of data intricacies.

title: Early Glaucoma Screening Using YOLO and Deep Features from Fundus Images
authors: Lavanya Singh, Divyanshi Singh, Nirbhay Kumar Tagore, Y N Singh
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: A precise and early diagnosis is necessary for glaucoma, a complicated pattern of eye illnesses that can cause irreversible deterioration of the optic nerve. Conventional techniques that depend on manually created features are not resilient to image noise and inter-subject variability. This study proposes an automated glaucoma detection framework integrating YOLOv8 for region of interest (ROI) localization and ResNet50 for deep feature extraction and classification. The model pipeline comprises fundus image acquisition, preprocessing, ONH segmentation, and classification. Superior performance is demonstrated by experimental validation on benchmark datasets, such as Drishti-GS, ORIGA, and G1020. On Drishti-GS, the model’s accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity are 97.06%, 97.14%, and 95.77%, respectively. The suggested system achieves accuracy and sensitivity of up to 99.70% and 99.56% on G1020 and ORIGA, respectively. It attests to the effectiveness of the suggested model for accurate glaucoma screening.

title: Torsional Vibration Suppression Control Based on Weighted Average Speed Feedback
authors: Zhenhua Liang, Xiong Liu, Yuanpeng Guan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The spring-mass mechanical systems inherently exhibit a torsional resonance that will generate detrimental vibrations and shorten mechanical component life. To suppress these vibrations, this paper proposes a weighted average speed (WAS) control strategy that reduces the inverter-driven motor with two-mass system from third order control plant to first order, thereby circumventing the mechanical resonance and greatly simplifying the controller design. Based on the WAS control framework, the system torsional vibration is analyzed and an active damping strategy incorporating both speed- difference feedback and load speed derivative feedback is introduced to suppress torsional vibrations while preserving the control plant order-reduction property. Finally, the efficacy of the proposed control strategy is validated through simulations.

title: High Efficiency High Power Density SRC-DCX Considering the DC bias of the Resonant Capacitor
authors: Siyuan Gao, Xinke Wu, Xinlong Huang, Chaowei Fu, Yuhao Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The half-bridge series resonant converter (SRC) dc transformer (DCX) has been widely adopted in DC bus converters. However, in applications with a wide output voltage range, the nonlinear characteristics of the switch output junction capacitance become significant. As the output voltage decreases, the output junction capacitance increases. With a fixed dead time, this leads to insufficient magnetizing current to fully discharge the junction capacitance, thereby causing zero-voltage switching (ZVS) realization failure. Furthermore, the nonlinearity of the resonant capacitor will cause the switching frequency and the resonant frequency to not match. This paper proposes a variable frequency control strategy to solve the problem of mismatch between switching frequency and resonant frequency and the problem of ZVS loss in a half bridge. Finally, a 400-800V /16.7-33.3V/2.5kW DCX prototype DCX is optimized by achieving a peak efficiency of 99%.

title: Digital Alignment of E-Commerce, Marketing Technology, and IT Governance for MSMEs to Achieve Sustainable Development Goal 8
authors: Resad Setyadi, Kevin Binsar Sinaga, Beki Subaeki
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce creates both opportunities and challenges for Micro, Small, and Medium-sized Enterprises (MSMEs) such as Gressoy, a local food producer. Traditional marketing limitations hinder scalability, emphasizing the need for digital transformation aligned with Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth. This study focuses on two key priorities: developing a comprehensive digital platform to broaden Gressoy’s market reach and establishing a structured IT Governance framework to ensure system security, reliability, and strategic alignment. Using the System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) with the Waterfall model, the research designed and implemented an e-commerce platform, while qualitative methods guided the creation of the governance structure. COBIT principles were adapted to fit the MSME context through stakeholder interviews and technical analysis. The resulting platform integrates multiple payment gateways and personalized marketing features, supported by governance-based operational procedures. This integration promotes secure, transparent, and efficient business operations, strengthening Gressoy’s competitiveness and contributing to local economic development. The study concludes that continuous improvement and periodic internal audits are essential to maintaining governance integrity, enhancing digital resilience, and ensuring MSMEs’ sustained contribution to SDG 8 objectives.

title: Condensation-Concatenation Framework for Dynamic Graph Continual Learning
authors: Tingxu Yan, Ye Yuan
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Dynamic graphs are prevalent in real-world scenarios, where continuous structural changes induce catastrophic forgetting in graph neural networks (GNNs). While continual learning has been extended to dynamic graphs, existing methods overlook the effects of topological changes on existing nodes. To address it, we propose a novel framework for continual learning on dynamic graphs, named Condensation-Concatenation-based Continual Learning (CCC). Specifically, CCC first condenses historical graph snapshots into compact semantic representations while aiming to preserve the original label distribution and topological properties. Then it concatenates these historical embeddings with current graph representations selectively. Moreover, we refine the forgetting measure (FM) to better adapt to dynamic graph scenarios by quantifying the predictive performance degradation of existing nodes caused by structural updates. CCC demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art baselines across four real-world datasets in extensive experiments.

title: FGC-Comp: Adaptive Neighbor-Grouped Attribute Completion for Graph-based Anomaly Detection
authors: Junpeng Wu, Pinheng Zong
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Graph-based Anomaly Detection models have gained widespread adoption in recent years, identifying suspicious nodes by aggregating neighborhood information. However, most existing studies overlook the pervasive issues of missing and adversarially obscured node attributes, which can undermine aggregation stability and prediction reliability. To mitigate this, we propose FGC-Comp—a lightweight, classifier-agnostic, and deployment-friendly attribute completion module—designed to enhance neighborhood aggregation under incomplete attributes. We partition each node’s neighbors into three label-based groups, apply group-specific transforms to the labeled groups while a node-conditioned gate handles unknowns, fuse messages via residual connections, And train end-to-end with a binary classification objective to improve aggregation stability and prediction reliability under missing attributes. Experiments on two real-world fraud datasets validate the effectiveness of the approach with negligible computational overhead.

title: SMOTE-Assisted BiLSTM Modeling for Reliable Industrial Fault Classification in Imbalanced Monitoring Data
authors: Teng Wan, Yang Zhong, Bin Pan, Kai Lv, Fenggang Sun, Peng Lan
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Fault diagnosis in industrial machinery is challenging due to nonlinear, noisy, and highly imbalanced sensor data. To address these issues, this study proposes a SMOTE-augmented BiLSTM framework that integrates synthetic oversampling, feature standardization, and bidirectional sequence modeling for multivariate IoT signals. The core synergy lies in the complementary roles of both components: SMOTE enriches minority fault samples by interpolating within local neighborhoods, mitigating the data imbalance that biases BiLSTM training; meanwhile, BiLSTM captures forward–backward temporal dependencies, enabling the extraction of fault precursor patterns that static oversampling alone cannot reveal. Experiments on the Industrial IoT Fault Detection Dataset show that SMOTE boosts BiLSTM accuracy from 59.50% to 91.21%. Cross-dataset evaluation on the CWRU benchmark further demonstrates strong generalization, achieving 96.74% accuracy without retraining. These results confirm that the proposed method enhances minority fault recognition and provides a reliable solution for intelligent industrial monitoring.

title: Pose-Invariant Grasping via Equidistant Travel Control of an Underactuated Three-Finger Gripper
authors: Yabo Wang, Wenjie Chen, Xiantao Sun, Cong Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To solve part pose variations affecting robotic assembly precision, this paper proposes a pose-invariant grasping method based on equidistant travel control for underactuated three-finger grippers. It adopts single-motor synchronous belt drive, integrates flexible hinge force perception, and builds a force-position coupling model. The core of the method is to ensure the three fingers undergo equal and synchronized radial travel from the gripper's center, which inherently leads to near-simultaneous contact and stable pose grip. Candidate poses (0°-120°, 10° steps) are generated and screened via force closure, with equidistant travel error verified. Experiments on 4 irregular parts show <0.18s finger contact time difference and <0.2N force variation, ensuring pose consistency and supporting robotic precision assembly.

title: Data Augmentation for Continual Learning of Fast and Smooth Imitation Motions Using Model Predictive Control
authors: Akira Kanazawa, Hiroshi Ito, Hideyuki Ichiwara, Yoshiki Kanai, Takahiro Yoshida, Hiroyuki Yamada, Naoaki Noguchi
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Research aimed at enabling robots to acquire not only repetitive and simple tasks but also complex and delicate tasks is receiving significant attention to expand the use of robots to support and replace workers. Imitation learning is one of the promising approaches to enable robots to learn complex human skills with minimal learning cost. However, since the motion data generated by human-operated robots serves as the reference motion for the robot, it is difficult for the robot to acquire fast and smooth motions that surpass human operation. In this paper, we propose a data augmentation method to generate faster and smoother motion data by adjusting the robot’s motion output from the trained model. By utilizing model predictive control (MPC) for motion adjustment, it becomes possible to balance trackability to the original motion and smoothness of the motion through the design of the evaluation functions and constraints. The robot can perform tasks efficiently and robustly by continuously learning augmented motion data that has been optimized using MPC. We demonstrate through experiments on object picking and placing task that higher-quality motion data generated in the real-world.

title: ISED 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)

title: Adaptive DC Voltage Control for Receiving End Converter of Phase-Shifted Diode Rectifier Based Distant Offshore Wind Power Transmission System
authors: Haiping Guo, Siwei Li, Liansong Xiong, Yihan Xie, Wenhui Wang, Zilan Sun, Zeqi Hong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The phase-shifted diode rectifier-based high-voltage direct current (PSDR-HVDC) system is one of the critical solutions for achieving low-cost and system-lightweighting transmission of distant offshore wind power. However, when AC voltage drop fault occurs at sending-end offshore wind farm (OWF), the conventional constant DC voltage control applied at the receiving end voltage source converter (VSC) cause voltage mismatch in PSDR, preventing it from maintaining conduction and thus blocking the power transmission from OWF. This study proposes a communicationless adaptive DC voltage control strategy for receiving end VSC to maintain continuous power delivery during sending end faults. By dynamically adjusting the DC voltage, the proposed method ensures DRs remain conducting under sending end fault. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of proposed control.

title: Hourly Load Forecasting of EV Charging Stations Using ARIMA and LSTM Model
authors: J.Jega jeevanram, B. Bhavika, Ankur Singh Rana
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Accurate forecasting of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging demand is a crucial step in planning and managing sustainable charging infrastructure. Traditional statistical methods often fail to capture the nonlinear and dynamic patterns present in EV charging data, while purely deep learning-based methods may struggle with short-term dependencies and require large datasets for effective training. To address these challenges, this work employs a hybrid forecasting model combining the Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The ARIMA model is effective in handling linear trends and seasonality in the time-series data, whereas the LSTM model, a recurrent neural network, excels at learning nonlinear dependencies and long-term temporal relationships. By integrating these two approaches, the hybrid ARIMA-LSTM framework leverages the strengths of both models to improve forecasting accuracy. The methodology is applied to hourly EV charging load data, where ARIMA captures baseline patterns and residual errors are further modeled using LSTM to refine predictions. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid model outperforms individual ARIMA and LSTM models in terms of forecasting accuracy, capturing both shortterm fluctuations and long-term variations in demand. Such enhanced forecasting contributes significantly to effective EV charging station planning, grid stability, and energy resource optimization. This approach establishes a reliable forecasting framework that can support the large-scale deployment of EV infrastructure in smart cities.

title: Localized Large Model Trusted Computing System Based on LAN Computing Resource Pool Construction
authors: Xueni He, Hao Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This study adopts Kubernetes container orchestration technology to build 9 consumer-grade computers (equipped with Intel Core i9-14900KF and NVIDIA RTX 4080 Super) into a unified computing power cluster. A unified scheduling of computing resources is achieved through an architecture consisting of 1 master node and 8 worker nodes. To address the high hardware resource requirements of large models such as DeepSeek-R1-671B and Qwen, Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) technology is used to reduce the model precision from FP32 to INT4. Meanwhile, distributed slicing deployment of the models is realized via technologies including tensor parallelism and pipeline parallelism. The system constructs a Node.js-based backend API service and a React frontend interface, enabling a user-friendly interaction experience with large models. In addition, Docker multi-stage build technology is applied to achieve rapid containerized deployment of the system.

title: Multi-Resonant Control with Online Gain Optimization for Enhanced Harmonic Voltage Suppression in Voltage Source Inverters
authors: Zhengyang Zhou, Zeng Liu, Yifan Song, Jinjun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The output voltages of voltage source inverters (VSIs) are prone to be polluted by the harmonic currents from nonlinear loads. Conventional multi-resonant control applies fixed resonant gains, leading to poor output voltage quality under varying load conditions. This paper resolves this issue by proposing an online resonant gain optimization method for multi-resonant control. First, a simplified relationship between the resonant gain and the harmonic output impedance is deduced. Then, to achieve harmonic voltage minimization and maintain system stability, a resonant gain optimization model is formulated, considering the effect of resonant gains on stability. Furthermore, based on the online optimization algorithm, the resonant gains are optimized online using the real-time measured harmonic output current information. The proposed method can theoretically achieve optimal harmonic voltage suppression performance and maintains system stability during parameter adjustment. Experimental results are presented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Data-Driven Machine Learning and NSGA-II Based Parameter Prediction and Optimization Method for Wireless Power Transfer Couplers
authors: Jun Cheng, Binjiang Hu, Wencong Wu, Zhicong Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for predicting and optimizing wireless power transfer (WPT) coupler parameters based on data-driven machine learning and the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II(NSGA-II). The aim is to address the limitations of traditional finite element method(FEM) optimization , such as low efficiency, complexity in multi-objective decision-making, and high computational resource consumption. A high-precision dataset is constructed using the ANSYS Maxwell, and an artificial neural network (ANN) model is developed to establish the nonlinear mapping between input parameters and electromagnetic performance. The ANN is then embedded into the fitness evaluation module of the NSGA-II to form a hybrid optimization framework, enabling efficient prediction and multi-objective collaborative optimization of the coupler parameters.

title: The Influence of Personality Traits, Digital Culture, and Digital Leadership on Digital Transformation in a Security Outsourcing Company
authors: Salindria Wiratama Perkasa, Dicky Hida Syahchari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: In this era of Digital Transformation, security outsourcing companies must adapt with digital attribute to help sustainability of the company. this studies analyse the influences of personality trait, digital culture, digital leadership on digital transformation at PT. Rakatak delapan Tujuh that operate mainly in Jakarta and Batam using survey methodology. Survey data were collected from 139 employee with related categories for the study purposes through online questionnaire using google form that adapted from previous studies and analysed with Partial Least Square-Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM) via SmartPLS. The result from SmartPLS calculation shows that Digital Culture and Digital Leadership have a significant effects on Digital Transformation while personality Trait do not have any effects. The R-square value of 0.576 shows that the three independent variable have 57,6% variance on Digital Transformation. This result shows that Digital culture and Digital Leadership have crucial roles on driving PT. Rakatak Delapan Tujuh to its Digital Transformation Phase and gives insight to other companies in a similar sector.

title: The Influence of E-Service Quality on Customer Loyalty and Positive Word of Mouth through Customer Experience and Customer Satisfaction in Banks Classified under Core Capital Group IV
authors: Ferdinandus Levin, Fikri Muhammad Baihaqi, Francisca Yani Widiyanti, Stefanus Rumangkit, Christopher Joshua Leksana
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study aims to analyze the influence of eservice quality on customer loyalty and positive word of mouth through customer experience and customer satisfaction. A quantitative research approach was employed in this study. The sampling technique used was purposive sampling, with the criteria being customers of Core Capital Group IV Banks. A total of 222 respondents participated, all of whom were users of digital banking facilities provided. Data analysis was conducted using the Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) technique. The results indicate that e-service quality significantly affects both customer experience and customer satisfaction. Furthermore, customer satisfaction significantly influences customer loyalty and positive word of mouth. Customer loyalty also significantly impacts positive word of mouth. However, customer experience and customer education, as moderating variables, do not have a significant effect on customer loyalty and positive word of mouth. Digital banking adoption is rapidly increasing. This study highlights the challenges faced by financial institutions in maintaining e-service quality amid diverse customer needs and varying levels of digital literacy.

title: Identification of Oils by ARS and ECOC-SVM Classifier Framework
authors: Md Qaiser Reza, Munna Khan, Ashok Kumar Salhan, Musarrat Jahan, Praburaja Ramasamy, Muzammil Mushtaq
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Acoustic signals transmitted through a material collect extensive analytical information, acting as a unique identifier for material classification. Therefore, this paper proposes Acoustic Resonance Spectrometry (ARS) based a noninvasive and effective method, utilizing the resonance characteristics of oil for identification. It is simple, costeffective, and does not require sample preparation. It has been developed with a V-shaped glass strip featuring two piezoelectric transducers, one functioning as a transmitter and the other as a receiver. The oil was excited by white noise using a transmitter and recorded acoustic signal from the receiver end to acquire its resonance signatures. Modified covariance spectral analysis was performed on six oils (Mustard, Almond, Olive, Castor, Petrol, Tarpin) acoustic signals, revealing seven resonance frequencies.
