The ECOCSVM classifier using an RBF kernel was assessed by two coding strategies: one versus one (1VS1) and one versus all (1VSA). SVM1VS1 attained the highest accuracy of 94.38%, indicating that spectral features based on resonance offer a rapid, nondestructive, and precise method for oil classification.

title: An Advanced Power Cycling Test Method for SiC MOSFETs Based on Real-Time Junction Temperature Control
authors: Chenyi Wang, Cen Chen, Haodong Wang, Xuerong Ye
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The reliability of Silicon Carbide MOSFETs remains a critical bottleneck for high-performance power converters. While Power Cycling Tests are standard for lifetime assessment, conventional methods generate thermal stress indirectly through open-loop power control. Even some advanced PCTs often rely on pre-calibrated models, leading to a potential mismatch with the true thermal dynamics experienced in diverse applications. To bridge this gap, this digest proposes an advanced Temperature Controlled Power Cycling Test method. The core of Temperature Controlled Power Cycling Test represents a fundamental shift: from controlling the electrical input to directly regulating the primary failure-inducing stressor—the junction temperature. By implementing a high-fidelity, real-time, closed-loop control, the TCPCT platform forces the SiC MOSFET to follow a pre-defined junction temperature profile with high precision. This approach decouples the thermal stress assessment from the specific electrical operating mode, making it a more universal and application-agnostic tool. A prototype has been successfully developed and validated on commercial SiC MOSFETs, demonstrating its capability to accurately replicate application-specific thermal profiles. The results confirm that Temperature Controlled Power Cycling Test provides a more direct, repeatable, and physically meaningful approach to reliability assessment, overcoming key limitations of existing methodologies.

title: Adaptive Iterative Learning-Based Waveform Replication Control
authors: Zhiying Zhu, Weijian Kong, Chen Guo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issues of low convergence efficiency and instability caused by modeling errors in the iterative process of Time Waveform Replication (TWR). An adaptive iterative learning-based waveform replication control method is proposed. The method introduces two major improvements: (i) a coherence function-based excitation optimization mechanism, which dynamically adjusts the spectral parameters of White–Pink noise to improve the accuracy of the Frequency Response Function (FRF) model; and (ii) an Adaptive Model Updating scheme with a Model Update Evaluation mechanism, which dynamically corrects the FRF model and filters valid updates by quantitatively assessing the robustness and convergence of model revisions. The proposed approach is validated on an electrohydraulic servo system. Simulation results demonstrate that the method outperforms conventional TWR in both convergence efficiency and signal replication accuracy. When convergence is achieved, the RMS error decreases to approximately 4.84%, significantly lower than the 12.71% of the baseline TWR. These results confirm that the proposed method enhances the convergence speed and robustness of TWR, thereby laying the foundation for its practical implementation in real-world controllers.

title: A Study of Reinforcement Learning for Indoor Path Planning with Dynamic Obstacles
authors: Ziyi Yuan, Guo Yang, Ruqi Xiao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: Indoor navigation for unmanned vehicles presents significant challenges due to confined spaces and dynamic obstacles. This study comparatively investigates Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) and Advantage Actor-Critic (A2C) for dynamic path planning. We construct a simulation platform based on ROS and Gazebo with an enclosed environment, featuring a dynamically moving cylindrical obstacle that emulates human walking patterns. Both PPO and A2C are trained and evaluated under identical settings, enabling a comparative analysis of their stability, efficiency, and obstacle-avoidance performance. Results show that PPO consistently achieves higher success rates and smoother trajectories with fewer collisions.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Child Autism Detection via EEG and Facial Features with Confidence-Based Decision-Level Fusion
authors: Sapthak Mohajon Turjya, Joyeta Mondal Kotha, Tusher Bhakta, Rahul Biswas, Md Shahriar Hosan Parvez, Shovro Nandi
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel multimodal method for the diagnosis of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) in children using neurophysiological and facial image-based features. Both a BCIAUT P300 dataset EEG signal and a facial image dataset downloaded from Kaggle were utilized to build two complementary deep learning pipelines, namely a BiLSTM-Transformer for EEG and a fine-tuned DenseNet169 for facial images. Following independent training, a confidence-based decision-level fusion approach was implemented to dynamically select the more reliable modality for each prediction. The proposed system achieved 99% accuracy, surpassing unimodal approaches (EEG-alone: 92%, image-alone: 91%). Careful ablation studies validated the effectiveness of the fusion strategy, and SHAP and Grad-CAM explainability analyses highlighted the clinical utility of the model by highlighting attention to current ASD indicators. This work emphasizes the potential of explainable AI and multimodal learning for robust and interpretable pediatric autism screening.

title: Towards Synergistic Human-Robot Co-Adaptation via Reciprocal Feedback for Shared Contact Tasks
authors: Deniz Yilmaz, Shinya Chiyohara, Jun-Ichiro Furukawa, Erhan Oztop, Hiroshi Imamizu, Jun Morimoto, Barkan Ugurlu
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In this work, we propose a human-robot physical interaction scheme designed to facilitate contact-rich manipulation tasks. In the proposed framework, neither the robot nor the human agent can complete the task independently, but a shared cost function aligns their efforts and drives them toward success. The robot agent, governed by a reinforcement learning algorithm, can exert forces and modulate its Cartesian impedance while continuously receiving evaluative feedback in the standard RL training paradigm. Simultaneously, the human agent applies forces via a standard PS4 joystick and receives both vibrotactile and visual feedback reflecting task performance. During training, the learning algorithm receives the superposition of its own and the human’s actions, allowing it to implicitly benefit from the human’s rapidly adapting strategy. We hypothesize that human agents can adapt more rapidly than RL and, when provided with feedback grounded in real measurements, can make more quantifiable decisions. During this rapid human adaptation phase, the robot concurrently acquires skills from the human, thereby accelerating training and improving overall efficiency. The proposed interaction scheme was evaluated in a realistic simulation environment involving 10 participants. Preliminary results indicate that participants receiving vibrotactile feedback adapted more quickly, enabling the robot to acquire the desired skill in only a few episodes for simple tasks. For more challenging tasks, human-trained RL agents required additional autonomous training, yet still achieved convergence far faster than PPO-only training. This co-adaptive framework combines the complementary strengths of humans and robots, providing a versatile foundation for contact-rich manipulation that may be extended to diverse tasks and robotic platforms.

title: Ultra-Low Cross Polarization Broadband Compact Magneto-Electric Dipole Antenna Array for 5G Applications
authors: Songsong Qian, Pengcheng Yang, Jiahao Xiao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: This paper proposes a magneto-electric dipole antenna array fed by a two-layer substrate integrated coaxial line (SICL) for 5G millimeter-wave applications. The first SICL layer directly feeds the antenna elements through slots, while the second layer forms a more compact power distribution network. The resulting array features an element spacing not exceeding half-wavelength at the high-frequency operating band. Notably, the array exhibits exceptionally low cross polarization due to its completely symmetric radiating structure and fully enclosed feeding configuration. Simulations of a 4×4 prototype demonstrate over 57 dB suppression of peak cross-polarization relative to peak co-polarization from 24.25 to 29.5 GHz.

title: Real-Time Threat Detection in Networks Using Machine Learning: A Predictive Approach to Cybersecurity
authors: VigneshKumar. M, N.R. Sathis Kumar, Mary Selsiaa P, M.Vishnu Sai, M.Syam Sundhar Yadav, A.Siva Nagaraju
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In today's hyper-connected world, the growing frequency and sophistication of cyberattacks demand intelligent and proactive network defense mechanisms. Traditional signature-based intrusion detection systems (IDS) struggle to detect novel and evolving threats in real-time, leaving systems vulnerable. This research presents a machine learning- based approach for real-time threat detection in computer networks, leveraging supervised learning techniques to classify network traffic as normal or malicious. Using benchmark datasets such as NSL- KDD and CICIDS2017, we preprocessed and extracted key network features to train models including Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost. The trained models were evaluated based on accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and detection latency to assess their suitability for real-time deployment. Among the tested models, XGBoost achieved the highest accuracy of 97.3% with minimal false positives, making it ideal for practical implementation.

title: A Study on the Impact of HFT (PCB) Winding Structure on Common-Mode EMI in Isolated Half-Bridge DC/DC Converters
authors: Dejun Ba, Yihe Wang, Yuxin Niu, Xinran Chen, Qi Cao, Xiaofeng Lyu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of the winding interleaved structure of high-frequency printed circuit board transformers (HFT-PCB) on common-mode EMI in isolated half-bridge DC/DC converters. As operating frequencies increase, suppressing electromagnetic interference (EMI) is critical for the reliable operation of circuit topologies and compliance with EMC standards. This study designs different winding structures and conducts experimental analysis to verify the impact of the winding interleaved structure on common-mode (CM) EMI in half-bridge DC/DC converters. A multi-branch parallel winding interleaved structure is proposed. Experimental results show that the proposed winding structure improves CM EMI suppression by 9.7% compared to traditional interleaved winding, and increases overall output efficiency by 0.5% at a 1.1 MHz operating frequency.

title: Optimizing Public Transport Scheduling Through IoT-Enabled Vehicle Demand Prediction with LSTM-MDN
authors: M. Suresh, S. Sivasankaran, P. Mathivanan, R. Rajmohan
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of urban populations and rising mobility needs have placed immense pressure on transportation networks, often leading to excess vehicle usage, traffic congestion, and environmental pollution. Existing public transport scheduling systems fail to adapt dynamically to fluctuating passenger demand, resulting in inefficiencies and underutilization of resources. IoT sensors and connected devices are employed to collect real-time traffic flow, passenger count, and environmental data, which are then preprocessed and fed into the LSTM-MDN model for accurate demand forecasting. The objective is to optimize public transport scheduling by predicting demand variations, thereby reducing unnecessary vehicle deployment, improving passenger satisfaction, and enhancing system efficiency. These findings highlight the potential of integrating IoT-driven data intelligence with deep learning-based demand prediction to support the development of sustainable and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Vision Based Traffic Control System for Pedestrian Safety
authors: M. Jayalakshmi, N. Sai Sidhardha, M. Sumanth Reddy, N. Deepak, N. Lokesh
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Contemporary cityscapes confront increasing issues in traffic rationalization and road safety due to constraints in traditional fixed-time signals and human-based enforcement. This study introduces a scalable Intelligent Traffic Management System based on state-of-the-art computer vision and deep learning the YOLOv8n model trained on real-world data for dynamic, real-time signal control as well as self-sustaining violation detection. The system utilizes video-based vehicle detection for object recognition of cars, pedestrians, traffic signs, and lights and fuses these with adaptive timing algorithms to minimize unnecessary delays and actively respond to changing road conditions. Stringent testing demonstrates that the YOLOv8n detection pipeline obtains 95.7 % traffic participant accuracy with a false positive rate of 4.3 %, while violation detection module attains 93 % accuracy and enables fast adaptation with an average response time of 0.42 seconds per frame. Real-world deployment demonstrates significant gains, accelerating traffic flow by up to 50 % and shortening vehicle wait times by over 70 % compared with fixed-schedule implementations. In addition, the solution has a module-based architecture that is open to digital signal controllers and urban observation, which can integrate easily with different infrastructure configurations for smart city deployment. Through adaptive signal, density control, violation recording, and centralized reporting, the system enables traffic authorities to make sound, fact-based decisions. This strategy significantly promotes urban mobility, pedestrian security, and enforcement clarity, setting a new benchmark in smart transportation infrastructure with the integration of deep learning, scalable deployment, and operational effectiveness.

title: Design of Intelligent Tennis Ball Retrieval Robot System Based on STM32U5 and OpenMV
authors: Yipeng Chen, Jinhong Guo, Qin Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Aiming at the pain points of low efficiency and high labor intensity of manual tennis ball picking in tennis training scenarios, this paper designs and implements an intelligent tennis ball retrieval robot based on the STM32U575 ultra-low power MCU and OpenMV module. The system adopts the STM32U5 development board as the core controller, integrates the OpenMV H7 camera to realize real-time visual recognition and positioning of tennis balls, controls the four-wheel Mecanum Wheel chassis through a 4-channel TB6612 motor driver module, and drives four tennis retrieval motors via an additional 4-channel TB6612 module to achieve omnidirectional movement, and is equipped with the SR-04 ultrasonic sensor to realize dynamic obstacle avoidance. In terms of control strategy, the PID closed-loop control algorithm is innovatively combined with Visual Servo Control to achieve accurate tracking and stable retrieval of moving targets. Experimental results show that: within a standard tennis court (23.77m×8.23m), the robot’s recognition accuracy for yellow/white tennis balls reaches 98.3%, the average response time is 0.25 seconds, the retrieval efficiency is 22 balls per minute, and the battery life is 4.5 hours. All indicators are significantly better than the traditional manual method. The system supports dual-mode switching between Bluetooth manual remote control and visual autonomous operation, and has modular expansion interfaces, which can be adapted to peripheral devices such as pressure sensors and RFID. This design provides a highly reliable and low-power technical solution for the intellectualization of sports training, and the relevant architecture can be reused in multi-ball retrieval scenarios such as golf and table tennis.

title: System-Level Simulation of a Fully-Immersed Evaporative Cooling System for High-Speed Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Based on a Lumped-Parameter Thermal Model
authors: MengHu Zhu, ShuHeng Qiu, ZhanPeng Fang, YeHong Huang, XianBei Sun, Chi Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Addressing the thermal management challenges associated with high-power-density operation of high-speed permanent magnet synchronous motors (HSPMSMs), this paper focuses on the fully-immersed evaporative cooling technique and conducts a system-level simulation study. Initially, the limitations of conventional cooling methods in high-speed applications are analyzed, highlighting the technical advantages of fully-immersed evaporative cooling. Subsequently, a system-level lumped-parameter model centered around a "set-temperature heat source" is constructed. Simulations are performed to analyze the boiling characteristics, heat transfer capacity, and system temperature stability of the coolant under different set heat source temperatures. The results indicate that the system-level fully-immersed evaporative cooling approach can effectively meet the requirements of the set heat source temperature, maintain stable heat exchange between the cooling system and the motor, and thereby provide an efficient simulation methodology and theoretical foundation for the preliminary thermal design of high-speed electric machines.

title: Instability of Rain-induced Attenuation due to Rain Height Variability for Earth-Space Links operating at SHF and EHF in Cape Town, South Africa
authors: Yusuf Babatunde Lawal, Owolawi Pius Adewale, Chunling Tu, Etienne A. Van Wyk, Joseph Sunday Ojo
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: Rain-induced attenuation strongly limits Earth-space links at Super High Frequency (SHF) and Extremely High Frequency (EHF), especially in coastal, convectively active regions. In Cape Town, South Africa, proximity to the ocean and seasonal thermodynamics modulate the rain height ($\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}$), the effective altitude where precipitation contributes to the effective path loss, making the annual fixed values potentially inaccurate. This study quantifies the impact of seasonal $\mathbf{H}_{\mathbf{r}}$ variability on attenuation for Ka-band ($20 / 30 \mathrm{GHz}$) and V-band ($40 / 50 \mathrm{GHz}$) links. Two approaches are compared: the ITU-R fixed annual mean $\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}$ model and locally derived four-season $\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}$ values from recent regional analyses. Using standard slant-path geometry and exceedance statistics, attenuation is computed for multiple percentages of time at both downlink and uplink frequencies. Results show systematic discrepancies between ITU-R fixed annual $\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}$ and seasonal $\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}$-based attenuation predictions across all seasons. While ITU-R $\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{r}}$-based attenuation performs reasonably in winter ($\leq 5.2 \%$ deviation at 99.99% availability across all frequencies), spring, summer, and autumn exhibit substantially larger differences of $\approx \mathbf{1 5} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 9 \%} \boldsymbol{,} \boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{3 0 - 3 9 \%}$, and $\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{2 2 -} 28 \%$, respectively. Peak summer conditions associated with deep convection can yield attenuations up to 33 dB, underscoring the need for season-aware design. The study provides localized propagation inputs for Cape Town link budgets and recommends fade-mitigation techniques, including adaptive coding and modulation, uplink power control, site diversity methods, and cross-band switching strategies to sustain high availability in Ka and V bands. These findings demonstrate that incorporating seasonal rain-height climatology meaningfully improves prediction accuracy and system robustness in coastal subtropical environments.

title: Automating Infrastructure Surveying: A Framework for Geometric Measurements and Compliance Assessment Using Point Cloud Data_supp1-3667090.mp4
authors: Iman Soltani
publish: N/A
abstract: Automation can play a prominent role in improving efficiency, accuracy, and scalability in infrastructure surveying and assessing construction and compliance standards. This paper presents a framework for automation of geometric measurements and compliance assessment using point cloud data. We integrate deep learning-based detection and segmentation with geometric and signal processing techniques, to automate surveying tasks. We apply this framework to automatically evaluate the compliance of curb ramps with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), demonstrating the utility of point cloud data in survey automation. We leverage a newly collected, large annotated dataset of curb ramps, made publicly available as part of this work, to facilitate robust model training and evaluation. Experimental results, including a 87.9% compliance ruling match with manual field measurements of several ramps, highlight the method’s potential to significantly reduce manual effort. While we evaluate the proposed framework only on ADA compliance of curb ramps, the modular design of the pipeline is intended to be adaptable to a wider domain of automation tasks in infrastructure surveying and beyond. The annotated database, manual ramp survey data, and developed algorithms are publicly available on the project’s GitHub page: github.com/Soltanilara/SurveyAutomation.

title: Dynamic Facial Motion Analysis Using High-precision Geometry from a High-speed Metric 3D Camera System
authors: Wang Liao, Chen Zhang, Shiyao Gao, Maik Rosenberger, Gunther Notni
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This work proposes a metric 3D-based concept for dynamic facial motion analysis and presents its metrological validation using a high-speed metric 3D camera system with a color camera and 3D unit composed of GOBO (GOes Before Optics) projector at 850 nm and two calibrated near infrared cameras at the same wavelength. The concept aims to shift facial expression analysis from inference-based estimation toward direct high precision geometric measurement by integrating synchronized color and metric 3D data. After calibration and image registration, 478 standardized facial landmarks are extracted via MediaPipe and transformed into metric 3D coordinates. Based on these landmarks, as proof of concept, 17 geometric features corresponding to selected facial action units (AUs) are defined to provide an interpretable, measurement-driven representation of some facial muscle activations. A systematic uncertainty evaluation framework with different-level metrics, inspired by the GUM and ISO 5725 standards, is established to assess the reliability and repeatability of the measured geometric features. Experimental validation on 10 participants demonstrates that the used metric 3D camera system can precisely measure the facial geometric features with average combined uncertainties of 0.78 mm for distance features and 1.55° for angular features. Under dynamic conditions, measured facial motion induced geometric feature variations are much greater than the uncertainty of measurement, exhibiting strong statistical significance. These results confirm the feasibility of the proposed metric 3D-based concept for reliable and interpretable dynamic facial motion analysis, with potential applications in scenarios like micro-expression recognition, lie detection, and healthcare monitoring.

title: Digital Twin and Scheduling of Parallel Walking Beam Reheating Furnaces
authors: Siyi Li, Yuying Jiang, Ziyan Zhao, Jianhai Song, Shixin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Digital twin (DT) is a novel technology with a promising prospect in smart manufacturing. It can realize high-fidelity mapping of physical entities, data fusion of physical and virtual space, and real-time monitoring of physical entities. This makes it easy to make optimal decisions and precise control. The steel industry has long production processes, complex equipment operation mechanisms, and dynamic production environment. Due to the lack of digital representation of its production process, many optimization and control schemes obtained by existing methods have large deviations in their implementation processes. How to provide accurate digital representation and make optimal decisions based on it is a great challenge. The development of DT technology in industrial scenarios provides a solution to this issue. In the steel production process, reheating furnaces are highly energy-consuming facilities connecting the continuous casting and hot rolling processes. This work proposes a five-dimension DT faced to parallel walking beam reheating furnaces. In it, we construct a three-stage model for virtual entities. It can accurately obtain the charging/discharging times and real-time positions of slabs, along with simulating the slabs’ logistics processes. An adaptive large neighborhood tabu search algorithm based on DT, named as ALTS-DT, is further proposed for parallel reheating furnace scheduling (PRFS) problems. By conducting numerous computational experiments, we verify the following: first, the high accuracy of the proposed three-stage model of DT, with relative error under 0.98%; second, the vital role of DT in PRFS; third, the excellent performance of the proposed ALTS-DT in solving actual PRFS problems.

title: ISAC-Empowered LEO Constellation Networked Sensing: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Zhiqing Wei, Haotian Liu, Yunxin Geng, Zhiyong Feng, Guan Gui, Kan Zheng
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: The pursuit of seamless global coverage in sixth-generation (6G) mobile networks has driven the development of low earth orbit (LEO) constellation networks. In addition to communication, the emerging 6G applications, such as low-altitude economy and smart city, also require high-accuracy, large-scale, and continuous sensing. Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) has emerged as a key enabler, which could empower LEO constellations with dual capabilities of communication and sensing. With the implementation of networked sensing in ISAC-empowered LEO constellations, the requirements of emerging 6G applications can be satisfied. This article presents a comprehensive overview of networked sensing in ISAC-empowered LEO constellations. We first introduce the concepts, sensing types, application scenarios, and payload enhancements. Then, we delve into critical technical challenges of networked sensing in ISAC-empowered LEO constellations and discuss the promising solutions tailored to the unique characteristics of LEO constellations, such as high mobility and long distances to the targets. Finally, we highlight future research trends to further promote the performance of ISAC-empowered LEO constellations.

title: Image Dehazing Using Patch-wise Nonlinear Brightness Prior
authors: Xiaoyue Wu, Tianyi Lyu, Mingye Ju
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Currently available dehazing methods, whether based on hand-crafted priors or learned from datasets, typically ignore the brightness consistency between hazy images and their dehazed results, which often leads to over-enhancement and color cast. To address this issue, we first investigate a patch-wise nonlinear brightness prior (PNBP) that explicitly characterizes the relationship between the brightness of hazy patches and that of their clear counterparts. By combining PNBP with the atmospheric scattering model, the single image dehazing problem can be recast as a restoration formula with only three parameters, substantially shrinking the solution space for haze removal. Under a multi-objective joint optimization that simultaneously considers information gain, exposure, and preservation of the pixel-histogram distribution, this restoration formula can directly produce high-quality dehazed images. Thanks to PNBP, our method inherits brightness consistency from the prior and thereby avoids the risk of over-enhancement while reducing the possibility of color cast. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in terms of defogging quality, robustness, and computational efficiency.

title: Cross-Domain Standardization and Secure Edge Intelligence for Real-Time Digital Twin Deployments in Next-Generation Communication Systems
authors: Sai Raghavendra Varanasi, Sesha Sai Sravanthi Valiveti, Muhammad Adnan, Md Imtiaz Faruk, Md Jubayar Hossain, Mia Md Tofayel Gonee Manik
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: The digital twins (DTs) are central to the next-generation communication system, and it allows real-time simulation and control. Their development under cross-domain ecosystems however reveals the fundamental weakness in interoperability, security and trust which is made worse by heterogeneous data model stipulations and antagonistic policies. This paper offers an analytical report of the DT standards environment, where major shortages are observed in security architecture and federated intelligence. To overcome these problems, we introduce a new framework of Intelligence Secure Edge Framework (SEIF) that incorporates Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), blockchain and federated learning. Also, we introduce a road-map towards cross-domain standardization that is discussed stratified and propose a list of urgent research directions to make acquiring a secure and scalable deployment of DT in 6G and 6G.

title: Efficient Dataset Distillation via Generative Pruning
authors: Yingyi Ma, Muquan Li, Guiduo Duan, Ke Qin, Shuang Liang, Dongyang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Dataset distillation (DD) has demonstrated the promise of synthesizing smaller datasets that enable competitive performance. While most of DD methods operate in pixel space, they often suffer from poor scalability on high-resolution datasets. Recent works have thus shifted towards parameterizing synthetic data using deep generative priors. However, these approaches apply latent updates to all generator layers, leading to substantial computational overhead. To address this limitation, we propose Generative Lightweight Distillation (GLiD), a unified framework that jointly compresses the generator and accelerates latent optimization for efficient distillation. GLiD introduces two key components: (1) We introduce a Classification-Diversity Sensitivity Pruning mechanism that quantifies both discriminative utility and semantic diversity of each output channel to guide structural pruning and selective layer-wise optimization. (2) We also present a Layer-Adaptive Scheduling strategy that dynamically allocates latent update steps across generator stages based on convergence behavior. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet-1K and its subsets demonstrate that our GLiD achieves up to 10× acceleration across different datasets, while maintaining performance competitive with state-of-the-art methods.

title: Label Correction via Contrastive Embedding for Noisy Multi-label Learning
authors: Anhui Tan, Hui Xiang, Weiping Ding, Wei-Zhi Wu, Jinjin Li, Jiye Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Multi-label learning plays a crucial role in numerous real-world applications, where each instance may be associated with multiple semantic labels. However, in practical scenarios, label noise is widespread and poses a significant challenge, as it can simultaneously distort multiple labels due to ambiguous annotations, human fatigue, or automated labeling inaccuracies. While considerable progress has been made in developing noise-robust methods for single-label learning, addressing noise in multi-label settings remains substantially more challenging due to the intricate interplay among multiple potentially corrupted labels. This underscores the pressing need for effective label correction strategies tailored to noisy multi-label learning. To address this gap, we propose a latent contrastive embedding framework designed for noisy multi-label scenarios. The approach not only learns robust feature representations through supervised contrastive learning but also dynamically identifies clean labels via a small-loss guided sample selection strategy. Moreover, the embedding and label correction processes are jointly optimized, allowing the model to capture the semantic structure of both features and labels under noisy conditions. Specifically, we adopt a small-loss criterion to distinguish clean from noisy samples during the early training phase. In addition, a balanced loss is introduced to mitigate the effects of label imbalance and sample difficulty. Finally, comprehensive experiments conducted under various patterns and levels of label noise demonstrate the superior robustness and generalization ability of the proposed method in noisy multi-label classification tasks.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs

title: Energy-Aware Task Offloading and Resource Management in Delay-Sensitive Edge-Cloud Computing Environment Optimization
authors: Harish Chamarthi, Mohanraju Muppala
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Delay-sensitive applications-think autonomous driving, augmented reality, industrial Io T-are everywhere now, and they're pushing us to rethink how we handle computation. Edge computing stands out as a solution: we push heavy tasks to nearby servers to cut down on lag. But here's the problem. Edge nodes don't have endless resources, and both devices and servers burn through energy fast. So, what's the fix? This paper lays out a new optimization framework for joint task offloading and resource allocation in a hybrid edge-cloud setup. Our main goal: cut total system energy use, but never blow past the strict delay limits these sensitive tasks demand. We cast this as mixed-integer non-linear programming (MINLP) problem-yes, it's a tough one, NP-hard by nature. To make it practical, we introduce the Energy and Delay-aware Cooperative Offloading (EDCO) algorithm. It's heuristic-based and adapts on the fly, choosing whether a task should run right on the user's device, on an edge server, or out in the cloud. EDCO also brings in a query fragment caching strategy at the edge, so repeated tasks finish faster and with less computational load. Our simulations show real gains. EDCO cuts total system energy use by up to 28 %, and slashes task failures from delay violations by more than 45 % compared to standard approaches like Greedy Offloading or Cloud-Only processing.

title: A Hybrid Modulation Strategy for Hybrid 4-leg Three-level FC-NPC Inverter to Suppress Common-Mode Voltage
authors: Chujun Li, Dong Jiang, Jianan Chen, Zeyuan Du, Daoliang Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In a photovoltaic energy storage system, the DC-side voltage does not experience significant fluctuations, so the requirements for the DC-side capacitors are low. The FC-NPC three-level inverter uses the FC leg to replace the DC-side capacitors, and this topology does not require small vectors to balance the midpoint voltage in common-mode voltage suppression. The midpoint voltage balance is achieved simply by controlling the FC bridge arm. Therefore, the use of the FC-NPC topology in photovoltaic systems offers inherent advantages. This paper uses seven middle vectors to suppress the common-mode current and analyzes the relationship between the switching frequencies of the FC bridge arm and NPC to achieve midpoint voltage balance. CME and frequency spreading are combined to eliminate spikes in common-mode EMI. Finally, verification through simulations shows that the combination can effectively reduce common-mode EMI, decrease phase current THD, and reduce switching losses.

title: Design of a Wireless Power Transfer System Based on Multi-Objective Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Huanjun Niu, Fei Gao, Kai He, Zhenghao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) is an innovative technology that has gained significant attention in recent years. These challenges are particularly pronounced in dual-side LCC-compensated WPT systems, where the complexity of their resonant networks renders parameters like capacitors and inductors exceptionally sensitive to manufacturing variations, environmental changes, and degradation over time. However, due to the impact of parameter deviation, the system may not maintain a constant current or constant voltage output. This article investigates the system characteristics sensitivity analysis on compensation component errors. The worst-case scenarios are used to evaluate the effect of capacitor and inductor errors. Multi-parameter Monte Carlo simulation is used to validate the theoretic analysis. By integrating sensitivity analysis with cost-aware design principles, this work presents a scalable framework to enhance the robustness of dual-side LCC WPT systems, offering a structured approach to balance technical specifications with production budget constraints by multi-objective particle optimization.

title: Motion Generation for Surgical Robots using Task-Aware Attention based on Deep Predictive Learning *
authors: Hidetoshi Taira, Maina Sogabe, Tetsuro Miyazaki, Kenji Kawashima
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In this study, we propose a method for autonomously operating a surgical robot by controlling visual attention using the robot’s own state in deep predictive learning. The proposed method, named TAIRNN (Task-Attentive Informed Recurrent Neural Network) uses a state-conditioned Query to retrieve visual Keys to deep predictive model. Experimental results of point-to-point movement showed that this method can reach the final target with improved accuracy and in fewer steps compared to conventional methods that rely solely on image information. The results demonstrate that an approach that incorporates a robot’s self-state awareness into its visual attention mechanism is effective in suppressing task-irrelevant visual noise and improving control stability.

title: Ldir: Natural Language Instruction Based Database Information Retrieval Using Local Lightweight Large Language Model
authors: Akshaya V, Sivanantham S, Renuka Devi S, Sekar K, Rohit P, Sri Pranauv M
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The deployment of text-to-SQL systems in sensitive and resource-constrained environments remains challenging due to the reliance on cloud-based large language models with substantial computational requirements. The research work proposes LDIR (Local Database Information Retriever), a lightweight, offline-solvable text-to-SQL model aimed at creating structured database queries from natural language directions. As opposed to standard large language models with cloud accessibility and high-end requirements, LDIR runs purely on low-end local hardware on the basis of a distilled T5small model. Major innovations are a confidence-based query selection mechanism, low-rank adaptation (LoRA) and prefixtuning integration for light model updates, and complete offline usability. Tested on the Clinton SQL dataset, LDIR attains nearteacher performance with ROUGE-1 of 0.91, BLEU of 0.71, METEOR of 0.88 and Exact Match of 0.44, while reducing computational needs by more than 50 %. These findings show LDIR's promise for safe, effective deployment in privacysensitive and edge computing applications.

title: Research on Sensorless Control Strategy for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Based on ASEKF
authors: Zheliang Shi, Xindong Shu, Jie Zhang, Haiyang Cao, Zuguo Bai, Hantian Ye, Xianjin Wan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: In permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) and high-speed sensorless control, the extended Kalman filter (EKF) observer has garnered significant attention for its superior position estimation accuracy and noise suppression capabilities. However, its complex matrix operations and high computational cost severely limit its application in engineering practice. To address these issues, this paper proposes an adaptive simplification strategy for the EKF observer. This strategy significantly reduces computational burden by establishing a hierarchical execution framework that decouples prediction and update steps. It also defines a stable lower limit for the update frequency based on the Nyquist sampling theorem. Through a Simulink simulation model, the paper systematically analyzes the influence of different update frequencies on system dynamic response and steady-state accuracy, leading to the design of an adaptive update frequency regulation mechanism based on rotational speed error and angular acceleration. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces computation time while maintaining state estimation accuracy, providing an effective solution for engineering applications of sensorless control for permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs).

title: A Novel PWM Scheme to Suppress the Intra-phase Circulating Current in Two-Parallel Three-Phase Two-Level Converters
authors: Weiyun Huang, Xiaoliang Jin, Lei Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This article proposes a method to suppress intra-phase circulating current in a two-paralleled three-phase converter. Each 60° large sector of the space vector plane is divided into three subsectors, with each applying an optimal vector sequence to minimize both peak and RMS values of intra-phase circulating current. A unified carrier-based modulation scheme and an implementation-friendly flowchart are introduced to facilitate practical application. Compared to existing methods, the proposed approach achieves superior intra-phase current suppression. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Novel Circulating Current Suppression Strategy for Isolated Modular Multilevel DC Transformer Arms
authors: Feng Li, Xuantong Zhou, Shuxin Luo, Sui Peng, Yanfeng Wang, Jianqiao Zhou, Renxin Yang, Gang Shi, Jianwen Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The DC transformer is a fundamental component in DC distribution networks, enabling isolation, voltage conversion, and power transfer, and has therefore attracted significant research interest. Among available topologies, the isolated modular multilevel DC transformer (iMMDCT) integrates the advantages of the modular multilevel converter (MMC) and the dual-active bridge (DAB), offering high-voltage capability, large power handling, modular scalability, and flexible control. With these features, it is regarded as a strong candidate for medium-voltage (MV) DC applications. However, the iMMDCT requires a large number of submodules (SMs) capacitors as energy storage elements. The frequent insertion and bypassing of SMs generate circulating currents in the converter arms, leading to increased losses, elevated device stress, and reduced system reliability. To address this problem, this paper proposes a circulating current suppression method by incorporating an additional full-bridge SM into each arm of the iMMDCT. Simulation studies conducted in MATLAB/Simulink validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Position and Attitude Estimation for Hydraulic Excavator Using External Sensors and Joint Angle Sensors
authors: Kohki Yamamoto, Ryo Kikuuwe
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper proposes an end-effector position and attitude estimation method for hydraulic excavators using total stations and potentiometers. The total station is an external sensor for measuring positions in a ground-fixed coordinate, but its sampling interval is too long for a position controller. The potentiometer is a joint angle sensor whose sampling interval suits the controller. The proposed method is an extended Kalman filter that simultaneously estimates position and attitude. It combines measurements from total stations and potentiometers with different sampling intervals to estimate accurate end-effector position and attitude with a short sampling interval. The proposed method’s validity and applicability to the end-effector position control are verified using a real-time simulator of a hydraulic excavator.

title: Grid-Tied Solar EV Charging Infrastructure: A Feasibility Study for Urban Deployment in India
authors: Mohd Saim, Syed Maaz Ahmed, Mohd Faisal Jalil, Safia Akhtar Kazmi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The rising demand for electric vehicles (EVs) presents both an opportunity and a challenge. While EVs offer a low-emission alternative to conventional transport, their charging infrastructure mostly depends upon fossil fuel powered grids, reducing their environmental benefits. This paper addresses the problem by proposing a solar photovoltaic based EV charging station as a sustainable and economically viable solution. A 29.7 kWp grid-connected PV system was designed and simulated for Noida Sector 105 using PVsyst, targeting specifically four wheelers charging demand. The system demonstrated an annual energy generation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$41,171 \text{kWh}$</tex>, with a performance ratio of 79.5 %, offsetting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$33.76 \text{tCO}_{2}$</tex> emissions annually. Financially, the project yields strong results: a Net Present Value (NPV) of Rs.77.31 lakh, an Internal Rate of Return (IRR) of 40 %, a payback period of 2.5 years, and a Return on Investment (ROI) of 265 % over a 25 -year lifecycle. The Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) was estimated at Rs.8.8 per kWh, competitive with prevailing commercial grid tariffs. Compared to conventional grid-powered EV charging systems, the proposed solar-integrated model not only reduces long term operating costs but also improves environmental performance by reducing the significant amount of GHG emissions. This study proves that solar PV-based EV charging infrastructure is not only feasible but also provides a scalable and replicable model for sustainable urban transport.

title: Predictive Modeling of Viewer Migration: Analyzing the Shift from Theatres to OTT Platforms in Urban India Using Decision Trees
authors: Avneet Kaur Bhatia, Pushpendra Sachan, Sanchita Gupta, Adarsh Kumar Singh, Rajender Singh Thakur, Jayant Rathee
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This study examines the rising preference for Over-the-Top (OTT) platforms among urban Indian audiences and the gradual decline of traditional cinema. A structured survey of 72 participants from varied age groups and professions was conducted to understand this shift. Results show that OTT platforms are strongly favored, especially by younger viewers and creative professionals, though differences across age and occupation were not statistically significant. To predict preference patterns, a decision tree model was applied using factors such as occupation, age, views on industry impact, and opinions about content variety and convenience. The most influential predictors were audience perceptions of OTT's impact on the film industry and the belief that streaming offers greater content diversity. While the model performed well for OTT supporters, it was less accurate for those preferring theatres. Overall, the findings indicate that positive attitudes and the richness of content are more decisive than demographics, suggesting that the digital transformation of Indian cinema is being driven by changing perceptions and personal engagement.

title: Joint Trajectory and Power Optimization in UAV-assisted USVs Communication Network
authors: Qinghui Li, Feng Wang, Peng Zhang, Queping Zou, Yang Wu, Guohua Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This study explores the deployment of an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) serving as an airborne base station to provide downlink data services to mobile unmanned surface vehicles. To optimize system throughput while maintaining fairness among users, we formulate an optimization problem based on the oceanic two-ray channel model. Due to the problem's non-convex and complex nature, it is decomposed into two subproblems, which are solved iteratively using block coordinate descent and successive convex approximation techniques. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm meets users' quality of service (QoS) requirements and outperforms existing benchmark methods in terms of performance gains.

title: Research on the Prediction Model of Remaining Service Life of Industrial Water Pumps Based on STACM-BiLSTM
authors: Wuzhou Wen, Xinyuan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: As the core equipment of modern industrial systems such as underground drainage in mines, the operational reliability of water pumps directly determines the continuity and safety of the project. However, the traditional regular maintenance mode, due to the inability to perceive the real-time health status of the equipment, is prone to problems such as over-maintenance or under-maintenance. The remaining life prediction in fault prediction and health management is the key to solving this problem. However, the traditional Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) network has limitations such as a large number of parameters, complex computation, and insufficient extraction of local spatial features of multi-sensor channels. To this end, this paper proposes an STACM-BILSTM pump RUL prediction model that integrates the spatio-temporal Attention Convolution Module (STACM) and bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM). The experiment was verified with incomplete life cycle data of water pumps and compared with LSTM and BiLSTM models. The results showed that: The coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of STACM-BiLSTM reached 0.9729, the root mean square error (RMSE) was 22.7703, and the mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) was only 20.03%, which was significantly better than the comparison models. This model effectively enhances the RUL prediction accuracy of water pumps and provides reliable technical support for the predictive maintenance of industrial water pumps.

title: Infrared Thickness Measurement of SiC/Si Epitaxial Layers with Multibeam Interference Modeling
authors: Keli Wang, Jialiang Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Neglecting multibeam interference and refractive-index dispersion in infrared interference thickness metrology causes systematic errors. We propose a stable inversion scheme that combines a dynamic refractive-index model, a multibeam-corrected reflectance expression and numerical fitting to retrieve epitaxial-layer thickness from measured spectra. Experiments on Si and SiC wafers yield thicknesses of about 5.97 μm and 7.38 μm with consistent results across incidence angles, verifying the effectiveness of the method..

title: Research on Deep Learning-Based Point Cloud Completion and Recognition Methods for Mechanical Parts
authors: Ying Liu, Ming Zhang, Changqing Zou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To enhance the recognition capability of mechanical parts under point cloud structural deficiencies, an integrated deep neural network architecture combining point cloud reconstruction and multi-scale recognition is constructed. Morphological restoration is achieved through a combination of autoencoder and Transformer structures, while robust classification is realized by introducing a multi-channel attention mechanism. Experimental results demonstrate that under 40% occlusion and noise level σ=0.02, the model maintains recognition accuracies of 86.3% and 89.4%, respectively, outperforming DGCNN and PointTransformer. This validates its structural recovery capability and interference resistance.

title: Heart Rate Measurement using an Earphone-Type Wearable Device Equipped with Biodegradable Piezoelectric Sensors
authors: Keitaro Nonaka, Shumma Jomyo, Hideki Aiko, Shuichi Tasaka, Harutoyo Hirano, Toshio Tsuji
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In recent years, there has been increased interest in using wearable devices for continuous physiological monitoring, which has promising applications in personal health management and clinical practice. This study proposes a method for estimating heart rate using an earphone-type device equipped with a low-power, structurally simple piezoelectric sensor that captures pressure pulse wave signals in the external auditory canal. The proposed method applies two-stage noise removal filtering based on physiologically plausible R–R intervals (RRI) and signal amplitude components, enabling stable heart rate detection even under conditions with external disturbances. When applied to both resting and stimulating periods, the proposed method achieved an agreement rate exceeding 95% after noise removal and demonstrated a strong correlation with heart rate values obtained from ECG. Notably, motion and pain stimuli introduced noise into the signal during the stimulus periods; nevertheless, the proposed method effectively suppressed spurious peak detections arising from transient disturbances.

title: Hybrid Solar/Diesel Central Heating Experimental Investigation & Control
authors: Musa Abdalla, Jannat Al-Saidi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: The traditional design of conventional central heating systems typically involves a circulating pump operating at a fixed speed and does not account for ambient variables, hourly/daily changes. On the other hand, Hybrid Solar/Diesel systems that use a preheating process via solar collectors or vacuum tubes also employ similar fixed-flow circulating pumps. This research investigates improvements to hybrid central heating techniques that preheat the inlet fluid to the boiler system using renewable energy sources, such as solar energy. A complete experimental setup of a Hybrid Solar/Diesel system was designed and installed on the Mechanical Engineering rooftop at the University of Jordan. The system includes state-of-the-art Internet of Things instruments capable of accurately measuring the diversity of the required system's process and environmental variables. The research work findings indicated that the tested Hybrid Solar/Diesel performance and efficiencies were strongly correlated with changes in ambient environmental conditions and the system's flow rate for both the Solar and boiler pump loops. When an online closed-loop PLC controller is incorporated to regulate the circulating pumps' speed, accounting for changes in ambient conditions, component degradation, and aging, the system operates in the best performance region. Furthermore, using an AI-based controller and optimizer enables better optimization and operation of the Hybrid Solar/Diesel system.

title: Sensorless Detection of Axial Position in a 3-DOF Suspension System Based on a Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Flux Observer
authors: Chaoning Zhu, Jinhua Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Axial stability is critical for the safe and efficient operation of magnetically levitated pumps. Traditional active control relies on physical displacement sensors, which face installation challenges in space-constrained or harsh environments. This paper proposes a sensorless axial position detection method using a super-twisting sliding mode flux observer. Based on the electrical model of the axial-force winding, a second-order sliding mode observer is designed to estimate the permanent magnet leakage flux linkage in real-time by exploiting the q-axis current dynamics. The observer employs a continuous nonlinear control law that preserves sliding mode control’s strong robustness while effectively suppressing chattering inherent in first-order methods. Stability analysis guarantees finite-time convergence of the current estimation error. Simulation and experimental results validate the observer’s robustness to electrical parameter variations and show the method accurately reconstructs the leakage flux, which exhibits a clear linear relationship with axial displacement. This confirms the feasibility of using the estimated flux for position feedback, offering a practical solution for sensorless closed-loop axial position control in magnetically levitated pumps.

title: Power Oscillation and Stability Analysis of Parallel VSG Systems with Induction Motor Loads Based on [P Q] - [ω V] Modeling
authors: Jiawei Tan, Xiaochao Hou, Wencong Wu, Haiqing Cai, Wei Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Complex stability issues exist among multiple Virtual Synchronous Generators (VSGs). The heterogeneity of control parameters in VSG tends to induce power, frequency, and voltage oscillation. This phenomenon may be exacerbated in systems with induction motor loads. To address this issue, this paper models with power, frequency, and voltage as key variables, and establishes a [P Q]-[ω V] model for parallel VSGs with induction motor loads, enabling intuitive analysis of power, frequency, and voltage oscillations among converter interactions. Oscillation mechanisms and stability characteristics are analytically derived through Norton equivalent circuit and determinant-based stability criteria. The proposed model reveals the interactions among different VSGs. In addition, it analyzes the impact of key parameters on power oscillation. Simulation results validate the accuracy of the proposed analysis.

title: Exploration of Novel Heat Sink and Cooling Technologies for SiC Power Modules
authors: Xiaoshuang Hui, Puqi Ning, Yuhui Kang, Jiajun Yang, Songze Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The continuous increase in power density and switching frequency in modern power modules, particularly those based on wide bandgap semiconductors such as silicon carbide (SiC), imposes stringent requirements on thermal management strategies. Conventional cooling approaches are often constrained by high thermal resistance, localized hotspots, and limited scalability. Recent research trends have shifted toward integrated and hybrid cooling architectures that combine passive and active mechanisms to enhance heat dissipation effectiveness and system reliability. This work explores two emerging thermal management techniques: the structural optimization of microchannel-based AlSiC heat sinks and the integration of thermoelectric coolers (TECs) for active heat extraction. The proposed approaches aim to reduce junction temperature under high-power operating conditions. Experimental and simulation-based evaluations suggest the feasibility and advantages of combining these advanced cooling strategies in the context of compact and high-performance power module.

title: Dynamic Resource Management and Routing Algorithm for Low-Earth Orbit Satellite Networks Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Jiayi Qiao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This study aims to design an autonomous decision-making algorithm based on deep reinforcement learning (GNN-MAPPO, Graph Neural Network-Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization). This algorithm enables LEO satellite networks to perceive network status in real time and adaptively jointly optimize routing and resource allocation strategies, significantly improving end-to-end performance and resource utilization efficiency in dynamic environments. The satellite network is first modeled as a dynamic graph. A GCN layer is used to aggregate node and link states, generating an embedding vector that reflects topological relationships. Each satellite then acts as an intelligent agent, outputting mixed actions based on this vector through an actor network. During the training phase, a centralized critic and PPO objective function are employed to ensure policy learning stability and collaborative efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed GNN-MAPPO algorithm achieves average latency of 28.5ms, 46.5ms, and 71.3ms under low, medium, and high load conditions, respectively. Total throughput reaches 8.9 Gbps, 18.7 Gbps, and 21.8 Gbps, respectively, while achieving power consumption and load balancing. This research provides key technical support for building intelligent and efficient next-generation satellite networks.

title: A Novel Precursor for Online Gate Oxide Degradation Monitoring in SiC MOSFETs
authors: Hui Meng, Junwei Liu, Yi Zhang, Chiyung Chung, Yang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs suffer from gate oxide degradation (GOD), which threatens system reliability. Existing monitoring parameters, such as threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>) and on-state resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds,on</inf>), are susceptible to junction temperature (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">j</inf>) variations and package aging, leading to inaccurate assessments. This paper proposes a novel, robust precursor for in-situ gate oxide health monitoring: the time delay at a fixed rate of change of the drain-source current (i<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>) during turn-on (Delay-di<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>/dt). Theoretically, this parameter is directly associated with gate oxide degradation while being negligibly affected by T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">j</inf> or package aging. The method is validated through accelerated positive high-temperature gate bias (P-HTGB) tests. After 245 hours of aging, the Delay- di<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>/dt exhibits a significant increase of 37-57%. Double-pulse tests (DPT) confirm stability and insensitivity at temperatures ranging from 30 to 120 °C and currents of 10-20 A. This technique provides a reliable, online solution for predictive maintenance of high-reliability power electronic systems.

title: Active Damping Control for Grid-Connected Inverter with the Magnetic Integrated LCL Filter
authors: Zixiu Sun, Yitao Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The LCL filter grid-connected system exhibits resonance phenomena. To suppress resonance issues, it is necessary to dampen resonance peaks. Currently, the commonly used suppression method is a control strategy based on active damping, which involves sampling the current of the filter capacitor for feedback. However, to improve the power density of inverters, fully integrated LCL filters are typically used. Sampling the capacitor current of a fully integrated filter is extremely inconvenient. This paper proposes an active damping control strategy without capacitor current sensor sampling. This strategy indirectly derives the capacitor current by sampling the inverter current and grid-side current, thereby introducing virtual damping into the control loop to effectively suppress resonance modes. This overcomes the design complexity and other issues associated with traditional active damping methods when using fully integrated filters.

title: Multi-Agent Federated Edge Learning for UAV-IDS in Smart City IoT Environment_supp1-3667039.pdf
authors: Shakila Basheer
publish: N/A
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have become integral to modern consumer electronics (CE) ecosystem-based smart city infrastructures, providing dynamic connectivity, environmental monitoring, and situational awareness through distributed consumer Internet of Things (IoT) networks. However, their reliance on wireless communication links exposes them to a wide range of cyber and physical-layer attacks, demanding intelligent yet lightweight defense mechanisms. This paper presents a Multi-Agent Federated Edge Intrusion Detection System (UAV-IDS) designed to ensure privacy-preserving, real-time protection for UAV-enabled smart city IoT environments. The proposed framework integrates a hybrid Autoencoder–LSTM architecture deployed across edge nodes, where multiple intelligent agents collaboratively perform feature extraction, anomaly detection, and policy adaptation. A UAV-based coordinator manages federated learning rounds, aggregating model updates from distributed edge nodes without sharing raw data. Extensive experiments conducted on the T-ITS dataset demonstrate that the proposed system achieves 99.44% accuracy, 99.31% precision, and a 46.8 ms average detection latency, outperforming existing deep and federated IDS frameworks. Moreover, the lightweight deployment (8.42 MB) and low communication cost confirm the feasibility of the proposed design for embedded UAV operations. The results validate the framework’s potential as an adaptive, scalable, and privacy-conscious solution for next-generation smart city security infrastructures.

title: Online Battery Impedance Identification Based on Flying-Capacitor Leg of FC-NPC Inverter
authors: Yikang Mao, Dong Jiang, Min Zhou, Tao Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Traditional electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) detection method has high accuracy, but the equipment cost is high. This paper proposes an online battery impedance identification method based on the flying-capacitor leg of a flying-capacitor-type neutral-point clamped (FC-NPC) inverter. The method uses the control freedom of the flying-capacitor leg to generate a specific frequency of charge and discharge current by adding a small AC disturbance signal on the capacitor steady-state voltage reference value, exciting the dynamic impedance response of the battery. By adjusting the frequency and amplitude of the disturbance signal, the method enables online identification of battery impedance spectra across a wide frequency range of 1 Hz to 1 kHz. The method simplifies the hardware structure and provides an effective solution for real-time state monitoring of the battery management system (BMS).

title: Design of a Winograd-Based CNN Accelerator with Reconfigurable Convolution Core for FPGA
authors: Yixuan Liu, Haike Liu, Feng Lyu, Liangbo Xie, Qian Huang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: The increasing use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) in computationally intensive applications has made the Winograd algorithm a key technique for reducing the multiplication complexity of convolution operations on FPGAs. However, its inherent precision limitations restrict deployment for CNNs with large input feature maps. To overcome this challenge, we propose a Winograd-based CNN accelerator that integrates a novel reconfigurable convolution method. This method dynamically adapts computation logic and data flow, enabling flexible support for diverse CNN architectures while maintaining efficiency. In addition, the accelerator employs an input feature map tiling strategy to enhance parallelism in Winograd computation. Deployment on the ZYNQ XCZU15EG platform demonstrates that the accelerator achieves a throughput of 187.8 GOPs and a computational efficiency of 0.87 GOPs/DSP, highlighting the effectiveness of the proposed reconfigurable convolution method in balancing scalability and performance.

title:
A 48-V to 12-V/120-A Hybrid Switched-Capacitor Converter with 6200-W/in
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    Power Density
authors: Shunliang Liu, Yenan Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents a two-phase 48-V to 12-V hybrid-switched-capacitor (HSC) converter with high output current capability and automatic phase voltage balancing, specifically designed for data center applications. The proposed topology adopts a modular structure based on a series-input parallel-output configuration, which significantly enhances the current output capability of the circuit. To reduce voltage stress on the devices and suppress output current ripple, a CLC-type output filter is employed. The operational principle of the proposed converter is described, and a specialized gate driver sequence is introduced to achieve soft switching for all main switches. A prototype capable of delivering 120-A output current has been designed and implemented, achieving a peak efficiency of 98.4% and a full-load efficiency of 95.74%, with a power density of 6200 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>.

title: Delay Optimization for RIS-Assisted SWIPT-MEC Joint Uplink and Downlink System
authors: Xianhao Liu, Wenle Bai, Xiangwei Dang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This paper studies a joint uplink and downlink mobile edge computing (MEC) system assisted by reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) and simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT), aiming to minimize delay by jointly optimizing the offloading transmit power, the local computing tasks, the power splitting (PS) ratio, and the uplink and downlink phase shift matrices of RIS. To solve this problem, an alternating optimization (AO) algorithm is proposed, which decomposes the problem into two subproblems and solves them using the successive convex approximation (SCA) method. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Application of Dual-Stream Network with Fusion Attention Mechanism in Defense Sliding Step Posture Defect Detection
authors: Lin Huang, Jing Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Basketball defense sliding step is a fundamental defensive technique that requires precise posture execution. Traditional manual assessment methods are subjective and time-consuming. This paper proposes a novel dual-stream network architecture integrated with fusion attention mechanism for automated detection of defense sliding step posture defects. The proposed method consists of a backbone network that extracts visual features from input images, followed by two parallel processing streams: a conventional feature stream and a specialized pose stream. A fusion attention mechanism intelligently combines information from both streams to enhance defect detection accuracy. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves superior performance compared to existing methods, with 94.2% accuracy in defect classification. The fusion attention mechanism contributes 3.8% improvement over baseline approaches, validating the effectiveness of our multi-stream architecture in sports posture analysis.

title: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Pilot Overhead in Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface-Assisted Wireless Communication Channel Estimation - Art Review
authors: Mohamadou Ndiaye, Papis Ndiaye, Papa Alioune Fall
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) technology is the main candidate for 5G-beyond. However, the increased number of passive components within the structure increases the number of links to be estimated. As a result, existing RIS-assisted channel estimation schemes are more error-prone than traditional communications, thus confounding a very large pilot load. Many approaches have been used to overcome this obstacle. This paper reviews different options studied ranging from RIS configuration optimization, channel feature exploitation, deep learning to blind estimation types with the assistance of adaptive mMIMO smart antennas.

title: Domain Adaptive Network for Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings Under Time-Varying Speed
authors: Xue Wang, Qingkun He, Jingbo Fu, Guo Li, Sheng Zhang, Jinke Cheng
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In modern industrial systems, rolling bearings serve as critical components of rotating machinery, with their operational status directly impacting equipment safety and production efficiency. To prevent failures from disrupting normal operation, implementing early fault diagnosis and preventive maintenance is essential. In practical scenarios, rolling bearing data is collected from diverse mechanical equipment, involving different bearing types, acquisition devices, and environmental conditions. This complexity increases the difficulty of fault diagnosis. Therefore, this paper proposes a domain adaptive time-varying speed rolling bearing fault diagnosis network. This method first employs a dual-branch feature extraction module in both time and frequency domains to capture fault characteristics under variable rotational speeds. These features undergo domain adversarial training to further learn domain-invariant properties. This process forces the model to learn equipment-independent features, ultimately enabling cross-device fault diagnosis for rolling bearings.

title: A Novel On-state Voltage Measurement Circuit for High-voltage IGBTs Based on Cascaded SiC JEFTs
authors: Cheng Zhao, Yankun Xiang, Wentong Shi, Shijie Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: As core components in ultra-high voltage voltage source converter (UHV VSC) valves, high-voltage IGBTs require on-state voltage monitoring for condition assessment. However, existing on-state voltage monitoring circuits fail at 4.5 kV and above (4.5 kV+) levels due to inadequate clamping design. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CE(on)</inf> monitoring circuit employing cascaded normally-on silicon carbide (SiC) JFETs. This configuration clamps the sampled output below 5 V during 4.5 kV+ blocking operations. Validated on a 4.5 kV press-pack IGBT prototype, experimental results confirm accurate on-state voltage transmission within 414 ns propagation delay, with the output voltage exhibiting close alignment to the drain-source voltage during conduction, without auxiliary power. The solution provides a fast-response, cost-effective approach for converter valve health monitoring.

title: Hierarchical Planning and Scheduling for Reconfigurable Multi-Robot Disassembly Systems under Structural Constraints
authors: Takuya Kiyokawa, Tomoki Ishikura, Shingo Hamada, Genichiro Matsuda, Kensuke Harada
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study presents a system integration approach for planning schedules, sequences, tasks, and motions for reconfigurable robots to automatically disassemble constrained structures in a non-destructive manner. Such systems must adapt their configuration and coordination to the target structure, but the large and complex search space makes them prone to local optima. To address this, we integrate multiple robot arms equipped with different types of tools, together with a rotary stage, into a reconfigurable setup. This flexible system is based on a hierarchical optimization method that generates plans meeting multiple preferred conditions under mandatory requirements within a realistic timeframe. The approach employs two many-objective genetic algorithms for sequence and task planning with motion evaluations, followed by constraint programming for scheduling. Because sequence planning has a much larger search space, we introduce a chromosome initialization method tailored to constrained structures to mitigate the risk of local optima. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively solves complex problems in reconfigurable robotic disassembly.

title: Impact Attenuation in Robotic Hammering Tasks
authors: Haruto Ueda, Ryo Kikuuwe
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper proposes a control method for robotic hammering that can attenuate the reaction torques on the robot’s joints while maintaining a sufficient impact on the environment. A key challenge in hammering tasks is this trade-off between delivering a sufficient impact and preventing damage to the manipulator. The proposed method addresses this conflict by generating the entire pre-collision trajectory, including the required initial posture, by calculating backward in time from a desired state at the moment of impact. The trajectory consists of a preparatory backswing and a striking motion, both tracked using a PD controller.
