To further attenuate reaction torques, a feedforward torque pulse is applied to the final joint just before impact. After impact, a force-controlled lift-up mode is activated to raise the hammer and return the robot to its initial posture. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through experiments, demonstrating an attenuation in joint torque.

title: Personal Space Toward Human-like and Non-human-like Robots: Effects of Robot Appearance and Likability
authors: Rio Taguchi, Kaoruko Shinkawa, Yoshihiro Nakata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In this study, we investigated how robot appearance—human-like versus non-human-like—affects personal space during direct robot approaches from eight directions and examined the relationship between personal space and perceived likability. Twenty-three Japanese male participants participated in the experiment. The participants remained stationary while each robot approached from different directions (0°–315° in 45° increments), and the distance at which discomfort was perceived was recorded as the personal space threshold. Likability was assessed via a questionnaire. Across all conditions, a consistent negative correlation was observed between likability and personal space: participants who rated the robot more favorably tended to allow it to approach them more closely. No significant differences in personal space or likability were found between the two appearance conditions. However, the Human-like condition showed an almost circular personal space shape with an approximate radius of 1.35 m, slightly extended in its rear part, whereas the Non-human-like condition tended to have a personal space shape with a larger front and smaller rear parts. These results indicate that likability is closely associated with interpersonal distance regulation and suggest that manipulating perceived likability may serve as an effective strategy for managing personal space in service and interactive robot contexts. These findings may inform robot design approaches aimed at maintaining socially comfortable distances and improving overall user acceptance.

title: Design and Analysis of the Joint Module for a Modular Wire-Actuated Robotic Arm
authors: Qianchen Zhao, Haodong He, Wenhao Jiang, Haifeng Li, Kaisheng Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper presents a robotic arm that is modular, wire-actuated, and designed for safe human-robot interaction. Its core innovation is the integration of symmetric variable-stiffness units (VSUs) based on a flexure mechanism, which are structurally simple yet achieve a wide stiffness-adjustment range. This design enables simultaneous and independent control of joint orientation and stiffness through strategic manipulation of wire lengths and tensions. A direct stiffness-oriented wire-tension distribution (SWTD) method is proposed to efficiently allocate wire tensions for desired joint stiffness. Simulations confirm high accuracy in stiffness regulation, demonstrating a mechanically simple and robust design combined with a sophisticated control algorithm for precise motion and stiffness control.

title: 3D Printing Concrete Path Planning System Based on Machine Vision
authors: Haibin Yin, Pengfei Li, Yufeng Li, Mao Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To address the problem of insufficient forming accuracy caused by cumulative error accumulation in traditional 3D concrete printing trajectory planning, this study proposes a layer-wise adaptive trajectory planning system based on machine vision detection. First, the system performs slicing processing on the 3D model and prints the first layer according to the preset trajectory, which serves as the reference trajectory. For the second and subsequent layers, a camera is used for real-time acquisition of the printed morphology image of the previous layer. The YOLOv8 model is then employed to analyze and process this 2D image, detecting the deviation between the actual morphology and the preset trajectory. Finally, the motion trajectory and printing parameters of the current layer are updated based on the deviation data to accurately match the current trajectory with the actual formed trajectory of the previous layer, thereby suppressing cumulative errors. Experimental results demonstrate that the mean absolute error (MAE) of the proposed system reaches 3.925 mm, the dimensional accuracy error of the printed structure is controlled within ±2.537%, and the cumulative error is reduced by 67.2% compared with the traditional method. This system effectively improves forming accuracy and stability, providing an efficient technical solution for trajectory error correction in 3D concrete printing.

title: Optimizing Large Language Models for Financial Analytics: A Comprehensive Framework for Domain-Specific Adaptation
authors: Ho Huong Thien, Bay Nguyen Van, Vinh Truong Hoang, Kiet Tran Trung, Nghia Dinh
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated important capabilities in natural language understanding and generation, their application, and financial analytics. This paper introduces a comprehensive framework for optimizing LLMs specifically for financial analytics applications. This framework supports to address challenges in domain adaptation, temporal sensitivity, numerical reasoning, and regulatory compliance. FinBERT-XL is proposed to be an enhanced architecture that combines transformer-based language modeling with specialized financial knowledge graphs and temporal attention mechanisms. Our approach offers three key innovations: (1) a hierarchical fine-tuning strategy using multitask learning through financial subdomains, (2) a novel numerical reasoning module that integrates symbolic computation with neural architectures, and (3) a compliance-aware generation framework ensuring financial regulations. Experimental results on multiple financial benchmarks prove that our model achieves a 34.7% improvement in financial entity recognition, 28.3% better performance in sentiment analysis for earnings calls, and 41.2% higher accuracy in financial question answering compared to baseline LLMs.

title: DA-ERL: Demand-Aware Partitioned Collaborative Inference for On-Device Models
authors: Lin Tan, Kehan Guo, Zhiya Tan, Songtao Guo, Zhufang Kuang, Jun Zhao, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The growing demand for intelligent mobile applications has made the deployment and operation of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) on mobile Edge Devices (EDs) increasingly essential. However, the highly dynamic nature of edge environments and the limited computational resources of EDs result in significant energy consumption and compromised inference quality. To address these issues, we propose the Demand-Aware Evolutionary Reinforcement Learning (DA-ERL) framework, a novel approach for optimizing Partitioned Collaborative Inference (PCI) across multiple EDs and Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) servers. At the core of DA-ERL is a Demand-Aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network (DA-STGCN). This new architecture creates a predictive state representation by uniquely integrating two channels: a Spatial Graph Channel using Graph Convolutional Networks to model the network topology, and a Temporal Prediction Channel using Temporal Convolutional Networks to capture the evolution of system dynamics. Moreover, we design and formulate a task dynamic demand index to model the dynamic task characteristics, which guides the agent's learning policy. Furthermore, we train DA-ERL within a Cross-Entropy Method (CEM) based evolutionary framework that leverages elite-guided exploration to enhance sample efficiency in complex search spaces. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed DA-ERL framework significantly outperforms conventional methods, achieving a 23.4% reduction in system cost while maintaining a near-perfect task completion rate in high-density scenarios.

title: LLM-Empowered Agentic AI for QoE-Aware Network Slicing Management in Industrial IoT
authors: Xudong Wang, Lei Feng, Ruichen Zhang, Fanqin Zhou, Hongyang Du, Wenjing Li, Dusit Niyato, Abbas Jamalipour, Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) requires networks that deliver ultra-low latency, high reliability, and cost efficiency, which traditional optimization methods and deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based approaches struggle to provide under dynamic and heterogeneous workloads. To address this gap, large language model (LLM)-empowered agentic AI has emerged as a promising paradigm, integrating reasoning, planning, and adaptation to enable QoE-aware network management. In this paper, we explore the integration of agentic AI into QoE-aware network slicing for IIoT. We first review the network slicing management architecture, QoE metrics for IIoT applications, and the challenges of dynamically managing heterogeneous network slices, while highlighting the motivations and advantages of adopting agentic AI. We then present the workflow of agentic AI-based slicing management, illustrating the full lifecycle of AI agents from processing slice requests to constructing slice instances and performing dynamic adjustments. Furthermore, we propose an LLM-empowered agentic AI approach for slicing management, which integrates a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) module for semantic intent inference, a DRL-based orchestrator for slicing configuration, and an incremental memory mechanism for continual learning and adaptation. Through a case study on heterogeneous slice management, we demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly outperforms other baselines in balancing latency, reliability, and cost, and achieves up to a 19% improvement in slice availability ratio.

title: Robust Tensor Completion via Gradient Tensor Nuclear ℓ 1 - ℓ 2 Norm for Traffic Data Recovery
authors: Hao Shu, Jicheng Li, Tianyu Lei, Lijun Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In real-world scenarios, spatiotemporal traffic data frequently experiences dual degradation from missing values and noise caused by sensor malfunctions and communication failures. Therefore, effective data recovery methods are essential to ensure the reliability of downstream data-driven applications. while classical tensor completion methods have been widely adopted, they are incapable of modeling noise, making them unsuitable for complex scenarios involving simultaneous data missingness and noise interference. Existing Robust Tensor Completion (RTC) approaches offer potential solutions by separately modeling the actual tensor data and noise. However, their effectiveness is often constrained by the over-relaxation of convex rank surrogates and the suboptimal utilization of local consistency. To address these limitations, we introduce the gradient tensor nuclear $\ell _{1}$ - $\ell _{2}$ norm via a non-convex tensor rank surrogate and an advanced feature fusion strategy, and integrate it into the RTC framework to propose the Robust Tensor Completion via Gradient Tensor Nuclear $\ell _{1}$ - $\ell _{2}$ Norm (RTC-GTNLN) model. The proposed model not only fully exploits both global low-rankness and local consistency without a trade-off parameter, but also effectively handles the dual degradation challenges of missing data and noise in traffic data. Extensive experiments conducted on multiple real-world traffic datasets demonstrate that the RTC-GTNLN model consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in complex recovery scenarios involving simultaneous missing values and noise. The code is available at https://github.com/HaoShu2000/RTC-GTNLN

title: CustomVideo: Customizing Text-to-Video Generation With Multiple Subjects
authors: Zhao Wang, Aoxue Li, Lingting Zhu, Yong Guo, Qi Dou, Zhenguo Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Customized text-to-video generation aims to generate high-quality videos guided by text prompts and subject references. Current approaches for personalizing text-to-video generation suffer from tackling multiple subjects, which is a more challenging and practical scenario. In this work, our aim is to promote multi-subject guided text-to-video customization. We propose CustomVideo, a novel framework that can generate identity-preserving videos with the guidance of multiple subjects. To be specific, firstly, we encourage the co-occurrence of multiple subjects via composing them in a single image. Further, upon a basic text-to-video diffusion model, we design a simple yet effective attention control strategy to disentangle different subjects in the latent space of diffusion model. Moreover, to help the model focus on the specific area of the object, we segment the object from given reference images and provide a corresponding object mask for attention learning. Also, we collect a multi-subject text-to-video generation dataset as a comprehensive benchmark. Extensive qualitative, quantitative, and user study results demonstrate the superiority of our method compared to previous state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Power Quality Enhancement in Grid connected Renewable Energy Microgrids with Energy Storage: A Comprehensive Review
authors: R L Rekhasree, R S Sreelekshmi, Manjula G Nair
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The integration of Renewable Energy Sources (RES) into Microgrid introduces many power quality issues in the system due to the inherent intermittent characteristics of RES systems. This paper gives a review of Power Quality Improvement (PQI) strategies used in RES integrated microgrids operating in both grid-integrated and independent modes. Even though conventional approaches of PQI provide fundamental power quality support, they fail to operate in dynamic conditions due to limited responsiveness. Advanced custom power devices such as FACTS and filters give real time voltage regulation and efficient harmonic compensation. Filters effectively mitigate harmonics produced by power electronic converters. Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) helps microgrids to enhance voltage and frequency stability, especially in islanded operation. Hybrid Energy Storage Systems (HESS) integrate energy storage systems with complementary characteristics to improve dynamic performance and power quality in the system. The coordinated implementation of these PQ solutions ensures a stable, resilient, and high-quality power in a renewable energy source microgrid. The findings were supported by simulating an HESS supported DVR for power quality improvement in a grid.

title: Maintenance Decision Support System for Multiple Units Based on High-Quality Knowledge Graphs
authors: Juexiang Ye, Xue Li, Lanshun Nie, Keqiang Song, Dechen Zhan, Chengjie Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: As rail transit rapidly develops and its applications expand, the maintenance of multiple units (MUs) plays a pivotal role in ensuring the safety of train operations and enhancing operational efficiency. However, the maintenance process encompasses a vast amount of complex and dispersed professional knowledge, presenting significant challenges in the effective management and utilization of this information. Existing knowledge management methodologies often struggle to integrate maintenance-related knowledge of MUs, leading to low rates of knowledge utilization. To address these issues, this study proposes a decision support system (DSS) that integrates knowledge graphs with large language models (LLMs). We have designed an automated graph construction framework to systematically extract knowledge from professional literature, thereby building high-quality knowledge graphs. By incorporating the BGM-RSRL prompt framework with LLMs, we have developed an efficient DSS. Experimental results demonstrate that the system accurately identifies complex maintenance requirements for MUs and provides comprehensive decision support.

title: Hybrid Metaheuristic Optimization for Real-Time Tuning of Extended PID Controllers for DC–DC Buck Converter
authors: Ounnas Djamel, Guiza Dhaouadi, Yousfi Laatra, Faris H. Alotaibi, Ahmad F. Tazay, Aymen Flah
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid metaheuristic optimization approach for real-time tuning of an Extended Incremental Discrete Proportional–Integral–Derivative (EID–PID) controller applied to a DC–DC buck converter. The proposed method integrates Adaptive Particle Swarm Optimization with Local Refinement (APSO–L) and the Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) to achieve an effective balance between global exploration and local exploitation. The EID–PID structure enhances conventional PID control through dynamic filtering and anti-windup mechanisms, providing superior robustness and adaptability for nonlinear, time-varying power converter systems. The optimization process is executed in MATLAB, which communicates optimal parameters in real time to an STM32F429I microcontroller implementing the controller law. Simulation studies using MATLAB/Simulink models and experimental validation confirm the algorithm’s fast convergence, high precision, and high robustness through input voltage and load variations. The results demonstrate that the proposed APSO–L–WOA-based EID–PID control scheme offers a powerful and scalable solution for real-time embedded control in power electronics applications.

title: Scalable Tactile Coding
authors: Jie Song, Yun Gao, Dan Wu, Liang Zhou, Shiwen Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: With the rise of the Tactile Internet, delivering real-time and high-fidelity tactile feedback is crucial for enhancing immersion in multimedia services. However, existing tactile coding methods fail to simultaneously adapt to the diverse delay requirements of multimedia services and the time-varying network bandwidth. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Scalable Tactile Coding (STC) method, which provides flexible coding delays and bitrates across multiple levels while ensuring human perceptual quality. Specifically, we first propose a tactile coding framework based on a non-stationary autoencoder for efficient compression, which features both delay-scalable and rate-scalable advantages. Second, by trading the balance between delay and computation, we design a sliding window approach that utilizes overlapping coding to reduce buffer delay. This approach provides multiple delay options to accommodate diverse delay requirements, thus realizing the desired delay-scalable effect. Third, inspired by the base-enhancement strategy of scalable video coding, we design a non-uniform quantization method to compress residual signals, which can be leveraged to dynamically enhance tactile signal fidelity. By adjusting the quantization bit-width, this method provides multiple levels of bitrates, thereby achieving a rate-scalable effect. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that STC achieves a bitrate reduction of over 92.3 % while maintaining satisfactory perceptual quality. Additionally, STC further supports flexible bitrate adjustment and satisfy various delay requirements.

title: A Dual-Band Simultaneous RF Energy Harvesting System With Globally Optimized 3-D MPPT and Efficiency Enhancement
authors: Xiaguang Li, Qing Miao, Shiheng Yang, Tianting Zhao, Sijun Du, Xiaoyang Zeng, Zhiyuan Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a globally optimized radio frequency (RF) energy harvesting system that leverages the novel concepts of 3-D maximum power point tracking (3-D MPPT) and collaborative source reconfiguration to achieve high MPPT accuracy and a wide input power range. The proposed 3-D MPPT coordinates the energy sources, optimizes the rectifier outputs, and regulates the rectifier stages for global efficiency optimization. A multi-level regulating DC–DC converter integrated with a reconfigurable rectifier enables synchronized dual-band RF energy harvesting. The reconfigurable buck–boost converter employs hysteretic control to regulate each rectifier output voltage at its respective maximum power point (MPP) and delivers a stable output through pulse-frequency modulation (PFM) with adaptive inductor <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-time. The DC–DC converter dynamically connects a rechargeable battery (emulated by a power source and a resistor) to either the source or the load, supplementing insufficient energy or recovering surplus energy, thereby significantly extending the input power range. Fabricated in a 180-nm CMOS process, the prototype dual-band RF energy harvesting interface achieves a peak end-to-end efficiency of 71%, a sensitivity of −24.1 dBm, and wide high-power conversion efficiency (PCE) input power ranges of 15.5 and 20.5 dB at 433 and 900 MHz, respectively.

title: Digital-Twin-Enabled Outage Detection and Uplink Availability Optimization with UAV Assistance
authors: Abdullah Othman, Georges Kaddoum, João V. C. Evangelista, Minh Au, Basile L. Agba
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Digital Twins (DTs) offer a powerful tool for outage management in wireless networks, but their direct deployment during emergencies is limited by the high cost of near real-time ray tracing. This work develops a DT-enabled outage management framework that combines vision-based outage detection with availability-oriented uplink control. A lightweight detector (DTOD), trained on DT renders and SINR-consistent labels, produces calibrated outage probabilities in almost real time. These predictions trigger a threshold-capped (ThreshCap) uplink power allocator, which leverages UAV relays to sustain service during macro base station (MBS) failures. Using Markovian MBS outages, realistic DT mobility, and surrogate channels fitted to ray-traced propagation, we show that UAV relays substantially improve availability and that ThreshCap consistently outperforms baselines under varying load and outage intensities.

title: How Ready are Indonesians for Digital Governmentƒ Measuring Citizens' Skills in Majalengka
authors: Nadya Putri Shabrina, Refi Rifaldi Windya Giri, Nila Sartika Achmadi
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: As Indonesia undergoes rapid digital transformation, limited empirical evidence exists on citizens’ ability to effectively use e-government services, hindering the development of inclusive and evidence-based digital participation strategies. This study empirically develops and validates an instrument for measuring citizens’ digital skills within the context of Majalengka’s e-government system. A 25-item questionnaire adapted from van Deursen’s digital skills framework was administered to 207 respondents with heterogeneous demographic profiles in age, gender, education, and employment status, reflecting the socio-economic diversity of a semi-urban regency. Exploratory factor analysis (Principal Axis Factoring, Promax rotation) revealed two dominant factors. The first, medium-related skills, refers to citizens’ technical ability to operate and navigate e-government systems. The second, content-related skills, encompasses understanding, communication, creation, and decision-making. Together, these two factors explained 84.04% of the total variance. The findings indicate that citizens’ digital skills remain concentrated in basic, task-oriented activities, reflecting limited participatory engagement in local e-government systems which underscores the need to strengthen higher-order digital competencies for inclusive digital transformation. This study contributes a validated framework for assessing e-government-related digital skills in smaller urban contexts and offers practical guidance for digital inclusion, data-driven local governance, and citizen-centered smart city readiness and service innovation.

title: Orthogonal Manipulability Optimization for OMRM With Neural Networks and Nonconvex Constraints
authors: Shijun Tang, Yunfeng Hu, Zhishuo Zhang, Zhiguo Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Manipulability optimization is critical for avoiding singularities in an omnidirectional mobile redundant manipulator (OMRM), yet its intrinsic nonlinearity and nonconvexity introduce substantial computational challenges. Furthermore, the coupling between manipulability optimization and position error inevitably results in a theoretically nonzero error, thereby compromising control accuracy. To address these issues, this article proposes an orthogonal manipulability maximization optimization under non-convex constraints (OMMONC) scheme, which decouples position error and manipulability through an orthogonal projection matrix. It drives the position error to theoretically converge to zero, while simultaneously reducing the risk of singular postures in motion control. To generate smooth control signals, a non-convex recurrent neural network (NCRNN) is developed, which realizes real-time computation of the OMMONC scheme via an inverse-free solution, thereby mitigating lag-induced errors and enhancing computational efficiency. The proposed OMMONC scheme is theoretically proven to decouple position error and manipulability, and the convergence of the NCRNN model is also demonstrated. Numerical simulations, comparative results and platform experiments verify the validity and superiority of the proposed scheme and model.

title: Towards Acceptance of Virtual Validation: The Indirect Technology Acceptance Framework
authors: Cindy Demuth, Nick Schade, Henrik Lampe, Theodor Behrens, Jürgen Pannek
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The development of autonomous and respective multi-agent systems creates both technological innovations and significant societal and regulatory challenges. Of particular note is the social acceptance of these systems once they are integrated into their usage environment. This is a critical factor that is typically limited to the end product, while the development process and the underlying technologies remain largely disregarded. However, there are indirect technologies like virtual testing, which are increasingly used in the respective product development, for which their potential effects on the acceptance among stakeholders are overlooked. To address this source of indirect skepticism, this paper introduces the Indirect Technology Acceptance Framework (iTAF) to investigate the practical applicability and acceptance dimensions using the example of virtual validation methods. The framework reveals specific implications for each framework layer: the technological layer requires procedural transparency; the institutional layer necessitates harmonized standards across jurisdictions; the societal layer highlights the influence of communication strategies and safety demonstrations; and the interactive layer emphasizes continuous stakeholder engagement throughout the development process. The model successfully integrates technical, regulatory, and societal dimensions, acknowledging the unique position of indirect users as critical stakeholders in societal legitimation and integration. It also offers a foundation to align development processes and especially system validation to the stakeholders’ needs regarding the trustworthiness of the system of interest (SOI). To illustrate the iTAF, it is applied as a demonstrative example to the social acceptance of virtual validation in the presence of legal uncertainty.

title: A Heuristic Search Path Planning Algorithm for Complex Dynamic Environments
authors: Haibo Huang, Hengrong Cui, Jiashun Wang, Meiyue Diao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Reliable, efficient path planning is the foundation for intelligent systems (mobile robots, UAVs, autonomous driving). In complex environments with dense dynamic obstacles, conventional static path planning algorithms are limited in adapting to dynamic changes, facing challenges in real-time performance, safety, and path quality. This paper proposes a heuristically enhanced A*-based algorithm. Using the classic A* framework, it introduces a multi-objective cost heuristic function, embedding dynamic obstacle trajectory prediction and obstacle density perception into heuristic evaluation to proactively mitigate risks. Additionally, it integrates multi-objective optimization to build a weighted cost function, which quantifies path travel cost, heuristic cost, smoothness, and energy consumption, balancing metrics to generate an optimized path.Comparative experiments were conducted in scenarios with dynamic obstacles of different densities and speeds. Results show that the proposed STC-DA* reduces average planning time by 60.8% vs. D* Lite and 87.8% vs. RRT*; its path safety score is 13% higher than D* Lite and 14.7% higher than RRT*. It also minimizes expanded nodes during search, showing advantages in efficiency and comprehensive performance in complex dynamic environments. This study provides an efficient, safe algorithm reference for dynamic path planning.

title: Mixed Competitive—Cooperative Pricing for EV Charging Stations: A Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Approach with Heterogeneous Hierarchical Attention
authors: Yujing Li, Qiang Xing, Si Lv, Zening Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: The charging price influences electric vehicle (EV) users’ charging decisions. A reasonable pricing strategy not only reduces the operating cost of the power distribution network (PDN), but also enhances the profitability of charging station operators (CSOs). EV charging stations (EVCSs) engage in both competition and cooperation to maximize their own interests or the CSOs. The dynamic states and strategic interactions among heterogeneous EVCSs pose significant challenges to pricing decisions. This paper proposes a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) approach for optimal pricing model of EVCSs exhibiting mix competitive and cooperative behaviors in coupled power and transportation networks (CPTNs). First, a mixed game pricing model is developed, incorporating the economic dispatch of the PDN and user charging decision responses. The pricing problem is formulated as a general-sum Markov game, and a MARL algorithm based on a hierarchical attention mechanism is designed to approximate the Nash equilibrium of pricing games. The algorithm captures interaction dependencies among heterogeneous agents through a hierarchical graph and leverages an attention mechanism to integrate local and global features from neighboring agents for policy generation and value estimation, thereby ensuring stability in multi-agent environments. Finally, the effectiveness of the algorithm is validated on a CPTN system in Xi’an, China.

title: A Novel Lightweight Hybrid MLP and Quantum Neural Network Model for Bit Error Rate Prediction in NOMA-Enabled Visible Light Communication Systems
authors: Vipul Dixit, Bhuvan Unhelkar, Abhay Bansal, Mandeep Mittal
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, a lightweight hybrid Multi-Layer Perceptron-Quantum Neural Network (MLP-QNN) architecture for Bit Error Rate (BER) prediction in Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access-Visible Light Communication (NOMA-VLC) systems is proposed. The proposed approach integrates classical preprocessing with variational quantum circuits (VQC) using ZFeatureMap encoding and TwoLocal ansatz in a compact 170-parameter structure implemented through Qiskit-PyTorch framework. The quantum circuits serve as enhanced feature extractors to capture complex NOMA-VLC dynamics. Simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the hybrid quantum-classical approach, achieving R2 scores of 0.9989 and 0.9985 for far and near users, with 1 – 1.5dB SNR gains over classical MLP, advanced classical and analytical methods at reduced computational requirements. The model achieves the high accuracy and ≈0.8dB SNR gains under noisy simulation, confirming quantum noise robustness.

title: URL2Path: A Robust Graph Learning Approach for Malicious URL Detection
authors: Mahdi Rabbani, Morteza Eskandarian, Mansur Mirani, Gunjan Piya, Igor Opushnyev, Rongxing Lu, Sajjad Dadkhah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Phishing attacks remain one of the most popular, damaging cyber threats, with malicious Uniform Resource Locators (URLs) acting as a primary vector for credential theft, malware distribution. Traditional detection approaches, based on phishing keyword matching, static rule-based systems, struggle against modern phishing attempts due to syntactic variability, semantic obfuscation, adversarial manipulation. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have further enabled attackers to generate visually deceptive, syntactically diverse URLs that evade lexical similarity filters. To address these emerging challenges, this paper introduces a content-independent phishing URL detection framework that combines lexical feature extraction with graph-based reasoning. The proposed technique, URL2Path, tokenizes URL strings into sequential segments, maps them onto a homogeneous directed graph, where structural, semantic patterns are captured using DeepWalk-based node embeddings. This approach addresses three key limitations in existing systems: limited scalability of content-based detection, weak robustness of traditional models against LLM generated adversarial URLs, the absence of graph-structured reasoning to vectorize raw URLs into expressive representations. Experimental evaluations show that URL2Path achieves superior precision, recall, F1-score, particularly under cross-dataset validation, adversarial stress testing, imbalanced training conditions. The model is benchmarked against recent lightweight LLMs (BERT-Tiny, DeBERTa-v3, ModernBERT, DeepSeek), demonstrating improved detection accuracy, faster inference. Additionally, we assess its scalability to million-scale datasets, cross-domain generalization, robustness against adversarial perturbations.

title: Combating Banana Crop Losses: A CNN-Random Forest Model for Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Deepak Banerjee, Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Vatsala Anand, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Computing, Communication and Automation (ICCCA)
abstract: This work proposes a hybrid classification model using CNN-Random Forest for the six banana leaf diseases from the 12,825 image dataset. The model is trained and tested against the classes Panama Disease, Black Sigatoka, Banana Bunchy Top Virus, Banana Leaf Spot, Banana Mosaic Disease, and Anthracnose. The model performed very well consistently, with precision, recall, and F1-score all above 98%. Specifically, Banana Mosaic Disease reached the highest precision of 98.43% and F1score of 98.32 %, whereas Banana Leaf Spot reached the highest recall of 98.40 %. The other classes, viz., Panama Disease (98.29 %, 98.05%, 98.17%), Black Sigatoka (98.12%, 98.35%, 98.24%), and Anthracnose (98.29%, 98.05%, 98.17%), also showed good results. All the classes had support in the range of 0.16 to 0.17, and the support values were in the range of 2040 to 2230. All the classes achieved classification accuracy of 99 %, while the model accuracy was 98.24 %, proving the reliability of the model. These findings confirm the efficacy of the hybrid model for the recognition of banana leaf disease with high accuracy, towards precision agriculture.

title: A Game-Theoretic Incentive Mechanism for Blockchain-based Semi-Asynchronous Federated Learning
authors: Rekhasree Manthu, Rakesh Tripathi, Tirath Prasad Sahu, Bala Prakasa Rao Killi
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a privacy-preserving technique that focuses on sharing intermediate training parameters rather than raw data, which helps to prevent privacy violations and facilitates effective data collaboration. The integration of blockchain technology with FL has generated significant interest due to its benefits, including decentralization and enhanced privacy protection for raw data. While existing asynchronous FL methods have successfully tackled the low training efficiency associated with synchronous approaches, they often overlook the need for incentives for participating parties due to the limitations of a centralized trust model. Numerous existing incentive models depend on centralized trust mechanisms, which can undermine the decentralized essence of blockchain and create potential vulnerabilities. To overcome these difficulties, an FL model is developed in the blockchain network to enhance the performance of the model. Further, the strategies of participants are analyzed by implementing the game theory model along with the incentive mechanism based on smart contracts. This incentive mechanism is helpful in minimizing malicious action based on the Nash equilibrium. In addition, the theory models based on the Age of Information (AoI) are considered to motivate the data sharing in a user-centric manner. Several simulations are conducted to evaluate the security model of the incentive mechanism. The experimental results demonstrate the validity of our proposed schemes.

title: Vision-Guided Flexible Material Sorting and Palletizing System Based on HALCON
authors: Rui Chen, Qing Wang, Jiale Wang, Jing Liu, Shunyu Wang, Yaoming Luo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: As industrial automation evolves toward flexibility and intelligence, conventional sorting and palletizing systems face challenges including labor dependency, limited adaptability, and inadequate precision. This paper presents an adaptive material sorting and palletizing system based on HALCON machine vision. The system employs a three-layer "visual-decision-execution" architecture centered on HALCON algorithms. Through image preprocessing, HSV-based feature extraction, and shape template matching, it achieves robust recognition and precise localization of multi-category materials. Integrated with hand-eye calibration and TCP/IP communication, the system enables real-time coordination between vision and a six-axis robot. Experimental results demonstrate over 98.5% sorting success for block and cylindrical materials, with positioning errors under ±0.5 mm and cycle times within 10 seconds. The system offers an efficient, cost-effective automation solution for small and medium-sized manufacturers, showing significant application potential.

title: Combining High Level Scheduling and Low Level Control to Manage Fleets of Mobile Robots
authors: Sabino Francesco Roselli, Ze Zhang, Knut Åkesson
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The deployment of mobile robots for material handling in industrial environments requires scalable coordination of large fleets in dynamic settings. This paper presents a two-layer framework that combines high-level scheduling with low-level control. Tasks are assigned and scheduled using the compositional algorithm ComSat, which generates time-parameterized routes for each robot. These schedules are then used by a distributed Model Predictive Control (MPC) system in real time to compute local reference trajectories, accounting for static and dynamic obstacles. The approach ensures safe, collision-free operation and supports rapid rescheduling in response to disruptions such as robot failures or environmental changes. We evaluate the method in simulated 2D environments with varying road capacities and traffic conditions, demonstrating high task completion rates and robust behavior even under congestion. The modular structure of the framework allows for computational tractability and flexibility, making it suitable for deployment in complex, real-world industrial scenarios.

title: Research on Torque-Based Bolt Removal Identification Algorithm
authors: Shaolun Liu, Hong Lu, Zhi Liu, Dongyu Wang, He Huang, Tianyu Han, Yongquan Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Aiming at the real-time control problem of laboratory as well as actual disassembling robots, this paper proposes a feedback control strategy based on torque data. First, the workflow of the laboratory disassembly robot for removing bolts is analyzed, and a corresponding torque control model is established. Secondly, a set of data processing algorithms is designed to process the data collected by the sensor through filtering, feature extraction and state classification, so that it can be input to the robot as feedback. Finally, the reliability of the processed data and the feasibility of the proposed method are verified through experiments. The experimental results show that the method can correctly process the data collected by the sensors and has strong adaptability.

title: Comparative Analysis of the Effect of Merging Layers on Vision Transformers and Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Oditi, Palak Handa, Nidhi Goel
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs), inspired by the selfattention mechanism of transformers, have emerged as powerful alternatives to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image classification, offering global context modeling and enhanced semantic representation. The different layers within ViT and CNN architectures play a crucial role in feature extraction and representation, warranting deeper investigation. The objective of this work is to analyze the effect of Keras merging layers on ViT architectures. Additionally, we compare these findings with CNN architectures to understand the role of merging operations across different model types. We design sixteen models, eight ViT variants and eight CNN variants, incorporating one of the seven merging operations. These models are trained on the Intel Image Classification dataset. Their interpretability is assessed using post-hoc explainability techniques such as Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME), while their performance was analyzed using evaluation metrics. Among the ViT variants, average and subtract show high performance and more intense attention maps, while CNN variants with concatenate and subtract merging layers show superior spatial localization and structural clarity. ViTs generally exhibit higher sensitivity to architectural changes, whereas CNNs remain more robust across merging strategies. These findings highlight the significance of internal merging mechanisms in shaping model performance and interpretability.

title: A Predictive Study on Early Alzheimer's Diagnosis Using Statistical Learning Methods
authors: Amrutha Reddy Medapati, Krishika S, Logeshwari Dhavamani
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease is a progressive, degenerative brain illness that is more common in elderly people, this is the most common type of dementia. In order to curtail mortality as well as increase the efficacy of treatments, detection at an early stage is vital. Researchers in this research constructed predictive frameworks of Alzheimer's disease using anonymized longitudinal MRI images derived from the OASIS dataset. They utilized 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNN), which have been acknowledged as highly effective at recognizing subtle patterns in 3D brain images, along with sophisticated deep learning techniques. Also, in order to perform an in-depth comparison, they investigated traditional machine learning classifiers like Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), Decision Tree (DT), AdaBoost, Deep Neural Networks (DNN), and Random Forest (RF). Some of the important factors such as accuracy, error rate, Area Under Curve (AUC), Recall and computative time established for validating the model. Notably, the 3D-CNN model had the largest overall accuracy at 97.2 %, surpassing the other models. These findings demonstrate the potential of new artificial intelligence techniques to advance early detection and diagnosis, ultimately saving the lives of those who may contract the disease.

title: Grid Connected PV Energy Stable Control with Distributed Generation and Energy Storage Integration
authors: Swati Kumari, Md Sarwar, Trijeet Mullick
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The microgrid system that has one or more distributed generators (DG) and one energy storage unit is fully analyzed within the context of this research study in terms of its control strategies in both grid-connected and islanded modes. To enhance the resilience and reliability of the energy supply, the paper examines how different renewable sources of energy can be combined with the latest energy storage systems. It provides a critical discussion of stability control methods, including predictive modelling and real-time data management, which ensures maximum performance in various operational conditions. The paper also highlights the importance of advanced control systems enabling the seamless transition of modes in a way that will promote sustainability and energy autonomy. The results also advance the development of microgrid technology as they provide the appropriate information that could be employed in future in the energy management system and policy development.

title: A Review of Data-Driven Frameworks for State of Health (SOH) Estimation and Remaining Useful Life (RUL) Prediction of Supercapacitors
authors: Caiying Xu, Hengzhao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To enhance the safety and economy of supercapacitor-based energy storage systems, numerous physics-based and data-driven frameworks have been proposed for supercapacitor state of health (SOH) estimation and remaining useful life (RUL) prediction. This paper reviews the data-driven frameworks for such applications. First, three supercapacitor datasets are summarized to illustrate the data collection process in the laboratory. Next, four categories of neural network (NN)-based frameworks are reviewed: feedforward neural network (FFN)-based, recurrent neural network (RNN)-based, convolutional neural network (CNN)-based, and emerging structures such as physics-informed neural network (PINN)-based frameworks. In general, these frameworks perform well as they lead to relatively low errors.

title: High-Precision Trajectory Generation Based on Data-Driven Model Predictive Control With Weight Optimization
authors: Junya Fukui, Takashi Nammoto
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive framework for trajectory generation based on data-driven model predictive control (MPC) for industrial applications. The proposed method effectively combines a transfer function model, which is identified from operational data, with a neural network to address and mitigate modeling errors. In contrast to conventional online MPC approaches, the method formulated in this paper treats trajectory generation as an offline optimization problem, wherein the position sequence is directly optimized. Multiple performance metrics are jointly optimized, with the cost function weights being automatically tuned through machine learning-based optimization techniques, all while adhering to explicit tracking error constraints. Experimental validation conducted on a linear guide system demonstrates that the proposed method achieves both high tracking performance and smooth, practical trajectories, eliminating the need for manual parameter adjustments. This framework offers a robust and adaptable solution for advanced trajectory design applicable across a variety of industrial applications.

title: Explainable AI for Preclinical Risk Detection of Bile Duct Cancer Using Multi-Omics, Microbiome Data and Clinical Biomarkers
authors: Amit Singh, Aditya Kumar, Akhilesh KumarGupta, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Chiranjib Parida
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Cholangiocarcinoma (CCA) also known as bile duct cancer which is a highly malignant form of cancer has very poor prognosis. Due to lack of early diagnosis CCA is typically asymptomatic in its initial stages. By the time patients begin experiencing noticeable symptoms the disease is often at a more advanced stage. This late-stage diagnosis based on subjective patient reports which serves as a significant barrier to improve treatment outcomes. To resolve some of these challenges we have developed and validated an innovative paradigm for preclinical CCA risk prediction. To create a more comprehensive model of cholangiocarcinoma we have developed a new model. The proposed model captures the disease’s complexity by integrating several types of data. It incorporates multi-omics data, microbial data, and longitudinal biomarker data. Proposed model includes an Explainable AI (XAI) layer using SHAP and LIME. Which makes inferences from the model more transparent and interpretable. This work aims to resolve the “blackbox” problem longstanding with AI models along with achieving a balance between predictive power and clinical utility. The framework has shown consistent performance on independent validation cohorts just like AUC>0.91. Which outperforms single-omics data based models. More impressively this innovative work identifies high-risk patients 12-18 months prior to a definitive clinical diagnosis. This proposed framework provides a novel approach to accurately drive away the biological disease risk and has now been shown to validity.

title: Substrate-Type High-Temperature and Large-Strain Optical Fiber Sensor
authors: Lei Yu, Zikun Lin, Jiaqi Liu, Tianyu Gai, Ruiya Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper proposes a welded substrate-type high-temperature strain optical fiber sensor. The aim is to ensure the sensor operates stably within a wide temperature range from room temperature to 800°C by optimizing substrate materials and packaging processes. A temperature-strain decoupling algorithm and a compensation structure are designed to achieve high strain measurement accuracy at high temperatures. Meanwhile, the substrate-type packaging is adopted to facilitate on-site installation and arrangement of the sensor. The sensor maintains good linearity within 800°C, which proves that the sensor does not debond at high temperatures and realizes effective strain feedback. Experiments show that the sensor has a temperature sensitivity of 15.3 pm/°C and a strain sensitivity of 24.5 pm/με.

title: Artificial Intelligence Integrated Microsoft Power Apps for Streamlining Small-Scale Businesses
authors: Jyothsna Radha Salla, Pamula Udayaraju
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Small-scale businesses often face operational challenges due to manual workflows, limited access to digital tools, and high costs of implementing modern business solutions. Developing even a basic solution can be expensive, time-consuming, and difficult to automate. With recent advancements, businesses now require intelligent, low-code, and cost-effective solutions that can overcome these limitations while improving efficiency. This paper proposes the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with Microsoft Power Apps to create smarter, more accessible business applications. Unlike traditional methods, this approach requires minimal coding, reduces costs, and supports intelligent decision-making. The AI-integrated Power Apps streamline operations, improve productivity, and enable data-driven insights—making them highly practical for small-scale businesses. The model incorporates advanced AI features such as predictive analytics, NLP-powered chatbots, and pattern recognition. These are deployed within Power Apps to automate core functions like purchase, sales, logistics, customer support, production, and quality control. By leveraging Microsoft AI Builder, Azure services, and Power Automate, the solution ensures seamless integration with existing business processes while keeping complexity low. Simulation results demonstrate clear benefits for small-scale industries: a 55% reduction in manual management efforts, 30% faster response times, and more accurate sales-driven production predictions. Overall, the proposed system delivers scalable, adaptable, and cost-efficient digital transformation, making AI-powered Power Apps a highly effective solution for small-scale business growth and sustainability.

title: Flexible AC-AC Voltage Regulation Strategy for Low-Voltage Distribution Networks with High-Penetration Distributed Scenarios
authors: Yumin Cui, Tao Xu, Jianqiao Zhou, Gang Shi, Yajun Jia, Jianwen Zhang, Dongmin Xi, Ruijia Guan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the widespread integration of large-scale new-energy source-load systems into low-voltage distribution networks (LVDNs), sensitive loads demand increasingly stringent voltage quality on the user side. Distributed photovoltaic (PV) integration particularly triggers voltage overlimit issues in distribution grids, which may cause damage to electrical equipment. Based on this application scenario, this paper introduces a compact and cost-effective flexible voltage regulator (CCFVR) for distribution networks utilizing direct AC-AC conversion. Featuring reduced switch count, simplified control, compact structure, and high cost-effectiveness, the device effectively stabilizes user-side voltage in LVDNs. Furthermore, a control strategy is introduced for LVDNs based on CCFVR during voltage limit violations, which employs the coordination of mechanical-power electronic switches for surge-free switching operations, reducing switching losses and arc generation during the process. Finally, a simulation model for flexible voltage regulation in distribution networks with distributed photovoltaic integration has been developed to validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed regulation strategy.

title: YOLOv8s-AeroLite: A Lightweight and Aerial-Optimized Detector for UAV-Based Object Detection on the VisDrone Dataset
authors: Manjit Jaiswal, Kapil Kumar Nagwanshi, Amit Kumar Chandanan
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: UAV-based vision systems face major challenges including small object size, dense scenes, occlusions, and limited onboard computation, yet they are crucial for applications such as traffic monitoring, public safety, and urban surveillance. This paper presents YOLOv8s-AeroLite, a lightweight detection framework tailored for UAV-based vehicle monitoring in complex aerial environments. Building on YOLOv8s, the model integrates GhostC2f for efficient feature extraction, CBAM-Lite and AerialLKA for spatial-channel attention enhancement, and a GFLv2 prediction head for quality-aware localization, with GELU activation improving convergence in compact networks. Evaluated on the VisDrone benchmark, YOLOv8s-AeroLite achieves 51.6% $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 0.5$ using only 8.2 M parameters and 21.1 GFLOPs, delivering strong small-object performance while maintaining low computational overhead. These results demonstrate its suitability for real-time UAV surveillance and edge deployment.

title: Zero-Rendered Lightweight Digital Twin Campus Network Operation and Maintenance System Based on AI Foundation
authors: Tong Ming, Ren Lu
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: As campus networks expand, device numbers surge and scenarios diversify, traditional O&M struggles with real-time accuracy and cost control. This paper proposes a lightweight digital-twin framework for campus network O&M. It deploys a locally-hosted high-performance AI large model (e.g., DeepSeek) as the foundation, fuses a graph database with a zero-rendered twin engine, uses an “entity-attribute-event” logical data model (e.g., “AP-signal strength-alarm”) instead of 3D meshes to achieve precise, real-time mapping and millisecond-level synchronization of the physical campus network. Driven by JSON-LD semantic data streams, the system supports millisecond-response O&M interactions and enables a fully AI-driven closed-loop. Experiments show that the solution outperforms traditional GPU-rendering-heavy digital twins in resource consumption, response speed and automation, offering a quickly replicable and efficient path for campus network O&M.

title: An Analytical Study on the Gender Digital Divide With Reference to Women's Digital Literacy
authors: Neha, Sheel Nidhi Tripathi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The digital revolution has created a large impact on humanity but like a coin has two sides digital revolution also has some gaps in its optimization specially when it comes to gender uses. It has been observed that in developing country like India where there is a persistent divide between gender, a kind of digital divide is also existing because of the lack of digital literacy which is creating gender divide. The present study is aimed to understand the women's access to knowledge and their need of digital literacy in international context. For this systematic literature review from 2015-2025 has been done. This literature review examines women's access to technology and digital literacy. To gather the relevant data, almost fifty scholarly articles and reports were examined. Gender stereotypes, safety concerns, lack of educational options, and restricted access served as the foundation for these studies. Women are probably far less likely than males to have access to digital gadgets, mobile internet, and when it comes to participate in ICT-driven economies in middle- and low-income nations like India then this gender divide becomes a threat. The research reveals that there is a need of such digital strategies that can take into account the race, gender and other factors to make sure that women have equal access, meaningful use, and empowerment in digital era.

title: Threshold Strategies in Swarm Robotics: A Game-Theoretic Approach to Equilibrium in Task Allocation
authors: Harish Kasireddy
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Threshold-based task allocation strategies are central to effective collaboration in swarm robotics, where agents must complete tasks without communication or centralized control. By modeling task allocation as a global game, this paper offers the first formal proof that individual agents' threshold-response strategies lead to system-level equilibrium in collaborative tasks. The equilibrium ensures that no agent can improve its utility by changing its behavior, even under noisy sensory conditions. The study also shows how Gaussian noise in perception generates the commonly observed sigmoid threshold responses, bridging the gap between biological models and robotic applications. This game-theoretic framework provides a rigorous explanation for the success of threshold strategies in decentralized, large-scale robotic systems and opens new pathways for their application in real-world challenges such as multi-robot coordination and environmental monitoring.

title: Research on a Calibration Method for Optoelectronic Parameters of Medical Electronic Endoscopes
authors: Beixun Qi, Donghao Xiu, Lin Yu, Baihui Zhuang, Weidong An, Lei Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper presents a calibration method for the optoelectronic parameters of medical electronic endoscopes. Based on this method, a compact system was developed, integrating standard light sources, electronic targets, automatic image acquisition, and multi-threaded image processing. It enables rapid and accurate on-site measurement of key parameters, including luminance response, signal-to-noise ratio, spatial frequency response, and static tolerance. Applicable to mainstream endoscopes, the method provides a reliable and efficient approach for quality control, supporting consistent assessment of optoelectronic performance in clinical settings.

title: Aging Behavior of Transformer Oil: A Comparative Experimental Investigation Between Accelerated and Real-Time Aging
authors: Abdulrahman Omar Ali Mohammad, Asfar Ali Khan, Obaidur Rahman, Abdul Suboor
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Reliable operation of transformers depends critically on the condition of insulating oil, whose dielectric properties degrade progressively with aging. This paper presents a comparative study of real-time aging and accelerated aging of transformer oil to evaluate their performance and correlation. Mineral oil samples were obtained from a 15 MVA power transformer in service at substations in Aligarh, representing natural degradation over 1–5 years. In parallel, accelerated aging was induced under controlled thermal stress using copper strips, copper strings, and kraft paper to simulate insulation-oil interactions. Key dielectric parameters, namely dielectric constant, dielectric dissipation factor (DDF), and DC resistivity, were systematically measured for both sets of samples over equivalent aging durations. The experimental results revealed consistent trends in degradation, with accelerated-aged oil showing close agreement with real-time aged samples, particularly in dielectric loss and resistivity variations. This confirms the reliability of accelerated aging as a practical and cost-effective approach to replicate long-term service conditions within a shorter time frame. The findings contribute to improved diagnostic practices, predictive maintenance strategies, and transformer health monitoring frameworks, thereby enhancing the overall reliability and service life of power transformers.

title: Per-Unit Normalized High-Power Three-Level Converter Prototype and Research on Harmonic Optimization Strategies
authors: Yuying Zheng, Zefan Rong, Heya Yang, Xin Xiang, Wuhua Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a systematic design methodology for three-level neutral-point-clamped converters that emphasizes harmonic minimization and efficiency enhancement. The approach integrates hybrid topology optimization with advanced modulation strategies, enabling a balanced trade-off between waveform quality and switching losses. A comparative analysis of Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation and Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation demonstrates that Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation achieves superior harmonic suppression and higher DC-link voltage utilization. Furthermore, a scalable teaching and research platform is constructed using per-unit parameter normalization, which bridges laboratory small-scale laboratory experiments with industrial high-power benchmarks. This work addresses key limitations of existing power electronics training tools, offering a reliable and scalable solution suitable for both educational and industrial applications.

title: Stability Analysis of A 100% Renewable Energy System with Different Power allocation of Grid-Forming and Grid-Following Inverters
authors: Shiwei Chen, Ziqian Yang, Wangqianyun Tang, Ye Zhang, Yu Xia, Wanning Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy, grid-forming inverters (GFMIs) and grid-following inverters (GFLIs) have become core components in 100% renewable energy systems (RES). For such a system, how to allocate the power allocation (PA) of GFMIs and GFLIs so as to improve the small-signal stability of the system is a significant issue. To address this issue, this paper proposes a new stability analysis method to investigate the impact of GFMIs and GFLIs PA on system stability: First, a stability criterion for multiple input multiple output (MIMO) system is established based on the ν-gap metric theory. Furthermore, based on the proposed ν-gap metric stability criterion, we develop an algorithm to calculate the stable range of operating points of GFMIs and GFLIs. The impact of their PA on the stability of 100% RES is systematically analyzed, and the stable range of active power for each GFMI and GFLI is quantitatively determined. Finally, several simulations validate the proposed method.

title: Low-latency online estimation of human upper-limb pose and kinematics from a single 360° camera
authors: Mathis D’Haene, Guillaume Caron, Yusuke Yoshiyasu, Bruno Watier
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: We present a fully online framework for streaming human upper-limb kinematics estimation from a single 360° camera. Incoming frames are processed sequentially through vertical-boundary-aware tracking, pseudo-perspective rendering, and Neural Localizer Fields to estimate a sparse set of 3D anatomical landmarks in real time. These landmarks are mapped to an OpenSim-compatible biomechanical model, with joint angles computed on the fly via an online inverse kinematics solver. The system achieves end-to-end latencies as low as 22.9 ms on a high-performance setup. Evaluated in a single-participant scenario involving an initial T-pose calibration and repeated object displacement toward the camera, it demonstrates robust performance under moderate self-occlusion and spherical distortion. While tested in a constrained setting, its modular, real-time design makes it a promising candidate for human–robot interaction and other motion analysis applications, enabling minimal, markerless, and anatomically interpretable upper-limb tracking from omnidirectional vision.

title: Stable In-hand Manipulation for a Lightweight Four-motor Prosthetic Hand
authors: Yuki Kuroda, Tomoya Takahashi, Cristian C. Beltran-Hernandez, Kazutoshi Tanaka, Masashi Hamaya
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Electric prosthetic hands should be lightweight to decrease the burden on the user, shaped like human hands for cosmetic purposes, and designed with motors enclosed inside to protect them from damage and dirt. Additionally, in-hand manipulation is necessary to perform daily activities such as transitioning between different postures, particularly through rotational movements, such as reorienting a pen into a writing posture after picking it up from a desk. We previously developed PLEXUS hand (Precision–Lateral dEXteroUS manipulation hand), a lightweight (311 g) prosthetic hand driven by four motors. This prosthetic performed reorientation between precision and lateral grasps with various objects. However, its controller required predefined object widths and was limited to handling lightweight objects (of weight up to 34 g). This study addresses these limitations by employing motor current feedback. Combined with the hand’s previously optimized single-axis thumb, this approach achieves more stable manipulation by estimating the object’s width and adjusting the index finger position to maintain stable object holding during the reorientation. Experimental validation using primitive objects of various widths (5–30 mm) and shapes (cylinders and prisms) resulted in a 100% success rate with lightweight objects and maintained a high success rate (≧80%) even with heavy aluminum prisms (of weight up to 289 g). By contrast, the performance without index finger coordination dropped to just 40% on the heaviest 289 g prism. The hand also successfully executed several daily tasks, including closing bottle caps and orienting a pen for writing.

title: An Active EMI Filter for On-Board Chargers Based on a Three-Winding Common Mode Choke
authors: Shimin Lian, Wenjie Chen, Jingye Shen, Hongpeng Wang, Huibin Wu, Yongqi Huo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a current sensing current compensation (CSCC) active EMI filter (AEF) for an On-Board Charger (OBC). The proposed circuit utilizes a three-winding common-mode choke to sense the noise current while maintaining the primary side's impedance. The sensed noise current is converted into a voltage signal, which is then inversely amplified by an operational amplifier (op-amp) and injected back into the bus to compensate for common-mode EMI noise. The paper analyzes the circuit's operating principles, outlines the parameter design for the transfer function and compensation network, and validates the design through simulations and a prototype. Both simulation and experimental results demonstrate the circuit's feasibility and show that the AEF effectively reduces the overall filter size.

title: The Analytical Model of Redistribution Layers for Thermal Management in Heterogeneous Multi-chips
authors: Minjun Wu, Zhongliang Pan
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In the heterogeneous three-dimensional integrated circuits (3D ICs), thermal management is essential. Redistribution layers (RDLs) and through silicon vias (TSVs) represent a remarkable approach to addressing heat dissipation. This paper proposes a novel analytical model for redistribution layers, considering various RDL layouts and widths in heterogeneous 3D ICs. The thermal resistance network is constructed to mitigate thermal challenges, with TSVs and RDLs. Results demonstrate that the temperatures decrease by over 14K in various schemes of RDLs in the heterogeneous multi-layer model.

title: Trajectory Generation for Tip-Over Prevention and Collision Avoidance of Automatic Excavators
authors: Kenshiro Yamashita, Ryo Kikuuwe
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for generating end-effector trajectories for automatic excavators to prevent tipping over and avoid collisions with obstacles. The criterion for determining whether the excavator is at risk of tipping over is derived using the Zero-Moment Point (ZMP), which represents the center of reaction forces. A simplified model of the excavator is used in this derivation to avoid complexity. Additionally, the end-effector trajectories are automatically generated by using an algorithm based on the penalty function method, which incorporates constraints to prevent both tipping over and collisions with nearby obstacles. The automated excavator followed the generated trajectories in a simulator, and it was confirmed that tipping over was prevented and collisions with obstacles were avoided.

title: Exploratory Factor Analysis of AI Chatbot Use in Supporting University Students’ Academic Mental Health
authors: Rhiziel P. Manalese, Mark Anthony A. Castro, Laharni S. Simpao, Ivan G. Liwanag, Armand Vincent A. Sese, Robin T. Arcilla, Eden D. Garcia, John Paul P. Miranda
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The purpose of this study was to examine the role of AI chatbots in supporting the academic mental health of university students. A self-developed survey was administered to 536 students in the Philippines between April and August 2025. Data were analyzed using exploratory factor analysis. The analysis produced a three-factor solution labeled Relational Quality with AI, Academic Emotional Strain, and Perceived Usefulness and Intention. The results suggest that students’ stress experiences and the way they relate to chatbots may influence how they evaluate usefulness and adoption. The study emphasizes the need for confirmatory factor analysis and longitudinal research to determine whether this exploratory framework is stable across different settings.

title: Classroom Teacher Behavior Recognition Algorithm Based on Deep learning
authors: Hongye Fu, Wei Wei, Xin Qi, Qiyuan Wu, Keyu Chen, Xiang Ren
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In response to the current inability to accurately locate teachers in classroom environments and accurately analyze specific classroom behaviors, this paper studies the YOLO object recognition algorithm and proposes a classroom teacher behavior detection model based on the improved YOLOv10n. Firstly, this research proposes an improved EMA attention mechanism (F-EMA) to replace the MHSA attention in PSA, enabling faster data processing during forward propagation and enhancing the model's adaptability to complex scenarios. Secondly, this research proposes the FHY Loss function to replace the original loss function, improving the detection and recognition of blurry small targets. Finally, the Mamba algorithm is combined with the backbone network, and the C2f - Mamba module replaces the C2f module in the backbone network to enhance the network's feature extraction capability. After experimental testing, the average detection accuracy of the improved algorithm model reaches 82.5%, with a weight size of 5.07MB and a recognition speed of 106 frames per second. The experimental results show that this model maintains high-precision classroom teacher behavior recognition while being lightweight.

title: Editing 3D Scenes via Text Prompts without Retraining
authors: Shuangkang Fang, Yufeng Wang, Yi-Hsuan Tsai, Wenrui Ding, Yi Yang, Shuchang Zhou, Ming-Hsuan Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Numerous diffusion models have been developed for 2D image synthesis and editing, and recently they are extended to 3D scene editing tasks. However, editing 3D scenes is still in its early stages, and the challenges of scene representations and multi-view consistency need to be addressed. A notable limitation of existing approaches is the need for specific modules for different edits and model retraining for each scene. To tackle these issues, we propose a novel and versatile text-driven 3D scene editing method, termed DN2N, which allows for the direct acquisition of the editing results without the requirement for retraining. Our method employs off-the-shelf text-based editing models of 2D images to modify the multi-view images of a 3D scene. A content filtering process is then applied to discard poorly edited images that disrupt 3D consistency. We consider the remaining inconsistency as a problem of removing noise perturbations and solve it by generating data with similar perturbation characteristics for training. We develop a versatile NeRF model structure and propose two novel cross-view regularization terms to help the DN2N mitigate these perturbations. Empirical results show that our method achieves multiple editing types based solely on text prompts, including but not limited to appearance editing, weather transition, object changing, and style transfer. Most importantly, DN2N exhibits a versatility of editing capabilities, eliminating the need to customize or retrain editing models for specific scenes or editing types. Namely, DN2N achieves comparable total editing time to the 3DGS-based editing method, enhancing its practical value.

title: Design and Evaluation of A High Step-Down DC-DC Converter With Current-Doubler Rectifier and Heterogeneous Integrated Transformers
authors: Yiming Xu, Chuntao Qian, Suxin Qin, Yan Xing, Hongfei Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This work investigates a high-voltage input current-doubler rectifier (CDR) topology based on a matrix transformer to address the challenges of efficient energy conversion in high-current applications. The matrix transformer structure integrates multiple sub-transformer units to effectively manage large output current. The CDR sub-transformer modules employ a winding integration method based on current cancellation, enabling further structural integration. Such integration reduces winding length and magnetic core area, contributing to improved system efficiency and power density. Magnetic core parameter design is provided to ensure appropriate flux density and power transfer capability. The PCB winding structure is also described. Experimental results indicate that the prototype achieves a peak efficiency of 98.36% and a power density of 600 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, validating the applicability of the matrix transformer-based CDR circuit under high-voltage input and high-current output conditions.

title: A Concise Review of Suicide Surveillance and Prevention Technologies on Bridges
authors: Phisit Pornpongtechavanich, Therdpong Daengsi, Nippol Sukniyom, Korn Puangnak, Puantawat Prabgree, Pitak Uthaithum
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Suicide prevention in high-risk public spaces, especially bridges, remains a critical global challenge. This systematic review synthesizes 28 studies published between 2020 and 2025, categorized into four thematic groups: 1) national and real-time surveillance systems, 2) AI-based prediction and ethical considerations, 3) smart surveillance and anomaly detection in public spaces, and 4) social, environmental, and behavioral interventions. Findings reveal a shift from traditional statistical monitoring to intelligent, real-time, multimodal systems integrating AI, machine learning, and computer vision. While national systems provide population-level insights, AI models enhance predictive capabilities but face ethical and integration challenges. Smart surveillance demonstrates high accuracy in detecting abnormal behaviors on bridges, yet lacks linkage with healthcare data. Social and environmental strategies remain minimally integrated with digital systems. The review highlights a research gap in fully integrated, proactive suicide prevention approaches. Insights from this study contribute to the design of bridge-specific surveillance systems that enable real-time detection, predictive alerting, and evidence-based decision-making for authorities.

title: Vision-Based Grasp Section Recognition and Pose Estimation for Bent Metal Tubing
