authors: Jiaguang Li, Feng Li, Bengang Liu, Dawei Jia, Changdi Yue, Jun Luan, Zeguang Dong, Xiaofeng Han
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: In industrial automation, the grasp of metal bent tubes by robots remains inefficient. The current process relies on manual, trial-and-error pre-setting of grasp points for each tube type. This approach is particularly time-consuming and labor-intensive in multi-variety, small-batch production modes, where frequent product changeovers are required. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a vision-based robotic grasp method that utilizes the YOLOv8-pose model. Our method can automatically detect the grasp segments of the tube, locate the optimal grasp points, and simultaneously predict the required grasp angle. The model is trained based on pre-trained weights and employs various data augmentation techniques to enhance its robustness and accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively and accurately provide the position and angle information required for the robotic grasp process, contributing to reduced labor costs, improved operational efficiency, and facilitating flexible production.

title: Modeling and Diagnosis for Steel Surface Defects Based on Feature Selection of Gray Level Distribution
authors: Chenggui Li, Zhiyu Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: Surface defect diagnosis is widely used in quality control management of industrial manufacturing. Diagnostic tasks without prior knowledge often rely on models with complex structure to mine surface defect features, but the output shows a lack of reasoning process and supporting evidence. This paper proposes a surface defect diagnosis model that uses SHAP theory to interpret classification results based on gray level distribution features and provide the basis for model structure optimization. First, using sampled grayscale images of the surface as input, an overcomplete feature set of gray level distribution state is constructed. Perform defect diagnosis on this set using an Extra-Trees Classifier. SHAP values are then calculated to assess feature importance, which enabling feature selection and model simplification. Finally, in the experiment conducted on a steel surface defect dataset, the model achieved a diagnostic accuracy of 97.59% and the optimized pruning of its structure was realized on the premise of effectively maintaining the accuracy.

title: DGF-Net: A Dilated Gating Fusion Network for Brain Tumor Segmentation
authors: Qili Hu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In brain tumor segmentation, effectively fusing multi-scale features and accurately modeling contextual information are core challenges that determine segmentation precision. Existing methods, largely reliant on the U-Net architecture, employ simple skip connections to merge deep semantics with shallow details. However, this hard-wired connection easily introduces background noise from shallow encoder layers and possesses limited contextual modeling capabilities, often leading to blurred tumor boundaries. To address this, we propose the Dilated Gating Fusion Network (DGF-Net). This network follows the encoder-decoder paradigm but introduces a novel Dilated Gating Fusion Block (DGF Block) to replace traditional skip connections. The DGF Block utilizes a lightweight gating mechanism to adaptively filter the feature stream from the encoder, suppressing irrelevant information. Concurrently, it employs parallel multi-scale dilated convolutions to expand the receptive field, capturing rich contextual dependencies. Experimental results on the LGG dataset show that DGF-Net achieves an average Dice coefficient of 0.9235, outperforming several advanced methods and demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed design.

title: TGSA: An Elite Perturbation Strategy for Balanced Exploration-Exploitation
authors: Boyang Han, Shibo Dong, Haotian Li, Jiayi Li, Shangce Gao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: The gravitational search algorithm (GSA) is an effective global optimization metaheuristic that is widely recognized for its strong search capability. However, its relatively weak exploitation ability often leads to premature convergence, which has long posed a challenge for researchers. To address this issue, we propose a novel variant named TGSA. The algorithm incorporates a perturbation strategy applied to the top three elite individuals identified by the original GSA, thereby enhancing its exploitation capability. At the same time, TGSA adopts an external archive mechanism to reduce the risk of converging to local optima. The interaction between elite individuals and the external archive enables TGSA to maintain strong global exploration while improving local exploitation around promising solutions. We validate the effectiveness of TGSA on the IEEE CEC2017 benchmark suite, which consists of 29 functions. The results demonstrate that TGSA outperforms other algorithms in its ability to locate global optima.

title: Security-Aware Joint Sensing, Communication, and Computing Optimization in Low Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Jiacheng Wang, Changyuan Zhao, Jialing He, Geng Sun, Weijie Yuan, Dusit Niyato, Liehuang Zhu, Tao Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: As terrestrial resources become increasingly saturated, the developing attention is gradually shifting from the ground to the low-altitude airspace, which supports many emerging applications such as urban air taxis and aerial inspection. For these applications, low-altitude wireless networks (LAWNs) are the foundation, with integrated sensing, communications, and computing (ISCC) being one of the core parts. However, the openness of low-altitude airspace poses a serious threat to communications, degrading ISCC performance and ultimately compromising the reliability of applications supported by LAWNs. To address these challenges, this paper studies joint performance optimization of ISCC while considering security of the communications. Specifically, we derive beampattern error, secrecy rate, and age of information (AoI) as performance metrics for sensing, secure communication, and computing. Building on these metrics, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem, which aims to balance sensing and computing performance while enhancing the secrecy rate of communications. We then propose a deep Q-network (DQN)-based multi-objective evolutionary algorithm, which adaptively selects evolutionary operators according to the evolving optimization objectives, thereby leading to more effective solutions. Extensive simulations show that the proposed method brings an average performance gain of about 14% compared to existing methods, thereby ensuring ISCC performance for applications supported by LAWNs.

title: Efficient Voxel-Based mmWave Radar HAR with Early Temporal Fusion and a Compact 3D–2D Hybrid Network
authors: Rubin Zhao, Fucheng Miao, Yuanjian Liu, Yue Yin, Tomoaki Ohtsuki, Guan Gui, Fumiyuki Adachi
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar has emerged as a powerful sensing modality for human activity recognition (HAR) owing to its capability to capture 3D point cloud sequences without privacy concerns. However, effectively modeling the sparse, irregular, and non-uniform nature of radar data remains a major challenge. Existing approaches often rely on highly complex network architectures to improve accuracy, which leads to excessive computational overhead and poor scalability. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a compact hybrid feature extraction network for voxelized radar point cloud classification, which performs early-stage spatio-temporal fusion and is termed STFusionNet (Spatial-Temporal Fusion Network). The STFusionNet comprises (i) a lightweight 3D convolutional front-end, which treats consecutive temporal frames as input channels to encode motion dynamics into a compact volumetric representation and further aggregates features along the depth axis; (ii) a minimalist 2D convolutional backbone after a Depth-to-Channel Folding operation, which captures spatial features with minimal computational cost. Extensive experiments on the public MMActivity and MiliPoint datasets demonstrate that our model achieves competitive accuracy (e.g., 92.20% on MMActivity and 72.86% on MiliPoint) with only about 50K parameters and 46 MMac, outperforming or matching representative spatio-temporal baselines under a much lower computational budget. Extensive ablation experiments verify the contribution of key components, while statistical significance tests confirm the reliability of the performance improvements. These results confirm that STFusionNet offers a robust, efficient, and generalizable solution for mmWave radar-based HAR in Internet of Things (IoT) applications.

title: Computational Thinking in Rural Education: Evidence of Gains in Problem-Solving Abilities
authors: Elkin Martínez-Caro, Miguel Garcés-Prettel
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: Most research on computational thinking has been conducted in urban or technologically advantaged settings, leaving its applicability in rural environments largely unexplored. This study addresses that gap by evaluating the effectiveness of a computational thinking-based pedagogical strategy on problem-solving skills among tenth-grade students in a rural Colombian school. Using a quasi-experimental design with control group and pre/post-test measurements, three dimensions were assessed: computational concepts, required tasks, and evaluative capacities. The intervention, consisting of six constructivist-oriented sessions mediated through the MakeCode environment, led to significant improvements in the experimental group. A repeated measures analysis of variance confirmed substantial effects across all dimensions, with no significant influence from sociodemographic or academic variables. These findings indicate that complex problem-solving skills can be developed through accessible pedagogical strategies within rural school contexts characterized by limited technological infrastructure. The study provides empirical evidence supporting the integration of computational thinking in rural education through replicable and context-sensitive approaches.

title: Surrogate-Assisted Evolutionary Multi-Objective Optimization Grouped by Significant Influence for High-Dimensional Problems
authors: Weichao Chen, Ziyang Li, Yu Xue, Yonglin Pu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithms (SAEAs) have demonstrated strong performance in solving low- and medium-dimensional expensive multi-objective optimization problems (EMOPs). However, their scalability to high-dimensional problems remains a critical challenge. To address this issue, several SAEAs based on variable grouping have been proposed. Yet they often fail to deliver satisfactory results under limited real function evaluations. In this paper, we propose a SAEA grouped by significant influence (SAEA-SI), in which variable grouping is adaptively adjusted according to the optimization state. The method is motivated by the observation that different decision variables often contribute unequally to different objectives. SAEA-SI adopts a two-phase optimization framework, where the first phase focuses on convergence, while the second phase enhances diversity through adaptive variable grouping. Specifically, before each optimization process, correlation analysis in the objective space is performed to identify solution combinations for each objective. These combinations are used to estimate the influence of each decision variable, and decision variables exhibiting consistent and significant influence are grouped accordingly. Each group is then optimized independently using surrogate-assisted Differential Evolution. We conduct comparative experiments with the five state-of-the-art SAEAs on multiple test suites with up to 1000 decision variables. The results demonstrate that SAEA-SI is able to strike a good balance between convergence and diversity under limited real function evaluations. Furthermore, experiments on real-world test problems confirm the effectiveness of SAEA-SI in high-dimensional EMOPs.

title: IEEE Circuits and Systems Society Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs

title: Prototype Perturbation for Relaxing Alignment Constraints in Backward-Compatible Learning
authors: Zikun Zhou, Yushuai Sun, Wenjie Pei, Xin Li, Yaowei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: The traditional paradigm to update retrieval models requires re-computing the embeddings of the gallery data, a time-consuming and computationally intensive process known as backfilling. To circumvent backfilling, Backward-Compatible Learning (BCL) has been widely explored, which aims to train a new model compatible with the old one. Many previous works focus on effectively aligning the embeddings of the new model with those of the old one to enhance backward compatibility. Nevertheless, such strong alignment constraints would compromise the discriminative ability of the new model, particularly when different classes are closely clustered and hard to distinguish in the old feature space. To address this issue, we propose to relax the constraints by introducing perturbations to the old feature prototypes. This allows us to align the new feature space with a pseudo-old feature space defined by these perturbed prototypes, thereby preserving the discriminative ability of the new model in backward-compatible learning. We have developed two approaches for calculating the perturbations: Neighbor-Driven Prototype Perturbation (NDPP) and Optimization-Driven Prototype Perturbation (ODPP). Particularly, they take into account the feature distributions of not only the old but also the new models to obtain proper perturbations along with new model updating. Extensive experiments on the landmark and commodity datasets demonstrate that our approaches perform favorably against state-of-the-art BCL algorithms.

title: Multi-Contrastive and Dynamic Topological Matching Network for Cross-Scene Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Yujie Ning, Na Chen, Jiangtao Peng, Weiwei Sun, Zhijing Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Due to the complex acquisition environment and scarcity of labels, domain adaptation (DA) techniques are widely applied to cross-scenario hyperspectral image (HSI) classification to achieve more precise labeling. Many existing approaches mainly rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to capture local spatial contextual relationships, supplemented by graph convolutional networks (GCNs) for long-range modeling. However, GCNs usually require full batch training and fixed initial graph structures, which significantly limits the exploration of topological structures. To address this, a multi-contrastive and dynamic topological matching network (MCDTM) is introduced to accomplish cross-domain HSI classification. Unlike fixed graph construction methods, mini-batches of samples are utilized to construct dynamic subgraphs within the source and target domains, respectively, with locally extracted features from CNNs serving as the basis for graph construction. More importantly, as the model is optimized and the domain gap narrows, the dynamic graph structure is adaptively adjusted according to the evolving samples, thereby boosting the accuracy of the graph and enhancing the discriminative power of the model. Moreover, the integration of weighted multi-positive contrastive learning and graph matching achieves distribution alignment and graph alignment, enhancing the model's capacity to distinguish and align complex patterns in HSI. Experimental results in three tasks show that the MCDTM surpasses several advanced DA methods, achieving impressive accuracies of 80.10%, 70.01%, and 94.17% on the Houston, HyRank, and YC-YC tasks, thereby showcasing its superior performance.

title: Edge-Aided Radar-Based Exercise Form Classification Using Lightweight Ensemble Learning for Personalized Healthcare
authors: Jose Maria Sosa Gomez, Sajid Ahmed, Slim Souissi, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Real-time, non-contact monitoring of exercise form is important for personalized rehabilitation, yet cameras and wearables raise privacy and robustness concerns. We present RehabRadar, an edge-aided system using a TI IWR1642 mmWave FMCW radar that trains personalized models to classify execution as correct or one of two common error types. The signal pipeline isolates the subject via range-gating and FIR high-pass filtering, then extracts complementary micro-Doppler spectrograms and range-Doppler sequences. A hybrid ensemble—MobileNetV3 for micro-Doppler images and a CNN–LSTM for range-Doppler sequences—performs parallel inference with late fusion. The models are optimized for a Raspberry Pi 4B via magnitude-based pruning and post-training INT8 quantization. We evaluated the system in a proof-of-concept study with 10 healthy adults performing eight clinician-curated exercises; per-participant two-fold cross-validation yields 91.5% accuracy and 92.0% macro-F1, outperforming single-branch baselines. On-device end-to-end latency averages 1.5 s, enabling near real-time, rep-level feedback while preserving data locally. The final model footprint is 2.1 MB, and all processing occurs on-device; raw radar is discarded after feedback, and only minimal encrypted metrics are transmitted externally. This work demonstrates the feasibility of personalized on-device error detection under edge constraints. Establishing clinical efficacy and generalizability beyond the studied cohort, broader exercise taxonomies, and patient populations is outside the present scope and motivates planned multi-site clinical validation. These results indicate that the mmWave radar, combined with compact neural models, can balance accuracy, latency, and footprint for private, contactless home rehabilitation analytics, and advance decentralized learning in IoT healthcare.

title: Privacy-Preserving Continuous Sign Language Recognition
authors: Wanli Xue, Bitao Tan, Leming Guo, Tiantian Yuan, Yunpeng Li, Jia Guo, Shengyong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Responsible continuous sign language recognition (CSLR) needs to consider the privacy concerns of hearing impaired people, which are ignored by CSLR methods. And simple privacy-preserving methods, such as face blurring, re placement, and pruning low-frequency components, degrade recognition performance. In this study, we propose PrivSign, the first privacy-preserving framework for CSLR. PrivSign transforms the RGB video sequence into the frequency domain and prunes low- to partial high-frequency components. The generated high-frequency videos are invisible to the human eye and conceal biometric information effectively. To solve the dilemma between concealing biological information and maintaining recognition performance, PrivSign conducts the Semantic Rectification. Specifically, the RGB-Frequency pre-training between RGB and high-frequency video enables the CSLR model to learn comprehensive sign semantics. Furthermore, the fine-tuning process combined with learnable Gabor filters is performed on high-frequency videos to mitigate noise and emphasize the significance of sign-relevant semantics. Recognition performance and black and white box attack results demonstrate that PrivSign achieves a balance among concealing biological information, impeding recovery, and recognition performance.

title: Grounding is All You Need? Dual Temporal Grounding for Video Dialog
authors: You Qin, Wei Ji, Xinze Lan, Hao Fei, Xun Yang, Dan Guo, Roger Zimmermann, Lizi Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: In the realm of video dialog response generation, capturing both the essence of video content and the temporal nuances of conversation history is crucial. While some approaches rely on large-scale pretrained visual-language models, often neglecting temporal dynamics, others emphasize spatial-temporal relationships within videos but demand intricate object trajectory pre-extractions and overlook dialog temporal dynamics. This paper introduces the Dual Temporal Grounding-enhanced Video Dialog model (DTGVD), designed to bridge the gap between these two approaches. DTGVD uniquely integrates the strengths of both by emphasizing dual temporal relationships. It achieves this by predicting dialog turn-specific temporal regions, selectively filtering video content, and grounding responses in both video and dialog contexts.A key innovation of DTGVD is its advanced handling of chronological interplay within dialogs. By effectively capturing and leveraging dependencies between dialog turns, it enables a more nuanced understanding of conversational dynamics. To further align video and dialog temporal dynamics, we introduce a list-wise contrastive learning strategy. In this framework, accurately grounded turn-clip pairings are treated as positive samples, while less precise pairings serve as negative samples. This refined classification is then seamlessly integrated into our end-to-end response generation mechanism.Evaluations using AVSD@DSTC-7 and AVSD@DSTC-8 datasets underscore the superiority of our methodology.

title: Quadtree-Based Optimized MLS Protocol with Inactive-User Isolation
authors: Yiyang Guo, Kerun Wang, Lifan Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: For long-term asynchronous group instant messaging, the Messaging Layer Security (MLS) protocol is required to provide both forward secrecy and post-compromise security over an untrusted server. However, when post-quantum cryptographic suites are adopted, the TreeKEM core based on a perfect binary tree cannot achieve optimal communication and computation costs, and only supports a simple eviction strategy for long-term offline inactive users, which introduces both performance and security concerns. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an optimized MLS scheme based on a quadtree structure with support for inactive-user isolation. By restructuring the TreeKEM tree and introducing a threshold secret-sharing–based isolation mechanism, the scheme jointly reduces communication and computation overhead while preserving the original security guarantees and enhancing the overall group security. Our experimental results further demonstrate that the bottom-expanded quadtree TreeKEM achieves lower overall cost than its binary and ternary counterparts in post-quantum settings, and can significantly improve the performance of MLS while strengthening group security.

title: IEEE Guide for Databases Used in Utility Automation Systems
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: The contents of this guide presents database characteristics to be considered by protection and automation engineers in discussions with the information technology specialists on desired database requirements to meet the needs of the power system applications.

title: A Survey of Large Language Models for Traffic Forecasting: Methods and Applications
authors: Qingqing Long, Shuai Liu, Ning Cao, Zhicheng Ren, Xiao Luo, Wei Ju, Chen Fang, Zhihong Zhu, Hengshu Zhu, Yuanchun Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved prominent success in various applications, driven by their foundational capabilities and strong generalization potential. While their impact on natural language processing is well-established, recent works highlight their significant promise in other applications as well. One such area is traffic forecasting, where LLMs demonstrated the ability to generate powerful analytical insights, offering new opportunities for advancing Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). This survey summarizes 160 related works, providing a comprehensive review of methods, applications, and challenges. Specifically, we summarize the efforts to bridge the gap between time series data and language models, exploring their design principles and applications in various traffic scenarios, including traffic forecasting, traffic recommendation, mobility forecasting, urban management, signal control, and safety analysis. For deeper insights, we discuss existing challenges and highlight future research directions. This survey identifies three key limitations of current works: (1) LLM-based traffic models face practical deployment hurdles; (2) there lacks a unified benchmark across various applications; (3) resident privacy protection demands attention in their real-world applications. By providing a foundational understanding of LLMs for traffic forecasting, this survey aims to benefit not only the traffic mining community but also contribute to the broader advancement of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI).

title: Comments on “Optimal dual frames of probabilistic erasures"
authors: Shankhadeep Mondal, Ram Narayan Mohapatra
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In [1], the authors claim uniqueness of the canonical dual as the 1−erasure probabilistic optimal dual under certain conditions.We provide counterexamples and showed that these conditions are insufficient and we correct the main theorem with less assumptions. We also refute a related corollary for tight frames and offer a refined characterization of optimal duals.

title:
Identifying Bottlenecks in Student Learning using Eye Tracking Measurements in Lathe Operation Training
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: M. Kimizuka, Y. Ito, H. Saito, D. Chugo, H.Hashimoto
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper aims to identify bottlenecks in learning lathe operation and find ways to reduce the workload of the identification, in order to analyze effective and efficient learning processes for lathe operation training for beginner students. This paper considers bottlenecks suitable for practical training and proposes a method for measuring bottlenecks using an eye tracker to reduce the workload required for identification. Based on this method, bottlenecks during student training are identified and discussed.

title: ANature-InspiredOptimizationFrameworkfor EnhancedIntrusionDetectionUsingMultiple Machine Learning Models
authors: Rudra Koteswaramma, Rudra Koteswaramma, Mohammed AliShaik
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Protectingcontemporarynetworksagainstmore complexcyberattacksrequirestheuseofintrusiondetectionsystems,or IDS. Conventional detection methods frequentlyhave poorfeatureselection,largefalsepositiverates,andlittle flexibilityinrespondingtochangingthreats.Thehybridintrusion detectionframeworkproposedinthispapercombinesseveralma-chine learning classifiers, such as Logistic Regression, k-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Ada Boost, Gradient Boosting,SupportVectorMachine,Multi- LayerPerceptron,andXGBoost,withanoveloptimizationalgorithmi nspiredbynature.Theoptimizerreducescomputingcostandimproves classificationperformancebyconcurrentlyperformingfeature selection and hyperparameter adjustment. In order toassess de- tection accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC, extensive experiments were carried out on two benchmark datasets, NSLKDD and CIC-IDS2017.The outcomesshow that alloptimized modelsperformsignificantlybetterthantheirnon-optimized counterparts. With near-perfect AUC scores of 0.997 and 0.999, respectively,andthehighestaccuracyof98.7%onNSLKDDand 99.2%onCIC-IDS2017,XGBoostexhibitsthebestperformance. Compared to related research, the proposed framework shows significant generalizability across datasets, scalability for real- time deployment, and superior detection capability, making it a dependable and efficient solution for modern intrusion detection applications.

title: Relative Geometrical Constraint on Finger Motion for Dexterous Teleoperation of Multifingered Hand
authors: Yohei Kitahara, Manoj Bhadu
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents a method that enables intuitive and stable in-hand manipulation in teleoperation of a multifingered robotic hand. Our method comprehensively handles the fundamental components of in-hand manipulation, such as object pose control, finger sliding on an object, and finger gaiting. This is achieved by constraining the operator’s finger commands relative to the intended object motion, preventing their penetration into the object. Moreover, this method does not rely on any visual-based sensors and instead utilizes the six-axis force-torque sensors at the fingertips. The proposed method was evaluated with hardware through in-hand manipulation of a screwdriver, and the intuitive manipulation with high tracking performance of the operator’s finger motion was demonstrated. Additionally, we present imitation learning results using the data collected by the proposed method. Policy learned with constrained finger motion as expert action maintained the performance even without the constraint during policy execution. This shows that the dataset is scalable as it is usable even without implementing the proposed constraint.

title: Image Resolution Enhancement Using Time-of-Flight-Based Backscatter Rejection in Compton Camera
authors: Yosuke Kobayashi, Koki Nakamura, Agus Nur Rachman, Tadashi Orita, Mizuki Uenomachi, Hideki Tomita, Yusuke Tamura, Kei Kamada, Fumihiko Ishida, Eiji Takada, Jun Kawarabayashi, Kosuke Tanabe, Ken'Ichi Tsuchiya, Hanwool Woo, Kenji Shimazoe
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study presents a technique to enhance Compton camera imaging used for radioactive source localization in security applications. A key issue in Compton imaging is "backscattering events," where gamma-ray interactions are misordered—such as when the absorber records a hit before the scatterer—leading to incorrect Compton cone reconstruction and image artifacts. To address this, the research applies the Time-of-Flight (TOF) principle to determine the correct interaction sequence.A detector system comprising GFAG scintillators, SiPMs, and a custom ASIC/DAQ setup achieves a 296 ps coincidence time resolution, sufficient to resolve 4.4 cm-scale interaction order. TOF values are calculated per event after correcting for systematic effects, allowing identification and rejection of backscattering events. Applying this TOF-based filtering significantly improves the signal-to-noise ratio. The resulting data, processed with the MLEM algorithm, yields an angular resolution (ARM) of 13.3°–16.5° (FWHM), aligning well with Geant4 simulations. This demonstrates TOF filtering as an effective method for enhancing Compton imaging quality.

title: Voltage Loop Controller Design for Grid-Forming Current Source Inverter Considering Low-Frequency Range Differentiator Characteristics of Control Plant
authors: Yubin Zheng, Xiao Liu, Xiong Liu, Hongyuan Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Current source inverters (CSIs) are attractive in energy conversion and energy storage applications due to the voltage boosting capability. Grid-forming (GFM) inverters can provide system inertia and voltage support. However, research on GFM-CSIs remain insufficiently explored. Moreover, GFM-CSIs are integrated into the grid through CL filters, which result in CL resonance. This article reveals the differentiator characteristics of the voltage control plant of GFM-CSI in the low-frequency domain, which leads to a poor dynamic performance in the voltage loop. The capacitor voltage feedback is adopted to suppress the CL resonance. To enhance the dynamic performance of the voltage loop, a resonant (R) controller is employed. Through detailed analysis, the controller parameters are designed. Finally, experimental results validate the analysis and effectiveness of the proposed controller.

title: Finite Element Simulation Based Thermal Field Analysis of Power Modules Combine with Heatsink Thermal Network Models
authors: Jianghua Zhuo, Jingnan Xie, Ximing Zhang, Yangjun Zheng, Huan Yang, Haoze Luo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Accurate temperature prediction of critical power module components, including semiconductor chips, bonding wires, and solder layers, is essential for their development and reliable application. Conventional finite element method (FEM) simulations introduce substantial inaccuracies by modeling heatsink thermal boundaries through oversimplified assumptions, such as constant temperature or uniform heat transfer coefficients. To overcome this limitation, this study integrates a power module FEM model with a dedicated heatsink thermal network model. The FEM model is developed in COMSOL Multiphysics and the thermal network in Simulink, with bidirectional coupling achieved via the COMSOL Multiphysics with Simulink interface. Experimental validation confirms that the proposed method achieves a good balance between computational efficiency and prediction accuracy.

title: Fast and Realistic Automated Scenario Simulations and Reporting for an Autonomous Racing Stack
authors: Giovanni Lambertini, Matteo Pini, Eugenio Mascaro, Francesco Moretti, Ayoub Raji, Marko Bertogna
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In this paper, we describe the automated simulation and reporting pipeline implemented for our autonomous racing stack, ur.autopilot. The backbone of the simulation is based on a high-fidelity model of the vehicle interfaced as a Functional Mockup Unit (FMU). The pipeline can execute the software stack and the simulation up to three times faster than real-time, locally or on GitHub for Continuous Integration/-Continuous Delivery (CI/CD). As the most important input of the pipeline, there is a set of running scenarios. Each scenario allows the initialization of the ego vehicle in different initial conditions (position and speed), as well as the initialization of any other configuration of the stack. This functionality is essential to validate efficiently critical modules, like the one responsible for high-speed overtaking maneuvers or localization, which are among the most challenging aspects of autonomous racing. Moreover, we describe how we implemented a fault injection module, capable of introducing sensor delays and perturbations as well as modifying outputs of any node of the stack. Finally, we describe the design of our automated reporting process, aimed at maximizing the effectiveness of the simulation analysis.

title: From Feedback to Insights: Analyzing User Reviews of BRPL Power Utility App
authors: Syed Abdullah Ashraf, Syed Mohammad Ashraf, Syed Ahmad Abdullah
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Mobile applications have become essential interfaces between consumers and service providers, and the energy sector is rapidly adopting these digital platforms. User reviews in app stores provide valuable insights into consumer experiences, yet they remain underutilized in the context of power utilities. This study examines user feedback on the BSES Rajdhani Power Limited (BRPL) app, one of Delhi's largest electricity distribution companies. By analyzing thousands of reviews from Android and iOS platforms, we identify dominant themes in user perceptions, ranging from frustrations with login and billing to appreciation of convenience features. The findings reveal highly polarized sentiment, with strong clusters of both praise and criticism, highlighting the opportunities and challenges of digital service delivery in the utility sector. To our knowledge, this is the first systematic analysis of user reviews for an Indian power app, offering insights that can guide service design, customer engagement, and the digital transformation of electricity distribution.

title: Multi-Disease Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Om Sahu, Arya Singh, Deo Prakash
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Early and correct diagnosis is still a major difficulty in today’s healthcare system, especially in impoverished and distant areas with limited access to professional medical staff and testing facilities. worse treatment expenses and worse death rates are frequently the results of delayed diagnosis. Using cutting-edge Deep Learning (DL) and Machine Learning (ML) architectures combined with edge computing for real-time deployment, this study provides a reliable, scalable, and multi-modal disease detection system. The suggested system can identify more than 50 diseases by using ensemble learning techniques for structured clinical data and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image-based diagnosis. These include infectious diseases like sickle cell anemia and malaria, complicated pathologies like brain tumors and different types of cancer, and chronic problems like diabetes and cardiovascular disorders.Offline inference in low-bandwidth contexts is made possible by the system’s deployment optimization on the NVIDIA Jetson Nano. With diabetes prediction at 94%, obesity classification at 99%, brain tumor detection at 96%, and chronic kidney disease prediction at 99%, experimental results show remarkable predictive accuracy across different models. This approach offers an affordable answer to global health issues by bridging the gap between cutting-edge AI capabilities and easily accessible healthcare infrastructure.

title: Effect of Vertical Force Sensation on Fingertips by Grasping a Balloon on Muscle Activity
authors: Yosei Kanai, Hayato Mikami, Tianyi Wang, Koji Shimatani, Keisuke Shima
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Postural sway and muscle co–contraction are known to increase during upright standing with aging. A previous study demonstrated that grasping a helium–filled balloon stabilized postural. In this study, we measured the center of pressure (COP) and electromyography (EMG) of the trunk and ankle muscles in 17 healthy young adults while they grasped a balloon during standing and analyzed sway and muscle activity characteristics. The results indicated that the sway–reducing effect was observed not only with upward force sensation but also with downward force sensation. These findings suggest that sensory feedback processed at the spinal cord level primarily modulates trunk muscle activity, enhancing postural control and thereby improving stability.

title: Time-Coding Metasurface for SAR Deceptive Jamming of Three-Dimensional False Targets
authors: Jiacheng Song, Shao Nan Chen, Hui Dong Li, Jun Yan Da, Qiang Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: This paper proposes a novel deceptive jamming method for miniaturized synthetic aperture radar (SAR) using time-coding metasurface. A reflection coefficient model with joint fast-time and slow-time modulation enables precise control of false targets in both range and azimuth, which ensures high-fidelity jamming while significantly reducing system complexity and cost.

title: BERTopic Analysis of Autism-Related Twitter Conversations: Insights into Public Discourse on ASD
authors: Soofi Shafiya, Suraiya Jabin
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Understanding public discourse around Neurodevelopmental Disorders, such as Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), is critical for designing effective interventions and support systems. However, large-scale, temporal analyses of how these conversations evolve, especially during global crises like the COVID-19 pandemic, remain limited. This study addresses this gap by analyzing over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,014,723$</tex> publicly available tweets from 2017 to 2022, distinguishing between individuals with ASD and their caregivers, and dividing the dataset into pre- and post-COVID periods. We employed BERTopic, a transformer-based topic modeling approach, to extract dominant themes and compare discussion patterns across four groups: ASD pre-COVID, ASD post-COVID, Non-ASD pre-COVID, and Non-ASD post-COVID. Our analysis reveals significant shifts in discourse following the pandemic: individuals with ASD increasingly discussed mental health challenges and social isolation, while caregivers focused on parenting concerns, educational disruptions, and family stress. These findings highlight distinct emotional tones and priorities between the two groups and demonstrate the potential of social media analysis to capture real-time community concerns, providing actionable insights for targeted interventions during public health crises.

title: A Broadband Transparent Circularly Polarized Antenna for GNSS Applications
authors: Keying Huang, Zhipeng Zhang, Wenhai Zhang, Xueguan Liu, Chi Hou Chio, Kam-Weng Tam, Zhixing Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: This paper presents a broadband transparent circularly polarized (CP) coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed antenna for Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) applications, which is printed on the single-layer glass substrate. An asymmetric ground plane with a slot is etched on the substrate, a L-shaped and U-shaped structure along with four pairs of parallel coupled stubs at each corner are successively introduced to enhance the bandwidth. To prove the concept, the transparent antenna is fabricated with indium-doped tin oxide (ITO) process. The results show that the antenna achieves −10 dB impedance bandwidth of 62.07% (1–1.9 GHz) and a 3 dB axial ratio (AR) bandwidth of 48.65% (1.12–1.84 GHz).

title: Real-Time Detection of DNS Spoofing Attacks using LSTM-Based Neural Networks
authors: Kalyan Sudarsan, AmrithKrishna Vignesh, Rm Bhavadharini
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: DNS spoofing is a critical network security threat wherein attackers forge DNS responses to redirect users to malicious destinations. This study discusses a machine learning method for identifying spoofed DNS response packets utilizing a Sequential Neural Network. The model utilizes characteristics like latency, source and destination MAC and IP addresses., Time to Live (TTL), and DNS query/response content to classify packets as either spoofed (1) or legitimate (0). The neural network architecture includes two hidden layers with 128 and 64 neurons, incorporating batch normalization and dropout for improved generalization. Training was conducted on a dataset of approximately 17,186 packets, split 75:25 for training and testing, achieving an accuracy of 93%. Data was collected by intercepting DNS traffic between a target system and its router, with a subset of packets manually labelled as spoofed. Packets lacking sufficient information or with incomplete DNS transactions were excluded. The findings indicate that the suggested model is capable of effectively identify spoofed DNS responses, with potential for improved performance through enhanced data collection and feature engineering.

title: Secure and Intelligent Healthcare: Evaluating Blockchain with AI for Chronic Disease Prediction
authors: Dhirendra Kumar Shukla, Arvind Dagur, Shabir Ali, Sambit Satpathy, Akhilesh Kosta
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Chronic diseases require long-term monitoring and accurate prediction to support timely clinical decisions. AIenabled wearable devices have emerged as powerful tools for generating predictive insights, but concerns related to data security, privacy, and interoperability limit their adoption. Blockchain technology, with its decentralized, immutable, and transparent architecture, has the potential to address these challenges, yet most existing frameworks remain conceptual without prototype deployment or empirical validation. This paper compares traditional AI models SVM, ANN, and Decision Tree-with a proposed blockchain-enabled AI framework that integrates predictive modelling with Hyperledger Fabric for secure data management. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves 95.4% accuracy and an F1-score of 0.95, outperforming baseline models while ensuring tamper-proof storage and rolebased access control. Although inference time increases slightly due to blockchain validation, the trade-off enhances trust, compliance readiness, and real-world applicability. These findings establish the superiority of the proposed framework for secure and intelligent chronic disease management.

title: A Systematic Design Methodology of Time-Based Non-Uniform In-Pixel Quantizers for IR Sensors
authors: Rico Jossel Maestro, Patrick Merken, Filip Tavernier
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Trends in image sensor design increasingly demand high resolution, small pixel pitch, low power consumption, and high dynamic range. Digital pixel sensors, in which each pixel directly produces a digital output, are an emerging technology capable of meeting these requirements, with the potential to incorporate in-array image post-processing. This paper presents an implementation for infrared readout circuits based on pulse-frequency modulation. A non-uniform time-based second conversion stage featuring an input-dependent resolution selection enhances dynamic range without increasing pixel area. The effects of key design variables are analyzed, and design guidelines are provided for optimizing system performance. A prototype chip with pixel-parallel conversion in a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10~{\mu }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>m pixel pitch is developed following these guidelines, and mathematical simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed methodology. Finally, even if this work focuses on infrared readout, the design guidelines provided in this work applies and can be extended to any current-sensing system.

title: Generative Diffusion Contrastive Network for Multi-View Clustering
authors: Jian Zhu, Xin Zou, Xi Wang, Lei Liu, Chang Tang, Li-Rong Dai
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: In recent years, Multi-View Clustering (MVC) has been significantly advanced under the influence of deep learning. By integrating heterogeneous data from multiple views, MVC enhances clustering analysis, making multi-view fusion critical to clustering performance. However, multi-view fusion remains challenged by low-quality data, primarily stemming from two reasons: 1) Certain views are contaminated by noisy data. 2) Some views suffer from missing data. This paper proposes a novel Stochastic Generative Diffusion Fusion (SGDF) method to address this problem. SGDF leverages a multiple generative mechanism for the multi-view feature of each sample. It exhibits robustness against low-quality data. Building on SGDF, we further present the Generative Diffusion Contrastive Network (GDCN). Extensive experiments show that GDCN achieves the state-of-the-art results in deep MVC tasks. The source code is publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/HackerHyper/GDCN</uri>.

title: DHMCNN: Deep Hybrid Morphological CNN with Learnable White Top-Hat Pooling for Infrared UAV Classification
authors: Yifan Bu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This paper proposes a lightweight Deep Hybrid Morphological and Convolutional Neural Network (DHMCNN) tailored for IR small-target UAV classification. Building upon the morphological–convolutional DMCNN architecture, this research introduces a learnable Top-hat Morphological Pooling Layer that replaces conventional morphological pooling. The proposed white Top-hat operator is implemented in a differentiable form with trainable structuring elements, enabling structure-sensitive features that emphasize local high-intensity targets while suppressing complex backgrounds. Extensive experiments on the HALMSTAD multi-sensor UAV dataset, demonstrate that the 1-layer white Top-hat DHMCNN achieves 97.9% accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.979 on the CLOSE split, outperforming both the 4-layer DMCNN (95.67% accuracy) and YOLOv2/YOLO11 baselines. Across all distance groups, DHMCNN consistently improves precision and recall, while maintaining a theoretical inference speed of 7,000–12,000 FPS, making it suitable for embedded real-time deployment. These results highlight the effectiveness of integrating learnable Top-hat morphology with CNNs for IR UAV defence applications.

title: Exploring Presence Patterns in an Educational Setting through Electromagnetic Activity
authors: Ruben Nietvelt, Dennis Joosens, Maarten Weyn, Rafael Berkvens
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Congestion in parts of the radio spectrum presents an opportunity to explore presence patterns, potentially providing valuable insights into smart living environments. However, the relationship between human presence and Electromagnetic (EM) activity remains poorly understood. Our research focuses on an innovative approach to estimate presence patterns using EM activity within an educational setting. We concentrate specifically on the frequency bands corresponding toWi-Fi channels 1 and 11. Experiments were conducted in a computer classroom with student participation, accompanied by detailed ground-truth manual counting to validate the sensing methods. As a key contribution, we extend prior studies conducted in large, open environments to a confined computer classroom. This enables analysis under different environmental and propagation conditions.We further compare EM activity-based presence estimation with two commonly used alternatives, namely an attenuation-based device-free sensing method andWi-Fi Media Access Control (MAC) address counting. The analysis revealed significant, weak-to-moderate, positive correlations between EM activity in the Wi-Fi channels and an attenuation-based device-free crowd-size sensing method, while correlations with the number of detected Wi-Fi MAC addresses were relatively weaker. Comparisons with the ground-truth manual counts confirm these observations, indicating that EM activity reflects occupancy dynamics but with reduced sensitivity compared to the attenuation-based approach. These findings indicate that EM activity provides complementary information to existing presence-detection approaches and may be used as a supplementary indicator of occupancy dynamics in smart environments. The potential implications of this work extend beyond the experiment performed, offering valuable insights for applications in crowd behaviour and occupancy analysis. However, further refinement of the sensing methods is necessary to improve accuracy and real-time data processing.

title: SimDEM: Audio-driven Emotional Talking Head Generation with Simplified and Decoupled Expression Modeling
authors: Jianzhi Long, Rong-Cheng Tu, Dacheng Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Emotional talking head generation has advanced significantly because of its potential to enhance naturalness and expressiveness in applications like gaming, virtual reality, and video conferencing. However, existing methods struggle with overcomplicated facial expression modeling, which introduces redundancies, reduces learning efficiency, and limits emotion accuracy. To address these limitations, we propose SimDEM, a novel framework for emotional talking head generation that leverages simplified and decoupled expression modeling. By retaining only critical dimensions of the expression representation via key expression modeling, we reduce noise in training and improve emotion accuracy. Furthermore, the decoupling of facial regions into independent dynamics for the upper and lower face enables more effective learning of localized emotional expressions, enhancing the visual fidelity of the output. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that SimDEM outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving superior emotion accuracy and visual quality in synthesizing expressive talking head videos.

title: Unsupervised Spectral-Geometric Fusion for Single Building Stereo Segmentation for High-Resolution Satellite Images
authors: Shuoya Tian, Li Zhao, Qiang Zhi, Bingqian Zhou
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Building segmentation is an important part of urban scene 3D reconstruction and smart city applications. However, current approaches frequently struggle to precisely define boundary details due to the complexity of urban environments and the morphological diversity of buildings, which lowers segmentation precision. Therefore, this paper proposes an unsupervised stereo segmentation method integrating spectral and geometric features. By adding the three-dimensional structure information represented by disparity maps, the proposed method enhances the accuracy and completeness of individual building boundaries. The method first performs initial pre-segmentation within a joint spectral-geometric feature space. Subsequently, to further preserve building integrity, spectral and geometric information are incorporated into region fusion during belief propagation. Finally, geometric information is re-utilised for edge refinement to obtain the segmentation result. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms mainstream algorithms in both region and boundary localization. On the WHU-Stereo dataset, the BR score reaches 0.825, representing a 9.0% improvement over the state-of-the-art method. On the ISPRS Vaihingen dataset, the BR score reaches 0.712, yielding a 7.9% improvement compared with the state-of-the-art method.

title: An Iterative Electric-Magnetic-Thermal Bidirectional Coupled Method for Multi-Chip SiC Power Modules Considering Current Sharing
authors: Gaojia Zhu, Rui Zhang, Bingru Li, Youzheng Wang, Longnv Li, Yun-Hui Mei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the ongoing increase in power densities, thermal design has become a critical issue in developing SiC power modules, necessitating higher accuracy requirements for their thermal simulation techniques. However, thermal characteristic analysis of SiC power modules is not a straightforward issue, particularly from constant heat sources and fixed material parameters, especially in the case of multichip paralleled modules. Indeed, during operations, differences in switching and conduction losses between paralleled chips, along with the differences in their thermal coupling conditions, can cause temperature variations among those chips. In turn, these temperature differences of the chips will affect current sharing and both the switching and conducting losses of the paralleled chips. To achieve precise thermal predictions of multi-chip paralleled power modules, this paper proposes an iterative electric-magnetic-thermal bidirectional coupled method. Take the N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-step iteration as an example, first, based on the temperature calculation results of the (N-1)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-step (or an initial assumed temperature value when N=1), the temperature impacts on the chips’ characteristics, such as the on-state resistances (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dson</inf>), threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>), gate-source capacitance (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gs</inf>), transconductance (g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>), etc., are investigated. Secondly, the steady-state current field and the dynamic waveforms of the module, considering differences between paralleled chips, are simulated for the steady-state and dynamic current sharing characteristics. The corresponding conduction loss (p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">cond</inf>) and switching losses (p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sw</inf>) for each chip are investigated. Then, the module’s temperature distribution in the N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-step is calculated with the simulated p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">cond</inf> and p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sw</inf> of the chips. Suppose the temperature difference between the junction temperatures from the (N-1)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-step and N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-step iterations is less than a preset value (5% in this paper). In that case, the calculation is considered converged, and the coupled results are output. Otherwise, the (N+1)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup>-step iteration starts. As the bidirectional coupled method in this paper considers the interrelation between the temperatures of paralleled chips and their parameters, as well as dynamic and static current sharing, in the module’s temperature simulations, it can adapt temperature distributions to the losses, therefore ensuring accuracy.

title: Efficient VVC Intra Partitioning With Shared Feature Extraction and Complexity-Aware Threshold Decision
authors: Tianyi Sun, Yanze Wang, Jun Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Versatile Video Coding (VVC) achieves significant compression efficiency by adopting various techniques, especially the Quad-Tree Plus Multi-Type Tree (QTMT) partition structure, but this leads to a dramatic rise in encoding complexity. Some accelerating methods have relatively low accuracy in predicting partition modes, while others increase accuracy at the cost of larger network inference time. When setting thresholds for skipping partition modes, it is common to determine them empirically or based on prediction accuracy, often neglecting their impact on encoding complexity. To achieve a balance in accuracy and efficiency, we propose to only extract the feature map of each 32×32 CU once and share it among sub-CUs. Padding between layers is removed to keep consistency between training and inference. Considering that encoding complexity of each partition mode differs, an algorithm is designed to search for the optimal thresholds with a balance between RD performance and encoding complexity. Then, we integrate a gradient-based method and a novel partition mode reuse method into VTM, which achieve a better RD-complexity trade-off at a large time-saving rate. Lastly, we remove several existing accelerating methods in VTM and find that our method is a good substitute. Experiments implemented single-threaded on CPU demonstrate that our method can reduce 25.29%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>73.98% complexity of VVC intra encoding with -0.04%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>1.93% BDRate increase. All the code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/cppppp/FastIntra</uri>.

title: Fixed-time disturbance rejection control for PMSM drives via estimation-error compensation and a fuzzy event-triggering mechanism
authors: Simeng Song, Zhilin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Disturbance observers are cornerstone techniques for enhancing the robustness of permanent magnet synchronous motor drives. However, restricted observer bandwidth and time varying external disturbances often result in inevitable estimation errors, thereby compromising high-precision tracking performance. Existing estimation-error compensation methods often rely on discontinuous switching terms, which inevitably lead to severe chattering and require prior knowledge of disturbance bounds that is difficult to obtain. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a fixed-time disturbance rejection control strategy featuring an active estimation-error compensation mechanism without prior knowledge. First, a fixed-time disturbance observer is constructed to rapidly reconstruct system disturbances. Unlike conventional asymptotic or finite-time observers, the proposed observer guarantees a deterministic settling time, regardless of initial estimation errors, ensuring its applicability in all situations. To counteract the detrimental effects of observation residues on tracking precision, an active compensation mechanism is incorporated into the control law. Furthermore, a fuzzy hybrid event triggering mechanism is introduced to optimize communication resource utilization. By dynamically adjusting the triggering threshold based on system states, the computational burden is significantly reduced while maintaining satisfactory control performance. Theoretically, the closed-loop system is proven to be practically fixed-time stable via Lyapunov analysis. Extensive comparative experiments have demonstrated that the proposed approach is superior to other state-of-the-art approaches in terms of both disturbance rejection and dynamic response.

title: AraOptical Testbed: Design, Field Trials, and Channel Analysis of Long-Range FSOC System With COTS Transceivers_supp1-3667493.pdf
authors: Xun Li
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper presents araoptical, a 10.15 km rural free-space optical communication (FSOC) testbed designed using commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) 10 G SFP+ transceivers and integrated into the ARA wireless living lab. The system features a multi-level autonomous alignment algorithm and real-time link monitoring, enabling stable high-throughput communication in dynamically varying atmospheric conditions. A continuous 2-hour UDP-based bit rate test was conducted, and environmental data were logged to investigate turbulence-induced impairments. A two-stage polynomial regression approach was developed to estimate the refractive index structure parameter (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C_{n}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) from weather station data and predict link throughput by combining one-minute block-averaged received power with the Rytov variance predicted from the measured meteorological data. The proposed model achieved <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2} = 89.92\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> for turbulence estimation and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2} = 62.22\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> for throughput prediction. Our results demonstrate the feasibility of using in-situ environmental sensing and data-driven models to characterize and predict FSOC performance under real-world conditions.

title: MambaGesture2: Co-Speech Gesture Generation via Hierarchical Fusion and Spatiotemporal Aggregation
authors: Chencan Fu, Yabiao Wang, Haoyang He, Shuo Wang, Chengjie Wang, Ying Tai, Yong Liu, Jiangning Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Co-speech gesture generation plays a vital role in producing synchronized and natural human gestures, thereby enhancing the realism of avatars in virtual environments. Although diffusion models have shown strong generative capabilities, their combination with transformer-based architectures often incurs high computational costs due to the quadratic complexity of self-attention. Moreover, as a temporal sequence modeling task, existing methods frequently struggle to effectively capture multi-scale temporal dynamics inherent in speech and gesture signals. To address these challenges, we propose MambaGesture2, a novel framework that integrates a Mamba-based denoising network, Hierarchical U-Net Gesture Mamba (HUG-Mamba), with a multimodal feature fusion module, SEAD. HUG-Mamba combines the efficient state-space modeling of Mamba blocks with the hierarchical sampling of the U-Net architecture, significantly improving temporal coherence and computational efficiency. We further introduce the Temporal-Stratified Fusion (TSF) module to capture diverse temporal scales via multi-scale learning, and the Spatial-Temporal Cascaded Aggregation (STCA) module to enhance spatial-temporal feature aggregation. Extensive experiments on the multi-modal BEAT2 and SHOW datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple quantitative metrics, while substantially reducing model complexity and inference time. The results validate the effectiveness of our architectural innovations in generating diverse, realistic, and temporally consistent co-speech gestures. Project page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://fcchit.github.io/mambagesture2</uri>.

title: Dynamic Key-Based Certificateless Authentication Protocol for Seamless RSU switching in VANETs
authors: Kun Wang, Yuanyuan Zhang, Ying Fang, Yang Hu
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks (VANETs) are fundamental to intelligent transportation systems, enabling real-time V2V and V2I communication. However, the highly dynamic topology of modern VANETs introduces significant challenges, specifically the inefficiencies caused by frequent Roadside Unit (RSU) switching and the heavy verification burden of massive broadcast messages. To resolve these security and efficiency bottlenecks, this paper presents a certificateless cryptography framework designed for rapid RSU switching and streamlined message verification. We initially introduce a fast switching mechanism utilizing a two-layer key architecture. By leveraging a long-term key from a Trusted Authority alongside short-term RSU session keys, this approach achieves seamless cross-RSU authentication and eliminates redundant verification steps. Furthermore, we propose a lightweight certificateless message authentication scheme that integrates Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC), prediction tables, and an adaptive Huffman tree. This combination effectively optimizes cumulative verification time and support real-time validation. Performance evaluations using Python simulations and OMNeT++ demonstrate that our framework significantly outperforms existing methods. The results confirm reduced verification latency, lower computational overhead, and robust security, offering a scalable solution for dynamic vehicular environments.

title: Design and Control of PI Controller Based ZVS Boost Converter
authors: Arijit Nath, Dheeraj Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: With the application of renewable energy systems in power systems and EV segment, boost converters become interesting and viable option as DC DC Converters. Its efficiency is affected by its design and due to it soft switching techniques (ZVS and ZCS) plays major role. In the present paper ZVS boost converter is designed in detail and its performance is shown in open loop. Further PI controller is designed for conventional boost converter and ZVS boost converter performance is checked for load regulation. MATLAB R2023a version is used for validation of proposed methodology. It has been found that proposed boost converter is working well under different operating conditions.

title: Disaster Management in the Digital Age: A Study on the Use of Digital Media During Delhi's 2023 Yamuna Flood
authors: Sidharth Verma, Durgesh Tripathi, Ramalakshmi Lakshmanan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Climate change increases urban communities' risks, making them more vulnerable to environmental hazards and extreme weather conditions. Recurring flooding events on the banks of Yamuna River, Delhi, threaten the lives and livelihoods of residents. Flooding in 2023 surpassed the rise in water levels when compared to the flooding in 1978. Mitigation and coping strategies at the grassroots level have become a focal point of scholarly interest. The study aims to investigate the mitigation and coping strategies adopted by residents in floodprone banks of river Yamuna in Delhi. The qualitative study employs focus group discussions as the primary data collection method. The study finds that new media platforms, such as social media, emerged as crucial in disseminating timely information and warnings to residents along the Yamuna River. The residents checked real-time weather forecasts and water level updates through Facebook, YouTube and WhatsApp, enabling them to make informed decisions and take necessary precautions to safeguard themselves. Those living in low-lying areas responded swiftly and proactively based on the floodrelated alerts they received through new media. Despite the absence of the government's timely relief measures, their speedy action resulted in fewer losses than those residing in higher parts of the Yamuna Bank. The study establishes the significant role of new media in disaster mitigation, as it facilitates real, timely communication and informed decision-making among affected communities, ultimately reducing the impact of the disaster on vulnerable populations. The study informs key stakeholders about the potential of integrating new media into disaster management strategies.

title: A Calibration Method Based on Binocular Vision for Eye-to-hand Configuration
authors: Jinsheng Fu, Haiting Xu, Qi Li, Yimin Song
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: In order to ensure the collaboration accuracy between vision and robot, a hand-eye calibration method based on binocular vision is proposed. The binocular vision system is placed on the outside of the robot. By collecting the position information of the encoded marker points fixed at the end of the robot under different poses, the homogeneous transformation matrix of the vision relative to the robot base and the position of the coded marker points relative to the end of the robot are simultaneously solved. Firstly, a calibration model considering the comprehensive effect of various error factors under the robot base frame is established. Then, a parameter adjustment solution method based on constraints is proposed to obtain the initial value of the model. Finally, a nonlinear solution method is proposed in combination with the binocular measurement error. The experimental platform is established based on the six-degree-of-freedom parallel robot and verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Optimized Current Limiting Protection Strategy for the MMC-HVDC System based on the Finite-Control-Set Model Predictive Control
authors: Yifeng Su, Zheng Gong, Ruiqi Wang, Lingxiang Qin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Flexible DC grids face the challenge of a rapid increase in DC short-circuit current. To address this challenge, the active current limiting control method has been demonstrated as an effective solution for suppressing the fault current, but it may cause arm overcurrent and cause converter station blocking. An optimized current limiting control is proposed to limit the rise rate of fault currents. The advantages of finite-control-set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) with multi-objective control are utilized to achieve rapid tracking of AC-side current and reducing the imbalance in energy between modular multilevel converters (MMC) phases and arms. Compared to the existing method of combining the active current limiting control with proportional-integral (PI) controller, the proposed strategy can alleviate the overcurrent in arms and AC side caused by reconstructing the DC voltage of MMC. The results of the protection strategy is validated by a four-terminal DC grid in MATLAB/Simulink.

title: A High-PSRR, Ultra-Low Quiescent Current, Capless LDO Regulator Using a Self-Biased Recycling Folded-Cascode Error Amplifier in 90nm CMOS
authors: A. Ilyas, A. Khan, N. Alam, G. S. Javed
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This paper presents the design of a fully integrated, capacitor-less low-dropout (LDO) voltage regulator employing a self-biased recycling folded-cascode (RFC) error amplifier. By adapting a self-biasing scheme to the RFC topology, all bias currents are generated on-chip, eliminating external bias circuitry and improving area efficiency. Implemented in PTM 90 nm CMOS technology, the regulator delivers a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{O U T}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 0}$</tex> V from a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{I N}$</tex> of 1.0 V with up to 10 mA of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{L O A D}$</tex> and only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \mathbf{~ p F}$</tex> of on-chip <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C_{O U T}$</tex>. Pre-layout simulations report <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 4 2. 3} \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> PSRR at 100 kHz, quiescent current (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{Q}$</tex>) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.72 \mu ~\mathrm{A}, 200 \text{mV}$</tex> dropout voltage, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.117 \text{mV} / \mathrm{V}$</tex> line regulation, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.169 \text{mV} / \text{mA}$</tex> load regulation, demonstrating its suitability for compact power management in modern system-on-chip (SoC) platforms.

title: Proposal and Fundamental Evaluation of a Hand Posture Guidance Method Using Air-Jet-Based Force Feedback
authors: H. Ohara, M. Okui
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In the context of preserving traditional performing arts and facilitating efficient motor skill acquisition, there is a need to develop posture guidance methods. Traditionally, visual imitation and verbal instruction have been the primary means of guidance; however, haptic feedback is considered particularly effective for posture instruction and form correction, as it can directly convey movement nuances that are difficult to communicate through visual or auditory channels alone. Although various haptic information transmission devices have been developed, they often present issues such as discomfort caused by reaction force support structures or restrictions on joint range of motion. To address these limitations, we focused on haptic feedback using air jets. Air jets can present forces in arbitrary directions relative to the body part on which the nozzle is mounted, and the approach can be extended to the entire body, enabling posture guidance even for joints involved in complex movements. In this study, we proposed and developed a haptic feedback device for guiding whole-body posture. We demonstrated that the device can provide effective posture guidance and clarified the influence of the frequency characteristics of the presented haptic feedback. Furthermore, we experimentally demonstrated that the system enables complex, multi-joint, and large-range posture guidance.

title: Scalable, Fault-Tolerant Quantum Algorithms for Cryptanalysis-Resistant Communications
authors: Ankith Konda
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Future quantum computers pose a direct threat to classical cryptographic protocols used in secure communications. While post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and quantum key distribution (QKD) individually offer promising defense mechanisms, neither alone sufficiently addresses the challenges of scalability, fault tolerance, and long-term cryptanalysis resistance. In this work, we present Q-STARCOM, a hybrid architecture that integrates PQC-based key encapsulation, optional QKD-derived entropy, authenticated quantum channel probing using unitary 2-designs, and an adaptive ratcheted key lifecycle all designed for compatibility with fault-tolerant surface-code quantum systems. Q-STARCOM ensures composable security by combining robust key establishment, channel tamper detection, and forward secrecy in a single framework. We evaluate the system under various error models and adversarial tampering conditions, showing that it achieves competitive key rates, high tamper detection probability, and efficient T-gate budgeting. The architecture aligns with existing standards (e.g., FIPS 203/204, ITU-T Y.3800, ETSI QKD 004) and offers a scalable path forward for building quantum-secure communication infrastructure.
