Our methodology and results show that Q-STARCOM provides a practical, futureproof foundation for securing long-term communications in the post-quantum era.

title: Detecting Fraud on the Ethereum Blockchain Using the XGBoost Algorithm
authors: Shaik Jeelani Basha, Pragya, Shreya Majumdar, Ayushi Prasad, Prachi Mehta, Zeesha Mishra
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: In recent years, blockchain technologies such as Ethereum have secured widespread adoption, yet they have also become increasingly targeted by fraudulent activities. Discovering these fraudulent patterns is challenging due to the complexity and size of transaction data. This study investigates the application of the XGBoost algorithm, a gradient boosting technique optimized for performance plus scalability, in discovering fraudulent transactions on the Ethereum network. The model is instructed to distinguish between valid as well as suspicious behaviour based on transactional and behavioural characteristics by examining a dataset of Ethereum transaction records. XGBoost is a popular machine learning algorithm used for a range of tasks, including fraud detection on Ethereum and other blockchain networks. It is a highly effective model due to its performance, flexibility, and ability to handle complex, imbalanced, as well as large datasets. This study emphasises accuracy and precision, also recalling key metrics, demonstrating that XGBoost not only enhances prediction performance but also minimizes false positives over other classification algorithms. Our findings: XGBoost is the optimal model for real-time fraudulent detection in a blockchain context. The accuracy of the XGBOOST algorithm achieves a high level of accuracy.

title: Explainable Ensemble Machine Learning Approach for severity prediction of Hemophilia A
authors: Vikalp Kumar Singh, Maheshwari Prasad Singh, Ashutosh Mani
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: With the increasing use of machine learning in healthcare, explainable AI (XAI) has become essential to ensure transparency and trust in model predictions. In this study, we experimented with various machine learning classifiers, including an ensemble model combining XG-Boost with Random Forest, which demonstrated superior performance in predicting hemophilia A (HA) severity. To ensure model transparency, LIME explainable AI was used to explain the predictions of the best-performing model and identify features that could aid decision-making. We also applied it to a subset of 25 cases in each of the mild, moderate, and severe categories. LIME provided instance-level explanations, showing prediction probabilities for the categories, as well as feature values and their contribution to each prediction. The analysis showed that the features processed by PSM were influential, with the average contribution of cDNA to severity prediction being most clearly reflected. Visualization of frequency and mean absolute contribution provided additional insight, highlighting the consistent and robust influence of variables on the model’s predictions of the HA genetic profile.This LIME-based interpretability framework demonstrates how explainable AI can bridge the gap between predictive performance and clinical transparency. Furthermore, this study can be used to improve severity prediction models in the context of mutation-based disorders and can be extended to other SNP-based disorders.

title: Submodule Capacitor Voltage Control for T-Type Modular Multilevel DC/DC Converter
authors: Yipeng Su, Hao Zhang, Ke Cheng, Heya Yang, Xin Xiang, Chushan Li, Wuhua Li, Xiangning He
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The T type modular multilevel DC/DC converter (T MMDC) has emerged as a promising solution for high voltage direct current (HVdc) grid interconnection and renewable energy transmission. Nevertheless, the inherent asymmetry of its three arm configuration inevitably leads to energy imbalances among the arms, primarily due to capacitor voltage ripple, operational losses, and parameter asymmetries within the networks. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a hierarchical capacitor voltage balancing control framework specifically designed for T MMDCs in HVdc grids. The proposed approach ensures effective and stable balancing of submodule (SM) capacitor voltages throughout the entire operating range. Comprehensive PLECS simulations of a 500 MW, 320 kV/100 kV system demonstrate the robustness of the proposed methodology, confirming both stable converter operation and efficient power transfer. The obtained results verify the technical feasibility of implementing the T MMDC with the proposed control strategy for large scale renewable energy integration and multi voltage level HVdc transmission applications.

title: Feature Attention-Enhanced CNN-LSTM for Real Time Stress Prediction & Management
authors: G. Vallathan, K. Kavitha, S. Selvin Ebenezer, Chintala Nageswara Rao, R.Manoj Kumar, E. Manjula
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Stress prediction and management play a crucial role in mental health monitoring. Conventional machine learning approaches such as Naive Bayes (NB), Support Vector Machines (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Deep Belief Networks (DBN), and Enhanced LSTM (ELSTM) have limitations in capturing complex temporal dependencies and extracting meaningful features, leading to reduced predictive performance. To address these drawbacks, a CNN-LSTM-based attention mechanism has been proposed to enhance stress prediction by combining CNN's feature extraction, LSTM's sequential learning, and attention's ability to emphasize critical patterns. This hybrid approach improves model interpretability by capturing intricate stress-related needs in time-series statistics. The proposed model is assessed on the KNHANES VI dataset using crucial concert metrics. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements over traditional methods, achieving a 6.27 % increase in recall, 5.3 % in F1-score, 3.72 % in accuracy, and 3.95% in precision. This framework enhances stress detection accuracy, making it highly effective for realworld applications, including early stress diagnosis, workplace mental health monitoring, and personalized stress management solutions. The results validate its superiority in stress prediction and management.

title: Solar-Powered Wearable Device for Real-Time Health Monitoring with Integrated SOS and GPS Tracking
authors: Roselin Thomsi Gc, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a solar powered Wearable device that combines real-time health monitoring with GPS. tracking, and a dual-mode SOS alert system. The device continuous monitoring of vital parameters like heart rate, SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, blood pressure, body temperature and ECG using embedded biomedi-cal sensors. Data is displayed locally and transmitted via GSM to caregivers and emergency contacts. The SOS mechanism works manually by a dedicated button and automatically when Abnormal conditions are detected, with the GPS coordinates transmitted to predefined contacts for quick intervention. To enhance reliability, a univariate ensemble filter feature selection approach and a hybrid ensemble classifier combing Random Forest, The methods used in this experiment are based on ensemble learning: Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost. Experimental Evaluation reveals a $\mathbf{9 5 - 9 8 \%}$ accuracy for heart rate and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, with the ensemble achieving an AUC of 0.97. Integrated solar charging extends device autonomy by up to 30% under outdoor conditions. The proposed solution provides a reliable, Energy-efficient Platform for Continuous Health Monitoring and particularly, emergency response in resource-constrained settings.

title: Design and Implementation of a 32-bit RISC-V Pipelined Processor by using Verilog for IoT
authors: Suman Kumari, Ashutosh Gupta, Kaushiki, Piyush Kumar Singh, Pooja Kumari, Pragyan Parimita Padhan, Prashant Pranav, Vineet Kumar, Vijay Nath
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: In this paper, we discuss the need for customized, resource-efficient processing units in low-power Internet of Things (IoT) edge devices. The challenge in these environments is to balance high instruction throughput with a small hardware footprint and low energy use. We present the design and implementation of a 32-bit RISC-V pipelined processor using Verilog HDL. This processor is used in a real-time system that integrates humidity sensing and data display, and it runs on an FPGA platform. Sensor data comes in via UART, gets processed by the RISC-V core, and is sent to an ESP32 microcontroller for output display. The proposed design has a traditional five-stage pipeline (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB) made for embedded applications with memory-mapped peripheral interfaces. Simulation and synthesis results on an Artix-7 FPGA show that the design is functionally correct, uses hardware efficiently (approximately 18percent LUT utilization), and is suitable for real-time, low-power IoT systems.

title: Basic Study for Drive Mechanism with Synthetic Fiber Rope : Feasibility Study of a Tension Measurable High-Strength Synthetic Fiber Rope
authors: Takuma Nishimura, Shinya Sadachika, Koki Hasegawa, Takahiro Aruga, Gen Endo
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Lightweight and high-strength synthetic fiber ropes are widely used as components in tendon-driven manipulators. However, measuring their tension involves structural and electrical complexity, which makes it difficult to take measurements in the middle of the rope. Through a comparison of three commercially available conductive ropes, we confirmed that the organic conductive fiber Thunderon exhibits high linearity between rope tension and electrical resistance. However, Thunderon alone has a low breaking strength, making it insufficient for application in a tendon-driven manipulator that requires high tension. To improve the breaking strength, we prototyped a composite rope using the high-strength fiber Zylon as the jacket. Tensile tests showed that the breaking strength was improved by more than 25 times and that a stable electrical response was obtained even under high tension. By implementing this rope in a coupled tendon-driven manipulator, we demonstrated that tension changes along the rope could be detected through resistance changes. This method is expected to be a new approach that enables tension measurement in coupled tendon-driven manipulators.

title: Large-Model-Based Knowledge Transfer for Industrial Data Fusion and Interoperability Research
authors: Yuan Gao, Sicong Yu, Ying Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Industrial data fusion and interoperability represent critical technical challenges in the digital transformation of manufacturing. Processing multi-source heterogeneous data in industrial environments is complex, involving issues such as inefficient data annotation, inconsistent quality, and poor interoperability. To address these challenges, this study proposes a knowledge transfer method based on large-scale models, constructs a technical framework for intelligent annotation and quality processing, and designs a collaborative optimization mechanism for fusion and interoperability. This framework employs deep learning for feature extraction and fusion, utilizes knowledge graphs for cross-protocol data mapping, and incorporates reinforcement learning to optimize system performance. The results were validated on an intelligent manufacturing production line, achieving a fusion accuracy of 96.3% and a protocol conversion accuracy of 98.5%, with an average system response time below 120 milliseconds. This provides a novel technical pathway for intelligent industrial data processing and holds significant practical implications for advancing the digital transformation of manufacturing.

title:
Mobile Manipulation System for In-Store Product Stocking, Disposal, and Customer Detection with Minimal Environment Modification
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Ryusei Sawada, Ryota Nakamura, Asahi Miyaji, Kousuke Shimizu, Koki Sunaga, Koji Aikawa, Kyohei Fujita, Ryunosuke Maruta, Shota Nakajima, Mihoko Niitsuma
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In recent years, competitions such as the World Robot Summit (WRS) have spurred the development of robotic systems aimed at automating operations in convenience stores and other retail outlets. However, many existing methods require extensive modifications to products and shelves, and are highly dependent on the environment, making their deployment in brick-and-mortar stores problematic. In this study, we propose a mobile manipulation system that performs highly accurate product stocking, disposal, and customer detection while minimizing environmental modifications. The proposed system consists of three elements: (1) highly accurate product position recognition using an industrial camera and ArUco markers; (2) robot arm control with variable grasping strategy, coordinate correction, collision prevention, and retry functions; and (3) customer approach detection using 2D LiDAR. Testing in a competition scenario simulating a real-world environment demonstrated improved grasping success rates, detection of discarded products under obstructed visibility, and safety, confirming both practicality and competition performance.

title: Inter-turn Short Circuit Fault Diagnosis of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Using an Adaptive Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Observer
authors: Meron Sisay Abebe, Ge Xinglai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: PMSMs frequently experience inter-turn shortcircuit (ITSC) problems. If undetected, it can cause serious secondary damage and system failure. This study introduces Adaptive Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Observer (AST-SMO), a model-based ITSC fault diagnosis tool, so that the system reliability will be enhanced. A key feature of the proposed method is its adaptive gain mechanism, which adjusts the observer gains based on motor speed to accurately estimate the back electromotive force (EMF). This enables accurate back EMF estimation across several operating conditions-providing higher sensitivity at low speeds and enhanced robustness at high speeds. First, AST-SMO is used for stator back (EMF) estimation while reducing chattering effects. Second, second-order generalized integrator (SOGI) is proposed to filter back EMF for improved fault detection. To detect faults, the residuals between the estimated and reference three-phase back EMF are compared. The proposed strategy is validated by MATLAB simulations.

title: Power Optimized AES Using CFA for Secure Memory Access and FPGA Implementation
authors: Kavin Kumar K, Meera Devi T, Suriya B, Thirugnanaguru S, Vishal Varma V, Priyanka Dwivedi
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Ensuring secure boot and protected memory access is essential for embedded and IoT systems operating in safety- and security-critical domains. However, software-based cryptographic mechanisms often introduce significant performance overheads and remain vulnerable to tampering. This paper presents a hardware-accelerated framework for secure boot and memory access control using the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) implemented on a Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA). The proposed design integrates an optimized AES core with onchip memory controllers to authenticate firmware at startup and enforce controlled access to sensitive memory regions. To enhance efficiency, parallelization and pipelining techniques are employed to reduce latency and improve hardware utilization. Implementation on a Xilinx FPGA demonstrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.67 \times$</tex> reduction in power consumption and reduced boot-time exposure to unauthorized code compared to conventional software-based approaches. The proposed architecture enhances system resilience against tampering and replay attacks while offering a low-power, scalable solution for real-time security in embedded and IoT applications..

title: Agrivoltaics: A Review of Systems, Performance and Future Directions
authors: Harshita Mishra, Omveer Singh, Md. Rihan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Agrivoltaics (AV), the co-location of photovoltaic (PV) systems with agricultural activity, has surfaced as a dual land-use strategy addressing energy generation and food security contemporaneously. This review consolidates research advances, examining multiple aspects: typologies, performance modeling, ecological integration, and regional policy development. A wide range of configurations have been studied, including fixed-tilt and vertical bifacial modules. Certainly, many recent studies have appraised the energy efficiency, as well as the agricultural viability, of diverse Agrivoltaic (AV) configurations. These configurations have not only been studied for their co-optimization potential (i.e., their efficiency in producing both energy and crops), but also analyzed in terms of several performance metrics: Land Equivalent Ratio (LER), Light Productivity Factor (LPF), and Capacity Utilization Factor (CUF). In the recent studies, a combination of Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulation plus terrestrial and remote sensing analyses to assess the energy efficiency of these configurations in terms of their effect on system-level dynamics (part of performance modeling). In this paper not we only study the findings from these recent studies, but also develops the narrative in some detail, discussing modeling approaches that employ a set of analytical (and also numerical) tools and scales to accomplish this difficult venture. (Field trials across recent geography have demonstrated the sustainability of some potential crops.

title: Artificial Neural Network Approach for Forecasting the Mechanical Behavior of Prosopis Juliflora Fibre-Reinforced Epoxy Composites
authors: Sivakumar Sambath, Rajasekar Rajendran, Sabarish Rajagopalan
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: This study presents an artificial neural network (ANN) methodology for forecasting the mechanical properties of Prosopis juliflora fibre-reinforced epoxy composites. Composites were produced with fibre concentrations ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0 \text{wt} \%$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \text{wt} \%$</tex>, and their tensile, flexural, impact, and hardness properties were assessed experimentally. A feedforward backpropagation artificial neural network model was created, utilizing fibre weight fraction as the input and the assessed mechanical properties as outputs. The network underwent training and validation, utilizing the Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm (trainlm) technique to reduce prediction errors. The ANN exhibited outstanding predictive efficacy, attaining correlation values (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) surpassing 0.99991 for all mechanical parameters and mean square errors less than 0.4. The findings indicate that the proposed ANN model effectively represents the nonlinear relationship between fibre content and mechanical characteristics. Additionally, it provides a reliable computational tool for enhancing natural fibre-reinforced composites. The ANN model can forecast the mechanical properties of composites with different fibre compositions, reducing experimental expenses and time expenditures.

title: Dual-Active-Bridge Converters for Large-step Time -Domine Simulation
authors: Yudong Zhang, Hongchang Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Dual-active-bridge (DAB) converters are widely used in high-power scenarios such as DC microgrids and electric vehicles for their bidirectional symmetry and the ease of achieving soft switching. In these systems, owing to discrepancies in switching frequencies, numerous dynamic interactions and frequency couplings exist between different converters. Simulating this behavior of such complex systems in the time domain is not only highly challenging but also consumes substantial computational resources. This paper presents a DAB modeling method based on the high-order generalized Averaging method. By taking into account the ripple information of the converter, it not only establishes an accurate large-signal model of DAB but also enables the prediction of frequency coupling behaviors in parallel systems with a step size of 1/20 switching period when the DAB converters have different switching frequencies.

title: An Improved Dynamic Power Decoupling Method for Grid-Forming Inverters
authors: Jiayu Shang, Zeng Liu, Xiangping Xiao, Wenchen Wang, Jinjun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In grid-forming inverters, strong power coupling can lead to steady-state errors, slow dynamic response and large overshoot in output power, even system instability. In existing methods, the grid information is needed to achieve power decoupling, and decoupling expressions with fixed parameters fail to achieve dynamic power decoupling in full range of operating conditions. To address these issues, this paper proposes an improved dynamic power decoupling method based on voltage and phase angle cross-feedforward. In this method, the output power is utilized for deriving the decoupling compensation and variable parameter decoupling expressions to achieve dynamic power decoupling in all operating conditions, where the extraction of dynamic components is optimized, and the need for grid voltage and frequency measurement is avoided. Finally, simulation and experimental results verified the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Analytical Model for Series-Connected SiC MOSFETs Under Heavy and Light Load Scenario
authors: Hao Yan, Han Peng, Junhao Lei, Jingbo Feng, Chao Li, Weiming Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Series connection of Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs is a pivotal technique to enhance voltage ratings and power density in medium- and high-voltage power converters. However, the dynamic voltage distribution and overshoot during switching transients—key factors affecting device reliability—are strongly influenced by load conditions, which are inadequately addressed in existing models. Conventional approaches often neglect the fundamental shift in dominant transient mechanisms between load scenarios and oversimplify the nonlinear characteristics of parasitic capacitances, leading to significant prediction errors, especially under light-load conditions. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a load-dependent analytical model for multi-device series-connected SiC MOSFETs. The model explicitly divides the turn-off process into distinct stages for heavy- and light-load conditions and incorporates the nonlinear behavior of parasitic capacitances (e.g., C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gd</inf> and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>,) using an integral average method. Closed-form solutions are derived for transient voltage distributions, enabling efficient and accurate prediction of key switching parameters. Experimental validation on a double-pulse test platform with 1700 V SiC MOSFETs confirms the model’s high accuracy, with maximum errors in critical transient parameters within 10.4% under light-load (10 A) and 9.01% under heavy-load (50 A) conditions. The proposed model provides a reliable analytical tool for predicting switching transients in series-connected configurations, supporting optimal voltage balancing and improved device protection in high-voltage SiC converter systems.

title: Embedding Remote Center of Motion Constraints into Robotic Manipulator Control via Recurrent Neural Networks
authors: Delong Zhao, Fuyong Zhang, Yuming Li, Haifei Zhu, Zhihao Xu, Yangmin Ou, Xiaojing Lv, Serestina Viriri, Ganggang Yang, Donglin Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper introduces an RNN-based control frame-work for redundant robotic manipulators under Remote Center of Motion (RCM) constraints in minimally invasive surgery. Unlike conventional task-prioritization or dynamic α-adjustment methods, that suffer from task conflicts, fixed weights, and delays, the proposed approach integrates the RCM constraint directly into the kinematic model by modeling the surgical tool as a fixed-length end-effector extension. This enables conflict-free trajectory tracking and RCM enforcement through quadratic optimization under joint limits, solved via an RNN to achieve synchronous error convergence. The simulation platform based on a 7-DoF Franka model achieves sub-millimeter accuracy in circular path and figure-8 path. Hardware experiments on a Franka Panda validate real-time performance. The proposed method enhances the precision and safety of surgical robotics, and has potential for multi-arm extensions.

title: AI in Financial Forecasting: Enhancing Portfolio Optimization with Deep Learning
authors: S. Avinash, G. N Shunmathi, A. Isaac Jeremiah, S. M Lisanth, V. Harish, S. Kumaran
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Financial planning and portfolio optimization are important to achieve the highest returns with the lowest risks in fluctuating markets. Classical methods like Markowitz's MeanVariance Optimization and the Capital Asset Pricing Model do not always capture intricate financial trends, resulting in optimum asset allocation. New developments in deep learning have demonstrated the potential to improve financial decisionmaking. This work introduces a new Transformer-based Deep Reinforcement Learning (T-DRL) framework that exploits sequential market data and reinforcement learning to adaptively optimize asset allocation. The framework utilizes a Transformer encoder to model long-term patterns in financial time series and utilizes Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) for adaptive portfolio rebalancing. Experimental findings on actual financial data sets show that the proposed method excels over conventional methods in profitability, Sharpe ratio, and riskadjusted return. The results show the prospects of deep learning for improving portfolio optimization approaches, offering investors a more resilient and responsive way to financial decision-making.

title: HybridNet: A High-Accuracy Attention-Based Lightweight CNN for UAV-Based Aerial Disaster Image Classification
authors: Subhrajeet Ganguly, Ningthoujam Johny Singh
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: For an effective emergency response, it’s important to have quick and accurate situational awareness during natural disasters. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are useful for taking real-time aerial pictures, but their limited onboard computing power makes it hard to use deep learning models. This paper introduces HybridNet, a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) with high accuracy and enhanced attention, created for real-time disaster image classification on UAV platforms. HybridNet combines dilated separable convolutions, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention, and a learnable feature-fusion gate to find a good balance between representational power and computational efficiency. A global weighted pooling mechanism makes spatial attention even better by letting the model adaptively focus on semantically important areas. HybridNet was trained and tested on Aerial Image Database for Emergency Response (AIDER). It has an accuracy of 97.74% and a weighted F1-score of 97.77%, which is better than other lightweight CNNs. It also has a small footprint of 33.83 MB 8.8 M parameters) and an average inference latency of 4.43 ms. The proposed architecture shows a lot of promise for use in real-time UAV-based disaster monitoring and emergency response systems.

title: A Reinforcement Learning Approach for Fast and Efficient Fault Recovery in Cloud-Based Microservices
authors: Wenya Zhang, Qingliang Wang, Yajie Cai, Hanyuan Li, Tongxin Wu, Ruibo Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of microservice architectures, ensuring fast and effective fault recovery has become a critical issue for maintaining system reliability. Traditional fault recovery methods, often relying on rules or case-based reasoning, struggle to handle the complex and dynamic failure patterns present in microservices environments. To address this, this paper proposes a reinforcement learning-based fault recovery method for microservices. The fault recovery process is modeled as a Markov Decision Process , and the policy is optimized using Deep Q-Networks combined with Prioritized Experience Replay to achieve efficient recovery across multiple fault scenarios. The recovery action space is mapped based on Rancher APIs, ensuring compatibility with existing platform operations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves recovery efficiency, achieving an average recovery time of 40.64 seconds and a worst-case recovery time under 74 seconds on the Sock-Shop benchmark system. This method provides a novel solution for automated fault recovery in microservice architectures.

title: Improved HSI Classification via Hybrid Spatial-Spectral Deep Features and Kernel ELM
authors: Suchismita Behera, Partha Pratim Sarangi, Bhabani Shankar Prasad Mishra, Sayak Hatui
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) classification is a challenging task in remote sensing image due to inter-class spectral similarity, intra-class variability, and limited labeled data. This paper presents a novel hybrid framework that combines 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNN) for spatial-spectral feature extraction with a Kernel Extreme Learning Machine (KELM) classifier to enhance classification accuracy and computational efficiency. The proposed method employs a four-block 3D-CNN to process overlapping 3D patches, capturing hierarchical spatialspectral features, followed by a RBF kernel-based KELM for improving classification performance. To address spectral redundancy, we integrate the Jaya optimization algorithm for band selection prior to feature extraction. Experiments on benchmark datasets such as Indian Pines, Pavia University, Salinas demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, achieving 99.44% OA (Indian Pines), 99.91 % OA (Pavia University), and 99.27% OA (Salinas). The 3D-CNN-KELM framework outperforms traditional 1D/2D CNNs and other deep learning models, while KELM classifier offers rapid training and efficient classification.

title: Dynamic Bimanual Cloth Manipulation via Dynamic Movement Primitives and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Matija Mavsar, Aleš Ude, Andrej Gams
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Learning effective control strategies for deformable object manipulation remains a major challenge in robotics, especially when tasks require fast, coordinated motions between multiple manipulators. In this work we address the problem of dynamic bimanual cloth manipulation, where two robotic arms must coordinate fast, fluid motions to place a cloth onto a surface. Our method uses Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with Dynamic Movement Primitives (DMPs) as the policy output, enabling smooth and parameterized trajectory generation. We compute rewards such as cloth height, corner alignment, and movement direction only at the end of each training episode, while the robot control continues at a higher frequency, and introduce a probing phase to obtain knowledge about cloth dynamics. We implement our approach in NVIDIA Isaac Sim with realistic cloth dynamics. Experiments show that this setup allows the robots to learn fast, coordinated bimanual cloth placement using only occasional reward feedback.

title: ‘Aquarencia’ Serious Game as an Effort to Educate and Revitalize the Conservation of Endangered Sea Turtle
authors: Justin Tjokro, Philips Sanjaya, Galih Dea Pratama, Muhamad Fajar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Sea turtles are critically endangered due to habitat degradation, human activities, and climate changes, creating an urgent need for innovative methods and conservation strategies. Despite various approaches and efforts, traditional educational approaches often fail to sustain public’s engagement, especially among younger audiences. This study introduces Aquarencia, a serious game developed to promote and raise awareness towards sea turtle conservation and eco-tourism. The game combines interactive elements such as underwater photography, turtle incubation management, and eco-tourism conservancy todeliver engaging and educational experiences. To evaluate its effectiveness, Aquarencia was compared to Idle Safari, a market-available game, using the Game Testing Questionnaire (GTQ) framework. Thirty Indonesian Computer Science students (aged 17-24) assessed both games on System Usability Scale (SUS), Socio-Economic Aspects (SEA), Game Experience (GX), and After Gaming Experience (AGX). Statistical analysis using paired t-tests showed that Aquarencia achieved significantly higher scores in SEA, GX, and AGX categories (p < .001), highlighting its effectiveness in delivering educational content, fostering engagement, and providing satisfaction. These findings demonstrate that Aquarencia effectively raised awareness and supports eco-tourism through meaningful and enjoyable gameplay.

title: A Single-Stage AC-DC Converter Based Resonant Semi-DAB with Hybrid Discontinuous Conduction Mode and Fixed Switching Frequency Control
authors: Peisong Ma, Deshang Sha, Xiao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid DCM modulation and control strategy for the resonant semi-DAB-based single-stage high frequency isolated AC-DC converter. Two simple DCM modulation modes are used and the smooth transition between them can be obtained. The primary side switches can achieve ZVS within full operating range, and the secondary side diodes also can achieve ZCS within full working range. Compared with the non-resonant semi-DAB and modulation strategy, the proposed topology and modulation method can reduce the turn-off current and turn-off loss of switches. And this converter can achieve the function of step-up and step-down for voltage. The effectiveness of the proposed converter and control method is verified by simulation results.

title: A Relative Assessment of Data Privacy Standards in AI Personalization of Media Platforms
authors: Ayushi Chopra, Jyoti Gupta, Vanshika Bhatia, Ritika Wason, Sheel Nidhi Tripathi, Jagriti Basera
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Media platforms are offering notable personalization choices to their audience due to the effective application of Artificial intelligence (AI). This launch of usercentered mechanisms for content personalization and personalized recommendations have their set of advancements as well as challenges. These new advancements consume huge amount of user personal data. This raises essential legal and confidentiality apprehensions related to data use, management and customer awareness. This research investigates the compliance of popular media platforms with global data safety rules namely the GDPR- General Data Protection Regulation, the DPDPA - Digital Personal Data Protection Act and the CCPA - California Consumer Privacy Act. We employ a structured approach to assess the data protection mechanisms of chosen platforms. Analysis reveals that though social media companies claim different degrees of compliance yet they struggle significantly with explaining their data activities and obtaining active user permissions and providing individual data management tools. Analysis also reveal that majority of the users are unaware about their privacy status. This research thus reiterates the need of media entities to make their data practices more transparent as well as update users about the usage of their data.

title: Human-in-the-Loop Control of a Soft Pneumatic Exosuit for Step Width Guidance via Hip Abduction/Adduction
authors: Jinnosuke Kamimura, Tetsuro Miyazaki, Kenji Kawashima
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The constraints of cable-driven actuation have largely limited mediolateral (ML) assistance from soft exosuits to hip abduction. To address this limitation, we developed a soft pneumatic exosuit actuated by pneumatic artificial muscles (PAMs) capable of providing bidirectional assistance to both hip abduction and adduction to guide the wearer’s step width. Given the challenges in accurately modeling PAM-driven systems, we propose a Human-in-the-Loop (HIL) approach that treats the human-exosuit system as a black box and uses Bayesian optimization to tune control parameters. In walking experiments with two healthy participants, the proposed HIL framework successfully identified participant-specific PI gains during the parameter tuning session. In the subsequent validation, the controller based on the optimized gains reduced the mean step-width RMSE by 43.4% for Participant 1 and 26.8% for Participant 2 compared with the condition without assistance. The optimized gains also provided an additional improvement of 3.76% for Participant 1 and 1.07% for Participant 2 relative to the controller with initial gains. These results indicate that the proposed approach offers a practical method for personalizing assistance in soft pneumatic exosuits and contributes to the development of devices that enhance ML balance.

title: A Decentralized Control Scheme for SoC-Balancing in Single-Phase Cascaded H-Bridge Energy Storage System in PV Plant
authors: Zengyang Liu, Kai Sun, Jingjing Qi, Haining Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Cascaded H-Bridge (CHB) converter is a promising solution for energy storage system to connect to the grid. To mitigate the intermittence of renewable resources such as photovoltaic, energy storage modules are adopted in CHB structure. However, due to some factors such as aging, the capacity of the energy storage modules varies from each other. Therefore, the necessity of state of charge (SoC) balance has risen. A decentralized control for SoC balance is proposed. This method is designed for single phase CHB converters and does not need high-bandwidth communication which costs much and lack for robustness. Simulation has been performed to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: ISCEIC 2025 Author Information Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)

title: An Intelligent Task Scheduling Framework in Fog Computing Using Q-Learning
authors: Kapil Kumar, Md Zaka Al Shahariar Tanvir, Manjit Singh, Samiul Alam, Md. Shanto Islam, Md Abdullah Ibne Hayan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Tasks that are efficient to reduce latency and make the best use of resources in distributed networks, it is important to program in fog computing. Conventional programming algorithms frequently struggle to accommodate dynamic workloads and diverse resources. In this research, we introduce DQS (Deep Q Learning Planner), a framework grounded in reinforcement learning (RL) that utilizes a hybrid Markov Decision Process (POMDP) and an optimized reward function to enhance task success and energy efficiency. DQS is assessed in various reference scenarios and is compared with standard reinforcement learning methodologies, including Q-Learning and Sarsa, as well as traditional heuristic programming techniques. Experimental results indicate that DQS attains a success rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 4. 2 \%} \pm \text{0. 9 \%}$</tex> for tasks, decreases average latency by 18 %, and enhances energy efficiency by 12 % relative to the optimal performance baseline. DQS also has ways to handle failures to make sure it works reliably even when the network is not stable. These results show that combining a hybrid state representation with a carefully designed reward function makes programming fog tasks much easier. This is a strong, flexible, and scalable solution that works well in real-world computing environments.

title: An efficient Low overshoot Driving Circuit For High-performance XPU Power Supply
authors: Xiaohuan You, Shaowei Zhen, Guanzhi Chen, Wentao Kang, Guanting Liu, Zuyue Pang, Ziyi Cui, Shuhai Chen, Chi Zhang, Bo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents an overshoot Feedback Gate Driver (OFGD) for Driver-MOSFET (DrMOS) applications in xPU power delivery systems. The proposed architecture employs real-time switching node voltage peak detection to dynamically regulate the gate charging current of high-side power MOSFETs, effectively constraining voltage overshoot within predetermined safe limits. This adaptive control mechanism enables the utilization of power MOSFETs with reduced breakdown voltage specifications while maintaining the same area, thereby achieving lower on-resistance for enhanced power efficiency and improved device reliability. Fabricated in a 180nm BCD process, the OFGD demonstrates superior performance in a DrMOS implementation operating with 4-7.6 V input, 0.9 V output, and 90 A maximum load current. Experimental results show 0.2%-0.65% light-load efficiency improvement and 40% overshoot voltage reduction across the entire load range compared to conventional fixed-drive solutions.

title: Collaborative Optimization of 2-DOF PI Parameters for PMSM Based on Visualization
authors: Yiliang Luo, Hua Wang, Liangliang Chen, Xiaoguang Jin, Jiaju Wu, Peng Xu, Futao Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To achieve high-performance speed control of permanent magnet synchronous motors, this paper addresses the problems of large overshoot and weak load disturbance resistance in the speed response process of traditional PI speed controllers. A multi-objective optimization control strategy for a two-degree-of-freedom PI speed control system based on multi-dimensional visualization methods is proposed. This strategy takes the overshoot of the speed response, the adjustment time, and the recovery time after a step disturbance of the load torque as the optimization objectives. Multidimensional visualization technology is used to perform multi-objective optimization on the key parameters of the two-degree- of-freedom PI controller, thereby obtaining the optimal feasible regions of the proportional coefficient, integral coefficient, and gain. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through simulation. The simulation and experimental results show that the control algorithm proposed in this paper exhibits good stability, small overshoot, short adjustment time, and strong anti-interference ability in speed control.

title: An Improved DVM-PLL Structure Considering Large Disturbance
authors: Yazhao Yang, Li Zhang, Qi Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To against the variation of grid voltage amplitude, dividing voltage magnitude phase-locked loop (DVM-PLL) is widely used due to high amplitude normalization accuracy. However, conventional DVM-PLL fail to track the grid phase under large disturbances due to the nonlinearity. Thus, this letter proposes an improved DVM-PLL structure in the view of large disturbance. The improvement is achieved by reconstruction of the nonlinear adaptive gain, lead to a significant increase of the dynamic response during large disturbance. Theoretical analysis and experimental validate the effectiveness of the proposed structure.

title: Improved DQ-Current Decoupling Control for Single-Stage EV OBCs Considering DAB Outer Mode Operation
authors: Zhenfeng Qiu, Xu Yang, Wenjie Du, Jingyi Wang, Wenjie Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Single-stage on-board chargers (OBCs) have emerged as a research hotspot in EV charging systems due to their high-efficiency energy conversion and superior power density, yet their development is constrained by grid-side harmonic issues and control complexity. This paper proposes an improved DQ-current decoupling control strategy based on the power transfer mathematical model of matrix-converter-based single-stage OBCs, leveraging dual active bridge (DAB) outer mode characteristics. Compared to conventional methods, the proposed approach remarkably reduces grid current THD from 6.05% to 1.41% under rated conditions while achieving significant harmonic suppression across wide load ranges. Furthermore, implementation in an interleaved totem-pole single-stage OBC demonstrates superior THD performance throughout operational loads versus traditional methods. The implementation in both topologies shows considerable improvement of current THD and a hardware prototype is built for further experimental validation.

title: Modified Series Active Power Filter for L-type Single-phase Bridge Inverter
authors: Xiaochen Zhu, Shuang Xu, Haitham Elmasry, Guichen Zhang, Liuchen Chang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In single-phase bridge inverters, low DC voltage utilization results in high total harmonic distortion (THD) in AC current when filtering parameters are held constant. This high THD forces conventional L-filter designs to use higher inductance to meet standards, which in turn increases system volume and cost. This paper presents a modified series active power filter (APF) that mitigates current distortion without increasing inductance. Compared to the previous series APF, the modified topology borrows the idea of cascaded H-bridge structure, eliminating the need for an extra AC source. This paper discusses the operating principle of the modified series APF, highlighting its difference from conventional cascaded H-bridge modulation techniques. Simulation results show that the proposed APF reduces the required inductance by over 50%— from 3mH to 1.4mH—while maintaining comparable current THD performance.

title: Control Strategy of VSC-HVDC System based on Frequency Trajectory Planning
authors: Li Liu, Jinhao Xu, Tian Yuan Duan, Xupeng Wang, Liansong Xiong, Qiushuo Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In the voltage sourced converter based high voltage direct current (VSC-HVDC) system, the continuous increase of the proportion of new energy leads to the reduction of the system equivalent inertia level, and the frequency regulation faces serious challenges. The existing converter frequency regulation strategy relies on the time-varying inertia/damping parameters of the system and the random disturbances that are difficult to measure and fail to achieve a safe and economical frequency regulation effect. In this article, we propose a VSC-HVDC system frequency regulation (FR) strategy based on Frequency Trajectory Planning (FTP), which adds FR dead zones and safety margins and compares the system frequency with the planned frequency trajectory in real time. When the system frequency exceeds the safety range composed of the planning frequency trajectory and the rated frequency of the system, the converter provides additional FR energy, which not only guarantees the stability of the system frequency but also reduces the cost of FR. Finally, the advancement and economy of the FTP are verified by simulation.

title: Transformer-Enhanced Indonesian Cryptocurrency Sentiment Analysis for Social Media Trading Prediction
authors: Sabar Aritonang Rajagukguk
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Indonesia represents the world's fourth-largest population with rapidly growing cryptocurrency adoption, yet investor behavior patterns remain understudied in sentiment-driven trading prediction models. This study addresses the critical gap by developing the first comprehensive sentiment analysis framework specifically designed for Indonesian cryptocurrency discourse on social media platforms. We collected and analyzed 2.1 million Indonesian-language tweets from cryptocurrency communities over 18 months (January 2023-June 2024), employing a hybrid transformer architecture combining IndoBERT with bidirectional LSTM networks for enhanced sentiment extraction. The model leverages IndoBERT, specifically tailored for Bahasa Indonesia linguistic features including morphological complexity and semantic ambiguity, integrated with financial time series analysis for trading signal generation. Results demonstrate 89.3% sentiment classification accuracy and 76.8% directional price prediction accuracy for Bitcoin, significantly outperforming baseline models by 12.4% in F1-score. The framework successfully identified sentiment-driven market movements with 0.82 precision for bullish signals and 0.79 for bearish signals. This research contributes the first Indonesian-specific cryptocurrency sentiment lexicon, a novel hybrid architecture for multilingual financial sentiment analysis, and demonstrates how localized sentiment models can enhance social mediadriven trading strategies in emerging markets.

title: Sparse Gaussian Markov Modeling for Robust and Trustworthy Unknown Cyber Defense
authors: Chao Zha, Tian Liu, Chungang Lin, Bing Bai, Ruyun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Network intrusion detection systems (NIDS) are essential for enhancing network security by detecting and responding to attacks promptly. NIDS based on artificial intelligence (AI) offer better adaptability to unknown (out-of-distribution (OOD)) attacks compared to traditional anomaly detection methods. However, many existing AI-based NIDS directly adopt methods from other domains without adequately considering OOD data, which often leads to severe overfitting in trained classifiers and insufficient model reliability and robustness. This issue largely arises because most approaches focus solely on the relationship between individual features and predictions while neglecting the interactions among features, resulting in overfitting and over-confident predictions. Moreover, the retention of certain weak parameters may cause the model to maintain artificially high confidence when confronted with OOD data. To address this, we propose a Gaussian Markov Random Field that better captures traffic data distribution and feature correlations. Furthermore, our GMRF-based classification model uses sparsification with regularization L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> to remove weak parameters. L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> regularization counts the number of non-zero parameters and penalizes model complexity, thereby forcing insignificant weights to become exactly zero and improving generalization. This prevents high prediction probabilities for unknown attacks and effectively distinguishes them from benign traffic. Finally, we evaluated our method in the CICIDS-2017 and CSE-CICIDS-2018 datasets, achieving recall rates of 57% and 77% for unknown attacks while maintaining a recall of more than 95% for known traffic. Our approach significantly outperforms existing SOTA methods, and performance tests on a low-specification machine show a low detection latency of approximately 130 microseconds, making it suitable for real-time applications.

title: Extended State Observer-Based Predefined Time Composite Anti-Disturbance Control for Hydraulic Cutting Arm
authors: Liyan Liu, Gang Shen, Wei Wang, Qing Guo, Yuxing Peng, Xiang Li, Qing-Guo Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: During tunnel excavation, designing a trajectory tracking controller based solely on a pure rigid body model is insufficient to achieve optimal section forming quality. Because when the cutting head is subjected to complex coal-rock loads, the presence of hydraulic oil elasticity will inevitably arise system vibration, further causing roadway over-excavation, ultimately deteriorating section quality. Hence, a rigid-flexible coupling model of the cutting mechanism, considering hydraulic system stiffness and damping, is first established in this article, and it is further decomposed into a slow-varying subsystem (SVS) and boundary layer subsystem (BLS) using singular perturbation theory for independent design of controllers. Secondly, a new time scale function is proposed for designing a predefined-time (PT) extended state observer, achieving estimation of the disturbance term suffered by the cutting system within a PT for the first time. Then, a PT dynamic surface controller with tunable convergence time is developed for SVS. A PT state constraint controller is designed for BLS to suppress vibration acceleration. Ultimately, through comparative experiments on a roadheader prototype, it is verified that the constructed composite controller can effectively attenuate system vibration while tracking cutting trajectory within a PT, thereby significantly improving section quality and beneficially extending key component lifespan.

title: Prompt-Image-Caption Consistency for AI-Generated Image Quality Assessment
authors: Wen Sun, Chaofeng Chen, Liang Liao, Weisi Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: With the advancement of generative AI, generative models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and diffusion-based models have significantly enhanced the capabilities in generating photorealistic images. Despite significant progress in AI-Generated Images (AIGIs), the evaluation of AIGI quality remains underexplored in multiple aspects, including AI artifacts, unnatural content, and the availability of input text prompts. However, existing methods are insufficient to capture both feature representations and correlations between image and text for AIGI quality assessment in aspects of perceptual image quality and text-to-image alignment. To address this, we proposed a novel Prompt-Image-Caption Consistency (PICC) framework, designed to adapt a pre-trained vision-language model for AIGI quality assessment by considering both perceptual image quality and text-to-image alignment at the same time. This framework effectively explores the prompt-image-caption triplet by adapting image embedding and multimodal embedding via Quality-Aware Attention to capture quality-aware features and calculating consistency scores to build the correlations among the prompt, image, and caption. Additionally, we propose a multilevel strategy to integrate both local and global information from multiple prompt-image-caption triplets, further enhancing prediction performance. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, including AIGIQA-20K and AGIQA-3K, demonstrate that the proposed PICC achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to baseline methods. The code will be made publicly available.

title: The Role of Reinforcement Learning in Production Control: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: J. Schneider, C. Pfannschmidt, P. Nyhuis, M. Schmidt
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The expansion of product portfolios, the reduction of product life cycles and the volatility of markets pose significant challenges for production systems and their control. Concurrently, these trends present a challenge in achieving an optimal balance between logistical performance and internal company costs. The discordance between the escalating demands of customers on logistics performance, exemplified by metrics such as throughput time and schedule reliability, and the cost-driven corporate objective of minimizing work-in-process and maximizing machine utilization, is becoming increasingly challenging to reconcile. The application of reinforcement learning (RL) is a significant machine learning (ML) approach for overcoming these challenges. In comparison with other ML approaches, RL facilitates direct interaction with production systems and is consequently well suited for controlling them in operational use. Despite the extensive body of research on RL approaches for production control tasks, there is a paucity of literature addressing the influence of these approaches on key logistical targets for logistics performance and costs. The article’s added value derives from the systematic literature review it conducts, which provides researchers and practitioners with an overview of how existing RL approaches influence central logistical target variables. Furthermore, it highlights blind spots in the research landscape. The results indicate the existence of a substantial number of approaches; however, their distribution across control tasks is disproportionate. Furthermore, it is evident that there are distinct discrepancies in the classification system with respect to the impact on logistical target variables.

title: I've Got Proof! Dataset-Specific Watermarking for Detecting Excessive Dataset Usage in Text-to-Image Diffusion Model Fine-Tuning
authors: Zhihui Huang, Xiangli Xiao, Yushu Zhang, Yuming Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Currently, text-to-image diffusion models, which exhibit remarkable proficiency in image generation, have prompted the emergence of diverse fine-tuning methodologies due to the considerable resource demands entailed in their training. Simultaneously, apprehensions have arisen regarding the excessive utilization of open-source datasets for fine-tuning the models. Therefore, intellectual property protection for such datasets is important and necessary. In this regard, watermarking is a commonly employed method. However, the existing watermarking methods fail to establish an accurate correspondence between the watermarked dataset and the malicious model, which may lead to erroneous accusations against the model. In this paper, we propose a dataset-specific watermarking method, DSW, to detect excessive usage of protected datasets by suspect diffusion models fine-tuned using LoRA. DSW can establish a one-to-one correspondence between the watermarked dataset and the malicious model, thereby enhancing the accuracy of intellectual property verification. We simultaneously train an encoder and a decoder based on a bi-level optimization strategy. The encoder embeds a watermark image, which symbolizes the dataset owner's identity, into the dataset samples to yield a watermarked dataset. For a text-to-image diffusion model fine-tuned on this watermarked dataset, the decoder extracts the watermark from the generated image. Comprehensive experiments validate the availability, effectiveness, and stealthiness of DSW. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/hzhwis/DSW</uri>.

title: Joint Trajectory and RIS Phase Shift Design for RIS-assisted SAGIN: A FL-MADRL Approach
authors: Wendenda Nathanael Kabore, Rong-Terng Juang, Hsin-Piao Lin, Ming-Cheng Tsai, Konpal Shaukat Ali, Belayneh Abebe Tesfaw
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Wireless systems must deliver ubiquitous QoS for massive IoT, but in urban areas, the blockages severely degrade satellite links. We consider a RIS-enabled space–air–ground integrated network (SAGIN) where a moving LEO satellite provides coverage and multiple drone base stations (DBSs) act as mobile relays to restore connectivity for ground users. Some Reflecting Intelligent Surfaces (RISs) panels, mounted on buildings, create controllable reflect paths to overcome non-line-of-sight conditions. A key challenge is that DBS's 3D trajectory simultaneously affects the satellite–DBS and the DBS-RIS–user access links, while also determining their energy consumption. We formulate a joint optimization problem to maximize the communication coverage, system throughput, and fairness under mobility, energy, and connectivity constraints. To address the issue, we develop a Federated Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (FL-MADRL) method that coordinates multiple agents: each agent learns online from local experience, while periodic federated aggregation shares policies without exchanging raw data. Simulations in an urban scenario demonstrate that the proposed approach maintains uninterrupted service in terms of coverage, throughput, and fairness while reducing DBS energy consumption compared to conventional convex approaches and non-federated baselines, indicating its suitability for RIS-assisted SAGIN systems.

title: GSO-SLAM: Bidirectionally Coupled Gaussian Splatting and Direct Visual Odometry
authors: Jiung Yeon, Seongbo Ha, Hyeonwoo Yu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: We propose GSO-SLAM, a real-time monocular dense SLAM system that leverages Gaussian scene representation. Unlike existing methods that couple tracking and mapping with a unified scene, incurring computational costs, or loosely integrate them with well-structured tracking frameworks, introducing redundancies, our method bidirectionally couples Visual Odometry (VO) and Gaussian Splatting (GS). Specifically, our approach formulates joint optimization within an Expectation-Maximization (EM) framework, enabling the simultaneous refinement of VO-derived semi-dense depth estimates and the GS representation without additional computational overhead. Moreover, we present Gaussian Splat Initialization, which utilizes image information, keyframe poses, and pixel associations from VO to produce close approximations to the final Gaussian scene, thereby eliminating the need for heuristic methods. Through extensive experiments, we validate the effectiveness of our method, showing that it not only operates in real time but also achieves state-of-the-art geometric/photometric fidelity of the reconstructed scene and tracking accuracy.

title: Efficient Modeling of Quasi-Periodic Structures Using a Physics-Informed Deep Operator Network
authors: Shutong Qi, Costas D. Sarris
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced physics-informed deep operator network (PI-DON) for efficient and accurate electromagnetic modeling of quasi-periodic structures. While PI-DONs can efficiently model practical three-dimensional electromagnetic geometries, their application to electrically large and multiscale structures is limited by GPU memory constraints and the need for global mesh refinement to resolve fine geometric features. To address these limitations, we integrate the Dey–Mittra (DM) subcell method, domain decomposition (DD), and adaptive loss selection into a unified framework, referred to as the DD-PI-DON. The DD-PI-DON addresses three key challenges: accurate modeling of fine geometric details, scalability to electrically large domains, and faster convergence during training. In addition, the DD-PI-DON demonstrates strong generalizability, enabling the reuse of a single trained model for geometries with statistical variations or defects with minimal additional training. Numerical results on finite and defect frequency-selective surfaces (FSSs) and Huygens’ metasurfaces demonstrate that the proposed DD-PI-DON provides accuracy comparable to that of finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) solvers while reducing runtime by at least one order of magnitude, even when training time is included.

title: T-Mamba: A Unified Framework with Long-Range Dependency in Dual-Domain for 2D & 3D Tooth Segmentation
authors: Jing Hao, Yonghui Zhu, Lei He, Moyun Liu, James Kit Hon Tsoi, Kuo Feng Hung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Tooth segmentation is an essential step in digital dental diagnosis and treatment planning, playing a crucial role across various dental specialties. Despite its importance, this process is fraught with challenges due to the high noise and low contrast inherent in 2D and 3D dental radiographic images. Both convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformers have shown promise in tooth image segmentation, yet each method has limitations in handling long-range dependencies and computational complexity. To address this issue, this paper introduces T-Mamba, integrating frequency-based features and shared bi-positional encoding into vision mamba to address limitations in modeling global features efficiently. Besides, we designed a gate selection unit to integrate two features in the spatial domain and one feature in the frequency domain adaptively. T-Mamba is the first to integrate frequency-based features into vision mamba, offering the flexibility to process both 2D and 3D tooth image data without the need for separate modules. Additionally, the TED, a large-scale public 2D dental X-ray dataset, has been presented in this paper. Extensive experiments demonstrated that T-Mamba achieved new SOTA performance on both the 3D dental CBCT dataset and our proposed 2D TED dataset. The code and models are publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/isbrycee/T-Mamba</uri>.

title: Bimodal Commuting Alignment Network for Zero-shot Recognition
authors: Mengyu Gao, Qiulei Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Zero-shot recognition has attracted increasing attention for recognizing unseen classes by aligning image visual features and class semantic features. Although various visual and semantic feature types have been explored in literature, e.g., semantic-unaware/aware visual features and attribute/name-based semantic features, most existing methods are tailored to specific bimodal feature pairs, leading to degraded performance when generalized to other feature combinations. To address this issue, we analyze the commonly-used visual and semantic features and reveal that single-modal features generally contain either redundant or deficient information, and bimodal features generally suffer from cross-modal inconsistency. Accordingly, we propose a bimodal commuting alignment network, called BCA-Net, which could accommodate various bimodal feature combinations. Specifically, an entropy-based perception module is first designed to learn bimodal prompts by strengthening discriminative visual-channel features and semantic tokens respectively. Then, a bimodal complementing module is proposed to refine the learned bimodal prompts for complementing inconsistent information across feature modalities. Extensive experimental results on 13 public datasets demonstrate that BCA-Net consistently outperforms 20 state-of-the-art methods by utilizing 4 bimodal feature combinations in most cases, highlighting its strong generalizability beyond designs tailored to specific bimodal pairs.

title: Model Parameter Free Observer based-Robust Predictive Current Control for PMSM Drives
authors: Xiaofeng Chai, Yue Sun, Xiaoyu Bian, Qiang Hao, Haitao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The existing research indicates that the model predictive current control is sensitive against motor parameter mismatches. For better parameter robustness, various model-free predictive control (MFPC) have been proposed. Generally, MFPC relies on the measured current to update current predictions. For stable operation and satisfactory performance, immunity against sensing noises is crucial especially for a inverter-fed motor drive due to electromagnetic interference. In this paper, a observer is developed for current difference prediction without using motor parameters. The observer gains are online updated based on the estimation error. Due to filtering effect and online adaptability of the observer, the system is robust against measurement noises and motor parameter variations. Both offline s imulation and real-time experimental tests on a permanent magnet synchronous motor drive platform justify good performance and parameter robustness of the proposed MFPC.

title: HGC-Herd: Efficient Heterogeneous Graph Condensation via Representative Node Herding
authors: Fuyan Ou, Siqi Ai, Yulin Hu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Heterogeneous graph neural networks (HGNNs) have demonstrated strong capability in modeling complex semantics across multi-type nodes and relations. However, their scalability to large-scale graphs remains challenging due to structural redundancy and high-dimensional node features. Existing graph condensation approaches, such as GCond, are primarily developed for homogeneous graphs and rely on gradient matching, resulting in considerable computational, memory, and optimization overhead. We propose HGC-Herd, a training-free condensation framework that generates compact yet informative heterogeneous graphs while maintaining both semantic and structural fidelity. HGC-Herd integrates lightweight feature propagation to encode multi-hop relational context and employs a class-wise herding mechanism to identify representative nodes per class, producing balanced and discriminative subsets for downstream learning tasks. Extensive experiments on ACM, DBLP, and Freebase validate that HGC-Herd attains comparable or superior accuracy to full-graph training while markedly reducing both runtime and memory consumption. These results underscore its practical value for efficient and scalable heterogeneous graph representation learning.

title: SurveiLens: Natural Language-Based Moment Retrieval for Surveillance Video Analysis
authors: Mohammad Owais, Ariba Siddiqui, Ayan Alam, Saad Ahmed Siddiqui, Saif Ali
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Closed Circuit Television (CCTV) systems generate vast amounts of video footage daily, yet the process of manually reviewing these recordings remains highly inefficient. Security personnel often spend hours, sifting through long duration videos to locate relevant incidents, a process that delays crucial decision making and the delivery of justice. This work proposes an automated solution that eliminates the need for manual video sifting by enabling natural language-based video retrieval. Our approach leverages a sophisticated processing pipeline consisting of video frame sampling, image captioning, semantic similarity computation, and temporal localization to extract precise timestamps or video segments corresponding to a given natural language query. This system allows investigators to describe an event and rapidly retrieve the relevant portion of CCTV footage from hours of recordings. By significantly reducing the time and human effort required to analyze surveillance videos, our method can accelerate investigations and facilitate timely delivery of justice.

title: Laugh, Engage, and Remember through Online Humor Advertising on Social Media
authors: Aryo Bismo, Hartiwi Prabowo, Engr Noe Enriquez, Yuniarty, Jenny Ohliati
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study examines the effect of humorous advertising on brand recognition, with positive electronic word-of-mouth (E-WOM) serving as a mediating variable. The research focuses on Generation Z consumers, a segment often characterized by short attention spans when exposed to lengthy advertising content, making it increasingly challenging for brands to capture and retain their attention. In the current digital era, intense business competition and the proliferation of similar new brands have further complicated efforts to achieve strong brand recognition. To address these challenges, survey data were collected from 297 respondents aged 19–27 and analyzed using the SEM-PLS method. The findings reveal that humorous advertising has a significant positive effect on brand recognition, both directly and indirectly through positive E-WOM. Moreover, the mediating role of positive E-WOM underscores its importance as a mechanism through which humorous advertising enhances consumer perceptions and brand visibility. These results contribute to a deeper understanding of how humor-based strategies and consumer-driven communication can be leveraged to strengthen brand recognition in highly competitive digital markets.

title: Fast Action Generation via Knowledge Distillation with Flow Matching for Social Navigation
authors: Yuki Tomita, Kohei Matsumoto, Yuki Hyodo, Kazuto Nakashima, Ryo Kurazume
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Mobile robot navigation in dynamic environments that contain pedestrians is one of the key challenges in the development of autonomous mobile service robots. This field, known as social navigation, has seen significant research progress using reinforcement learning approaches. In recent years, numerous diffusion-based reinforcement learning methods capable of generating diverse actions have been proposed. However, compared to conventional reinforcement learning approaches, the diffusion model’s slow generation process presents a significant barrier to real-time processing. To address this, we propose a method for knowledge distillation of conditional diffusion models by combining Gaussian Prior with Flow Matching to enable faster action generation in dynamic environments. Experiments using a crowd navigation benchmark in simulation environments demonstrate that a significant reduction of the time required for action generation is possible while maintaining nearly the same performance as teacher models.

title: Action Transition Recognition using ST-GCN for Worker Following in Agricultural Support Robots
authors: Go Oya, Akihisa Ohya, Takashi Tsubouchi, Rui Fukui, Ayanori Yorozu
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In recent years, the increasing labor burden in Japanese agriculture hs become a serious issue, driving the development of robots to assist in transporting harvested crops. This study proposes a method that recognizes the action transitions of agricultural workers and enables smooth transport assistance by utilizing three-dimensional skeletal information obtained from RGB-D images. Specifically, we employ Spatial Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (ST-GCN) to detect the transition from "harvesting" to "loading." The recognition results are used to control the robot so that it approaches the worker before the loading action begins. The proposed method introduces a new labeling scheme tailored to harvesting and crop-loading motions, thereby improving recognition performance with ST-GCN. Evaluation experiments verified its generalization capability to different harvesting postures and workers, demonstrating an 18.7% improvement in action transition recognition accuracy compared with conventional methods. Furthermore, in robot-following experiments with the proposed method implemented, we confirmed that the system could both recognize action transitions and adjust the target following distance before the worker started loading. These results show that an ST-GCN specialized for agricultural tasks can effectively recognize harvesting action transitions, contributing to reducing the burden on workers during crop transportation.

title: Design of Load-Mismatch-Resilient Doherty PA Using Multiload Dual-State Optimization
authors: Zhengcong Xie, Jing Xia, Wa Kong, Wence Zhang, Chao Yu, Xiao-Wei Zhu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a multiload dual-state matching optimization method for load-mismatch-resilient Doherty power amplifiers (DPAs). The combination impedances under different external loads are analyzed, along with the corresponding load characteristics of the carrier and peaking amplifiers in both the saturation and back-off states. Then, the carrier and peaking output matching networks are optimized using the dual-state optimization under external load variations within 2:1 VSWR. For verification, a 2.8-GHz load-mismatch-resilient DPA is designed and measured. The results show that, under a 50-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> load and the loads for a 2:1 VSWR, the proposed DPA achieves a saturated output power of 43.4–43.7 dBm with a corresponding drain efficiency (DE) of 56.3%–65.9% and a DE of 50.2%–55.1% at 6-dB back-off output power. In addition, with a 20-MHz-modulated signal and digital predistortion (DPD), the DPA achieves an adjacent channel leakage ratio (ACLR) better than −47.2 dBc.

title: Distributed Flow Shop Scheduling for Prefabricated Components with Soft Time Windows and Resource Constraints
authors: Mengting Li, Baigang Du
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This study investigates the Distributed Precast Concrete Flow Shop Scheduling Problem with Worker Constraints (DPCFSPW), considering soft delivery time windows and worker-limited production requirements.
