A multi-objective scheduling model is developed to minimize the makespan, maximum cost of factories, and maximum dissatisfaction of workers. Correspondingly, an Hybrid Memetic Algorithm (HMA) is proposed, integrating the framework of NSGA-II. A two-layer chromosome representation based on jobs and workers is designed, and four neighborhood search operators tailored to this representation are developed to strengthen the local search capability of the HMA and accelerate convergence. Thirty benchmarks are constructed to evaluate the performance of the HMA. Extensive experiments show that, in most cases, the HMA performs better than NSGA-II and ABC, demonstrating its effectiveness in solving the DPCFSPW.

title: A Survey on Quantum Resistant Dead Drop Communication System
authors: B. Ananth, SHIVRAM ALIAS VIGNESH S, P.V.S VIJAY PRASANTH, DEEPAK RAJ R
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital communication has brought great convenience, but it also presents serious security and privacy issues. Traditional secure messaging platforms like Signal, WhatsApp, and Telegram offer strong end-to-end encryption. However, they still expose important metadata, use persistent accounts, and may be at risk from future quantum threats. Meanwhile, new methods like mixnets, secure dropboxes, and temporary messaging apps provide some solutions for anonymity and deniability, but they do not fully integrate with post-quantum cryptographic standards. This survey looks at privacy-focused communication systems, emphasizing metadata reduction, temporary message exchange, and resistance to quantum threats. We review classic cryptographic protocols, metadata protection strategies, and ongoing efforts to integrate NIST-standardized post-quantum algorithms such as Kyber and Dilithium into communication platforms. Building on this foundation, we present the Quantum-Resistant Dead Drop Communication System (QRDDCS) as a unique approach that combines the dead drop model, inspired by espionage, with quantum-safe cryptography, anonymous one-time message delivery, and automatic data destruction. Through a comparative analysis, this paper points out the shortcomings of current solutions and shows how QRDDCS offers a forward-thinking model for secure, anonymous, and quantum-resistant communication in high-risk situations.

title: Enhancing Accuracy in Heart Disease Prediction Using GAN
authors: Abhinay Verma, Nikita Singh, Dharmender Singh Kushwaha
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Heart diseases remain a leading cause of mortality worldwide, emphasizing the need for accurate and early prediction models to support clinical decision making. Traditional machine learning models have been widely applied in the prediction of heart strokes, but often face challenges due to limited and imbalanced medical datasets, which can compromise predictive performance. This study investigates the use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), particularly Conditional Tabular GANs (CTGAN), to overcome these limitations by generating realistic synthetic data. We first developed a machine learning model trained solely on real medical datasets, achieving an accuracy of $\mathbf{8 8 \%}$. Recognizing the impact of data scarcity on performance, we improved the training data set with synthetic samples generated by CTGAN, resulting in a notable accuracy improvement of 100% when evaluated with real test data. This significant performance improvement highlights the potential of GAN-based data enhancement to mitigate data set limitations, improve model generalization, and improve prediction accuracy in heart disease prediction tasks. Our findings underscore the role of synthetic data generation in addressing critical challenges in medical data analysis, such as data imbalance and limited sample size. The study concludes that integrating GAN-generated data can advance machine learning-based predictive models, providing a robust framework for developing more accurate and reliable heart disease prediction systems.

title: A Novel Blockchain Framework for Agriculture: Enhancing Security and Transparency with Weighted Bidding and MBP-AgroBFT
authors: V Balaji Vijayan, Meenakshi Sharma, Jhansi Rani Mettu, Kotteeswaran Rangasamy, R Thiagarajan, Gunasundari B
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The proposed work examines the disruptive power of blockchain technology in Indian agricultural supply chains for greater transparency, security, and efficiency. The main goals are to overcome data integrity issues, transaction security problems, and effective producer-consumer relationships. In order to do so, a strong blockchain infrastructure is developed with SHA-based cryptography, quick block-level access to data, and smooth producer-provider integration. A Weighted Bid Analysis Algorithm is proposed to validate consumer bids, enhance decision-making, and secure market interactions. To double-check security and scalability, the research recommends MBP-AgroBFT, a Byzantine Fault Tolerance scheme modified for agriculture. MBP-AgroBFT provides increased resistance to Sybil, Eclipse, DDoS, and collusion attacks, achieving 5-7% more security, scalability, and energy efficiency compared to PBFT and MBPBFT. Experimental findings verify that the suggested system guarantees traceability, makes transactions secure, and empowers agri-stakeholders, making blockchain's real value for sustainable and reliable agri-supply chains evident.

title: Exploring Energy Arbitrage Opportunities for Battery Energy Storage Systems in United States and India
authors: Shri Ram Vaishya, Partik Kumar, Mandhir Sahni
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This paper explores the potential for energy arbitrage opportunities for Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESSs) in day-ahead and real-time electricity markets managed by various ISOs/RTOs in the United States and power exchanges in India. It examines regulatory frameworks, market structures, and technological advancements that impact BESS deployments in both countries. Through the analysis of historical data, market trends, and economic models, the research evaluates the profitability of BESS in the energy arbitrage market. Energy arbitrage revenues are compared for CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, NYISO, PJM, and SPP from the US electricity market and IEX from the Indian electricity market based on the last 10 years' historical energy prices. The effect of BESS duration and number of cycles on the energy arbitrage revenues are also comprehensively studied. By comparing the two distinct energy markets, the study aims to demonstrate the practical and effective deployment of BESS for energy arbitrage in diverse economic environments. The findings contribute to understanding how BESS can facilitate the transition towards sustainable energy systems, offering valuable implications for policymakers, energy investors, and grid operators in both the United States and India, and potentially informing strategies for other regions globally.

title: Multimodal Signal Fusion via Transformer for Underwater Thruster Fault Diagnosis
authors: Anchi Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper presents a cross-modal Transformer framework for intelligent fault diagnosis of underwater thrusters. The model simultaneously processes three physical modalities— control voltage, rotational speed, and current—through convolutional embedding and attention-based temporal modeling. A physics-based simulation platform is developed to generate multimodal data for seven representative fault types. On this dataset, the proposed method achieves 100% classification accuracy and outperforms CNN, LSTM, and TCN baselines by up to 28.6%, while maintaining a compact configuration. These results demonstrate that cross-modal fusion effectively enhances inter-sensor correlation and fault discriminability, providing a concise and robust solution for multimodal time-series analysis of underwater thrusters.

title: scikit-robot: An Integrated Framework for Solving Structural Challenges in Dynamic Modeling for Reconfigurable Robotics
authors: Iori Yanokura, Kento Matsuo, Hirokazu Ishida, Sousuke Morita, Aoi Nakane, Naoya Yamaguchi, Tasuku Makabe, Kei Okada
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Modular reconfigurable robots offer a high degree of flexibility through dynamic hardware morphology changes, but their potential has been constrained by software limitations, particularly the static design philosophy of URDF, the de facto standard format. This paper identifies three fundamental research gaps that hinder the development of reconfigurable robots: (1) the disconnect between programmatic "decomposition" of models and runtime "composition," (2) the lack of standard mechanisms for updating kinematic topology at runtime, and (3) the limited semantic expressiveness of the URDF format itself. To address these challenges, we propose scikit-robot, a unified framework that integrates multiple components for reconfigurable robotics. The framework establishes a design-to-deployment workflow through: (1) an integrated URDF toolchain handling decomposition, reconfiguration, and mesh optimization, (2) a dynamic root transformation algorithm enabling flexible physical connections, and (3) a comprehensive hash-based model management system providing asset identity verification and supporting simulation-to-hardware integration. Experimental validation on multiple real modular robot systems demonstrates stable operation and direct model transfer from simulation to physical hardware, confirming the effectiveness of our integrated workflow approach. scikit-robot provides researchers and developers with a practical foundation for dynamic and adaptive robotic systems.

title: Design and Control Technology of Four-Switch DC Converter for Battery Energy Storage System
authors: Jieming Huang, Xiaorong Huang, Zhiliang Cai, Xiquan Li, Rijing Lai, Xiaotian Qin
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Energy storage represents the crucial approach in addressing the high-proportion application of new energy. Among various energy storage technologies, battery energy storage system gains significant attention due to its advantages like flexibility, convenience, and high energy density. Four-switch Buck-Boost converters are suitable for the DC transformation stage of the power conversion system, and the research on their design and control technology is key to improving efficiency. Based on the quadrilateral inductor current modulation strategy, this paper adds the constraint of minimizing operating loss in the soft-switching state and proposes a method for calculating the switching time with optimal operating efficiency. Secondly, a software-based negative current control scheme and a double closed-loop control system were designed. Finally, an experimental prototype with a switching frequency of 100kHz and a rated power of 600W was built to verify the effectiveness of the theoretical analysis and control strategy.

title: Nature-Inspired Optimization of FO-LQR for the Control of Rotary Double Inverted Pendulum
authors: Mohammad Saif, Vijander Singh, Asha Rani
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This paper explores the control and stabilization of upper pendulum of the Rotary Double Inverted Pendulum (RDIP) utilizing a Fractional Order Linear Quadratic Regulator (FOLQR) controller optimized through Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO). The RDIP, a quintessential nonlinear and unstable system, presents huge difficulties in keeping up with the equilibrium of its double pendulums. By optimizing the FO-LQR controller parameters with GWO, the aim of work is to enhance the control accuracy and stability of the RDIP system. The simulations are conducted on MATLAB and results are compared with the performance of the GWO optimized FO-LQR controller against conventionally LQR controllers. The results illustrate that the GWO-optimized FO-LQR controller significantly improves the system's response, minimizing the trajectory tracking error and offering faster stabilization, with reduced overshoot, and lower steady-state error. Intelligent optimization algorithms with classical control methods have the potential to achieve superior performance in dynamic and unstable environments.

title: Design and Implementation of a Hall Effect-Based Pressure Monitoring System with IoT Connectivity
authors: Praveen Maurya, Anamika Lata, Siva Ramakrishna Pillutla
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: A Hall Effect-based pressure transmitting system is proposed in this paper. The system consists of a primary sensing and data transmission unit that captures pressure variations and transmits the data wirelessly for remote monitoring. The sensing mechanism utilises a Bourdon tube arrangement with a magnet fixed at its top. As pressure is applied, the magnet's displacement is detected using a Hall probe sensor. Since the raw output of the Hall sensor is in the millivolt range, it is first amplified using an instrumentation amplifier and then processed through a signal conditioning circuit to make it suitable for digital conversion. The conditioned signal is fed into an Arduino board, which digitizes the signal and transmits it wirelessly using a Wi-Fi module to a remote server. This IoT-enabled architecture makes the system well-suited for real-time monitoring in applications where wired transmission is impractical or hazardous, such as in inflammable environments. The system is simple, cost-effective, and provides good linearity and repeatability with the applied pressure

title: Power Transmission Limiting Design of the Grid-Forming Converter Under Different SCRs
authors: Jiansong Zhang, Jing Liu, Zonghui Chen, Yangbin Ke, Huaiyuan Ma, Jianbo Cao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Under ultra-weak grid, the ac terminal voltage of the grid-forming converter can be effectively supported, even if the source voltage falls dramatically. Based on the transfer function of the active power versus load angle, the maximum active power transmission is limited, whereas the converter cannot sense it based on its ac terminal voltage. To solve this problem, the theoretical power transmission limit design procedure is proposed in this paper based on the prediction of the source voltage under different Short Circuit Ratios. Finally, the experimental verification is conducted based on a 105 kW TBEA grid-forming converter under different transient scenarios with SCR =1.

title: Planar Magnetic Integration for Three-phase LCL Filter Based on Y-type Magnetic Core and Planar Litz Wire
authors: Haiyan Liang, Yitao Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To address the issues of large volume and asymmetric inductance in traditional three-phase LCL filters, this paper proposes a planar magnetic integration scheme for three-phase LCL filters based on a planar Y-type symmetric magnetic core structure. Based on the magnetic circuit theory, the equivalent reluctance model and inductance expression for the Y-type core are established. Finite element simulations are utilized to verify the inductance balance, and saturation control capabilities, with an EIE-type magnetic core set up as a control group. The simulation results indicate that the designed Y-type structure exhibits significantly better three-phase inductance symmetry than the EIE-type magnetic core, and the Y-type core demonstrates a relatively weaker saturation trend. A Litz wire-type multilayer PCB winding structure is introduced, and a design method for planar Litz PCB windings aimed at high-frequency loss suppression is proposed.

title: A Zero-Input-Current-Ripple Quasi-Single-Stage High-Frequency Isolated Soft-Switching High-Power Factor Single-Phase Rectifier
authors: Xulong Zhang, Haichun Liu, Shaojun Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: A quasi-single-stage high-frequency isolated single-phase rectifier is proposed. The input side employs a dual boost converter with current-fed characteristics operating at complementary 50% duty cycles, theoretically achieving zero high-frequency current ripple on the AC input. Active clamping technology on the input side combined with quasi-resonant technology on the output side enables wide-range soft switching for all power switches. The circuit topology, operating principles, and characteristics are analyzed. Simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed circuit.

title: Adaptive Admittance Control of Robot Joints Based on LSTM Network Optimization
authors: Jiyu Sun, Liang Yan, Xiaoshan Gao, Hanzhi Hu, Keyi Liu, Gexin Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Compliant motion control of the collaborative robot is crucial for human-robot interaction. However, traditional admittance control relies on fixed parameters, which is difficult to cope with the influence of external disturbances and dynamic human-computer interaction forces, resulting in a decrease in tracking performance. In view of the above problems, this paper proposes a joint adaptive admittance control method based on LSTM network optimization. By constructing the parameter adaptive law under Lyapunov stability, the admittance parameters are adjusted online to enhance the robustness of the system. The long-term and short-term memory network ( LSTM ) is further used to model and predict the historical interaction torque and state sequence, compensate the system hysteresis and optimize the dynamic response of the parameters. The simulation results show that the proposed method significantly improves the trajectory tracking accuracy, suppresses the parameter fluctuation and response hysteresis, and improves the safety and adaptability of human-computer interaction under the premise of maintaining low stiffness and damping levels.

title: Partial Discharge Detection Method for Power Modules Based on Balanced Circuit
authors: Hao Sun, Lingyu Zhu, Yuntian Gao, Yingbin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In order to address the problem that the electromagnetic interference and the displacement current generated by the rising and falling edges of the square wave seriously interfere with the detection of partial discharge (PD) in high-voltage power modules under actual working conditions, resulting in inaccurate discharge detection, this paper proposes a partial discharge detection method for power modules based on balanced circuits. On the basis of ultra-high frequency (UHF) detection method, this method uses UHF signal to locate the discharge phase, extracts the high-frequency current transformer (HFCT) signal at the same time to obtain the discharge amplitude information, and reflects the severity of the discharge. The results indicate that this method significantly reduces the displacement current in HFCT detection and preserves more frequency bands of discharge signals.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Smart Detection and Mapping of Illegal Waste Using Satellite Imagery
authors: M Arunkumar, Harini S
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Ilegal waste disposal poses a critical threat to environmental sustainability, public health, and urban development. Traditional field-based monitoring methods are often labor-intensive, time-consuming, and spatially constrained, highlighting the need for automated, intelligent detection systems. This study proposes a hybrid deep learning framework for the automatic detection and mapping of illegal waste sites using high-resolution satellite imagery. The framework integrates three complementary modules: WasteVisionNet for spectral-spatial feature extraction, EcoSegNet for precise segmentation of heterogeneous waste regions, and MapFusion-X for spatio-temporal mapping through attention-based graph modeling. By combining convolutional, transformer, and temporal learning paradigms, the system effectively distinguishes waste accumulation zones from natural and built-up surroundings under varying illumination and terrain conditions. Experimental evaluation on multispectral datasets demonstrates superior accuracy, recall, and mean intersection-over-union compared with existing deep learning baselines. The generated geospatial waste maps provide actionable insights for environmental authorities, supporting early detection, efficient cleanup planning, and long-term monitoring. This work illustrates the potential of integrating hybrid deep learning with remote sensing as a scalable solution for environmental surveillance and sustainable waste management.

title: 3D Object Detection based on Semi-Supervised Learning in Complex Traffic Environments
authors: Ruoxu Xiao, Weiyi Yang, Da Xie, Salman Ijaz, Adam Rushworth
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Based on 3D Point Cloud Object Detection, this paper optimizes the Pillar Feature Net through a semi-supervised learning approach. This enhancement improves the model's ability to supervise and utilize unlabeled data, thereby equipping it with greater data comprehension capabilities and bolstering its adaptability to complex real-world scenarios. Additionally, the study employs a full attention feature representation encoder provided by the Transformer framework, followed by the substitution of VGG-16 with MobilenetV3. This reduction in model complexity accelerates the achievement of desired outcomes, making the model more suitable for real-time or resource-constrained scenarios. The optimization methods utilized in this paper not only improve the accuracy and efficiency of object detection but also positively impact the model's generalization ability and deployment practicality. Validation on the KITTI 3D public dataset results in an AP value of 81.77% for the hard detection difficulty level in the test set. Compared to the 75.46% achieved by the PointPillars model, the proposed method achieves an improvement of 6.31%.

title: Split-inductor implementation of a double-gain non-inverting buck-boost Y-Inverter
authors: Maria Kazakova, Alberto Castellazzi
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper is about the implementation of a modified four-switch buck-boost Y-Inverter, where each phase inductor is replaced by a split-inductor assembly, to achieve higher boost gain and better capability to operate from low-voltage input sources. The conventional split-inductor is designed with two inductors and three diodes and does not allow for bi-directional current conduction capability, which is however required by the Y-Inverter for full load range operation. In this work, the diodes are replaced by transistors allowing bi-directional current conduction capability. Circuit simulation is used for the design phase and experimental proof-of-concept demonstration is carried out on an all GaN HEMT based high-frequency inverter, using a single-duty control function and modulation.

title: An integrated solution for high turns ratio transformers suitable in 3D applications
authors: Keliang Chen, Xu Yang, Changtao Chen, Panming Li, Haonan Li, Jiaxuan Niu, Wenjie Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The advent of the AI era has driven an increasing demand for high-efficiency, high-density power supplies with high-current output capabilities in data centers. High-frequency resonant converters are particularly attractive for industrial applications due to their high power density and efficiency. This paper presents an integrated planar transformer scheme based on GaN devices for a high-frequency LLC resonant converter, targeting high-voltage single-stage DC transformers (DCX). The integration is achieved by arranging fractional-turn transformers in a four-post matrix configuration. This approach maintains high efficiency while leveraging the matrix structure's inherent high power density. Simulation results demonstrate a peak efficiency of 98.2% under the following conditions: a switching frequency of 1 MHz, a voltage conversion ratio from 400 V to 4 V, and an output power level of 2000 W.

title: Series Elastic Actuated Needle Valve and Sealing Equilibrium Flow Rate Models for Precise Position Control in Single-Acting Water-Hydraulic Actuators
authors: Shuto Yoshimura, Yuki Nakamura, Tomoyuki Noda, Yoshihito Nakata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Water hydraulics offer low viscosity, high-pressure capability, and environmental compatibility, making them ideal for teleoperated systems in extreme environments. However, achieving stable low-flow control and reliable sealing remains a key challenge owing to the complexity of current valve designs, and their limited precision at low-flow rates. To address this issue, we developed a compact needle valve—called Series Elastic Actuated Needle Valve (SEANV)—which incorporates a series elastic element, along with a sealing equilibrium model to predict its sealing behavior. Preliminary experiments using a single-acting actuator confirmed improved position tracking; however, these did not quantitatively characterize the flow control performance. Therefore, we conducted a detailed experimental evaluation of the flow and sealing characteristics of SEANV under various single-acting actuator conditions. The results demonstrate the enhanced low-flow resolution, achieving an RMSE of 0.516 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>/s, and robust sealing performance, with leakage maintained within ±50 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>/s, thereby validating the applicability of the proposed model to compact water-hydraulic systems.

title: A Federated Learning Framework for Multi-Parameter Optimization in Edge Computing
authors: Rajesh Soma, Subham Kumar Sahoo, Fahad Amin, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables distributed model training across edge devices without sharing raw data to preserve privacy and reduce communication overhead. However, FL can sometimes hinder edge settings in terms of energy efficiency and communication. The present research looks into an energy-aware device-to-device (D2D)-assisted FL paradigm in edge computing. With data quality, device reliability, and throughput as multiple parameters, we begin by implementing two baseline approaches from the original D2D-assisted model and optimizing them. We then present an algorithm for adaptive data sampling by modifying a heuristic algorithm that chooses participants dynamically based on energy and performance criteria, thereby enhancing training efficiency. The proposed approach achieves 95.13% accuracy in fewer rounds compared to 94.68 % (optimal) and 71.07% (heuristic), with 30% lower communication overhead, as shown by simulation results, verifying the effectiveness of multi-parameter optimization in FL for edge computing.

title: Towards Torque-Driven Reinforcement Learning for Quadruped Locomotion
authors: Jordan Dowdy, Jean Chagas Vaz
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) for legged robots is advancing locomotion, demonstrating its ability to adapt to new and challenging terrain. Traditionally, these RL locomotion frameworks are position-based, making the policy less adaptable to terrain types and requiring state estimation techniques in the observation space, i.e., linear velocity. Moreover, these RL frameworks often use small, lightweight quadrupeds that are limited in their viability for high-complexity tasks due to hardware constraints. This work explores an RL torque control framework for heavyweight high-torque quadrupeds. The RL framework in this paper can traverse rough terrain and effectively track a desired linear velocity without requiring knowledge of the agent’s current velocity. Using Nvidia’s Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab, simulation results of the RL torque control policy are shown on the Unitree B1 quadruped, achieving speeds of 3.5 m/s and 1.5 rad/s. In addition, the quadruped can walk up and down stairs without the aid of an exteroceptive sensor.

title: A Meta Learning Based Stacked Ensemble Model (MetaCervX) for Enhanced Cervical Cancer Prediction
authors: Chandani Bhagchandani, Tripti Swarnkar
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Cervical cancer remains a significant contributor to global cancer mortality in women, ranking second after breast cancer. A higher survival rate and successful treatment depend on early detection. Machine learning techniques help in detecting cervical cancer in early treatable stages and ultimately lead to better treatment of the disease. This study uses a stacked ensemble learning model with Logistic Regression (LR) as the meta model and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Extra Trees Classifier (ETC), and Random Forest (RF) as base models to accurately diagnose cervical cancer, thereby enhancing early detection and reducing false negatives. This work utilizes a cervical cancer risk factor dataset that contains missing values and class imbalance, which impact the reliability of the model. SMOTE and SMOTE-ENN are utilized by the MetaCervX model to address these issues and stabilize the data, while a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) imputer is used to fill in the missing values, resulting in more reliable and consistent model performance. The accuracy was found to be 95.93%, while the sensitivity was barely 45.55% in the initial results with row-imbalanced data. The model’s performance was enhanced by SMOTE by increasing the sensitivity to 98.14% and the accuracy to 98.76%. The final stacked ensemble model (MetaCervX), combined with SMOTEENN, showed an accuracy of 99.29% and ideal sensitivity. The noisy and misclassified data were refined by the ENN component, which reduces class overlap and improves the overall quality of the dataset. This improvement allowed the MetaCervX (SMOTE-ENN) model to achieve stable and reliable learning, showing improved performance over the SMOTE-based version. These major things that these experiments demonstrate are that MetaCervX with SMOTE-ENN effectively increases cervical cancer detection, resulting in improved diagnostic and treatment strategies.

title: COM-PACT: COMponent-Aware Pruning for Accelerated Control Tasks in Latent Space Models
authors: Ganesh Sundaram, Jonas Ulmen, Amjad Haider, Daniel Görges
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The rapid expansion of resource-constrained mobile platforms, such as mobile robots, wearable systems, and Internet-of-Things devices, has heightened the need for computationally efficient neural network controllers (NNCs) that can operate within strict hardware limitations. Although deep neural networks (DNNs) achieve high performance in control applications, their considerable computational complexity and memory demands hinder practical deployment on edge devices. This study presents a comprehensive model compression methodology that employs component-aware structured pruning to determine the optimal pruning magnitude for each group, thereby balancing compression and stability for NNC deployment. The proposed approach is rigorously evaluated on Temporal Difference Model Predictive Control (TD-MPC), a leading model-based reinforcement learning algorithm, with systematic integration of mathematical stability guarantees, specifically Lyapunov criteria. The principal contribution is a principled framework for identifying the theoretical limits of model compression while preserving controller stability. Experimental results indicate that the methodology effectively reduces model complexity while maintaining essential control performance and stability. Additionally, the approach defines a quantitative boundary for safe compression ratios, enabling practitioners to systematically determine the maximum permissible model reduction before compromising critical stability properties, thus supporting the reliable deployment of com-pressed NNCs in resource-limited environments.

title: NBCFCM:A Segmentation Algorithm for MR Images with Concurrent Capability of Bias Field and Noise
authors: Peng Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In the segmentation of brain magnetic resonance (MR) images using conventional segmentation algorithms (e.g., Fuzzy C-Means (FCM)), the segmentation accuracy is susceptible to a direct degradation attributable to bias field interference and noise. To mitigate this issue, this paper proposes the NBCFCM method, which performs segmentation with the algebraic distance from points to planes as a constraint. It introduces the concept of hyper-clustering centers (defined as planes), optimizes these planes to complete clustering, and thereby avoids nonlinear challenges.The algorithm integrates both grayscale and spatial information of pixels, and employs polynomial fitting to partition planes—this enhances both bias field correction and segmentation performance. Additionally, it constructs a noise-resistant fuzzy factor that incorporates local window variance. The correlational ties between neighborhoods are reinforced by this factor, and at the same time, the impact of noise is alleviated. Finally, the technology of singular value decomposition (SVD) is applied to realize the denoising and restoration of images. Experimental findings demonstrate that the NBCFCM model exhibits superior performance in bias field correction and noise suppression compared with other comparative models, enabling it to achieve favorable image segmentation accuracy.

title: A Wideband Circularly Polarized Metasurface Glass Antenna for Satellite Navigation Systems
authors: Keying Huang, Wenhai Zhang, Xueguan Liu, Kam-Weng Tam, Zhixing Chen, Quan Xue
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: A wideband circularly polarized (CP) glass metasurface antenna (MSA) is proposed for Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS). It consists of two types of metasurface unit cells and four rotated Z-shaped slots etched on the ground. The inner unit cells function as the primary radiator, while the outer unit cells act as parasitic structures, which help enhance its gain at high frequencies effectively. The proposed antenna has −10 dB impedance bandwidth of 37.46% (1.15–1.68 GHz). The antenna maintains a gain above 6 dBic across the full operating band.

title: Nature-Inspired Optimization of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks: A Comparative Study for Medical Diagnostics
authors: Ritik Mandal, Mayank Dave
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Hyperparameter optimization is a stable optimization issue in Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) for clinical support decisions because their performance is sensitive to spline order, hidden layer size, and regularization terms. To achieve this, we employ eight nature-inspired optimisation techniques—Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Differential Evolution (DE), Genetic Algorithms (GA), Firefly Algorithm, Whale Optimization Algorithm, Ant Colony Optimisation, Simulated Annealing, and Bayesian Optimisation as the baseline—to optimize KANs for heart disease prediction tasks. Cleveland-Hungary heart disease dataset (1,190 samples, 13 features) was used in experiments for comparison on a level playing field with stratified cross-validation. Outcomes show that research and development of PSO and DE were consistently the highest performers, scoring up to 85.15 % for computational efficiency compared to research and development suffering due to premature convergence for ACO and SA. Convergence behaviors and parameter sensitivity analyses aside from raw accuracy show strengths that are optimizer-dependent. This study offers a reproducible benchmarking environment and actionable guidelines for applying KANs to medical diagnosis.

title: Towards a lightweight ROS–CoppeliaSim simulator for the BEATRIX robotic education platform
authors: Janaardhanan Alagarraja, Adrian Rubio-Solis, Uriel Martinez-Hernandez
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This work presents the design and development of BEATRIXsim, a simulation environment for integration into the educational BEATRIX robot platform. BEATRIXsim is implemented in CoppeliaSim due to its support for kinematics and low computational load. Both the physical robot (BEAT-RIX) and digital robot (BEATRIXsim) are interfaced using an Arduino Mega microcontroller and the Robot Operating System (ROS) using the rosserial protocol. Custom-built ROS messages, services and a bridge are developed for synchronised operation, communication, control and real-time feedback of both the physical and digital robots. This simulation environment includes a Graphical User Interface (GUI) to allow users to simultaneously control both the physical hardware and the digital robot, but also their individual control. BEATRIXsim serves as a tool for experimentation and testing processes without the need for its physical counterpart, reducing risks, costs and development time. Overall, this work shows that an affordable humanoid robot for education purposes can be enhanced by the use of digital technology, providing a robust platform for real-time control, experimentation and teaching in robotics.

title: Developing a Secure, Scalable MERN Web Platform for Real-Time Food Ordering and Financial Transactions
authors: R Mukesh, P Sivaramakrishnan, Najeem Dheen Abdul Majeeth
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The MERN tech stack (Mongo DB, Express) has been used to architect a digitally-enabled framework to-food commerce. js, React. js, and Node. js, in order to add more operational agility in the web based food service transaction space. The suggested component offers a thoroughly responsive user interface which is based on react. École Polytéchnique I Applicable to js, so easy to browse in dishes, Update cart, and order placed in an orderly manner. The server-side logic, designed using Node. js in conjunction with Express. js, which is responsible for authentication protocols, transaction processing, and backend data orchestration. Tokenized authentication using JSON Web Tokens (JWT) enforces session integrity and secure communication protocols along service layers with JWT-based validation. MongoDB serves as the main data store, providing for a structured persistence of user IDs, bundled menus, transaction metadata and payment receipts. PayPal SDK integration translates to financial interoperability without friction, enabling digital transactions to take place continuously, securely, and at scale. This architectural paradigm focuses on modular deployment, horizontal scaling capability, and on device responsiveness. Consumers experience increased interaction with real-time order tracking systems, and the backend admin panel allows owners to easily track performance metrics, control inventory databases, and process transaction traffic. Noting the final technical configuration of the suggested solution, we consider that it can serve as a useful scientific input on the implementation of semanticallyenhanced and privacy-preserving e-commerce ecosystems for food service settings.

title: Developing an Efficient Underwater Object Detection System YOLO
authors: N.R. Sathis Kumar, K. Yeshaswini, M. Gowthami, V. Jahnavi, A. Bhargavi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Underwater object detection plays a crucial role in security systems, environmental monitoring, and ocean science. However, detection accuracy is challenged by light absorption, dispersion, limited visibility. This research introduces a deep learning-based approach using advanced YOLOv8. We integrate image enhancement methods feature extraction and minimizing underwater deformations to enhance detection performance. An aquarium dataset with diverse realistic underwater images is employed in designing, training, and testing the models. In accordance with experimental results, our enhanced pipeline significantly improves detection accuracy. The system supports both image and video inputs, detecting objects such as fish, marine plants and humans with high efficiency. The model is evaluated on custom and public datasets, and integrated with a Streamlit based web application for easy interaction and visualization.

title: Brain Tumour Detection Using DenseNet121: Exploring the Role of Data Augmentation and Test-Time Augmentation for Improved Accuracy
authors: Sanchit Grover, Sanjay Kumar Singh, Divyanshu Yadav, Prakhar Agrawal
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Early detection and correct classification of brain tumours through MRI scans are highly essential for appropriate treatment and the outcome of a patient. In this paper, the DenseNet121 architecture, which is the latest state-of-the-art convolutional neural network, is exploited along with data augmentation and Test-Time Augmentation to increase the classification accuracy. The dataset consists of preprocessed MRI images that are labelled as tumour-present or tumour-absent and further augmented with rotation, shift, and flip transformations. DenseNet121, pre-trained on ImageNet, with additional layers fine-tuned for more than 30 epochs to suit the binary classification model, resulted in excellent performance with an accuracy of 99.41%, high precision, recall, and F1-scores. TTA increased the reliability of the predictions by averaging the results of the augmented test samples. This approach proves the effectiveness of combining DenseNet121 with advanced techniques for augmentation excellent foundation for improving detection of brain tumours and leading the way to develop dependable diagnostic equipment in medical image analysis.

title: Detecting Fake Reviews on E-Commerce Platforms Using Nlp and Machine Learning
authors: N. R. Sathis Kumar, P. Supraja, P. RupaSree, P. Varshitha, Shaik Mahammad Sami
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Customers are misled by fake reviews, which also damage online platforms' reputation. Using supervised machine learning models and Natural Language Processing (NLP), this project aims to detect such misleading reviews. The system was trained to identify reviews that were deemed suspicious or fraudulent by using linguistic and behavioral patterns found in datasets from sites such as Yelp and Amazon. Real-time alerts are created and emailed to moderators, demonstrating an end-to-end detection pipeline.

title: Enhanced CNN-based DoS Attack detection in WSN and IoT using SMOTE for class imbalance handling
authors: Ingudam Gomita, Takhellambam Sonamani Singh, Hanjabam Saratchandra Sharma
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Network (WSN) and Internet of Things (IoT) collect various data from the environments, particularly in scenarios where the use of manpower is deemed less useful, harmful, and costly. Although they are very helpful in collecting data, the security of the collected data is always at risk. Numerous Intrusion detection systems have been developed along with or without Machine Learning (ML) techniques to detect various attacks affecting the WSN and IoT. We proposed a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based IDS with SMOTE and f_classif to identify various Denial of Service (DoS) attacks in IoT devices and WSN. The proposed method employs a one-dimensional CNN (1D-CNN) for the detection of attacks in the WSN-DS dataset and twodimensional CNN (2D-CNN) along with the reshaping of the features for the detection of attacks in the BoT-IoT dataset. The performance of the proposed IDS is measure across the datasets i.e. WSN-DS and BoT IoT with metrics such as Sensitivity or True Positive Rate (TPR), F1-score, False Positive Rate (FPR) or Fall-Out, Specificity or True Negative Rate (TNR), Precision or Positive Predictive Value (PPV), Negative Predictive Value (NPV), False Negative Rate (FNR), and False Detection Rate (FDR) is compared with against other existing benchmark machine learning techniques such as Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Dead-Time Compensation and Neutral-Point Voltage Control for Three-Level Inverters with Zero Common-Mode Voltage Modulation
authors: Chang Liu, Bing Li, Guozhu Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Zero common-mode voltage (ZCMV) modulation using medium and zero vectors can reduce the common-mode voltage of three-level inverters, and therefore is suitable for transformerless photovoltaic systems, as the common-mode filter for leakage current rejection can be reduced. However, common-mode voltage spikes arising from dead-time effect diminish its performance, and the ZCMV modulation also suffers from the issue of neutral-point voltage imbalance. To address these two issues, a novel dead-time compensation (DTC) and neutral-point voltage control (NPVC) strategy for a carrier-based ZCMV modulation is proposed in this paper. The proposed method expands the P level in the maximum phase for DTC and adjusts the N level in the minimum phase for NPVC, or vice versa. It is simple and suitable for practical applications. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy has been validated by experimental results.

title: Phishing Website Detection Using Deep Learning Based on URL Feature Extraction
authors: Hung Dao Nguyen, Thien Ho Huong
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: In the rapidly evolving digital landscape of recent years, Internet users are increasingly exposed to sophisticated cybersecurity threats, particularly phishing websites. To address this challenge, this paper employs four deep learning architectures for phishing detection using URL-based feature extraction: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) with Attention, Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) with Attention, and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) with Attention. The models were evaluated on two distinct datasets, Mendeley and PhiUSIIL, to assess their predictive performance. Experimental results show that models trained on the PhiUSIIL achieved significantly higher performance than those trained on Mendeley, with accuracy and F1-scores reaching up to 99.9%. Among the evaluated models, BiLSTM achieved the highest predictive performance, while MLP-Attention provided competitive accuracy with lower computational cost. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining robust feature engineering with attention mechanisms for phishing detection and offer practical insights into balancing model complexity and performance in scalable, real-time security systems.

title: System Optimization of Implantable IPT Systems Considering Conduction and Eddy Current Loss
authors: Ruochen Jia, Siyi Yao, Minfan Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The escalating power demands of next-generation implantable brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) are intensifying thermal management challenges in inductive power transfer (IPT) systems, primarily due to eddy current loss (ECL) in metallic enclosures and conduction loss in coils. To address this challenge, this paper establishes an analytical three-coil coupling model for ECL, precisely quantifying the independent contributions of transmitter (TX) and receiver (RX) magnetic fields to ECL. Combined with finite element simulation and decoupled measurement experiments, this paper proposes the Loss Equilibrium Theory: within a continuous wire diameter parameter space, the total system loss remains invariant. This theory shifts the IPT design strategy from conventional single-objective minimization to multidimensional loss reallocation, thereby enhancing thermal management flexibility and overcoming a critical barrier in high-power implantable BCIs.

title: QR or NFC? Understanding Indonesian Mobile Payment Adoption Through UTAUT
authors: Muhammad Stiven Pratama, Nanda Yuvika Amolika, Fellicia Firliandini, Adele Mailangkay
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Quick Response (QR) code-based systems, such as QRIS, are widely used in Indonesia. In contrast, Near Field Communication (NFC) mobile payments, though convenient, are a limited niche. The need for understanding the factors which affect the adoption of both technologies is made apparent by the recent release of QRIS Tap, an NFC-based extension. Using the UTAUT framework, we study the intention of Indonesian mobile users to adopt NFC and QR-based payment systems and analyzes the relative strength of these characteristics for the two technologies. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) was used to gather and analyze quantitative data.Findings reveal that Performance Expectancy, Social Influence, Facilitating Conditions, Trust, and Lifestyle Compatibility significantly and positively influence Behavioral Intention for both NFC and QR payments, with Lifestyle Compatibility shows as the strongest predictor. Critically, our comparative analysis indicated no statistically significant differences in the strength of these core adoption drivers between the NFC and QR models. These results suggest a common set of user considerations for adopting these distinct mobile payment technologies, giving insights for developing unified strategies while focusing on the importance of lifestyle integration and specific infrastructural aspects for NFC.

title: PBRS for Sim-to-Real Cyber RL: Training Agents in a High-Fidelity CyberBattleSim
authors: Pan Jia, Chuanjun Yi, Zhengkun Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Training autonomous reinforcement learning (RL) agents for cyber operations is a critical research area, yet the high-level abstraction in existing simulators, such as CyberBattleSim, creates a significant "sim-to-real" gap that undermines the real-world robustness of trained policies. To address this, we propose a framework that enhances CyberBattleSim’s fidelity while maintaining training efficiency. We first integrate critical real-world constraints to create a high-fidelity environment. However, this introduces reward sparsity and convergence difficulties. To overcome these challenges, we design and implement a potential-based reward shaping mechanism that provides dense shaping rewards (e.g., for credential acquisition), accelerating exploration while theoretically guaranteeing optimal policy invariance. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach enables agents trained in this high-fidelity environment to develop resilient policies adapted to complex network conditions. These agents not only achieve convergence speeds and success rates comparable to those in the original abstract environment but also learn more realistic strategies, such as actively circumventing high-latency paths and strongly defended regions. Our work thus provides a viable path toward bridging the gap between cybersecurity simulation and real-world application.

title: 2019 AAAI/ACM SIGAI Dissertation Honorable Mention - Aishwarya Agrawal
authors: Aishwarya Agrawal
publish: AI Matters
abstract: None

title: Stability Margin Analysis of A Black-Box DC Power Distribution System Over A Wide Operation Range By Current Perturbation Injection
authors: Zhengge Chen, Zhiwei Zhou, Jiheng Cai, Lei Bai, Ping Yang, Jianping Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the prevalence of renewable energy and DC storage units, DC-DC converters are widely used in power distribution systems (PDSs), which has raised concerns about bus voltage stability over a wide load range. This paper proposes a current perturbation-based impedance ratio (CPBIR) method for stability analysis of bus voltage to estimate stability in a wide operation range. Without any internal converter topology control information, the proposed approach can offer a visible stability estimation over a wide load range by merging each load’s test result. Besides, current perturbation injection is used to avoid increasing the source voltage impedance and breaking the bus wire connection, which occurs in voltage perturbation injection. Simulations based on a boost and buck cascaded power system are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Symmetric uncertainty-based feature selection for high-dimensional data
authors: Zijing Yuan, Chunzhi Hou, Zhenyu Lei, Shangce Gao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: High-dimensional datasets often contain a large number of irrelevant, redundant, or noisy features, presenting significant challenges for feature selection and modeling. As the dimensionality increases, models become prone to overfitting, and the computational cost rises. To address these issues, this paper proposes a symmetry-based feature selection evolutionary algorithm (SSFE), which aims to effectively reduce data dimensionality and improve model prediction performance. By calculating the Symmetry Uncertainty SU values of features and combining the roulette wheel selection strategy, the algorithm is able to filter out the most informative features and avoid local optima by incorporating an evolutionary algorithm. The method gradually reduces the number of features, optimizing the feature selection process for high-dimensional data, significantly improving the efficiency and accuracy of feature selection. Experimental results show that SSFE outperforms traditional feature selection algorithms on multiple high-dimensional datasets, effectively reducing data dimensionality and enhancing model performance.

title: Optimization of target motion parameter calculation strategy based on DQN
authors: Ziheng Han, Huapeng Yu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: To address the poor adaptability of traditional methods in target motion parameter calculation for maritime unmanned systems under complex sea conditions, this paper proposes a DQN-based adaptive calculation strategy optimization scheme. It constructs a modular DQN model, transforms the calculation task into a Markov decision process, defines complete/restricted state spaces, designs discrete solution strategies as an action space, and uses an S-shaped reward function to quantify decision benefits. Experiments with 10,000 non-cooperative target simulation samples show the model converges stably in both states: in the complete state, training loss stabilizes at 0.2 and reward at 86; in the restricted state, loss at 0.005 and reward at 70.5. Test solution accuracy reaches 84.96% (complete state) and 87.26% (restricted state). This confirms the DQN model breaks traditional limitations, enables dynamic strategy adaptation in complex scenes, improves calculation accuracy, supports autonomous operations of maritime unmanned systems, and provides a new paradigm for target motion solution strategy optimization.

title: A computationally efficient deep learning model for real-time image dehazing on edge devices
authors: Gavin Halford, Arthur C. Depoian, Colleen P. Bailey
publish: 2025 IEEE MetroCon
abstract: Reliable image restoration in particulate environments is essential for high-stakes applications such as robotic surgery and autonomous navigation. However, existing dehazing models often struggle with a “computational bottleneck,” requiring massive parameter counts that are unsuitable for edge deployment. This paper introduces CED-RIDE, an ultra-lightweight architecture containing only 24,659 parameters. By utilizing channel-separated skip connections and the Lion optimizer, the model addresses the non-uniform scattering and spectral distortion characteristic of real-world smoke.Evaluated on the SMOKE dataset, CED-RIDE achieves a validation PSNR of 20.11 dB and an SSIM of 0.8405, outperforming existing state-of-the-art benchmarks by 1.28 dB in PSNR and 35% in structural fidelity. These results demonstrate that high-quality, real-time smoke removal can be achieved with a minimal computational footprint, bridging the gap between theoretical restoration and operational edge-computing requirements.

title: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Developing Customer Lifetime Value Prediction in the Banking Sector Using LightGBM: A Case Study of Bank XYZ
authors: Meilina Kurniawati, Evaristus Didik Madyatmadja
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study proposes a Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) prediction model for the Indonesian banking sector utilizing the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) algorithm. The study applied the data of real customer Bank XYZ, one of the most extensive banks in Indonesia. This study used the CRISP-DM framework composed of business understanding, data preparation, modeling, evaluation, and deployment. The dataset included demographic, relational, and transactional features; i.e., Recency, Frequency, Monetary, tenure, and average balance. These features were gathered from May 2024 until May 2025. As a result, the LightGBM model performed better than the Moving Average baseline, which showed a 73.91percent improvement in RMSE. From this study’s analysis, the number of products has the biggest effect on predicting CLV, followed by average balance, income, and monetary. This research demonstrated that the LightGBM model allows to effectively capture nonlinear customer behavior patterns and provides a more accurate and scalable basis for value-based customer retention tactics in the banking area.

title: A digital twin based method for parameters identification of three-phase three-level NPC inverter and sensors
authors: Qinghua Zheng, Xiao Yang, Mingyao Ma, Mo Liping
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Ensuring the operational reliability of power converters is paramount for the stability and efficiency of modern power systems. Digital twin technology has recently gained prominence as a robust solution for real-time condition monitoring and predictive maintenance. This paper presents a digital twin-based framework dedicated to the monitoring and parameter identification of three-phase three-level Neutral Point Clamped (NPC) inverters and their critical current sensors. The core of our approach involves the construction of a high-fidelity digital twin model that accurately mirrors the physical system's dynamics. To achieve precise parameter estimation, an Adaptive Particle Swarm Optimization (APSO) algorithm is implemented, which enhances the model's accuracy through iterative optimization. The proposed method's efficacy is rigorously validated through experimental studies, which confirm its capability to reliably track parameter variations and assess the system's health status.

title: Hierarchical Image-Based Visual-Servoing Control for Stewart Manipulators via Data-Driven Adaptation and Incremental Prediction
authors: Shengyou Zhou, Junzheng Wang, Dongchen Liu, Hao Yu, Renjian Hao, Jing Li, Haowei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This work presents a hierarchical image-based visual servoing (IBVS) framework for a Stewart manipulator, integrating target recognition, tracking, and adaptive control. To mitigate calibration uncertainties and external disturbances inherent in IBVS systems, a data-driven adaptive incremental predictive control scheme is developed by constructing a linear image-plane model from visual feedback. A structured incremental prediction model and a data-driven adaptive law are introduced to avoid updating the full image Jacobian, a process subject to pose-observation difficulty, delay-induced instability, and parameter divergence in eye-in-hand (EIH) Stewart manipulators. The proposed controller employs an online saturated recursive least-squares algorithm with a forgetting factor to optimize a multi-objective cost function that incorporates image-pixel errors, base disturbances, and observed manipulator poses. In addition, a Newton-Raphson iteration with adaptive termination thresholds is used to ensure efficient and accurate kinematic computation. Comparative experiments against feature-space control and proportional-derivative predictive control validate that the proposed method significantly reduces pixel errors and enhances tracking robustness, demonstrating the effectiveness for dynamic target tracking of Stewart manipulators.

title: Collaborative Feedback Discriminative Propagation for Video Super-Resolution
authors: Hao Li, Xiang Chen, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang, Jinshan Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: The key success of existing video super-resolution (VSR) methods stems mainly from exploring spatial and temporal information that is usually achieved by a temporal propagation with alignment strategies. However, inaccurate alignment usually leads to significant artifacts that will be accumulated during propagation and thus affect video restoration. Moreover, only propagating the same timestep features forward or backward does not handle the videos with complex motion or occlusion. To address these issues, we propose a collaborative feedback discriminative (CFD) method to correct inaccurate aligned features and better model spatial and temporal information for VSR. Specifically, we first develop a discriminative alignment correction (DAC) method to reduce the influences of the artifacts caused by inaccurate alignment. Then, we propose a collaborative feedback propagation (CFP) module based on feedback and gating mechanisms to explore spatial and temporal information of different timestep features from forward and backward propagation simultaneously. Finally, we embed the proposed DAC and CFP into commonly used VSR networks to verify the effectiveness of our method. Experimental results demonstrate that our method improves the performance of existing VSR models while maintaining a lower model complexity.

title: The DL-DAM-Based Dust Accumulation Detection Model
authors: Shixian Ji, Mingyue He, Tianyi Sun, Shengxian Cao, Han Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Dust deposition on the surface of photovoltaic (PV) panels can degrade the efficiency of photovoltaic power generation. Conventional methods for identifying PV panel dust accumulation status suffer from low recognition accuracy and slow processing speed To address this issue, this paper develops a DL-DAM-based dust concentration detection model, which quantitatively estimates PV dust accumulation levels using transmittance. Firstly, a dataset is constructed for training the model’s transmittance estimation module, leveraging the principle of atmospheric light attenuation combined with image processing techniques. Secondly, multi-scale convolution and a dual attention mechanism are fused to extract image features. Finally, deep learning and wavelet transform are integrated to achieve both transmittance estimation and visual distribution mapping of dust accumulation severity. Experimental results demonstrate that the training error of the DL-DAM model stabilizes at approximately 0.025. In transmittance estimation, the model achieves a mean square error (MSE) of only 0.0005 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.0224, significantly outperforming the three comparative models. Furthermore, it exhibits high detection accuracy for high-concentration dust accumulation.

title: Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation via Complementary Attention Fusion
authors: Le Li, Yu Sang, Siman Li, Wenxin Ji
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Image-level weakly supervised semantic segmentation (WSSS) has become popular because it only requires low-cost image-level annotations. Most existing approaches obtain pseudo labels by exploiting class activation maps (CAMs). However, CAMs mainly emphasize the most discriminative regions of objects, so the resulting pseudo labels are often sparse and noisy and cannot serve as reliable supervision. This is because pseudo-labels inevitably contain partial noisy labels, which in turn limits the subsequent segmentation performance. To overcome this limitation, we employ a complementary attention modules in the ResNet38 classification network to enhance CAM quality. By introducing ECA and EMA modules, we employ Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module as a channel selector to suppress redundant and co-occurring channels and highlight class-discriminating channels, and apply Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) module further refines spatial features by modeling multi-scale pixel-level relationships, improving connectivity and suppressing background leakage. Based on this, we use the improved CAM to generate pseudo-labels and use them to supervise DeepLabV2 segmentation training. Compared with the baseline using only the original CAM, our method improves both seed coverage and purity, and the segmentation model shows a significant improvement in mIoU on standard datasets.

title: Integrated Green Facades on Different Residential Buildings Using Clustering Method
authors: Alaine Tess A. Cabije
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This research aims to evaluate the application of green façades in residential buildings as perceived by the real estate industry. The goal is to assess the impact of the architectural configuration on the adaptability residents’ perception of the green facade residential buildings utilizing advanced clustering techniques using fuzzy K-Means and Hierarchical Clustering methods. The research further examines the suitability of various types of residential buildings the application of green façades to 187 respondents. A high variance of 79. 37% between clusters on AHC. 37% indicated a strong separation between clusters, confirmed by silhouette analysis. Clusters’ cohesion and clear-cut features. It reflected less distinct boundaries. The three-cluster model shows interpretability and cluster compactness on Fuzzy K-Means. The results illustrate notable preference groups and highlight low- and mid-rise one- to two-story buildings as the best candidates for green façades. The validation of these clustering techniques underscores their utility in supporting sustainable urban design. The results are of great value for designers and environmental planners in creating energy-efficient residential zones and ecological residential areas. Integrating environmental simulations and extending the data will enable for more precise design suggestions.

title: Local Velocity Field Control of a Nonholonomic Base for Kinesthetic Interaction with a Collaborative Arm
authors: Tadej Petrič, Leon Žlajpah
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper introduces a local control framework for mobile manipulators that enables coordinated motion between a nonholonomic base and a robotic arm without relying on any sensors mounted on the platform. The method is based on velocity vector fields defined in the mobile base frame and uses only internal joint measurements from the manipulator. A reduced kinematic model provides the translational end-effector (TEE) position, which drives all base motion decisions. By evaluating distance- and angle-based thresholds, the controller generates smooth linear and angular velocity commands that guide the base through extension, alignment, and retreat behaviors. The system supports real-time human interaction, such as kinesthetic guidance across the full reachable workspace, while respecting the nonholonomic constraints of the platform. Since the base controller is independent of the arm’s control strategy, it is compatible with autonomous planning, teleoperation, or demonstration-based teaching. The proposed control strategy is particularly suited for collaborative scenarios where intuitive human guidance is essential and reliance on global localization is unnecessary. Experimental results with physical kinesthetic guidance demonstrate the platform’s ability to reactively follow end-effector motion, preserving reachability and manipulability across the full workspace.

title: Research on the Technical Principles and Performance Verification of SoVITS Speech Synthesis Model
authors: Bolun Zhang, Linxiang Shi, Changxin Song
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In recent years, the naturalness and quality of speech generated by text-to-speech (TTS) framework based on deep learning have been greatly improved. The purpose of synthesizing understandable and natural audio from text has long been the research topic of artificial intelligence, natural language and speech processing. Aiming at the problems existing in traditional text-to-speech (TTS) technology, such as weak training ability with few samples, low naturalness of cross-language synthesis, poor controllability of emotional expression, lack of emotional expression and large amount of target speaker corpus, this paper systematically analyzes the technical framework and operating mechanism of SoVITS speech synthesis model, and focuses on the principle of its acoustic generation part. It aims to transform the emotional state of speech from one to another, while retaining the language information and the identity of the speaker. By designing multi-dimensional experiments involving few samples training and emotion control, the performance of the model is finally verified on LibriSpeech and AISHELL standard data sets, and the joint optimization goal is constructed by introducing reconstruction loss, confrontation loss and semantic alignment loss. The experimental results show that when the training sample duration is only 5 minutes, the naturalness (MOS) of SoVITS synthesized speech is 4.63, and the similarity (COS) of the embedded cosine of the speaker is 0.835. This model provides an efficient solution for the generation of speech synthesis content with low resources and few samples.

title: Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis for Neutral Point Clamped Three-Level Grid-Connected Inverters Based on a Gain-Adaptive Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Observer
authors: Jiarong Zhang, Liang Xu, Liping Mo, Mingyao Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: A fault diagnosis method based on a gain-adaptive super-twisting sliding mode observer (ASTSMO) is proposed for single-switch open-circuit faults in neutral point clamped (NPC) three-level grid-connected inverters. First, a mixed logical dynamical (MLD) model of the NPC inverter is established. Second, a gain-adaptive STSMO with fast convergence and chattering suppression capabilities is designed to accurately estimate the three-phase currents. Then, a fault detection variable is constructed based on the root mean square (RMS) values of the actual currents and the observer-estimated currents, combined with an adaptive threshold for fault detection. Finally, a fault localization variable is derived from the current residuals to identify the faulty switch. A simulation model for open-circuit fault diagnosis of switches in the NPC three-level grid-connected inverter is built in MATLAB/Simulink for validation. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed diagnostic method.

title: A Decision-Making Multiple-Scenarios Primary Frequency Regulation Method for Renewable Energy Considering Power Backup
authors: Yuxin Li, Chen Chen, Zhenxiong Wang, Ruiqi Ma, Hao Yi, Fang Zhuo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The power output of renewable energy generators is inherently variable. High penetration of renewable energy into the grid increases frequency fluctuations, making effective frequency control necessary. Renewable energy units offer fast response, high precision, and low cost in primary frequency control. However, their real-time available output is often limited below the rated capacity due to maximum power constraints. This makes reserve allocation and primary frequency control strategies more complex compared to traditional synchronous generators. As a result, their strong frequency regulation potential remains underutilized. This paper proposes four primary frequency control modes based on different reserve strategies: MPPT mode without power reserve, power ratio mode, delta-P mode, and fixed-power mode. To enhance its adaptability to varying operating conditions, a hierarchical decision tree is designed, which prioritizes installed capacity penetration, followed by output fluctuation, generation level, and finally regulation demand for dynamic mode selection. Multi-scenario simulations demonstrate the solution's effectiveness in balancing reserve allocation with consumption efficiency, enhancing frequency regulation capability in high- renewable-penetration grids.

title: Attentional Event-RGB Sensor Fusion for Fast Drone Detection
authors: Antoine Zundel, Cédric Demonceaux, Nicolas Hueber, Guillaume Strub, Sébastien Changey
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents an embedded multi-modal vision system for drone detection, combining an event-based camera, an IMU, and an RGB sensor. The method leverages an attentional mechanism on the event stream and is robust to rotations along all three axes (roll, pitch, and yaw) of a rotating platform. The event-based sensor enables localization of fast moving objects, while the RGB camera provides classification, with the entire system optimized for embedded computational constraints. Performance analysis, with and without attention mechanisms and across various algorithmic variants, assesses the trade-off between computational cost and detection accuracy. The study identifies optimal operating situation for each configuration, validated on an outdoor test data samples.

title: Ultra-lightweight Speech Enhancement Model for Voice-Controlled Exoskeletons
authors: Sixuan Hua, Wenjie Chen, Xiantao Sun, Nana Ding
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To address the issue that voice-controlled exoskeletons have limited performance due to noise interference in complex environments such as medical and industrial settings, this paper proposes a speech enhancement model based on a dual-path lightweight Transformer network. The model is designed with a dual-path lightweight Transformer module, which combines a grouping strategy with Transformer and optimizes the multi-head attention mechanism, thereby realizing the design of a lightweight model for speech enhancement. Experimental results show that with 28.3K parameters and 49.76 MMACs computation, this model outperforms lightweight models such as RNNoise and GTCRN in various indicators. It can significantly reduce residual noise and distortion, meeting the edge deployment requirements of exoskeletons.

title: Improved YOLOv8-Based Monocular Visual Recognition Method for Pallets
authors: Huijuan Zhang, Guoyun Ye, Feng Tian, Zhehang Qiu, Jie Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Pallet and forklift coordination and handling is an important part of automated warehouse environments. Addressing the issue of pallet recognition failure caused by interference from various factors in complex warehouse scenarios for unmanned forklift AGVs (Autonomous Guided Vehicles) , a pallet recognition method based on improved YOLOv8-Pose is proposed in this paper. Firstly, part of C2f (channel-to-pixel) is replaced with C2f_DCN (Deformable convolution network, DCN) to adapt to geometric changes of pallets. The SEAttention module is added to reduce the influence of irrelevant features and improve network performance. And the SIoU loss considering pallet angle information is introduced to improve training accuracy. Secondly, based on obtaining target box and keypoint information, Canny edge detection is utilized to adaptively adjust edge features to obtain more accurate contour information. Through geometric constraint analysis, the calculation relationship between the tilt angle and distance of the pallet relative to the forklift is obtained. Finally, a self-made pallet dataset is constructed to verify the algorithm through the dataset and actual environment. Experimental results show that the proposed method is superior to the unmodified YOLOv8s algorithm in both target detection and keypoint detection, achieving an average precision of 93.3%, a recall rate of 98.3%, and an average keypoint accuracy improvement of 5.7%. This method is applicable for pallet recognition of unmanned forklift AGVs.

title: A Capacitor Coupling Method of V2 Adaptive On-time Control for Multi-phase Buck Converter
