authors: Zhuhaobo Zhang, Jasper Li, Yangwei Yu, Zheyuan Li, Delai Jiang, Ong Wee Liat, Hao Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Ripple-based V2 adaptive on-time (AOT) control has gained extensive adoption in both single-phase and multiphase buck converters for voltage regulators (VRs). The control has streamlined architecture, elevated system bandwidth, and excellent light-load efficiency. Its rapid dynamic transient response capability enables the control to cope with large load current variations and high slew rate changes while keeping low output voltage fluctuations. Because of the quite low value of the output ceramic capacitor ESR, the V2-AOT control stability should be satisfied by introducing triangular or sawtooth ramps. In this paper, a capacitor coupling method is proposed to inject external triangular or sawtooth ramps into the V2-AOT control modulator effectively. The small-signal modeling is presented using the describing function method and the system stability condition is analyzed. Emulated triangular ramps synchronized with the inductor currents can provide effective control damping and can help to eliminate oscillations. Compared to conventional dc extraction methods, the proposed method has a simpler structure and can eliminate the requirements of a complex dc bias sensing circuit. Theoretical analysis is verified with a fabricated four-phase buck controller chip and practical design implementations are presented.

title: Non-Invasive Sensor Based Detection of 2-FAL and Moisture in Transformer Oil
authors: Adeeba Asif, Khan Azmat, Shakeb Ahmad Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The reliable assessment of insulation health in power transformers is vital for the prevention of catastrophic failures and operational disruptions. Among the key indicators of insulation degradation, 2-Furfuraldehyde (2-FAL) and moisture content in transformer oil have emerged as primary markers due to their direct correlation with cellulose insulation aging. Traditional techniques such as high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) and Karl Fischer titration, while accurate, are inherently offline, labor-intensive, and unsuitable for continuous monitoring. This study presents a novel coplanar capacitive sensor design tailored for real-time, non-invasive detection of 2-FAL and moisture in transformer oil. Leveraging the principles of fringing field capacitance, the sensor operates without direct immersion, thereby mitigating issues related to contamination, calibration drift, and dielectric variability. Comprehensive simulations using COMSOL Multiphysics were conducted to evaluate the impact of electrode geometry, spacing, and the inclusion of shielding and guard electrodes on electric field propagation, penetration depth, and sensitivity. Results demonstrate that optimized configurations—specifically wider electrodes and reduced inter-electrode spacing—substantially enhance sensitivity, with shielding and guard structures improving field confinement and noise immunity. The proposed sensor exhibits high sensitivity and structural simplicity, making it a promising candidate for in-situ insulation diagnostics in high-voltage equipment.

title: Shattered Aspirations in the Age of AI: Investigating the Impact of the Glass Ceiling on the Mental Health of Working Women
authors: Pooja Chahal, Hardeep Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This study looks at how the glass ceiling affects working women psychologically and how it persists, with an emphasis on how artificial intelligence (AI) might be used in identifying, assessing, and potentially mitigating these effects. By examining empirical research and integrating AI-based solutions into workplace assessments, the study aims to bridge the gap between modern technological interventions and conventional gender equity initiatives. The study conducted on 35 employees from the pharmaceutical industry used a mixed-methods approach to examine women's qualitative experiences, as well as the quantitative metrics generated by AI tools. The findings highlight the advantages and disadvantages of AI in advancing gender parity and mental health in the workplace.

title: A Two-Stage GaN-Based ZVS DC-DC Converter with Wide Input Voltage Range
authors: Kang Nie, Yueshi Guan, Xiaosheng Liu, Yijie Wang, Dianguo Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents a high-frequency, zero-voltage-switching (ZVS) isolated DC-DC converter for applications requiring high efficiency, high power density, and a wide voltage range. The first regulated stage is a four-switch buck-boost (FSBB) converter that uses quadrangle current modulation for the inductor current. Constraints on the control variables are derived, and a quantitative relationship between the RMS value of inductor current and the control variables is established through mathematical calculations. Then a simplified, single-variable, closed-loop control system is proposed to achieve ZVS of all switching and minimum inductor current RMS. The second stage is an LLC DC transformer (DCX) that uses a matrix transformer and flux cancellation to reduce core size and loss. Finally, a 500-W prototype was built to verify performance, achieving a peak efficiency of 95.4% and a power density of 110 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>.

title: Autonomous Drainage Control Systems Enabled by AIoT for Improved Building Infrastructure Efficiency
authors: Lovkesh Singh Vermani
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: As cities grow rapidly and climate continue to change, the use of historical techniques to manage drainage systems is becoming difficult. Within quickly urbanizing areas, like the National Capital Region (NCR) and Zirakpur in Punjab frequent waterlogging, basement flooding and poor drainage in high-rise residential and commercial buildings are ongoing issues, especially in monsoon months. In smarter buildings, where sustainability and efficiency are paramount, historic methods of drainage and monitoring are ineffective. When these cities and buildings previously relied on mechanical, fixed devices and basic sensor monitoring, personnel responsibility and inaction limited to when or after damage had already occurred, was the standard. The advent of connected technologies offered slight improvements with cloud technology enabling access to live water level, flow rates, and blockage detection, after the fact but still did not provide foresight to allow problem avoidance. Modern buildings use “smarter” buildings and systems with flexible infrastructures (i.e., connective technology, etc.) and flexible monitoring (e.g., sensor monitoring, etc.). Most of these systems can learn from historical data like recent trends or changes, assess live conditions, and ultimately can make autonomous decisions, e.g., adjustment of drainage flow or issuing alerts prior to a building emergency. When enabled by cloud platforms and edge devices, the speed of decision and directed monitoring will be more thorough and immediate. This paper will comment on the improvements to drainage monitoring and management systems and propose a framework for the future in urban cities like NCR and Zirakpur, Punjab.

title: Development of a Compact Collar-Mounted Feeder for Remote Dog Feeding
authors: Kai Fukuzawa, Tsige Tadesse Alemayoh, Shotaro Kojima, Ranulfo Bezerra, Miho Nagasawa, Takefumi Kikusui, Kazunori Ohno
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Timely food rewards are crucial for effective dog training, but providing immediate reinforcement is difficult when the handler cannot be near the dog. Existing systems, such as stationary feeders, restrict the dog's movement and cannot deliver rewards flexibly. Our previous back-mounted feeder (1.2 kg) enabled successful remote food delivery, but its size and weight meant it could only be used for medium- to large-sized dogs. Remotely controlled food-dispensing devices solve this by allowing spatially unconstrained rewards without limiting the dog's movement. However, for real-world use, such devices must be compact, lightweight, and unobtrusive to avoid disrupting natural behavior or requiring long habituation periods.Hence, in this study, we present the development and validation of a collar-mounted feeder (hereafter, "collar-feeder") capable of remotely dispensing food multiple times. The newly developed collar-feeder weighs only 143.6 g and can store up to 20 food items. A compact internal design with fewer electronic parts reduces its size and weight. Experiments showed a 100% feeding success rate among dogs unbothered by the device, while some dogs mistook it for a toy. These findings highlight the potential applicability of the collar-feeder in dog training and behavioral discipline. With further functional improvement, the collar-feeder presented in this paper may gain widespread adoption. For instance, by integrating biometric sensors and a food-tracking system, the device could serve as a valuable tool in households and veterinary clinics for pet health monitoring and management.

title: State-of-Charge Estimation of Lithium-ion Batteries with BiLSTM Architecture
authors: Asra Irfan, Majid Jamil, Sheeraz Kirmani
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The safe and effective operation of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) depends on an accurate state of charge (SoC) estimation, as it influences their performance, longevity, and safety. Conventional direct and model-based techniques often come with drawbacks such as cumulative errors, dependency on precise battery models, and limited adaptability under changing operating conditions. To overcome these issues, this paper investigates deep learning-driven approaches for SoC estimation, with particular emphasis on the bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) framework. By exploiting its capability to process information from both past and future time instants, the BiLSTM architecture is employed to improve SoC estimation accuracy. The performance of BiLSTM is assessed and compared against other deep learning counterparts [deep neural network (DNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM)] under three thermal conditions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, 25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right.{}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left.40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$</tex> using unified driving schedule (LA92) drive cycle. With mean absolute error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 3 1 \%, ~} \mathbf{0. 1 8 \%}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 4 \%}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, \mathbf{2 5}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and 40°C, respectively, BiLSTM performs better than both DNN and LSTM, indicating its ability to provide highly accurate and reliable SoC estimation.

title: Advanced Cryptographic Solutions for Enhanced Cloud Security, Auditing, and Key Management in 6G Smart Cities
authors: Vikas Dubey, Abhishek Shukla
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The rapid expansion of cloud computing, smart cities, and new 6 G networks has drastically transformed the manner of data storage and computing. The flip side of this rapid change is significant risk to privacy and cybersecurity. Traditional audit frameworks often fail to meet the dynamic and complex demands of modern-day cloud environments, prompting the need for innovative processes. This research addresses important issues through the development and testing of four different processes to enhance cloud infrastructure security, data integrity, and privacy and protect privacy. The proposed processes include: an efficient and privacy-preserving public auditing method that utilize the Witerbnitz Signature Scheme (WSA); an Improved Sunflower Optimization Algorithm with Public Auditing Scheme (ISFOAE-PAS) aimed at better encryption and DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) detection; a Hybrid Optimization Algorithm (KG-COS) for reliable key generation; and, lastly, an Artificial Bee Colony (ABC) algorithm, to improve key management and key authentication in 6 G smart cities. All processes have been thoroughly vetted through research and implementation, and represent significant improvements in computational efficiency, data security, and threat detection, and would enable the development of robust and dependable digital ecosystems in smart cities.

title: Analysis and Suppression of Voltage Harmonic in Single-Phase Islanded Microgrids Under Nonlinear Load Disturbance
authors: Yanzhong Huang, Yang Shao, Lunbo Deng, Chenxuan Xu, Haoqing Zhen, Linjun Si, Junjie Yang, Yongheng Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Due to the lack of support from the main power grid, the output voltage of the single-phase islanded microgrid is prone to severe harmonic distortions under the disturbance of nonlinear loads. To address this issue, a voltage harmonic feed-forward control is proposed to reshape the harmonic impedance of the single-phase inverter in microgrids. Under the proposed control strategy, the impedance magnitude of the single-phase inverter is significantly reduced in the studied harmonic frequency range, which effectively suppresses the voltage harmonics in islanded microgrids. Experimental results verify that the microgrid system with the proposed control has good harmonic suppression performance under different nonlinear load disturbances.

title: Next-Gen Sports Analytics: Enhancing Player Performance Evaluation with Hybrid RNN-LSTM Models
authors: Jasmini Kumari, Likhith Badiga, Sambit Kumar Mishra, Sugyan Kumar Mishra, Sushmita Kumari
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: With the development of deep learning and availability of large datasets, the evaluation of player performance has expanded the scope of sports analytics. Predicting the outcomes of players is still of utmost importance for tactical decisions. While Ridge Regression, Gradient Boosting, and Support Vector Regression (SVR) and other traditional machine learning techniques used in sports analytics, the true potential of the complex temporal dependencies of player statistics is still not achieved. These temporal dependencies in the performance data can inspire the design of advanced techniques, so this paper proposes a hybrid RNN-LSTM architecture suitable for player performance evaluation. The proposed model utilizes the forecasting abilities of RNNs coupled with LSTM cells to synthesize memory and guarantee performance. The simulation involved a dataset containing 1007 player-season records spanning 2008 to 2024 and including various bowling and batting performance metrics. All model training and evaluation were performed in the TensorFlow and Keras libraries. The experimental results show that our proposed RNN-LSTM has an accuracy of 0.9635, $R^{2}$ score of 0.9910, Mean Square Error (MSE) of 301.4782, and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 7.6366 that outperform Ridge Regression, Gradient Boosting and SVR baselines. In the end, a hybrid RNN-LSTM model will be highly accurate and scalable. More importantly, it will set the benchmark for player performance prediction. It can predict better than regular machine learning techniques because it captures temporal dependencies.

title: Ensemble GBM-Based Capsule Networks for Malicious URL Detection
authors: S SRI SAYE Lakshmi, K SHANTHA Kumari
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Phishing URLs (Uniform Resource Locator) are the primary concern for cybercrime in Internet-based systems. It leads to malicious websites where fraudulent activities take place. Neural Network based AI techniques are used to classify URLs as malicious or not. A URL is a combination of lexical features; therefore, concentrating on the arrangements, position, and domain-based features helps in the detection of malicious URLs from authentic ones. Thus, this paper proposes a CapsNet-based Ensemble gradient boosting learning approach for malicious URLs detection. The feature extraction of lexical URLs is performed using capsules and the dynamic routing mechanism in CapsNet, and classification is carried out using the XGBoost, Light GBM, and CatBoost methods for ensemble learning. The proposed work was evaluated using the malicious phishing URL dataset from Kaggle, which is based on lexical features. The work achieved 94.15 % accuracy with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.95 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex> score, yielding the lowest FPR and FNR values.

title: GeoSense: Symbolic Regression based Localization Without Global Positioning System
authors: P Ranjan, G Tejeswar, R Vidhyashankar, V Pritick, Prakash Venugopal
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Although navigation often deals with the use of Global Positioning System (GPS) based localization, the localization often fails or produces inaccurate results in confined spaces such as indoor environments, disaster zones, or underground areas. This shortcoming provides a possibility to implement GPSfree, offline navigation of the IoT-based systems. The presented paper suggests such a lightweight localization framework, exploiting a combination of motion sensors (accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer) and normalized Wi-Fi Received Signal Strength Indicator(RSSI), which is attainable on a small and lightweight hardware device. They use symbolic regressions that formulate understandable equations to estimate geographical coordinates without the use of GPS and the internet. The complementary filters and Exponential Moving Average (EMA) filters are applied to sensor data to remove noise and drift and a dynamic Look-Up Table (LUT) correction mechanism adapts and compensates in real-time for any residual errors. Comparisons on benchmarking reveal that symbolic regression depicts mean Haversine loss of $\mathbf{1 6. 7 5}$ meters consuming only 0.025 MiB memory, better than decision trees (23.13 m) and random forests (16.91 m) with tremendous efficiency in memory. Hardware testing on Raspberry Pi 4B gave an average error in localization as 52.38 meters confirming the feasibility of the system to go real time. The proposed method can result in energyefficient and explainable localization on embedded systems and the internet of things (across all platforms). This makes it especially useful in a case like a rescue mission or military operations or even a sustainable installation of IoT based on the unavailability of the conventional GPS.

title: A Teleoperation Framework for an Articulated Aerial Robot with Full DoF Mapping of Base Pose and Joint Angles
authors: Kotaro Kaneko, Junichiro Sugihara, Kazuki Sugihara, Masaki Kitagawa, Keisuke Nagato, Moju Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Robots are increasingly being used to replace humans in performing dangerous tasks, and aerial robots are particularly popular for work at heights. Both underactuated and fully actuated multirotors have mainly been used, but the range of tasks they can perform is limited due to their low degree of operational freedom. Articulated aerial robots are attracting attention as one solution to this problem. Due to the complexity and numerous disturbances involved in high-altitude work, teleoperation by humans is still necessary, and research is ongoing. Most of these studies focus on conventional multi-rotors, and it is difficult to intuitively control articulated aerial robots. Therefore, in this study, we propose a new teleoperation framework that allows operators to intuitively control all degrees of freedom of an articulated aerial robot simultaneously. The proposed framework consists of a floating-based device that acquires operating inputs by utilizing the freedom of movement of both human hands, and a system that generates commands to the robot from those inputs. The effectiveness of the proposed framework was verified through operating experiments using a real robot and wall cleaning task.

title: A Double-Side Cooled SiC MOSFET Power Module with Embedded Common-Mode Screen and Integrated Gate Driver
authors: Jialong Dou, Zhiqiang Wang, Chunyang Man, Deao Shen, Xingshan Li, Guofei Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs offer superior performance in power electronics but face significant common-mode (CM) electromagnetic interference (EMI) challenges at high switching speeds. This paper introduces a double-side cooled (DSC) SiC MOSFET power module with two main features: 1) an integrated CM EMI shielding layer and 2) a co-packaged gate driver with minimized parasitic inductance. The proposed design utilizes two sets of stacked substrates. The potential of the middle layers of both stacked substrates is clamped to half of the DC-link voltage through Cu-Mo alloy connection blocks and integrated ceramic decoupling capacitors. The integrated gate driver is placed adjacent to the SiC MOSFET chips, featuring an ultra-compact layout design with parasitic inductance as low as 3 nH. Experimental results show that the module has excellent power handling capability with a junction-to-fluid thermal resistance of 0.344 K/W. In addition, at a high dv/dt of 15 V/ns, the proposed module achieves a CM current of 222 mA (equivalent to 14.8 pF CM parasitic capacitance), representing an 81.5% reduction compared to the inherent CM parasitic capacitance of 80 pF in the unshielded module.

title: Study on Roller-Walker: Development of Roller-Walker II Using 3D Printed Structural Components
authors: Hana Ito, Akifumi Okubo, Kurumi Osawa, Takahiro Aruga, Gen Endo
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: We developed Roller-Walker II, a leg-wheeled hybrid robot that primarily uses 3D-printed plastic as its structural material. This robot achieves both walking and wheel-based locomotion called "Roller-Walk" by rotating its ankle joints 90 degrees to switch between different foot configurations. The ankle switching mechanism also employs 3D-printed lead screws, realizing a compact, lightweight, and high load-bearing capacity mechanical configuration. Locomotion experiments were conducted for both walking and Roller-Walk modes, confirming that the robot can successfully propel itself in both modes without issues. Particularly in Roller-Walk mode, the robot achieved a maximum velocity of 3.43 m/s and a CoT of 0.14, placing it among the group of walking robots with the highest mobility performance when compared to other walking robots.

title: Wavelet-enhanced Convolutional neural network integrated with the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (WCKAN) for Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Ashish Kumar, Rahul Dev Garg
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Nowadays, remote sensing is becoming a vast and important field, whether for environmental monitoring, agriculture monitoring, forest fire detection, coastal monitoring, urban planning, or many other related fields. Private and government firms are also involved in this field, whether for commercial use, public use or in the defense sector. A very important subfield related to this is, land cover classification. Many researchers have worked on this and are still working, in which the land has to be classified remotely and accurately. Because there are many changes in land from time to time, correctly classifying land is very complicated. For which many machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models are made, which categorize land by using labeled data. This study presents a Wavelet-enhanced Convolutional neural network integrated with the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (WCKAN) model to classify hyperspectral satellite data accurately and efficiently, to achieve high accuracy. This model is applied on three different geographical benchmarked datasets and found that this model works very well and achieves 98.67%, 99.41% and 99.70% overall accuracy on Indian Pines_(IP), Salinas_(SA) and Pavia University_(PU) data sets, along with 0.9970,0.9940, and 0.9968 kappa coefficients, respectively.

title: Mobile Robot Motion Planning Based on Time-Delay CNN with Open-Space Image Inputs for Multi-Obstacle Avoidance
authors: Kenji Shibata, Satoshi Hoshino
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: During autonomous navigation, mobile robots of-ten need to avoid obstacles in their path. To address this obstacle avoidance issue, we have proposed various motion planners based on deep neural networks. Focusing on obstacle avoidance, a mobile robot is typically required to move straight for a certain duration after avoiding an obstacle before reorienting itself toward the destination. However, in such cases, it is difficult for the robot to plan these different motions based on similar image inputs. To address this challenge, we propose a novel motion planner based on a Time-Delay CNN that utilizes visually distinct time-series image inputs. Through experiments, we demonstrate that the robot is able to plan appropriate avoidance motions as described above and navigate toward the destination in both simulation and real-world environments with multiple dynamic obstacles.

title: The Architecture of AI Agents in Web3: Decentralized Networks to Semi-Autonomous Systems
authors: Karanvir Singh, Vishav Pratap Singh, Arpit Mahajan, Jaideep Singh, Avreet Singh, Dhruv Soni
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: AI's merger with Web3 tech is changing the game leading to open, see-through, and somewhat self-running systems. This research explores the AI-agent paradigm in Web3 considering spread-out networks, blockchain rules, smart deals, and partial self-rule as new concepts. The main focus of this paper is to redefine the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) with Web3 technologies and create a semi-autonomous architecture that transcends decentralized and centralized approaches. While the majority of literature surveys AI agents that function on blockchain and decentralized protocols, our study presents a layered model that exploits off-chain AI inference with on-chain consensus mechanisms, (DID) management, and governance. We explain the main components, such as shared record-keeping, distributed ID management, reward systems, and agreement methods, that allow AI agents to function efficiently in the absence of a central boss. Moreover, the research analyzes significant issues such as scalability, safety, data privacy, and interoperability. It offers a number of improvements in off-chain AI-based agents for decentralized environments. Results demonstrate that hybrid on-chain/off-chain AI clusters can reduce inference costs and increase transaction throughput while preserving decentralization and data privacy. The team-up of AI and Web3 opens doors to new uses like spread-out money systems (DeFi), self-running groups (DAOs), and marketplaces without middlemen creating tough, clear, and user-focused digital worlds.

title: Motion Capture and Machine Learning-Based Evaluation of Surgical Skills in Laparoscopic Cadaver Training*
authors: Lingbo Yan, Takashige Abe, Koki Ebina, Masafumi Kon, Madoka Higuchi, Kiyohiko Hotta, Jun Furumido, Naoya Iwahara, Shunsuke Komizunai, Terpei Tsujita, Kazuya Sase, Xiaoshuai Chen, Hiroshi Kikuchi, Haruka Miyata, Ryuji Matsumoto, Takahiro Osawa, Sachiyo Murai, Toshiaki Shichinohe, Soichi Murakami, Taku Senoo, Masahiko Watanabe, Atsushi Konno
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: To promote efficient laparoscopic surgery education, a system utilizing machine learning has been developed to quantify surgical skill levels based on the movement of surgical instruments. In this system, the movements of surgical instruments operated by surgeons during laparoscopic cadaver surgery training are recorded using an optical motion capture system, and kinematic features are extracted from the recorded data. These extracted features are then used as input for machine learning models, with expert-evaluated scores—based on the Global Operative Assessment of Laparoscopic Skills (GOALS)—serving as the training data. The entire nephrectomy procedure was divided into three distinct processes: colon mobilization (Process 1), renal vascular dissection (Process 2), and incision and removal of the remaining tissues (Process 3). In this study, interpretable kinematic features were extracted from instrument movements during the colon mobilization phase (Phase 1). These features were used to train three regression models: Principal Component Analysis followed by Support Vector Regression (PCA-SVR), Partial Least Squares regression (PLS), and Ridge Regression. The models aimed to predict GOALS scores across five key domains: depth perception, bimanual dexterity, efficiency, tissue handling, and autonomy. Model performance was evaluated using 5-fold nested cross-validation repeated 100 times. Among the models, Ridge Regression consistently demonstrated high accuracy, with median mean absolute errors (MAEs) below 0.82 in most domains. This system is expected to contribute to more effective surgical education by providing multidimensional, objective feedback on surgical performance.

title: Diagnosis of Leaf Disease using Jaya Optimized Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Shantilata Palei, Puspanjali Mohapatra, Bhabatosh Mahanta
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Since leaf diseases pose a major danger to agricultural productivity and global food security, effective diagnostic and control methods are required. This study proposes a hybrid method to improve disease diagnosis accuracy by fusing the Jaya Optimization Algorithm (JOA) with the Deep Learning (DL) model ResNet50 and XceptionNet. In this work, 4,000 images of mango leaves from eight different classes are used as the dataset. The proposed method uses XceptionNet to extract features, and uses JOA to fine-tune hyperparameters to improve classification performance. Tested against numerous reference models, our methodology achieves 94.13% accuracy.

title: Machine Learning-Based SVPWM Modulation for T-Type Three-Level Inverters
authors: Zhengxiang Hu, Lei Wan, Shuo Wang, Jingyu Yao, Jingjing Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven approach to simplify Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) in T-type three-level inverters by replacing conventional modulation logic with supervised learning models. Traditional SVPWM requires sector identification, vector timing computation, and waveform construction, which introduce complexity and hinder real-time applications on embedded platforms. To overcome this, four regression models—a five-layer multi-layer perceptron (MLP), Random Forest, Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost)—are trained to map three-phase reference voltages directly to horn-shaped modulation signals. Training data are generated from conventional SVPWM across varying modulation indices, and models are evaluated in terms of waveform accuracy. Results show that the five-layer MLP achieves the highest accuracy with mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.00125, while Random Forest also demonstrates strong performance. The trained neural model is integrated into a Simulink-based NPC inverter, where it reproduces modulation waveforms with negligible error and maintains Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) at the same level as traditional SVPWM. These findings confirm that machine learning provides an efficient and accurate alternative to conventional SVPWM, enabling reduced computational complexity while preserving modulation quality in inverter control systems.

title: Hybrid CEEMDAN-BiLSTM-XGBoost Framework for AQI Prediction
authors: Aditya Sharma, Ronit Singh Negi, Amit Kukker, Sweta Gupta, Gaurav Pandey
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This study proposes a hybrid forecasting framework that integrates Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (CEEMDAN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). CEEMDAN decomposes the highly nonlinear AQI series into more stable components, which are modeled individually using BiLSTM for structured temporal patterns and XGBoost for irregular fluctuations. The predictions of all components are then combined to reconstruct the final AQI forecast. Using Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) data from Delhi spanning 2017-2022, the hybrid model was evaluated against standalone XGBoost, BiLSTM, and a simple XGBoost-BiLSTM framework. Results show that the CEEMDAN-BiLSTM-XGBoost model achieved the highest accuracy, with MAE of 3.59, RMSE of 4.44, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 8}$</tex>, substantially outperforming the baselines. The findings highlight the importance of decomposition-assisted hybrid approaches for improving AQI prediction in complex urban environments. This model provides a practical foundation for real-time air quality monitoring systems and decision-support tools aimed at mitigating health risks in highly polluted cities.

title: Performance Analysis of Traditional and Deep Learning Models for Agricultural Yield Estimation
authors: Khush Aghera K, Ruchi Patel, Megha Mudholkar, Pankaj Mudholkar
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Getting a good estimate on crop yields really matters for making smart policies, sorting out resources, and keeping food production going in a sustainable way. This paper looks into how standard machine learning setups and deeper learning ones do at predicting crop yields, using a real dataset from Rajasthan in India. They went through some tough cleaning and checking out the data first, then used it to train up five different models. Those included the Random Forest Regressor, an Artificial Neural Network called ANN, a Recurrent Neural Network or RNN, a Convolutional Neural Network known as CNN, and the Long Short-Term Memory model, LSTM. The outcomes showed the Random Forest Regressor nailed it pretty much spot on, hitting an R squared value of 0.9999, with RMSE at 0.0625 and MAE down to 0.0060. Among the deep learning ones, the CNN came out ahead of the rest, reaching an R squared of 0.9776. Overall, the work highlights how key the data prep steps are, and it breaks down what each approach does well or falls short on. These results give some solid ideas for building yield prediction tools that are dependable, easy to scale up, and make sense in farming data work.

title: MedAug-Net Powered Hybrid CNN-SVM: A Robust Model for Capsule Endoscopy Image Classification
authors: Mohan D N, Vidya V, Veena S, Prasanna Lakshmi G S, R Thiagarajan, Gunasundari B
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Since capsule endoscopy offers high-resolution, non-invasive imaging of the digestive tract, it has completely changed gastrointestinal diagnostics. However, the necessity for high diagnostic prediction precision, imbalanced datasets, and the variety of medical situations make automated classification of endoscopic images a considerable issue. In order to increase the precision and resilience of capsule endoscopy image classification, we provide a Hybrid CNN-SVM model in this work that combines the deep feature extraction capabilities of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with the higher classification margins of Support Vector Machines (SVMs). To ensure realistic variability free of structural distortions, the model uses MedAug-Net, a region-aware augmentation technique, to selectively improve non-ROI areas while retaining important diagnostic features. Furthermore, a Global Average Pooling (GAP) layer and depth wise separable convolutions are employed to improve computational efficiency without sacrificing robust feature representation. Better generalization and lower misclassification rates are ensured by replacing the traditional softmax layer with an SVM classifier with an RBF kernel. According to experimental results, the suggested model outperforms traditional Standard CNN architectures, which reach 98.30% accuracy under the identical experimental settings, with an accuracy of 99.47%. According to the findings, the Hybrid CNN- SVM model greatly increases classification accuracy when used in alongside MedAug-Net, making it an effective and clinically dependable method for analyzing capsule endoscopy images.

title: A Multivariate Parallel LSTM based Model for Bond Credit Rating Prediction
authors: Rohit Ahuja, Santosh Singh Rathore
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Bond ratings reflect a corporation’s level of riskiness and reliability to meet financial obligations. To determine bond ratings, financial organizations use models, but most are manual or semi-automated, taking significant time to provide new ratings and update older ones. Therefore, bond rating prediction using artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) has gained a lot of interest in recent years. However, previous models have shown limited prediction performance and higher misclassification errors. In this work, we develop a bondrating prediction model using the multivariate parallel LSTM technique. Our approach consists of two parts. Firstly, we extract significant stock movements and collect relevant pre, post, and contemporaneous text from various financial news sources. We assign each piece of news a positive or negative label. Secondly, we train a multivariate parallel LSTM model on the collected dataset and use it to make predictions for future news. We evaluate the performance of our model using S&P 500 Index Companies data collected from Stockrow and Yahoo Finance. The results showed that the developed model performed well for long-term and overall credit ratings, with predicted and actual values closely aligned. However, the model performed moderately for short-term credit ratings.

title: Physics Parameter-Free Stator Winding Temperature Estimation of PMSM via Black-Box Machine Learning
authors: Yuxin Fu, Dalei Ha, Chenwei Ma, Wensheng Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: A data-driven black-box machine learning approach is introduced for estimating stator winding temperature of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM), independent of traditional heat transfer physical models. An extended feature engineering strategy is developed using motor operating parameters, introducing exponentially weighted moving average (EWMA) and standard deviation (EWMS). Enhanced temporal characterization is achieved through this approach. Seven machine learning models are methodically evaluated, with extreme random trees (ETs) demonstrating superior performance compared to traditional physical models and deep learning methods. The study confirms the effectiveness and practicality of employing black-box machine learning for temperature estimation tasks.

title: Automated Bone Fracture Detection and Radiology Report Generation from X-Rays
authors: Charanya J, Vidhya R, Punitha Gowri R, Shahidkhan S, Naveen Ram C E, Naveen Kumar K
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Detecting bone fractures through medical imaging is very important for helping radiologists and making diagnoses more accurate. Interpreting X-rays by hand takes a long time and is easy to make mistakes, especially when dealing with complicated fractures. We suggest a framework for automatic bone fracture detection, localization, and report generation that combines deep learning and a large language model (LLM). Our system takes X-ray images as input, sorts them into fractured and non-fractured groups, and then uses bounding boxes to find the areas of the fractures. After that, the identified class information is sent to a Groq-powered LLM, which makes a structured report like a radiology report. We assess our methodology using the publicly accessible XR-bones dataset, which encompasses various anatomical regions (elbow, finger, forearm, hand, and shoulder) featuring both positive (fracture) and negative (no fracture) classifications. The experimental results show that this system is a useful tool for computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) in healthcare because it can accurately find fractures, pinpoint their locations, and create reports that take the context into account.

title: An Empirical Study Challenging the Ability of Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks to Effectively Learn Topological Structures
authors: Shuxin Chen, Xuan Guo, Huan Liu, Hongjiang Chen, Pengfei Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: The utilization of graph neural networks in heterogeneous networks has been rapidly increasing in recent years. The topological structure, fundamental to the nature of graphs, is often claimed to be jointly addressed with attribute information by most heterogeneous graph neural network (HGNN) approaches. However, this assertion lacks sufficient empirical validation. We empirically challenge these claims within existing evaluation frameworks. We propose a straightforward multi-layer perceptron (MLP) based model which relies solely on adjacency relationships for feature aggregation, without fully exploiting the graph's intricate topological structures. Unexpectedly, the model achieved nearly state-of-the-art performance on several real-world datasets. This discrepancy indicates that current evaluation protocols contain systemic biases and often fail to distinguish superficial adjacency exploitation from genuine structural pattern learning. It also points to the need for future benchmark datasets and metrics that more faithfully reflect the real requirements of topological learning. We hope this work stimulates further research into the role and significance of topological information in HGNNs.

title: Impedance-based Stability Characterization of Extended Finite Control Set MPC under Realistic Switching Constraints
authors: Chirag R. Shah, Margarita Norambuena, Marta Molinas, Jose Rodriguez, Sjur Føyen, Roy Nilsen
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Advances in digital processing have made finite control set Model predictive control (FCS-MPC) increasingly viable for real-world power electronics applications. However, the maximum switching frequency of FCS-MPC is determined by the controller sampling time, which leads to the maximum switching frequency in the range of tens of kHz to hundreds of kHz. The finite switching capabilities of semiconductor devices limit the achievable switching frequency in practical power converters. To address this, an extended FCS-MPC is proposed, which effectively limits the maximum switching frequency without explicitly modifying the cost function. To assess the impact of introducing these realistic switching constraints on the system’s behavior, a small-signal stability analysis of the proposed MPC is performed. An impedance-based analytical model of the proposed MPC is developed, verified, and analyzed to evaluate its stability characteristics. Furthermore, since the considered FCS-MPC formulation does not include explicit tunable parameters for impedance reshaping—such as the gain adjustments typically available in conventional PI-based control—an analytical equivalence between MPC and PI-based current control is established to provide a reference framework and to highlight their differences in converter impedance characteristics. It is shown through the analysis that the impedance of the FCS-MPC-based converter is an intrinsic property of the controller, inherently exhibiting a stable profile and serving as a benchmark for PI-based designs. The theoretical findings and stability assessments are experimentally validated using a Control-Hardware-in-the-Loop set up.

title: EETalk: Expression Enhancement in Speech-Driven 3D Facial Animation
authors: Zhaojie Chu, Kailing Guo, Xiaofen Xing, Bolun Cai, Lin Wang, Xiangmin Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Speech-driven 3D facial animation aims to generate natural and expressive facial movements from speech. Although significant progress has been made, existing methods still face challenges in generating realistic upper facial expressions. Specifically, existing methods that jointly optimize the holistic face tend to overlook fine-grained spatial movements due to motion differences across facial regions. Pre-trained speech feature extractors, which emphasize long-term dependencies, provide limited fine-grained temporal cues. In this work, we propose a novel framework, EETalk, to enhance the realism of facial expressions, which captures fine-grained spatial information and f ine-grained temporal dynamics from speech. To alleviate loss of fine-grained spatial information, we propose a novel disassemble and-reassemble modeling strategy. This strategy constructs two independent motion representation spaces for the upper and lower faces, allowing for the capture of weak upper-face movements while preserving motion diversity. Then, we propose a Cross-Region Coordination Module to ensure the synchronization and coordination of the movements from the independent upper and lower faces. To effectively capture facial micro-expressions, we incorporate fine-grained time-varying features to compensate for the short-timescale details underrepresented in the long term semantic features extracted by pre-trained self-supervised models, thereby improving the generation of fast, subtle facial expressions. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly improves motion accuracy, expression consistency, and perceptual quality compared to existing methods.

title: Intelligent Airspace Planning via Graph-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sui Zhou, Yunxiang Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Airspace planning is a critical component of Air Traffic Management (ATM), and its rationality directly impacts controller workload, airspace capacity, and operational safety. However, traditional planning methods, which rely on expert experience or static models, struggle to adapt to the increasingly complex and dynamic traffic environment and have limitations in achieving global optimality. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a novel intelligent airspace planning method based on machine learning. We abstract the airspace planning problem into an undirected weighted graph partitioning problem, where nodes represent key traffic load points in the airspace and their weights represent their load values. The objective is to balance the controller workload across partitions by assigning these nodes. To solve this graph partitioning problem, we construct a deep reinforcement learning framework that integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm. This framework leverages the GNN's capability to extract features from graph structures, effectively capturing the complex interdependencies between airspace nodes. Combined with the advantages of PPO in stable policy learning, the agent learns the optimal allocation strategy through interaction with the environment. The results demonstrate that the PPO method enhanced by GNNs slightly outperforms most comparison methods in achieving sector load balancing and exhibits excellent convergence stability.

title: Fractional-Order Online Policy Iteration-Based Approximate Optimal Control for Fractional-Order Nonlinear Systems
authors: Jie Kong, Guoling Yuan, Bo Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: In this article, a fractional-order online policy iteration (FOOPI) algorithm-based approximate optimal control scheme is developed for fractional-order nonlinear systems (FONSs). Using the fractional Taylor series and the property of Hadamard product, the fractional Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman (FHJB) equation corresponding to FONSs is formulated. Motivated by the procedure of solving the optimal control problem of integer-order nonlinear systems (IONSs), the design of the fractional-order (FO) controller for FONSs is transformed into solving the FHJB equation utilizing a FOOPI algorithm. By constructing a critic neural network (NN) and using the fractional calculus theory, the FO derivatives of the value function are established, followed by the approximate fractional Hamiltonian, which is obtained via the FOOPI algorithm. Hereafter, the FO control policy is obtained approximately to guarantee the stability of closed-loop FONSs via the FO Lyapunov’s direct method. Simulation results guarantee the effectiveness of the proposed FOOPI-based approximate optimal control scheme.

title: TMM-Enhanced Reliable, Concealed and Secure ISAC Architecture for Smart Grid Networks
authors: Yingwei Zhao, Yixin Jiang, Hong Wen, Li Jiang, Runhui Zhao, Yunying Man
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: As critical national infrastructure, the reliability and security of wireless communication and environmental sensing under the smart grid system face significant challenges in complex electromagnetic environments. This paper proposes an Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) architecture based on Time-Modulated Metasurface (TMM) to address these challenges. Leveraging TMM’s capability to simultaneously generate multiple controllable harmonics, the system enables highly reliable communication, sensing, and active electronic countermeasure functions on a single hardware platform, applicable to conventional data transmission, Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle (UAV) inspection, and other scenarios. Using a time-modulated reflection with angle-adaptive linear phase, the system achieves deception in both range and Doppler, effectively shielding critical power infrastructure from radar detection. For communication security, TMM is used for directional modulation, focusing signals toward legitimate receivers while radiating jamming signals toward potential eavesdropping directions, thereby achieving physical-layer security. Simulation results demonstrate excellent performance in electronic countermeasure scenarios, providing an innovative solution for the secure operation of critical power facilities in complex electromagnetic environments.

title: Relay Selection in Wireless Networks as Restless Bandits
authors: Mandar R. Nalavade, Ravindra S. Tomar, Gaurav S. Kasbekar
publish: IEEE Networking Letters
abstract: We consider a wireless network in which a source node needs to transmit a large file to a destination node. The direct wireless link between the source and the destination is assumed to be blocked. Multiple candidate relays are available to forward packets from the source to the destination. A holding cost is incurred for each packet stored at every relay in each time slot. The objective is to design a policy for selecting a relay in each time slot to which the source attempts to send a packet, so as to minimize the expected long-run time-averaged total packet holding cost at the relays. This problem is an instance of the restless multi-armed bandit (RMAB) problem, which is provably hard to solve. We establish the Whittle indexability of this relay selection problem and propose a computationally efficient policy to compute the Whittle index of each relay in every time slot. In each time slot, our relay selection policy transmits a packet to the relay with the smallest Whittle index. Using simulations, we show that our proposed policy outperforms several relay selection policies proposed in prior work in terms of the average cost, delay, as well as throughput.

title: Compiler-Enhanced Language for Scalable Data Workflows
authors: Himanshu Arora
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: This paper introduces a sophisticated programming language specifically designed for high-performance, data-parallel operations across diverse data types, including streams and data frames. Our framework integrates with advanced compiler infrastructure to facilitate the efficient translation and deployment of data-intensive programs on distributed systems. This innovative language aims to simplify the development of robust, scalable data science applications by abstracting away low-level distributed programming complexities and enabling powerful domain-specific optimizations.

title:
Rewarding Change Beyond State: Directional VLM Rewards for Sample-Efficient Robot Reinforcement Learning
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Linus Lundgren, Wenhao Lu, Zhitao Liang, Ze Zhang, Karinne Ramirez-Amaro, Emmanuel Dean
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Sparse rewards are a persistent bottleneck for robotic manipulation with Reinforcement Learning (RL), primarily because RL agents must discover long-horizon, multi-step behaviors while receiving infrequent and weakly informative feedback. Recent work uses pre-trained Vision Language Models (VLMs) to provide dense per-step rewards, yet most approaches score only a single image against a goal text, ignoring whether the recent change actually moves the system toward success. We argue that this omission impairs exploration (e.g., goal-like detours, wrong-way progress, action aliasing) and propose to make time explicit in VLM rewards by adding a directional signal that evaluates short-horizon change. Concretely, we pair visual change over a few steps with a text description of the desired change, and finetune lightweight heads with RL; the resulting directional signal is combined with a standard positional signal into a single shaping reward. We evaluated our approach in six MetaWorld manipulation tasks with fixed goals. This directional shaping improves running average success at a fixed budget to 78.2%, versus 63.8% for the best-tuned positional baseline (improvements were observed in five of six tasks). Ablations identify key design choices for the proposed directional term to be effective and show its synergy with the positional term when supplying dense VLM rewards, demonstrating improved exploration and sample efficiency.

title: Explainable AI Techniques on CBCT Image Quality Improvement model for Breast Cancer Surgery Patients
authors: Papia Banerjee, Kasturi Barik, Rajashree Nayak
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The dependability and interpretability of Deep Learning (DL) models for improving the quality of Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) images of Breast Conserving Surgery (BCS) patients are the key to their clinical incorporation. The primary focus of the paper is not to develop any novel DL architectures for CBCT image quality enhancement. The novelty of the proposed work is to perform a comprehensive cross-validation of model decisions using post hoc Explainable AI (XAI) techniques. XAI-interpreted decisions ensure reliable and consistent predictions for real-time clinical adaptation. Pretrained models such as VGG16, DenseNet169, ResNet50, and InceptionV3, leveraging the transfer learning (TL) approaches, improve the quality of CBCT images. These model outputs are interpreted using LIME, Grad-CAM, and Grad-CAM++ XAI techniques. Heatmaps, strip plots, swarm plots, and bounding box plots interpret the XAI outputs by highlighting the most influential regions in the model predictions. Intersection over Union (IoU), R-squared ($\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$), and Spearman’s rank correlation coefficient further validate the consistency and reliability of XAI outputs. Extensive experimental analysis demonstrates that Zscore outperforms the other compared normalization techniques in generating improved CBCT (iCBCT) images. The feature importance ranking stability is highest for Grad-CAM++, followed by LIME and Grad-CAM, with Spearman’s rank coefficient score of 0.6418,0.6113 and 0.6024, respectively. ICBCT images predicted from the hybrid (DenseNet169 + InceptionV3) TL model integrated with GradCAM++ achieved the best FSIM, SSIM and PSNR values of 0.8632,0.5443, and 16.83, respectively. Thus, GradCAM++ is the best recommended XAI technique to produce clinically interpretable heatmaps.

title: Grid Integration of Machine Learning -Based MPPT Photovoltaic System for Power Quality Improvement
authors: Riddhi Maratha, Sanju Tanwar, Seema Agrawal
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This work suggests a machine learning (ML)-based control approach for a grid-integrated photovoltaic (PV) system to improve power quality and energy extraction. A data-driven MPPT (maximum power point tracking) technique that dynamically adjusts the PV array's operating point under fluctuating temperature and sun irradiation circumstances is constructed using convolutional neural networks (CNN). The controller ensures optimal tracking efficiency and robustness to environmental changes by employing machine learning's capabilities to make intelligent real-time modifications. Additionally, to increase the efficiency of DC-DC conversion, the CNN generates the best pulsewidth modulation (PWM) signals for boost converter control. To provide smoother power injection into the grid and suppress highfrequency harmonics, an LCL filter is included at the grid interface. The proposed ML-driven control architecture significantly enhances system responsiveness, minimizes harmonic content, and improves power quality. Because convolutional layers preserve local features, CNNs perform better than ANNs in tasks involving spatial or grid-like data. CNNs have fewer parameters than ANNs and can automatically extract hierarchical patterns, which improves accuracy and generalization in applications like PV signal analysis and image processing. Simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink validate the system's effectiveness, demonstrating a low total harmonic distortion (THD) of 2.57%, in compliance with IEEE 519 standards.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Microsoft Semantic Kernel: Architecture, Capabilities, and Research Directions
authors: Angel Gupta, Mohit Goyal
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The incorporation of large language models (LLMs) in company software amplified the necessity for efficient AI orchestration systems. Microsoft Semantic Kernel is an optimized open-source system specifically for the implementation of LLMs in customary software systems. Despite its widespread popularity in corporate settings, specifically in Fortune 500 organizations, there is limited scientific research available pertaining to its architecture, characteristics, and disadvantages. This report gives an in-depth analysis of Semantic Kernel, including its fundamental constituents—kernel orchestration, plugin ecosystem, semantic memory, and agent framework—and implementations in multiple languages in C, Python, and Java. A comparative report with other frameworks like LangChain outlines the advantages of Semantic Kernel in maintaining dynamic and concurrent task orchestration. The work outlines key performance characteristics, integration possibilities, and current limitations and outlines useful insights into its applicability in practical AI engineering. In addition, the directions for future improvements are discussed in order to broaden the use in enterprise-scale AI systems.

title: ISED 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)

title: Performance Evaluation and Comparison of YOLOv8, MobileNet-SSD, and MTCNN for Face Detection in Smart Attendance System
authors: Arvinder Singh Dhoul, Burhanuddin Saifee, Ruchi Patel, Santosh Vishwakarma, Ritu Ahluwalia
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: In this study, three well-known face detection models YOLOv8, MobileNet-SSD, and MTCNN are thoroughly assessed and contrasted in relation to smart attendance systems. Finding the best detection algorithm for scalable, dependable, and realtime attendance tracking applications is the goal. The study uses OpenCV to process real-time video streams and combines face detection with a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) based face recognition system. The WiderFace dataset was used for detection in the experimental evaluations, and a custom dataset with 200 face embeddings per subject was used for recognition. In addition to other important metrics like precision, recall, F1-score, and IoU, the models were evaluated based on mean Average Precision (mAP), Frames Per Second (FPS), and model size. Although it had a comparatively larger model size (26 MB), YOLOv8 performed the best among the models, with a mAP of 0.94, precision of 0.95, and a frame rate of up to 120 FPS. Whereas MobileNet-SSD prioritized speed and portability (FPS up to 60) with modest detection accuracy (mAP 0.70), MTCNN provided a balance between accuracy (mAP 0.75) and model size ($5-10$ MB).The KNN-based recognition system achieved 92.5% overall accuracy, 96.2% sensitivity, and low error rates, with a False Non-Match Rate (FNMR) of 2.7% and a False Match Rate (FMR) of 0.5%. The results show that, particularly on hardware with limited resources, optimized YOLOv8 variants offer the best balance between accuracy and efficiency for real-time deployment in smart attendance solutions.

authors: Raghuveer Yerneni
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Agentic AI systems increasingly serve critical enterprise functions-planning, decision support, and customer interactions-but their memory substrates remain brittle, ungoverned, and unauditable. We introduce EMA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, an Enterprise Memory Architecture for Agentic AI, which unifies multi-tier memory (context cache, working set, semantic store, cold archive) with policy-aware read/write enforcement and verifiable W3C PROV-based lineage. EMA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> embeds a Policy Guardrail Engine aligned with GDPR, the EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO/IEC 42001 to enforce least-privilege access and retention controls, and uses a Retrieval Planner that optimizes a novel rate-distortionrisk (RDR) objective balancing task utility, compression loss, and governance risk. Experiments across enterprise QA and task agents show that EMA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> significantly improves retrieval quality, reduces hallucination, eliminates unauthorized memory access, and achieves $\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$ provenance traceability-without compromising latency or task performance. EMA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> provides a deployable, auditable memory substrate for trustworthy enterprise AI.

title: Approximate Computing-Based VLSI Architecture for Optimized 2D FIR Filter Implementation
authors: Abhishek Inguva, Nikhil Srinivas Charugundla, Shreya Reddy Konatham, Venkata Krishna Odugu, Harish Babu Gade
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The evolution of VLSI design methodologies has led to significant advancements in optimizing digital circuits in terms of Area Complexity (AC), Power Consumption (PC), and Latency. One of the promising approaches for achieving optimizations is the use of approximate computation. The approximate computations enhance the area, reduce power, and drastically improve the speed of the arithmetic architecture with adequate precision. The architecture of a Two-Dimensional FIR (2DFIR) filter can be optimized by incorporating the approximation in applications such as image processing. The approximation can be introduced in the 2DFIR Filter Architecture (2DFIRFA) by employing approximate compressors in multipliers with tolerable accuracy. The Exact 4:2 Compressor (E42C) is altered into an Approximate 4:2 Compressor (A42C) by bypassing certain inputs. In this work, the 2D FIR architecture is designed with the incorporation of Circular Symmetry (CS) to reduce the utilization of hardware components. The combined approach of using approximate computing and CS leads to a highly optimized 2DFIRFA. The designed work is synthesized by the Vivado tool from Xilinx and the Genus tool from Cadence 45 nm CMOS technology. As a result, both hardware and delay constraints are optimized, making it particularly advantageous for real-time image processing. The design leverages this approach to enhance performance while reducing complexity.

title: Grid Black-start Strategy for Offshore Wind Power and VSC-HVDC Transmission System
authors: Zhiyu Zhuo, Renxin Yang, Haiyang Tang, Xu Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The access of a high proportion of new energy leads to a decline in the inertia of the power system, reduces the stability and reliability of the grid, and increases the risk of fault outages. As an important part of new energy generation, offshore wind power should assume more grid responsibility, with its own black start and auxiliary grid black start capability. Based on this, this paper proposes a sequential black-start strategy, which contains offshore wind farm black-start, voltage source converter based high voltage direct current (VSC-HVDC) transmission system black-start and grid black-start. In this paper, a grid-forming offshore wind farm is used with a double-ended grid-forming VSC-HVDC transmission system, so that the whole system has black-start capability and grid-constructed operation capability, and the offshore wind power VSC-HVDC system can be used as the black-start power source of the onshore grid to assist in its restoration. This paper constructs a simulation model of offshore wind farm VSC-HVDC transmission system based on PSCAD/EMTDC simulation software, simulates and analyses the whole process of black start, and verifies the feasibility of the proposed strategy.

title: A Smooth Switching Strategy Between FOC and DTC for Robotic Joint Motor Based on Predictive PI Control
authors: Qiu Wu, Shuheng Qiu, Junzhou Wang, Yehong Huang, Xianbei Sun, Chi Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: In response to the high-performance control requirements of permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) in legged robotic joint drives, this paper proposes a hybrid field-oriented control (FOC) and space vector modulation–based direct torque control (SVM-DTC) strategy incorporating predictive Proportional-Integral (PI) control. Tailored to the dynamic characteristics of robotic jumping motions—comprising loading, burst, and reset phases—the method deploys FOC for smooth motion control and switches to SVM-DTC to achieve rapid torque response. To address current fluctuations caused by integral windup in PI controllers during mode switching, a voltage predictor based on the forward Euler method is designed, which resets the PI output to the predicted voltage value at the instant of transition. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively suppresses switching transients and enhances overall system control performance.

title: Integrating AI and Human-in-The-Loop Feedback for Enhanced Cyber Defense and Cryptanalysis
authors: Jay Gandhi, Shaveta, Ram Ashish Chouksey
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Traditional cybersecurity systems often rely on static intrusion detection and manual cryptanalysis, limiting their effectiveness against evolving threats. This paper introduces an AI-powered Cyber Defense and Cryptanalysis Automation prototype that integrates machine learning-based network anomaly detection with automated Vigenère cipher cryptanalysis. The anomaly detector uses a pre-trained Random Forest model to efficiently identify suspicious patterns in the NSL-KDD dataset, while the cryptanalysis module performs automated key estimation and decryption, enhanced with a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) mechanism for iterative user-guided improvements. Delivered using intuitive Streamlit web application, the prototype combines automated analysis with human insight, offering a practical and extensible framework for adaptive cyber defense. Unlike traditional IDS or cryptanalysis tools, this system learns from both data and user feedback, enabling continuous improvement and more effective defense against dynamic cyber threats.

title: Flexible Power Control of Bidirectional Modular Multilevel Resonant DC-DC Converter for Wide Voltage Range Applications
authors: Xiaosheng Zhang, Jing Sheng, Heya Yang, Xin Xiang, Wuhua Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The modular multilevel resonant DC-DC converters (MMRDC) are becoming a promising solution for medium voltage DC-DC conversion, owing to high reliability, flexible adjustment and high conversion efficiency. In this paper, a flexible power control method of bidirectional modular multilevel resonant DC-DC converter (BMMRDC) is proposed for wide voltage range applications. First, the gain characteristics of the BMMRDC topology are analyzed, and the limitations of the switching frequency regulation control method in achieving a wide voltage range are studied. Then, a dual-loop power control method is proposed, which combines the regulation of the continuously inserted submodule number and the switching frequency. Switching frequency regulation enables continuously bidirectional power regulation within a small voltage range, while regulation of the continuously inserted submodule number allows operation within a wide voltage range. Furthermore, a typical design of the dual-loop control method is provided. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulations on the Plexim/PLECS platform and experiments on a BMMRDC prototype. The results demonstrate that the proposed control method achieves wide voltage range control on the basis of bidirectional power flow.

title: Design of a High Current Low Voltage 48V-to-PoL DC-DC Converter for Data Center Applications
authors: Jingye Shen, Xu Yang, Zhihao Lou, Panming Li, Chufan Zhou, Jingyi Wang, Shimin Lian, Wenjie Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a 48V to the Point-of-Load(PoL) DC-DC converter for data center applications. The proposed design achieves single-stage conversion from 48V to PoL, maintains a high conversion ratio, enables automatic current balancing among multiple phases, and reduces the volume of magnetic components.

title: A Hybrid Predictive Framework for Career Path Recommendation using Ensemble Learning and Fuzzy-Logic Based Plausibility Assessment
authors: Ashirvad Samanta, K. Nikhil, Rama Ranjan Panda, Soumyajit Mohanty, Swayam Patnaik
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The selection of an optimal career trajectory is a critical decision-making problem, yet traditional guidance systems often lack the capability to evaluate non-linear career paths or provide nuanced, data-driven recommendations. This paper addresses this gap by proposing a hybrid predictive framework for intelligent career recommendation. The proposed work first conducts an extensive comparative analysis of eleven machine learning models to identify the most robust classifier for career prediction based on a holistic user profile. A key contribution of this work is the integration of a fuzzy logicbased system to subsequently assign a “plausibility score” to each predicted career, thereby validating unconventional trajectories by quantifying the compensatory effect of specialized skills. The proposed framework, utilizing an XGBoost classifier, achieves a mean Accuracy of 0.8961, demonstrating its effectiveness in providing accurate and contextually aware career guidance.

title: Mathematical Modeling and Simulation of Multimodal-Aware Virtual Traffic Lights for Safe and Efficient Urban Intersections
authors: Parul Arora, Devansh Arora, M.N. Hoda
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Conventional Virtual Traffic Light (VTL) systems have primarily been designed with motor vehicles in mind, often overlooking the requirements of pedestrians, cyclists, e-scooters, and autonomous shuttles. This oversight reduces equity and increases risk at multimodal intersections. In this work, we present a Multimodal-Aware Virtual Traffic Light (MAVTL) framework that extends VTLs to incorporate all categories of road users through a priority negotiation mechanism. The method assigns a dynamic priority score to each mode of transport by combining queue length, accumulated waiting time, and safety sensitivity. The resulting optimization problem is formulated with explicit fairness and safety constraints, while ties among competing flows are resolved using a Nash bargaining-based negotiation strategy. To validate the framework, a detailed simulation study was carried out using SUMO, with comparisons against traditional adaptive signal control and vehicle-only VTL approaches. The proposed MAVTL reduced average waiting times for vulnerable road users by 27%, improved overall intersection throughput by 15%, and lowered near-conflict risks by 22%. These findings demonstrate that incorporating a mathematically grounded negotiation protocol within VTL systems can significantly improve both efficiency and safety in urban intersections, while offering a scalable pathway for future deployment in smart mobility infrastructures.

title: A New Design Methodology for LLC Resonant Converters by Considering both Steady State and Dynamic Performance
authors: Shuo Zhang, Ziang Li, Junhong Yu, Jinjun Liu, Yuqi Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents a new design methodology for LLC resonant converters that optimizes both steady state and dynamic performance. Unlike conventional design methods focusing solely on steady state metrics, the proposed framework incorporates dynamic response characteristic analysis into the design stage. Based on an accurate dynamic time domain model, the design candidates are effectively reduced through two key considerations: 1) applying common steady state constraints, including voltage gain range, zero voltage switching (ZVS) operation, voltage stress for resonant capacitor, and the primary side root-mean-square (RMS) current; 2) evaluating closed-loop bandwidth derived from a standardized PI controller tuning procedure. Within the reduced design space, multi-objective optimization is performed to obtain Pareto-optimal trade-offs between steady state and dynamic performance. Simulation results validate that the proposed design method can achieve great balance between steady state and dynamic performance, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed methodology.

title: Frequency-deviation feedforward-based synchronization enhancement for grid-connected inverters
authors: Cheng Luo, Mi Qi, Jianbo Cao, Zonghui Chen, Dapeng Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The synchronization stability of grid-connected inverters is crucial for the reliable operation of renewable energy system. It is found in this article that under weak grid conditions, as the increase of active power, the inverter is prone to collapse due to phase locking failure when the output reactive power is insufficient. The reason lies in the positive feedback loop in the phase-locked loop. An increase in the active power and a decrease in reactive power can both lead to the expansion of the positive feedback effect and even result in the collapse of the phase-locked loop. A frequency deviation-based feedforward control strategy is proposed in this paper, which can weaken the response of the positive feedback loop and enhance the synchronization stability. Finally, the simulation results verify the theoretical analysis.

title: Compliance-by-Design for IoT Cloud Platforms: A Reference Architecture and Case Study
authors: Kunal Khanvilkar
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: As IoT ecosystems increasingly rely on cloud platforms to store, process, and manage sensitive data, ensuring compliance with evolving privacy and security regulations has become critical. This paper presents a novel compliance-by-design reference architecture for IoT cloud platforms, which embeds regulatory controls into the system’s operational fabric from the outset. Unlike traditional post-hoc audit models, our approach integrates consent tracking, data retention enforcement, access control, and audit logging as modular, enforceable services across device, gateway, and cloud layers.
