We validate the architecture through a smart energy monitoring case study, demonstrating high compliance enforcement accuracy, audit readiness, and scalability. Comparative evaluation against state-of-the-art compliance frameworks highlights significant improvements in policy enforcement and latency overhead. The proposed architecture offers a practical and extensible blueprint for building regulationready IoT systems.

title: Fuzzy-Enhanced Deep Learning Model for Robust Cardiac Disease Prediction using CFDLNet Algorithm
authors: A. Divya, T.Ananth Kumar
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Cardiac disease continues to be a major contributor to global mortality, highlighting the urgency of timely and precise prediction to enhance patient outcomes. Traditional machine learning and deep learning techniques have shown promising outcomes in predicting cardiac diseases, but their accuracy is influenced by noisy clinical data, imbalanced features and lack of interpretability. To address these issues, this paper introduces a novel algorithm named CFDLNet (Cardiac Disease Prediction based on Fuzzy-Enhanced Deep Learning Network) which involves using fuzzy logic with deep learning to achieve robust and stable prediction outcomes. Fuzzy preprocessing handles uncertainties and ambiguity in medical records, while the deep learning architecture predicting nonlinear relationships among clinical parameters. The interpretability of fuzzy logic and the representation power of deep learning enables CFDLNet to reduce misclassification, and show generalization with various datasets. Experiments on benchmark heart disease datasets show that CFDLNet significantly outperforms classical machine learning and recent deep learning models achieving an accuracy of 97.36%, precision of 96.34%, recall of 95.65% and F1- Score of 93.87%. The results indicate that CFDLNet offers an explainable and predictable framework of forecast which may be implemented in clinical decision support systems to detect the cardiac disease in real-time.

title: AI and Human Collaboration in Media: Preparing Media Professionals for AI-Integrated Workplaces
authors: Sania, Ambooj, Arpita Nagpal, Preeti Rai, Rinku Sethi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is altering the media industry by automating content generation, enabling personalization and automating decision-making. This manuscript evaluates how AI amalgamates with media professionals and highlights the need for ethics, and the creation of adaptive competencies. We also analyze how the key issues like trustworthiness, ethical challenges and skill deficiencies can be overcome. This investigation also underscores the emergent trends including collective leadership and tailored AI systems which are poised to transform future media methodologies.

title: Motion Planning Leveraging High-Speed Sensors for Conventional Industrial Manipulator
authors: Misato Koreki, Usukhbayar Chuluunbat, Hikaru Arita, Kazuto Nakashima, Kenji Tahara
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: High temporal resolution sensors have become increasingly widespread in recent years, enabling detailed and accurate observation of fast physical phenomena and contributing to the understanding of their essential properties. However, a fundamental challenge remains in bridging the temporal resolution gap between such sensors and conventional industrial robots. Typical high-speed visual feedback control require all system components to operate at high speed, inherently restricting their applicability to phenomena within the robot’s dynamic capabilities. As a result, valuable high-speed sensing data often cannot be fully utilized, especially when target phenomena evolve faster than the robot can respond. This study proposes a novel motion planning method that extracts essential, task-relevant information from high-speed sensor data to bridge the timescale gap between sensors and robots. Based on the extracted information, feedforward control is executed with consideration of the robot’s motion characteristics. To explore practical solutions, we developed an integrated system combining a high-speed camera and a proximity sensor module―both with existing applications in robotics―with a general-purpose industrial robot and gripper. A case study involving the grasping of a pendulum-swinging object by a low-speed robot demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach in utilizing high-frequency measurements for tasks beyond the limits of conventional feedback control.

title: Precision Diagnostics in Prostate Cancer: The Role of AI and Advanced Imaging
authors: Amit Khatri, Shahzad Alam, Danish Raza Rizvi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Prostate cancer is among the most common cancers affecting men worldwide, and timely, accurate diagnosis plays a vital role in ensuring effective treatment and better patient outcomes. Conventional diagnostic approaches, such as digital rectal examination (DRE) and prostate specific antigen (PSA) testing, remain widely practiced but are often limited by issues of sensitivity, specificity, and subjective interpretation. The introduction of multiparametric magnetic resonance imaging (mpMRI) has improved the ability to detect suspicious lesions and guide biopsies, thereby reducing unnecessary procedures. In recent years, artificial intelligence (AI) particularly machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) has emerged as a powerful addition to prostate cancer diagnostics. These tools are showing results that match, and in some cases surpass, radiologist performance in areas such as lesion identification, risk assessment, and integrating multimodal clinical data. However, several hurdles remain, including dataset bias, the need for broader validation, challenges in interpretability, and unequal access to advanced technologies across healthcare systems. This review discusses current diagnostic practices, examines the growing role of AI in prostate cancer detection, and outlines existing challenges and research gaps. Addressing these barriers will be key to developing diagnostic strategies that are more accurate, accessible, and patient-focused.

title: SII 2026 Commentary
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)

title:
Impact of Bi
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Nanoparticle Dispersion on the Electrical Properties of Natural Ester Oils for High Voltage Insulation
authors: Md Anas Habib, Abdul Suboor, Sumbul Afroz, Obaidur Rahman, Asfar Ali Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable, highperformance insulating fluids in power transformers and other high-voltage equipment has led to the development of esterbased nanofluids as an eco-friendly alternative to conventional mineral oils. This study investigates the impact of dispersing bismuth oxide (Bi<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) nanoparticles into natural ester oil and evaluates its suitability as a next-generation insulating medium. Nanofluids were synthesized at varying Bi<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> concentrations (5 mg, 10 mg, and 15 mg per 400 ml) using a two-step preparation method involving surfactant-assisted ultrasonication. Electrical characterizations were performed according to IEC 60156 and IEC 60247 standards, measuring AC breakdown voltage (BDV), dielectric dissipation factor (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tan \delta$</tex>), dielectric constant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{r}$</tex>), insulation resistance (IR), and DC resistivity. Results revealed that an optimal concentration of 10 mg Bi<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> yielded the most significant improvement in insulation performance, reducing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tan \delta$</tex> by 57.69%, increasing BDV by 9.43%, and enhancing insulation resistance at both 25 °C and 90 °C. Comparative analysis with conventional oils and previously reported nanofluids highlights the advantages of Bi<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-ester nanofluids in terms of environmental sustainability, safety, and insulation reliability. These findings demonstrate that Bi<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> nanoparticledispersed natural ester oils hold strong potential as environmentally benign insulating fluids for high-voltage equipment.

title: Deep Learning Models for Accurate Moisture Deficit Prediction: A Fusion of Weather and Soil Information
authors: A. Balachandar, S. Mohan, A. Ashmitha
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: An extended duration of precipitation that is Water is caused by below average shortages, which have a negative impact on ecosystems, agriculture, economies, human health, and energy resources. It starts with less rainfall, which depletes moisture content of the soil and lowers the amount of water within rivers and reservoirs. Drought worsens and spreads over time, leading to crop failures, environmental damage, and widespread shortage of water. Approximately 40% of the global populace is impacted by the increasingly frequent and severe droughts that affect about 40% of the land. Climate change and record global temperatures have caused some of the most destructive droughts in recorded history, according to recent data from 2023 to 2025. Millions of people are now food insecure as a result of these droughts, and in areas that are already at risk, agricultural output has significantly decreased. Economic losses are large and are predicted to increase dramatically by 2035. Droughts have become more frequent by 29% in the last 20 years, and by 2050, they may affect more than 75% of the world's population, potentially uprooting millions of people and exacerbating global food and water insecurity. To help lesson the consequences of this crisis, improved drought monitoring, preliminary warning systems, and sustainable water management are desperately needed. In order to predict daily evaporation rates across seven synoptic stations with varying climatic conditions, this research study presents a novel approach to drought forecasting that makes use of an optimization algorithm. Using soil data from the Earth's surface, the study forecasts drought-related evaporation using Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbour (KNN) models.

title: Semantic-Guided Channel Denoising for Robust Wireless Image Transmission
authors: Huiping Liu, Xuyang Chen, Yuxi Gan, Dayang Liu, Yapeng Zhao, Daquan Feng
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: Semantic communication (SemCom) methods currently demonstrate significant advantages in perceptual quality but generate semantic distortions and image artifacts under strong channel interference. To address this, a novel semanticguided channel denoising model (SGCDM) based framework is proposed for SemCom to achieve robust image transmission over noisy channels. The framework deploys the SGCDM at the receiver, which leverages one-hot encoded semantic segmentation maps as conditional prior information to precisely eliminate channel noise and facilitate reconstructing images with strong semantic consistency and visual fidelity. Meanwhile, the model’s forward noise injection process aligns with real-world channel noise characteristics, allowing the receiver to initiate conditional reverse sampling based on the received corrupted signals and estimated channel state information, thus significantly accelerating image reconstruction. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms baseline schemes across various channel conditions. Remarkably, even in severely degraded channels, its LPIPS metric is 25% lower than that of traditional schemes under favorable channel conditions, reconstructing semantically valid images with high perceptual quality, demonstrating its strong robustness and semantic preservation capabilities.

title: Simulation and Analysis of Strong Coupling Effect on the Dynamics of Double-Output Boost Converter
authors: Adnan Mukhtar, Pyare Mohan Tiwari, Mohammed Ali Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: At strong coupling, coupled inductor-based double-output boot converters suffer from issues, such as cross-coupling and cross-regulation, especially under varying duty cycles and changing loads. This work delineates the circuit simulation of a double-output boost converter, analyzes how strong coupling affects the dynamics of the converter, and illustrates the time-domain variations with duty cycles and changing loads. The boost converter's performance is evaluated in MATLAB using extensive simulations, with a special emphasis on its capacity to adjust output voltages and respond dynamically to changing operating conditions. In addition, a Type-II compensator is designed to show the importance of closed-loop control in double-output DC-DC converters for effective suppression of cross-coupling and cross-regulation. This research sheds insight on the converter's behavior and demonstrates its appropriateness for practical applications in power electronics.

title: Evaluating the Cloud Access Protocols: The Art of Minimalist Security by Applying the Principle of Least Privileges
authors: Vikas Dubey, Bharti Dubey, Divyarth Rai, Anamika Singh
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: In the real-world, organizations gradually recognize the critical importance of implementing a streamlined security protocol, emphasize efficiency while preserving protective integrity. This review paper identifies cloud vulnerabilities by ensuring minimum privileges. It is important to have a guarantee that cloud services offer minimal privileges to their end users. Several strategies and approaches are available in the domain for the implementation of the principle of Least Privilege, including Role-Based Access, Attribute Based Access, Policy Based Access and many more, newly evolved AI Based Techniques. This review is designed to point out the vulnerabilities in the cloud environment that may be present within current operations, Through, a thorough assessment and evaluation of each methodology is performed still, the manuscript aspires to investigate the most optimal approach for organizations to implement PoLP. It also explores modern Access Control Protocols and compares to identify the best protocol to follow PoLP.

title: LINI: LFM Signal Source Identification Method Based on Non-source Component Suppression and Interpretable Time-frequency Network
authors: Lei Yan, Siya Mi, Hao Dai, Yile Kuang
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: Linear Frequency Modulated (LFM) signals are widely used in radar and electronic warfare systems. LFM signal source identification is of great significance. As an emerging technology, Radio frequency fingerprint(RFF) is unique and non-clonable. It has become a promising tool for signal source identification. The modeling of the radio frequency fingerprint generation mechanism determines the effectiveness of feature extraction. Existing modeling methods cannot sufficiently suppress non-source components, which can cause the extracted RFF to be easily interfered with by other components. This results in RFF deviation and reduces recognition stability. Moreover, the identification based on nerual network is a black box, which makes it difficult to explain the results. In this paper, an LFM signal source Identification method is proposed, which is based on non-source components suppression and interpretable time-frequency network. By analyzing the generation of the transmitted signal, non-source components can be suppressed and the effective features of the signal can be extracted. Then a time-frequency network (TFN) is used to fully extract the features of the RFF and perform source identification to improve the identification effect. The experimental results show that the proposed method has strong robustness and high identification accuracy in actual scenarios. More importantly, the proposed method allows the trained model to be quickly applied to different receivers without retraining.

title: Design and Sensitivity Analysis of Strip AT-Cut Quartz Force Sensors for Multi-Axis Robotic Applications
authors: Huodong Zhu, Jiaoying Jiang, Chin-Yin Chen, Chengyi Wu, Min-Chiang Chao, Guilin Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: High-precision multi-axis force sensing is crucial for advanced robotic applications such as precision assembly and adaptive grasping. This paper presents the design and sensitivity analysis of a strip-type AT-cut quartz crystal force sensor for these applications. An analytical model, derived from perturbation theory, establishes that the ratio of force-frequency coefficients between the primary (x-axis) and transverse (y-axis) directions is governed by the crystal’s aspect ratio and their stress sensitivity coefficients. This relationship provides a key guideline for structural design. Finite element simulations on five geometric designs validate this model, showing a stable sensitivity scaling factor of 3.88. Experimental results from a prototype sensor demonstrate a highly linear force-frequency response, confirming the practical viability of the optimized design. This work provides an effective strategy for developing high-performance, multi-axis force sensors for robotic systems.

title: Intelligent Traffic Management System Using AI and DL Opencv to Optimize Traffic Flow and Reduce Congestion in Cities
authors: Senthil G. A, Manoranjitham S, K. Arthi, Sasikumar R, Rukmangathan D, S Vaheethabanu
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Urbanization has greatly intensified traffic congestion in major cities, leading to extended travel durations, excessive fuel consumption, and heightened environmental pollution. Traditional traffic control mechanisms are often inadequate for handling the dynamic nature of present-day urban road systems. This study proposes an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based Intelligent Traffic Management System (ITMS) designed to forecast and alleviate traffic congestion using the YOLOv4 model integrated with OpenCV. The system employs computer vision to process live video streams from traffic surveillance cameras, enabling precise detection of vehicles, pedestrians, and movement behaviors at intersections. Leveraging adaptive AIbased traffic signal optimization, the system intelligently modifies signal durations to reduce delays and enhance traffic flow efficiency. OpenCV supports real-time detection and classification, while deep learning algorithms forecast congestion patterns and suggest alternate routes to ensure smoother mobility. Moreover, IoT-enabled sensors supply additional information such as vehicle velocity and environmental parameters further improving system accuracy and responsiveness. Experimental simulations under real-world traffic conditions demonstrate a 30 % decrease in average waiting time and a significant enhancement in traffic circulation. The proposed framework also minimizes vehicle idling, contributing to lower fuel usage and reduced carbon emissions. With a vehicle detection accuracy of 95.2 % achieved through YOLOv4, the AIdriven ITMS offers a dependable, scalable, and energy-efficient approach to modern traffic management, advancing the goals of smart city development and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Passpotron: An Integrated Framework for Real-Time Passport Authentication Using OCR, Facial Biometrics, and Eye-Blink Liveness Validation
authors: Krishna Chaitanya Chirravuri, Shamneesh Sharma, Saikat Gochhait, Aman Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Digital environments today require immediate systems to authenticate digital identities in order to verify users. Passpotron operates as a slim software platform which merges passport text scanning and face recognition along with living presence check capabilities into a single integration method. The software implements PaddleOCR for document reading and uses LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instant with Groq for data processing and face authentication tasks through Dlib's ResNet model. Two detection approaches determine present living status - Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR) measurements track eye-blinks and facial landmark detection establishes bodily presence. The Streamlit interface brings users of the system to an accessible and user-friendly interface. The system functions perfectly under various environmental conditions including low lighting and poor camera quality video. Results from multiple tests of OCR software demonstrate that PaddleOCR stands out from alternative solutions through its superior speed and precision performance. The research shows that authentication systems made up of multiple verification methods work effectively with high accuracy and operation rates for eKYC and identity verification tasks.

title: A Brief Survey of Persistence Approaches in Real World Malwares
authors: Bhavesh Gilhare, Vikas Sihag, Gaurav Dave
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Malware is designed with various malicious objectives, such as encrypting files in the case of ransomware or capturing user inputs through keyloggers. Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs), in particular, rely heavily on persistence techniques to maintain long-term access to compromised systems. These techniques enable malware to survive system reboots, user logins, and shutdowns. This paper explores a range of realworld persistence methods employed by malware, analyzed in the context of the MITRE ATT&CK framework

title: Scalable Deep Reinforcement Learning-based User Pairing for Cell-Free MIMO Network
authors: Fan Meng, Xiao Bai, Zening Liu, Cheng Zhang, Huan Ni, Jingze Wu, Zhiyu Yang, Jianjie You, Yongming Huang, Dong Han
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Wireless networks continuously evolve to support higher data rates and accommodate a growing number of wireless devices. However, conventional scheduling methods either incur high computational complexity and excessive latency, or suffer from significant performance degradation under imperfect system conditions. In this work, we employ a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework to address the NP-hard user pairing problem in cell-free multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) networks. The proposed algorithm operates across multiple resource-block-groups (RBGs) in parallel, leveraging a pointer network that observes RBG-specific, transmit-power-scaled channel state information (CSI) to select users of this RBG. Then, RBG-specific uplink throughput serves as a feedback to optimize the user pairing policy without requiring labeled data. Simulation results show that the proposed approach is highly scalable across different deployment scenarios with varying network configurations. Meanwhile, the proposed DRL-based scheme achieves a favorable trade-off between computational complexity and throughput performance. Furthermore, DRL consistently outperforms all baselines particularly in the presence of CSI estimation errors and unknown noise, owing to its feedback-driven learning mechanism.

title: Stability Assessment of Spire and PlanetiQ Receiver Clocks and Its Implications for GNSS-RO Atmospheric Profiles
authors: Zhe Li, Pengyue Sun, Xiaoming Wang, Dingyi Liu, Haobo Li, Ying Xu, Jinglei Zhang, Sizhe Shen, Hongxin Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Commercial SmallSats offer cost-effective alternatives to traditional GNSS radio occultation (GNSS-RO) missions through scalable constellation deployments. In GNSS-RO processing, the short-term stability of the LEO receiver clock is a key constraint on the feasibility and accuracy of undifferenced (UD) retrievals. Specifically, limited stability in compact receivers can introduce clock noise that degrades retrieval profiles and increases uncertainty. This study evaluated Spire and PlanetiQ onboard clock stability and quantified the impact on bending-angle and refractivity retrievals. Spire exhibited lower short-term clock stability, with 1-s clock stability exceeding <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-6}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, making UD infeasible, while its single-differenced (SD)-derived profiles remained consistent with UCAR and ECMWF reference datasets. In contrast, PlanetiQ exhibited better short-term stability, with the 1-s clock stability typically better than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-9}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Subsequent analyses were confined to clock segments with 1-s clock stability better than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-12}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, sufficient for accurate SD and UD processing. For PlanetiQ, refractivity derived from both methods was in agreement between 10 and 25 km (mean bias <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$< 0.05\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, standard deviation <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$< 1\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>); above 25 km, SD showed slightly larger deviations due to reference-link noise. Across constellations, GPS showed the lowest deviations while GLONASS had the highest. Sensitivity tests with injected clock noise targeting 1-s clock stability over the range of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-12}$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-10}$</tex-math></inline-formula> showed that UD and SD were statistically comparable when 1-s clock stability was about <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.0\times 10^{-11}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and the retrieval deviations increased with both altitude and noise amplitude. These results confirm that PlanetiQ's high clock stability supports accurate SD and UD retrievals and provide valuable insights for oscillator selection, quality control, and processing strategy in cost-effective GNSS-RO missions.

title: Energy-Aware and Loss-Resilient Data Collection for UAV-Assisted Unbalanced IoT Networks
authors: Yingying Ren, Shaolin Liu, Chaohong Tan, Sijing Duan, Zuqiang Meng, Yongxin Liu, Anfeng Liu, Houbing Herbert Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: In infrastructure-limited Internet of Things (IoTs) networks, sensing devices are sparsely and irregularly distributed, with limited or intermittent access. Transmiting the data to cloud in real-time is unreliable due to constrained connectivity, harsh environmental conditions, or the energy limitations of nodes. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) offer an energy-efficient solution by periodically collecting sensing data via short-range communication. To address energy constraints and data loss in unbalanced IoTs networks, we propose DQGDC, an energy-aware and loss-resilient UAV data collection framework. DQGDC jointly optimizes hovering point deployment, flight trajectory, and missing data recovery to improve efficiency under energy limitations. Firstly, a clustering and particle swarm optimization-based hovering point design minimizes the number of UAV hover points and redundantly covered nodes. Secondly, a graph convolutional network and double deep Q-Network-based trajectory planner enables adaptive, energy-efficient flight paths. Thirdly, to enhance resilience against sensing loss from inaccessible or unvisited nodes, we develop a Q-learning driven deep nonnegative matrix factorization model for reconstructing missing sensing data. Extensive experiments on realistic and simulated IoTs scenarios demonstrate that DQGDC effectively balances UAV energy consumption and data collection completion.

title: Robust Bidirectional Power Control of Grid-Tied Inverters under Unbalanced Grid Currents Conditions Using a Proportional-Integral-Resonant Controller and Symmetrical Component Extraction Method
authors: Haneen Ghanayem, Ma’Moun Al-Smadi, Mohammad Alathamneh, R. M. Nelms
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a robust control strategy for grid-tied three-phase inverters operating under unbalanced grid currents conditions using a proportional-integral-resonant (PIR) controller integrated with a symmetrical component extraction method (SCEM-PIR). The proposed technique aims to enable precise bidirectional control of real and reactive power, mitigate power quality issues, and maintain voltage and current balance at the point of common coupling (PCC), even in the presence of grid impedance and nonlinear or unbalanced loads. Unlike conventional PI- or PR-based strategies that struggle under weak or distorted grid scenarios, the SCEM-PIR approach combines the advantages of both control techniques and adaptively tracks frequency variations. Simulation and experimental results validate the proposed method across various test conditions, including unbalanced resistive loads and rectifier-based nonlinear loads. The SCEM-PIR method significantly reduces total harmonic distortion (THD), enhances transient and steady-state performance, balances the grid, and ensures reliable power delivery regardless of the load or grid imbalance.

title: LLM with Relation Classifier for Document-Level Relation Extraction
authors: Xingzuo Li, Kehai Chen, Yunfei Long, Min Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have created a new paradigm for natural language processing. Despite their advancement, LLM-based methods still lag behind traditional approaches in document-level relation extraction (DocRE), a critical task for understanding complex entity relations within long context. This paper investigates the causes of this performance gap, identifying the dispersion of attention by LLMs due to entity pairs without relations as a key factor. We then introduce a novel classifier-LLM approach to DocRE. Particularly, the proposed approach begins with a classifier designed to select entity pair candidates that exhibit potential relations and then feed them to LLM for final relation classification. This method ensures that the LLM's attention is directed at relation-expressing entity pairs instead of those without relations during inference. Experiments on DocRE benchmarks reveal that our method significantly outperforms recent LLM-based DocRE models and narrows the performance gap with state-of-the-art BERT-based models.

title: Context-Aware Embodied Question Answering Agent With Large Language Model
authors: Zihang Wang, Haonan Luo, Botao Jiang, Jiahe Li, Jin Zheng, Xiao Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Embodied Question Answering (EQA) requires agents to explore the environment actively and gather reasonable visual information to answer the questions raised by users. Despite the significant progress attained, previous works lack a comprehensive understanding of the observed environment. Particularly, in the question answering task, little attention is paid to the temporal context in multiple observations and the semantic understanding of the environment. Meanwhile, the navigator struggles when combining global spatial context and individual observations to comprehend the geometric environment. In this paper, we propose Context-aware Embodied Question Answering (CaEQA) to address the issues from two aspects. Firstly, to integrate temporal context into image embeddings obtained from the local observations at the navigation endpoint, we propose a local temporal context-aware visual question answering module (LTC-VQA). Specifically, we utilize the large language model in the question answering module to enhance the temporal and semantic understanding of complex unseen environments by introducing general knowledge obtained in the pretraining stage. Secondly, to model the complex interdependence within the overall navigation procedure, we propose a global-geometric-guided navigation module (G3-Nav), facilitating the interpretation of global historical information. In G3-Nav, we use a transformer that incorporates RGB images, depth maps and actions from all past positions to provide geometric hints from inter-image and intra-image perspectives. Experimental results on the Matterport3D and House3D datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms the SoTA methods in terms of navigation, question answering and the overall EQA system.

title: Beneficial Noise Learning for Robust Multimodal Fusion
authors: Aoqiang Zhu, Min Hu, Xiaohua Wang, Fuji Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Multimodal fusion aims to integrate complementary information from different modalities, yet it is often hindered by data distribution imbalance and cross-modal conflicts. In this paper, we propose Beneficial Noise Learning for Robust Multimodal Fusion (BRMF), a novel framework that reframes these challenges as beneficial perturbations signals for robust training. Specifically, BRMF first learns a stable and consistent joint representation within a Gaussian latent space under distributional constraints. Based on this joint representation, it then constructs diverse distributional environments and further encourages invariance by extending invariant risk minimization (IRM) to multimodal settings. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that BRMF exhibits superior robustness to noise and establishes new state-of-the-art performance.

title: Rendered 2D Semantic and Generative Priors Guided 3D Multi-Object Grounding
authors: Peng Guo, Hongyuan Zhu, Hancheng Ye, Yihang Yang, Fukun Yin, Tao Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: 3D multi-object visual grounding aims to identify and localize all objects in a 3D scene that correspond to a given text description. Unlike traditional single-object grounding, this task presents additional challenges as point clouds inherently lack fine-grained details, making it difficult to capture subtle object features and contextual information. Moreover, textual descriptions are inherently limited in perceiving and understanding complex 3D environments, especially in scenarios with high object similarity or intricate spatial arrangements. To tackle the above challenges, we propose SGMG, a Rendered 2D Semantic and Generative priors guided 3D Multi-object Grounding Framework. The SGMG framework introduces two key innovations that work cohesively to enhance grounding accuracy. First, a Generative-Assistant(GA) Module leverages the capabilities of a generative model to provide enriched scene prior information and capture the fine-grained scene details. Second, the Semantic-Augment Fusion(SAF) Module is designed to improve the representation of text features from the vision features, thereby boosting the accuracy of multimodal information interactions. Furthermore, we introduce a multi-level fusion mechanism, ensuring that semantic and spatial relationships between objects are preserved and effectively leveraged during the grounding process. Experimental results demonstrate that SGMG achieves state-of-the-art performance in multi-object 3D grounding and competitive results in traditional single-object tasks, highlighting its effectiveness in diverse scenarios.

title: LightQANet: Quantized and Adaptive Feature Learning for Low-Light Image Enhancement
authors: Xu Wu, Zhihui Lai, Xianxu Hou, Jie Zhou, Ya-nan Zhang, Linlin Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Low-light image enhancement (LLIE) aims to improve illumination while preserving high-quality color and texture. However, existing methods often fail to extract reliable feature representations due to severely degraded pixel-level information under low-light conditions, resulting in poor texture restoration, color inconsistency, and artifact. To address these challenges, we propose LightQANet, a novel framework that introduces quantized and adaptive feature learning for low-light enhancement, aiming to achieve consistent and robust image quality across diverse lighting conditions. From the static modeling perspective, we design a Light Quantization Module (LQM) to explicitly extract and quantify illumination-related factors from image features. By enforcing structured light factor learning, LQM enhances the extraction of light-invariant representations and mitigates feature inconsistency across varying illumination levels. From the dynamic adaptation perspective, we introduce a Light-Aware Prompt Module (LAPM), which encodes illumination priors into learnable prompts to dynamically guide the feature learning process. LAPM enables the model to flexibly adapt to complex and continuously changing lighting conditions, further improving image enhancement. Extensive experiments on multiple low-light datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, delivering superior qualitative and quantitative results across various challenging lighting scenarios.

title: Hall-Effect–Sensor–Based Simultaneous Translational and Rotational Position Sensing System
authors: Seongjin Hur, Ye gu Kang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a novel Hall sensor–based methodology capable of simultaneously measuring rotary and translational positions for magnetic levitation systems and robotic joints. The sensing structure employs twelve low-cost Hall-effect sensors, arranged as four sets of three-phase sensors, to measure the radial magnetic flux generated by permanent magnets mounted on the moving part. By strategically placing the sensors in radial symmetry, differential information among the sensor sets is utilized to extract both x–y translational displacements and the rotary angle θr. The proposed algorithm estimates positions using only simple addition and multiplication operations, with minimal signal filtering applied solely for noise suppression, resulting in nearly negligible phase delay. Using zero-sequence components derived from Clarke transformation, the method enables fast and accurate estimation of all three position components. The fabricated prototype achieves a rotary range of 0 to 2π and a translational range of ±3 mm along both x- and y-axes, while maintaining a compact and low-cost configuration suitable for integrated multi-axis position sensing.

title: SIM-TRGAN: A Two-stage Text Repair Model based-on Semantic Injection Module
authors: Jiaxing Chen, Ting Xu, Jingsong Duan
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Due to the particularity of ancient books and the limitations of digital technology, digital images of ancient books are often accompanied by significant noise pollution, which seriously restricts the further application and dissemination of digital achievements of ancient books. Therefore, how to effectively remove the noise in the ancient book image and improve the image quality has become an urgent problem in the field of ancient book digitization. The SIM-TRGAN generator proposed in this paper mainly includes the two-stage repair machine of Semantic Repairer and Text Renderer. This model first repairs the semantic mapping of text images, then text repair and pixel level rendering. In addition, the semantic condition vector is injected into the target text image during training, which drives the generator to actively adjust the repaired effect.

title: The Anti-Arc Corrosion Performance of CuCr Alloys at Different Micro–Nano Scales Based on Magnetohydrodynamics (MHD) and Equivalent Heat Capacity Method
authors: Rong Liang, Yuwei Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This article presents a numerical investigation on the arc erosion resistance of three types of CuCr structures: a conventional disordered alloy (A), a surface-nanostructured alloy (B), and a micrometer-scale layered composite (C), with a focus on their interaction with arc plasma under high-voltage conditions. Coupled magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) simulations are utilized to analyze multiphysics behavior in the arc zone, encompassing electromagnetic fields, fluid flow, heat transfer, and molten pool dynamics. The results show that Structure B attains the highest critical breakdown field strength of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.58\times 106$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V/m, which is 41% greater than that of Structure A, indicating superior dielectric strength and arc erosion resistance. The self-induced magnetic field in the arc plasma and the resulting Lorentz force are found to significantly affect plasma flow patterns. Structure B exhibits the lowest magnetic flux density, which contributes to a higher plasma flow velocity—12.7% greater than that of Structure A—and enhances convective cooling, thereby reducing thermal accumulation. Consequently, Structure B achieves the lowest surface heat flux and the smallest ablation depth, measuring 0.13 mm, which is 48% less than that of Structure A. Meanwhile, the ablation depth of Structure C is 0.17 mm, which is 32% less than that of Structure A. Structure C demonstrates intermediate performance, which is attributed to its anisotropic thermal conductivity that restrains vertical energy dissipation. These findings highlight the critical role of micro–nano structural design in regulating arc plasma dynamics and provide valuable insights for the development of advanced contact materials for plasma-facing applications.

title: Benchmarking Dehazing Methods for Traffic Scenes: A Systematic Evaluation Framework
authors: Haolei Mao, Huxiong Li, Jun Zhang, Jiale Zhang, Yu Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Single-image dehazing has witnessed remarkable progress in recent years, with numerous algorithms proposed to enhance visibility in adverse weather conditions. However, existing review articles often lack systematic performance comparisons under standardized experimental settings, making it challenging to objectively assess the true capabilities and limitations of different approaches. This paper presents the first comprehensive benchmark for single-image dehazing in traffic scenarios, addressing the critical need for fair and systematic evaluation. We meticulously review the evolution of dehazing methodologies, from prior-based to learning-based approaches, and establish a unified evaluation framework using the Foggy Cityscapes dataset. Under consistent training protocols and parameter settings, we systematically evaluate 12 representative dehazing algorithms across multiple dimensions: reference-based metrics (PSNR, SSIM, CIEDE2000), no-reference metrics (NIQE), computational efficiency, and practical impact on downstream tasks (object detection). Our experimental results reveal significant performance disparities among state-of-the-art methods and highlight the domain gap between synthetic and real-world hazy images. Furthermore, we identify key challenges and promising research directions, including the need for improved generalization to real-world scenarios, better balance between performance and efficiency, and more effective handling of non-uniform haze distributions. This benchmark provides valuable insights for researchers and practitioners in selecting and developing dehazing methods for autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Fine-Grained Image Classification Based on Attention Mechanism and GeM Feature Fusion
authors: Haitao Xin, Qilu Jiang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Fine-grained image classification aims to distinguish sub-categories under a general category, characterized by small inter-class differences and large intra-class variations. This paper proposes an improved fine-grained image classification model based on the Squeeze and Excitation attention mechanism, Generalized Mean pooling, and multi-level feature fusion. The SE module enhances the discriminative power of important feature channels, GeM pooling replaces the traditional average pooling to retain more global context, and multi-level feature fusion integrates representations from different depths of the network to improve local detail perception. Experiments conducted on the CUB-200-2011 dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves higher accuracy compared with the baseline ResNet50, validating the effectiveness of the combined strategies.

title: Based on Self-Supervised Features and Discriminative Learning for Zero-Shot Voice Cloning
authors: Yiyao Gao, Long Zhang, Shengya Yu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Voice cloning aims to generate synthetic speech that closely resembles a reference voice. However, traditional speaker encoders have limited feature extraction capabilities under low-resource conditions, which affects the quality and similarity of synthesized speech. To address this issue, this paper proposes a speaker encoder that integrates self-supervised speech representations with a Speaker-Discriminative Representation Learner (SDRL). The proposed method leverages self-supervised models to obtain rich speech priors, thereby reducing dependence on large datasets, and employs a multi-scale TDNN as the backbone of SDRL. Through discriminative training, the model enhances the compactness and separability of speaker features. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms traditional methods in terms of speaker similarity (SECS), Mel-cepstral distortion (MCD), and subjective MOS and SMOS scores, achieving a significant improvement in preserving speaker individuality in synthesized speech.

title: Command and control network fault detection method based on improved LSTM
authors: Fengchao Lin, Yu Ren, Yanzhong Zhao, Ruigang Zhao, Heyong Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Aiming at the three typical faults that are easy to occur in the equipment in the command and control network, such as service stop, service slow down and error output, this paper proposes a fault detection method based on multi-head self-attention mechanism and long short-term memory network. Simulation experiments show that the proposed method can detect the equipment failure and normal time in the network in time. Compared with the traditional fault detection method based on long short-term memory network, the average accuracy of the detection is higher.

title: Fast Prediction of Wave Overtopping Discharge over Mangrove-Lined Seawalls using MLP
authors: Pintian Huang, Zhenshan Xu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Accurate and rapid prediction of wave overtopping discharge is a core coastal defense task, yet existing methods struggle with the complex hydrodynamics of Nature-Based Solutions (NBS) like mangrove-lined seawalls. Empirical formulas lack accuracy for such systems, while high-fidelity numerical simulations are too computationally expensive for real-time warnings. Furthermore, existing machine learning models suffer from a critical "scale mismatch," failing to efficiently downscale large-scale operational storm surge forecasts to specific seawall sections. This study develops a high-precision, high-efficiency Artificial Neural Network (ANN) as an advanced predictive tool to bridge this gap. We first established and validated a SWASH non-hydrostatic model to generate a large-scale training dataset accurately representing the complex mangrove-seawall hydrodynamics. Based on this data, we trained a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) to construct a non-linear mapping from nearshore boundary input parameters directly to the overtopping discharge. The primary contribution is an efficient downscaling tool that solves the speed-versus-accuracy trade-off, effectively connecting operational forecasts with the refined, site-specific overtopping assessment required for emergency response.

title: Neural Tucker Convolutional Network for Water Quality Analysis
authors: Hongnan Si, Tong Li, Yujie Chen, Xin Liao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Water quality monitoring is a core component of ecological environmental protection. However, due to sensor failure or other inevitable factors, data missing often exists in long-term monitoring, posing great challenges in water quality analysis. This paper proposes a Neural Tucker Convolutional Network (NTCN) model for water quality data imputation, which features the following key components: a) Encode different mode entities into respective embedding vectors, and construct a Tucker interaction tensor by outer product operations to capture the complex mode-wise feature interactions; b) Use 3D convolution to extract fine-grained spatiotemporal features from the interaction tensor. Experiments on three real-world water quality datasets show that the proposed NTCN model outperforms several state-of-the-art imputation models in terms of accuracy.

title: Research on 5G Signal Coverage Optimization Model for Smart Cities
authors: Qin Wu, Shuyan Wang, Xiangping Rong, Yuting Yang, Jin Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of smart city construction, the requirements for the accuracy and stability of 5G network coverage are constantly increasing. Traditional two-dimensional network planning methods can not match the multi-scenario and multi-level communication needs in complex urban spatial structures. Focusing on the typical environments of smart cities, this paper proposes a 5G signal coverage optimization model for three-dimensional space and designs an adaptive compensation method for path loss. By constructing a simplified three-dimensional urban model and combining heuristic optimization algorithms, the collaborative optimization of base station layout and transmission parameters is realized, which effectively improves the uniformity and continuity of signal coverage.

title:
Empirical Evaluation of Service Robot for Library Helpdesk Using Cloud Computing Services with Artificial Intelligence
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Tomohiro Umetani, Sachiko Enomoto, Hiroto Tsutsui, Sotaro Tanigawa, Tatsuya Kitamura
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study presents a library service robot rapidly developed using cloud artificial intelligence (AI) services and empirically demonstrates the robot’s service in a public library. The developed robot system interacts with a cloud service for speech recognition and synthesis with high accuracy and low latency over a short duration. The robot has been in operation for three years in a university library. To conduct demonstration experiments at a public library, a book search system that does not use personal information was used, which differs from the system that has been in operation at the university library for several years. In addition, for the demonstration experiment at the public library, we formally negotiated with the library and set conditions such as the questioning method for the verification experiment. We show the feasibility of the proposed system with a spoken dialogue service robot by conducting demonstration experiments in the university and public libraries.

title: A Bidirectional Closed-Loop Data Collaboration Method for Multi-Network Integration in Smart Ports
authors: Yongkang Wang, Jun Wu, Yi Ding
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: Smart ports' digital transformation faces data asynchrony and command delays in multi-network systems. This paper proposes a bidirectional closed-loop method integrating dynamic event-triggered and prescribed performance control through error transformation. The architecture co-optimizes communication and performance while ensuring stability without Zeno behavior. Simulations demonstrate superior accuracy, efficiency, and convergence for enhanced port safety management.

title: A Novel AI-Enhanced Textile-Embedded Wearable Biosensor for Non-Invasive Vocal-Fold Monitoring_supp1-3667224.mp4
authors: Hyoungsuk Yoo
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper presents a novel, AI-enhanced, textile-integrated spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) biosensor for real-time, noninvasive monitoring of vocal-fold activity. The sensor is embedded in a soft smart choker, operates at 2.45 GHz (ISM band), and is interrogated by a software-defined radio (SDR) to measure minute phase variations induced by vocal-fold vibration. A combined driven-modal/eigenmode design flow yields a compact tapered SSPP structure with strong through-transmission and enhanced evanescent coupling. A cotton-based prototype fabricated with conductive adhesive tape and flexible SMA terminations was evaluated on volunteers. Recorded RF data were processed using a phase-demodulation/windowing pipeline and classified with lightweight multilayer perceptrons (MLPs), achieving 95.9 % mean accuracy (SD = 0.0092) under five-fold cross-validation. Specific absorption rate (SAR) analysis on a realistic human model measured 0.616 W/kg (1 g) and 0.163 W/kg (10 g), below IEEE limits, supporting long-term wear. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first SSPP wearable biosensor enabling vocal-fold monitoring, providing a foundation for intelligent, real-time laryngeal diagnostics.

title: Stability analysis method for grid-forming wind turbine and energy storage interconnected system
authors: Xiao Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the development of grid-forming control technology, the future power system features coordinated operation of multiple grid-forming devices. However, it is not clear how the interaction between the grid-forming devices affects the frequency oscillation. This paper takes wind turbine as an example, and firstly constructs the interconnected system of wind turbine and energy storage. Secondly, the closed-loop transfer function of the phase perturbation of wind turbine including energy storage dynamics is derived, used to analyzes the influence of different grid strengths on the system's small-signal stability. Finally, the simulation results in Simulink illustrate the validity of the proposed analytical method.

title: ADS: A Math Word Problem Solver Based on LLM Prompt Engineering
authors: Fuxiao Ma, Long Zhang, Xiaonan Chang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Solving math word problems (MWP) is a crucial task in natural language processing, with methodologies having evolved from traditional rule-based approaches to deep learning and large language models (LLMs). However, existing methods still face challenges in handling complex MWPs, such as maintaining consistency in multi-step reasoning, mitigating error propagation, and improving language comprehension. To address these issues, this study proposes an Adaptive Deep-Shallow Prompting (ADS) framework, grounded in the dual-process theory of cognition. The ADS framework dynamically selects either a rapid derivation or an in-depth analysis strategy through a three-step process: strategic selection, problem decomposition, and answer generation. Experimental results demonstrate that ADS achieves higher accuracy and enhanced interpretability on both Chinese and English MWP datasets, outperforming traditional deep learning methods and existing LLM-based approaches. The proposed framework effectively improves the efficiency and adaptability of LLMs in solving complex problems.

title: A Gate Driver Based on LLC Resonant Converter and Enhanced Insulation Planar Transformer with Overcurrent Protection for 15 kV SiC MOSFETs
authors: Qiling Chen, Hong Zhang, Yisen Lv, Dingkun Ma, Tianshu Yuan, Peiyang Ding, Jiacheng Guo, Laili Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To meet the stringent requirements of gate driver power supplies for 15  kV silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs including high isolation voltage, superior dv/dt immunity, and fast fault protection, this paper proposes a high-performance isolated gate driver based on an LLC resonant converter and a planar PCB transformer with enhanced insulation. The proposed driver incorporates a full-bridge LLC topology with open-loop LDO regulation to optimize the circuit structure to simplify transformer implementation. A planar transformer with multi-layer PCBs and Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) interlayer insulation is developed, achieving an isolation strength exceeding 15 kV and a low common-mode capacitance of 3.53 pF, enhancing noise immunity during fast switching transients. Furthermore, a fast desaturation protection circuit is implemented by eliminating the blanking capacitor, significantly reducing response time. Experimental results validate the design: partial discharge (PD) test confirms insulation reliability at 15  kV; an 8  kV double pulse test (DPT) demonstrates a dv/dt of 61.17  V/ns with suppressed common-mode current; the desaturation protection achieves a net response time of 270  ns. This compact, robust gate driver offers an effective solution for medium-voltage (MV), high-frequency SiC applications.

title: Study on the Selection of Periodic Frequency Modulation Parameters Based on Common-Mode Noise Suppression
authors: Yong Mo, Wenjie Chen, Yongqi Huo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper investigates the common-mode noise suppression effectiveness of periodic frequency modulation (PFM) across center frequency (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>) ranging from 100kHz to 1MHz. It focuses on the influence of key PFM parameters—modulation waveform, center frequency (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>), modulation frequency (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>), and maximum frequency deviation (Δf)—on noise reduction. The study utilize an EMI prediction model incorporating the resolution bandwidth (RBW) of EMI test receiver. The results demonstrate that sawtooth wave modulation outperforms sinusoidal and triangular wave modulation in noise reduction. And show that the higher f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>, the greater maximum noise suppression, with a range of 5dB to 15dB observed across the studied f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>. As f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf> increases, The lower bound of the ratio of the optimal Δf and f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf> also increases. Furthermore, constrained by the RBW, the optimal f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf> range is found to lie between 8kHz and 14kHz.

title: Advancing Face Recognition Systems for Attendance: Overcoming Lighting and Expression Challenges
authors: Luke Jonathan, Amalia Zahra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Facial recognition for attendance applications remains a challenging task due to environmental variability such as lighting changes and diverse facial expressions. A hybrid architecture approach is proposed in the paper, integrating deep learning and traditional machine learning methods to handle these limitations. CNNs extract both abstract and fine-grained facial features, which are optimized by PCA to reduce dimensionality and redundancy. Then, a robust decision boundary under a Support Vector Machine classifier is utilized to classify results. The experimental results indicated that the fine-tuned CNN–PCA–SVM model achieved a test accuracy of 0.94 and an F1-score of 0.93, superior to that of the baseline CNN by 29% with an inference time reduced by 4.6×. These results confirm the high efficiency and generalization capability of the model in unconstrained lighting and expression scenarios. It provides a practical and scalable method for automated attendance verification and extends the real-time face recognition systems in the dynamic environment.

title: A Low-Cost UAV-Based Framework for Post-Seismic Crack Detection with CNN and Gesture Control
authors: Oscar Ramirez-Ayala, Carlos G. Valerio-Naranjo, Ruben Fuentes-Alvarez
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Traditional post–earthquake inspections are slow, costly, and subject to human error. This paper presents an autonomous structural inspection system that combines a low–cost unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) with a Proportional–Derivative (PD) flight controller, a convolutional neural network (CNN) for crack detection, and a gesture–based user interface for intuitive operation. Implemented on a DJI Tello platform, the system achieves 98% validation accuracy on a dataset of 15,594 images while maintaining stable flight and executing predefined inspection trajectories via hand gestures. Results indicate the feasibility of integrating UAVs and deep learning to optimize post–seismic inspection workflows.

title: Domain-Adaptive One-Shot Target Presence Detection
authors: Somesh Agrawal, Sudipan Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of target presence detection across domains in scenarios where training data is scarce or nonexistent. Building upon recent advancements in foundation models, we leverage the Segment Anything Model (SAM) with carefully selected image-based prompts to detect the presence of a target object in a query image using just a single example (key) image. Unlike prior methods that rely on adversarial training, our approach is completely training-free and robust to changes in domain, modality, and distribution. We propose two complementary strategies: one based on combining multiple key images to enhance prediction confidence, and another using cyclic consistency via reverse masking and IoU validation. Experimental results demonstrate that confidence-based prompt ensemble methods significantly outperform IoU-based selections. Our findings show strong adaptability across domains and highlight the potential for extending this framework to multi-class object detection.

title: Accelerating Teleoperation Skill Acquisition through Visuo-Haptic Replay
authors: Francisco Ribeiro, Jihoon Park, Minoru Asada, Erhan Oztop
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Training humans to control dynamic systems such as prosthetic limbs or teleoperated robots typically requires extensive practice. In this study, we investigate whether passive exposure to skilled control behavior—delivered via synchronized haptic and visual playback—can accelerate visuomotor learning in a non-trivial control task. Using a simulated cart-pole environment, we compare performance between participants who received an initial session of passive visuo-haptic replay and those who directly began with active control. Our results show that passive exposure yields two clear benefits: improved initial performance and a steeper learning curve across subsequent sessions. These findings suggest that passive sensorimotor experience, even without haptic guidance during active control, can support the acquisition of motor skills necessary for dynamic control. This approach may provide a low-effort and scalable training paradigm for enhancing skill acquisition in robotic and assistive technologies.

title: Evaluating LIME-Based Explainability for Phishing URL Detection Across Multiple Machine Learning Models
authors: Dhanu Ahsan Fatih, Akbar Hamonangan, Tonny Hidayat
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Phishing websites remain a major cybersecurity threat, exploiting deceptive URLs to mislead users and steal sensitive data. While machine learning (ML) models such as Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, and ensemble-based algorithms have shown high performance in phishing detection, their decision processes often lack transparency. This study evaluates the Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) framework as the primary explainability method across five ML classifiers: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost. Using a labeled phishing URL dataset, models were assessed with standard performance metrics and further analyzed using LIME through fidelity, consistency, fragility, stability, and computation time. The Decision Tree model achieved the highest consistency score (0.171), followed by LightGBM (0.164), while the feature nb_hyperlinks consistently emerged as the most influential across all models. Results indicate that boosting-based classifiers provide more stable and faithful explanations but at higher computational cost, whereas simpler models offer faster yet less robust interpretability. This evaluation highlights how explanation quality varies across model architectures and demonstrates that LIME can enhance understanding of phishing detection systems, bridging the gap between accuracy and interpretability.

title: Movement-Specific Analysis for FIM Score Classification Using Spatio-Temporal Deep Learning
authors: Jun Masaki, Ariaki Higashi, Naoko Shinagawa, Kazuhiko Hirata, Yuichi Kurita, Akira Furui
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: The functional independence measure (FIM) is widely used to evaluate patients’ physical independence in activities of daily living. However, traditional FIM assessment imposes a significant burden on both patients and healthcare professionals. To address this challenge, we propose an automated FIM score estimation method that utilizes simple exercises different from the designated FIM assessment actions. Our approach employs a deep neural network architecture integrating a spatial-temporal graph convolutional network (ST-GCN), bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM), and an attention mechanism to estimate FIM motor item scores. The model effectively captures long-term temporal dependencies and identifies key body-joint contributions through learned attention weights. We evaluated our method in a study of 277 rehabilitation patients, focusing on FIM transfer and locomotion items. Our approach successfully distinguishes between completely independent patients and those requiring assistance, achieving balanced accuracies of 70.09-78.79% across different FIM items. Additionally, our analysis reveals specific movement patterns that serve as reliable predictors for particular FIM evaluation items.

title: Optimization of Multi-Network Port and Time Synchronization for the Tunnel ORB-SLAM System
authors: Yewen Zhang, Hong Wang, Guowen Ding, Yilei Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: In tunnel environments, ORB-SLAM2 suffers from multi-sensor time misalignment and serial transmission bottlenecks. This paper introduces a multi-port transmission and hardware-level synchronization scheme. Using the IMU’s PPS signal as a reference, cameras are synchronized via hardware frequency division, while Precision Time Protocol (PTP) calibrates dual-port image output. Dual Ethernet bonding overcomes bandwidth limitations, enabling low-latency stereo data transmission. Experiments in low-texture tunnels show increased feature matches per frame and up to 39.7% higher trajectory tracking success, supporting accurate equipment positioning.

title: A Bibliometric Study on Unskilled and Reskilled in the Era of Robotics and Automation
authors: Shilpa Wadhwa, Neha Garg
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The rapid advancements in robotics and automation have significantly impacted global labor markets, raising concerns about the displacement of unskilled workers and the growing need for workforce reskilling. This study conducts a comprehensive bibliometric analysis to investigate the research trends related to unskilled labor, workforce reskilling, and robotics over the past fifteen years. Utilizing data from prominent academic databases, including Scopus database, the analysis identifies key themes, influential authors, prolific institutions, and emerging keywords in the field. The results reveal a shift in research focus from concerns about labor displacement to adaptive strategies such as digital upskilling and lifelong learning. The study highlights an increasing interdisciplinary approach encompassing economics, education, technology, and policy. Co-authorship patterns indicate strong collaboration networks among European and Asian countries, with the United States and Hong Kong showing moderate collaboration. The co-citation analysis reveals influential authors bridging technical research with broader socio-economic implications. The study also identifies research gaps, emphasizing the need for human centered, inclusive, and policy-oriented perspectives in the context of Industry 4.0. Future research should address the emotional, psychological, and social needs of diverse worker populations, while policymakers should prioritize inclusive labor transition frameworks and invest in public-private upskilling partnerships. The findings provide valuable insights for policymakers, educators, and researchers to develop strategies that mitigate the impact of automation on vulnerable labor groups and foster an inclusive workforce in the era of robotics

title: Eddy Current Brake Design: An Axial Type Technique
authors: Arunesh Kumar Singh, Rohit Kumar, Reyaz Ahmad Dar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: An electric braking system known as an Eddy Current Brake (ECB) uses eddy currents to produce braking forces. The design of axial ECBs, which have a lot of promises as an alternate braking method, is analyzed in this paper. There are three types of axial Eddy Current Brakes (ECBs): unipolar, double disc, and single disc variants. The arrangement of the coils, which produces eddy currents in the disk's axial region, and the electromagnet's design determine the categorization. Enhancing braking performance is one of the difficulties in creating axial ECBs, and it is also covered. The report notes that little study has been done on changing the electromagnetic ECB's pole-shoe form in axial configurations to change the magnetic field's direction. This opens up an intriguing line of inquiry for further study.

title: Sectoral Impact Analysis of Trump’s Election and Policies on LQ45 Stock Prices
authors: Tora Fahrudin, Adithya Krishnamoorthi, Thinesh D, Farissa Rejingga, Hermalia Putri, Velani Febriana Putri
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study investigates the sectoral impact of Donald Trump’s election and subsequent policy implementations on the stock performance of LQ45 companies in Indonesia. By leveraging unsupervised learning techniques, we aim to cluster the price performance of 45 listed companies to identify underlying market behavior patterns during this geopolitical shift. Three clustering methods are employed to capture different structural characteristics of the data: K-Means (partition-based), DBSCAN (density-based), and Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering (AHC). The resulting clusters are evaluated using internal cluster evaluation such as Silhouette Coefficient, Davies–Bouldin Index, and Calinski–Harabasz Index to ensure robustness and interpretability. The analysis reveals that most LQ45 stocks grouped in Cluster 0 experienced significant foreign capital outflows. In contrast, Cluster 1 contained a single outlier stock (GOTO), which exhibited strong foreign inflows and high transaction volume, indicating strategic investor interest likely tied to expectations of benefiting from trade reallocation or domestic resilience.These findings offer valuable insights into market segmentation under external political shocks and highlight the importance of sector-specific resilience in Indonesia's capital market.

title: Solar and Biomass Based Hybrid Power System for Institutions in Delhi-NCR
authors: Amit Atri, Anita Khosla, Ashish Grover
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid power system consisting of rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) and biogas powered Solid Oxide Fuel Cell (SOFC) installed in an educational institute in Faridabad, India. Geographical location of the institute is suitable for PV installation and the nearby villages are having good number of animals which is suitable for biogas system. The southern area of the campus is having good solar access and PV system is installed here. Animal waste is collected from nearby villages and biogas is obtained from it using the process of anaerobic digestion. SOFC uses the biogas for generating electric power. This isolated system is simulated in Homer which is totally fulfilling only the institute's energy requirements with optimal ratings of PV system as 3.2 MW, SOFC as 250 kW and cost of energy as US $ 0.0899 (Rs. 7.4) which is lesser than the cost which institute is currently paying ie. US $ 0.121 (Rs. 10).

title: 3DSPC-ResNet3D: A ResNet-3D Architecture Tuned by 3DSPC for Precise Hyperspectral Classification
authors: Ravikant Kumar Nirala, Gautam Kumar, Rishav Singh, Chandra Prakash
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The hyperspectral image (HSI) classification is a challenging task based on high dimensionality in the spectral form, scarce labeled data, and variance across space. To overcome these problems, we introduce the idea of a new deep learning structure that is founded on a 3D CNN with Residuals (ResNet3D) to successfully perform spectral and spatial feature extraction and be able to classify the data successfully. In contrast to traditional 3D CNNs which can in deep networks tend towards vanishing gradients and over fitting, ResNet3D adds residual connections based on the identity. This allows parametrization of much deeper networks with good performance, and jointly with structures of reflectors sequential processing of spectral signatures and spatial context to be efficiently trained. The given model is tested on the benchmark Indian pines data set through the parameters Overall Accuracy (OA), Average Accuracy (AA) and Kappa coefficient. The comparison of experimental results proves that our model is better than the various state-of-the-art methods, where it shows around 98.2% accuracy. By comparative analysis, it is seen that there is greater accuracy in the classification of many classes, with the most apparent enhancement in the spectrum overlapping classes or classes that are minority, also it visualizes with

title: Deep Feature Learning and Hybrid Classification for Indian Coin Recognition
authors: Deepak Banerjee, Raghavendra Sridhar, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Vatsala Anand, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Computing, Communication and Automation (ICCCA)
abstract: This research promotes a deep learning framework for automatically identifying Indian coin denominations, along with the problems raised in coin classification in India due to a number of coins being visually similar. Five classes, which are ₹1, ₹2, ₹5, ₹10, and ₹20, have been trained for the conducted model.
