Evaluation metrics show a very high precision of 98.56 % (₹1), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 5 5 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{2 2} \boldsymbol{)} \boldsymbol{,} \mathbf{9 8. 5 2 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{2 5} \boldsymbol{)} \boldsymbol{,} \mathbf{9 8. 6 2 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{2 1 0} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, and 98.60% (₹20). The recall and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score being the same as their respective precision for each class indicates that the model has been able to predict both ways fairly in a balanced manner. Support values range between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 3 0}$</tex> for ₹5 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 1 8 0}$</tex> for ₹10, with each class contributing to about 20 percent of the total dataset. All of the classes reached classification accuracy levels of 99 %, confirming the consistency of the model. These results indicated that the model could make distinctions between coin types, even if they looked nearly similar in size and design. Also credited to the success of the model is a well-built database and strong architecture of the neural network. Practically, this could go a long way in improving the coin handling efficiency of machines such as vending machines, banking kiosks, and automatic sorting systems. In conclusion, the overall high performance of the model across all having strong promises of testing future pathways into practical deployment in the financial and retail sectors, requiring accurate coin recognition as a musthave.

title: A Transparent Deep Learning Model for Detecting Dyslexia Through Handwriting Patterns: Enhancing Interpretability with Grad-CAM
authors: Mohanapriya D, Priyatharsini S, Mathivanan P, Nithya Sri P R, Agashya B, Saranya N
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Dyslexia is one of the most common learning disorders, making it difficult for children and adults to read, write, and process information. Detecting it early can change the course of learning, yet current methods rely heavily on therapist evaluations, which are often subjective, timeconsuming, and limited by access to specialists. To make the process more reliable and accessible, we propose an explainable deep learning framework that uses handwriting as a simple, non-invasive way to detect and monitor dyslexia. Our model is built on EfficientNet, which learns to recognize subtle handwriting patterns, while Grad-CAM provides visual explanations that highlight the parts of the writing most relevant to the decision. This makes the system transparent and easier for therapists to trust. Beyond detection, the framework generates adaptive exercises tailored to each learner's challenges and records progress over time, allowing therapists to remotely guide and support students. Initial evaluations suggest that our system can achieve high accuracy (around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0}-\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>), while also giving clear insights into its predictions. By combining accuracy, explainability, and continuous monitoring, this approach offers a practical step toward improving dyslexia diagnosis and long-term learning support.

title: Enhanced Extremum Seeking Control (EESC) for DAHB DC-DC Converters
authors: Zhuoqun Wu, Paolo Sbabo, Ahmed Ibrahim, Paolo Mattavelli, Simone Buso
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper investigates the phenomenon of output voltage collapse in dual-active half-bridge (DAHB) converters under load perturbations when conventional extremum seeking control (ESC) strategy is employed. To address this issue, an enhanced extremum seeking control (EESC) is proposed, which provides fast voltage reference tracking under large-signal dynamic transients. Similarly to ESC, the proposed EESC maintains the capability to perform online duty-cycle optimization to minimize input current and improve efficiency. The key advancement lies in extending the applicability of ESC beyond steady-state operation to critical dynamic scenarios. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through experimental results.

title: Problem Solving Lab: An Integrated Platform for Algorithm Visualization and Learning
authors: B. J. Dange, Prajkta Nandurkar, Ashwini Landge, Manish Kharche, Gayatri Rajput, Sarwadnya Maile
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: With the shift toward remote and digital learning, an integrated platform addressing the effective learning needs of students is essential. The Problem Solving Lab is a comprehensive online web platform designed to facilitate effective teaching and learning of algorithms and data structures. It emphasizes algorithm visualization to enhance understanding of algorithmic flow and operations. The platform integrates algorithm explanation, visualization, and coding into a single application, supporting over 30 algorithms. It features an online code editor that allows students to write, edit, and visualize code execution, fostering a deeper understanding of data structures and algorithms. This tool assists students in tackling real-world problems efficiently by providing interactive and engaging learning experiences.

title: Hemodynamics of Active Ankle Motion in a Seated Position Using a Soft Robotic Wearable Actuator: A Comparative Study of Exercise Protocols and Cycles
authors: A. Kobayashi, A. Irie, R. Nishihama, T. Nakamura
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study evaluates a soft robotic wearable device, equipped with a Hyper-extension Pneumatic Actuator (HPA), to prevent deep vein thrombosis (DVT). We investigate the hemodynamic efficacy of a novel "Combined" exercise protocol, which strategically integrates the high-load advantage of resistance exercise with the wide range of motion from induced exercise, all delivered by a single soft actuator system. The effects of four distinct exercise modalities on key hemodynamic parameters, including time-averaged maximum blood flow velocity (TAMAX), were systematically evaluated in healthy participants. Results demonstrated that all active exercise modalities showed a strong tendency to augment blood flow velocity compared to rest (effect size r>0.8). Notably, the "Combined" protocol yielded a statistically significant increase in TAMAX compared to exercise without the device (p < .05). This enhancement is attributed to the protocol's ability to elicit high-quality, high-tension muscle contractions by balancing exercise load with a sufficient range of motion, thereby optimizing the muscle pump mechanism. These findings provide engineering guidelines for developing effective DVT prevention strategies, demonstrating that the intelligent integration of a control protocol—not just the hardware alone—is key to maximizing hemodynamic efficacy.

title: Hybrid Quantum Optimization and Sensing for Real-Time Hospital Operations: OR Scheduling, ICU Staffing, and MRI Throughput
authors: Vishnukumar A, B Dinesh, Harini Pachaiyappan, Lakshmi Priya V, Rakesh R, Suvathika K
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid quantum-classical framework for real-time hospital operations that addresses three routine sources of delay and risk: operating room (OR) rescheduling under disruptions, dynamic ICU/ED staffing under shocks, and MRI throughput with quality control. The approach integrates shallow, latency-bounded quantum optimization within established scheduling pipelines to accelerate diversified search over combinatorial decision spaces under strict deadlines, while classical solvers enforce feasibility, safety, and deterministic fallbacks. In parallel, sensing-guided MRI control provides realtime field/SNR characterization that drives adaptive acquisition and stabilizes AI reconstruction, reducing rescans without compromising diagnostic integrity. The system comprises: (1) a realtime OR rescheduling module that preserves credentialing, safety, and resource constraints; (2) a dynamic ICU/ED assignment module that maintains safe coverage and fairness with acuityand skill-mix awareness; and (3) an MRI module that couples on-scanner quality control with physics-consistent reconstruction to shorten scan times. Evaluation emphasizes wall-clock latency, constraint satisfaction, utilization, and image-quality indices-metrics aligned to patient access, safety, and operational resilience. Results indicate that shallow hybrid quantum methods, when fused with robust classical controllers and sensing feedback, can deliver reliable, incremental improvements under real-time hospital conditions, while providing auditable guardrails and failsafe rollbacks suitable for near-term deployment.

title: Research on Selective Maintenance Strategy of Equipment Based on Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Zilong Wei, Yabo Luo, Ruixin Wang, Zhiyun Su, Yang Jin, Yimin Liu, Jiangnuo Mei, Hong Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Aiming at the selective maintenance optimization of multi-mission equipment under limited resources, this paper constructs an optimization model minimizing the weighted sum of total break time and maintenance cost, considering repairperson levels. An improved genetic algorithm (IGA) is proposed, featuring encoding of maintenance and repairperson assignment, alongside tournament selection, adaptive crossover, and non-uniform mutation. Example results show that compared with the traditional genetic algorithm (GA), the IGA reduces total break duration by 87.80% and saves maintenance cost by 85.45%, significantly improving maintenance decision efficiency.

title: PEAS 2025 Contributor Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)

title: Multi-Mode Extended Phase-Shift Control Strategy for Single-Stage DAB AC-DC Converters Considering Minimum Current Stress
authors: Zhiyong Tu, Yangwei Yu, Lijun Hang, Yuanbin He, Sai Tang, Xiang Zheng, Miao Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To broaden the zero-voltage turn-on range and reduce current stress for adapting to the sinusoidally varying AC-side voltage in single-stage AC-DC converters, this paper proposes a multi-mode extended phase-shift control strategy for double active bridge (DAB) converters. An adaptive control mode was designed based on voltage gain and current stress. Based on the operating principle of the modes, the constraints of current stress optimization, soft switching, and power factor correction are analyzed. The phase-shift calculation formula is derived, enabling smooth switching between modes. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy was validated by constructing a 500W experimental platform.

title: A New Asymmetric Permanent Magnet Variable Flux Memory Machine with Magnetic-Field-Shifting Effect
authors: Jiamou Zhang, Wei Liu, Chi Zhang, Yiyi Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Existing variable flux memory machines (VFMMs) exhibit narrow flux adjustment (FA) range and low torque issues under the weakened-flux (WF) state, which limits the overall efficiency of the machines. A novel asymmetric permanent magnet VFMM (APM-VFMM) is proposed in this paper, which integrates a dual-layer asymmetric magnetic circuit with the magnetic-field-shifting (MFS) effect. In the APM design, the low coercive force (LCF) and high coercive force (HCF) PMs are asymmetrically arranged on both sides. Besides, the occurrence of MFS diminishes the angular disparity between the PM torque and reluctance torque components under the WF state, effectively enhancing the total torque. Additionally, a dual-layer asymmetric PM circuit structure is adopted, addressing the issue of flux saturation under WF state to improve the FA capability of the VFMM. Initially, the topology and operating principles of the APM-VFMM are presented. The mirror APM (MAPM) structure is also proposed to enable electromagnetic performance comparisons. Then, the MFS effects in two VFMMs are analyzed to reveal their differential MFS effects at various magnetization states (MSs). Moreover, the electromagnetic performance at different MSs is analyzed, focusing on air-gap flux density, magnetization characteristics, torque, power and efficiency maps. The results demonstrate that the APM-VFMM offers a wider FA range and higher output torque under the WF state, which exhibits better overall performance than the MAPM structure.

title: Digital Health Records and E-Governance: Advancing Age-Friendly Healthcare Systems
authors: Rakesh Kumar, Divya Mishra, Raju Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The issue of an aging presents significant challenges for societies worldwide. E-governance and digital health records (DHRs) are two vital components of agefriendly healthcare systems that will be addressed in the present paper. It explores how the digitization of medical records and straightening of governance structures can an influence on the rates of accessibility, the continuity of care, and patient empowerment of older adults. The paper examines the best practice and existing solutions in different countries, policy- or technology- based obstacles. Of special consideration are digital literacy, data confidentiality, and interoperability of aging populations. The multi-stakeholder approach as used by the government in ensuring successful enlargement of inclusive digital health ecosystems, which is part of government policy.

title: Walking Sensation in Low- and High-Viscosity Fluids Using a Lower-Limb Force-Feedback Exoskeleton with MR Fluid Brake –The Effect of Changes in Visual on Users' Subjective Evaluation–
authors: T. Sugino, K. Ogura, R. Sawahashi, R. Nishihama, T. Nakamura
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In recent years, various haptic devices have been used to reproduce the senses of touch and force when interacting with virtual fluids. This study focuses on reproducing the resistance force experienced during underwater walking movements in a virtual reality (VR) space using a lower-limb force-feedback exoskeleton. To realize disaster simulations with a greater sense of fear and realism for the user, we focused on the necessity of parameter design based on human subjective evaluation when constructing a force-feedback model for the sensation of underwater motion. This study aims to clarify the influence of visual viscosity information on the user's subjective evaluation. Based on the drag model and the added mass force model proposed in previous research, we constructed a composite model in which the contribution ratio of both can be varied. It is conducted an evaluation experiment by combining two types of fluid visuals with different viscosities and four force-feedback conditions. The analysis revealed that visual viscosity information affects the criteria for judging the sensation of viscosity and the way force feedback is perceived. Furthermore, it became clear that as the viscosity changes, the correlation structure among the subjective evaluation items also changes.

title:
Experimental Evaluation of a 3D-Printed Trochoidal Gear Reducer Using Potassium Titanate Fiber-Reinforced Filament
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Chihiro Matayoshi, Akifumi Okubo, Pauline Barberan, Takahiro Aruga, Gen Endo
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: 3D printing technology offers a promising approach for fabricating lightweight and low cost versions of traditionally heavy and expensive components, such as gear reducers. While previous work exists on plastic based reducers, challenges related to low stiffness remain. In this paper, we designed and prototyped a trochoidal gear reducer primarily constructed from a high strength potassium titanate fiber-reinforced material. We conducted a comparative evaluation of two material variants, the nylon based NTL34M and the more stiff PPS based RT4. The experimental results showed no significant difference between the two materials such as no-load running current, joint stiffness, and static torque efficiency. However, in dynamic torque efficiency measurement, the RT4 reducer outperformed the NTL34M version.

title: Design and Posture Regulation of a Wheel-Legged All-Terrain Mobile Robot
authors: Yishaso Dai, Chuanyi Fu, Yangfei Ruan, Hao Chen, Chenghao Zong, Wenhao Jiang, Kaisheng Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: The agricultural terrain in the hilly and mountainous regions of Zhejiang Province is characterized by varied topography and complex landform, which imposes higher demands on the mobility of agricultural robots, particularly in terms of obstacle-crossing capability, flexibility and maneuverability. This paper proposes a novel wheel-legged all-terrain mobile robot. The robot primarily his paper focuses on the kinematic modeling of curved motion and spot turning for the mobile robot, as well as spatial posture adjustment methods. The relationship between the robots motion posture, the extension and retraction of driving electric cylinders, and wheel attitudes is established. The effectiveness and accuracy of the spatial posture adjustment methods are validated through numerical simulations in MATLAB and co-simulations using ADAMS and MATLAB.

title: SII 2026 Welcome Message
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)

title: Position Sensorless Control for Doubly Salient Electromagnetic Wind Generator Based on Line-Excited Back-EMF Sliding Mode Observer
authors: Xingwei Zhou, Chuyuan Yong, Xia Hua, Shuran Qu, Yujun Zeng, Lei Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Controllable rectification enhances output power density of DSEG. This paper proposes a sensorless control method using a line excitation back- electromotive force (EMF) sliding mode observer (SMO). The SMO extracts commutation-point line excitation back-EMF signals via detected line voltage, current, and phase inductance maxima; comparing these with a preset threshold identifies rotor commutation positions. To avoid misjudgment from interphase flyback-induced back-EMF peaks, full-cycle conduction modes are analyzed, and three line current constraints are established to prevent miscommutation. Full-cycle rotor position is estimated using three reference angles and angular velocity. The hardware-free method is experimentally verified for correctness and flexibility.

title: Hybrid Modulation and Fault-Tolerant Control for Asymmetric Cascaded H-Bridge Inverter
authors: Yingyi Liang, Xiaogang Wang, Ziyan Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper introduces a hybrid modulation and fault-tolerant cooperative control strategy for asymmetrical cascaded H-bridge inverters operating under fault conditions. The proposed hybrid modulation method prevents reversed voltage polarity between high- and low-voltage cell by coordinating their switching logic. When a fault occurs in the low-voltage cell, the strategy reconstructs the modulation waveform by injecting a zero-sequence voltage and optimizing the carrier frequency of the faulty phase. Consequently, the high-voltage cell in the faulty phase operates at a high frequency, ensuring that the asymmetrically cascaded H-bridge inverter outputs symmetrical three-phase AC signals. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that this strategy effectively avoids the current backflow issues and enhances system stability and fault recovery capabilities.

title: Integrative Framework for TP53 Mutational Impact Assessment and Drug Repurposing Through Structural Bioinformatics
authors: Ashlin Reena R, Sandhiya A
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Tumor suppressor protein TP53, or the “guardian of the genome,” is one of the most frequently mutated genes in human cancer. TP53 mutations compromise its structural integrity and DNA-binding function to lead to incompetent tumor suppression and poor clinical outcomes. In this study, we present a computational model integrating structural bioinformatics and molecular docking to investigate the conformational impact of TP53 mutations and identify potential drug repurposing targets. The method begins with wild-type and mutant TP53 protein structure preprocessing, cleaning, hydrogen addition, and conversion to formats compatible with docking. Structural differences are quantified in terms of parameters such as RMSD, TM-score, and IDDT to compare global and local stability differences between the wild and mutant conformations. A well-annotated set of 19 FDA-approved anticancer ligands was taken and standardized to the PDBQT format by Open Babel and MGL Tools. Docking simulations were conducted with Auto Dock Vina against mutant-specific pockets. Comparative docking scores (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta$</tex> Score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{=}$</tex> MUT <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{-}$</tex> WT) were employed to rank ligands with preferred affinity towards mutant TP53. Through procedurally combining structural analysis, docking, and biological annotation, the approach is an inexpensive as well as scalable method for the targeting of TP53 mutation. Future directions involve integration with molecular dynamics simulations, machine learning-driven scoring, and extension to other oncogenic targets.

title: Deep Hybrid Feature Extraction with Ensemble Strategies for Tweet Sentiment Analysis
authors: K. Sangeetha, Arjun Ghosh, Sushil Kumar, Himanshu Barad, K Murugan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Sentiment analysis platforms like Twitter presents unique challenges due to the informal, noisy, and contextsensitive nature of user-generated content. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid sentiment classification framework that combines deep neural representations with traditional machine learning classifiers. The model leverages a parallel feature extraction architecture comprising BiLSTM with attention and CNN-GRU branches to capture both sequential and spatial dependencies in text data. The extracted features are fed into a suite of classical classifiers including Logistic Regression, SVM, Random Forest, Decision Tree, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Ensemble predictions are obtained using both majority voting and weighted voting strategies. Experimental results on benchmark Twitter sentiment datasets demonstrate that the proposed ensemble framework achieves robust and consistent performance, outperforming base classifier and most of the state-of-the-art models. Further, class-wise confidence score analysis and visualizations offer interpretability into the behavior of each ensemble strategy The proposed approach shows promise for scalable and accurate sentiment classification in real-world social media applications.

title: Fractional-Order Modeling and Analysis of Switched Boost Converter Based on Equivalent Small Parameter Method
authors: Haochi He, Xiaoquan Zhu, Chentao Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In this paper, based on the fractional-order characteristics of actual inductance and capacitance, the fractional-order mathematical model of quasi-switched boost converter under continuous conduction mode is constructed on the basis of equivalent small parameter method (ESPM). By combining the ESPM with harmonic balance principle, the general state vector differential equation of fractional-order switched boost converter (FO-SBC) and its steady-state analytical appropriate periodic solution are obtained, which reveals that the dc parts and ripples of voltage of C or current of L are all related to the order of FO components. Ultimately, the accuracy and correctness of the theoretical analysis is validated by simulation on Matlab/Simulink platform.

title: Stability Analysis and Voltage Controller Design for Novel Series-Connected Dc-Link Topology for MVDC Multiphase Drives
authors: Shusen Ni, Zedong Zheng, Kaiyuan Liu, Lie Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The series-connected dc-link topology for multiphase drives is an effective solution for medium-voltage DC(MVDC) propulsion systems, as it can reduce the number of switches and system complexity compared to multi-level configurations. This paper addresses the voltage stability and controller design for the novel series-connected dc-link inverter topology. A nonlinear state model for the system is derived, followed by linearization to obtain a characteristic equation. The analysis reveals that the system is self-stabilizing in generating mode but unstable in motoring mode, necessitating the implementation of a voltage-balancing control strategy. The parameter of voltage controller is designed to guarantee asymptotic stability. Simulations and experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed controller.

title: OEW-PMSM Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Output Voltage Residual
authors: Chunyang Jiang, Hongbin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a multiple open-circuit (OC) fault diagnosis method for open-end winding permanent magnet synchronous motor (OEW-PMSM) based on output voltage residual analysis. OC fault diagnosis operations for single-phase single-switching and single-phase double-switching can be performed simultaneously. Different characteristics are exhibited by analyzing the output voltage residuals during OC faults, and the output voltage residuals under different fault states can be calculated simultaneously. Compared to the software method, this method has a simpler structure and significantly improves the system's reliability and safety. Simulation results demonstrate the method's effectiveness.

title: Context-Aware and Behavior-Driven Authentication System Using AI and Machine Learning
authors: Santa Priya Sahoo, Junali Jas mine Jena
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Traditional forms of authentication, such as passwords and multi-factor authentication (MFA), are adversely affected by phishing, theft of credentials, and user friction. Contextaware systems similarly often misevaluate risk and end up with weak or too restrictive protection. As a solution, we present Risk and Adaptive-Based Authentication (RABA), which employs AI and machine learning to adapt authentication needs on a need-by-need basis by evaluating contextual and behavioral risk on a real-time basis. Combining data from geolocation, device security, history of login, user behavior, and threat intelligence, RABA computes a composite risk score and makes corresponding authentication policy changes to find a middle ground between security and user ease of use. Testing within enterprise settings reveals that RABA presents a better security solution as well as user experience than conventional MFA and behavioral-only approaches, and can find usage within bank and healthcare as well as e-commerce settings.

title: Design and Implementation of a Small-Scale Batch Mixing System using RISC-V Architecture
authors: Angira Mukherjee, Debjit Kar, Pragyan Parimita Padhan, Vineet Kumar, Nutan Lata, Prashant Pranav, Apurva Smita, Nikhat Anjum, Priyanshu Kumar, Aditya Sankar Sengupta, Pooja Kumari, Vijay Nath
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a low-cost, small-scale batch mixing control system, targeting niche manufacturing applications such as custom cosmetics, specialty adhesives, and artisanal food products. At the core of the system is a custom-designed 32-bit non-pipelined RISC-V processor based on the RV32I instruction set architecture. The processor executes a finite state machine (FSM) to control multiple valves for ingredient dispensing, activate mixing and heating units, and follow user-defined recipes entered through a basic digital interface. While the physical hardware such as valves, sensors, and heaters was not implemented, the processor was fully developed and simulated to validate its ability to manage the automation sequence. This work demonstrates that even simple, non-pipelined RISC architectures can effectively support real-time automation tasks in resource-constrained, small-scale production environments. The proposed design offers improved process consistency, reduced manual effort, and scalability for additional ingredients or stages.

title: IoT-enabled Waterlogging Prediction using a YOLO-based Deep Learning Model
authors: Saurin Parikh, Manav Patel, Sarthak Methaniya, Gaurang Raval, Sharada Valiveti, Vrajesh Kumar Chawra
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Real-time flood monitoring is very crucial for disaster management, however conventional sensor-based systems deployed on the ground have very limited coverage also needs high maintenance, increasing overall cost. This paper proposes a new method to measure the actual water level from an unobstructed region beneath it; using a customized waterlogging sensors mounted on light poles. However, to identify an unobstructed region for measuring the depth of the water, it is necessary to identify objects lying on top of the waterlogged roads before measuring the water level. Our proposed waterlogging sensing device detects objects that are below the light pole and makes angular movements to search for the space that is clear of any objects. Moreover, to accurately detect various objects, the comparative analysis of three object detection models: YOLO, FasterRCNN + InceptionResNetV2 and SSDMobilenet V2 is performed to determine the optimal model that can be used for object detection. YOLO v9 outperformed other models in object detection precision with up to 97% accuracy. The model also demonstrated consistent accuracy under conditions that included complex scenes and occlusion. To ensure a comprehensive evaluation and reduce overfitting, the data set was enhanced using augmentation techniques such as flipping and rotation, increasing its robustness for comparative analysis. The proposed system, powered by YOLO v9, offers a feasible and effective solution for real-time monitoring of water levels in waterlogged areas, enabling improved disaster management capabilities.

title: PSDR-Based DC Wind Turbine Topology and Control Strategy
authors: Zilan Sun, LianSong Xiong, Siwei Li, Yihan Xie, Wenhui Wang, Jianzhong Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The phase-shifted diode rectifier (PSDR) topology enables multiplexed rectification and voltage boosting while eliminating the need for filtering components, offering both cost-efficiency and high reliability for far-offshore wind power applications. To this end, this paper proposes a novel DC wind turbine topology based on PSDR, where the generator output is converted to medium-voltage DC (MVDC) through PSDR-based boost rectification, while an active compensator connected in parallel at the AC-side of PSDR regulates the active power at the turbine output. The paper first presents the detailed turbine topology structure, then describes the corresponding control strategy, and finally verifies the effectiveness of the proposed solution through simulations.

title: Junction temperature prediction of discrete IGBTs based on the case top temperature
authors: Xiang Wang, Xiaokui Sang, Xiaobin Mu, Xiahao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a non-invasive prediction method for the junction temperature of discrete IGBT devices based on the relationship between the temperature at the top of the device casing and the junction temperature. Correspondingly, a heat transfer model has been constructed. This method overcomes the limitations of traditional techniques: optical methods and physical contact methods require destructing the packaging; the temperature-sensitive parameter method interferes with the device's operating state. The study first confirms an approximately linear relationship between the device top temperature and the junction temperature under the condition of a single tube running independently. Next, the junction temperature measured using the temperature-sensitive parameter method serves as a reference value, and the top temperature is utilized to predict the junction temperature, with prediction errors calculated through comparison. Finally, the accuracy of this method is further validated by simulating actual operating conditions.

title: Dvice - Dynamic Vehicle Interface Simulator for Checkout Evaluation
authors: Shiv Kumar Yadav, B Anupama, Karthika S, Gadhave Amol Nanasaheb, Midhun George, Anil Abraham Samuel, Sateesh Kumar R
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Launch Vehicle checkout systems ensure the health of the vehicle and readiness for a launch by carrying out various health checks. The software residing in the checkout systems are highly complex, by virtue of the inherent complex nature of the vehicle systems they act upon. With advent of human rated Launch Vehicle that are safety critical, the design ideology demands increased redundancy to ensure crew safety even with two similar failure conditions. Proportionally, software validation principles are required to be upgraded to handle redundancy of sources and frequency of information exchanged between the systems. This paper provides an overview of checkout systems and software employed, the existing methodology used for the validation of the software, the test beds developed in-house for efficient and effective testing, and augmentation of the test bed with DVICE, a Dynamic Vehicle Interface simulator for Checkout Evaluation.

title: MRF-Based Mapping of Fetal and Maternal Deformations for Understanding and Supporting Fetal Movement Perception
authors: Haruka Matoba, Takashi Kusaka, Koji Shimatani, Dongmin Kim, Hoshinori Kanazawa, Yasuo Kuniyoshi, Takayuki Tanaka
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study proposes a foundational method for objectively classifying whether mothers can easily perceive fetal movements, a crucial indicator in perinatal care. Traditional fetal movement assessments rely on subjective maternal reports or wearable sensor-based methods susceptible to external noise.This paper applies the previously established Multi-Resolution Feature (MRF) method to independently quantify both fetal and maternal deformation from simulated fetal movement videos. We constructed a novel fetal-maternal deformation map using these deformation values as axes. The results demonstrate that this map can visually represent the relationship between fetal and maternal deformation. Furthermore, exploratory analysis of the data distribution on the map suggested the existence of distinct fetal movement patterns: movements accompanied by significant maternal abdominal wall deformation (likely perceived by the mother) and movements where the fetus is active but less transmission occurs to the mother (likely difficult for the mother to perceive). This method is expected to be a stepping stone towards establishing a new approach for more detailed assessment of fetal health and, consequently, improving the quality of perinatal medical care.

title: A Human Upper-Limb Motion Tracking System for Humanoid Robots
authors: Lexin Xie, Kunpeng Wu, Zhaozheng Chen, Xiaoqi Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: The upper limbs of humanoid robots are their core executive mechanisms and the ultimate effectors for performing tasks. We present research on a method for humanoid robots tracking human motions on the upper limbs, which includes 14 degrees of freedom for both arms (7 degrees of freedom for a single arm) and 22 degrees of freedom for two dexterous hands (11 degrees of freedom for a single dexterous hand), totaling 36 degrees of freedom for the entire system. We first retargeted existing human motion datasets to our humanoid robot to obtain a robot motion dataset. Based on this dataset, we trained a policy for tracking human motion using reinforcement learning. By using mixed reality devices to obtain operator posture, we tested the system's performance in both simulation and real-world environments. Experimental results demonstrate that in the simulation environment, the average error of the arm tracking 14 key points at each time step does not exceed 4 centimeters. Both in the simulation environment and in the real world, the hand accurately tracks the operator's hand movements and performs various gestures, including simple actions and fine-grained movements such as pinching. Therefore, our approach enables the upper limbs of humanoid robots to effectively track human movements and achieve high degrees of freedom in motion, while also possessing the capability to execute fine-grained manipulation tasks.

title: A Lightweight CNN Framework for Multi-Class Cassava Leaf Disease Detection
authors: Devashish Ghate, Shrey Chamoli, Sneha Chauhan
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Cassava cultivation is an important source of income for many people across the world. However, its yield is commonly affected by foliar diseases which decreases the plant growth. Therefore, it is important to detect disease early and accurately. This study focuses on developing a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network framework for multiclass classification of diseases in cassava leaves. The model uses Cassava Leaf Disease Dataset, comprising of 21,397 annotated images showing diverse pathological conditions. The CNN architecture consists of batch normalization, dropout layers and max pooling which helps in learning and optimizing overfitting problems and enhance feature extraction. The model achieved a validation accuracy of 95.02%, which makes it applicable for the real time detection of diseases in crops.

title: ISED 2025 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)

title: Integrated ML-Calibrated Sensing with Neural Network Control for Horticultural Lighting
authors: Afagh Mohagheghi, Mehrdad Moallem
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents the design and experimental validation of a modular intelligent platform for energy-efficient lighting and monitoring in controlled environment agriculture (CEA). The proposed architecture integrates dimmable LED lighting with neural network–based spectral optimization, machine learning–based light intensity estimation, image-based plant monitoring, and IoT-enabled data acquisition. The plat-form emphasizes a unified control framework that bridges perception, control, and application layers, enabling adaptive real-time decision-making and modular scalability. The system was implemented and tested in a greenhouse environment, demonstrating a 28% reduction in energy consumption per gram of dry biomass while maintaining plant health and productivity. The results underscore the effectiveness of the proposed architecture in advancing intelligent control and automation strategies for sustainable horticultural systems.

title: Performance Analysis of Cross-Medium Link for Air-Sea CV-QKD Based on Monte Carlo Simulation
authors: Pei Zhou, Hang Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Addressing the key technical challenges in air-sea cross-medium quantum communication between aircraft and submarines, this paper focuses on quantifying the link attenuation disparity between the uplink (submarine-to-aircraft) and downlink (aircraft-to-submarine) paths and optimizing system parameters. Using the Monte Carlo (MC) simulation method, a multi-medium transmission model encompassing the atmosphere, the air-sea interface, and seawater was constructed to precisely characterize the absorption and scattering behavior of optical signals in the complex cross-medium environment. Building on these attenuation characteristics, a secret key rate calculation model was derived in conjunction with the Gaussian-modulated coherent state (GMCS) Continuous-Variable Quantum Key Distribution (CV-QKD) protocol. The simulation results analyze the influence pattern of depth on the uplink and downlink key rates and clarify the underlying mechanisms for the attenuation disparity between the two paths. The study verifies that the downlink path possesses superior key rate performance due to weaker scattering and angular divergence effects. Furthermore, the paper investigates the modulating effect of modulation variance on the key rate and determines its optimal range.

title: Neural Factorization-based Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Zhenhao Li, Xu Cheng, Yi Zhou
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This paper studies the key problems of bearing fault diagnosis of high-speed train. As the core component of the train operation system, the health of bearings is directly related to the safety of train operation. The traditional diagnostic methods are facing the challenge of insufficient diagnostic accuracy under complex conditions. To solve these problems, we propose a novel Neural Factorization-based Classification (NFC) framework for bearing fault diagnosis. It is built on two core idea: 1) Embedding vibration time series into multiple mode-wise latent feature vectors to capture diverse fault-related patterns; 2) Leveraging neural factorization principles to fuse these vectors into a unified vibration representation. This design enables effective mining of complex latent fault characteristics from raw time-series data. We further instantiate the framework with two models CP-NFC and Tucker-NFC based on CP and Tucker fusion schemes, respectively. Experimental results show that both models achieve superior diagnostic performance compared with traditional machine learning methods. The comparative analysis provides valuable empirical evidence and practical guidance for selecting effective diagnostic strategies in high-speed train bearing monitoring.

title: Blockchain-Enabled, Machine Learning-Driven Validation of BIM Models in Secure Project Information Management
authors: Seno Hardijanto Purnomo, I Gede Upeksa Negara, Djarot Hindarto, Bayu Yasa Wdha, Asrul Sani, Endah Tri Esti Handayani, Ira Diana Sholihati, Agus Iskandar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This research introduces a unified framework that integrates Building Information Modeling (BIM), Machine Learning (ML), and Blockchain technologies within a secure Project Information Management (PIM) ecosystem to improve BIM model validation, data reliability, and audit transparency in construction projects. The system employs a Random Forest– based ML model trained on annotated BIM datasets to automatically detect modeling inconsistencies, missing parameters, and spatial conflicts with high precision. Experimental results indicate that the model achieved 94.2% accuracy, 92.8% precision, 93.5% recall, and an F1-score of 93.1%, confirming its strong capability for automated quality assessment. A permissioned blockchain network built on Hyperledger Fabric provides immutable and verifiable validation records, achieving an average transaction time of 2.8 seconds and 100% anomaly detection in integrity tests. The complete workflow demonstrated an average processing time of 15.4 seconds, 99.3% uptime, and high user acceptance levels. Overall, the framework delivers an intelligent, transparent, and trustworthy digital validation environment that enhances collaboration and data governance across BIM-based project workflows.

title: Blockchain-Driven Traceability and Quality-Assured Procurement for Sustainable Fishery Supply Chains
authors: Muhammad Fawwazi Azhim Nurdin, Herry Irawan, Andry Alamsyah
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The global fishery supply chain still suffers from opaque traceability, inconsistent data, and weak quality enforcement, leading to frequent fraud and compliance issues. This study presents a blockchain-driven traceability and quality-assured procurement model that enables trusted, event-driven automation across fisheries. A consortium blockchain with three smart contracts (QualitySLA, EscrowPayment, ProcurementTrigger) verifies laboratory and IoT data, automates conditional settlements, and initiates procurement decisions. The architecture integrates four layers: data capture, blockchain consortium, smart contract automation, and enterprise system integration. Each transaction in the chain—from fishing and processing to quality validation and distribution—is recorded as immutable evidence. The implemented proof-of-concept (PoC) demonstrated stable performance under 200 transaction iterations, with average execution times below 100 ms and consistent gas efficiency across core operations. Results confirm that blockchain-based automation enforces deterministic business rules and minimizes disputes through cryptographic verification rather than relying on predictive AI or centralized authorities. The model strengthens trust, transparency, and sustainability across multi-stakeholder fisheries, making blockchain a viable foundation for digital transformation in the maritime economy.

title: Novel Bandpass Filter Employing Hybrid Circular and Cambered SIW Resonators
authors: Kelong Sheng, Xiang Wang, Songsong Qian, Zhiyuan Zong, Wen Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: In this paper, a novel third-order bandpass filter (BPF) employing hybrid quarter-mode circular (QMC) and quarter cambered (QC) SIW cavity is proposed. Differing from traditional filters, the three-order BPF proposed in this work is asymmetric. By adjusting the size of the last resonator, the frequency of the higher-order mode can be brought close to the passband, resulting in a transmission zero appearing on the right side of the passband. The proposed BPF is simulated, fabricated and measured. Measurement results show that the BPF operates at the central frequency of 3.96 GHz with a 3-dB FBW of 30.1% (1.2 GHz), featuring a transmission zero (TZ) located at 4.9 GHz. In addition, the return loss is more than 16 dB within the entire passband, while the insertion loss is approximately 0.6 dB. There is a good agreement between the simulation and the measurement results.

title: Dual-Method Stability Improvement for Voltage-Controlled DC-DC Converters Interfaced with Constant Power Loads in DC Microgrids
authors: Lizheng Sun, Jiachen Tian, Bofeng Xu, Ke Luo, Beihai Chen, Quance Wang, Zhenqiang Tang, Siyue Cheng, Feng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The interaction between voltage-controlled DC-DC converters and constant power loads (CPLs) in DC microgrids often leads to system instability due to the negative incremental impedance effect. This paper investigates the small-signal stability characteristics of such cascaded systems and proposes two complementary control strategies to mitigate the instability issue. First, a capacitor current feedback scheme is introduced to enhance damping and stabilize the source-CPL interaction. Analytical loop-gain-based models are established to evaluate stability margins and control impacts. Second, an inertia droop control method is developed to emulate virtual inertia and further improve the dynamic performance. A comparison is conducted to reveal their respective advantages and application scenarios. Simulation results have verified the effectiveness of the proposed dual methods.

title: Switched Resonant Branch Self-Balancing DC Transformer with Backflow Power Optimization
authors: Runze Wang, Heyang Sun, Haiyang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To address power imbalance issues caused by parametric variations in Input Series Output Parallel (ISOP) - dual active bridge (DAB), this article adopts the Switched Resonant Branch (SRB) connected between every two DAB module, achieving input voltage self-balanced but no any other switch is added. First of all, the working principle of SRB-DAB is demonstrated before the analyzing of SRB loss. The additional SRB branches do not affect the DAB control strategy or IGBT operating boundaries. Moreover, the SRB's inherent self-balancing property eliminates the need for decoupling input/output voltage regulation in control design. Further, aiming to have a better performance in high transmission power, triple phase shift (TPS) modulation with backflow power optimation calculated by Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) constrain equation is employed to control the output voltage of entire converter. Last, the effectiveness and validity of SRB-DAB converter is verified by experiments.

title: Learning Dynamic Non-Prehensile Object Reorientation via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Abdullah Mustafa, Ryo Hanai, Ixchel G. Ramirez-Alpizar, Floris Erich, Ryoichi Nakajo, Yukiyasu Domae, Tetsuya Ogata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This work proposes a learning-based approach to dynamic non-prehensile object reorientation, enabling fast reorientation of large, grasp-infeasible objects using uni-manual manipulation. Our policy is trained in simulation via reinforcement learning, utilizing a carefully designed observation space, action space, and reward function to reorient randomly sized cuboids with varied physical properties. Given an object model and a target rotation direction, the policy plans offline trajectories suitable for both simulation and real-world deployment. Although the policy is sensitive to modeling uncertainties, experimental results show that accurate modeling enables successful sim-to-real transfer across different objects and rotation directions. Videos are available at https://tinyurl.com/DNPR2L.

title: Controller Design for Enhancing Transient Overcurrent Capability in Grid Following Converters via Switching Frequency Adjustment
authors: Enqi Li, Yi Long, Yonghao Li, Cong Chen, Xin Xiang, Wuhua Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the significant increase in renewable energy penetration, grid following (GFL) converters is widely adopted in power systems. However, when the power grid experiences a failure, the lack of fault current can increase the risk of system transient instability. Although the transient overcurrent (TOC) capability enhancement strategy based on switching frequency adjustment has developed in recent studies, these solutions focus on grid forming converters. To fill this gap, this paper proposes a novel discretization method for the proportional-resonant controller and applies the D-partition method to design parameters enabling switching frequency adjustment in GFL converters. Finally, the proposed TOC capability enhancement strategy is verified in the electrothermal simulation.

title: Sharpness-aware Second-order Latent Factor Model for High-dimensional and Incomplete Data
authors: Jialiang Wang, Xueyan Bao, Hao Wu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Second-order Latent Factor (SLF) model, a class of low-rank representation learning methods, has proven effective at extracting node-to-node interaction patterns from High-dimensional and Incomplete (HDI) data. However, its optimization is notoriously difficult due to its bilinear and non-convex nature. Sharpness-aware Minimization (SAM) has recently proposed to find flat local minima when minimizing non-convex objectives, thereby improving the generalization of representation-learning models. To address this challenge, we propose a Sharpness-aware SLF (SSLF) model. SSLF embodies two key ideas: (1) acquiring second-order information via Hessian–vector products; and (2) injecting a sharpness term into the curvature through the designed Hessian–vector products. Experiments on multiple industrial datasets demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.

title: An Upper-Limb Endpoint Stiffness Estimation Method for High-Load Tasks
authors: Dehao Wang, Silu Chen, Chan Xu, Junjie Li, Chi Zhang, Guilin Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: In human-robot physical interaction, real-time estimation of endpoint stiffness is crucial. However, existing models are often too complex for real-time use or are inaccurate under high loads due to simplified linear assumptions. To address this, we propose a nonlinear model that combines a simple geometric approach for stiffness orientation with a nonlinear function for electromyography (EMG)-based stiffness magnitude. A proof-of-concept study on a single subject demonstrates that, for high-load scenarios, our model improves magnitude estimation accuracy by up to 50% compared to linear approaches. The proposed framework presents a promising direction for developing stiffness estimation solutions that balance simplicity, real-time capability, and precision.

title: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Safe Fairness Guarantees Without Demographics in Classification: Spectral Uncertainty Set Perspective_supp1-3664937.pdf
authors: Ainhize Barrainkua
publish: N/A
abstract: As automated classification systems become increasingly prevalent, concerns have emerged over their potential to reinforce and amplify existing societal biases. In the light of this issue, many methods have been proposed to enhance the fairness guarantees of classifiers. Most of the existing interventions assume access to group information for all instances, a requirement rarely met in practice. Fairness without access to demographic information has often been approached through robust optimization techniques, which target worst-case outcomes over a set of plausible distributions known as the uncertainty set. However, their effectiveness is strongly influenced by the chosen uncertainty set. In fact, existing approaches often overemphasize outliers or overly pessimistic scenarios, compromising both overall performance and fairness. To overcome these limitations, we introduce <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SPECTRE</monospace>, a minimax-fair method that adjusts the spectrum of a simple Fourier feature mapping and constrains the extent to which the worst-case distribution can deviate from the empirical distribution. We perform extensive experiments on the American Community Survey datasets involving 20 states. The safeness of <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SPECTRE</monospace> comes as it provides the highest average values on fairness guarantees together with the smallest interquartile range in comparison to state-of-the-art approaches, even compared to those with access to demographic group information. In addition, we provide a theoretical analysis that derives computable bounds on the worst-case error for both individual groups and the overall population, as well as characterizes the worst-case distributions responsible for these extremal performances.

title: IEEE Standard for Intelligent Electronic Devices Cybersecurity Capabilities--Corrigendum 1
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: The functions and features to be provided in intelligent electronic devices (IEDs) to accommodate cybersecurity programs are defined in this standard. Security regarding access, operation, configuration, firmware revision, and data retrieval from an IED is addressed. Confidentiality, integrity, and availability of external interfaces of the IED are also addressed. This corrigendum clarifies the definition of value forcing and updates outdated bibliographic references.

title: Toward Following Changes in a Human’s Posture: Stroking Motion Generation Using a Mobile Manipulator and an RGB-D Camera
authors: Akishige Yuguchi, Sora Nii, Naoyuki Aikawa, Yoshio Matsumoto
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: While the conventional stroking motions using robot arms in physical human-robot interaction were planned from the pre-recognized shape of the target, it’s not practical because the movements of the target body during stroking are not considered. In this paper, we propose a stroking motion generation method using a mobile manipulator and an RGB-D camera to follow changes in the target human’s posture. Specifically, we first extract the target subject with image recognition and segmentation, and point cloud processing. Next, we generate the target part’s 3D model and a motion trajectory on the model. Finally, we repeatedly update the trajectory by following changes in the target part’s posture using an Iterative Closest Point (ICP) algorithm. For evaluation, the proposed method was implemented on a mobile manipulator HSR. Then, stroking motions were generated on a human-shaped robot’s back with various movements, and the alignment success score and the alignment error were measured. From the results, we confirmed that the motion generation was highly successful and occlusion by the manipulator’s arm affected the alignment.

title: Hydraulic and Electrical Design for Lightweight Modular Mountain Intelligent Cranes
authors: Zhen Chen, Bo Yu, Shishun Xiao, Gaisheng Sun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: To address the challenges of poor adaptability, low operational efficiency, and high safety risks associated with conventional lifting equipment in power grid construction within western mountainous regions, this paper investigates the integrated design of hydraulic and electrical systems for a lightweight, modular mountain spider crane. The hydraulic system employs a ‘ diesel engine + load-sensing variable displacement piston pump’ as its power core, optimising control valve configurations and actuator arrangements. Multiple safety circuits ensure operational stability in mountainous terrain. The electrical system adopts a distributed control architecture, integrating torque limitation, condition monitoring, and anti-interference remote control functions to achieve comprehensive parameter monitoring and intelligent safety management under all working conditions. Through coordinated hydraulic-electrical design, core performance metrics were achieved: modular lightweight construction (individual modules not exceeding 1.5 tonnes), adaptive operation on 35° inclines, and stable remote control up to 200 metres. Performance validation confirms the system meets mountainous construction requirements for weight reduction, safety, and operational efficiency, providing critical technological support for mechanised and intelligent upgrades in mountainous power construction.

title: Feshdock: A Divide-and-Conquer Protein–Protein Complex Conformation Prediction Algorithm
authors: Jin Zhou, Juan Zhang, Junkai Ji, Qiuzhen Lin, Zexuan Zhu, Jianqiang Li, Carlos A. Coello Coello
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Understanding protein interactions is vital for unraveling cellular biological processes, disease mechanisms, and drug development. Numerous in-silico protein–protein docking algorithms have been developed to substitute time-consuming and expensive experimental methods for deciphering such interactions. However, few algorithms can provide satisfactory complex conformation prediction performance due to the vast and multimodal landscapes of protein surfaces and to the complicated conformation changes of side chains. This motivated the development of a novel protein–protein docking algorithm called Feshdock, which is introduced in this paper. Specifically, Feshdock leverages the Fast Fourier transform to globally scan potential binding positions, which are then partitioned into multiple clusters using the K-means method. These clusters are locally optimized by a swarm intelligence algorithm in a divide-and-conquer manner, accompanied by an anisotropic network model considering protein flexibility. Finally, hierarchical clustering is employed to determine the final candidate complex conformations. The effectiveness of our proposed Feshdock approach is verified on the benchmark dataset of 230 protein complexes. Our experimental results show that the proposed approach performs better than almost all commonly used docking methods containing ZDOCK, MEGADOCK, and LightDock. In addition, being a lightweight algorithm, Feshdock is still very competitive in terms of docking accuracy and efficiency, being even comparable to emergent deep learning-based approaches, including ColabFold and AlphaFold2-refined ClusPro. The source code of Feshdock can be downloaded for free at https://github.com/SZU-ADDG/Feshdock.

title: Image Diffusion Models with Multimodal Conditional Control in Zero-shot Semantic Segmentation Synthesis
authors: Leilei Wang, Renjie Lu, Fengzhao Sun, Yunxiang Zhang, Jun Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: In the era of booming large models, the significance of data scale in deep learning has been well-acknowledged. Nevertheless, obtaining large-scale datasets remains a challenging task. Large pre-trained diffusion models, boasting remarkable generative capabilities, offer a promising solution for dataset generation. This study zeroes in on the labor-intensive semantic segmentation task, where image annotation is a major bottleneck. We introduce a novel data generation pipeline leveraging multimodal conditional control. This pipeline not only enhances the universality and practicality of our method but also ensures high-precision alignment between generated images and corresponding masks. Specifically, we feed mask, Canny edge, and depth information into ControlNet and employ captions generated by the BLIP model as prompts to precisely control image generation. Notably, we pioneer the exploration of zero-shot generation in this context. This approach enables direct image generation without the need for fine-tuning or alignment with segmentation protocols. Experimental results demonstrate its effectiveness: in the CityScapes dataset, zero-shot generation leads to a 0.6% increase in the mean Intersection over Union (mIOU). Even without zero-shot generation, our method achieves significant mIOU improvements of up to 2.53% on the ADE20K dataset and 1.93% on the COCO-Stuff dataset. These findings highlight the potential of our proposed method in advancing semantic segmentation tasks.

title: SLNeRF: Joint Optimization of Structured Light and NeRF
authors: Xiaofang Li, Tong Jia, Shuyang Lin, Dongyue Chen, Ping Xiao, Cuiwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Recently, based on multi-view stereo (MVS) methods, utilizing stereo prior information to guide novel-view synthesis has become an important approach to addressing the generalization problem of neural radiance fields (NeRF). However, this approach faces challenges in handling certain difficult scenarios, such as weak texture regions, where it struggles to effectively extract geometric features information. As a result, it encounters limitations in stereo image feature representation and inaccuracies in prior depth estimation. To tackle these problems, we propose the first generalizable novel-view synthesis method that jointly optimizes structured light and NeRF. Considering that active stereo methods based on structured light optimization can enhance geometric feature extraction capability in weak texture regions through the addition of a texture layer, we integrate active stereo vision into novel-view synthesis. To this end, we propose a novel framework, dubbed SLNeRF. First, we design a structured light generation scheme based on Fourier transform and establish a differentiable imaging model using geometric optics and the Lambertian model to generate active stereo images. Then, we obtain stereo image features, as well as prior depth information through the feature extractor, which are used to construct 3D feature volumes. Finally, we accomplish the novel-view synthesis task through a neural renderer. Compared to state-of-the-art generalizable NeRF methods, our method reports encouraging results on public datasets as well as in real-world scenarios.

title: Four-in-Three-Out Coupling Serial Spherical Photoelectric Stabilization Platform and Its Adaptive Antidisturbance Control
authors: Dapeng Tian, Bowen Chen, Wen Wang, Zhishu Wang, Zhongshi Wang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: The photoelectric stabilization platform is mounted on the moving carrier, which isolates the carrier's attitude fluctuations and maintains the stable pointing of the optical system's line of sight within it. This is the key to achieving dynamic imaging. However, the conventional mechanism adopts a closed-frame structure with orthogonal axes, which limits the load capacity. This article proposes a three-degrees-of-freedom serial spherical platform driven by four arc-shaped voice coil motors and its overall controller design. The kinematic and dynamic models of the 4-in-3-out serial spherical platform are formulated. The controller consists of two parts. The data-driven gravity compensation is designed to deal with the gravity-induced unbalance. The adaptive sliding-mode-assisted disturbance observer based control is designed to suppress the dynamic coupling and complex disturbances. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of the proposal.

title: Fast Diffusion-Based Camouflaged Object Detection via Asynchronous Denoising and Linear Attention
authors: Tianyu Wang, Xinghua Xu, Changchong Sheng, Shaohua Qiu, Li Liu, Denghua Guo, Xianqiang Bao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Camouflaged object detection (COD) aims to precisely locate and identify objects concealed within their surrounding backgrounds, a task that has been significantly advanced by recent powerful diffusion models. Nevertheless, existing diffusion-based COD methods demonstrate suboptimal performance in inference speed, attributable to two key factors: time-consuming iterative sampling processes and the quadratic complexity introduced by self-attention mechanisms. To address these limitations, we propose Fast CamoDiff, a novel and efficient diffusion-based model for COD. To tackle the computational overhead, we incorporate an asynchronous denoising paradigm leveraging dynamic encoding and early termination, which significantly reduces computational costs during the sampling process. Additionally, we introduce the linear attention mechanism from state space models (SSM) into the diffusion process to achieve linear computational complexity. Meanwhile, to address the side effect of compromising fine-grained feature preservation through information compression, we design a novel bidirectional pooling layer that enhances the model's capability of preserving detailed features without sacrificing computational benefits. Extensive experimental results on four public datasets demonstrate that Fast-CamoDiff achieves superior detection accuracy with only 36.5M parameters (67.4% reduction) and 28 FPS (2× speedup) compared to previous state-of-the-art diffusion-based models. The source code will be available at https://github.com/wty-team/diff-ssm.

title: PEXP: A Scalable Parallel Tree-Based Framework for Interpreting Models on Big Data
authors: Wen-Dong Jiang, Chih-Yung Chang, Tzu-Chia Huang, Yu-Ting Chin, Diptendu Sinha Roy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: The proliferation of big data has fueled the success of deep learning; however, its inherent ”black box” nature poses significant challenges for its adoption in safety-critical domains. Existing interpretable machine learning methods offer partial solutions but often struggle with model fidelity, inconsistent explanations, a lack of holistic model understanding, and critically, computational inefficiency, especially when applied to models trained on large-scale datasets. To overcome these hurdles, this paper introduces Parallel Explainer (PEXP), an innovative parallel tree-based interpretation framework designed for scalability and comprehensive understanding. PEXP initiates by generating a localized sample set around a target instance through data distribution-aware perturbations. It then computes similarity scores and employs a kernel function to weight these samples effectively. Leveraging concepts from Bagging and Boosting, PEXP efficiently constructs Parallel Ensemble Trees as its core interpretable model. This model provides feature importance-based explanations and aggregates insights across all samples to achieve a global understanding of the model's behavior on the entire dataset. Experimental results demonstrate PEXP's significant advantages over mainstream interpretable methods in both runtime efficiency and the quality of explanations, particularly crucial for big data analytics. Furthermore, a case study illustrates PEXP's application in enhancing the interpretability of video anomaly detection systems within smart cities, a domain characterized by large volumes of data and offers insights for improving Transformer-based architectures.

title: Compact In-Band Full-Duplex Antenna for Deeply Implantable Biomedical IoT Applications
authors: Abdullah Alshammari, Muhammad Zada, Abdul Basir, Amjad Iqbal, Ismail Ben Mabrouk
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Traditional duplexing antennas for implantable biomedical devices often rely on half-duplex or multi-band designs, which allow either transmission or reception at a given time or require separate frequency bands for uplink and downlink. Such configurations result in reduced data rates, increased latency, higher hardware complexity, and potential spectral congestion, thereby limiting their suitability for advanced in-body communication. In this work, an innovative in-band full-duplex (IBFD) concept is introduced in a miniaturized implantable antenna optimized for deep implantation at a depth of 65 mm in a human torso phantom. The antenna operates at 2.4 GHz on both Port 1 and Port 2 with independently controllable bands. A compact volume of 5.81 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (2.7)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.254) is achieved through the integration of shorting pins, open-ended slots, and multiple semi-circular slots. High port-to-port isolation of 29.5 dB is obtained using an ultra-thin substrate, superstrate, and a narrow 0.4 mm separation between the radiating patches. The antenna exhibits omnidirectional radiation patterns at both ports, with measured gains of -20.2 dBi and -20.3 dBi for Port 1 and Port 2, respectively. The proposed design demonstrates ultra-miniaturization, high isolation, reliable impedance matching, acceptable radiation performance, and independently controllable duplexing bands. These characteristics make it highly suitable for deeply implanted biomedical devices, significantly improving spectral efficiency and enabling simultaneous transmission and reception for real-time bidirectional communication in next-generation implantable systems.

title: O band TO optical switch based on silica platform
authors: Minghui Zhou, Yuexin Yin, Guoyan Zeng, Xinru Xu, Daming Zhang
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Optical switch plays an important role in the modern optical communication. In this letter, we demonstrate an O band 8×8 switch array based on 2% Δ silica platform. The 8×8 switch array is designed with BENES topology having 20 balanced Mach–Zehnder interferometer based switch elements. At 1310 nm, the switch shows good performance. The on-chip loss is lower than 1.18 dB and the crosstalk is better than −16.32 dB. The rise time (10%-90%) and fall time (90%-10%) of the switch are both 1.3 ms at 1310 nm. The transmission experiments of 10 Gbps on-off keying are successfully realized.

title: Deep Neural Network-Based High-Precision Identification of Weak Stability Boundary Structures
authors: Shuyue Fu, Ziqi Xu, Di Wu, Shengping Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Weak stability boundary structures have been widely applied to the analysis on ballistic capture and the construction of low-energy transfers. The first step of this application is to compute/identify weak stability boundary structures. Conventional numerical and analytical methods cannot simultaneously achieve computational efficiency and identification precision. In this paper, we propose an efficient and precise method to identify weak stability boundary structures in the Earth-Moon planar circular restricted three-body problem based on deep neural network. The geometric and dynamical properties of weak stability boundary structures are firstly analyzed, which provides further insights into the training of the deep neural network models. Then, the optimal hyperparameter combinations of the DNN model are determined by prioritizing the identification precision while ensuring the recall. The performance of the models with the optimal hyperparameter combinations is further validated using the representative test datasets, achieving the precision of 97.26-99.91%. The trained models are applied to constructing weak stability boundary structures, improving the computational efficiency over the conventional numerical method. Finally, the identified weak stability boundary structures are applied to the construction of ballistic capture trajectories with respect to the Moon.

title: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence

title: Occlusion-Aware Diffusion Model for Pedestrian Intention Prediction
authors: Yu Liu, Zhijie Liu, Zedong Yang, You-Fu Li, He Kong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Predicting pedestrian crossing intentions is crucial for the navigation of mobile robots and intelligent vehicles. Although recent deep learning-based models have shown significant success in forecasting intentions, few consider incomplete observation under occlusion scenarios. To tackle this challenge, we propose an Occlusion-Aware Diffusion Model (ODM) that reconstructs occluded motion patterns and leverages them to guide future intention prediction. During the denoising stage, we introduce an occlusion-aware diffusion transformer architecture to estimate noise features associated with occluded patterns, thereby enhancing the model’s ability to capture contextual relationships in occluded semantic scenarios. Furthermore, an occlusion mask-guided reverse process is introduced to effectively utilize observation information, reducing the accumulation of prediction errors and enhancing the accuracy of reconstructed motion features. The performance of the proposed method under various occlusion scenarios is comprehensively evaluated and compared with existing methods on popular benchmarks, namely PIE and JAAD. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves more robust performance than existing methods in the literature. To benefit the community, we open-source our code at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/AISLAB-sustech/ODM</uri>

authors: Jiajun Wu
publish: AI Matters
abstract: None

title: Balancing Global Majority AI Ambitions with Environmental Considerations
authors: Chinasa T. Okolo
publish: ACM SIGCAS Computers and Society
abstract: None

title: A Cloud–Edge Manufacturing Service Combination Collaborative Optimization Method Based on Federated Learning Framework
authors: Lei Wang, Wenhui Zhang, Zihan Li, Yang Xu, Jun Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Privacy protection and efficient collaboration are critical challenges in optimizing manufacturing service combination. This article proposes an innovative solution that deeply embeds a federated learning architecture within a cloud-edge collaborative framework. Utilizing an uncertainty modeling approach for Manufacturing Service Capability (MSC) metrics as the coordination mechanism, the solution implements a three-tier architecture comprising edge node layer, federated coordination layer, and cloud platform layer. This enables "data retention at the edge, parameter-based collaborative interaction, and iterative closed-loop feedback."

title: Design and Evaluation of a Direct-Solar Pumping System for Automatic Drip Irrigation of Gramineous Forages
authors: Martin Aguirre Medina, Santos Miguel Orozco Soto
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
