abstract: With the aim of mitigating the impacts caused by variations in the rainy season that directly affect the production of forage grasses in San Lucas, Michoacán, this work presents the design and evaluation of a direct-solar pumping system for drip irrigation over an area of 0.74 ha. This development considered the meteorological data from the National Renewable Energy Laboratory database, as well as the hydraulic characteristics of the plot and the water requirements of the crop, in order to design both the hydraulic infrastructure and the photovoltaic array. The proposal was evaluated using the specialized software, System Advisor Model, resulting in a system capable of supplying a required flow rate of 3.52 lps during the dry season, ensuring continuous drip irrigation operation, without the use of battery banks, from 10:00 AM. to 4:00 PM. The obtained results support the technical and economic feasibility of the system, therefore, its implementation is recommended as a cost-effective and sustainable alternative for local agricultural production.

title: Transcriptomic Signatures and Pathway Pertubations in Wilson Disease Liver Tissue: an in-Silico Approach
authors: Akansha Bisht, Yasha Hasija
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Wilson Disease is a rare hereditary disorder that involves copper accumulation and severe liver dysfunction. Herein, we describe an in-silico investigation of the GSE197406 microarray data set employing pipelines written in Python, which we used to describe changes in gene expression in the liver between subjects with Wilson Disease and healthy controls. After data preprocessing and quality control steps were completed using predetermined analytical methods and open-source libraries in Python, differential expression analysis revealed a number of upregulated genes including GOLM1 and CD24, and downregulated genes including HADH, PBLD, and ACSM3 in liver samples. Functional enrichment analyses, including Gene Ontology and KEGG pathway mapping, demonstrated significant changes to metabolic processes, amino acid degradation pathways, extracellular matrix organization, and fatty acid oxidation. Additionally, to further elucidate molecular interaction, we conducted a protein-protein interaction (PPI) network analysis to identify central hub proteins and potential interaction modules leading to the pathology of Wilson Disease. Pathway enrichment analysis and PPI network analyses revealed essential nodes and subnetworks associated with peroxisomal function and cancer signaling pathways. Together, this computational pipeline identifies a new potential understanding of the molecular pathogenesis of Wilson Disease and illustrates the utility of combining differential expression and functional annotation with protein-protein interaction network analysis for transcriptomic analysis and future biomarker discovery.

title: Design and Analysis of a Synchronous Reluctance Motor with Low-Cost Ferrite Magnets for EVs
authors: M Deepak, Monika P, Gowtham P, Thapasya P
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Synchronous reluctance motors (SynRMs) have attracted a lot of attention lately because of major improvements in their overall efficiency, control schemes, and design. Our response to these needs has been to provide an alternative Electric Vehicle(EVs) that is both efficient and reliable at a low price. However, costly rare-earth elements are still used in a large number of modern EV motors, which raises production costs and restricts sustainability. In order to overcome these obstacles, this study suggests using ferrite magnets as a cost-effective and sustainable substitute. The advantages of reluctance-based operation can be obtained while lowering reliance on rare-earth elements by integrating ferrite materials into a SynRM. With an emphasis on improving efficiency and cost-effectiveness through material selection and performance evaluation, this study offers a methodical design and development methodology for a FASynRMs meant for EVs. The motor is both modeled simulated using ANSYS software, which allows for a thorough examination of how magnet type affects electromagnetic performance. The final design is chosen and a rotor prototype is made once it has been optimized for both efficiency and manufacturing. The practicality of the suggested design is then confirmed by testing the prototype under a range of operational situations.

title: A SysML v2-Based Framework for Multi-Disciplinary System Virtual Integration
authors: Tianxiao Xu, Néjib Moalla, Mohand-Lounes Bentaha, Hazal Aktekin, Giuseppe Cereda, Claudia Agostinelli
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In the automotive industry, as market demands continue to grow, the complexity of vehicle systems has been increasing accordingly. Vehicle manufacturers are thus confronted with the challenge of offering more design options. Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) has been introduced to manage the complexity of product development. Meanwhile, its integration with Multidisciplinary Design Analysis and Optimization (MDAO) provides a harmonized methodology for the design and analysis of complex systems, offering a system-level virtual integration environment to support decision-making processes. However, current MBSE modeling languages such as SysML v1.x are extensions of the graphical modeling language UML and are considered semi-formal. Their limited capability to extend and integrate with modeling languages across different disciplines significantly restricts their effectiveness. SysML v2, built upon the formal modeling language KerML and supporting standardized APIs, enhances model interoperability. By utilizing SysML v2 to develop a domain-specific modeling language tailored for multidisciplinary engineering, it becomes possible to effectively maintain data consistency between system design and analysis processes, establish a virtual integration platform, and ensure interoperability with other environments. This paper presents a SysML v2-based multidisciplinary system virtual integration framework, implemented by constructing a coupled multidisciplinary problem model in the context of automotive systems.

title: Real-Time Detection of Driver Distraction Due to Alcohol and Fatigue
authors: Ramkumar. M.O, Jayakumar. D, Arockiya Kriyashini. H, Dheepika. S, R. Rajmohan, Deepika. S
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Health hazards posed by drowsy driving, as well as alcohol use, are some of the leading causes of death. In addition to being manual devices that require action from the driver, existing safety devices, such as breath analysers and drowsiness warning systems, are inadequate. Hence, there is an urgent need for a system that can automatically detect the impairment and does not need the driver to intervene. The majority of systems in the market administer breath alcoholic tests with the help of a sensor, whereas drowsiness warning systems, even those that employ a camera based system, do only assist. These solutions are limited because they rely on the cooperation of the driver to a great extent and could be ineffective in the real-time, particularly where the driver is already incapacitated or unresponsive. Such systems also allow the operation of a vehicle in undesired conditions. The main idea of this proposed model is to construct entirely autonomous driver monitoring system aimed at both alcohol consumption and driver fatigue. While an alcohol sensor like the MQ- 3 will sense an increase in concentrations of alcohol, a camera for observation will employ the Haar Cascade algorithm to track the driver's eye movements, the rate of blink and head position for drowsiness detection in real time. Intervention of the system will also be done to inhibit starting the engine of the vehicle and sending notifications to the owner, where impairment is observed. To make sure the driver reacts to drowsiness alert, the system consists of other physical alarms like vibrating seats and a robotic hand module.

title: Deep Learning-Powered Weapon Detection and Classification System using Efficientdet for Public Safety
authors: S. Ashok Kumar, Resmi R Nair, K. Francis David, S. Muthukumaran, D. Senthilkumar, N. Suriya
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Real-time weapon detection in surveillance systems is critical for public safety but remains challenging due to occlusions, varying lighting, and the need for high computational efficiency. This paper presents a robust weapon detection and classification framework based on the EfficientDet object detection model. Leveraging its compound scaling method and Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN), our system achieves a high balance between accuracy and speed. We trained and evaluated our model on a curated dataset containing handguns, knives, and rifles under diverse conditions. The proposed system attained a mean Average Precision(mAP@0.5) of 91.5% and an average inference speed of 28 FPS on an NVIDIA GTX 1080Ti, demonstrating its suitability for real-time deployment. Furthermore, we integrated an automated alert mechanism that triggers email notifications with detection snapshots upon identifying a threat with high confidence. This work provides a practical and efficient solution for enhancing security in smart surveillance systems.

title: Real Time Vitals Monitoring Using STM32F429 Microcontroller
authors: Bellam Vyshnavi, Sabbasani Shivani Reddy, Bodampati Srujan Goud, J. Swetha Priyanka
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This work showcases the design and development of a real-time health monitoring system with the STM32F429 microcontroller. The system is designed to monitor continuously and analyze fundamental physiological parameters such as heart rate, respiratory rate, and body temperature. These vital signs are essential indicators of an individual's health condition and can help identify medical abnormalities early. The core processing unit is the STM32F429 microcontroller, which is renowned for its low power usage, high performance, and extensive peripheral support. It communicates with biomedical sensors to read data, which is processed and output on an LCD display. Abnormality detection logic is also provided as part of the system to notify users when any of the parameters exceed the specified safe limits. With portability, accuracy, and affordability as the designing philosophy, this embedded solution provides enormous opportunities for tracking personal health, remote monitoring of patients, and integration with Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems. The project showcases the effective application of embedded systems to enhance healthcare delivery, particularly in resource-limited or remote settings.

title: Calibration Method for Mixed-mode Return Loss Parameters of Network Cable Analyzer
authors: Lei Yu, Jianqiang Gao, Junwen Xia
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: The network cable analyzer is mainly used for the certification testing of network cables in commercial buildings. It can generate test reports on the scattering parameters of network cables within the specified frequency range in just a few seconds. Currently, the national measurement technical specification only clarifies the measurement methods and corresponding measurement equipment for the scattering parameters in the differential mode. For the scattering parameters in the common mode and mixed mode, no technical requirements or measurement methods have been provided. However, most commercial network cable analyzers on the market can provide measurement results for different modes. The design, production, installation and acceptance test standards for twisted-pair cables used in the structured cabling system also include parameter indicators for different modes. Therefore, it is necessary to test the scattering parameters of network cable analyzers under different modes to ensure their performance. Based on the above situation, this paper analyzes the measurement method of the return loss parameters of network cable analyzers in the mixed network mode, as well as the measurement equipment and devices used, and provides uncertainty assessment content for some measurement results, providing a reference for subsequent related measurement activities.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Sentiment Analysis: Comparative Insights into Sequential, Convolutional, and Transformer Architectures
authors: Vandana Jaiswal, Mahesh Parmar, Nishant Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Sentiment analysis, a significant task within Natural Language Processing (NLP), plays a crucial role in understanding public opinion, customer satisfaction, and emerging trends through online platforms such as Twitter, Instagram, WhatsApp, and e-commerce portals. We proposed a hybrid sentiment analysis model (CNN+BERT) leveraging deep learning techniques to achieve superior accuracy compared to individual models. Using the Twitter US Airline Sentiment dataset, preprocessing steps including data cleaning, label encoding, train-test splitting, and tokenization were applied, followed by the implementation of CNN, LSTM, GRU, BERT, DistilBERT, and a proposed hybrid (CNN+BERT) model. Experimental evaluation revealed that the hybrid model achieved the highest accuracy of 92.46%. The proposed approach has potential applications in brand monitoring, product review analysis, market research, customer feedback evaluation, and real-time trend detection.

title: Analysing Social Media as a Digital Tool for Inclusive Higher Education in India
authors: Sheel Nidhi Tripathi, Jagriti Basera, Ayushi Chopra, Vanshika Bhatia
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The impact of Higher Education in information sharing and its mechanics could be greatly enhanced if social media is being incorporated with Digital Pedagogy. This study appraises the usage of social media as a tool for digital education in colleges and universities. The study attempts to comprehend how these social media platforms can enhance learning when it comes to online by providing an analysis of established educational theories like constructivism, connectivism, and sociocultural theory. A comprehensive review of recent literature was undertaken to assess various implications of social media in academics. An empirical analysis of the benefits and drawbacks has also been performed. Real-time cases have also been analyzed. The study concludes that despite interest, a noteworthy gap is apparent in achieving pedagogical goals despite the usage of social media. Therefore, future ways for pedagogical innovation are also suggested. The results are significant for teachers, course designers, and policymakers who wish to incorporate social media for creating immersive digital learning experiences.

title: DeepMarineSegNet: A Multi-Scale Attention-Enhanced Network for Underwater Semantic Segmentation
authors: Rajat Amat, Mourina Ghosh, Amitava Nag, Robin Pathak
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Underwater semantic segmentation is essential for marine monitoring and autonomous navigation but is hindered by poor visibility and complex object boundaries. This paper proposes ‘DeepMarineSegNet’, a multi-scale deep learning framework combining parallel dilated convolutions and spatial-channel attention for enhanced feature extraction. Residual connections improve gradient flow and training efficiency. Evaluated on a custom underwater dataset with coral, fish, and background classes, DeepMarineSegNet achieves 96.10% IoU, 98.31% Dice, and 98.87% pixel accuracy, demonstrating its effectiveness for high-precision underwater segmentation and AUV perception.

title: Artificial Intelligence Powered Cyber Threat Detection and Defence Mechanism Using Blockchain Security for Financial Attack Based on Deep Feature Engineering
authors: R. Saranya, Sumathy Kingslin
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The escalating sophistication and frequency of financial cyberattacks pose a significant threat to the stability of financial sectors and the security of user data. Traditional cybersecurity methods are increasingly inadequate in the face of evolving attack vectors, often failing to analyse the complex nature of cyber threats accurately. This inadequacy stems from a reliance on outdated techniques, poor detection accuracy due to limited feature engineering, and vulnerabilities in cryptographic security, leading to authentication failures and data breaches. To address these critical shortcomings, a novel approach is proposed an Artificial Intelligence-powered Cyber Threat Detection and Defense Mechanism utilizing blockchain security, underpinned by deep feature engineering, specifically tailored to combat financial attacks. The process begins with data preprocessing using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex>-score and min-max normalization techniques. This normalization ensures that all features are within a comparable range, preventing features with larger scales from dominating the analysis and improving the performance of subsequent algorithms. Following normalization, the Cyber Intrusion Impact Defect Rate (CIIR) is analysed using Support Vector Scaling Margin (SVSM). SVSM assesses the potential impact of cyber intrusions by evaluating the margins between different classes in the feature space. Crucially, feature selection is then performed using the Ant Intensive Social Spider Optimization Technique (AISSOT). The core of the threat detection phase lies in the Deep Scalar Recurrent Neural Network (DSRNN). DSRNNs are a specialised type of recurrent neural network (RNN) designed to process sequential data efficiently. This phase aims to create a secure and tamper-proof environment for financial transactions. The foundation of this phase is the Hash Index Shuffle Chain Technique (HISCT). To further enhance data security, a Creates Homomorphic Hyper Lattice Encryption Crypto Policy (HH-LECP) is employed. Finally, the Master Node Authorizations Policy (MNAP) governs access to the blockchain and its data. The proposed system is expected to produce higher detection accuracy, improve cyber-attack detection by ensuring feature relation-based intrusion detection, enhance precision, recall rate, and increase true positives, F1 score, while also improving crypto security with blockchain technologies, thereby safeguarding financial institutions and their customers from the ever-increasing threat of cyberattacks.

title:
Evaluation of AC Loss Characteristics of MgB
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Coil Under Rotating Magnetic Field Generated by PM Rotor
authors: Satsuki Okumura, Koki Okubo, Yutaka Terao, Shuichiro Fuchino, Hiroyuki Ohsaki, Keiichi Okai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Fully superconducting rotating machines, which have superconducting field and armature windings, attract attention because they achieve high efficiency and high-power density. In the design of a fully superconducting rotating machine, it is very important to estimate the AC losses in the armature windings to which AC magnetic fields and AC currents are applied. Therefore, we measure the AC losses of an armature winding using an AC loss measurement equipment, calculating the AC loss from the difference between the mechanical inputs obtained with and without the superconducting coil present. The rotation of the permanent magnet rotor causes transient phenomena due to the change in magnetic field distribution. In addition, temperature distributions occur along the coil. In this study, we conduct experiments and perform a numerical analysis to evaluate the AC loss characteristics of an MgB<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> coil under a rotating magnetic field, taking into account the magnetic field distributions. We evaluate the validity of the AC loss measurement using this equipment by comparing the measurement and the numerical analysis results.

title: WaterSplat-SLAM: Photorealistic Monocular SLAM in Underwater Environment
authors: Kangxu Wang, Shaofeng Zou, Chenxing Jiang, Yixiang Dai, Siang Chen, Shaojie Shen, Guijin Wang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Underwater monocular SLAM is a challenging problem with applications from autonomous underwater vehicles to marine archaeology. However, existing underwater SLAM methods struggle to produce maps with high-fidelity rendering. In this paper, we propose WaterSplat-SLAM, a novel monocular underwater SLAM system that achieves robust pose estimation and photorealistic dense mapping. Specifically, we couple semantic medium filtering into two-view 3D reconstruction prior to enable underwater-adapted camera tracking and depth estimation. Furthermore, we present a semantic-guided rendering and adaptive map management strategy with an online medium-aware Gaussian map, modeling underwater environment in a photorealistic and compact manner. Experiments on multiple underwater datasets demonstrate that WaterSplat-SLAM achieves robust camera tracking and high-fidelity rendering in underwater environments. The code will be realeased at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/KX-Wang77/WaterSplat-SLAM</uri>.

title: Wireless Power Transfer and IoT in EV Charging: Opportunities, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Shahid Karim, Sohaib Tahir Chauhdary, Haval Kamil, Faisal Mumtaz, Ghulam Farid
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The widespread adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has spurred the development of advanced charging infrastructures that are efficient, intelligent, and user-friendly. Among these, Wireless Electric Vehicle Charging Systems (EVWCS) have emerged as a transformative solution, enabling contactless power transfer through various Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technologies, such as inductive coupling, resonant inductive coupling, capacitive power transfer, and magnetic gear-based systems. The review will conduct an extensive evaluation of these WPT methods through detailed analysis of their operational mechanisms and their advantages and limitations for both stationary and dynamic charging applications. The paper will examine IoT applications for EV charging system enhancement through smart grid communication and real-time data exchange and cybersecurity protection. The paper also examines the challenges encountered in the implementation of EVWCS which include technical limitations, health and safety issues, electromagnetic compatibility as well as high cost of deployment. Owing to the summary of the main contributions in existing literature, as well as the development of major research gaps, the paper presents articulated approach to future research to implement secure, efficient, and scalable wireless EV charging infrastructures.

title: IEEE Control Systems Society Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control

title: Hierarchical Trajectory Planning of Floating-Base Multi-Link Robot for Maneuvering in Confined Environments
authors: Yicheng Chen, Jinjie Li, Haokun Liu, Zicheng Luo, Kotaro Kaneko, Moju Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Floating-base multi-link robots can change their shape during flight, making them well-suited for applications in confined environments such as autonomous inspection and search and rescue. However, trajectory planning for such systems remains an open challenge because the problem lies in a high-dimensional, constraint-rich space where collision avoidance must be addressed together with kinematic limits and dynamic feasibility. This work introduces a hierarchical trajectory planning framework that integrates global guidance with configuration-aware local optimization. First, we exploit the dual nature of these robots—the root link as a rigid body for guidance and the articulated joints for flexibility—to generate global anchor states that decompose the planning problem into tractable segments. Second, we design a local trajectory planner that optimizes each segment in parallel with differentiable objectives and constraints, systematically enforcing kinematic feasibility and maintaining dynamic feasibility by avoiding control singularities. Third, we implement a complete system that directly processes point-cloud data, eliminating the need for handcrafted obstacle models. Extensive simulations and real-world experiments confirm that this framework enables an articulated aerial robot to exploit its morphology for maneuvering that rigid robots cannot achieve. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first planning framework for floating-base multi-link robots that has been demonstrated on a real robot to generate continuous, collision-free, and dynamically feasible trajectories directly from raw point-cloud inputs, without relying on handcrafted obstacle models.

title: Context Modeling with Multimodal Prompts for Emotion Recognition in Conversation
authors: Qian Dong, Weihong Ren, Yu Gao, Jianzhuang Liu, Honghai Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Emotion Recognition in Conversation (ERC) plays an important role in driving the development of human-machine interaction. After the extensive exploration of the text modality, visual and audio information has attracted considerable attention. Most of the existing approaches adopt either the attention mechanism or graph neural networks to conduct multi-modal fusion by directly utilizing pre-extracted single modal features, but they ignore the inherent priors and characteristics (highly related to emotions) contained in different modalities. For example, for visual modality, facial expression can well reveal a person's emotional state, and the intonation, speech rate and volume of audio modality also reflect emotional fluctuation. Thus, in this work, we aim to promote context fusion among multi-modal features by exploring inherent modal priors. Firstly, we create textual descriptions for different emotion categories belonging to different modalities, denoted as multimodal prompts which can be generated either from common sense or using large language models (e.g., ChatGPT). Then, we propose an adaptive gating fusion module which dynamically learns the weights between unimodal features and prompts, and allows the multimodal prompts to participate in encoding and enriching modal information. Finally, to facilitate multimodal fusion, we design a multimodal progressive encoder to learn inter-modal interactions among conversational utterances. Experimental results show that our model outperforms state-of-the-art models in ERC on two popular benchmark datasets.

title: A Hybrid Fractional Chebyshev–Legendre Spectral Collocation Method for Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman Equations
authors: Alvian Alif Hidayatullah, Subchan Subchan, Sena Safarina, Tahiyatul Asfihani, R. Mohamad Atok, Irma Fitria, Andriyansah, Kasno Pamungkas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Stochastic optimal control problems are commonly formulated as optimization problems constrained by stochastic dynamical systems, whose value functions satisfy Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman (HJB) equations. Owing to their strong nonlinearity and high dimensionality, closed-form solutions of HJB equations are rarely available, thereby motivating the development of robust and highly accurate numerical methods. This research introduces two hybrid spectral–collocation strategies for the numerical solution of stochastic HJB equations, constructed from different combinations of orthogonal polynomial bases. The first strategy utilizes shifted Chebyshev polynomials for time approximation and fractional-order Legendre polynomials for state approximation, while the second utilizes shifted Legendre polynomials in time and fractional-order Chebyshev polynomials in state. A convergence analysis is developed within the Caputo fractional derivative framework to justify the proposed methods and to establish the associated error estimates. The resulting nonlinear algebraic system is then solved using the collocation method. Numerical simulations, including an application to a resource extraction model, confirm that the proposed methods attain a high level of accuracy and exhibit convergence rates in strong agreement with the theoretical predictions. These results demonstrate that the developed hybrid spectral–collocation frameworks constitute reliable and efficient tools for addressing stochastic optimal control problems based on HJB equations.

title: Enhancing Fine-Grained Sketch-based Image Retrieval through Contextual Information
authors: Jinyu Xu, Qing Xie, Jiachen Li, Zhifeng Bao, Yanchun Ma, Yongjian Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Fine-grained sketch-based image retrieval aims to retrieve matched natural photos at instance level based on hand-drawn sketches. Understanding sketches and aligning them with photos in the joint embedding space are the primary challenges. Previous works have attempted to improve the understanding and alignment of fine-grained semantics by leveraging a selected subset of local features based on the local matching strategy. However, these approaches rely heavily on local features to capture regional visual details, which introduces unavoidable uncertainty and noise in the matching process. In addition, existing works lack the ability to effectively separate unmatched pairs with similar overall semantics in the common space, which may disrupt fine-grained semantic alignment. In this paper, we present CIGSPA: a Contextual Information Guided Sketch-Photo Alignment framework that leverages latent contextual information to enhance sketch understanding and sketch-photo matching without additional knowledge and prior supervision. Specifically, we first design an Adaptive Local Feature Filter module to refine feature similarity based on the context of local features and select more appropriate features, which ensure the semantic fidelity and spatial compactness of salient features. Secondly, we design a Contextual Weighted Contrastive loss to guide global sketch-photo matching by leveraging discriminative contextual information from photos, and hence aligns the sketch and photo at the instance level. Our comprehensive experiments on six public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed CIGSPA. The code is available at https://github.com/jinyuxu-whut/CIGSPA4FG-SBIR.

title: Communication Specific Emitter Identification Based on Deep Convolutional Feature Matching
authors: Jie Ren, Xiaoyang Chen, Ming Li, Shuai Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Communication specific emitter identification (CSEI) exploits the unintentional modulation features embedded in an emitter’s transmitted signals, known as radio frequency fingerprints (RFFs), to distinguish individual emitters. CSEI has been widely applied in both military and civilian domains for reliable emitter identification. However, traditional approaches often depend on prior information and handcrafted features, which restrict their identification performance. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a novel deep convolutional feature matching framework for communication specific emitter identification. The method leverages data augmentation and preprocessing strategies to enrich the training dataset, thereby enhancing model identification accuracy and robustness. A multi-dimensional convolution architecture is designed, integrating two-dimensional, one-dimensional, and dilated convolutions for hierarchical feature extraction. Moreover, a joint loss function combining cross-entropy and matrix correlation loss is introduced for effective model optimization. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that, compared to state-of-the-art methods, the proposed approach achieves superior identification accuracy and robustness in complex electromagnetic environments.

title: Modeling, Evaluation, and Comparison of Battery Equalizers Based on the Conservation of Energy
authors: Nasim Hasanpour, Mohammad Reza Mohammadi, S. Ali Khajehoddin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Equalization systems significantly affect the performance and lifespan of battery packs. As battery packs increase in size and complexity, accurate and computationally efficient tools for evaluating equalizers become increasingly important. Mathematical modeling offers a practical alternative to real-time simulations and experimental testing, which can be costly and time-consuming. However, conventional modeling approaches often experience accuracy limitations during long-duration equalization, reducing the reliability of estimations. This paper presents simplified yet highly accurate mathematical models for various equalizers based on the conservation of energy. The models incorporate converter efficiency and use a closed-form interval-based approach that significantly reduces memory usage and computational load. A comprehensive comparison of equalizer performance is conducted using the proposed models, and novel metrics are introduced for improved evaluation of energy redistribution and loss. The results demonstrate that the proposed models predict equalization time and voltage with less than 1% error. In simulation and cycle-by-cycle-based approaches, the processing time increases to days as battery capacity grows; However, the closed-form model exhibits capacity-independent computation time, enabling high scalability for large EV packs. Furthermore, the results show that direct-cell-to-cell equalizers achieve higher energy transfer efficiency, while adjacent-cell-to- cell structures provide faster equalization, offering practical guidance for topology selection in EV applications.

title:
A ROS-Based Hardware Abstraction Layer for Multifunction End-Effector Integration
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Victor R. Passos, Wesley R. De Oliveira
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper brings the architecture for a ROSbased Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) to interface with the devices of a mechatronic end-effector. A HAL is a middleware between an application and the hardware, responsible for abstracting low-level operations for the client applications. A key feature of this approach is the scalability and modularity delivered by the ROS framework. To illustrate this, the paper presents a case study related to the modeling and integration of a critical-safety gripping function of the given robotic end-effector. This function is modelled and simulated using the timed automata formalism in UPPAAL, which is then implemented within the HAL using the proposed architecture. The system’s HAL controller is implemented and tested directly on the Toradex Verdin hardware. The results highlight how this design approach enables the early verification of critical mechatronic functions in robotic systems.

title: Design of a Gate Drive Circuit for Thyristor Performance Enhancement
authors: Yu Lu, Kaikai Zou, Haiying Li, Gang Li, Ke Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: High-power semiconductor devices form the cornerstone of modern high-voltage power electronic systems. Owing to their high blocking voltage, large current-carrying capability, and low on-state losses, thyristors remain an indispensable component in applications such as High-Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) transmission and Static VAR Compensators (SVCs). Nevertheless, conventional thyristors exhibit two inherent limitations. First, their turn-on speed is constrained by the spreading velocity of the internal carrier plasma, which restricts their use in high-frequency applications. Second, their PNPN structure is highly sensitive to a high rate of rise of anode voltage (dv/dt), making them susceptible to spurious turn-on, thereby compromising system reliability. To address these challenges, this paper presents the design and analysis of a high-performance gate drive circuit. The proposed circuit optimizes the thyristor's switching characteristics through a dual-mode operation: during turn-on, a storage capacitor delivers a high-peak, fast-rising current pulse to accelerate the formation of the initial conduction region, significantly enhancing the device's turn-on speed and di/dt withstand capability. Subsequently, during the off-state blocking period, a stable negative gate bias is applied to provide a low-impedance path for the displacement current induced by dv/dt transients, thus effectively suppressing spurious turn-on. Simulation results from NI Multisim confirm that the circuit accurately generates the desired forward turn-on pulse and negative clamping voltage, validating the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed design.

title: Hybrid Analog-Digital SVPWM Strategy for High-Frequency Three-Phase Buck PFC Converter
authors: Qiang Chen, Huaiying Yang, Sheng Su, Xing Zhang, Mingyao Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The space vector pulse width modulation (SVPWM) strategy is commonly utilized in three-phase Buck power factor correction (PFC) converters with high-frequency and high-power density. The existing SVPWM strategies are computationally intensive and require complex voltage sampling circuits, which have restricted the high-frequency and high-power-density development of three-phase Buck PFC converters. In this paper, a hybrid analog-digital space vector pulse width modulation (HAD-SVPWM) strategy is proposed. The proposed HAD-SVPWM generates the modulation signals through simple analog circuits, avoiding complex mathematical calculations and significantly reducing the computational burden on digital processing. Finally, the theoretical analysis is verified through a 1kW experimental prototype.

title: Role of AI in Breast Cancer Detection by Using CNN Model
authors: Divya Khapra, Deepak Kumar Shamra, Liannaka Dadi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI), particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), is revolutionizing breast cancer detection by significantly enhancing diagnostic accuracy and efficiency. Breast cancer remains the most common cancer among women worldwide, with over 2.3 million cases diagnosed in 2020 by the WHO. Early detection significantly improves survival rates, yet traditional screening methods, such as mammography, often face challenges, including false positives, false negatives, and subjective interpretation. CNNs, a class of deep learning models designed for image analysis, have demonstrated remarkable success in identifying complex patterns in mammographic and histopathological images. Clinical studies, including Google Health's AI system trial published in The Lancet (McKinney et al., 2020), have shown that AI reduces false positives by 5.7 % and false negatives by 9.4 %, even outperforming human experts in some cases. This research reviews the role of CNNs in breast cancer detection, leveraging insights from peer-reviewed studies and public datasets such as INbreast and DDSM. The findings strongly suggest that CNN-based AI systems can improve early detection rates, enhance diagnostic precision, and reduce the workload on healthcare professionals. The integration of AI represents a promising step towards more accurate, accessible, and efficient breast cancer screening solutions.

title: RNA Transcription and Splicing Dysregulation in the Prefrontal Cortex of Huntington’s Disease
authors: Shyamsundar Gowda, Srithan Pothuri, Claudia Lennon, Cristina DeMeo
publish: 2025 IEEE MetroCon
abstract: Huntington’s disease (HD) is a neurodegenerative disorder caused by CAG-repeat expansion in the huntingtin (HTT) gene and is traditionally associated with striatal degeneration. Increasing evidence, however, implies the prefrontal cortex in HD-related cognitive decline. In this exploratory computational reanalysis, we examined publicly available RNA-sequencing data from post-mortem Brodmann area 9 tissue of HD patients and matched controls originally published by Labadorf et al. (2016). Differential gene expression (DGE) and alternative splicing (AS) analyses were performed to characterize RNA processing alterations in the HD cortex. DGE analysis identified approximately 1,200 significantly altered transcripts, including upregulation of DDX11L1, a pseudogene associated with ribosomal biogenesis pathways. AS analysis revealed 287 significant events, predominantly exon skipping, with notable splicing alterations in DDX19B, an RNA helicase involved in mRNA nuclear export. Together, these findings indicate dysregulation of RNA processing in the HD prefrontal cortex and support a role for RNA metabolic dysfunction in HD pathogenesis.

title: A Common Mode Voltage Suppression Method for Five-Level Neutral Point Clamped Inverter Based on Modified Carrier-Overlapped PWM strategy
authors: Wei Yuan, Zhangchao Wu, Feng Zhao, Zhengyu Gong, Hong Cheng, Cong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The industrial adoption of five-level neutral point clamped (NPC) inverters is primarily constrained by two challenges: neutral point potential imbalance and large-amplitude common mode voltage (CMV). While the recently introduced Carrier-Overlapped PWM (COPWM) technique effectively balances the neutral point voltage, this paper investigates its extension - the carrier phase opposition overlapped PWM (CPOOPWM) strategy - specifically for CMV suppression. The CMV amplitude can only be suppressed from 5V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dc</inf>/12 to V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dc</inf>/3 by CPOOPWM. This paper presents an enhanced technique that achieves a markedly lower CMV peak of V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dc</inf>/6. This performance is realized by applying the CPOOPWM strategy, which enables the alignment of three-phase multilevel voltage pulses. To accommodate the specifics of COPWM, a voltage level reconfiguration technique is also devised and implemented. The simultaneous management of both neutral point balance and CMV is investigated. Simulation studies confirm the effectiveness of the overall approach.

title: A Novel Wideband Phase Shifter Based on Pin-Controlling of Gap Waveguide
authors: Jingchun Fan, Jing-Yi Zhang, Jun-Jie Zhang, Qiao Yu Chen, Yan-Qun Liu, Gang Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: In this paper, a novel gap waveguide phase shifter utilizing tunable metal pins of the upper plate for phase shifting is proposed. This design is intended for precise phase control of high-frequency electromagnetic waves in the millimeter-wave band. The fundamental operating principle of the proposed phase shifter relies on altering the distribution of the metal pins to modify the propagation constant (β) along the transmission path, which results in stable and controllable phases in a wide bandwidth. To verify the scheme, a broadband 180-degree phase shifter is implemented and characterized, providing a complementary phase shift relative to the conventional transmission path. Promising simulation results across the 26–36 GHz frequency band validate its feasibility and superior performance. The designed phase shifter can be further used in specific millimeter-wave communication systems, multi-beam antenna arrays, and sophisticated RF front-ends.

title: RGB-D Fusion for Wide Field of View User Feedback in Teleoperation Context
authors: Raphaël D’Orfani, Antoine N. André, Mehdi Benallegue, Rafael Cisneros-Limón, Guillaume Caron
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Effective teleoperation involves immersive and responsive visual feedback to support depth perception and spatial understanding to achieve precise control. Standard camera views naturally constrain the operator’s Field of View (FoV) of the remote scene, especially in cluttered or dynamic scenarios. We present a real-time RGB-D fusion system that expands the operator’s FoV by employing immersive 3D reconstruction. Our system incorporates the Azure Kinect sensor into Unreal Engine using the Robot Operating System (ROS) communication, rendering live sensor information onto a spherical mesh. This allows for smooth, wide-FoV rendering of the scene with greater peripheral context and depth continuity. In contrast to planar or depth-free systems, the proposed method is enhanced by live depth retranscription for more interactive teleoperation, leading to better scene understanding. This architecture lays the basis for flexible, high-fidelity remote interaction for robotics applications. All our developments and implementations are publicly available at https://github.com/isri-aist/RGB-D_Fusion.

title: Improved YOLOv10 with ScConv and FLA for Real-Time Cast Pipe Character Detection
authors: Haoyu Lin, Yu Wang, Yan Jiang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced YOLOv10-based detector for real-time cast-pipe character recognition. The model integrates Spatial–Channel Reconstruction Convolution to reduce feature redundancy and Focused Linear Attention to improve the representation of small targets, forming a cascaded pipeline of early purification and focused attention. Experiments on an industrial dataset of 5,787 images demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves detection accuracy while reducing model complexity, achieving higher mean average precision and faster inference compared with the YOLOv10 baseline.

title: FPGA-Based Secure Automated Car Parking System Using FSM and VHDL
authors: Amrit Raj, Deepti Kakkar, Rishabh Upadhyay, Lavkush Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Rapid urbanization and the increasing number of vehicles have amplified the demand for secure, efficient, and real-time parking management. Conventional manual or microcontroller-based systems often suffer from congestion, delays, and limited reliability. This paper proposes a fully automated and hardware-centric car parking system implemented on a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) using Finite State Machine (FSM) logic coded in VHDL. The design integrates dual infrared (IR) sensors for vehicle detection, a dual-password authentication mechanism for secure access, relaydriven gate operation, and dual 7 -segment displays for real-time slot availability. The system was modeled and verified in Vivado and implemented on the Xilinx Artix-7 (Mimas A7) FPGA board. Experimental validation demonstrated deterministic FSM transitions with an average system response time of 0.25 seconds, 98 % slot detection accuracy, and errorfree password authentication. Compared to microcontroller-based approaches, the FPGA design achieves higher speed, improved security, and modular scalability. Furthermore, the architecture provides a foundation for future enhancements such as IoTenabled reservations, cloud monitoring, and license plate recognition, making it highly suitable for deployment in next-generation smart parking infrastructures.

title: Development of Mamdani Fuzzy Rule-Based System Using the Gray Wolf Optimizer (GWO) Algorithm for Maritime Safety Prediction
authors: Wahyu Hildan Syah, Ade Candra, Sutarman
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The Gray Wolf Optimizer (GWO) algorithm is an optimization method inspired by the social hierarchy and hunting behavior of gray wolves. Gray wolves are categorized as alpha, beta, delta, and omega. From the initial population of wolves ‘P’, the one closest to the prey becomes the alpha. In the fuzzy rule development system, the Gray Wolf Optimizer algorithm is used to optimize Mamdani fuzzy rules, contributing to improved accuracy in predicting maritime safety. This is essential to ensure operational safety in maritime navigation by considering various factors that influence sailing conditions. The Mamdani fuzzy system used in this model is capable of handling uncertainty and ambiguity in the data related to factors affecting maritime safety. The Gray Wolf Optimizer algorithm aids in optimizing the fuzzy rules to produce more accurate predictions. Based on a comparison of optimization results, the model developed using the Gray Wolf Optimizer and Mamdani fuzzy system demonstrates better performance than conventional methods in terms of maritime safety prediction accuracy.

title: Handwritten Input Using a Palm as a Writing Medium in MR Environments
authors: Kotaro Nemoto, Takeshi Sasaki
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper introduces a handwriting input method that uses the palm of the hand as a writing surface to address the limited input options currently available for MR devices. By providing users with an additional text entry method, this research aims to improve the overall input experience in mixed reality (MR) environments. We developed a prototype on the Microsoft HoloLens 2 and conducted a study comparing four input methods: aerial handwriting, palm-supported handwriting, whiteboard-supported handwriting, and the default virtual keyboard. The results demonstrated that having a physical writing surface significantly enhances input speed, accuracy, and usability compared to aerial handwriting. It also demonstrated the possibility of eyes-free input. This indicates that using body parts as writing surfaces is a promising approach for text entry on MR devices.

title: Health State Assessment of IGBT Bonding Wires Based on GWO-AAKR Under Varying Operating Conditions
authors: Zhijie Qiu, Xiaofeng Jiang, Dan Zheng, Tao Fan, Xuhui Wen, Chaohui Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBTs) are critical components in power electronic systems, and the degradation of their bonding wires is a key factor affecting reliability. This paper proposes a health state assessment method for IGBT bonding wires based on Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) and Auto-Associative Kernel Regression (AAKR) under varying operating conditions. First, the on-state voltage drop (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CE(on)</inf>) is identified as the primary aging indicator, considering its sensitivity to bonding wire degradation. However, V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CE(on)</inf> is easily affected by collector current and junction temperature fluctuations, which mask aging-related changes. To address this, the AAKR algorithm is used to establish a normal behavior model, with the GWO algorithm optimizing the critical bandwidth parameter in AAKR to improve model accuracy and reduce computational complexity. Subsequently, a Least Squares Support Vector Machine (LS-SVM) is trained using residuals from the AAKR model, along with operating condition data, to classify the health states of bonding wires. Experimental validation on an IGBT testbed, involving controlled fault injection by shearing bonding wires, demonstrates that the proposed method achieves high assessment accuracy (96.25% and 95% for two samples), effectively distinguishing between healthy, degraded, and failed states. This approach provides a reliable solution for IGBT health monitoring under dynamic operating conditions.

title: An Edge-Assisted Architecture for Context-Aware Matching in Real-Time Goods Transportation Systems
authors: N. R. Sathis Kumar, B. Rohith, B. Yaswanth Babu, A. Sai Prajwin, Y. Vinayasree
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Because of centralized architectures, slow response times, and reliance on middlemen, the logistics industry consistently struggles to effectively match the demands for the transportation of goods with the availability of vehicles. An edge-assisted architecture for context-aware, real-time matching in decentralized goods transportation systems is proposed in this paper. The system distributes computational loads among localized edge nodes, reduces latency, and supports offline-resilient operations by utilizing a hybrid edge-cloud infrastructure. In order to optimize vehicle allocation, a novel context-aware matching algorithm is presented that integrates driver trust scores, historical reliability, load compatibility, geospatial proximity, and real-time traffic conditions. By placing edge micro-controllers at key network locations, localized decision-making is made possible and the need for constant cloud connectivity is decreased. Comparing experimental evaluations to traditional centralized models reveals significant gains in booking efficiency, decreased vehicle idle time, and quicker response rates. Through transparent, technologically driven service accessibility, this architecture not only improves system responsiveness and scalability but also gives independent transport operators more power. The suggested solution is a first step toward distributed logistics platforms that are intelligent and flexible enough to operate in resource-constrained and urban settings.

title: A CPS-PWM Approach Based on Second-order Voltage Injection for Hybrid Modular Multilevel Converters Under Over-modulation Conditions
authors: Yu Tong, Jianqiao Zhou, Linpeng Yao, Songtao Shao, Hongxun Qi, Feng Huang, Gang Shi, Jianwen Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The modular multilevel converter (MMC) serves as an efficient power conversion system for battery energy storage applications. It eliminates the need for line-frequency transformers and thereby enhances overall efficiency while reducing system volume. When high-voltage battery packs are centrally deployed on the MMC's DC bus, the battery system can be decoupled from the PCS, simplifying maintenance. However, as the voltage of the high-voltage battery pack decreases, the half-bridge MMC may experience over-modulation problems. The hybrid MMC offers a cost advantage over the full-bridge MMC and addresses the over-modulation issue caused by the DC voltage drop. This paper introduces a carrier phase-shifted pulse width modulation (CPS-PWM) approach incorporating second-order voltage injection. This approach can eliminate the negative interval of the modulation voltage of the half-bridge submodules under over-modulation conditions. Compared to the conventional CPS-PWM, this approach exhibits better voltage and current harmonic characteristics in medium-voltage applications. Finally, simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in hybrid MMCs operating under over-modulation conditions.

title: Modelling and Current Control of Supercapacitor Controller for Lightweight Robots
authors: Xiuqi Wang, Chiew-Foong Kwong, Adam Rushworth, Salman Ijaz
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This paper presents a modelling and control method of a Supercapacitor controller for lightweight robots. We develop an averaged state-space model that involves arbitrary number of parallel capacitor branches with their equivalent series resistance (ESR) considered, and derive a direct transfer function from duty cycle to supercapacitor current. Based on power stage transfer function, We synthesise a Type-III compensator for closed-loop current regulation. PLECS simulations on a six-branch capacitor network show that the controller (i) limits battery peak current and reduces ripple under dynamic loading, (ii) achieves fast current transitions (0.4 ms) during load steps, and (iii) performs regenerative braking energy recovery while keeping the battery current regulated. Our approach reduces tuning complexity and enhances robustness, while ensuring predictable system response.

title: Automatic Sewing Pattern Generation from Garment Images Using Segmentation and Conditional GANs
authors: Hikaru Suzuki, Alessandro Moro, Sarthak Pathak, Kazunori Umeda
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: An automatic method for generating sewing patterns corresponding to dress images is proposed in this study. In garment production, the creation of sewing patterns, the blueprints for garment construction, from design sketches is a highly complex process that demands substantial expertise and experience. Most existing studies focus on learning from entire garments; however, they face the challenge of reduced shape reproduction accuracy for small parts with diverse shapes, such as collars and sleeves. The proposed method segments a garment image into three main parts—bodice, sleeve, and collar—and inputs each part into a specialized sewing pattern generation model, enabling faithful reproduction of even small and complex garment parts. A custom training dataset consisting of garment images and their corresponding sewing pattern images used in actual garment production is constructed. In addition, a part segmentation model and part-specific GAN-based sewing pattern generation models are developed. The proposed method is capable of adapting to diverse garment shapes and variations across parts, thereby enhancing both the accuracy and efficiency of sewing pattern creation in garment production workflows.

title: Semantic-Integrated Topological Mapping with Factor Graph Optimization for Small Robots in Unknown Environments
authors: Kosuke Sakamoto, Yasuharu Kunii
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Small, resource-constrained swarm robots require scene understanding that is both semantic and metric, yet most SLAM pipelines either ignore semantics or demand heavy sensors. We propose an online hybrid factor-graph optimisation (FGO) framework that jointly estimates continuous robot poses and discrete terrain labels using only low cost wheel encoder and IMU data. Continuous and discrete variables are modelled as nodes in a single factor graph; maximum-a-posteriori inference is carried out by an alternating optimisation scheme executed inside a fixed-size sliding window, allowing constant-time updates on embedded hardware. The method closes three longstanding gaps: (1) a unified probabilistic formulation for hybrid state estimation, (2) an online solver that scales with mission duration, and (3) automatic construction of a dynamic semantic topological map that captures both spatial layout and label transitions. The resulting graph supports high-level navigation and situational awareness without external infrastructure. We validate the approach in a 2D simulation comprising six terrain regions, random walks of 150 steps, and realistic odometry and classification noise. These results demonstrate that hybrid FGO can endow minimalist robots with robust, semantics-aware mapping capabilities, paving the way for long-duration exploration and cooperative task planning in GPS-denied, sensor-limited environments.

title: An Object Placement Optimization System for Efficient and Unbiased Imitation Learning Data Collection
authors: Hiromasa Yamaguchi, Yuga Yano, Hakaru Tamukoh
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Training data diversity is an important factor in improving the performance of imitation learning. However, object placement diversity and systems that support object placement during data collection have not been sufficiently investigated. In this paper, we analyze the effectiveness of object placement diversity in imitation learning. We evaluate how different placement conditions affect task success using a simulator. Based on the results, we design optimal placement conditions and propose the object placement support system. The proposed system enabled more effective and efficient object placement than human-judged placement, and achieved comparable performance with less data.

title: Recognition Method Based on Time–Frequency Features of Intermittent Sampling Repeater Jamming
authors: Mingjie Li, Jindong Zhang, Xinyuan Zhang, Jiahui Tang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Interrupted-sampling repeater jamming is an intrapulse coherent jamming technique formed by partially sampling, storing, and retransmitting the radar echo, while existing methods usually treat it as a single class with limited parameter coverage, making them inadequate for complex electronic countermeasure scenarios. To handle multiple retransmission modes and diverse sampling periods and widths, this paper proposes a jamming recognition method based on variable-bandwidth joint kernel density estimation (VB-KDE). Time–frequency features are first embedded into a supervised discriminative subspace, where class-conditional joint kernel densities are constructed using class-specific Mahalanobis metrics and locally kNN-based variable bandwidths; the bandwidths are discriminatively calibrated by minimizing the multiclass cross-entropy, yielding well-calibrated posterior probabilities and supporting open-set rejection. Simulation results demonstrate that, under the same data and feature conditions, VB-KDE achieves significantly higher recognition accuracy and robustness for interrupted-sampling repeater jamming than fixed-bandwidth joint kernel density estimation and threshold-based decision trees.

title: A Wide-Frequency Output Control Strategy for Three-Phase Inverters Based on GaN HEMTs*
authors: Guo Ke, Li Hui, Xiang Xuewei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the increasing adoption of wide-bandgap devices such as GaN, three-phase inverters are expected to meet growing demands for wide-frequency output and high efficiency in applications such as electric vehicles and microgrids. However, traditional control strategies often suffer from inadequate voltage regulation and deteriorated power quality under wide-frequency operation, thereby limiting system performance. This paper proposes a wide-frequency output control strategy for three-phase two-level inverters. The proposed method enables flexible output frequency adjustment by tuning the SPWM carrier ratio, employs a dual-loop voltage-current control structure, and introduces a load current feedforward compensation mechanism to enhance voltage regulation and dynamic performance. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through both MATLAB/Simulink simulations and experimental tests on a GaN-based prototype. The system achieves stable operation across the 50 Hz to 400 Hz frequency range, with a voltage THD of less than 0.3% and a voltage regulation rate within 0.39%, demonstrating the practicality and robustness of the method.

title: A Review on Compact Circular Polarized Microstrip Patch Antenna for 6G Application
authors: Ashika C, Abdul Quaiyum Ansari, Md Muzammil Sani
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Microstrip patch antennas (MPAs) are commonly employed in modern wireless systems due to their small size, low cost, and ease of manufacture. With the introduction of sixth-generation (6 G) networks, antennas must provide increased bandwidth, polarization stability, and downsizing to support massive MIMO, IoT, and satellite communications. Circularly polarized MPAs (CP-MPAs) are interesting for such applications since they provide orientation-independent communication and strong signals. This study discusses current improvements in small CP-MPA designs, with an emphasis on bandwidth enhancement, gain improvement, feeding approaches, and size reduction. Comparative studies reveal that SSPP and differential feeding systems can yield more bandwidth and axial ratio than conventional designs. Challenges include efficiency loss during downsizing and the requirement for AI-assisted optimization. Compact CP-MPAs are essential for future 6 G wireless networks.

title:
Secure Supervisory Control of Discrete Event Systems using Homomorphic Encryption
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Ana Clara P. Gonçalves, Patrícia N. Pena, Lucas V. R. Alves
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper addresses the critical challenge of securing Cyber-Physical Systems (CPSs) against passive communication attacks, specifically focusing on systems modeled as Discrete Event Systems (DES). We introduce a methodology, based on the Elliptic Curve ElGamal (EC-ElGamal), to protect the confidentiality of DES-based CPS using homomorphic encryption. Our approach employs the computational intractability of the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP) to fortify supervisory control systems operating over vulnerable communication channels. The work details the EC-ElGamal protocol, from key generation to encryption and decryption, emphasizing its direct adaptation for ensuring robust data confidentiality within distributed DES architectures. A practical example demonstrating the application of this methodology in a DES context is implemented and tested on an ESP32 microcontroller.

title: Optimized Design of Grid-Connected Pre-Synchronization for Virtual Synchronous Generator Based on Adaptive Control
authors: Siming Zhu, Aibei Yu, Feng Wang, Lantian Bi, Kai Peng, Zheng Zhu, Chenhao Li, Ying Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: During the switching between islanded and grid- connected modes in microgrids, excessive inrush currents may damage power electronics. Traditional pre-synchronization strategies are simple but compromise dynamic performance and robustness. As a critical grid-forming control, virtual synchronous generator (VSG) actively supports system frequency and voltage and provides inertia and damping. Thus, this paper proposes a parameter-adaptive VSG pre-synchronization strategy without phase-locked loop (PLL). By building a small-signal model, the impacts of inertia and damping parameters on system dynamic performance and stability are analyzed to guide optimization. MATLAB/Simulink simulations show that the strategy suppresses inrush currents, shortens adjustment time, reduces frequency fluctuations, and enables smooth grid-connected current transition, verifying the strategy’s feasibility.

title: Fake News Detection and Knowledge Verification on Social Media Using Deep Attention Networks
authors: Nallamilli Mounica Laxmi, Prabakaran N
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: With the exponential growth of user-generated content on social media platforms, the spread of fake news has become a critical challenge, undermining public trust and influencing societal behavior. This research focuses on fake news detection and knowledge verification using Deep Attention Networks, which can capture fine-grained semantic relationships and contextual dependencies in textual data. To achieve realistic and comprehensive evaluation, datasets are collected from diverse social media platforms including Twitter, Facebook, and Reddit, encompassing both verified and misleading content. The proposed methodology involves the implementation of a Deep Attentionbased model and its hierarchical comparison with existing state-of-the-art models such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Bi-LSTM, and Transformerbased architectures (e.g., BERT). Each model is evaluated using multiple performance metrics -accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC - under various conditions tailored to different social media dynamics. A detailed analysis is presented through graphical representations such as bar charts, ROC curves, and confusion matrices, offering a visual and quantitative understanding of model effectiveness. The results consistently show that the Deep Attention Network provides superior performance in identifying and verifying fake news, especially in linguistically complex and context-rich scenarios. This research underscores the significance of attention mechanisms in enhancing the reliability and interpretability of fake news detection systems and lays the foundation for more robust content verification strategies on digital platforms.

title: Design of a Communication and Monitoring System for Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks Based on Multimodal Perception and Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yuting Zhao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks (UWSNs) suffer from high latency and multipath effects, rendering traditional monitoring methods incompatible with their dynamic complex environments. Thus, this paper proposes UWSN-Monitor, an intelligent monitoring system integrating multimodal perception and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). The system adopts a three-layer architecture: Underwater Probe Layer enables second-level data collection, Intelligent Analysis Layer uses Gradient Boosting Decision Tree (GBDT) to quantify network degradation, and Visualization Layer supports dynamic topology reconstruction. Sea trials show it reduces interruption detection time by 89.6% (to 32.7 s), improves degradation recognition AUC to 0.92, and boosts link availability to 99.2%. UWSN-Monitor significantly enhances underwater network monitoring accuracy, fault location efficiency and overall stability, offering a robust one-stop solution for dynamic network optimization.

title: Progressive Query Pruning for Object Detection
authors: Zehong Qian, Hua Cheng, Shucheng Mao, Yingying Ding
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: The DETR and its variants achieve end-to-end object detection, but the fixed number of object queries substantially exceeds the actual demand, resulting in redundant or ineffective query focus. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Progressive Query Pruning (PQP) method. Specifically, an adaptive query quality estimator is integrated after each layer of the Transformer decoder to dynamically assess query importance. The estimator jointly evaluates classification confidence, attention concentration, and query distinctiveness to compute an importance score. Based on this score, PQP progressively prunes low-potential and highly redundant queries across layers. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method introduces no additional parameters, ensures high-quality query retention, and enhances both detection accuracy and model robustness.

title: Balancing Efficiency and Accuracy in Image Classification Using CNNs
authors: Hina Gupta, Monu Singh, Reshu Chaudhary, Sarika Keshri
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Image classification is a widely known application of computer vision, that utilizes the feature information provided by images to identify various objects accurately. The existing convolutional neural networks have significantly enhanced classification accuracy while minimizing loss, owing to technological advancements and the increasing demand for a higher quality of life. The work proposed in this paper utilized two datasets: Fashion MNIST, containing ten fashion categories, and Face Mask, containing two categories for masks or no masks. The results unequivocally demonstrate that we can achieve higher accuracy with the scratch model by employing appropriate hyperparameters, cross-entropy functions, and training epochs. Additionally, the findings of this work also show that pooling layers are effective for training large-scale projects with millions of trainable parameters, data augmentation is advantageous for smaller datasets, and although transfer learning is effective, it necessitates more time-consuming training due to the large number of layers and parameters used for training.

title: Noise-Robust Speech-Based Severity Assessment for Emergency Calls
authors: Kanji Okazaki, Keiichi Watanuki
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study aims to automatically classify emergency calls into serious (life-threatening) and minor (non-life-threatening) cases using acoustic features and machine learning models, thereby contributing to automated triage support in emergency response systems. Two enhancement strategies—noise reduction and data augmentation—are investigated to improve robustness in real-world call environments. Accurate triage during emergency calls is critical for optimizing resource allocation and ensuring timely medical response. Building on our previous exploratory analysis of acoustic features, this study advances toward practical deployment by addressing two key challenges: noisy real-world conditions and limited training data. To mitigate background noise and enhance feature stability, Wiener filtering was integrated into the preprocessing pipeline. Data scarcity was addressed through augmentation strategies, including moderate pitch shifting (±2 semitones) as well as comprehensive augmentation with pitch, volume, and noise perturbations. Acoustic features—including fundamental frequency statistics, Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients, and spectral descriptors— were extracted from call recordings provided by the Tokyo Fire Department. Three classifiers (Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, and Random Forest) were trained and evaluated using stratified cross-validation. Performance was primarily assessed by area under the ROC curve (AUC) and recall, given the critical importance of minimizing false negatives in emergency triage. Results showed that noise reduction improved robustness, while full augmentation yielded the greatest gains in predictive accuracy, with Random Forest achieving an AUC of 0.93. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of acoustic-based severity classification in emergency calls and highlight the potential of recall-oriented decision-support systems for emergency dispatchers. Future work will focus on real-time implementation and integration into dispatch operations.

title: Three-dimensional Knee Joint Load Estimation System During Walking Using IMU Sensors for the Prevention of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Fundamental Study
authors: R. Yoshizawa, A. Uehara, Y. Sankai, H. Kawamoto
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Knee osteoarthritis (KOA) is a serious disease affecting one-third of the world's population, and mechanical loading on the knee joint has been identified as a progressive factor. However, conventional knee contact force measurements are limited to laboratory environments, making daily preventive monitoring difficult. This fundamental study aims to establish the optimal deep learning approach for three-dimensional knee contact force estimation using wearable IMU sensors and to validate the technical feasibility for practical KOA prevention systems. Methodologically, four deep learning models were systematically compared: 1D-CNN, LSTM, RNN, and Transformer. These models were trained to map 45-channel IMU time-series data comprising three-axis acceleration, angular velocity, and magnetic field from five anatomical locations to six-dimensional knee contact forces through 7-fold cross-validation. The 1D-CNN achieved the optimal maximum error of 24.01 %, while LSTM demonstrated the most stable average error of 13.93 %. These results showed superior performance compared to existing IMU-based methods, realizing three-dimensional knee joint force estimation within the practical accuracy range of wearable healthcare devices. This fundamental study successfully established the technical feasibility of translating laboratory-based knee joint load assessment to daily environments and presented clear guidelines for developing non-invasive KOA prevention systems.

title: A Simplified Time-domain Model Based Feedforward Control Strategy for LLC Resonant Converters with Wide Operation Range
authors: Yue Yu, Sheng Qu, Junhong Yu, Jinjun Liu, Yuqi Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes an accurate simplified time-domain model based online feedforward control strategy covering all typical working conditions (i.e., PO, OPO, NP, NOP). By expanding inverse trigonometric functions from transcendental equations into polynomials and optimizing the order in which the variables are solved, the simplified equations can be solved quickly and iteratively while much more accurate than current advanced model. Meanwhile, a modified PI control strategy featuring desaturation capability is utilized, preventing integral saturation during transients while ensuring steady-state error compensation. Experiments are carried out to validate the proposed strategy. The results demonstrate that the settling time is 10x faster than the state-of-the-art methods and dramatically increases model accuracy, thus improving the dynamic performance of LLC converters.

title: Ultra-Compact KANSpotter with Noise Robustness for Voice-Controlled Exoskeletons
authors: Nana Ding, Wenjie Chen, Xiantao Sun, Sixuan Hua
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: This study introduces KANSpotter, a lightweight keyword spotting model enabling robust voice control for resource-constrained wearable exoskeletons. Inspired by Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks, the proposed solution employs a novel grouped dynamic convolutional layer that leverages learnable splines to generate sample-specific convolution kernels.
