This design optimally balances high accuracy and exceptional noise robustness with a remarkably compact 10K-parameter architecture. Experimental results demonstrate its superior performance, achieving 95.97% accuracy under 0-15 dB Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and maintaining 83.41% accuracy even at -5 dB SNR, with a robustness factor of 0.866. The model requires only 758.7K FLOPs (5.1% of SegmentedSA11k's 14.913M FLOPs), meeting stringent computational requirements for edge devices like exoskeletons.

title: Brain Tumor Classification using Hybrid Deep-Wavelet Feature Fusion with Probabilistic PCA-based Feature Reduction
authors: Akash Pandey, Suvendu Rup, Sudhakar Pandey
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Classification of brain tumor is considered as one of the most critical and challenging tasks in the field of medical image processing. The accurate classification of brain tumor types is crucial for timely diagnosis and potentially saving lives. The high degree of risk is involved if early symptoms and diagnosis is avoided. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is mostly studied non-invasive method for the examination of brain tumor. The present work proposes an efficient methodology for the classification of brain tumor. The proposed method operated as follows. First, a feature concatenation technique is used during feature extraction, taking into account handcrafted features, deep features utilizing Lifting Wavelet Transform (LWT), and MobileNet, respectively. MobileNet can recognize local hierarchical information, allowing for effective and precise visual identification, whereas the LWT is in charge of extracting discriminative data. After wavelet and deep features have been combined, the Probabilistic Principal Component Analysis (PPCA) method is then used to pick a subset of the concatenated feature set with fewer dimensions. The brain tumor is ultimately classified as normal or abnormal using a KNN classifier with a smaller set of PPCA features. The Br35H dataset is used to confirm the proposed method during the experiment. The suggested method has been found to have an accuracy of 99.17% and 99.97% AUC in the Br35H dataset based on the experimental findings. Moreover, the proposed method has the potential to be quantitatively better than the benchmark schemes.

title: Learning-Based Real-Time Shape and Force Estimation for Continuum Robots Using FBG-Embedded Actuation Cables_supp1-3659518.mp4
authors: Anzhu Gao
publish: N/A
abstract: Continuum robots are widely used in endoluminal intervention and treatment due to their dexterity and compliance. However, it is challenging to achieve simultaneous shape and force estimation of continuum robots under the requirement of robot miniaturization. To address it, this article proposes a learning-based real-time tip force and shape estimation for continuum robots using distributed tensions and proximal displacements of actuation fibers. It only needs the tension-sensing fibers for the actuation and sensing without using additional sensors. First, each utilized actuation cable is an optical fiber with inscribed distributed fiber Bragg grating sensors on it to provide distributed tension sensing. Finally, a neural network-based method is proposed to estimate the external force at the tip using the wavelength shifts of the fiber Bragg grating sensors on the actuating fibers and cable length changes as inputs, also the kinetostatic model is established to reconstruct the robot’s shape in real time. Then, experimental results show that the average static force estimation error is 6.2 mN and the average tip position error from shape estimation is 3 mm. Furthermore, an augmented reality platform was developed to overlay the real-time estimated external forces and robot shape onto two-dimensional video images from an external monocular camera. This work would provide a potential to promote the embodied intelligence of continuum robots with miniaturization and integration.

title: Towards Battery-Free Environmental Monitoring: A High-Efficiency Energy Harvesting System from Low-Intensity RF Signals
authors: Lizhang Chen, Bin Liao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents an environmental monitoring system based on radio frequency (RF) energy harvesting. The system operates on a wide band of 1.8–3.0GHz. It can harvest RF energy at input power levels as low as 0 dBm. At an input power of 10 dBm, the RF-DC power conversion efficiency (PCE) reaches 75%. The system comprises dual wideband rectifier circuits, a BQ25570 power management module, an STM32 microcontroller, an HDC1080 temperature and humidity sensor, and a LoRa communication module. The system achieves system-level integration and demonstrates a complete energy management chain, including harvesting, converting, storing, and ultimately powering low-power devices. This approach provides a low-maintenance, self-sustaining power solution suitable for battery-free IoT nodes and wearable electronics.

title: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: IEEE Standard for Testing Faulted Circuit Indicators
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: Definitions, service conditions, test procedures, and test conditions for faulted circuit indicators for use on power distribution systems are established in this test code.

title: Cavity-Grate Integrated All-Fiber Optic High Temperature Pressure Sensor with Enhanced Contrast and Sensitivity_supp1-3667231.pdf
authors: Zhenyin Hai
publish: N/A
abstract: An all-fiber dual-parameter pressure–temperature sensor with enhanced contrast and sensitivity is proposed, fabricated, and experimentally validated for gas monitoring in high-temperature environments. The sensing structure combines a Fabry–Pérot interferometer (FPI) for pressure detection and a fiber Bragg grating (FBG) for temperature measurement. A collimation-enhancement mechanism, based on the self-focusing effect of the graded-index fiber, is employed to improve signal contrast. The diaphragm-based closed-end FPI cavity is fabricated using precision mechanical cutting and arc discharge techniques. To further enhance the pressure sensitivity, the fiber end-face is treated using mechanical grinding followed by femtosecond laser roughening. The sensor's performance was evaluated at ambient conditions and under extreme conditions of 700 °C and 5 MPa. At 700 °C, the sensor exhibits a temperature sensitivity of 11.61 pm/°C and a pressure sensitivity of 46.96 nm/MPa. The full-temperature drift of the pressure sensitivity is less than 3 nm/MPa, with a maximum full-scale error of 2.95%. Owing to its high mechanical robustness and sensitivity, the proposed sensor is well suited for simultaneous gas temperature and pressure monitoring in harsh, high-temperature environments.

title: A Text-to-3D Framework for Joint Generation of CG-Ready Humans and Compatible Garments
authors: Zhiyao Sun, Yu-Hui Wen, Ho-Jui Fang, Sheng Ye, Matthieu Lin, Tian Lv, Yong-Jin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Creating detailed 3D human avatars with fitted garments traditionally requires specialized expertise and labor-intensive workflows. While recent advances in generative AI have enabled text-to-3D human and clothing synthesis, existing methods fall short in offering accessible, integrated pipelines for generating CG-ready 3D avatars with physically compatible outfits; here we use the term CG-ready for models following a technical aesthetic common in computer graphics (CG) and adopt standard CG polygonal meshes and strands representations (rather than radiance field representations like NeRF and 3DGS) that can be directly integrated into conventional CG pipelines and support downstream tasks such as physical simulation. To bridge this gap, we introduce Tailor, an integrated text-to-3D framework that generates high-fidelity, customizable 3D avatars dressed in simulation-ready garments. Tailor consists of three stages. (1) Semantic Parsing: we employ a large language model to interpret textual descriptions and translate them into parameterized human avatars and semantically matched garment templates. (2) Geometry-Aware Garment Generation: we propose topology-preserving deformation with novel geometric losses to generate body-aligned garments under text control. (3) Consistent Texture Synthesis: we propose a novel multi-view diffusion process optimized for garment texturing, which enforces view consistency, preserves photorealistic details, and optionally supports symmetric texture generation common in garments. Through comprehensive quantitative and qualitative evaluations, we demonstrate that Tailor outperforms state-of-the-art methods in fidelity, usability, and diversity.

title:
A Self-Powered and Temperature-Sensitive Photodetector Based on VO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Films With Reversible Dark Current Polarity
authors: Keyang Zhao, Yixing Pang, Haining Na, Jin Zhu, Wenwu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Self-powered photodetectors with low dark current and high sensitivity are highly desirable for advanced optoelectronic applications. Herein, we report a novel temperature-sensitive photodetector based on a CH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>NH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>PbI<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/VO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> composite film, which integrates the strong visible-light absorption of perovskite and the reversible metal-insulator transition (MIT) of VO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>. Operating under zero bias, the device exhibits excellent self-powered performance, achieving a high responsivity of 538 mAW<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{\text {-1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and a broad spectral response ranging from 450 to800nm. Notably, a unique reversible dark current polarity reversal is observed at temperatures below 306.3 K, where the dark current direction is opposite to that of the photocurrent. Temperature-dependent characterizations reveal enhanced photocurrent dynamics near the phase transition regions of both CH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>NH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>PbI<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> and VO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>. At 300 K, the device attains a detectivity of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.2\times 10^{{12}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> Jones and an on–off ratio of 342. This work proposes a novel strategy leveraging the synergistic effect of dual phase-transition materials, realizes a temperature-tunable self-powered mechanism induced by spin-coating-induced perovskite inhomogeneity, and bridges the gap between phase-transition materials and hybrid perovskite systems, paving the way for multifunctional photodetectors in dual-parameter sensing and adaptive optical systems.

title: How Do Market Signals Drive Carbon Emissions in Indonesia? A Predictive Modeling Perspective
authors: Alvin Muhammad Ainul Yaqin, Auliya Aisyah Putri, Vridayani Anggi Leksono, Noni Oktiana Setiowati, Dewa Gede Satriadharma, Stephanie Adeline Mudiono
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Indonesia’s rapid economic growth has driven higher energy consumption and carbon emissions, particularly in the infrastructure, transportation, and energy sectors. To address climate change, the government has committed to achieving net-zero emissions by 2060, making accurate forecasting of carbon emissions critical for strategic planning. This study develops a predictive modeling framework to estimate Indonesia’s carbon emissions by incorporating market signals such as stock market performance and commodity price dynamics. Implemented using a multivariate long short-term memory (LSTM) architecture, the model captures complex temporal relationships across economic and market variables, achieving accurate emission level forecasts. Analysis of variable importance highlights the most influential drivers, offering actionable insights for policymakers and business leaders seeking to design effective carbon reduction strategies. The findings underscore the potential of market-informed predictive modeling, supported by advanced deep learning techniques, to guide environmental management, policy formulation, and sustainable economic development in Indonesia.

title: Design of a SDRAM-Based Program Running Method for DSP6713
authors: Yuzhao Tian, Bo Xie, Yunhui Wang, Li Zhou
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue of limited on-chip RAM capacity in the TMS320C6713 DSP chip, which cannot meet the requirements of complex digital signal processing applications, by proposing a method for program execution based on SDRAM. By constructing a hardware architecture of DSP+FLASH+SDRAM and designing a two-stage program loading mechanism, the DSP automatically loads a 1KB BootLoader from FLASH into the on-chip RAM upon startup. The BootLoader then relocates partitioned user programs to both the on-chip RAM and SDRAM. At the software level, techniques such as partition mapping via linker scripts, multi-region relocation algorithms, and explicit specification through compiler directives are employed to enable coordinated program execution across heterogeneous memory spaces. Experiments demonstrate that this method effectively expands the storage capacity while ensuring functional correctness and long-term stability. Although the code execution efficiency in SDRAM is reduced, it still meets real-time requirements, providing a low-cost solution for resource-constrained DSP systems.

title: Sustainable Digital Tourism through Social Media: A TAM-Based Study on Food Waste Reduction in Hospitality
authors: Atthaya Zyrolyon, Andika Rizky Setiawan, Ahmad Furqon Tarigan, Tiurida Lily Anita, Yuliana Yuliana, Raden Nana Kusdiana
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study examines the adoption of social media–based food waste reduction programs within the hospitality sector, using Surplus Indonesia as a case study. Drawing on the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), the research investigates the relationships among perceived usefulness (PU), perceived ease of use (PEOU), attitudes (ATT), and actual system use (AU) in influencing public engagement with surplus food applications. Data were collected from active users of Surplus Indonesia who interacted with the app within the past six months. Results show that PEOU significantly shapes both PU and ATT, while ATT plays a mediating role in driving AU. Interestingly, PU does not directly affect ATT, suggesting that social and environmental motivations outweigh purely functional considerations. These findings highlight the role of digital platforms and social media as enablers of sustainable e-commerce and digital tourism practices in hospitality. The study offers practical implications for platform developers and hospitality businesses to enhance user engagement through usability improvements and sustainability-driven communication strategies.

title: A Supercapacitor-Enhanced Grid-Forming SVG with Fault Ride-Through Strategy for Symmetrical Fault
authors: Guangzeng You, Xianglin An, Shuming Zhou, Jinyu Wang, Bobo Zhang, Cuicui Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources into power grids has led to a steady decline in system inertia and a continuous reduction in damping capability. These changes contribute to recurring stability issues such as voltage fluctuations and frequency deviations. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an enhanced static var generator (ESVG) system incorporating supercapacitor-based energy storage. Unlike conventional static var generator (SVG), the proposed ESVG not only performs voltage regulation but also offers rapid inertial support and four-quadrant operation capability, significantly enhancing power system operational stability. This study demonstrates that through properly designed grid-forming control strategies, the ESVG can utilize active power exchange between its DC-link capacitor and the AC grid to emulate the inertial behavior of synchronous generators. This mechanism provides millisecond-level inertial response, effectively suppressing frequency fluctuations. Moreover, during short-duration voltage sags, the ESVG delivers rapid reactive power support, thereby enhancing voltage stability and preventing renewable generation units from tripping due to sudden voltage drops. This functionality also contributes to improved fault ride-through capability. In light of the global energy transition, the ESVG represents a solution with substantial theoretical and practical value, offering key technical support for building future power systems with high penetration of inverter-based renewable resources.

title: Independent Phase Current Control Strategy for Modular Multilevel Matrix Converter Fault Conditions
authors: Xiaokun Yang, Cheng Zhong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes an independent phase current control strategy for the modular multilevel matrix converter (M3C). The proposed scheme achieves accurate phase tracking via a phase-locked loop (PLL) and directly controls the phase currents , effectively limiting short-circuit currents while suppressing capacitor voltage fluctuations. Firstly, an modular multilevel matrix converter fault analysis model is established to elucidate the mechanisms of current exceeding limits and voltage imbalance under asymmetric faults. Subsequently, an independent phase controller is designed, achieving inter-phase decoupling through proportional-resonant (PR) regulators. Finally, a simulation model of a modular multilevel matrix converter is constructed, and simulation results validate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed scheme.

title: A Multi-Source Data Feature Fusion Method Based on FCN and Residual Attention Mechanism for Remaining Life Prediction of Gas Turbine
authors: Donghao Pi, Dali Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Predicting the remaining useful life (RUL) of gas turbines is crucial for industrial system reliability. To tackle the imbalanced, small-sample nature of degradation data and the overfitting of traditional attention mechanisms (AM) under data scarcity, this study proposes a multi-source feature fusion method integrating fully convolutional networks (FCN) and residual attention mechanisms (ResAM). The model alleviates gradient vanishing via skip connections in residual blocks and reduces parameter redundancy through a bottleneck structure, making it suitable for small-sample contexts. The FCN architecture preserves temporal dependencies in time-series data, while the fusion with attention enables adaptive multi-scale feature weighting, improving long-range dependency capture. A gradient accumulation strategy further balances training efficiency and memory constraints, aligning with edge computing requirements. Evaluated on the C-MAPSS dataset, the model achieves higher accuracy than conventional methods. Its lightweight design also shows strong potential for deployment in edge environments, offering an accurate, robust, and computationally efficient solution for RUL prediction of complex mechanical systems.

title: Development of a Compliant Mechanism for Series Elastic Actuators
authors: Amin Farjah, Jalal Taheri Kahnamouei, Mehrdad Moallem
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on the design and performance of a rotational spring mechanism for Series Elastic Actuators (SEAs), fabricated using 3D printing techniques. The spring consists of six symmetrically arranged elastic blades that connect concentric inner and outer rings, enabling bidirectional compliance and large angular deflection. By systematically varying geometric parameters, particularly blade thickness, we investigate the influence on torque-displacement behavior and overall stiffness. A lumped parameter model is developed to analyze the spring mechanics under rotational deformation, and closed-form expressions for torque response are derived. An experimental setup is developed using a DC motor to apply torque to the designed springs and assess the torque-displacement relationship. The results provide key insights into how design parameters affect spring performance and offer practical guidelines for the development of lightweight, customizable compliant elements in next-generation robotic actuator systems.

title: Performance of Multilayer Perceptron Models for Fetal Health Assessment
authors: Yessi Jusman, Olfat Raihan Anggoro Ramadhanni, Julnila Husna Lubis
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Fetal health is a critical factor in pregnancy, directly impacting the safety of both mother and baby. Cardiotocography (CTG) is commonly employed to monitor fetal conditions, but manual interpretation of CTG data can be subjective and prone to misclassification. This study seeks to automatically classify fetal health conditions using a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) trained with two backpropagation algorithms: Levenberg-Marquardt (LM) and Scaled Conjugate Gradient (SCG). The classification was conducted using an MLP architecture with 5 and 10 hidden neurons and evaluated using 10-fold cross-validation. The dataset, obtained from Kaggle, consisted of 2,126 CTG records extracted into 21 medical features, categorized into three classes: Normal, Suspect, and Pathological. The results uncovered that all models generated average accuracies above 90%. The best performance was achieved by the LM algorithm with 10 hidden neurons, which yielded an average training accuracy of 95% and a testing accuracy of 94%, indicating a strong generalization capability. These findings suggest that MLP with the proposed configuration can serve as an accurate and reliable decision support system for fetal health classification.

title: Experimental Verification of Vibration-Based Release for a Sticky-Food Handling Gripper
authors: Daiki Ueda, Dai Kurihara, Takahiro Aruga, Gen Endo
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In food processing sites, many tasks are still performed manually by workers, and automation through robotics is in high demand. However, research on the release motion of grasped objects remains limited, and the tendency of highly adhesive food materials to stick or remain on grippers has become a major obstacle to automation. In this study, multiple food release methods were compared and examined to ensure the reliable release of grasped food items. Based on the conditions of practically implemented combination weighing machines, particular attention was given to the method of vibrating the gripper fingers. we used a crank mechanism to induce vibrations for experiments involving pork slices laid on metal rods (modeled after the fingers of the Tsummori-Hand), while varying the vibration amplitude, frequency, and angle. The results demonstrated that vibrations exceeding a certain amplitude threshold effectively induced detachment of the adhered material. Furthermore, both higher vibration frequencies and larger vibration angles were found to enhance the release efficiency.

title: A Single-Phase Symmetric-Bipolar Buck-Boost AC-AC Converter With Continuous Input-Output Current
authors: Mostafa Karimi Hajiabadi, Ali Mosallanejad, Ahmad Salemnia, Alireza Lahooti Eshkevari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article presents a novel single-phase, nonisolated buck-boost AC-AC converter featuring lower active and passive components compared with the existing designs. The proposed topology offers symmetric in-phase and antiphase buck-boost voltage operations, as well as a step-changed output voltage frequency, making it suitable for a broad range of applications, such as dynamic voltage restorers (DVR). The offered converter features only two magnetic elements, one on the input side and another on the output side, which ensure continuous input and output currents and lower total harmonic distortions (THD) in both input and output currents compared with the existing designs. The proposed AC-AC converter lacks coupled inductors, eliminating the need for RC snubbers on power switches. Additionally, it inherently achieves safe commutation by avoiding bidirectional AC switches and discarding dead- or overlap times from the commutation strategy. Comparative assessments indicate that the proposed topology performs better than its counterpart circuits in terms of switching device power (SDP) index, volumetric indexes of capacitors and magnetic components, efficiency, and input and output THD currents. The experimental results demonstrate an efficiency of 95.6% and a THD of less than 2.5%.

title: Spatial Characteristics and Controlling Factors of Permafrost Deformation in the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau Revealed Through InSAR Measurements_supp1-3664223.docx
authors: Liming Jiang
publish: N/A

title: Data-Driven and Automated AIOps for IP Network Management
authors: Junya Huang, Huahong Zhu, Jiaqi Sun, Zhongmin Zheng
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of IP network scale and the increasing complexity of services, traditional fault management modes relying on manual expertise struggle to meet the requirements for high reliability and efficiency in operations. Based on a deployed IP network fault management system, this paper proposes and constructs an operation and maintenance (O&M) architecture centered on data fusion-driven processes and expert knowledge automation. By integrating multi-source heterogeneous data—including alarms, logs, performance metrics, OAM data, and service perception data—and combining knowledge graphs with automated workflow engines, the system achieves an intelligent, closed-loop process spanning fault perception, localization, analysis, decision-making, recovery, and verification. This paper elaborates on the technical approaches and practical outcomes of the system in areas such as data fusion mechanisms, expert knowledge modeling, fault identification and root cause localization, emergency decision-making, and verification. Multiple detailed real-world case studies demonstrate its processing speed and accuracy. Practice shows that the system reduces fault localization time from hours to minutes, achieves significant alarm ticket suppression, and attains an AI-assisted root cause localization accuracy rate exceeding 90%.

title: Reliability Enhancement of a 2.2 kV β -Ga 2 O 3 Schottky Barrier Diode With Junction Termination Extension Under High-Temperature Storage Stress
authors: Junpeng Wen, Jinyang Liu, Qiuyan Li, Lequan Wang, Minkai Zhang, Song He, Xuanze Zhou, Qin Hu, Guangwei Xu, Shu Yang, Shibing Long
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Beta-gallium oxide (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>) power diodes have the potential in applications at high-temperature and high-voltage environments. The Nickel oxide (NiO)-based junction termination extension (JTE) can prevent the diodes’ premature breakdown, fully leveraging the high <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${E} _{\text {C}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>. However, the reliability of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> Schottky barrier diode (SBD) featuring JTE under high-temperature storage stress (HTSS) warrants attention. In this study, a HTSS test at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$250~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C was conducted on JTE-SBD and the changes in device performance over the storage time were investigated. During the test, the turn-on voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of diodes increased from 0.90 to 1.10 V, while the breakdown voltage (BV) showed a phenomenon of first increasing and then decreasing. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$C$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> measurements shows that the carrier concentration (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${N} _{\text {A}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of NiO initially decreases and subsequently increases, leading to the variations of the peak electric field. To enhance the device reliability, an oxygen annealing process has been proposed. By this approach, the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {on}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of JTE-SBD only increased 0.10 V during the test. Due to the lower and more stable <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${N} _{\text {A}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of NiO, the average BV of the annealed JTE-SBD maintained 2231 V even after 124h HTSS. Finally, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) spectra illustrate the reasons for the changes of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${N} _{\text {A}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of NiO. This study enhances the understanding of the reliability of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> and NiO, providing effective strategy for improving the stability of NiO/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> JTE-SBD.

title: Investigating the Optimal Assistive Strategy for Freezing of Gait: A Comparison of Unilateral and Bilateral Artificial Muscle Assistance
authors: Aoi Yamamoto, Kakeru Yamasaki, Wataru Fujita, Tomohiro Shibata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: As the global population continues to age, the number of individuals with Parkinson’s disease (PD) is projected to increase significantly. Freezing of Gait (FoG), a common motor symptom in PD, affects more than half of patients and becomes more prevalent with disease progression. Suppression of FoG is essential for improving quality of life (QoL), and various assistive strategies have been proposed. This study evaluates the effectiveness of unilateral versus bilateral assistance using the Unplugged Suit for PD Patients (UPS-PD), a wearable gait assistive device powered by pneumatic artificial muscles. While prior UPS-PD studies examined only unilateral assist, bilateral assist has not been comparatively investigated. A slalom walking experiment was conducted as a single-case study with one PD participant under both assist conditions. The results indicated that bilateral assist produced more favorable gait patterns, including reduced gait asymmetry, increased swing duration, lower %DLS, and smaller variability, compared with unilateral assist. These improvements were supported by medium-to-large effect sizes in key parameters. Although generalization is limited due to the single-case design, these findings provide preliminary insights into assistive strategies for FoG suppression. Future studies involving larger and more diverse cohorts are required to validate these results.

title: The Influence of Blockchain Understanding on Cryptocurrency Usage Intentions Among Young People
authors: Kevin Danendra, Fathy Radhia
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: A Study of how blockchain understanding can affect Indonesian youths' cryptocurrency continuance intentions by extending the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) with user interface (UI), technology awareness (TA), security (SE), and trust (T). Survey data based on 409 respondents (ages 17–30) were analyzed using PLS-SEM. The measurement model was valid and reliable. Consumer attitude (CA) acts as the key predictor of continuance intention (CONT). Security strongly builds trust. Trust, in turn, increases perceived ease of use (PEOU) and CA. Technology awareness substantially improves perceived usefulness (PU) and modestly PEOU. PU and PEOU both raise CA. UI does not significantly affect PEOU. The model explains substantial variance across core constructs. Practically, strengthening security and trust and scaling digital literacy are more decisive for cryptocurrency adoption among Indonesian youth than interface refinement, informing developers, service providers, and policymakers.

title: Extreme Programming Implementation for Diabetes Mellitus Early Detection Expert System Using Forward Chaining Method
authors: Nurhayati, Akhiat, Ashar Abilowo, Fransiskus Panca Juniawan, Dwi Yuny Sylfania
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a chronic disease that affects millions of people worldwide and is a major cause of morbidity and mortality. Early detection and intervention are crucial in managing the disease and preventing complications. In this paper, we propose an expert system for the early detection of diabetes mellitus using the forward chaining method, which developed with extreme programming. By combining the knowledge of team research and doctor interna as internal medicine specialist, we agreed that this system will have 17 symptoms. This system will utilize data from patient symptoms to generate a diagnosis quickly and accurately by using the Forward Chaining approach, which has ability in provide a lot of information from small data. In addition, we use extreme programming as software development method in order to develop the system diagnose accurately and efficiently in its early stages. For the functional test, we conducted Blackbox Testing, with the result that all features were 100% working exactly as expected. Moreover, for Confusion Matrix, we get 78,26% accuracy, 84,62% precision, 78,57% recall, 77,78% specificity, and 81,48% F1-score. The final testing is 82,6% for Human Expert Validation Testing.

title: Wide Voltage Range Non-isolated Three-port Converter Based on Multi-voltage Mode Modulation
authors: Xi Yang, Jingzhong Zhou, Chong Gao, Ziping Peng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In renewable energy systems, traditional DC-DC converters face challenges such as bulky size and complex control. This paper introduces a wide voltage range non-isolated three-port DC-DC converter that integrates a photovoltaic (PV) port, a battery port, and a load port. It eliminates the need for electrical isolation devices, requires fewer components, and boasts high integration. By dividing three operational regions, a multi-controller cooperative control strategy based on region recognition is proposed, enabling free power flow among the three ports. Finally, the effectiveness of the topology and control strategy is verified through PSIM simulation software and steady-state experiments.

title: Fully Automated CAD/BIM Modeling of Pipe Structures from Plant Environment 3D Point Cloud
authors: Takashi Imabuchi, Kuniaki Kawabata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper describes a fully automated method for generating 3D pipe models with wall thickness from 3D point cloud data. In decommissioning of Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Station, rapid 3D modeling of plant structures is essential for dose assessment and remote operation planning. In our method, pipe regions are first discriminated, then scanned along three orthogonal axes to extract pipe instances, followed by geometric fitting with wall thickness for use in shielding calculations. Finally, generated models are exported in Computer-Aided Design (CAD) and Building Information Modeling (BIM) formats for using facility management. Our method was validated on a 3D point cloud measured in a mock-up plant environment. We confirmed a high conversion rate and reduced processing time by restricting computation to pipe regions. The output models were imported into existing software without errors.

title: Safety and Comfort-Aware Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework for Critical Scenarios Generation Towards ADS Testing
authors: Asalif Minas Gulent, Tiexin Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Autonomous Driving Systems (ADSs) demands realistic simulation environments to evaluate both safety and passenger comfort. Existing adversarial scenario-generation frameworks focus primarily on safety-critical interactions, often overlooking comfort-related responses of autonomous vehicles. To address this gap, we propose SCAMARL, a Safety and Comfort-Aware Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning framework designed to generate both safety and comfort-critical driving scenarios in the CARLA simulator. SCAMARL introduces a comfort-priority reward that enables adversarial Non-Player Characters (NPCs) to induce realistic discomfort, quantified by jerk and lateral acceleration, while maintaining physically plausible and safety-compliant behaviors. Experimental results across intersection and deformed intersection scenarios demonstrate that SCAMARL effectively balances adversarial aggressiveness and comfort disturbance. Specifically, the framework achieves collision rates comparable to existing baselines CR = 0.26–0.28, while exceeding comfort thresholds reported by ISO 2631-1 and, with jerk levels reaching up to 25.28 m/s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. These findings confirm SCAMARL’s capability to generate realistic, comfort-aware, and safety-relevant adversarial scenarios for autonomous vehicle testing.

title: Research on Two - stage Detection Method Based on Wide - band Signals
authors: Ouyang Xu, Junkai Ma
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of performance degradation of traditional energy detection in non - stationary noise, a two - stage detection method combining a fixed threshold and OS - CFAR is proposed. The first stage uses a fixed threshold to achieve rapid preliminary screening. The second stage conducts accurate identification in the candidate region through OS - CFAR. By integrating the spectrum interpolation technique, the accuracy of bandwidth estimation is improved. Simulations show that within the signal - to - noise ratio range of - 5 to 15 dB, this method can effectively balance the detection probability and false alarm probability, improve the accuracy of parameter estimation, and is applicable to wide - band signal detection.

title: Photonic Crystal Sensors in Microfluidics for Realtime Chemical Reaction Monitoring
authors: Vinoth M, Chandrasekaran T, Thirumoolan D, Arunkumar A, Santhanalakshmi K
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Real-time monitoring of chemical reactions to understand the reaction speed, improve the reaction conditions, and ensure the quality of the chemical products. In this work, we propose a new technique based on a photonic crystal sensor integrated with a microfluidic device to realize chemical detection at high sensitivity and real-time analysis. Photonic crystal sensors have been developed that are sensitive to changes in molecular structure that occur during a reaction. These sensors are made with specific photonic band gap properties. A microfluidic structure can be integrated into a measurement or analytic platform for efficient handling of liquids and integrated operation on demand in a small and portable platform. By looking closely at the sensors, we show sensitivity, specificity, and stability that tell us that the sensors can be used in various settings. We develop algorithms and software tools for real-time data processing and analysis to better understand sensor signals' physical meaning and correlate them with reaction kinetics. This study improves chemical sensing and reaction monitoring technologies by providing novel solutions to increase process efficiency and product quality in a wide range of chemical applications.

title: Soil Quality Analysis and Yield Prediction Using Remote Sensing and Machine Learning
authors: Dheerendra Pratap Singh, Kamlesh Mani
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Wheat yield prediction is crucial for ensuring food security in India, where Uttar Pradesh contributes significantly to national production. This paper introduces a machine learning-based framework that combines in-situ soil quality indicators with Sentinel-2A vegetation indices for forecasting wheat yield at the field level. A synthetic dataset comprising 85 farms, representing authentic agronomic conditions in the Indo-Gangetic plains, was created utilising soil nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), potassium (K), pH, electrical conductivity (EC), and organic carbon, in addition to vegetation indices such as NDVI, SAVI, and EVI. We trained and tested three supervised models—Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Gradient Boosting (GB)—using 10-fold cross-validation. RF had the best results ($\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.87$, $\mathrm{RMSE}=2.14 \mathrm{q} / \mathrm{ha}, \mathrm{MAE}=1.73 \mathrm{q} / \mathrm{ha}$), beating both kernel-based and boosting methods. Feature importance analysis revealed NDVI, organic carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, and SAVI as the most significant predictors, consistent with established agronomic principles. The study shows that ensemble tree-based models are better at capturing nonlinear interactions between soil and crops. It also shows how combining soil and remote sensing data can lead to accurate, understandable, and spatially explicit yield forecasts. This framework can help with precision farming, early warning systems, and planning for food security in a region. It also makes it possible to use multi-modal Earth Observation and cloud platforms in real time.

title: Advances and Open Challenges in Small Object Detection for UAV Imagery: A Deep Learning Perspective
authors: Saba Manzoor, Faiyaz Ahmad, Musheer Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Small object detection in unmanned aerial vehicle imagery has emerged as a critical area of computer vision research, driven by its applications in surveillance, disaster response, environmental monitoring, and defense. This study reviews progress in the field from recent literature 2010 to 2025, emphasizing deep learning-based approaches that have significantly improved detection accuracy and efficiency. Despite these advances, persistent challenges remain, including the loss of fine-grained features at low resolutions, dataset imbalance, domain variability, and the trade-off between real-time processing and accuracy. The paper also highlights the absence of standardized benchmarks, which hinders fair comparison across methods. To address these gaps, future research directions are explored, such as multimodal sensing with thermal-RGB fusion, the use of neural architecture search for UAV-specific models, edge-cloud collaboration frameworks for low-latency inference, and self-supervised learning to reduce annotation demands. Ethical considerations, particularly privacy in populated areas, are also discussed. By consolidating current findings and outlining emerging pathways, this work underscores the importance of collaborative, interdisciplinary efforts in advancing UAV-based small object detection toward reliable, ethical, and real-world deployment.

title: A Single-Stage Isolated Modular Multilevel Resonant DC-DC Converter for Wide-Range High Step-Down Applications
authors: Ke Yang, Yipeng Su, Jing Sheng, Nihan Tong, Chushan Li, Wuhua Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a single-stage isolated modular multilevel resonant DC-DC converter (MMRDC) without submodules (SMs) controllers for high-step-down application scenarios. Firstly, the proposed converter makes use of the phase-shift control degrees of freedom of modular multilevel converter (MMC) to achieve wide-range voltage regulation at fixed frequency. Secondly, by introducing the concept of circulating modulation, the proposed converter can realize self-balancing modulation of module voltages without voltage measurement, communication or sorting algorithm. Owing to its inherent voltage balancing of SM capacitors, there is no requirement for the proposed converter to use local sub-control. Since the converter allows for wide-range voltage regulation at a fixed frequency, it reduces the design complexity of the transformer and minimizes excitation current losses. Finaly, the simulation results under the voltage ratio of 420V6V and the experimental results under 60V-6V validate the effectiveness of the proposed modulation and control of the MMRDC converter.

title: SiC MOSFET Bidirectional Crosstalk Suppression Method Based on low Impedance Branch and Negative Voltage Modulation
authors: Dahan Deng, Helong Li, Shuang Zhao, Zhiqing Yang, Lijian Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Considering the high switching speed characteristics of silicon carbide power devices and the fact that parasitic parameters cannot be ignored, the crosstalk phenomenon of SiC MOSFET power modules under traditional driving methods is more significant than that of silicon-based modules. Existing crosstalk suppression schemes generally have the three contradictions of increased switching delay, increased switching loss and increased control complexity, which makes it difficult to effectively balance the synergistic relationship between positive and negative bidirectional crosstalk spike suppression and dynamic characteristic optimization. This paper proposes a bidirectional crosstalk suppression gate drive based on a low-impedance loop combined with a multi-level turn-off gate voltage. By establishing a time-domain analytical model of the crosstalk generation mechanism, the physical limitations of the active gate control strategy in negative crosstalk suppression are revealed. A dynamic gate voltage reconstruction mechanism is introduced, and the turn-off gate voltage is adaptively adjusted through a self-driven auxiliary network to achieve effective suppression of negative crosstalk. Experimental verification shows that the negative crosstalk peak is reduced by 2.33V, which can achieve a 36% reduction. Secondly, considering the coupling effect of the positive spike by the auxiliary network, the low-impedance path formed by the transistor auxiliary current branch is used to achieve decoupling, which can limit the positive crosstalk voltage to a safe threshold range. The proposed driving method directly uses the gate-source intrinsic voltage as the control signal and can be implemented without additional isolation circuits. The experiment is based on PSpice simulation and double pulse test of 1.2KV/80-A silicon carbide module to compare and evaluate the crosstalk suppression capability of the driving method.

title: Secgenai: A Security-Enhanced Generative AI Framework for Healthcare Chatbots
authors: Rajkumar K, Devikanniga Devarajan, Monica Ravishankar
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: In recent years, generative artificial intelligence (AI) models have demonstrated exceptional capabilities in producing human-like responses in various domains, including healthcare, and are increasingly being adopted in mission-critical systems, necessitating integration of security resilient architecture [1]. However, the adoption of these models in sensitive medical settings raises significant concerns about data privacy, security, and explainability. This paper presents SecGenAI, a securityintegrated generative AI model designed for privacy-preserving and explainable health chatbot interactions. SecGenAI integrates fine-tuned Generative Pre-trained Transformer 2 (GPT2)/Biomedical GPT (BioGPT) with 1) Advanced Encryption Standard (AES)256 encryption (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 3 ~ m s}$</tex> latency), 2) Opacuspowered differential privacy, and 3) clinician-validated explainability (78 % interpretability score). Experiment shows 6% attack success (vs. 41 % baseline) while maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.2 / 5$</tex> medical accuracy. The proposed architecture ensures encrypted communication, secure model training, and transparent response generation, making it suitable for deployment in real-world clinical environments. Evaluation metrics and experimental results validate the effectiveness of our approach in maintaining both data integrity and conversational quality.

title: A Novel Correction Strategy for High-Precision Testing of Power Devices Based on Real-Time Delay and Offset Compensation
authors: Xinyu Zou, Jin Zhang, Jing Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: SiC MOSFETs are widely used in high-frequency and high-power applications. However, their high switching speed means that measurement errors from testing systems can greatly affect dynamic performance characterization. For example, since switching loss is determined by integrating voltage and current waveforms, any delay or offset from the testing equipment between these signals can cause significant inaccuracies. To address the high error rates and low efficiency in the dynamic testing of silicon carbide (SiC) power semiconductor devices, this study designed an automated high-precision testing platform for SiC MOSFET power module switching characterization. A low-inductance loop was designed using the stacked-layer cancellation principle. Additionally, a simple algorithm was proposed to calculate and correct oscilloscope probe channel time delay and amplitude offset errors, enabling real-time calculation and simultaneous correction or compensation of delay and offset values. This facilitates high-precision measurement of SiC MOSFET dynamic characteristics.

title: SII 2026 Commentary
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)

title: AdSig-Detector: Adversarial Signal Detection Based on Wavelet and Variational Mode Decomposition
authors: Zhitao Ke, Haibin Zheng, Jingjing Jin, Baiyang Ji, Xuhua Pan, Jian Jin
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: To address the increasing threat of adversarial samples to deep learning models, this paper proposes two adversarial detection methods based on Wavelet Reconstruction (WR) and Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD). Wavelet-based signal denoising has been widely used for non-stationary electromagnetic signals due to its superior time-frequency localization capability. The WR-based method denoises electromagnetic signals via wavelet decomposition and detects adversarial samples using prediction confidence from a deep neural network, while the VMD-based method enhances robustness by isolating low-noise signal components. Experimental results show that both methods outperform conventional approaches, with the WR-based method exhibiting superior robustness by stabilizing prediction confidence after reconstruction.

title: ML-Driven Adaptive Resource Allocation for High-Performance Ultra-Dense WDM-Based Hybrid FSO/FTTx Networks Under Diverse Weather Conditions
authors: Ashenafi Paulos Forsido, Demissie Jobir Gelmecha, Ram Sewak Singh, Divya Sharma, Huma khan, Shubham Prasad
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Ultra-dense wavelength division multiplexing (UDWDM) free-space optical (FSO)-fiber-to-the-x (FTTx) hybrid networks were investigated as a critical enabler for nextgeneration <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 G / 6 G$</tex> backhaul systems. However, dynamic atmospheric turbulence and weather-induced impairments were identified as significant challenges. To solve these issues, a machine learning (ML)-based system that employs the eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithm for real-time cross-layer prediction of optical signal-to-noise ratio (OSNR), quality factor (QF), and bit error rate (BER) under different weather conditions was developed. Four modulation formats-on-off keying non-return-to-zero (OOK-NRZ), quadrature phase-shift keying (QPSK), polarization-division multiplexed QPSK (PDM-QPSK), and polarization shift keying (PolSK)-across input power levels (20 to 5 dBm), FSO link distances (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-12.5 \text{km}$</tex>), and weather conditions (clear, fog, haze, and rain) were created using numerical simulations. While BER <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}=0.6980\right.$</tex>, MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.56 \times 10^{-} { }^{1}$</tex>) had intermediate prediction accuracy, OSNR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2} \geqslant 0.99\right., R M S E=0.0238)$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q F\left(R^{2} \geqslant 0.99\right., \left.M A E=0.0039\right)$</tex> had good prediction accuracy. The final predictive node used a greedy approach that prioritizes reduced power consumption and higher modulation orders to dynamically find optimal configurations. This allows for dependable high-speed communication by repeatedly adjusting to the conditions of the channel. Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) analysis revealed that the main factors influencing performance were link length and weather intensity. The proposed model shown by statistical testing (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<$</tex> 0.05), indicates how the model is reliable. Thus, the theoretical machine learning models and real-world FSO network optimization were bridged by using the proposed study, which offers a scalable implementation technique.

title: Optimization for Collection System of Offshore Wind Farm Based on Particle Swarm Optimization With Simulated Annealing Strategy
authors: Jianwu Wang, Jinchuan Guo, Yang Huang, Weihan Hao, Yiping Lu, Zehaon Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In order to reduce the average power generation cost of offshore wind farm, it is necessary to optimize the location of substation and cable topology in the collection system, which is a typical combinatorial optimization problem. At present, the meta-heuristic algorithms represented by particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm have been proved to be effective in solving this problem. However, due to the coupling of the internal design variables and the need to consider a variety of constraints, there are generally a large number of local optimal solutions, which makes the conventional algorithms often fail to obtain satisfactory results. In order to improve the global optimization ability of the conventional algorithms, the simulated annealing (SA) strategy is introduced to the PSO algorithm, and several objective functions including cable purchase cost, cable laying cost and power loss cost are considered. Through the study case analysis, it is proved that the proposed method can effectively solve the collection system optimization problem of offshore wind farm, and is better than some traditional methods.

title: Neuromorphic Image Processing for Enhancing Simulated Prosthetic Vision
authors: Binitha Sara Mathew, K.M. Subhash, P.N. Pournami
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Simulated Prosthetic Vision (SPV) is used to assess the performance of visual prostheses in a controlled environment prior to clinical evaluation. Conventional image processing methods often miss biologically realistic visual details, reducing perceived image quality in prosthetic vision. This study presents a neuromorphic image processing approach that improves SPV by imitating how the human visual system processes information. By integrating simulated event-based vision with a Spiking Neural Network (SNN), the proposed system processes dynamic visual scenes efficiently while preserving important structural details, even at the low resolutions typical of prosthetic vision. The simulations show better edge preservation, lower energy use, and reduced latency compared to conventional processing methods. These improvements indicate that the approach could support the development of more effective future visual prostheses that combine computational neuroscience principles with practical biomedical applications.

title: Lung Cancer Detection and Classification using Deep Learning Methods: A Comparative Study
authors: Sivakumar R
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Globally, lung cancer accounts for a significant portion of cancer-related deaths, and enhancing patient outcomes and survival rates requires early identification. Artificial intelligence (AI) developments, particularly deep learning (DL), have demonstrated great promise in enhancing medical image processing, providing increased precision and effectiveness in the diagnosis of lung cancer. This work does a comparative examination of various DL models for the detection and classification of lung cancer utilizing medical imaging modalities, including chest X-rays. Models such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), VGGNet, ResNet, DenseNet, and MobileNet were implemented and assessed for their performance in accurately identifying benign and malignant lung nodules. The research employed preprocessing methods and data augmentation strategies to enhance the model's robustness and generalization capabilities. The comparative analysis was conducted using various metrics. Results indicate that MobileNet deeper architectures with transfer learning significantly outperform other CNNs in both accuracy and F1 score. The study offers a pathway toward reliable, automated lung cancer screening systems, potentially aiding radiologists in clinical decision-making and improving early diagnosis outcomes.

title: Design and Implementation of a Lightweight Platform Independent Ring Oscillator PUF for IoT Applications
authors: Surajit Dutta, Bibhash Sen, Toshiyapang Ozk, Mahabub Hasan Mahalat
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) accelerate the expansion of the Internet of Things by enabling seamless device integration, energy efficiency, low latency, and high determinism, thanks to their reconfigurability feature. However, FPGAs must be protected against various security threats to guarantee intended operation. Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs), which utilize a device’s intrinsic physical properties, have become a robust security primitive that overcomes the limitations of conventional security solutions. This work proposes a lightweight, platform-independent Ring Oscillator (RO) PUF that adapts the cross-platform RO PUF (CPRO PUF) technique. Its main goal is to minimize the hardware footprint and improve resource efficiency by optimizing the use of various logic components. The proposed PUF has been implemented and validated on Xilinx Artix-7 (28 nm) and Spartan-6 (45 nm) FPGAs. In comparison with earlier studies, the obtained results show improvements in resource utilization, reliability, uniqueness, and uniformity, while ensuring the intended security.

title: Thin-Film Thermal Discoloration Sensor for Distributed Material Identification
authors: Hirokazu Iwane, Yukiko Osawa
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper proposes a thin-film sensor that visualizes heat transfer at contact surfaces through color changes, using thermochromic pigments specifically designed for material identification. By capturing the color change as time-series image data, the spatial distribution of heat transfer across the contact surface can be obtained, enabling differentiation of contact materials. The sensor provides a visual representation of heat flow across the interface between the sensor and the object. Experimental results demonstrate the potential of the proposed sensor for material identification and spatial heat transfer analysis.

title: Design Concept and Applications of Inflatable Robots: Single and Dual-Arm with Internal Drop-Stitch Structure and Rigid Joints
authors: Gangadhara Naga Sai Gubbala, Hibiki Nakagawa, Hiroki Uchida, Masato Nagashima, Hiroki Mori, Young Ah Seong, Hiroki Sato, Ryuma Niiyama, Yuki Suga, Tetsuya Ogata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In this research, we focus on the design and applications of a 12DoF(degrees of freedom) dual-arm inflatable robot. The design utilizes a combination of soft bodies, such as inflatable links, and hard joints as servo actuators. This combination enables us to retain the passive safety and compliance of soft robots while leveraging the control accuracy of hard joints. First, we built a 6DoF inflatable arm with a gripper (1DoF). Then, we replicated the single arm to construct a 12DoF dual-arm robot. We then examine the feasibility of this robot for several contact-based tasks, like hammering a nail, pick and place of a handkerchief, and dressing assistance. For our evaluation, we report successful trials and servo faults observed during experiments, as verified by video recordings. With the tasks shown, we confirm the robot’s inherent compliance and showcase the qualitative report for the feasibility of contact-rich tasks based on a small number of trials.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Model to Enhance Intrusion Detection Systems in PySpark Big Data Framework
authors: Aditya Bhardwaj, Ananya Tiwari, Madhavan Deepak Panicker, Umesh Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: As cyber threats escalate in complexity and frequency, maintaining robust cybersecurity mechanisms is increasingly critical. Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) serve as a vital defense by monitoring and identifying malicious activity in network traffic. However, traditional detection techniques often struggle with the volume, velocity, and variability of modern network data. This study proposes a hybrid NIDS model implemented within the Apache Spark (PySpark) big data framework, combining the strengths of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for spatial feature extraction, Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTM) for temporal behaviour modelling, and Deep Neural Networks (DNN) for complex classification. The model was trained and evaluated using the NSL-KDD dataset-a benchmark intrusion detection dataset. The proposed hybrid model demonstrated superior performance over standalone deep learning architectures, achieving an accuracy of 98.21 %, a precision of 98.15 %, a recall of 98.03 %, and an F 1 score of 98.20 %. It also attained the lowest observed loss value of 7.72, indicating strong model convergence and optimization. Integrating multiple deep learning models within a scalable PySpark environment enhances the system's ability to detect a wide range of cyberattacks effectively. This research underscores the value of combining deep learning with big data frameworks to develop high-performance, scalable intrusion detection systems for modern cybersecurity environments.

title: Blockchain Based Peer-to-Peer Power Trading: An Analysis in India
authors: Talib Hasan, Abrar Ahmad, Shah Faisal, Fahad Iqbal, Syed Abbas Ali, Shameem Ahmad, Dilawar Husain
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Bypassing middlemen, P2P energy trading allows users to conduct direct energy transactions. Customers can now produce and sell excess energy thanks to its use of digital platforms and smart contracts, which enable transparent and effective energy exchange. Increased energy efficiency, lower costs, improved grid resilience, and encouragement of renewable energy production are just a few benefits of peer-to-peer energy trading. It encourages energy democratization by enabling people and groups to have an active role in the energy market.P2P energy trading presents a number of difficulties, according to the analysis, including complicated market structure, legal obstacles, technology demands, worries about data security and privacy, and the incorporation of intermittent renewable energy sources. The analysis looks at the state of P2P energy trading with a particular focus on India. It examines India's current pilot programs, market dynamics, and regulatory environment. The analysis also analyzes the unique potential and difficulties encountered in the Indian energy sector, as well as the steps the Indian government has made to encourage P2P trading. The prospects and potential for P2P energy trading in India are covered in the abstract's conclusion. It highlights how crucial infrastructure development, supportive policies, and cooperation among P2P energy traders are.

title: NAIS: A Modular ROS 2 Framework for Real-Time Scene Graph Construction and Language-Guided Navigation
authors: Jose M. Flores Gonzalez, Enrique Coronado, Natsuki Yamanobe
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Indoor robot navigation in unfamiliar environments requires accurate mapping, contextual understanding, and semantic reasoning to interpret user intent. While ROS 2 navigation frameworks provide strong 3D SLAM capabilities, they lack an integrated, modular approach that unifies perception, semantic representation, and high-level reasoning in a single, real-time system. We present Navigation AI Impulse by Scene Graph (NAIS), the first ROS 2 framework to continuously fuse SLAM, dynamic open-vocabulary scene graph construction, and foundation-model reasoning into a deployable and modular pipeline for mobile robots. NAIS operates online, using VLMs to describe objects and LLMs to infer spatial relations and resolve ambiguous natural language requests. This unified design links environment understanding directly to navigation planning, enabling context-aware goal selection without manual configuration or static class lists. Demonstrations on a TurtleBot 4 in office environments show NAIS handling high-level, context-dependent commands such as "I’m thirsty" or "I’m tired", illustrating its potential for robust, adaptable indoor navigation.

title: A Data-Driven Learning-from-Demonstration Framework for Robotic Grasping
authors: Lars Pedersen, Erik D. Lindby, Jeppe Langaa, Leon Bodenhagen, Aljaz Kramberger
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Autonomous grasp planning remains a key challenge in robotic manipulation, particularly in unstructured environments where object types, poses, and arrangements vary. This work presents a data-driven grasp planning method for a robotic manipulator tasked with clearing a table containing diverse objects. The method encodes human-demonstrated grasping strategies by representing Cartesian trajectories with Dynamic Movement Primitives (DMPs), whose parameters are predicted by a neural network from grasp-specific inputs. A second neural network estimates feasible grasp poses based on the object pose estimate data, which is used as the new goal parameter for the grasp trajectory generation.To reduce demonstration effort, synthetic datasets are generated via data augmentation of the recorded trajectories. The approach is implemented on a real system and evaluated on four objects with varying shapes and sizes. The experiments show a high success rate for grasping as well as the ability to incorporate new objects into the system through minimal additional effort.

title: Soft Switching in the Resonant Buck Converter Systems
authors: Tapas Halder
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In this paper, soft switching in the resonant buck converter is recommended for both zero current switching and zero voltage switching to enhance performance improvements with reduced stresses across the main switch. Two additional passive components are highly required in association with the main switch to eliminate the parasitic effects and mitigation of major switching power losses as activities and functionalities of the resonant soft switching of the projected resonant buck SMPS systems though controller complicacy is not the lump of critical concern due to single switch and single stage power transfer operations as objective of the paper

title: A Comparative Study on Segmentation Techniques for Context-Aware Safe Landing of UAVs
authors: Miguel S. Soriano-Garcia, Julio De La Torre-Vanegas, Diego Mercado-Ravell, Israel Becerra
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: As the use of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in various tasks within human-inhabited environments becomes increasingly common, critical aspects such as emergency landing need to be addressed. The use of deep learning has become widely adopted to provide context-sensitive solutions, where semantic segmentation has shown promising results. Therefore, this paper presents a comparative study that aims at evaluating several candidate semantic segmentation algorithms, offering a guide for an appropriate selection of the segmentation module in a UAV safe landing task in unstructured urban environments. More specifically, a comparison was made between three prominent segmentation models, U-Net, SegFormer, and MANet, using the Semantic Drone Dataset. First, the models were evaluated using the original 24 classes of the dataset. SegFormer performed slightly better than the other algorithms tested. In a second, more critical experiment, the classes were grouped into six risk levels based on the Specific Operations Risk Assessment (SORA) framework. To address class imbalance and prioritize high-risk categories, a weighted Cross-Entropy loss was employed, assigning higher penalties to misclassifications in critical risk levels. In this setup, MANet achieved the best results, showing its ability to adapt to risk-based classification and capture important features. Later, the three models were optimized to prove that even complex architectures can be enhanced for real-time inference. The optimization included converting the model to TensorRT format and using FP16 precision, which reduced the models’ size by at least 30%. Finally, the optimized U-Net, the largest model, was tested on a NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano platform achieving real-time inference. This shows that heavy models can run on embedded hardware like the Jetson Orin Nano, making them viable for safe, autonomous UAV applications.

title: Design of a Wireless Communication-Based Intelligent Wearable System for Mining Applications
authors: Jian Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To address the challenges of the heavy physical burden imposed on underground personnel by traditional wearable equipment and its limited intelligence, a mining-oriented intelligent wearable system has been developed. This system facilitates real-time monitoring of multi-source data, encompassing ambient environmental parameters, personnel physiological signs, operational audio and video feeds, and individual locations. Centered on human factors, it establishes a safety early-warning and emergency response regulation mechanism for underground operations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system exhibits high reliability in multi-source data online monitoring, emergency dispatch, and wireless communication. Consequently, it significantly alleviates the workload of personnel and enhances overall safety management and control capabilities in underground mines.

title: Research on the Positioning of Flanges Based on Mapping Projection Method
authors: Chao Yang, Gang Tian, Yongqiang Luo, Xiangpeng Cui, Hao Chen, Renjie Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Due to the noise in the original image, it is difficult to accurately extract the flange edge. Therefore, in order to improve the accuracy and efficiency of extracting the flange edge, this paper innovatively proposes a flange edge extraction method based on the mapping projection method. By combining the EPNP algorithm with the bolt hole positioning to calculate the pose R and T of the spatial flange in the camera coordinate system, that is, the pose of the projection plane, then the flange edge in the image coordinate system is inversely mapped to the projection plane, and the flange edge in the projection plane is optimized based on the RANSAC radius constraint to obtain the flange edge points after noise removal. Finally, the obtained Faran edge is mapped to the image coordinate system. This method can precisely extract the flange edge, improving the accuracy and efficiency of flange edge detection.

title: Design of Active Simulation Equipment for Radar Targets Based on Neural Network
authors: Chenghao Liu, Kai Huo, Xinyu Zhang, Mingxi Di
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: In recent years, the use of active devices to simulate the electromagnetic scattering characteristics of targets has become a direction continuously explored by technical personnel. With the rise of digital models, electromagnetic scattering characteristic models can provide more refined data, laying a foundation for neural networks to perform feature extraction. In this paper, neural networks are used to extract features from the electromagnetic scattering characteristics of typical targets, and active devices are employed to transmit radar echo signals. This enables the simulation of the electromagnetic scattering characteristics of radar targets, thereby achieving the effect of simulating targets using active devices. Compared with the traditional simulation using passive corner reflectors, this method further improves the similarity of the simulation.

title: GaN-Based High Efficiency Flying Capacitor 6-level Bidirectional AC-DC Converter
authors: Longjie Lv, Jiqing Dong, Chengyu Huang, Xinke Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: High efficiency and compact bidirectional AC-DC converters are increasingly critical for renewable energy utilization and electric vehicle charging infrastructure.
