Flying capacitor multilevel topology (FCML) totem-pole converters have great potential advantages in high efficiency applications where the size and weight are limited. This paper presents a design scheme of a 3kW bidirectional multilevel flying capacitor AC-DC converter. Based on the proposed scheme and given the device efficiency limit, the optimal frequency and flying capacitor cell count can be easily determined. Following this method, a 3kW prototype was constructed. achieving peak efficiencies of 99.4% in inverter mode and 99.38% in power factor correction (PFC) mode and a power density of 810W/in3.

title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Transfer Learning for Secure Cross-Domain Knowledge Sharing in Healthcare
authors: Murali Ponaganti, P Rohini
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The rate of expansion of medical data of every field, such as electronic health records, medical imaging, and wearable sensors has brought extensive scope of opportunities to help enhance diagnostics and treatment yet, have raised arising vital challenges of privacy, security, and interoperability. The proposal of this work suggests a Federated and with the Protection of Privacy. Transfer Learning (PPFTL) System of knowledge transfer across domains in healthcare which combines advanced cryptographic and deep learning algorithms. The framework uses Snowflake-based Miller Digital Rabin Signature Algorithm (MDRSA) to verify identity of a client and to manage keys effectively and Differential Viennet Privacy (DVP) as protection against model inversion attacks on sensitive patient data. To minimize data imbalance, overfitting, synthetic data generation through SMOTE and GANs is implemented and a new Jacobian Bidirectional ExpExpish GRU (JBiEEGRU) is created to improve prediction accuracy and efficiency. Moreover, Three-Hump SHapley Camel Additive Explanations (THSHCAP) guarantee the clarity of the model and confidence in the making of clinical decisions. The suggested system is tested on the dataset of coronary artery disease, kidney disease, and a brain stroke, and the performance is assessed with the measures of security, privacy, classification, clustering, and explainability. The strategy will allow delivering safe, precise and understandable AI-mediated healthcare-related solutions and guarantee the maintenance of patient confidentiality to the letter.

title: Exploring Power Electronic Interfaces for Minimizing Torque Ripple in SRM Systems - A Review
authors: Abrar Ahmad, Mohd Usman, Muzakkir Ali Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Switched Reluctance Motors (SRMs) offer an attractive solution for electric vehicle (EV) propulsion due to their simple structure, cost-effectiveness, and high-temperature tolerance. Nevertheless, high torque ripple, which causes vibrations, drivetrain stress, and acoustic noise, limits their widespread use. The power electronic converter topologies created especially to reduce torque ripple in SRM drives for EV applications are thoroughly reviewed in this paper. We examine the topology, switching strategy, control complexity, and ripple suppression capability of several converter classes, including front-end DC-DC, hard switching, soft switching, and modified dump-based configurations. While resonant and ZVS converters offer lower switching losses in bidirectional operation, topologies like ZETA, Passive Boost, and Asymmetric Half-Bridge converters perform better in torque smoothing and energy efficiency, according to comparative benchmarking. The review provides a consolidated reference for researchers and practitioners aiming to enhance SRM performance through optimal converter selection and control integration.

title: Research on Time Slot Allocation in Wireless Body Area Networks Related to Delay
authors: Zhaomiao Gao, Wei Zhao, Siyu Yao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: In the practical application of wireless body area networks, two time slot allocation strategies are proposed to reduce data extension time and packet transmission delay rate. We introduces a timeout threshold and compares the completion time of superframe transmission with the timeout threshold, prioritizing the most likely delayed data packets to minimize the number of delays or the shortest delay time. Transmitting packets without delay in the order of their timeout threshold does not decrease provides greater security space for the transmission of these packets. The simulation results show that the strategy proposed in this paper has certain advantages in network throughput and packet arrival rate.

title: Trajectory Tracking Control of Industrial Robotic Manipulators Based on FOPID with Improved BAS-PSO Algorithm
authors: Zhou Jiang, Cheng Deng, Wenjie Chen, Yabo Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: With continuous advancements in science and technology, robotic manipulators have been widely applied across various human industrial operations. Trajectory tracking accuracy as a critical performance metric for robotic manipulators, has been given increasingly high requirements. In this paper, fractional-order (FO) calculus is incorporated into the PID controller, and a fractional-order PID (FOPID) control method is proposed to optimize the manipulator system, aiming to enhance both control accuracy and convergence speed. Furthermore, an improved beetle antennae search particle swarm optimization (IBAS-PSO) algorithm is introduced to improve the optimization performance of the controller. The feasibility of the proposed FOPID control method optimized via the IBAS-PSO is validated through simulations conducted in the MATLAB/SIMULINK environment.

title: BigCounter: A Bidirectional-guided Network with Scene-Semantics Driven Fusion for RGB-Thermal Crowd Counting
authors: Xiaomin Fan, Feng Shao, Baoyang Mu, Xiongli Chai, Zhongjie Zhu, Zhiyi Mo
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Accurate crowd counting has become increasingly essential for public safety management and Internet of Video Things (IoVT) applications, driven by rapid population growth and urbanization. However, RGB-Thermal (RGB-T) crowd counting remains challenging due to poor recognition of small targets and degraded performance under extreme conditions such as low-light environments. To address these issues, we propose a bidirectional-guided network with scene-semantics driven fusion for RGB-T crowd counting (BigCounter) that enhances robustness and generalization in complex scenes. BigCounter comprises of three parallel branches: a primary branch, a dynamic illumination auxiliary enhancement branch (DIAEB), and a high-resolution auxiliary enhancement branch (HAEB), which respectively improve robustness under illumination variations and accuracy for small target detection. Moreover, a cross-layer scene-driven fusion module (CLSFM) and a cross-modal semantic-driven fusion module (CMSFM) are designed to strengthen structural consistency and explore semantic complementarity between modalities. Through multi-branch collaboration and semantic-aware fusion, BigCounter significantly enhances feature representation. Extensive experiments on two benchmark RGB-T datasets demonstrate that BigCounter achieves superior accuracy and generalization compared with state-of-the-art methods.

title: Novel medical image compression/decompression technique based on bicubic interpolation with matrix reduction algorithm
authors: Sazeen Taha Abdulrazzaq, Mohammed M. Siddeq, Mohd Asyraf Zulkifley, Mohd Hairi Mohd Zaman, Asraf Mohamed Moubark
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Medical imaging is an important contributor to diagnostic accuracy and monitoring of various health conditions, enabling healthcare professionals to gain valuable insights into the internal structures and functions of the body. With the prevalence of telemedicine and big data integration in healthcare, the effective storage and online transmission of these images have grown in importance. The main two challenges in this field are limited transmission bandwidth and storage capacity. Lossless compression techniques are necessary because information must be preserved to guarantee the integrity of medical images. Optimizing and decreasing the duration of compression and decompression processing is a fundamental aspect that warrants attention. In this work, an innovative technique for compressing medical images is developed and evaluated. It shows superior image reconstruction quality and a compression performance of up to 99%. The compression technique involves downscaling medical images through bicubic interpolation. Matrix reduction is subsequently employed to further reduce the size of the interpolated matrix to one-fourth of its dimensions. Finally, efficiency arithmetic coding is added to improve compression. The proposed algorithm emerges as a superior candidate in applications of image compression, outperforming all known advanced compression methods. The effectiveness of this method has been assessed by X-ray, ultrasound, CT, and MRI images sourced from an available database. Various performance metrics, including peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), mean square error, and structural similarity index measure (SSIM), have been utilized to evaluate the quality of the images compressed using the proposed algorithm. The results show that at a high compression ratio of 100:1 (up to 99%), the proposed method achieves a high PSNR and SSIM values.

title: mTIPs: Multimodal Time-Series Imaging and Prediction System for Early Differentiation of Retinal Organoids_supp1-3668206.pdf
authors: Mingzhai Sun
publish: N/A
abstract: Retinal organoids (ROs) hold immense promise for disease modeling and therapy, but their production is hindered by developmental heterogeneity, necessitating robust characterization and early prediction. However, the limited application of current methods to human stem cell–derived organoids reduces clinical relevance; the omission of 3D multimodal imaging leads to incomplete morphological assessment; and the lack of time-series analysis diminishes applicability. To address these gaps, we developed mTIPs (Multimodal Time Series Imaging and Prediction System), which enables automated, early-stage quality control of human embryonic stem cell (hESC)-derived ROs. Firstly, we present the first publicly available multimodal, time-series imaging dataset of hESC-derived retinal organoids, establishing a foundational resource with significant clinical relevance that was previously lacking. Furthermore, we highlight the advantages of 3D imaging, demonstrating that Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) derived volumetric data offers unique insights into development and significantly enhances early predictive accuracy compared to traditional 2D bright-field microscopy. Notably, we developed a practical, unified model using a single time-encoding network that achieves an AUROC exceeding 0.8 at all time points, including as early as Day 6—significantly outperforming expert manual classification. mTIPs offers a scalable framework to standardize organoid production, enhancing the reliability of ROs for research and future clinical applications.

title: Predicting Situation Awareness From Physiological Signals_supp1-3647796.docx
authors: Kieran J. Smith
publish: N/A
abstract: Situation awareness (SA)—comprising the ability to 1) perceive critical elements in the environment, 2) comprehend their meanings, and 3) project their future states—is critical for human operator performance. Due to the disruptive nature of gold-standard SA measures, researchers have sought physiological indicators to provide real-time information about SA. We extend prior work by using a multimodal suite of neurophysiological, psychophysiological, and behavioral signals, predicting all three levels of SA along a continuum, and predicting a comprehensive measure of SA in a complex multitasking simulation. We present a lab study in which 31 participants controlled an aircraft simulator task battery while wearing physiological sensors and responding to SA “freeze-probe” assessments. We demonstrate the validity of task and assessment for measuring SA. Multimodal physiological models predict SA with greater predictive performance (Q2 for levels 1–3 and total, respectively: 0.14, 0.00, 0.26, and 0.36) than models built with shuffled labels, demonstrating that multimodal physiological signals provide useful, albeit imperfect, information in predicting all SA levels. Level 3 SA (projection) was best predicted, and level 2 SA (comprehension) was the most challenging to predict. Ablation analysis and single-sensor models found EEG and eye-tracking signals to be particularly useful to predictions of level 3 and total SA. A reduced sensor fusion model showed that predictive performance can be maintained with a subset of sensors. This rigorous cross-validation assessment of predictive performance demonstrates the utility of multimodal physiological signals for inferring complex, holistic, objective measures of SA at all levels, nondisruptively, and along a continuum.

title: Preference-Aware Multimodal Contrastive Learning Recommendation
authors: Hairong Wang, Zhihang Yi, Zhaojing Xu, Jing Wang, Hongying Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Multimodal recommendation systems struggle to effectively integrate heterogeneous modalities and select informative negative samples, which limits their ability to capture users' true preferences. To address these challenges, we propose Preference-Aware Multimodal Contrastive Learning (PAMCL), a novel framework that leverages a lightweight large language model (LLM) to generate explicit user preference descriptions for guiding the recommendation process. Our approach introduces a simplified multimodal graph aggregation mechanism that unifies feature propagation and contrastive learning, enhancing semantic consistency while reducing computational complexity. Addition ally, we develop a preference-guided multiple negative sampling strategy that utilizes LLM-generated preference embeddings to identify challenging negative samples while avoiding false negatives. Experimental results on three public multimodal recommendation benchmarks demonstrate that PAMCL significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods by effectively addressing false negative issues in sampling and noise contamination in multimodal fusion.

title: Time Series Classification on Eye Tracking for Identification of Sequential-Global Cognitive Styles
authors: Hafzatin Nurlatifa, Teguh Bharata Adji, Igi Ardiyanto, Generosa Lukhayu Pritalia, Sunu Wibirama
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Classifying sequential–global cognitive styles is essential for developing adaptive and personalized learning systems. Existing studies have relied on aggregated gaze statistics from proprietary eye tracking software, limiting feature diversity and classification accuracy. To address this gap, this study proposes a novel framework for classification of cognitive styles based on the Felder–Silverman Learning Style Model (FSLSM) that leverages time series eye tracking data and deep learning. Features from x- and y-coordinate gaze data were extracted using eight temporal window scales. The experimental results show that the proposed framework accurately distinguishes sequential and global cognitive styles. Among the evaluated models, the Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) achieved the best performance, particularly when combined with Robust scaling, reaching an accuracy of 99.51%. The temporal window of approximately 2.0 seconds (121 samples) yielded the strongest performance. When combined with the feature set comprising gazeX, gazeY, speed, and direction, the proposed method achieved the most optimal discriminative capability. Our findings underscore the potential of time series eye tracking in identifying cognitive styles. This research serves as a crucial initial step in improving biometric-driven approaches to personalized education and adaptive learning technologies.

title: Manageable Convex Region Iteration without Half-Plane Optimization for Robot Navigation in 2D Cluttered Scenes
authors: Yinsong Qu, Yunxiang Li, Yudong Guo, Weiyun Yi, Hanwen Cui, Yuezu Lv, Shanlin Zhong
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: To address path safety and algorithm efficiency challenges in robot navigation within complex environments, this paper proposes an integrated path optimization-following framework with a novel Manageable Convex Region Iteration without Half-Plane Optimization (MCRIwHPO). The path planning problem is modeled as nonlinear optimization to generate a time-independent parameterized path. MCRIwHPO adopts a two-stage design: the first stage generates half-plane constraints via ellipse expansion without additional optimization, and the second stage addresses the seed point inclusion constraint in calculating the maximum-area inscribed ellipse, thus ensuring convex region manageability. This design iteratively increases the ellipse area (the lower bound of convex region area) to enhance the convex region's approximation of free space. For path following, obstacle expansion reserves a safety threshold, and a preset performance controller confines following errors within this threshold to avoid collision caused by external disturbances. MCRIwHPO is compared with existing algorithms in simulations with 5-20 obstacles, where it achieves the optimal convex region area with a stable computation time within 50 ms, satisfying real-time requirements. The integrated framework is further implemented on a four-wheel independent drive vehicle for real-world tests, and experimental results show the vehicle smoothly avoids obstacles with prescribed path following errors and maintains stable velocity during navigation, verifying the framework's effectiveness and practical applicability.

title: Neural Network-Aided Differential Evolution With Double Q-Learning for Dynamic Optimization
authors: Wei Song, Zhi Liu, Yaochu Jin, Yinan Guo, Shengxiang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: As the search space and hence the optimum vary through time, dynamic optimization problems (DOPs) bring tremendous difficulties. Changes in DOPs often manifest as diverse dynamics. Consequently, regulating individuals’ search to adapt to diverse dynamics is crucial to tackle DOPs. Besides, due to the inherent population nature in dynamic optimization algorithms (DOAs), loss of global and local diversities is a critical issue deteriorating the performance of DOAs. Faced with these difficulties, this article proposes a neural network-aided differential evolution with double Q-learning (NNDE-DQ), in which an evolutionary regulation network (ERN) is designed to maintain high global and local diversities over time and regulate individuals’ search that can adapt to diverse dynamics. NNDE-DQ first partitions the search space into multiple subspaces and in each subspace distant individuals are selected as the centers of ERN’s hidden nodes activated by radial basis function. Every input individual is mutated with two randomly selected hidden node centers from different subspaces as differential individuals, facilitating the maintenance of a high global diversity due to very distinct differential terms of the population. Moreover, each mutated individual selects a hidden node center from the subspace located by the mutated individual to undergo crossover. Due to distant hidden node centers in each subspace, a high local diversity can be maintained by individuals’ crossover. Besides, DQ is introduced to acquire ERN’s desired output by interactively estimating individuals’ state-action information, enabling ERN to learn the regulation of individuals’ search in dynamic environments and hence adapt to diverse dynamics. The experimental results demonstrate that NNDE-DQ significantly improves the performance in solving various DOPs comparing to seven state-of-the-art DOAs.

title: Improved the High-temperature Energy Storage Performance of PI Films via Multi-scale Optimization of Electrodes and Interface Engineering_supp1-3668744.docx
authors: Qingguo Chi
publish: N/A
abstract: In modern power electronics, dielectric capacitors are vital for high-temperature energy storage. In the advancement of modern power electronics toward hightemperature and high-reliability applications, dielectric capacitors based on polyimide (PI) films are promising but face critical challenges from high conduction losses at elevated temperatures, restricting their energy storage performance. This study focuses on PI films and employs magnetron sputtering to deposit functional layers, including metal electrodes (Al, Cu, Pt) and inorganic barrier layers (Al₂O₃, SiO₂, PbZrO₃). By investigating electrode materials, diameters (3mm, 6mm, 9mm), and barrier layer compositions, it is found that Al electrodes dominate due to a native insulating Al₂O₃ layer, while 3mm diameter minimizes edge effects and defects. Al₂O₃-PI-Al₂O₃ films achieve a discharge energy density of 1.75 J/cm³ at 150°C (35% higher than pure PI) with excellent cycling stability. This work offers a scalable strategy for high-temperature dielectric capacitors in new energy and pulsed power systems.

title: SyMFood: Synergistic Multi-Modal Prompting for Fine-Grained Zero-Shot Food Detection_supp1-3668226.pdf
authors: Guorui Sheng
publish: N/A
abstract: Fine-grained object detection in food computing is severely constrained by the vast diversity of food items and the high cost of data annotation. Existing Zero-Shot Food Detection (ZSFD) methods attempt to solve this by leveraging semantic information, but they suffer from two critical bottlenecks: (1) a "Semantic Dilemma" (SD) where textual descriptions are too ambiguous to distinguish visually similar food categories, and (2) an "Architectural Bottleneck" (AB) due to the granularity mismatch between high-level semantics and low-level visual features. In this article, we propose SyMFood (Synergistic Multi-modal Framework for Food Generalization), a novel ZSFD framework designed to systematically overcome these challenges. To resolve the SD, SyMFood employs a multi-modal prompt system, which combines rich descriptions from Large Language Models (LLMs) with unambiguous visual exemplars to provide precise semantic grounding. To break the AB, SyMFood introduces a “Refine-then-Fuse” architecture. This design first utilizes a Context-Aware Spatial-Channel Refinement (CaSC) block to enhance visual features independently. Subsequently, a Progressive Food Knowledge Fusion (ProFus) module performs bi-directional, iterative co-refinement between the enhanced visual features and multi-modal prompts across all scales. Extensive experiments across four challenging datasets, including food-specific (UEC FOOD 256, FOWA) and general-purpose (PASCAL VOC, MS COCO) benchmarks, validate our approach. The proposed method outperforms baselines, yielding a notable 8.5% improvement in Harmonic Mean on the FOWA dataset in the genearl ZSD (GZSD) setting. The source code will be available at https://github.com/Niko000202/SymFood0202.

title: Payload Vision: A Nanosatellite Payload System for Efficient Data Transmission
authors: Abhishek Sharma, Suryansh, Nitya Rastogi, Siddharth Samrat, Ruchita Gautam, Himanshu Chaudhary, Aditya Rajawat, Vipin Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In today's world of cutting-edge remote sensing and space exploration, payloads are essential for capturing and relaying analytical information from satellites, drones, and other platforms to the ground. This research presents Payload Vision, an innovative system crafted to manage nanosatellite payloads, initially focused on text-based data transmission, as a preliminary step towards comprehensive imaging capabilities. This manuscript explores the system's architecture and its implementation for the transmission and reception of data generated by the sensors implemented on the imaging satellite payload with the planned enhancements. Through simulated experiments, the output results demonstrate the effectiveness of the text-based module in environments with limited bandwidth. The proposed system tackles challenges related to data compression, transmission reliability, and scalability by leveraging past advancements in spacecraft imaging.

title: Speak Sign: ISL to Multilingual Speech Using Deep Learning Framework
authors: K. Manasa, H. Vishnu Teja, K. Komal Kiran, G. Vallathan, L. Uma Maheshwari, T.V. Chandra Shekhar
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: This research proposes a Hybrid Bi-LSTM with Attention Mechanism for Speak Sign: ISL to Multilingual Speech, leveraging a deep learning framework. Previous approaches for gesture recognition and sign-to-text translation, including CNN, GRU, LSTM, GRU-LSTM, LSTM-GRU, BiLSTM, and Bi-LSTM with Attention, primarily relied on static features or linear sequential models, which are limited in capturing complex spatiotemporal dependencies. To overcome these limitations, the proposed model integrates a Bi-LSTM with an attention mechanism to capture bidirectional temporal trends and emphasize key frames through attention weighting. This combination enhances sequential recognition of ISL gestures and improves sentence formulation. Experimental results on a personalized ISL dataset show that the proposed system outperforms GRU by 0.5 %, LSTM by 0.4 %, GRU-LSTM by 0.4 %, LSTM-GRU by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.3 \%$</tex>, and Bi-LSTM by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.3 \%$</tex> in Accuracy, Precision, and Recall, demonstrating improved robustness and generalization for real-time ISL-to-speech translation.

title: Real-time Path-recovery Method for Open-fiber-cross-connect-based Networks
authors: Hiroshi Ou, Ayako Iwaki, Tomoya Hatano, Tatsuya Shimada, Kenichi Nakura
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Cloud computing with direct optical paths utilizing fiber cross connects is a promising way to realize low-latency high-reliability applications cost effectively. However, path-recovery for fiber cross connect-based networks has not been fully investigated. In particular, overall recovery times obtained were of the order of seconds, and only static network configurations and failure points were considered in prior works, which fail to suit latency-critical applications such as remote drone control over networks. We propose a restoration-based path-recovery method with proactive recovery-candidate calculation for software-defined network -controlled fiber cross connect-based networks; it offers flexible operation in different network configurations without human involvement. Thanks to the proactive calculation imposing minimal processing delay in the controller, real-time path recovery is attained. This paper introduces experiments that use commercial products. The results indicate that the method can execute automatic fault localization in different network configurations and achieve real-time fault recovery within 15 milliseconds, which is sufficiently fast for the targeted application.

title: Task Placement Optimization of Redundant Robots Using Reliability Maps under Locked Joint Failures
authors: Yuchen Xing, Biyun Xie
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The failure-tolerant workspace of a robot is defined as the reachable workspace both before and after an arbitrary joint is locked at an arbitrary angle during a motion. Once the task is located within the failure-tolerant workspace, task completion can be guaranteed. However, a failure-tolerant workspace is typically only a small region within the robot workspace, and existing methods are limited to computing it in the positional workspace. This work introduces reliability maps, which provide information on the reliability of different regions across the entire workspace. A method is developed to compute reliability maps in both positional and orientational workspace accurately and efficiently. The computed reliability map can be applied to optimize end-effector task placement to maximize the task completion ratio. Examples of reliability maps for a planar 3R robot, a spatial 4R robot, and a spatial 7R robot are computed using the proposed method, along with uniform and random sampling for comparison. Simulation and physical experiments are conducted to further validate the proposed task placement optimization method. Results indicate that using the reliability map to optimize task placement can significantly improve the task completion ratio after experiencing arbitrary locked joint failures.

title: Cross-Domain Knowledge Distillation for Low-Resolution Human Pose Estimation
authors: Zejun Gu, Zhong-Qiu Zhao, Henghui Ding, Hao Shen, Zhao Zhang, De-Shuang Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: In practical applications of human pose estimation, low-resolution inputs frequently occur, and existing state-of-the-art models perform poorly with low-resolution images. This work focuses on boosting the performance of low-resolution models by distilling knowledge from a high-resolution model. However, we face the challenge of feature size mismatch and class number mismatch when applying knowledge distillation to networks with different input resolutions. To address this issue, we propose a novel cross-domain knowledge distillation (CDKD) framework. In this framework, we construct a scale-adaptive projector ensemble (SAPE) module to spatially align feature maps between models of varying input resolutions. It adopts a projector ensemble to map low-resolution features into multiple common spaces and adaptively merges them based on multi-scale information to match high-resolution features. Additionally, we construct a cross-class alignment (CCA) module to solve the problem of the mismatch of class numbers. By combining an easy-to-hard training (ETHT) strategy, the CCA module further enhances the distillation performance. The effectiveness and efficiency of our approach are demonstrated by extensive experiments on three common benchmark datasets: MPII, COCO, and Crowdpose.

title: Simulation-Based Optimization of Highway Active Traffic Management Strategy Designs
authors: Zheng Li, Jian Sun, Haoming Meng, Ye Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Microscopic traffic simulation models are promising for modeling detailed traffic features from the individual vehicle level, but they present challenges in optimization due to their computationally time-consuming, and non-analytic nature. This study addresses the difficulty of incorporating microscopic simulations into the optimization framework for multiple types of Active Traffic Management (ATM) strategies design by adopting the Simulation-Based Optimization (SBO) paradigm. A novel discrete SBO algorithm for highway ATM strategies designs, embedded within a Cell Transmission Model (CTM)-based meta model and a problem-specific Adaptive Hyperbox Algorithm (AHA) is proposed. The effectiveness of the proposed SBO method is validated through two cases: a toy highway and a real-world highway. The method is benchmarked versus the commonly utilized general-purpose model based-SBO algorithm. The experiments indicate that the proposed problem specific SBO algorithm outperforms the general-purpose model-based algorithms to obtain promising solutions in ATM strategies design problems, especially when the simulation resources are tight. Under ATM strategies derived by the proposed algorithm, the observed total delay on the highway shows a substantial 70% decrease. The ATM strategy obtained through the microscopic model-based optimization is proven to be superior to the macroscopic model-based approach. The introduced SBO algorithm in this work advances the current SBO theoretical framework by enhancing its applicability to highway traffic issues and overcoming obstacles related to the optimization of discrete variables.

title: A Fast Double Null Control Concept Enabling Compact Fusion Reactors_supp1-3662750.pdf
authors: Mark Lafferty
publish: N/A

title: Anomaly Detection in Electricity Consumption Based on a Hybrid CNN-LSTM Neural Network
authors: Yuming Sun, Bin Pan, Kai Lv, Fenggang Sun, Peng Lan
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: To address the challenges of strong concealment and high detection difficulty in anomalous electricity consumption behavior such as electricity theft, this paper proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM detection model. This model utilizes CNN to automatically extract local anomalous features from electricity consumption data, while employing LSTM to capture the long-term dependencies and periodic patterns in user consumption behavior. The features from these two modalities are fused through feature concatenation, forming a more discriminative comprehensive feature representation, which is ultimately fed into a fully connected layer for end-to-end classification of anomalous electricity consumption. Experimental results on a publicly available dataset from the State Grid Corporation of China demonstrate that the proposed hybrid model outperforms both individual deep learning models (CNN, LSTM) and other advanced baselines (LSTM with Attention, DeepAR) in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC, achieving an F1score of 0.958, which validates the model's effectiveness and superiority. With its simple structure and superior detection performance, the proposed method provides reliable technical support for power companies in identifying anomalous electricity consumption.

title: A Study on Rapid and Smooth Path Planning for UAVs in Multi-Obstacle and Narrow Environments
authors: Hou Jiabing, Jiaying Lu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Path planning in complex three-dimensional environments with multiple obstacles and narrow passages remains a critical challenge for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). To address this issue, this paper proposes an improved Rapidly-exploring Dense Tree (RDT) algorithm for rapid and smooth UAV path generation. Unlike conventional RRT-based planners that rely on uniform or weakly biased sampling, the proposed method explicitly enhances local exploration efficiency while preserving the global connectivity exploration capability of the sampling tree.Specifically, the concept of density in RDT is clarified as a localized and relative sampling property, rather than a global increase in sampling nodes. Based on this definition, candidate channels are identified through three-dimensional geometric gap analysis to represent potential narrow passages, which are then used to trigger adaptive local high-density sampling. In addition, a cost-based tree expansion strategy combined with heuristic guidance is introduced to reduce redundant searches and accelerate convergence toward high-quality paths. To ensure trajectory smoothness and dynamic feasibility, a post-processing stage using cubic B-spline smoothing with curvature constraints is employed, followed by multi-constraint re-optimization considering path length, curvature, climb rate, and collision risk.Simulation experiments are conducted in two representative environments, including multi-obstacle and narrow-channel scenarios, to validate the proposed approach. Comparative studies against FS-RRT and GVP-RRT demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a 17.6% reduction in average path length and a 62.4% reduction in planning time, while generating smoother trajectories and maintaining strong obstacle avoidance capability. Additional statistical evaluations under repeated trials further confirm the improved stability and robustness of the proposed RDT framework in narrow and highly constrained environments.

title: Research on the Buffering Characteristics of the Electromagnetic Repulsion Mechanism
authors: Feiyang Yu, Xiangyu Zhang, Lei Qi
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The fast mechanical switch based on the electromagnetic repulsion mechanism is one of the core equipment of hybrid HVDC breakers. The rapidity of the switch that completes the opening in a few milliseconds is crucial to HVDC breakers. However, a higher requirement of damping is need for the switch because of the rapidity. In this paper, a dual-threshold dynamic correction strategy of action speed based on a fully-controlled driving circuit is proposed to ensure the "soft landing" of the moving parts in the fast switch through the precise control and adjustment of the intervention time of the slower part. The influence of the buffer current direction and the buffer application moment on the electromagnetic buffer effect is revealed through simulation. The paper provides some guidance for improving the reliability of fast mechanical switch in long-term operation.

title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Game Design and NPC Behaviour
authors: Anshul Singh Rajput, Kundan Sachinkumar Patil, Radhakrishna Rambola
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The concept of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) has established itself in the reinterpretation of the functionality of non-player characters (NPC) in the new video games. Until recently, NPCs were predictable, and might be unbendable in their behaviour, although they can be written in finite state machines and behaviour trees. Nonetheless, concepts such as reinforcement learning, utility-based decision-making and use of large language models have also been introduced in recent times, where the characters can actively respond to environmental action, as well as to player action. The evolution of NPC behavior in this paper is evaluated with references to the classical methods, the hybrid methods where the rule structure is combined with the adaptive learning systems and the conversational systems. Using the case analysis of The Last of Us, F.A.C.U.L. companion, which was created by Arena Breakout, and NVIDIA demonstrations of the ACE/Convai technology, we describe the practical problems and opportunities of these technologies implementation into the production environment. We also remark on the technical trade-offs, computational cost and ethical issues of adaptive NPCs in particular, concerning privacy, fairness and emotional manipulation of players. To provide these discussions, we propose a systematic evaluation framework in the form of a task success, realism, and player immersion scale, and it offers an avenue in which the developers and researchers can analyze and streamline the following generation of game AI.

title: Investigation of Composite ESS with Hybrid Controller for Restructured AGC System
authors: Ram Naresh Mishra, Narendra Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The growing shift toward a free market framework across all sectors of the global economy has significantly influenced the power industry. In this evolving landscape, procurement flexibility has become a critical factor for power providers, enabling efficient energy supply to end-users and benefiting both consumers and businesses. The primary objective of this research is to evaluate the performance of a composite energy storage system (ESS) integrated with a hybrid control strategy that combines a cascade FOPI-FOPD controller and a polar fuzzy controller. The goal is to enhance the frequency regulation capability of a restructured nuclear-gas-solar (N-G-S) power system operating under deregulated conditions. The proposed methodology involves applying the same set of optimized controller parameters to multiple operating scenarios to ensure a fair and consistent performance assessment. Additionally, the Disco Participation Matrix (DPM) is varied to reflect the dynamic nature of the market economy, where load disturbances induced by distribution companies (DISCOs) and their corresponding power demands can fluctuate significantly. The key findings demonstrate that the proposed control strategy effectively improves system frequency response, enhances system stability under varying market conditions, and exhibits strong adaptability to different DPM scenarios. These results confirm that the approach is both practical and robust, making it suitable for realworld implementation in modern deregulated power systems.

title: A Hybrid CEEMDAN-CorrInformer Model for High-Accuracy Wind Power Prediction
authors: Lun Xu, Han Gao, Shengxian Cao, Tianyi Sun, Mingyue He
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Wind power prediction is critical for grid stability and efficient energy management, yet existing forecasting methods struggle with the inherent randomness and non-stationarity of wind power data. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel CEEM-CorrInformer hybrid model that integrates Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (CEEMDAN) and an enhanced CorrInformer deep learning architecture. The proposed framework first decomposes raw wind power signals into multiple Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs) and a residual component using CEEMDAN, effectively reducing data complexity. Subsequently, the CorrInformer model, optimized with sparse self-attention and an autocorrelation mechanism, captures both short-term cyclical patterns and long-term dependencies in the decomposed components. Additionally, a hybrid data preprocessing pipeline combining KNN imputation and CEEMDAN-wavelet denoising ensures robust handling of missing values and noise.

title: Innovative Hybrid Strategy for Energy-Efficient Clustering and Routing in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: C. Gokul Prasad, Ramkumar Natarajan, M. A. Amarnath, V. Ranjeeka Shree, J. Dhanasekar, S. Ramalingam
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are essential in fields such as agriculture, education, industry, and environmental monitoring. However, given their limited energy supplies and occasional maintenance, creating energy-efficient network topologies and routing methods is a considerable problem. This work presents an approach to enhance the longevity of networks in WSN. This approach employs advanced fuzzy C-means clustering, modified particle swarm optimization (MPSO) for cluster head selection, and routingbased adaptive zebra optimization (AZO). The adaptive zebra optimization approach relies on efficient routing paths chosen for every relay nodes to send data to the base station (BS). The proposed method illustrates that the relay node facilitates and optimizes communication between the sink and the cluster head, thereby enhancing energy efficiency. Ultimately, the quality of service (QoS) in WSNs improves, enabling the system to sustain long performance. The suggested technique demonstrated superior performance compared to prior methods for energy consumption, throughput, node viability, end-to-end latency (E2E), and packet delivery ratio (PDR).

title: Enhancing Credit Risk Assessment with Sentiment Analysis and Feature Engineering
authors: Sharanya Baidya, Raktim Kundu, Aaratrika Bhattacharya, Krishna Chakravarty, Jagannath Singh
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This study proposes a hybrid machine learning framework for credit risk prediction that integrates structured financial and demographic variables with sentiment analysis of borrower narratives. The proposed model leverages FinBERT, a financial-domain language model, to extract sentiment features from generated loan application texts, identifying qualitative risk factors that add to the numerical data. Additionally, a neural-network sub-module computes a composite “risk score” from categorical loan attributes (grade, intent, ownership), augmenting the feature set. Ensemble classifiers (XGBoost and CatBoost) are trained on a Kaggle-sourced loan dataset, using SMOTE to address class imbalance and SHAP (SHapley) values for model interpretability. The CatBoost+XGBoost ensemble achieves AUC 96.73% and F1 92.55%, significantly outperforming a baseline. These results demonstrate that incorporating FinBERT-derived sentiment and engineered risk features yields more accurate and robust credit default predictions. Overall, the findings indicate that ensemble models integrating text-derived sentiment and domain-specific features can substantially improve the reliability of credit risk assessment. This approach demonstrates the practical value of incorporating unstructured textual data and domain-driven feature engineering in financial risk modeling, providing a foundation for more nuanced and effective credit decision-making systems.

title: Collision Avoidance in Healthcare Robotics Using mm-Wave Radar Fusion: A Hygienic and Fail-Safe Approach
authors: Rolf Schmidt, Marco Masannek, Andreas Deinlein, Verena Schmidt, Stefan May, Andreas Nüchter
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Autonomous medical systems must meet stringent hygiene and safety requirements while operating reliably in dynamic clinical environments. This paper presents a collision avoidance system based on the fusion of two mm-wave radar technologies - frequency modulated continuous wave (FMCW) and pulsed coherent radar (PCR). The system is fully integrated behind sealed covers of medical devices and enables unobtrusive and hygienic use without compromising functionality. We demonstrate that both radar types provide robust detection of dynamic obstacles, even through layers of disinfectants, blood and polycarbonate materials. A fail-safe system architecture based on redundant sensor paths and dual microcontrollers ensures reliable operation under fault conditions. Experimental validation on a robotic X-ray system confirms the responsiveness, accuracy and suitability of the system for clinical integration. The results show that radar fusion offers a promising path to hygienic and certified safe motion planning in healthcare robotics.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Agentic AI for Resource-Efficient Optimization of National Power Grids
authors: Ankith Konda
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: National power grids are rapidly evolving into complex, dynamic ecosystems characterized by renewable intermittency, distributed generation, and critical reliability requirements. Classical optimization methods and isolated quantum solvers are insufficient to handle the scale, adaptability, and combinatorial complexity of modern grid operations. In this paper, we propose a novel hybrid quantum-classical agentic AI framework that unites decentralized learning agents with quantum-enhanced solvers to optimize grid-wide performance under real-time constraints. Each regional agent autonomously senses, plans, and acts based on local state information, while offloading NP-hard subproblems such as unit commitment to a quantum backend. A lightweight coordination layer harmonizes global constraints, ensuring stability and convergence. Experiments on IEEE-39 and IEEE-118 bus systems show that our approach significantly improves load balancing accuracy (up to 95.1%), reduces energy loss, and accelerates convergence compared to classical and hybrid baselines. This work establishes a new direction for resource-efficient, scalable, and intelligent power grid management.

title: PayGuard: An Event-Driven, Policy-Graph-Guided AI Pipeline for Real-Time Payroll Risk Detection and Conformal Remediation
authors: Raghuveer Yerneni
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: We propose PayGuard, a real-time, event-driven AI pipeline for detecting and mitigating payroll risk—including statutory breaches, fraud, benefit misclassification, and late adjustments—in complex, multi-jurisdiction environments. PayGuard integrates (i) complex event processing (CEP) for temporal pattern detection, (ii) temporal modeling via Transformer or Neural Hawkes networks, (iii) policy validation using a machineinterpretable knowledge graph (OWL/SHACL), and (iv) calibrated, conformal risk-to-action decisioning with fairness and privacy guarantees. The system processes event-time payroll streams with strict SLAs, providing verifiable, auditable, and policy-compliant remediations. Experiments on a synthetic eventstream benchmark demonstrate that PayGuard improves detection performance, calibration, and Expected Breach Loss (EBL) reduction, significantly outperforming rule-based and modelonly baselines. The system establishes a unified architecture for operationalizing real-time compliance in payroll AI systems.

title: Data-Driven Dynamic Parameter Learning of manipulator robots
authors: Mohammed Elseiagy, Tsige Tadesse Alemayoh, Ranulfo Bezerra, Shotaro Kojima, Kazunori Ohno
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Bridging the sim-to-real gap remains a fundamental challenge in robotics, as accurate dynamic parameter estimation is essential for reliable model-based control, realistic simulation, and safe deployment of manipulators. Traditional analytical approaches often fall short when faced with complex robot structures and interactions. Data-driven methods offer a promising alternative, yet conventional neural networks such as recurrent models struggle to capture long-range dependencies critical for accurate estimation. In this study, we propose a Transformer-based approach for dynamic parameter estimation, supported by an automated pipeline that generates diverse robot models and enriched trajectory data using Jacobian-derived features. The dataset consists of 8,192 robots with varied inertial and frictional properties. Leveraging attention mechanisms, our model effectively captures both temporal and spatial dependencies. Experimental results highlight the influence of sequence length, sampling rate, and architecture, with the best configuration (sequence length 64, 64 Hz, four layers, 32 heads) achieving a validation R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.8633. Mass and inertia are estimated with near-perfect accuracy, Coulomb friction with moderate-to-high accuracy, while Damping coefficient and distal link center-of-mass remain more challenging. These results demonstrate that combining Transformers with auto-mated dataset generation and kinematic enrichment enables scalable, accurate dynamic parameter estimation, contributing to improved sim-to-real transfer in robotic systems.

title: Low-Latency Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks Access Control Considering Time-Domain Holes
authors: Yan Song, Binjie Ying, Liwei Ren, Shuhan Shi, Wenze Lan, Ruqi Zhang, Jiamu Yang, Wen Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: The Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network (SAGINs) play a critical role in supporting multi-layer cooperative communication, data aggregation, and task relaying. However, dynamic channel conditions and bursty traffic flows can easily lead to excessive system queuing delays and time-domain coverage holes, severely impacting network quality of service and delay performance. To address these issues, we propose a low-latency SAGINs access control considering time-domain holes, aiming to achieve intelligent Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) admission and delay optimization within the integrated system. First, system models for admission control, queuing delay, and time-domain holes are established, formulating an optimization problem targeting the minimization of the overall average queuing delay. Under constraints of access requirements and long-term timedomain hole rates, Lyapunov optimization theory is applied to transform the long-term constraints into a real-time solvable short-term problem. Second, to tackle the high-dimensional state space and system nonlinearity, a Deep Q-Network (DQN)-based intelligent admission control mechanism is introduced. This mechanism learns the optimal access strategy for UAVs through continuous interaction with the environment, enabling adaptive decision making and long-term performance guarantee. Finally, simulation results verify that the proposed method can effectively reduce queuing delay and suppress time-domain coverage holes while ensuring system stability.

title: Real-Time Wildlife Intrusion Detection Using YOLOv8 and OpenCV
authors: Abia Antony, Achyuth V. A., Shifil Theres Sabu, Jacob Thomas
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The purpose of this work is to employ OpenCV, a Computer Vision library, to design an Animal Intrusion Detection and Alert System for farms. The system will monitor for animal movement near farm fields by utilizing camera video feeds. Based on shape, size, and motion patterns, OpenCV will analyze the video in real-time to detect animals and motion. The system will send an alarm and alert the farmer via SMS instantly once it identifies an animal intrusion. This method is intended to reduce crop loss resulting from wild or stray animals, hence ensuring reduced financial losses for farmers. The system is programmed to run round the clock and adjust to different light and weather conditions in order to ensure consistent performance. By combining Computer Vision and automated alerting, the solution offers a cost-efficient and effective way of securing agricultural land.

title: Comparative Machine Learning Approaches to Permafrost Probability Mapping in the Western Himalaya’s Jhelum Basin
authors: Rohit Kumar Singh, Sunil Kumar, Rakesh Sahu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Permafrost is a critical component of high-altitude mountain systems, influencing slope stability, hydrology, and climate-cryosphere interactions. However, its distribution in the Himalaya remains poorly constrained due to limited field measurements and the absence of openly available probability maps. This study presents the first reproducible permafrost probability maps for the Jhelum basin in the western Himalaya, developed using the openly accessible dataset of Remya et al. (2023). Rock glaciers were used as indicators of permafrost presence, while pseudo-absence points were generated through spatially stratified random sampling. Topographic and climatic variables-including elevation, mean annual air temperature, slope, aspect, and solar radiation-were extracted from coregistered raster datasets. Three machine learning classifiers, namely Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost, were evaluated using five-fold spatial block cross-validation ($\sim 20 \mathrm{~km}$ blocks).

title: A Comprehensive Review on SOC Estimation Techniques of Batteries
authors: Hariom, Mohd Zuhaib, Sanjay Maurya
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The correct estimate of the State of Charge (SOC) is significant for battery longevity and performance, especially for energy storage applications, electric cars, and emergency purposes. The current work discussed and compared different approaches for SOC estimate of different types of batteries-lithium-ion batteries (Li-B), valve-regulated lead acid (VRLA) or gel-acid batteries, and nickel-metal-hybrid Ni-H batteries. The main point of comparison are their accuracy, similarity, dependability, adaptability, and robust performance. It also gives the main advantages and disadvantages of each covered approach. Additionally, it offers methods for estimating SOC based on the particular battery's features and use.

title: Enhancing Contextual Retrieval and Generation of Text through RAG and Knowledge Graph with Custom Cosine Similarity
authors: Sanjib Kumar Raul, Kvtkn Prashanth, Nidhi Vadodariya, T. Ramakrishnudu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) models improve large language models by providing a context-aware prompt-before-prompt kind of mechanism. The RAG framework appends the strengths of retrieval methods with generative models. Consequently, it improves the performance of responsegenerating tasks, such as question answering, summarization, and conversational agents. However, the context of queries could be improved with knowledge graphs. To address this, we propose an enhanced RAG model that integrates knowledge graphs and a novel custom similarity function to improve the open-domain question answering. Our method uses dynamic chunking guided by the structure of the knowledge graph and agglomerative clustering with custom cosine similarity, where, each term of similarity function mimics progressive similarity aggregation. This improves the retrieval accuracy and performance of the proposed model by enhancing both the relevance and diversity of the retrieved chunks.

title: AI-Driven Sign Language Translation for Real-Time Interaction
authors: Rahan, Sheenam Naaz, Sanjeet, Kapil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The challenge of communication between normal individuals and those who are deaf or speech impaired is significant. This project addresses it by developing a real time sign language translation system to enable smooth interaction between both communities. It identifies hand gestures from Indian Sign Language (ISL) and translates them into their corresponding meanings for effective communications. To achieve this, we used deep learning models like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) for continuous gesture recognition, along with machine learning models such as Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Random Forest for static sign detection. The system also supports speech-to-sign translation, using Google Speech API for online and CMU Sphinx for offline recognition, ensuring functionality even without internet access. Using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and dictionary-based translation, speech is converted into meaningful sign outputs displayed through GIF-based gesture animations. The project is developed using Python, TensorFlow, OpenCV, NumPy and Tkinter and trained on the WLASL dataset containing over 12000 processed videos. This system enhances recognition accuracy, reduces latency and supports bidirectional communication-ultimately bridging the communication gap and promoting inclusivity through intelligent technology.

title: Optimal Design of Passive Link Systems and Sensor Fusion for Precise Position Measurement of Robot End-Effector
authors: Morito Sato, Atsushi Takata, Masafumi Okada
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Many industrial robots utilize feedback control to perform precise tasks such as welding. The trajectory tracking performance of feedback control critically depends on the accuracy of sensor information, which often suffers from link deflection and homing error of the joint displacement sensors. To address these problems, this paper introduces lightweight and highly rigid passive link systems (PL) for acquiring measurement information with reduced deflection effects. To optimally integrate these redundant measurements, a sensor fusion method is developed, based on the resolution ellipsoid, to achieve precise estimation of the end-effector position. An optimal design methodology for the PL is also proposed, and the effectiveness of both proposals is validated experimentally. Moreover, to address biases observed in the experimental data relative to the true values, we propose a bias removal method based on Recursive Least Squares (RLS). We demonstrate through experimental data that this method enables precise position measurement of the end-effector.

title: Zig-RAPM: A Cost Effective Zigbee-driven Realtime Remote Air Pollution Monitoring System in Industrial Belt, an Experimental Approach with Field Survey
authors: Sufyan Nadeem, Sunam Jana, Tahrim Fatma, Sneha Chowdhury, Zainab Fatima, Suman Paul
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Air pollution and poor air quality have a severe impact on the environment. Air pollution creates several health hazards and inconveniences on the quality of life of the people in an industrial environment due to industrial untreated pollutants. Accordingly, in an industrial belt periodic monitoring and measurement is essential to regulate pollution due to the poisonous gases and pollutant substances released into the air. Aiming measurement and monitoring, in this paper we present Zig-RAPM, our designed and developed costeffective real-time remote air pollution monitoring system driven by the Zigbee network in the paradigm of IIoT. The developed system provides a comprehensive solution for monitoring the concentration (densities) of different toxic gases in the atmosphere utilising different MQ gas sensors. The captured sensor data were forwarded utilizing Zigbee-driven DIGI Xbee S2C RF modules for remote monitoring of the pollution level at real-time. The signal strength including local and remote RSSI; and PLR were recorded. The developed ZigRAPM monitoring system was deployed and tested with field survey at different locations in a petrochemical industrial hub in eastern India.

title: An Analysis of Employee Engagement in IT Enterprises in Digital India: A SEM Based Approach
authors: Ritika Malik, Swati Chawla, Megha Sehgal, Romita Modi, Reena Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Employee engagement has far-reaching implications because the degree of engagement of an employee impacts organisation outcome both at micro and macro level. Hence, EE has attracted a lot of attention for academicians, researchers and several corporate experts. Moreover, the ever-evolving business environment reinstates the fact that it is necessary to outline the antecedents and implications of employee engagement as the connected teams are more productive. In this study, the primary contributors to employee engagement in the Indian IT sector have been outlined and explored how they influence employee performance and organisation performance at a large. The data was acquired via the use of a questionnaire, and structural equation modelling was used to evaluate the results. The findings investigate that the primary drivers of employee engagement are the work environment, learning and development, rewards and recognition and leadership. These have significant impact on the performance of the employees working in IT sector. By illuminating the methodology behind employee engagement forecasting, this research makes significant contributions to the Information Technology (IT) sector in India and the management of companies involved with the IT industry. The results of CFA reveal the fact that the work environment is the highly influencing factor and has a direct and largest impact on the performance of the employees followed by the rewards and recognition and learning and development whereas leadership has the lowest impact on the performance, which states that employees' performance and engagement is natural phenomena depends upon the surroundings and can't be developed forcefully by reinforcing rules or guidelines. In addition to this, it contributes to the little body of research that has been done on environmental ethics in the information technology sector. Cross-country comparison of employee engagement may enrich the understanding of employee engagement as a strategic tool to have an edge over competitors and reduce the employee turnover ratio.

title: Hybrid Ag-Al Grating based D-Shaped Optical Fiber Surface Plasmon Resonance Sensor
authors: Bijayananda Patnaik, Hansa Jha, Sudhanshu Lenka
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: A Surface Plasmon Resonance optical fiber sensor using hybrid silver aluminium rating on the surface of a D shaped Optical fiber is investigated in this study. The guided modes evanescent field interacts with free electrons on the metal surface to excite surface plasmon waves. Resonance and absorption Peak shifts are used to identify changes in the surrounding medium refractive indexes. By adjusting the metallic layer thickness sensitivity is optimised using Finite element method (FEM). When compared to traditional silver based structures, this result shows that the use of Ag-Al gratings greatly improve the sensor performance. A maximum sensitivity of $6.4 \mu \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{RIU}$ is achieved, when the external RI changes from 1.33 to 1.39 at metal thickness of 45 nm. The outcomes show that how the hybrid non material has better stability and sensing power which makes it up for missing option for high performance fiber optics sensing applications.

title: Visual and Linguistic Fusion: Building Effective Image Caption Generator
authors: Chetana Thaokar, Pratibha Kokardekar, Aniket Tiwari
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The process of creating descriptive text for images, or image captioning, has attracted a lot of interest in the domains of computer vision and natural language processing. This paper presents an advanced image caption generator specifically for images of humans and animals, with the goal of producing accurate and contextually relevant captions. By utilizing the potent union of Transformer-based architectures and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), our model is able to efficiently capture the semantic context and fine-grained visual details required to produce captions of good quality. The project tackles the shortcomings of current image captioning systems, which frequently fail to produce accurate and contextually relevant captions for particular categories, like pets, and human facial expressions. In order to ensure high-quality and pertinent captions, the training dataset is carefully selected to include a wide variety of images of both humans (particularly children and adolescents) and animals (dogs in particular).

title: Overload Current Suppression Strategy for Voltage-Source Inverters under Impact Loads
authors: Yanzhou Liu, Hao Zhang, Danrui Liu, Jiangfeng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The overcurrent suppression strategy is investigated for inverters handling impact loads. Traditional software-based current limiting methods cannot effectively balance the rapidity of current limiting action and the voltage recovery capability after overload/short circuit interruption. The investigated solution combines hardware-triggered fast voltage reduction with software-based current control to achieve microsecond-level response times, stable constant-current operation during fault conditions and smooth voltage recovery afterward. Operation principle and control theory of the overload current suppression Strategy are analyzed in detail. The effectiveness of the investigated strategy is validated by experimental results.

title: A Decentralized Framework for Inter-Domain Authentication Using Blockchain
authors: Poonam Yerpude, Preeti Chandrakar
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: In intelligent transportation systems, vehicle-tovehicle and vehicle-to-infrastructure communication play vital roles. The rapid evolution of VANET systems and increasing demand for interoperability need data security. Most of the conventional systems employ centralized authentication methods that suffer from scalability and are vulnerable in inter-domain and intra-domain communication. These systems lack revocation monitoring, increasing the unauthorized access to the vehicle during the inter-domain transfer. To address these issues, we provide the decentralized framework for inter-domain vehicle communication and direct authentication system using blockchain. Our system utilizes elliptic curve cryptography (ECC) and digital signatures for secure and reliable communication between vehicles and other domain infrastructures (V2I). This system can revoke access of vehicles that remain unregistered after a number of unsuccessful attempts. This system utilizes the lower communication and computation cost as compared with others and also calculates the gas efficiency. To validate the security and robustness of our authentication protocols, we perform formal analysis using scyther simulation it proves the system is resistant to different attacks.

title: Multi-Object Loco-Manipulation Using Body Holding Primitives for Humanoids
authors: Zhongkai Gu, Wei Zhu, Mitsuhiro Hayashibe
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Although considerable research in humanoid robotics focuses on developing general manipulation capabilities, whole-body manipulation—leveraging all body links for manipulation tasks—has received limited attention. To explore the potential of whole-body manipulation, we propose a framework enabling humanoid robots to carry multiple objects simultaneously using their entire body. We introduce two types of body holding primitives: under-arm holding and wrist-torso holding, significantly expanding humanoid robot manipulation capabilities. To achieve the task of multi-objects loco-manipulation, we leverage compliant control to effectively stabilizes object holding using body links. Furthermore, we integrate existing learning-based whole-body locomotion controllers with a dynamic feedforward PID controller to generate accurate upper-body movements for manipulation while maintaining stable locomotion.

title: Junction Temperature Measurement Method for Multi-Chip Welded IGBT Power Modules Based on Fluorescent Fiber-Optic Probes
authors: Xingfeng Du, Chenxi Zhang, Yuan Yang, Santiago Cobreces, Yucheng Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the rapid development of high-power and high-frequency applications such as electric transportation, renewable energy generation, and industrial frequency conversion, welded multi-chip IGBT power modules are operating under increasingly higher power densities and more severe thermal cycling. The accuracy of junction temperature monitoring directly affects system reliability and lifetime. Traditional temperature-sensitive electrical parameter (TSEP) methods yield only the average temperature of paralleled chips, while infrared thermography and thermocouple measurements are hindered by package shielding and strong electromagnetic interference, making online application difficult. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a junction temperature measurement method for multi-chip welded IGBT power modules based on fluorescent fi beroptic probes and develops a corresponding half-bridge driver and current-carrying experimental platform. Experimental results show that the measurement error does not exceed ±0.7 °C and that the method accurately captures temperature rise non-uniformity among multiple chips, providing a high-precision, EMI-immune solution for junction temperature monitoring of high-power-density modules.

title: ZMP Estimation from Wearable Sensor Using Deep Learning for Gait Analysis
authors: Nilesh Maske, Pratibha Tokas, Vijay Bhaskar Semwal, Neha Gaud
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Zero Moment Point (ZMP) is a key biomechanical indicator of dynamic gait stability, conventionally measured using force plates or pressure insoles that are costly and impractical for continuous wearable use. This work presents a low-cost wearable framework for ZMP estimation using only thigh–shank inertial measurement units (IMUs) and knee joint kinematics. A custom flex sensor-based insole was used solely to compute reference ZMP during training and evaluation. Data were collected from healthy subjects walking on flat and inclined treadmills. A BiLSTM–ANN model trained on synchronized IMU and knee angle data achieved subject-wise 5-fold cross-validation RMSEs of 0.56 cm (mediolateral) and 0.09 cm (anterior–posterior), with corresponding R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values of 0.82 and 0.90. Leave-one-subject-out evaluation demonstrated subject-independent generalization with a modest increase in error due to inter-subject variability. Real time deployment on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano achieved an average inference time of 9.2ms with a quantized model size of 1.8MB, enabling on-device ZMP estimation in controlled gait settings.

title: Data-Efficient Real-Time Control of an Artificial-Muscle-Driven Continuum Robot with Physics-Informed Koopman Operator
authors: Jiahe Wang, Eron Ristich, Eric Weissman, Yi Ren, Jiefeng Sun
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Data-driven techniques enable modeling of nonlinear continuum robots directly from input-output data without relying on physics-based modeling. Among these techniques, the Koopman method has been particularly successful as it enables the application of linear optimal control techniques such as model predictive control (MPC). However, this approach requires a large amount of experimental data to capture the complex dynamics of continuum robots. To reduce the data requirements, we implemented the physics-informed Koopman operator (PIKO) based on Strang splitting that combines simulation data from a static rod-muscle coupling model. Experimental results demonstrate successful trajectory tracking with reduced experimental data requirements. Comparing models trained on different amounts of simulation and experimental data shows the advantage of this method in a low experimental data scenario.

title: A Survey on Formal Methods for Railway Interlockings: From Relays to Satellite-Based Moving Block Signaling
authors: Wan Fokkink
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: An interlocking constitutes an arrangement of railway signaling equipment with the aim to guarantee the safe movement of trains. A wide range of formal methods have been applied in the analysis of interlockings, often in close collaboration with industry. Moreover, different formal frameworks and techniques have been developed that target the efficient analysis of interlockings and the integration of such mathematically rigorous methods into industrial design processes. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of this field, including a wide variety of case studies, ranging from relay-based interlocking systems to recent developments regarding satellite-based moving block technologies. It is also explained how computer-based interlockings for different railway yards can be developed in a uniform, object-oriented fashion, based on their track layouts. Furthermore, an overview is given of domain-specific languages for developing and analyzing interlockings using formal methods and tools in an industrial setting. The main aim is to fit the wide range of papers in this research area into coherent narratives, showing how the employment of formal methods to interlockings has progressed in the last decades. This survey concludes with discussing research gaps and important directions for future research.

title: Gait Planning &#x0026; Adaptive Impedance Control for Turning Walk Based on a Self-Balancing Lower Limb Exoskeleton_supp1-3668388.pdf
authors: Jinke Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Self-balancing lower limb exoskeletons (SBLLE) hold the promise of helping individuals with diverse mobility impairments regain their ability to walk. Equipping exoskeletons with stable turning capabilities is crucial for their real-world application. This paper presents an effective control framework that enables stable turning motions in SBLLE without relying on external assistive devices. A turn-specific gait planner has been designed to generate stable reference gaits. Moreover, an adaptive variable impedance controller (AVIC) is introduced, which adjusts the center of mass (CoM) motion in real time to maintain balance during walking, even when carrying different patients. The proposed approach is validated through simulations, comparative studies, and multi-stage human-subject experiments with ten participants, ranging from healthy users to individuals with severe lower-limb motor impairment. Results consistently demonstrate stable turning performance, with the Zero Moment Point (ZMP) remaining within the support polygon during the entire walking process.

title: Sustainability in FinTech: A UTAUT-Based Analysis of E-Wallet Continuance Intention Among Gen Z Users
authors: Hera Hera, Frans Idoyutan Sitorus, Zulfy Ramadhony, Lianna Wijaya, Aries Aries, Sudip Wagle
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study employs the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) to examine the factors influencing Generation Z's adoption and actual behaviour of e-wallets. In order to shed light on sustainable fintech adoption, the goal is to investigate how performance expectancy, effort expectancy, social influence, and facilitating conditions influence intention to use and how intention turns into actual behaviour. Purposive sampling was used to gather 202 valid responses from e-wallet users. To evaluate the measurement and structural models, data were analysed using the Structural Equation Modelling–Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS) method. The findings show that intention to use is significantly impacted by performance expectancy and facilitating conditions, but not by effort expectancy or social influence. The reliability of behavioural intention in explaining usage among digital natives is further supported by the fact that intention strongly predicts actual behaviour. Extending UTAUT from a sustainability perspective and providing empirical evidence from Indonesia, the research makes theoretical and empirical contributions that emphasize the significance of practical value and infrastructure support in influencing the adoption of fintech and give educators, regulators, and fintech companies practical advice on how to promote long-term e-wallet use.
