title: Federated Meta-Transfer Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Anomaly Detection in Low-Voltage Distribution Networks
authors: Zhenyu Zhao, Tao Hu, Chengkun Huang, Dongwen Wu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Anomaly detection in low-voltage distribution networks faces major challenges due to privacy constraints, heterogeneous data distributions, and limited labeled samples in newly deployed zones. This paper proposes a Federated Meta-Transfer Anomaly Detection (FMTAD) framework that enables privacy-preserving and adaptive detection across distributed grid zones. The framework integrates two sequential stages: (1) a federated meta-learning mechanism that trains a generalized initialization through bi-level optimization without sharing raw data, and (2) a lightweight transfer learning stage that fine-tunes the meta-initialized model using a small number of labeled samples from a new target zone. This design achieves fast adaptation and generalization under non-IID conditions. Experiments on the PowerGridSense dataset demonstrate that FMTAD outperforms single-source, federated, and fine-tuned baselines, achieving 0.9933 accuracy and 0.9793 macro F1 on unseen zones while maintaining data privacy.

title: Capacity Exploitation: A Distillation Framework for Black-Box Watermark Removal
authors: Chengcheng Wei, Aoting Hu, Deiqing Yang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: As deep neural networks continue to grow in scale and deployment scope, protecting model intellectual property has become increasingly important. Black-box watermarking embeds covert ownership signals into a model’s input–output behavior, offering a practical mechanism for IP verification. However, the common assumption that such watermarks survive model extraction creates a critical vulnerability. We identify and formalize an under-explored attack vector that exploits model capacity during extraction, showing that distilling a watermarked teacher into a compact student reshapes its representation space, preserving task-critical features while discarding watermark patterns. Building on this insight, we propose a black-box watermark removal framework that explicitly enforces capacity constraints on the surrogate throughout knowledge distillation. Experiments on Fashion-MNIST and CIFAR-100 demonstrate that our method reduces the watermark success rate of state-of-the-art schemes to as low as 14.34% and 0.00%, respectively, with minimal loss in clean accuracy. These results highlight model capacity as a key factor in watermark robustness and motivate capacity-aware watermarking and evaluation protocols for reliable deep model IP protection.

title: Design and Implementation of a Virtual Simulation Platform for Physics Experiments Supported by Digital Twin Technology
authors: Jinbei Ma
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: This paper designs and implements a virtual simulation platform for physics experiments based on digital twin technology. The platform adopts a five-layer architecture: "Physical Entity Layer-Data Acquisition Layer-Digital Twin Modeling Layer-Virtual Simulation Layer-Application Service Layer," achieving closed-loop data interaction between layers through RESTful APIs and the MQTT protocol. Core innovations include constructing a multi-physics coupling modeling method, proposing geometrically accurate modeling and lightweight optimization strategies, and designing a virtual-real synchronization mechanism based on Kalman filtering. The platform achieves high-fidelity simulations of three typical experiments: Newton's pendulum collision, verification of the law of electromagnetic induction, and verification of the ideal gas law. Test results show that the average relative error is ⩽0.28%, the average response time is ⩽2.4s, and the coefficient of variation is ⩽ 0.2%, all meeting the preset quantification standards. This platform provides an efficient, safe, and repeatable solution for physics experiments, effectively supporting teaching and research needs.

title: Beyond Transformers: Dendritic State-Space Models
authors: Shuqi Yang, Lin Zhong, Zhenyu Lei, Jiayi Li, Shangce Gao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Transformers have achieved remarkable success in vision and language modeling but are fundamentally constrained by the quadratic complexity of self-attention, making them computationally heavy and data-hungry. Structured State-Space Models (SSMs) have emerged as a promising alternative, offering linear-time modeling of global dependencies. However, their expressive power is often limited by a reliance on linear projections for final outputs, which may not adequately capture complex non-linear feature interactions. To address this gap, we propose the Dendritic State-Space Model (DSSM), a novel architecture that synergistically combines the efficiency of an SSM backbone with the non-linear representational power of a bio-inspired dendritic neural module. The latter mimics the computational properties of biological dendrites through parallel non-linear branches that integrate heterogeneous features before classification. We conduct extensive experiments on the CIFAR-10 image classification and text classification benchmarks, demonstrating that DSSM surpasses existing lightweight Transformers and convolutional neural networks in accuracy, parameter efficiency, and convergence behavior. A comprehensive empirical analysis highlights DSSM as a promising new paradigm for efficient and powerful representation learning.

title: A Transient Power Angle Reshaping Control Strategy for Grid Forming Converters
authors: Zhi Huang, Zou Xiao, Yuxuan Lin, Boxin Liu, Xin Xiang, Wuhua Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Grid forming (GFM) converters experience fault current surges far beyond their rated levels under asymmetrical faults, posing serious risks to the converter due to limited current-handling capability of power electronic devices. While conventional fault current limiting methods, such as current saturation and virtual impedance, although effective in constraining current magnitude, may adversely affect power angle stability and voltage support capability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel transient power angle reshaping (PAR) control strategy to enhance both the fault ride-through capability and dynamic stability of GFM converters. By introducing a power angle feedback mechanism during fault conditions, the proposed method simultaneously limits fault current and improves transient power angle behavior. The effectiveness of the approach is verified through phase-plane analysis, theoretical derivation under asymmetrical fault conditions, and comprehensive simulation studies.

title: Vector Flow Imaging in Layered Models With a High Speed of Sound Contrast Using Pulse-Echo Ultrasound and Photoacoustics
authors: Caitlin Smith, Guillaume Renaud, Kasper Van Wijk, Jami Shepherd
publish: IEEE Transactions on Ultrasonics
abstract: In this study, we develop vector flow imaging techniques for multi-layered models with a high wavespeed contrast using photoacoustic and ultrasonic imaging. Refraction-corrected delay-and-sum image reconstruction (RC-DAS) enforces Snell’s law to accurately calculate time delays within each layer. We compare RC-DAS against conventional delay-and-sum for vector flow imaging in benchtop phantoms made of transparent polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA) in a water bath. The flow of a slow-moving suspension of carbon microspheres (∼4 mm/s) beneath a PMMA layer is studied using two phantoms, where the PMMA layer has different shapes and thicknesses. We acquire data using interleaved photoacoustic and multi-angle plane wave ultrasound acquisitions measured with a 7.6 MHz linear ultrasound array. Photoacoustic waves are generated by a 1064 nm wavelength nanosecond-pulsed laser at 50 Hz, and multi-angle plane wave ultrasound data are acquired at 100 Hz for eleven steering angles between ±10°. RC-DAS improves the flow speed accuracy, reducing the mean absolute error by 0.56-0.70 mm/s compared to the theoretical parabolic flow profile. The error in direction estimates improves when we use RC-DAS, with the interdecile range reducing by up to 17°. This work emphasises the importance of refraction correction for accurate flow measurements in layered media with photoacoustics and ultrasonic imaging. While both imaging modalities can quantify flow in these multi-layered models, the modality best suited for a specific application will depend on the imaging target and flow dynamics. These techniques show promise for biomedical applications such as intraosseous and transcranial blood flow quantification, and in nondestructive testing to monitor fluid motion.

title: Gender Prediction Based on Large Language Models: An Analysis of Output Patterns and the Impact of Artificial Intelligence
authors: Alaine Tess A. Cabije
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Predicting gender from behavioral features based on large language models (LLMs) is a challenging task with significant implications for personalization, fairness, and privacy. This study evaluates the performance of gender prediction models by examining LLM output patterns, including rethinking assessment and exams (RAE), generic skills (GS) and balanced adoption of artificial intelligence (BA). None of the models showed any meaningful improvement compared to random guessing, considering the validation and test set accuracies were still comparable to chance levels. In particular, GS and BA report the highest correlation (r=0.819, r=0.819), thus suggesting a significant linear relationship between these two variables.However, the variable importance results identify RAE and BA as the most influential predictors in reducing the prediction error, highlighting the need for caution in future research and model development.

title: NNP-NET: Accelerating t-SNE Graph Drawing for Large Static and Dynamic Graphs by Neural Networks
authors: Ilan Hartskeerl, Tamara Mchedlidze, Simon van Wageningen, Peter Vangorp, Alexandru Telea
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Among recent graph drawing (GD) methods, tsNET creates high quality layouts but suffers from a very high runtime due to its underlying reliance on the t-SNE projection technique. We address this problem by presenting NNP-NET, a method that adapts NNP, a projection technique that can project high-dimensional datasets linearly in the data size, to handle both unweighted and weighted graphs, with layout quality being very close to the ground-truth tsNET. We also exploit NNP's built-in out-of-sample ability to enable NNP-NET to project time-dependent (dynamic) graphs while striking a good balance between layout stability and good layout quality. We show experiments that outline how NNP-NET can handle very large graphs – up to 50 million nodes and 108 million edges faster than all other comparable methods we are aware of while also yielding good quality metric values.

title: IWSA 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings

title: Collision-Free Navigation of Mobile Robots via Quadtree-Based Model Predictive Control
authors: Osama Al Sheikh Ali, Sotiris Koutsoftas, Ze Zhang, Knut Åkesson, Emmanuel Dean
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated navigation framework for Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs) that unifies environment representation, trajectory generation, and Model Predictive Control (MPC). The proposed approach incorporates a quadtree-based method to generate structured, axis-aligned collision-free regions from occupancy maps. These regions serve as both a basis for developing safe corridors and as linear constraints within the MPC formulation, enabling efficient and reliable navigation without requiring direct obstacle encoding. The complete pipeline combines safe-area extraction, connectivity graph construction, trajectory generation, and B-spline smoothing into one coherent system. Experimental results demonstrate consistent success and superior performance compared to baseline approaches across complex environments.

title: Design and Implementation of LLC-DCX With 1A/mm2 Current Density for High-Current XPU Power Systems
authors: Xu Zhang, Yufeng Song, Yan Xing, Hongfei Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In modern data centers utilizing a 48 V intermediate DC bus, voltage regulator modules (VRMs) are critical for supplying low-voltage, high-current terminal loads, which impose stringent demands on both efficiency and power density. To overcome these challenges, this paper introduces a heterogeneously integrated design for high-current windings employing a high-frequency transformer. The proposed method substantially reduces winding length, area, and associated losses by leveraging current cancellation and an integrated magnetic core. A vertical power delivery architecture is adopted and functionally verified, which enables lossless current scaling through modular parallel integration. Experimental results demonstrate a power density of 1.05 A/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in the high frequency transformer and a final output current of 450 A in the prototype. This design significantly reduces dependency on conventional PCB windings, particularly on the high-current secondary side, and markedly improves overall power density and system efficiency.

title: Control Strategies for Dual-three-phase Motors at Low Switching Frequencies
authors: Kai Tian, Shijian Cang, Qingjun Hu, Nan Li, Junhong Chen, Wenyuan Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid modulation strategy to address harmonic suppression and load current imbalance in dual three-phase machines under low switching frequencies. The method combines low-frequency 5th/7th harmonic rotating coordinate PI control for targeted harmonic mitigation, and mid/high-frequency synchronous symmetric PWM modulation to optimize harmonic distribution and balance load currents. Simulations confirm significant reductions in total harmonic distortion (THD), torque ripple, and current imbalance, demonstrating an effective solution for high-power, low-harmonic motor control while providing insights for future multiphase drive system optimization.

title: Research on Planar Magnetic Integration Technology for Aerospace Half-Bridge LLC Resonant Converters
authors: Duan Yonghui, Li Xiaochun, Li Mai, Guo Yibo, Shen Ang, Gong Yuke
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: High power density and high frequency of aerospace secondary power supply are the main development directions. The existing hard-switching technology increases switching frequency, which will bring problems such as loss and electromagnetic interference. In response, this paper focuses on the aerospace application environment and proposes a design based on a half-bridge LLC circuit with clamped diodes. It integrates the resonant inductor with the transformer magnetically to reduce the volume of magnetic components. By designing to counteract the magnetic flux in the central column, the hysteresis loss of components is reduced. A variable frequency control strategy is proposed. After the loop compensator performs calculations, the output signal is sent to a voltage-controlled oscillator to achieve variable frequency regulation. The design method of a dual-zero-dual-pole compensator for the LLC circuit is analyzed. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control method is verified on an experimental platform with input voltage 100V and output 12V/120W.

title: Leveraging Explainable AI based Classifiers for Localizing Road Damage with Drone Imagery
authors: Krishna Sameer, Jashmin Swain, Siba K. Udgata
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Automated inspection of roads is essential for public safety and cost-effective maintenance. While drone, UAV images provide useful data, current methods generally rely on computationally heavy object detection models to find pavement damage. This paper explores a simpler and efficient image classification framework to provide a reliable and explainable solution. We propose treating pavement/ road distress identification as an image-level classification problem rather than a detection one. Using the HighRPD drone image dataset, we trained well-known classification models (EfficientNet and ResNet) to identify damage like cracks, blocks, and pits, testing them in both multi-class and multi-label settings. The important part of our work, however, is in validating these classifiers. To understand how our models were making decisions, we used Grad-CAM, an explainable AI (XAI) technique. This Grad-CAM model creates visual heatmaps that pinpoint exactly the location of the image, where the model is looking to make a prediction. This insight is vital for users to trust the system’s results. Our findings show that the classifiers are not only highly accurate but can also localize the relevant damaged regions on the pavement/ road, much like the more complex object detectors do. This work puts forward a method for automated road monitoring that is computationally lighter and easier to interpret, proving that for many practical uses, a properly validated classification model can be just as effective as a complex detection one.

title: Current States of Modern Electric Vehicle Wireless Power Transmission. A Review!
authors: Gyanesh Singh, Manish Sabraj, Seraj Ahamad, Faheem Ahamad, Bhakti Shinde
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Wireless energy transfer between two vehicles has emerged as complex and demanding area of research in recent years. A critical challenges faced in electric vehicles is the repeated charging process, which directly affects battery life cycle management and time efficiency. This research article aims to explore the role of modern power electronic converters-such as choppers, cyclo-converters, inverters-and the application of advanced semiconductor switches. In addition, it highlights the potential of nanomaterials as alternatives to conventional lithium-ion batteries, along with various issues related to charging and discharging in storage systems. The study also investigates the optimization of power electronics-based systems for vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) charging, including standard protocols for switching operations during energy transfer. Key concerns such as energy efficiency, power losses, and transportation challenges are discussed in detail. Furthermore, the article emphasizes the future prospects of V2V power transfer technologies, while addressing critical issues of efficiency and sustainable transportation. In comparison with previous studies, this work provides a comprehensive review of vehicle-tovehicle energy transfer, covering both charging technologies and their implications for sustainable power development. A total of 121 research papers have been analyzed to discuss current trends and advancements in wireless vehicle-to-vehicle charging systems.

title: Comparisons of Different Transient Damping Strategies for Grid-Following Inverters based on Phase-Locked Loop Modifications
authors: Yindong Shi, Jinming Xu, Zihan Ling, Shaojun Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: For grid-following inverters, grid voltage sag may induce the loss of synchronization under weak grids. To ensure the stability of inverters under transient conditions, some transient damping strategies have been proposed to improve the transient synchronization stability. However, the comprehensive comparison is lacking so that it is still unclear how to select a desirable transient damping strategy under practical conditions. Hence, this paper focuses on the comparison of existing transient damping strategies. By conducting an equivalent analysis on the damping loops introduced by them, the transient damping effects and impacts on the system steady-state performance are then compared. Finally, some simulation verifications have been given. The outcome of this study can provide a reference for the selection of transient damping strategies.

title: Study on Actuation Speed Improvement of Pneumatic Artificial Muscle with Exhaust Mechanism
authors: Hayato Yase, Eigo Kohama, Kazuma Kotera, Atsutoshi Ikeda
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Pneumatic actuators exhibit non-linear characteristics in their pressure response to input to electromagnetic valves. This is because the discharge speed of compressed air supplied to the actuator is slower than the charge speed. The objective of this study is to develop an exhaust mechanism that improves the driving speed of pneumatic artificial muscles and mitigates non-linear characteristics. The proposed exhaust mechanism consists primarily of a bellows and a check valve, driven by the pressure passing through them. The exhaust valve is installed at the tip of the artificial muscle, enabling pressure discharge to the atmosphere at the position closest to the artificial muscle. In experiments, the pressure response was compared when a rectangular wave voltage signal was input to the electro-pneumatic valve, verifying the validity of the proposed method. The results confirmed that the artificial muscle equipped with the developed exhaust valve exhibited a slight decrease in charge speed but a significant improvement in exhaust speed compared to the conventional method. Further optimisation of the exhaust valve design is expected to mitigate nonlinear characteristics in the future.

title: A High Voltage Gain Pulse Power Supply Based on Sepic-Marx Circuit
authors: Jie Li, Guilin Wang, Shengrong Zhuo, Yao Wang, Yuhua Du, Yigeng Huangfu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This study proposes a high voltage gain pulse power supply based on the Sepic-Marx circuit, addressing the demands for compactness, portability, and high step-up ratios in low-voltage DC-powered applications such as outdoor portable devices and plasma propulsion. The topology integrates the Sepic structure with the classic Marx generator, retaining its modular design while utilizing Sepic inductors as isolation components to suppress rapid changes of input current. The working principle is analyzed, dividing each switching cycle into four operational modes: inductor charging preparation, high-voltage pulse output, capacitor charging with voltage pumping, and a waiting state. Simulation results in PSIM software show that a five-stage circuit achieves a pulse output of 1.7 kV with a 10 kHz repetition frequency and 5 μs pulse width under a 50V input, yielding a voltage gain of 34. Compared with classical Marx and Boost-Marx generators, the proposed topology requires fewer stages to achieve the same gain, demonstrating distinct advantages in voltage step-up capability and modular simplicity.

title: A Zero-shot framework Applied in the Object Detection Task of Unmanned Laboratory Scenarios
authors: Shenao Zhu, Zaojun Fang, Qiping Zhang, Sichao Ye
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Unmanned laboratories have gained increasing attention as artificial intelligence advances scientific research. However, automated experimental environments still encounter challenges such as complex object categories, recognition delays, and the high cost of annotated data. To mitigate these limitations, a zero-shot object detection and rapid localization framework is proposed. YOLO-World is combined with CLIP to achieve efficient zero-shot recognition, reducing reliance on dataset-specific annotations. For localization, a secondary strategy integrating depth point clouds with SAM-based segmentation ensures accurate three-dimensional coordinate extraction under challenging conditions. Experiments on a self-constructed laboratory dataset show that recognition time is reduced by 64.82% while achieving a recognition accuracy of 95.52%. The results significantly surpass conventional baselines, demonstrating the efficiency and robustness of the framework and its potential for deployment in future unmanned laboratory systems.

title: Common-Mode EMI Suppression Method for Four-Level Inverters under Full Modulation Index
authors: Hui Liu, Dong Jiang, Donghao Liu, Jiwen Gong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Severe electromagnetic interference (EMI) exists in power electronic inverters. This paper analyzes the common-mode voltage (CMV) corresponding to voltage vectors in a neutral point clamped (NPC) four-level inverter and proposes a common-mode voltage (CM) EMI suppression method under full modulation index. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve a 5-40 dB reduction in CM EMI.

title: RANSAC-Integrated Root-MUSIC: A Hybrid Algorithm for High-Precision BLE CS Ranging
authors: Yuan Nie, Zheng Miao, Yizhen Ding, Fengkai Liu, Qijian Zheng, Cheng Huang, Hao Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: The rapid proliferation of IoT applications has increased the demand for high-accuracy indoor positioning. Bluetooth 6.0 introduces Channel Sounding (CS) for phase-based ranging in BLE devices, but interference from Wi-Fi and ZigBee limits accuracy. To address this, we propose a hybrid algorithm combining RANSAC for outlier removal and Root-MUSIC for high-resolution delay estimation. Experiments show that the proposed method significantly reduces ranging errors, with PE90, RMSE, and STD reduced by approximately 74%, 67%, and 86%, respectively, compared with the MVDR method [10]. These results demonstrate that integrating interference mitigation with high-resolution estimation enables reliable submeter BLE localization.

title: A High-Efficiency Reconfigurable Metasurface Unit for UAV Wireless Charging Systems
authors: Le-Yan Shen, Ying-Xuan Huang, Zi-Han Huang, Yi-Jing Sha, Ye Liu, Kai Wang, Jia-Yi Wu, Guan-Long Huang, Xian-Hui He
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Wireless Sensing Technologies and Applications (IWSA2025) - Proceedings
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of two reconfigurable metasurface unit cells operating at 5.8 GHz and 11.6 GHz, respectively, intended for future use in dual-band UAV wireless charging systems. A PIN diode is incorporated at a critical current path within each unit cell, enabling switchable phase states through ON/OFF control. The antenna element features an overall structure of a rectangular ring patch with a triangular protruding structure integrated on one of the patch edges. Simulation results indicate that the unit cells exhibit low reflection loss with |S11| below 0.5 dB. The maximum realized gains reach 13.6 dB at 5.8 GHz and 14.5 dB at 11.6 GHz, and the radiation patterns exhibit strong directivity. The proposed metasurface unit demonstrates low loss, excellent radiation performance, and tunable phase characteristics. The validated single-frequency reconfigurable units provide a solid basis for future development of dual-band programmable reflective metasurfaces.

title: Indirect Torso Posture Control and Force-Guided Singularity Avoidance for Humanoid Teleoperation System using Exoskeleton Cockpit
authors: Hiroki Yoshioka, Tomoya Himeno, Kunio Kojima, Kei Okada
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Cockpit-style exoskeleton systems are a promising approach for the whole-body teleoperation of humanoid robots, yet they face two major challenges. The first is the difficulty of intuitive torso posture control, arising from the operator’s seated and constrained posture. The second is the lack of transparent feedback for kinematic singularities, which do not manifest as physical forces. This paper proposes two novel methods to address these respective challenges: 1) an indirect torso control interface that converts the operator’s foot- actuated torque into the robot’s torso angular velocity, and 2) a haptic feedback scheme that guides the operator’s arm away from singularities based on the gradient of the arm’s manipulability measure. We implemented these methods into a teleoperation system and successfully demonstrated tasks such as picking up an object from the floor and an operator’s active avoidance of singular postures.

title:
IoT-Based CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Gas Air Pollution Monitoring System In Residential Areas
authors: Muhammad Ilham Maulana, Mochammad Haldi Widianto
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Increasingly high air pollution levels have been seen in West Java, indicating the urgent need for a local monitoring system that is widely accessible. The project discusses the development and implementation of a real-time carbon dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) air quality monitoring system for residential locations, which utilizes the Internet of Things (IoT) in order to close this gap. The topic covers the useful calibration of the reasonably priced MQ135 sensor for CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> measurement. The gadget is powered by an ESP32 microcontroller that integrates an MQ135 gas sensor, a DHT22 temperature and humidity sensor, and an anemometer. The MQTT network protocol is made accessible to send data to Blynk, which provides an interactive real-time user interface that enables users to adjust to everyday activities in the environment, and to ThingSpeak, which provides graphical viewing and historical data logging. The successfully implemented system has proved itself to be reliable and cost-effective as well. Considering the testing environment's limited CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> observation fluctuations, the system was able to offer real-time CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration estimations with the help of temperature, humidity, and wind speed—all of which were crucial factors in the context. This research provides a valuable instrument for increasing public awareness and gathering significant environmental data.

title: Classification Systems of Lenke Scoliosis Using Moment Invariant and Decision Tree Method
authors: Anna Nur Nazilah Chamima, Hasimah Ali, Yessi Jusman, Ahmad Hadif Zaidin Bin Samsudin, Mohd Imran Bin Yusof, Devy Putri Mantikha
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study aims to develop a classification system to distinguish between normal spinal X-ray images and Lenke scoliosis using Hu Moment and Zernike Moment feature extraction methods, combined with a Decision Tree classification algorithm. These methods are effective in capturing shape descriptors invariant to rotation, scaling, and translation. The research stages include preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification. Images were enhanced using CLAHE and then processed using Hu and Zernike Moment extraction. Results were classified using Fine, Medium, and Coarse Decision Tree models. Experiments showed that Hu Moment with Fine DT and Medium DT achieved the highest testing accuracy of 98.1%. This system offers great potential as an efficient tool for early scoliosis detection in clinical settings.

title: WECAR: An End-Edge Collaborative Inference and Training Framework for WiFi-Based Continuous Human Activity Recognition
authors: Rong Li, Tao Deng, Siwei Feng, He Huang, Juncheng Jia, Di Yuan, Keqin Li
publish: ACM Transactions on Sensor Networks
abstract: None

title: AI Ethics & Policy Column: Top AI Ethics and Policy Issues of 2025 and What to Expect in 2026
authors: Larry R. Medsker
publish: AI Matters
abstract: None

title: A Systematic Comparison of Machine Learning Regressors for PM2.5 Concentration Forecasting: An AutoML-Driven Approach
authors: Ravi Shanker Pathak, Vinay Pathak, Amit Rai
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Fine particulate matter with a diameter of 2.5 micrometers or less (PM2.5) poses critical risks to public health and the environment, necessitating the development of accurate and timely predictive models. This study presents a systematic and broad comparative evaluation of over 20 machine learning regression algorithms for the task of hourly PM2.5 concentration forecasting. The analysis leverages a benchmark dataset from Beijing, which includes meteorological features alongside pollutant measurements. The experimental workflow, from data preprocessing to model training and evaluation, was streamlined using the PyCaret automated machine learning (AutoML) library. The results demonstrate a clear performance hierarchy among algorithmic families, with gradient boosting models achieving the highest predictive accuracy. These models have better prediction accuracy compared to other linear and bagging and boosting methods. The lower performance of decision tree regressor shows that the ensemble techniques is more effective in mitigating overfitting and building robust models for complex environmental data. This paper shows comprehensive performance baseline for a wide array of algorithms on pollution dataset which could guide future studies for optimal environmental informatics.

title: Toward Hybrid LLM-Assisted Vulnerability Detection for Continuous Integration: A Bias-Free Framework with a Context-Rich Dataset
authors: Ashmit Avinash Patil, Lavesh Anil Patil, Radhakrishna Rambola
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The recent research indicates that Large Language Models (LLMs) are more effective than conventional static analyzers when it comes to identifying vulnerabilities, but the application to real life suffers due to dataset bias and the necessity to limit the CI/CD latency. In the current paper, we introduce the concept of HYDRA, a hybrid solution that integrated pair analysis of code informed by an LLM with a wellchosen dynamic tests, occurring within 5-minute CI windows. The proposed hybridization could be achieved by the means of the Cross-Lang-Vuln (XLV) corpus that we build up to include <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 9. 4 K}$</tex> pairs of quality checked C, Rust, Go, JavaScript functions in 162 CWE tags and full interprocedural context. Comparative analytics demonstrates that HYDRA results in 21.3 percent higher precision@10 and 42.8 percent low false positive percentage over CodeBERT, and 42.8 percent low false positive percentage higher than CodeQL with CI latency limits. At the address, there are the reproducible artifacts that were published publicly, such as the dataset, Docker containers, and GitHub Actions, which can be deployed as quickly as possible.

title: Power Balance Droop Control Strategy for DC Interconnection Energy Router in Low Voltage Distribution Station
authors: Xiangdong Sun, Zhixuan Wang, Chen Hou, Feng Gao, Biying Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the widespread integration of distributed energy resources and diversified loads, the problem of energy imbalance between low-voltage distribution stations is becoming increasingly prominent, which can easily lead to uneven transformer loading rates. In order to prevent transformer overload, reduce the risk of power grid faults, and improve power supply reliability, the low-voltage distribution station DC interconnection energy router is used as the core, a power balancing droop control strategy for energy routers based on the curve between the load rate of the substation transformer and the reference DC-bus voltage is proposed in this paper. On the DC side of the energy router is a dual active bridge converter, and droop control is used. The DC-bus voltage runs at the intersection of two droop curves, and adjusting the reference voltage of droop control can change the transmission power capacity and direction. To reflect the degree of energy shortage in the substation area, the transformer load rate in the substation area is combined with the reference DC-bus voltage for droop control to construct a curve between transformer load rate and reference DC-bus voltage. By monitoring the load rate of transformers in real-time and dynamically adjusting the intercept of the droop curve, the direction and capacity of power transmission are autonomously adjusted to achieve relative balance of transformer load in the substation. The simulation results have verified the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Self-Healing Autonomic IoT Security: A Multiclass Ensemble-Based Intrusion Detection System
authors: Swati Das, Suvendu Chandan Nayak
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) has increased security threats in connected devices, which require effective intrusion detection systems (IDS). This paper suggests an adaptive, lightweight, and ensemble-based IDS system for IoT that takes advantage of Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and HistGradientBoosting classifiers. It is trained offline on a high-performance system using a feature selection step based on LightGBM’s importance in lowering the dimensionality and thus facilitating low-latency inference on resource-limited IoT devices. Our solution is built for multiclass classification and identifies and separates $\mathbf{3 3}$ distinct attack types and benign traffic. A MAPE-K (Monitor-Analyze-Plan-Execute over a shared Knowledge Base) loop is embedded for self-healing, promoting real-time adaptability with low on-device overhead through offloading retraining to a remote system. Tested on a real-time dataset subset of CICIoT2023, the framework attains 99.03 % ensemble accuracy, a 0.99 weighted F1 score, and robust anomaly recovery through the MAPE-K cycle, exhibiting superior performance and usability for real-world IoT security.

title: Digital Resonant Controller and Notch filter Design for LCL-Type Three-Phase PWM Rectifiers
authors: Mengyao Wang, Haitao Yang, Qiyan Qu, Yongchang Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: At present, the control technology of LCL-type three-phase voltage-source rectifiers mainly focuses on improving the quality and stability of the grid-connected current. The frequency response of the LCL filter exhibits resonance peaks, with a -180° phase shift occurring at the resonant frequency, which can cause output current oscillations and even lead to system instability. This paper focus on three-phase PWM rectifiers system and active damping strategy based on LCL filter is studied in detail. The mathematical model of three-phase PWM rectifiers based on LCL filter in two-phase static α − β coordinates is established primarily, then the resonant controller effectively reduces current steady-state error. To avoid power loss introduced by damping resistors, a virtual resistor is used instead of an actual resistor. A damping method based on a notch filter enhances system damping, reduces oscillations, and finally verifies the effectiveness of adding a notch filter to the resonant regulator through digitalization.

title: Utilizing Knowledge in Vision-Language Model for Robotic Grasp Force Estimation through Robot-to-Human Image Translation
authors: Shohei Hagane, Shigeaki Goto, Yoshihiro Ohama
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In recent years, general-purpose robots have been introduced into domains requiring delicate manipulation, such as materials experimentation. While advances have been made in automating specific processes, generalized robotic pick-and-place operations still pose a challenge due to the diversity of target objects and the need for appropriate force control. This study proposes a novel approach for zero-shot estimation of target grasping force by utilizing the prior knowledge about human motions embedded in Vision-Language Model (VLM). The key idea is to convert robot manipulation images into human-action images using a style transfer approach based on a fine-tuned Variational Auto-Encoder (VAE), enabling the VLM to better infer grasping force requirements. The VLM, specifically GPT-4o, is prompted to estimate target grasping force in discrete categories (no grasp, light grip, firm grip).Experimental results demonstrate that converting robot images into human representations improves the accuracy not only of target grasping force estimation but also of understanding the target objects. Furthermore, the inclusion of target object information in the prompt improves estimation accuracy across all input image types. These findings highlight the effectiveness of utilizing human-knowledge-trained VLM for robotic force control and open new avenues for general-purpose, cost-efficient manipulation without relying on large-scale robot force datasets.

title: Quantum Subroutine for Efficient SVD in Block-Diagonalized Precoding with fewer ancilla qubits
authors: Xinying Li, Xian Lu, Yongmei Li, Xin Yi, Shuai Hou, Chengkang Pan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Computing Technologies, Communications and Networking (CTCNet)
abstract: We propose a resource-efficient quantum accelerator for block-diagonalized precoding in massive multi-user MIMO downlink. By integrating quantum resonant singular value decomposition, the scheme replaces both classical SVD stages in block-diagonalized precoding with a single resonant-transition protocol that operates in poly-logarithmic time and requires only two ancilla qubits irrespective of target accuracy. The method removes the need for any prior knowledge of the overlap between the initial state and the target subspace by leveraging simulated transitions that let the quantum simulator evolve through large regions of Hilbert space, including states orthogonal to the initial one. In the current noisy intermediate-scale quantum era, the scheme may contribute to exploring real-time precoding in future wireless networks.

title: Using Eye Gaze and Body Location to Organize and Retrieve Information in Mixed Reality
authors: Yuu Takahashi, Takeshi Sasaki
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study proposes a new method for organizing and accessing information in MR environments by anchoring data to parts of the human body (such as the head or hands). The system utilizes Microsoft HoloLens 2 and eye-tracking technology to enable users to intuitively access virtual information through gaze-based operations. This approach introduces a relative spatial coordinate system based on the user’s body. User studies were conducted to verify the effectiveness of body-based tagging and information management in MR spaces. The results confirmed that this information access method is usable without issues, and it was revealed that rules exist governing the relationship between the access time and location of body-tagged information. These results suggest that body-based information tagging has the potential to enhance the usability of MR for everyday tasks.

title: EfficientNet-B0 Based Deep Transfer Learning for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification
authors: Sachin Ahuja, Abhishek Kumar, Pramod Singh Rathore
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is an advance and permanent eye disease, and the most usual avoidable eye condition of diabetic people globally. It is imperative to reduce its effect by timely and accurate diagnosis. A deep learning-based classification method based on the EfficientNet-B0 architecture with its compound scaling technique to achieve a balance of network depth, widths, and resolution to achieve a good performance on minimal computational resources is offered in this research study. In contrast to other bigger models including ResNet or VGG, the EfficientNet-B0 shows high accuracy. By using the advantage of transfer learning, a publicly-shared diabetic retinopathy dataset was used to finetune the pre-trained EfficientNet-B0 model and categorize retinal fundus images into five stages of DR. Embarking on the accuracy of testing and loss, the model attained a testing accuracy of 91.99 percent and the loss of 0.1417, confirming the robustness and generalization capacity of the model. Model consistency was also proven by visual graphs showing training/testing accuracy and loss over epochs. This research shows that EfficientNet-B0, besides producing the best-known results, offers a rather light, scalable method of screening DR, particularly in resource-limited clinical environments.

title: SMARC: A State-Repairing Multi-Agent Resilient Consensus Scheme
authors: Shanyao Ren, Chip-Hong Chang, Jianwei Liu, Zhicheng Sun, Dongyu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: In this paper, we analyze a recent algorithm for resilient consensus control in distributed multi-agent systems. While effective in theory, its reliance on security in communication and strong connectivity assumptions limits its practicality in dynamic electronic and cyber-physical systems, such as embedded device networks and uncrewed platforms. To address these limitations, we propose a State-repairing Multi-agent Resilient Consensus (SMARC) scheme to eliminate the need for normal agents to collect trustworthy state values from a fixed bounded threshold of neighbors to achieve reliable consensus. The core innovation of SMARC is a decentralized state-repair mechanism, which enables agents to obtain additional information from their reachable sets to repair unavailable or corrupted state values of malicious or faulty agents, and autonomously adjust their convergence speed. Additionally, without negatively impacting the consensus performance, SMARC employs a noise-masked surface state to protect the initial states of agents from eavesdropping. This approach avoids extra storage and reduces computations without adhering to strict security prerequisites, making it more suitable for resource-constrained electronic systems compared to existing methods. Theoretical convergence and security proofs demonstrate that SMARC can successfully resist passive attacks while ensuring accurate convergence on multi-dimensional data. Most importantly, from small- to large-scale networks, SMARC achieves a three- to four-fold increase in convergence speed compared to the most competitive recent state-of-the-art resilient consensus algorithm in both passive and active attacks. A prototype electronic of a MAS was also built using six Raspberry Pi devices to validate its performance and robustness in practical environments.

title: Advanced Database Testing and Verification Using Automated Tools and Vulnerability Analysis
authors: Manish Mahendra Patil, Radha Krishna Rambola, Sanket Kishor Mali
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Modern applications depend on complex database systems that are difficult to test due to intricate data dependencies, distributed architectures, and dynamic security threats. This paper discusses detailed methods for automating database testing and verification. The approach utilizes symbolic execution to automatically generate test cases, a mutation-based tool (DBGriller) to generate diverse database states, semantic analysis to identify SQL injection vulnerabilities, and data-aware unit testing for CRUD operations. The findings are promising: symbolic execution achieved full branch significantly enhance the reliability and security of databases, enabling their integration into continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines.

title: Fuzzy-Adaptive Force-Compliant Control and Sensorless Estimation of a Hybrid Aerial Manipulator for Contact-Based Pipeline Repair
authors: Ezeldin N. Moustafa, Mitsuhiro Kamezaki, Shota Miyake, Shigeki Sugano
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Aerial manipulators offer a compelling solution for maintenance tasks in hazardous or hard-to-reach environments. Contact-based operations, such as pipeline crack repair, demand not only precise trajectory tracking but also stable and controlled force application when interacting with cylindrical surfaces subject to friction. These dual objectives are challenging due to the aerial system’s inherent instability, strong nonlinearities, and sensitivity to disturbances. While impedance control enables direct interaction, it replaces the position control loop that can reduce robustness under dynamic conditions. In contrast, conventional admittance control wraps around existing loops but suffers from limited adaptability and challenging gain tuning. This paper proposes a unified force-compliant control framework for a quadrotor equipped with a hybrid manipulator. An adaptive backstepping-adaptive fast terminal sliding mode controller (AB-AFTSMC) governs the inner trajectory loop, ensuring reliable tracking performance. Over this structure, a fuzzy-admittance outer loop modulates the desired reference trajectories based on desired force, enabling compliant interaction during contact without altering the underlying control architecture. Interaction forces are estimated using a lightweight disturbance observer (DOB), enabling low-computation, sensorless feedback without the weight or complexity of force sensors. Validation is carried out in MATLAB Simscape through a 3D physics-based model. The results demonstrate reliable tracking, compliant force build-up, and consistent free-flight-to-contact transitions.

title: Voltage Control and Common-Mode Interference Suppression for Flying Capacitor Four-Level NPC Inverter
authors: Hui Liu, Dong Jiang, Donghao Liu, Chengjun Li, Zeyuan Du
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Multi-level inverters are widely used in high-voltage, high-power applications. Capacitor voltage fluctuations can degrade power quality, while electromagnetic interference (EMI) generated by switching operations may disrupt normal system operation. This paper proposes a novel four-level inverter topology capable of actively controlling clamp point voltages, along with a suppression method for common-mode (CM) EMI. Simulation results demonstrate that compared to four-level NPC inverters, the proposed FC-4NPC reduces capacitor voltage fluctuations by over 80% and attenuates CM EMI by 40dB.

title: Virtual direct power control for half-bridge-active-clamp converter with wide voltage gain and fast transient response
authors: Qian Kou, Jin Sha, Ruilong Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper examines the half-bridge-active-clamp converter as a solution to the efficiency and transient response challenges faced by dual active bridge converters in high voltage gain applications. The HBAC converter is characterized by a smaller transformer turns ratio, diminished current stress. A thorough analysis of the transmission power and soft-switching characteristics of the single-phase-shift modulated HBAC converter is conducted. Based on these analyses, a virtual direct power control is introduced for the HBAC converter to enhance transient performance during fluctuations in input voltage and load. By introducing a virtual voltage, the corresponding phase-shift ratio is derived for power regulation, achieving fast transient response. A 2.8kW HBAC converter is built on RT-LAB OP5700 platform, and the experimental results verify that the proposed VDPC achieves faster transient response compare to conventional PI control.

title:
Flexible State-Aware Planning for Robust Object Placement in Home Tidy-Up with Autonomous Mobile Manipulators
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Natee Buttawong, Kosei Isomoto, Hakaru Tamukoh
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper aims to achieve flexible placement onto tray-type storage areas using a mobile manipulator. In a previous study, tray-packing was accomplished by preparing object masks in advance before tidy-up and planning the arrangement by matching these masks with the available tray space. However, such an approach faced difficulties in handling unknown objects and postures for which prior information could not be obtained, and was unable to take the current state of the tray into account, resulting in limited flexibility. The proposed system estimates masks for previously unregistered objects from an RGB-D image, verifies and corrects object posture to a default, builds a tray mask from the current state of the tray, and computes collision-aware, space-efficient placements via a 2D irregular packing algorithm. The proposed system performed a tidy-up on five objects placed in various postures, achieving a success rate of 93%. These results indicate improved flexibility, robustness, and practicality for real-world tidy-up compared to the previous system.

title: Exploring Factors Influencing Cybersickness and Workload in VR Robot Teleoperation Systems under Spatial and Temporal Noise
authors: Mark Roland Aquino, Yuto Hattori, Yutaka Nakamura, Yuya Okadome
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Robot remote control systems proposed for society have included approaches that utilize VR equipment, but concerns related to cybersickness still persist. In this paper, we develop a robot remote control VR simulation system that implements spatial and temporal noises, robot vibration during moving, and network connection lag-induced delays and stalls. Using this system, we investigated factors influencing the onset of cybersickness and operational workload during remote control. The experimental results suggest that communication lag may influence the onset of cybersickness. It is considered that the unpredictable nature of stalls prevents user adaptation. Regarding workload, individual spatial or temporal noise had little effect. On the other hand, a combination of robot oscillation and communication lag led to a stronger perception of workload, suggesting that the interaction of these factors increases the operational burden. Designing a human-friendly control interface of a teleoperation mobile robot by using these insights is our future project.

title: FEM-Based Optimization of the Mechanical Properties of a Soft Robotic Neck
authors: Alberto Rodríguez–Sanz, Gerson Lipa, Baptiste Rouquette, Jorge Muñoz, Concepción A. Monje
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Finite element modeling (FEM) provides a powerful and flexible approach for accurately simulating soft robots and their dynamics. This work presents a simulation-based framework for identifying the mechanical properties of a 3D-printed, cable-driven soft robotic neck made of thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) with a Shore hardness of 82A. A Bayesian optimization method is employed during FEM simulations in the open-source SOFA framework to estimate material parameters by minimizing the discrepancy between experimental data and simulation results. Cyclic traction tests were conducted on a custom test bench to obtain force and displacement data from one of the three actuation cables of the neck. Based on these observations, a hyperelastic constitutive model was selected, and its parameters optimized. The simulated response remained consistent with experimental measurements, indicating that the model captures the essential mechanical behavior of the system. This approach opens a practical alternative to direct material characterization and lays the foundation for future extensions involving viscoplastic models adaptive force–based control.

title:
Soft-Switching Analysis and Distortion Suppression of SWISS Rectifier Considering C
                    <sub>oss</sub>
                    Nonlinearity of the Switches
authors: Gaofeng Shi, Shuaixuan Liu, Shaojun Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The influence of the dead-time designed to realize the soft switching of the T-type single active bridge SWISS rectifier (SAB SR) on the input current distortion is analyzed. Considering the nonlinear characteristics of the MOSFETs’ output capacitance (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">oss</inf>), the equivalent capacitance is used to describe this characteristic, and the energy-based method is used to determine the soft switching condition of the T-bridge. The soft-switching condition analysis results are used to set the dead-time reasonably and reduce the input current distortion, which is verified by experiments.

title: Enhancing Semi-supervised Texture Defect Detection with Pseudo-Label Optimization: An Improved SoftTeacher Approach on DAGM2007
authors: Kedi Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To address the challenges of high annotation costs and complex background interference in industrial defect detection, this paper proposes a semi-supervised framework integrating Dynamic Class-aware Thresholding (DCT) and Wavelet Transform Convolution (WTConv). DCT mitigates class imbalance by dynamically adjusting category-specific confidence thresholds, while WTConv enhances defect-background decoupling through frequency-domain feature fusion. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves 0.593 mAP and 0.53 recall with only 20% labeled data, significantly outperforming fully super-vised baselines. Although challenges remain in ultra-rare defect detection and edge-computing deployment, this study establishes a critical technical pathway for practical semi-supervised learning in industrial quality inspection.

title: Delay Optimized Hybrid 4 to 1 DGM-MUX Using Novel DGM-OR Gate at 180nm
authors: Ramsha Suhail, Richa Yadav, Anushka Chourey, Mahak Sahu, Pragya Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The increasing requirement for high speed and low power digital circuits in current VLSI design calls for innovative strategies that surpass the limitations of conventional CMOS logic. This study offers a unique architecture for a Dual Gate MOSFET (DG MOSFET)-based OR gate (DGMOR), implemented utilizing a newly developed DG MOSFET-based MCML inverter (DGMI), to create an efficient 4-to-1 multiplexer (DGM- MUX). The DGM-I and DGM-OR circuits are first developed, simulated, and examined separately. A hybrid DGMMUX is then created and assessed for important performance indicators, including Propagation Delay (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</inf>), Power Consumption (PWR), Power Delay Product (PDP), and Energy Delay Product (EDP), at the 180 nm technology node and a supply voltage of 0.7 V using the HSPICE simulator. The proposed DGM-MUX achieves a significantly reduced delay of 0.009 ns, improved power efficiency with a consumption of 9.96 µW, and a low PDP of 9.96 fJ. Additionally, the suggested circuit's applicability for dependable, high-performance VLSI applications is demonstrated by robustness study across a broad temperature range and process variation analysis to evaluate delay variability.

title: Design and Optimization of a Stand-Alone Hybrid Renewable Energy System for off-Grid Health Centers
authors: Diksha, Sakshi Sachdeva, Saloni, Bhavnesh Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This study presents a stand-alone hybrid renewable energy system that combines different clean energy sources to supply electricity without depending on the main grid to health centers. The designed system provides a reliable and uninterrupted power supply for the health centers in the off-grid village of Dulrasar, Rajasthan, India having Latitude 28° 17.2′ N and Longitude 74° 31.0′ E with estimated load of 200.59 kWh/day. The proposed system architecture investigates amalgamation of solar photovoltaic (PV) array, a wind turbine, a battery to ensure consistent power availability, leveraging the region's solar and wind potential. Technically viable configuration that provides complete energy autonomy for a critical rural healthcare facility in an economically sensible and ecofriendly manner has been examined. The findings offer a robust framework for improving healthcare infrastructure in unelectrified areas, directly enhancing service quality and contributing to community well-being. The system is modeled and optimized using HOMER pro software, taking into account climatic data from NASA and load data specific to the region.

title: Ride-Through Control Strategy of Wind and Solar Energy Storage Grid-Connected System Based on Power Control Loop Coefficient Optimization
authors: Duotong Yang, Jun Ge, Yixing Wang, Zhenglei Wang, Ning Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In order to fulfill low-voltage fault ride-through and optimize dynamic response in transient process, this paper proposes a strategy based on adaptive optimization of virtual synchronous machine control coefficients. Firstly, the VSG topology and control system are established, and the fault ride-through principle under virtual impedance control is analyzed. Then, based on the virtual coefficients of VSG, the curve of dynamic response and operating characteristics of the inverter are given. A control method with adaptive coefficients is proposed to accomplish fault ride-through and optimization of dynamic response. Finally, a VSG simulation model using adaptive optimized fault ride-through strategy is built in PSCAD. The simulation result verifies the feasibility of the strategy.

title: Semantic-Aware Obstacle Tracking and Avoidance for Ceiling-Mounted Healthcare Robots
authors: Marco Masannek, Rolf Schmidt, Andreas Deinlein, Thorsten Gecks, Stefan May, Andreas Nuchter
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Automation of healthcare workflows and devices demands safe and trustworthy robotic behavior, particularly in environments shared with patients and medical staff. For ceiling-mounted imaging robots, the key challenge lies in perceiving and monitoring the 3D workspace to plan safe, collision-free motions around people and equipment. Beyond simple obstacle avoidance, semantic understanding is essential to distinguish between object types — such as patients, walking aids, or medical tools — and to adapt motion behavior accordingly. We address this challenge with a semantic-aware obstacle tracking and avoidance pipeline that extends prior 2D semantic navigation concepts into full 3D space. The approach combines 2D semantic segmentation with depth projection to estimate object positions and dimensions in real time from RGB-D data. These detections are fused in a tracking module to build a continuous, semantic world model from which class-dependent safety margins are derived. The resulting information enables adaptive motion planning that increases distance from high-risk objects (e.g., persons) or reduces velocity when close interaction is required. Experiments on a real ceiling-mounted robot in laboratory scenarios demonstrate the system’s ability to enhance safety, predictability, and contextual awareness during automated healthcare procedures.

title: Role of Sampling Frequency in BiLSTM-Driven ECG Heart Disease Diagnosis
authors: Smita Kadam, S. D. Chede
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Cardiovascular disease remains a leading cause of mortality, motivating precise and efficient diagnostic pipelines. This study leverages electrocardiogram (ECG) signals with a Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) network augmented by temporal attention to detect cardiac abnormalities. The pipeline includes band-pass and powerline filtering, z-score normalization, Nyquist-aware resampling, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex>-peak-based beat segmentation to stabilize learning. A frequency sweep from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100-800 ~\text{Hz}$</tex> quantifies the effect of sampling on accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. Performance increases with frequency, with diminishing gains beyond <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 500-600 ~\text{Hz}$</tex>; a practical operating range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300-500 ~\text{Hz}$</tex> offers a strong accuracy-latency trade-off suitable for real-time or wearable scenarios. Experiments on representative ECG corpora (e.g., two-lead and twelve-lead settings) demonstrate that the BiLSTM-attention model captures forward-backward temporal context and remains robust under anti-aliased downsampling. The work delivers frequency-guided design recommendations and a reproducible preprocessing-modeling recipe for scalable, reliable ECG-based heart disease detection.

title:
State Estimation and Linear Discrete Model Predictive Control for the Hydration Process in a Lime-Based Thermochemical Energy Storage System
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Anja Rentz, Venizelos E. Sourmelis T., Viktor Kühl, Matthias Schmidt, Marc Linder, Oliver Sawodny
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Thermochemical energy storage based on the reversible reaction of CaO and H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O to Ca(OH)<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is a promising solution for sustainable storage of excessive renewable energy. For efficient and safe discharge of the system (hydration of CaO), a control strategy is necessary. Starting from a nonlinear dynamic model, the system is linearized and discretized to enable the design of a Kalman filter for state estimation and a Model Predictive Controller (MPC). Simulation results demonstrate that the Kalman filter provides accurate state reconstruction and effectively filters measurement noise from the system outputs. Four different MPC objectives, targeting key temperatures and thermal power, are evaluated. All variants show good tracking performance and are suitable for real-time application. A comparison between the linear discrete-time MPC with state estimation and a nonlinear continuous-time MPC with full state information reveals no significant performance loss, while achieving a reduction in computation time.

title: A Data-Assisted Method for Control Parameters Optimization of Series Resonant DAB Converters
authors: Zheng Zexu, Zhang Hang, Wu Xiaohua
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The efficiency optimization of Series Resonant Dual-Active Bridge (SR-DAB) converters has drawn significant attention due to their multiple control degrees of freedom and high performance potential. However, due to unmodeled factors, a notable gap often exists between theoretically calculated optimal control parameters and those found on actual hardware. This gap leads to suboptimal results in practice. To address this, this paper proposes a data-assisted method for SR-DAB converters. This method combines two sources of information: a fast analytical loss model serves as the "low-fidelity" source, while a small set of valuable experimental data from the hardware provides "high-fidelity" information. The method operates through two core components: Firstly, a Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) surrogate model is trained to learn and correct the discrepancy between the analytical model’s predictions and the physical reality. Secondly, an Expected Improvement (EI) acquisition function then utilizes the GPR model’s predictions and uncertainties to intelligently guide the selection of the next experimental point. This ensures the optimization process rapidly converges to the global efficiency peak with a minimal number of hardware evaluations. Finally, the proposed method was validated through experiments on a 1kW SR-DAB hardware prototype. In the experiments, our method was able to accurately and efficiently approximate the converter’s true optimal control parameters within 10 iterations, reducing the Sim2Real gap.

title: Multi-Attribute AI-based Storytelling Framework with IoT Deployment for Real-Time Narration: A Parameter-Efficient Approach
authors: Sankalp Nayak, Samriddhi Sharma, Aradhana Behura, Prabhu Prasad Dev, Jitendra Kumar Rout, Madhukrishna Priyadarsini
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Storytelling is an important framework for entertainment, sharing culture, and emotion; yet, AI techniques still struggle with some challenges: limited controllability and resource-intensive deployment. This research demonstrates a multi-objective-based storytelling framework that generates narratives conditioned on age group, genre, emotional tone, and paragraph length. Built on LLaMA v3-8B with Quantized Low-Rank Adapter (QLoRA) fine-tuning, the framework updates fewer than 1% of parameters while preserving efficiency. The proposed framework extends the DesigningPrompts corpus with ~31K labelled interesting stories using open-source techniques and heuristics, enabling modern conditioning. A multi-stage strategy—baseline fine-tuning, instruction-tuned prompting, and hybrid outline–expansion with End-of-Sequence (EOS) enforcement—enhances structural completeness and controllability. Stage 3 reduces incomplete results by 40% and boosts human-rated coherence by 25% over the baseline model. This deployment uses a hybrid edge-cloud framework: inference runs server-side, while a Raspberry Pi hardware gives a lightweight UI and real-time narration. The outcomes demonstrate that parameter-efficient fine–tuning, integrated with sensor deployment, enables cost-effective storytelling for education and infotainment-based applications.

title: ShoesMimic: One-Policy Whole-Body Control for Multi-Shoe Humanoid Locomotion
authors: Hanze Hu, Luying Feng, Wei Chen, Tianhong Wang, Yaochu Jin, Silu Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Humanoid robots are commonly designed with rigid and flat feet. Although this structure simplifies control, it often leads to large landing impacts and contact noise during walking, which limits the range of potential applications and shortens mechanical lifespan. Although wearing shoes can help alleviate the above problems by providing cushioning and compliance, it also introduces new challenges such as inconsistency among shoe types and reduced walking stability. To address this issue, a unified framework named ShoesMimic is proposed for humanoid walking control across multiple shoe types. In this framework, variations in sole geometry and physical properties are systematically introduced during both training and deployment, allowing stable whole-body gait control to be maintained under diverse contact conditions. A humanoid shoe asset library is constructed, containing various shoe types. During physical experiments, multiple shoes are tested, and stable gait and posture control are maintained by ShoesMimic using a single policy. These results demonstrate robustness and noise resistance under changes in contact stiffness and geometry. The study provides the first systematic solution for humanoid walking control across different shoe types and establishes a foundation for future research on physics-consistent imitation learning and contact-adaptive control.

title: DeepSense++: Robust HAR with Missing Data
authors: Suryakangeyan Kandasamy Gowdaman, Sayma Akther
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using wearable sensors is increasingly applied in healthcare, sports, and intelligent environments. Performance is however hindered in the majority of the cases by absent sensor values, class imbalance, and inter-subject variability. We present a robust HAR pipeline that utilizes Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for reducing dimensions and Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for realistic imputation of absent values and minority-class oversampling. This is integrated into an improved DeepSense architecture with convolutional and recurrent layers for spatial–temporal feature learning. Comparisons on the OPPORTUNITY dataset, in terms of K-Fold, Leave-One-Session-Out (LOSEO), and Leave-One-Subject-Out (LOSO) schemes, demonstrate improved accuracy (+3.7%) and F1 score (+2.9%) over baseline DeepSense. The results highlight the applicability of hybrid imputation-augmentation pipelines in bringing HAR to practical, noisy sensing scenarios.

title: Smart Automated Irrigation System with Pest Control & IoT Monitoring
authors: Yuvraj Ware, Devata Anekar, Sampada Yanpallewar, Yash Agrawal, Yash Lulla, Riya Wankhede, Yash Mantri
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This project showcases an affordable and eco-friendly smart agricultural system that integrates automated irrigation and pest repellent applications based on IoT and sensor technology. The system’s backbone is an ESP32 microcontroller, which manages real-time monitoring and control operations. Soil water content is monitored with a capacitive sensor; if water falls below a certain level (e.g., 40%), ESP32 will energize a relay to turn on a water pump, allowing accurate irrigation. For pest management, an IR sensor monitors movement surrounding crops and emits visual (LED) and audio (buzzer) signals to scare away pests, thereby minimizing crop damage without the use of chemical pesticides. The system is interfaced with the Blynk IoT platform for wireless control and data monitoring using a smartphone. With affordability (less than ₹3000) and low power requirements, the system can be scaled for applications in small farms, greenhouses, or city gardens. The proposed system is adoptable in sustainable agriculture through water conservation, labor reduction, and encouragement of green pest control management.

title: Tissue Engineering: Integrating IoT and Nanotechnology for Tissue Repair and Regeneration
authors: Bhavvya Mehta, Charvi Gupta, Vasudev Grover, Khushboo Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Tissue engineering, a field of study where engineering, medicine, and biology come together as an interdisciplinary one, aims at creating functional substitutes for damaged organs and tissues with the help of principles from each of these respective fields. What follows is the discussion of core concepts involved in tissue regeneration including cell proliferation, scaffold design, and bioreactor use, as well as the integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies with realtime monitoring and the realization of personalized therapeutics. Besides, the application of nanomaterials,such as graphene and metal nanoparticles, is highlighted for their ability to enhance mechanical strength and cell adhesion in engineered tissues. Significant challenges to clinical translation, including high cost of production, low scalability, ethical concerns about the use of nanomaterials, data privacy, and technical capabilities of IoT devices, are seriously debated. Finally, the paper spotlights the potential of sustainable practices, such as green synthesis and adopting the 3Rs (Reduce, Reuse, Recycle), to propel the field toward practical and customized regenerative medicine solutions.

title: Research on Low-Voltage Ride-Through of Large-Capacity Photovoltaic Grid-Connected System Based on Cascaded Power Electronic Transformer
authors: Jianbiao Lin, Bofeng Xu, Ke Luo, Beihai Chen, Quance Wang, Feng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: With the proliferation of high-penetration renewable energy systems and the development of next-generation power grids, grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) technologies have garnered significant attention. Conventional line-frequency transformers, characterized by large size, low no-load efficiency, and limited controllability, increasingly impede the efficient integration of clean energy. In response, the power electronic transformer (PET), which integrates power conversion and high-frequency magnetic technology, has emerged as a promising alternative. For high-voltage direct-connection high-capacity applications, grid-side voltage sags are inevitable, making low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) capability essential in PV grid-connected systems. Traditional phase-locked loops (PLLs) are inadequate under fault conditions due to their inability to accurately track voltage phase during unbalanced sags. This paper explores an LVRT strategy for cascaded PET-based PV systems using a decoupled double synchronous reference frame (DDSRF) method.
