authors: Tomoya Kouno, Yuichiro Kasahara, Kota Akinari, Akinosuke Tsutsumi, Takayoshi Hachijo, Shunsuke Kimura, Yutaro Fukase, Yuki Miyashita, Takashi Yokoshima, Taro Abe, Daisuke Endo, Takeshi Hashimoto, Keiji Nagatani, Genki Yamauchi, Ryo Kurazume
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: We are developing a Cyber-Physical System (CPS) for earthwork sites, called ROS2-TMS for Construction, utilizing OPERA, an autonomous construction platform under development by the Public Works Research Institute. In this paper, as a case study of the system’s field implementation, we automated the loading of cohesive soil into a hopper during soil improvement work using a hydraulic excavator. Experimental results demonstrated that the system was able to dynamically determine excavation positions and achieve continuous operation for more than one hour. Furthermore, we conducted an additional slope-collapsing experiment using a hydraulic excavator, which demonstrated the applicability of the proposed system to diverse earthwork tasks.

title: Edge—Cloud Multi-Agent Framework for Real-Time Thermal Runaway Prevention in Lithium Iron Phosphate Batteries Using Lightweight Deep Models
authors: Bal krishana Soni, Balram Kasniya, Prashant Mathur
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This paper presents an innovative edge-cloud multiagent system (MAS) integrated with lightweight GRU+LSTM deep learning models for real-time anomaly detection and thermal runaway prevention in Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) batteries. Edge agents on Jetson Nano perform low-latency inference, while the cloud layer conducts long-term analytics and supports over-the-air(OTA) updates. The proposed system achieves 97.8% classification accuracy, detects thermal deviations 8 cycles before runaway, and integrates with SCADA systems for automated alerts. Model optimization techniques, including TensorRT and INT8 quantization, reduce model size from 8.1 MB to 0.95 MB, ensuring compatibility with resource-constrained devices. Experimental results confirm 0.6 s edge latency, outperforming centralized approaches by 42 % in detection speed.

title: Digital Sustainability in Business Models: Role of Digital Enablers and Smart Technology
authors: Sudha S, Anitha Kumari D, Perumal
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: As responsible business practices gain momentum, business models subsequently have evolved into a mechanism in which value is generated, long-term viability is ensured, and environmental responsibilities are considered. Today's organizations must navigate the complex interactions that are at the crossroads of environmental responsibilities, digital transformation, and the United Nation's Sustainable Development Goals. This study investigates the processes of digital enablers (smart automation and data-driven decisions) assisted by digital resources ie smart technology (IoT) toward building economically digital sustainable business models. The study further explores the moderating effects of managerial capacity, which facilitate a stronger link between digital enablers, the Internet of Things (IoT), and sustainable outcomes. Using survey data collected from 250 professionals, the analysis employs regression models to explore relationships between digital enablers, IoT, and sustainable business outcomes. Findings reveal that IoT resources and managerial capacity are two of the most significant and consistent digital resource and moderating variable of economic sustainability. However, under varying organizational capacities, smart automation and data- driven decisions are contingent. Implications for theory related to the emerging digital sustainability aspects in business model development are discussed, along with practical strategies for organizations to leverage digital ecosystems to meet and ensure long-term viability.

title: RAG-Augmented Payroll Intelligence Stack: A Zero-Trust, Policy-Verified Architecture for Compliant and Explainable Payroll Automation
authors: Raghuveer Yerneni
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Payroll systems must execute jurisdiction-specific calculations, disclosures, and compliance checks under strict regulatory and audit constraints. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves factual grounding, conventional RAG pipelines lack formal guarantees of correctness, authorization control, and provenance—critical for payroll. We present the RAG-Augmented Payroll Intelligence Stack (RAPIS), a policy-verified architecture that fuses hybrid retrieval with tool-based computation, attribute-based access control (ABAC), formal verification via SMT (Z3), and W3C PROV lineage for auditability. RAPIS introduces PayLang, a declarative DSL for encoding payroll rules that compiles into both authorization policies (OPA/Cedar) and verifiable constraints. Evaluated on Payroll-Bench, a multi-jurisdiction synthetic benchmark based on IRS Pub. 15, HMRC PAYE, EPF/ESI, and EU transparency directives, RAPIS outperforms existing baselines in accuracy, explainability, prompt-injection robustness, and provenance coverage. The results demonstrate that verifiable, policy-bounded reasoning is essential for deploying AI in regulated, high-assurance financial domains.

title: A Data-Driven Civic Communication Model for Air Quality Governance in Smart Cities using IoT Sensor Technologies
authors: Kumar Mausam, Shivani Patel, Sunil Pratap Singh, Garima Sharma, Monu Singh Rajawat, Raghav Trivedi
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This study examines how hyperlocal journalism can employ data from IoT-based environmental sensors to support smart urban development in India. It analyses qualitative content from digital News reports alongside Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) air quality data (2020–2024). The research evaluates challenges in public interpretation obstacles, civic technology integration, and reporting trends. It proposes a framework for incorporating sensor-generated data into journalism to achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) 11 and 13 through inclusive, data-driven environmental communication. The findings show how participatory media can transform complex environmental data into accessible narratives for the public. The study underscores the need for collaboration among journalists, technologists, and urban stakeholders to strengthen environmental communication and promote sustainable urban futures.

title: Performance and Emission Characteristics of Soapnut Biodiesel Blends in a Crdi Diesel Engine Operated at Variable Injection Pressures
authors: C Manikandan, N Sethuraman, C Syed Aalam, D Karthikeyan, S Devi, K Manikandan
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: This study investigates the potential of soapnut biodiesel blends as a sustainable alternative to diesel fuel in a CRDI engine under varying injection pressures. The chemical, physical, and thermal properties of the blends were characterized using GCMS, FTIR, and SEM techniques, with nanoparticles added to improve fuel quality. Engine tests were performed at different load levels, identifying the 20 % blend as optimal for performance. Further experiments with injection pressures between 400 and 1000 bar revealed that appropriate pressure selection significantly improved efficiency and reduced emissions. Overall, the results demonstrate that soapnut biodiesel blends, combined with suitable injection parameters and additives, can enhance performance while lowering harmful emissions, supporting their role as an eco-friendly and renewable energy source.

title: Position-Based Force Control for In-Hand Manipulation Using a Soft-Rigid Hybrid Two-Finger Hand
authors: Keita Katamine, Hikaru Arita, Kazuto Nakashima, Kenji Tahara
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Soft grippers have attracted attention as a safe and adaptable means of grasping fragile or irregularly shaped objects. However, challenges remain in performing advanced tasks such as in-hand manipulation. This study proposes a soft-rigid hybrid two-finger hand that integrates position-controlled motors and flexible links, based on a design concept that combines the mechanical properties of rigid and flexible components. The proposed hand directly drives the flexible links using position-controlled motors, enabling active control of the interaction between the flexible structure and the environment. Leveraging this feature, a position-based force control strategy is introduced, in which fingertip positions are adjusted to shift the equilibrium of the flexible links, indirectly modulating the contact force through their passive deformation. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulations of object grasping tasks, demonstrating stable and dexterous in-hand manipulation.

title: Unsupervised Deep Learning for Multi-Modality Tumor Detection Using Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Shaswatik Giri, Ankita Khorai, Widhika Shah, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Chiranjib Parida
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: A tumor is a solid mass of tissue that forms when abnormal cells group together. They can form mostly anywhere in your body, including your bones, skin, tissue, organs and glands. Many tumors are benign, which means they’re not cancer. Cancerous, or malignant, tumors require cancer treatment.In this paper, we come up with an unsupervised framework for brain tumor segmentation that covers both unlabeled and partially labeled data to overcome the difficulties of unavailability of labeled data. The method involves three stages: Firstly, we used SimCLR to implement self-supervised training on unlabeled multi-modal scans. Then, We used CycleGAN cross-modality translation to generate missing MRI sequences, and lastly UNet++ segmentation model is used to initialize with a pretrained encoder and is fine-tuned on a labeled dataset whose scope is limited. We have carried out our evaluation on the BraTS 2021 dataset, our way of approaching the problem is better than the conventional training that helped us to achieve higher segmentation accuracy and better detection of the different types of tumor.

title: Dual Personality Matching System for Candidate – Company Compatibility Using AI and NLP
authors: Ravichandran M, Wishmali S, Sivasankaran S
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Recruitment often prioritizes technical skills or isolated personality tests, overlooking how well candidates align with organizational culture. This paper proposes a two-way personality matching framework that applies artificial intelligence (AI) and natural language processing (NLP) to assess candidate-organization compatibility. Resumes and job descriptions are analyzed to generate personality profiles using the OCEAN model, which are then compared through similarity-based scoring to compute a compatibility index. This enables recruiters to identify not only qualified applicants but also those most likely to adapt and grow within the work environment. Unlike traditional one-sided screening methods, the approach emphasizes mutual behavioral alignment, promoting cultural fit, long-term retention, and human-centric hiring practices.

title: Geographically Aware Spatio-Temporal Fusion Transformer Models for Enhanced Air Quality Prediction
authors: Akshaya V, Sivanantham S, Renukadevi S, Krishnamoorthy V
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Air quality prediction is vital for human health and in environmental conservation, yet many models fail to fully capture the complex spatio-temporal dynamics of air pollutants. The proposed research can be treated as an action against climate change and its impact. This research proposed a novel Spatial-Temporal Fusion Transformer (STFT) model which is an improvement on the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) model to capture temporal and geographical relationships. Experiments were conducted on the Air Quality Index dataset containing multiple monitoring stations across India to compare the performance of STFT with other baseline models such as Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, BiLSTM, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and the basic TFT. The STFT outperformed all models, achieving an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.95 and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 4.8, representing a 3.3% improvement in R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and an 11.1% reduction in MAE compared to TFT. Significant inter-station dependencies for spatial attention can be observed particularly among urban centres. These results indicate that STFT has a higher ability to capture intricate temporal and spatial relations, thus stressing the potential of utilizing STFT to improve the performance of air quality prediction for better policy making.

title: An Explainable and Optimal Deep Transfer Learning Approach for Forest Fire Detection Using Grad-CAM Visualizations
authors: Sameer Mansoori, Aishwarya Pitchumani, Nila Sangamitra Arul, S Vignesh, Arti Anuragi
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Forest fires pose severe environmental, economic, and human threats. Traditional detection methods such as satellite imagery, manual observation, and sensor-based technologies, suffer from high false alarm rates, slow response times, and high installation costs. Thus, this study proposes an Advanced Deep Learning-based Detection Framework that uses Transfer learning to enhance fire detection and response in real-time. The framework consists of several pretrained models-MobileNet, InceptionV3, ResNet50, VGG16, EfficientNet, and Xception-that are fine-tuned through hyperparameter tuning and LwF for maximized classification accuracy with minimized false positives. We used 3 datasets, each with 3 classes - Fire, Normal and Smoke. Performance evaluation was carried out using accuracy, precision, recall and confusion matrices. It was then established that MobileNet provided the highest classification accuracy of 98.2% with low computational cost while LwF greatly advanced generalization over complex datasets. Furthermore, to boost model interpretability and ensure trustworthy decisionmaking, Grad-CAM visualization was also put into play. The results suggest that integration of deep learning based wildfire detection with focus on explainability can result in significant improvement in early warning systems. Future research will focus on incorporating Vision Transformers (ViTs), multimodal sensor fusion, and edge computing architectures. This study indicates the advantages of pretrained models to enhance disaster preparedness by mitigating the impacts of climate change.

title: Team Sports Coaching System Using Vibrotactile Feedback
authors: Jose Victorio Salazar Luces, Ankit A. Ravankar, Masahiro Shiratori, Yasuhisa Hirata
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Recent advances in sports coaching technology enable feedback via two main approaches: (a) terminal feedback, which provides performance metrics after gameplay, and (b) continuous feedback, which delivers real-time cues through methods like vibrotactile or auditory stimuli. However, most systems focus on individual performance, making them unsuitable for team sports, where coordinated movements and strategies are essential. This paper proposes a framework for real-time, simultaneous guidance in team sports. The system tracks player positions using a single RGB camera and provides continuous vibrotactile feedback, preserving auditory and visual attention for gameplay. The key contributions are: a lightweight player tracking system using YOLOv4 and coordinate transformations, achieving position estimates at 20 Hz, and a simultaneous vibrotactile guidance system that enables teams to form target formations by following the stimuli. We validate our system through two experiments. First, we assess the tracking accuracy in a virtual environment with four color-tagged moving models, analyzing RMSE across different speeds. Second, we evaluate the effectiveness of vibrotactile guidance by tracking and guiding two subjects through six target formations while measuring performance metrics.

title: Non-Geometric Kinematic Error Estimation of the Articulated Robot Using Kernel Ridge Regression
authors: Jingbo Luo, Guilin Yang, Jianhui He, Yiyang Feng, Silu Chen, Chi Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Accurate positioning is critical for industrial robots performing precision manufacturing tasks. Although geometric calibration effectively compensates for structural parameter errors, residual pose deviations persist due to configuration-dependent non-geometric effects such as joint compliance, thermal deformation, and friction. This paper presents a kernel ridge regression (KRR)-based framework for non-geometric error estimation of the articulated robot. The proposed method establishes a direct multi-input-multi-output nonlinear mapping between joint displacements and residual pose deviations after geometric calibration. In contrast to Gaussian Process Regression, which typically requires training multiple single-output models, KRR performs unified closed-form analytical solution derived from the Representer Theorem. This formulation enables efficient learning and prediction with strong generalization from limited experimental data. Experiments conducted on a KUKA KR60-3 robot using a Leica AT901-MR laser tracker demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly enhances positioning accuracy, reducing the mean position error from 0.305~mm to 0.083~mm and the orientation error from 0.314~mrad to 0.250~mrad. These results confirm that the KRR-based framework effectively captures nonlinear non-geometric error patterns and provides a robust foundation for future self-calibration and adaptive accuracy enhancement in robotic systems.

title: Grid-Based Marking Prompt Framework for Spatial Understanding in Vision–Language Models
authors: Ryo Terashima, Yuga Yano, Koshun Arimura, Hakaru Tamukoh
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: In this study, we propose a grid-based marking prompt framework to enhance spatial understanding in vision-language models (VLMs). The framework integrates object detection, background masking, and number overlaying to enable VLMs to interpret spatial and contextual instructions more effectively. By inputting numbered images along with natural language instructions, a VLM selects the number corresponding to the most semantically appropriate location. The framework operates without requiring prior information such as 3D models or physical markers. Moreover, the proposed framework allows flexible rule adaptation through prompt engineering alone, providing general applicability across various objects and environments. We conducted two experiments for the object placement task. In experiment 1, shelf images captured by a service robot were used to evaluate the placement selection accuracy of a VLM. In experiment 2, the framework was implemented on a service robot and conducted the object placement task at positions selected by a VLM in a real-world environment. The framework achieved a high success rate in both experiments, demonstrating the effectiveness and practical utility of the framework in real-world environments.

title: Digital Twin Modelling and Rul Estimation of a Battery Powered Motor System in Matlab
authors: Lal Raja Singh R, Chandru G, Ganesh Kumar R, Gokam Gokul
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: The increasing adoption of electric vehicles and renewable energy systems has placed a strong emphasis on accurate prediction of battery lifetime to ensure safety, efficiency, and cost-effectiveness. Batteries undergo continuous price and discharge cycles, and their degradation over the years without delay affects the overall performance of the standard machine. This study applies machine learning techniques, specifically the Random Forest algorithm, to predict the final useful lives (RUL) of rechargeable batteries. Operational parameters such as state of charge (SOC), voltage, current, temperature, and cycle count number are utilized as enter features for version education. The Random Forest version is trained and tested the use of a dataset that reflects various operating and fault situations, enabling the algorithm to capture complex nonlinear relationships between battery behavior and degradation patterns. The predictive model provides accurate estimations of battery health and expected lifetime, offering a valuable tool for proactive maintenance planning and early replacement strategies. By implementing such a data-driven approach, battery management systems can move beyond traditional threshold-based monitoring to intelligent, predictive diagnostics. This contributes to improving system reliability, reducing downtime, and extending the overall lifecycle of electric vehicles and energy storage applications.

title: Computational Implementation of Group Theory in Cryptography and Chemistry: A Unified Mathematical Framework
authors: Syed Areeb Ahmad, Vaishnavi Chaubey, Surabhi Chaubey
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: A foundational area of mathematics, group theory has broad applications in many scientific domains. An integrated review of its use in cryptography and chemistry is presented in this work. Group theory simplifies structural predictions in chemistry by making it easier to analyze molecular symmetries, molecular orbitals, and spectroscopy. Group theory in cryptography provides the security underpinnings of contemporary encryption techniques, especially through discrete logarithm issues, homomorphisms, and permutation groups. We examine group-theoretical isomorphisms, homomorphism stability, and representations using both recent and classic literature, emphasizing their dual applicability in the information and biological sciences. Opportunities for interdisciplinary advances, such as improved chemical modeling and post-quantum cryptography protocols, are suggested by the synergy between these domains.

title: Secure Comparative Evaluation of Alzheimer MRI Classification Models Using Blockchain
authors: Abdhisuta Dash, Fahad Amin, Subham Kumar Sahoo, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a chronic neurodegenerative disorder profoundly affecting memory and cognitive functions for which an early and precise diagnosis is essential to achieve timely intervention and disease management. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is an important tool for detecting structural changes in the brain such as hippocampal shrinkage and ventricular enlargement, which can be correlated with Alzheimer’s disease’s stages of progression. In this work, we present a framework that couples deep learning-based Alzheimer’s MRI classification with blockchain-supported image authenticity verification. Our experimental setup compares five classification approaches, Xception, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, ResNet50, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting across different training durations. The best performing model is integrated to the local IPFS node and Ethereum smart contract through Ganache. This comparative investigation examines the balance of accuracy, efficiency, and training time in a variety of model designs. It also illustrates the viability of a secure, decentralized framework for both diagnostic accuracy and data integrity through blockchain.

title: AutoShard: Intelligent Multi-Tenant Sharding and Privacy-Aware Resource Allocation for Serverless Architectures on AWS
authors: Radhakrishnan Krishna Kripa
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Serverless computing platforms like AWS Lambda have revolutionized cloud application development by abstracting infrastructure management and enabling fine-grained scaling. However, challenges such as unpredictable cold start latency, inefficient resource allocation, and data privacy risks in multi-tenant environments continue to limit their effectiveness in productionscale deployments. In this paper, we introduce AutoShard, a novel framework that performs privacy-aware function decomposition and adaptive resource scheduling across AWS Lambda, Fargate, and ECS. AutoShard converts serverless functions into semantic execution graphs, uses transformer-based classifiers to identify sensitive computation paths, and applies spectral clustering to partition functions into privacy-aligned shards. These shards are then scheduled using a federated Q-learning model trained on anonymized telemetry data, enabling latency-aware, costoptimized, and privacy-compliant deployments. Empirical evaluations across diverse real-world workloads-including IoT, financial, and healthcare APIs-demonstrate that AutoShard reduces cold start latency by 40.8%, increases throughput by 39%, enhances isolation with a 0.91 privacy score, and improves cost efficiency by over 42% compared to AWS Compute Optimizer and Lambda Power Tuner. AutoShard represents a significant step toward making serverless architectures more intelligent, secure, and production-ready for multi-tenant cloud environments.

title: DataVault: Secure On-Field IoT Data Logging using Blockchain and IPFS
authors: Rashmi Gupta
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The growing deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in remote and resource-constrained environments has intensified the demand for secure, tamper-evident, and decentralized data logging frameworks. Conventional cloud-based storage solutions are often unsuitable due to their vulnerability to single points of failure, reliance on centralized trust, and lack of transparent auditability. In this paper, we introduce DataVault, a lightweight hybrid architecture that combines the immutability of blockchain with the scalability of the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) to enable secure on field IoT data logging. Sensor readings from edge devices are signed, uploaded to IPFS, and cryptographically anchored to a smart contract enabled blockchain, ensuring data provenance, integrity, and non repudiation without overburdening constrained devices. Our implementation on Raspberry Pi based edge nodes demonstrates that DataVault achieves low latency logging, minimal resource overhead, and rapid auditability, outperforming existing blockchain IoT frameworks such as IoTChain, MedRec, and BlockIPFS. The system design is modular, fault-tolerant, and scalable, offering a robust foundation for decentralized IoT deployments in agriculture, energy monitoring, and environmental sensing. Experimental results validate the system’s ability to operate efficiently in unreliable field conditions while maintaining end to end data trustworthiness.

title: Hardware-Enabled Root of Trust for Enhanced Security in Embedded Systems
authors: Siddharth Patel, Rohit Singh
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Security breaches and data theft remain critical challenges in the era of interconnected devices and smart systems. This paper introduces a hardware-enabled root of trust mechanism to strengthen device authentication by leveraging immutable, hardcoded identifiers—such as IMEI numbers, MAC addresses, and secure module IDs. Unlike traditional softwarebased authentication methods, the proposed approach integrates a dedicated hardware layer that ensures tamper resistance and non-replicability. The study details the system architecture and its deployment in real-world scenarios, demonstrating improved resilience against prevalent cybersecurity threats. By embedding a hardware-based security primitive into the authentication process, the research offers a seamless user experience while significantly enhancing security over conventional methods. This integration of hardware and software offers a promising path forward in addressing emerging vulnerabilities in IoT and embedded systems. This research contributes an innovative, hardware-integrated solution that is effective in current applications and adaptable for emerging technologies. Future work will investigate hardwareagnostic emulation techniques, practical methods for retrofitting legacy systems, and the feasibility of introducing quantumresistant cryptographic primitives — each subject to staged evaluation on representative hardware to quantify resource and performance trade-offs. The proposed approach aims to strengthen device authentication while preserving usability, and to provide a practical foundation for incremental security improvements in embedded systems.

title: Full-Mode Synchronous ZVS Modulation of the Cascaded Systems With Two Time Scales
authors: Kai He, Huanjun Niu, Zhenghao Wang, Siwen Ye, Fei Gao, Xijun Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The front-end converter of inductive power transfer (IPT) systems typically requires a cascaded four-switch buck-boost (FSBB) converter to interface with distributed power sources at different voltage levels. However, different operating frequencies and non-fully-decoupled operation of the two-stage system pose challenges for implementing synchronous zero voltage switching (ZVS) modulation. This paper proposes a unified full-mode-period synchronous pseudo-continuous conduction mode (PCCM) ZVS modulation strategy for cascaded FSBB-IPT systems. The proposed strategy achieves synchronization across different time scales in the full-mode operating period by leveraging the inner phase-shift angles between the two stages. The unified time scale is used to determine the operating mode durations of the front-end FSBB converter and minimizes losses by restricting the ZVS current to minimum value. Concurrently, minimizing the inductor current is achieved by adjusting the duration of the second operating stage. Experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Research on the Design of an Emotion Detection Wristband with Emotional Enhancement Based on an Improved BERT Mode
authors: Ming Lv, Yuchen Ma
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: To enhance the performance of smart wristbands in emotion recognition, this study proposes an innovative design scheme based on a user experience-oriented, emotion-enhanced BERT model. By monitoring users’ emotional fluctuations in real time during wear, the system helps improve emotional awareness and self-regulation capabilities, thereby optimizing the emotional management experience. Compared with mainstream market products, this design demonstrates significant advantages in hardware architecture, algorithm implementation, and user experience. In terms of hardware, it integrates multiple advanced physiological sensors (e.g., heart rate, galvanic skin response, and blood oxygen concentration) to comprehensively capture users’ physiological signals, providing multi-dimensional data support for emotion analysis. Algorithmically, it innovatively incorporates an emotion-enhanced BERT model that integrates multi-source physiological data, historical emotional records, and environmental factors, achieving more accurate emotion recognition and prediction through deep learning. Regarding user experience, the system supports real-time emotional feedback and personalized adjustment suggestions (such as meditation and breathing exercises), assisting users in alleviating negative emotions promptly. Through multi-dimensional comparative analysis, this study identifies the shortcomings of existing products in emotion detection accuracy and personalized recommendations, offering valuable insights for future optimization of emotion computing in smart wristbands.

title: Improvement Research on YOLOv5 Object Detection : Algorithm Based on Multi-Attention Mechanism
authors: Haoyang Zhou
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: To solve the problems of small object detection and background interference in YOLOv5 algorithm in the process of traffic signal detection, this paper proposes an improved algorithm based on a multiple attention mechanism. We introduce dynamic channel attention, coordinate attention and cross-scale attention module into the backbone network and neck feature fusion layer of YOLOv5, and optimize feature channel precisely, spatial location accurately and multi-scale information directly. The experimental results based on COCO 2017 dataset and self-built traffic signal dataset show that the improved model can achieve mAP@0.5 of 61.4%, which is 5.2% higher than that of original YOLOv5. The small object detection average precision (AP) increases by 9.3%, and the parameter amount increases by only 3.8M, while keeping the inference speed above 48 FPS. The convergence analysis of loss function shows that the improved model has better convergence performance and higher stability in the process of bounding box regression, class classification and object confidence prediction.

title: An Integrated LSTM-TabNet Ensemble with Elastic Net for Jakarta Composite Index Forecasting
authors: Muhammad Rangga Miftahul Falah, Gatot Soepriyono, Endah Tri Esti Handayani, Panca Dewi Pamungkasari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Forecasting the Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) remains challenging due to its high sensitivity to global financial dynamics and nonlinear market interactions. This study proposes an integrated ensemble framework combining a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and a TabNet model, with an Elastic Net meta-learner for final prediction aggregation. The LSTM captures temporal dependencies in sequential IHSG data, while TabNet processes engineered tabular features to enhance interpretability. Hyperparameters for all models are optimized using the Optuna framework. The model is trained on daily data from January 2010 to January 2025, incorporating global stock indices, commodity prices, exchange rates, and macroeconomic indicators. Experimental results on an unseen test set show that the proposed LSTM-TabNet-ElasticNet ensemble achieves a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 49.89 and a Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error (SMAPE) of 0.53%, outperforming standalone LSTM (RMSE 50.29), TabNet (RMSE 50.03), and serial TabNet-LSTM hybrids (RMSE 63.59). Feature importance analysis highlights lagged returns (S&P 500, Gold) and volatility (VIX) as dominant predictors, emphasizing the influence of recent global market movements on IHSG dynamics. The proposed framework offers a robust and interpretable approach for financial time-series forecasting in emerging markets.

title: Exploring Trends and Insights in the Steam Gaming Ecosystem Through Data Analysis
authors: Vimalakirti Muliawan, Haziq Ibrahim Omar, Erwin Indrawan, Ade Putera Kemala
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The video game industry has experienced remarkable growth, solidifying its position as a leading sector in the digital economy. Steam, a prominent gaming platform, generates extensive datasets that offer valuable insights into consumer behavior and gaming trends. This study employs machine learning classification methods to analyze Steam game metadata including tags, genres, and release information to predict review score categories (low-rated vs. high-rated). By leveraging advanced machine learning techniques, the study identifies significant patterns and trends within the gaming ecosystem. Experimental results showed that Decision Tree, Random Forest, and XGBoost models achieved accuracies of 76%, 80%, and 80%, respectively. While Random Forest and XGBoost performed similarly, XGBoost was selected as the final model due to its more interpretable feature importance results. Analysis revealed that release month, soundtrack quality, free-to-play status, number of tags, and classic genre were the most influential predictors. A user-friendly web application dashboard was subsequently developed to present these insights. The dashboard enables stakeholders, including developers, analysts, and researchers, to filter data by genre and other criteria, simplifying complex predictive tasks. Visualizing and analyzing machine learning results through the dashboard contributes to a deeper understanding of the trends and insights in the Steam gaming ecosystem

title: The Design of ICT Enabled Circular Economy for Duck Farming Ecosystem
authors: Ryan Brilian Fatsena, Lukito Edi Nugroho, Adhistya Erna Permanasari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This paper presents the design of an ICT-enabled system to improve coordination and circular economy (CE) practices in the Indonesian duck farming ecosystem. Ecosystem mapping and interviews reveal recurring structural problems, including weather-driven delays in rice harvesting and milling that cause bran shortages, higher feed costs, irregular feeding schedules, inconsistent duck quality, fragmented coordination, and underutilized manure. This study aims to design an ICT system that aligns with real operational workflows to address these challenges. A qualitative approach was used through ecosystem mapping, semi-structured interviews, and problem-driven system design involving use case diagrams, activity diagrams, wireframes, mockups, and an interactive prototype. The resulting system design supports input ordering, stock CRUD management, harvest information, delivery tracking, and the circulation of manure or bran. Evaluation using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and Empowerment Theory shows that users perceive the design as useful, easy to use, and aligned with daily practices, while also enhancing meaning, competence, autonomy, and operational impact. This study contributes a problem-driven ICT system architecture for duck farming. It demonstrates how ICT can structure and scale previously informal circular practices, offering practical insights for traceability, market linkages, and waste-to-value coordination in agri-food systems.

title: Smart Home Automation System: Secure Face Recognition and Gesture-Based Control
authors: Abhishek Kumar Singh, Jyotsana Singh, Mohammad Saqib, Nikhil upadhyay, Ajay Kumar dubey
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Smart home is a name given to a home that is provided with advanced technologies integrated to allow remote control and monitoring of multiple systems like lighting, heating, ventilation, air conditioning, electrical appliances, and security devices through a computer or a smartphone. Home automation brings more comfort, convenience, energy efficiency, and security. In this paper, an IoT-based smart home security system with facial recognition for automated door access and appliance gesture control is proposed. The device is based on a Raspberry Pi, a camera, and Passive Infrared (PIR) sensors. Upon approaching the door, the camera takes a picture of the person and applies real-time face recognition using computer vision. If identified, the door will automatically open up; otherwise, the owner receives an alert with the intruder's photo via email for immediate remote response. For controlling appliances, the system utilizes hand gesture control to control low-voltage appliances instead of conventional manual control. This type of approach provides a natural, touch-free way of interacting with home systems. The suggested configuration is not only economical and cost-effective but can be scaled to real-world applications as well. Being open to further development and marketability, the project presents itself as an intermediate step towards more intelligent, secure living spaces through accessible IoT solutions.

title: A Novel Modulation Scheme of Dual Active Bridge Converter with Zero Reactive Power
authors: Haibin Cao, Jianping Xu, Chenli Zhou, Xia Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To reduce the reactive power of dual active bridge (DAB) converter, the conventional modulation schemes, including single phase-shift (SPS) modulation scheme, dual phase-shift (DPS) modulation scheme, and triple phase-shift (TPS) modulation scheme are proposed and extensively investigated. However, it is very difficult for SPS and DPS to completely eliminate the reactive power. TPS can achieve zero reactive power, which use three phase shifts as control variables, and is thus rather complex and difficult to implement. To address the aforementioned issues in the DAB converter, a novel modulation scheme is proposed in this paper, which features zero reactive power and simple control system. The proposed method combines phase shift modulation (PSM) and pulse-width modulation (PWM), ensuring complete inductor energy transfer to the output in each half switching cycle without reactive circulation in the converter. To verify the effectiveness of the proposed modulation scheme, a 6kW simulation models are developed.

title: An Improved Junction-to-Case Thermal Resistance Test Method for In-Line Metal-Cased Package Solid State Relays
authors: Shuonan Ji, Yaokang Lai, Shengyue Yang, Zhen Xin, Chao Li, Qiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Solid state relays (SSRs) are non-contact semiconductor switching devices featuring electrical isolation, available in diverse package types. Among these, through-hole metal-cased SSRs offer significant advantages including radiation resistance, low thermal resistance, and corrosion resistance, making them widely adopted in aviation and aerospace applications. The junction-to-case thermal resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">θJC</inf>) serves as a critical metric for evaluating SSR thermal performance. Industry-standard Transient Dual Interface Method (TDIM) is typically employed for R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">θJC</inf> measurement. However, accurate determination of R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">θJC</inf> remains challenging for in-line metal SSRs due to their vertically oriented pins relative to the case, which prevents effective thermal contact with cold plates during testing. This paper proposes an improved TDIM specifically designed for in-line metal cased packages. We establish a thermal simulation model of the SSR to analyze its heat flow path. The accuracy and effectiveness of the proposed method are rigorously validated through comparative simulations and experimental characterization.

title: Comparative Analysis of Hydrogen Production Power Supplies and Auxiliary Systems under Fluctuating Operating Conditions
authors: Pengcheng Li, Linfei Li, Chuan Liu, Zuyu Zhang, Zichuan Diao, Shunwen Gao, Yingjun Guo, Guozhuang Liang, Hexu Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The volatility of renewable energy consumption demand makes the dynamic operational capability of alkaline water electrolysis (AWE) hydrogen production systems a key factor limiting the economic viability of green hydrogen. This paper analyses the parameter characteristics of mainstream hydrogen production power sources and the regulatory role of auxiliary equipment in large-scale alkaline hydrogen production systems under fluctuating operating conditions. It demonstrates that power sources and auxiliary equipment have evolved from mere functional units into core executive units ensuring system stability and enabling dynamic coordination.

title: Density-Aware Performance Profiling of A*, Dijkstra, and RRT Algorithms in Static 2D UAV Grid Navigation
authors: Md Danish Alam, Shahida Khatoon, Muzakkir Ali Khan, Md Muzammil Sani
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The rapid advancement of UAV navigation technology necessitates robust and scalable path planning algorithms for mission-critical operations, including autonomous navigation in war zones, battlefield surveillance, tactical supply drops, and post-conflict disaster assessment. This study evaluates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A}^{*}$</tex>, Dijkstra, and Rapidly-Exploring Random Tree (RRT) algorithms for UAV path planning in static 2D grids, targeting applications such as battlefield navigation, surveillance, and disaster assessment. A structured <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> grid with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \%, 20 {\%}$</tex>, and 30% obstacle densities was used, assessing computation time, memory usage, path length, path smoothness, and success rate. A* achieved the best trade-off, delivering short, smooth paths with low computation time (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.015-0.021 \mathrm{s}$</tex>), high success rates (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96 \%-45.8 {\%}$</tex>), and moderate memory use (166-169 MB). Dijkstra guaranteed optimal paths but had higher time (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.045-0.063 \mathrm{s}$</tex>) and memory (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$170-173 \text{MB}$</tex>), making it less suitable for real-time use. RRT offered lower memory but poor smoothness and reduced success under dense obstacles. The results guide algorithm selection based on environmental complexity and computational limits, and the framework supports future hybrid approaches for efficient, real-time UAV navigation.

title: Agentic Ads: A Multi-Agent AI Framework for Privacy-Safe and Explainable Ad Decisioning
authors: Chaitanya Kumar Seri
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Retail media networks (RMNs) and ad platforms must optimize sponsored ad delivery under strict privacy regimes, brand-safety requirements, and growing demands for transparency and fairness. We present Agentic Ads, an agentic AI framework for privacy-preserving, explainable ad decisioning across open-web and RMN surfaces. Rather than a monolithic ranker, Agentic Ads orchestrates specialized agents for (i) multimodal context and product understanding (text + computer vision), (ii) policy/consent enforcement and eligibility masking, (iii) constrained bidding and budget-aware pacing, (iv) fairness aware exposure control, and (v) auditor-ready explanations with human-in-the-loop review. The system combines catalogaware and contextual matching via shared embeddings, uses vision signals for brand safety and creative quality checks, and optimizes allocations with explicit exposure regularization while respecting campaign objectives and retailer rules. In offline simulations on realistic synthetic ad logs and catalog imagery, Agentic Ads improves click-through calibration and spend quality, reduces unsafe adjacency and creative QC violations, and narrows exposure skew across categories compared to utility only and heuristic baselines; ablations highlight the contribution of the privacy masks, CV signals, and fairness terms. These results suggest that a policy-aware, multi-agent design can deliver effective sponsored placements while meeting privacy, safety, and governance requirements central to modern AdTech and RMNs.

title: High-Density Integrated Transformer for Combined OBC and LDC System in Electric Vehicles
authors: Chenli Zhou, Jianping Xu, Zhengge Chen, Rui Huang, Zewen You
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: In this paper, a novel integrated transformer with adjustable leakage inductance is proposed to achieve high power density in electric-vehicle battery charger applications. By using a multi-winding transformer, the topological integration of on-boar charger (OBC) and low-voltage dc-dc converter (LDC) can be achieved, which effectively reduces the number of semiconductor devices, transformers, and ZVS inductors in the battery charger system (BCS). Additionally, the BCS enables simultaneous charging of low-voltage batteries and high-voltage batteries. Furthermore, the multi-winding transformer model is analyzed, and 3-D finite element analysis (FEA) is employed to evaluate the leakage inductances. To confirm the validity of the proposed transformer, a prototype with 6.6kW OBC and 2kW LDC is built. Experimental results demonstrate an average efficiency of 94.8% over the full voltage range of the high-voltage battery.

title: FedPrompt–MAML–MLID: Federated Meta-Learning for Few-Shot Multi-Label Text Classification
authors: Chengyao He
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: In scenarios with privacy constraints and data silos, few-shot multi-label text classification across institutions faces challenges such as non-IID client heterogeneity, partially overlapping label spaces, and difficulties in unifying decision thresholds. This paper proposes a federated meta-learning framework named FedPrompt–MAML–MLID, with core innovations in three aspects: a Label-Aware Prompt Routing mechanism for cross-client prompt sharing and personalized adaptation; the introduction of Multi-Label Intra-class/Inter-class Diversity Regularization to enhance the discriminability of tail labels; and the construction of Threshold Meta-Calibration to learn transferable label-level decision boundaries. In federated partitioning experiments on five multi-label datasets including RCV1-V2, compared to personalized federated learning baselines, our method achieves average improvements of 0.021–0.032 in micro-F1 and macro-F1, 0.012–0.026 in PR-AUC, and an average reduction of 0.023 in ECE, while maintaining acceptable communication and privacy overhead.

title: A Novel Two-stage Non-isolated Soft-switching Residential PCS with Partial Power Transfer Characteristic
authors: Renjing Song, Yunqing Pei, Zhiheng Huang, Jianing Quan, Panming Li, Wei Cao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To address the challenges of global green energy transition and energy crisis, and to promote the applications of clean energy on the client side, this paper proposes a novel two-stage non-isolated household energy storage converter (PCS). Its DC-DC stage adopts a Sigma-type structure consisting of an LLC resonant converter and a four-switch Buck-Boost (FSBB) converter, which can adapt to a wide DC voltage range from 32V to 62V while realizing full-range soft-switching through phase-shift control strategy of FSBB. The AC-DC stage adopts full-bridge structure with an LCL filter and employs unipolar frequency doubling modulation strategy and proportional resonance (PR) closed-loop control. Finally, a test prototype is constructed based on planar magnetic components and tested under 500W full load condition: the efficiency of DC-DC stage is 97.13%, the efficiency of AC-DC stage is 98.79%, and the two-stage efficiency reaches 95.76%. The experimental results fully verify the feasibility of the proposed converter.

title: QKD-Enhanced Attribute-Based Encryption for Secure and Access Control in Smart Grid Databases
authors: Cuiling Liu, Jing Zhang, Zhen Yao, Haoyu Hou, Zhiyuan Hu, Yuxiang Lv
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: The energy and power industry faces increasing data security challenges amidst its digital transformation. Databases, as the foundation of digital infrastructure, require advanced encryption techniques to safeguard sensitive information. This paper proposes a novel encryption framework, QKD-ABE, that integrates Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) with Attribute-Based Encryption (ABE) to enhance the confidentiality, integrity, and access control of power system data. QKD enables unconditional security in key distribution by leveraging quantum mechanics, effectively preventing key interception and tampering. ABE provides fine-grained access control by enforcing encryption policies based on user attributes, ensuring that only authorized individuals can access private data. The proposed scheme also demonstrates strong resistance to common security threats, including man-in-the-middle and eavesdropping attacks, through quantum measurement disturbance and Quantum Bit Error Rate (QBerr) verification. Experimental results and theoretical analysis indicate that QKD-ABE offers a secure, efficient, and scalable solution for protecting sensitive data in modern smart grid environments.

title: Solution of Forward Kinematics of Trifree Hybrid Robot Based on BP-N Method
authors: Longteng Chen, Hutian Feng, Manxing Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: Hybrid robots consist of parallel and serial mechanisms, with the forward kinematics of parallel mechanisms currently being a key challenge. Taking the Trifree hybrid robot as a case study, this paper proposes a novel forward kinematics solution method that integrates BP neural networks with Newtonian iteration. This approach overcomes the limitations of neural networks 'low accuracy and Newtonian iteration's poor robustness, achieving high precision and efficiency while maintaining strong robustness. Using closed-loop vector method, we completed the inverse kinematics modeling of Trifree's parallel mechanism and determined its workspace dimensions. By collecting numerous coordinate points within the robot's workspace, we employed inverse kinematics to generate training samples for the BP neural network. The SSA algorithm was used to optimize the initial weights and thresholds of the neural network, and the trained BP neural network model was applied to predict kinematic solutions for the parallel mechanism. Based on Trifree's structural characteristics, we developed a Newtonian iteration method for forward kinematics solving. Finally, by combining BP neural networks with Newtonian iteration, we established a forward kinematics solution method named BP-N. Tests demonstrate that this integrated approach ensures high stability, efficiency, and precision in obtaining accurate kinematic solutions.

title: Navigating Successful Automation on Challenged Programs: A Case Study and Practical Solutions
authors: Sandra Dawson, James Melby
publish: 2025 IEEE MetroCon
abstract: Challenged programs must become more effective and efficient to meet contractual obligations and to satisfy customer expectations. One way to achieve efficiency is to leverage automation. Employing automation incorrectly, such as a test-only focus, could exacerbate the program’s inefficiencies. Based on lessons learned from multiple challenged programs, this paper provides an automation plan which a program can utilize to leverage automation effectively. This plan outlines three phases to help establish and maintain a permanent and resilient automation implementation.

title: Fog Computing-Enabled Multi-User Interaction System using Soft Robotic Gloves
authors: Kadir Ozlem, Asli Tuncay Atalay, Ozgur Atalay, Gökhan Ince
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Soft robotics, with its flexible and lightweight structures, offers innovative solutions in fields such as medicine, rehabilitation, and assistive technologies. The development of textile-based soft robotics has significantly reduced integration challenges in wearable technologies. This study presents a novel fog computing architecture designed to support multi-user and multi-device operations used in textile-based soft robotics systems. For patients experiencing muscle weakness in one hand, Textile-based Internet of Things (T-IoT) gloves enable simultaneous bilateral actions such as grasping, lifting, and carrying through a mirroring technique. Sensor data from a sensing T-IoT glove worn on the healthy hand are processed by the fog computing system and transmitted to an actuating T-IoT glove worn on the impaired hand, enabling coordinated movement of both hands. By integrating various concurrency and interprocess communication techniques into worker devices, the system can serve up to 10 users with a single worker and up to 26 users with three workers operating simultaneously. In this way, the proposed system provides real-time computational services for multi-user scenarios while also delivering an assistive technology for patients.

title: Virtual Slicing: Achieving Control Plane Availability and Traffic Engineering Efficiency in Data Centers
authors: Brian Chang, Keqiang He, Shawn Shuoshuo Chen, Jiaxin Lin, Mingyang Zhang, Wenfei Wu, Fan Wu, Chen Tian, Aditya Akella
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Many proposals have demonstrated the efficiency advantages of software-defined networking (SDN) in managing data center networks. Common practices employ centralized traffic engineering (TE) in the SDN control plane to optimize load balancing and throughput. Meanwhile, for high availability purposes, the control plane is partitioned to ensure the impact of a single faulty controller is contained. However, the interaction between these two aspects is often overlooked. In particular, we show that the current control plane partitioning approach leads to imbalanced link loads and degraded application performance. To address this issue, we propose virtual slicing, a new control plane partitioning scheme. Virtual slicing achieves desirable traffic engineering performance while retaining the availability guarantees from the current approach. Virtual slicing is implemented and evaluated with real-world and synthetic traffic traces on production spine-free data center networks. Results show that virtual slicing reduces tail link utilizations by up to 28.4%, and improves flow completion times by up to 36%.

title: SII 2026 Commentary
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)

title: Design and Implementation of Smart Solar Tracker
authors: Indu Bhardwaj, Sachin Chawla, Rajni Prashar, Divya Sharma, Saurabh Singh, Dileep Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In the last few years, the world has begun concentrating on increasing renewable energy, and solar power has emerged as an excellent alternative to traditional energy sources. The position of solar panels in relation to the sun has significant consequences on how effectively they can transform sunlight into electrical power. A smart solar tracking system that tracks the movement of the sun across the sky in real time by dynamically adjusting the angle of a solar panel was developed to solve this problem. A pair of light-dependent resistors (LDRs) are used as sensors in the system to measure the amount of sunlight coming from different angles. An 89S52 microcontroller receives the data after these readings have been processed by an LM358 comparator. The microcontroller turns on a direct current (DC) motor based on the input received through a motor driver circuit, moving the solar panel to face the region with the strongest sunshine. This ongoing realignment increases energy production without requiring human involvement by optimizing the exposure of the panel to sunlight. This type of innovation is compatible with being experimented with both rural and urban working conditions and is both affordable and energy efficient. The method offers an invaluable way to further enhance solar panel performance, which encourages the creation of environmentally responsible, sustainable technologies.

title: Discrete Element Method Study of Hopping on Granular Media to Develop Analytical Model for Hopping Robot Design
authors: Rio Makino, Takao Maeda
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Small-scale planetary exploration missions have been attracting significant attention in recent years. To maximize the scientific return from these missions, compact rovers are required. Hopping is a promising locomotion strategy for achieving both high traversability and compactness. However, hopping on soft granular terrain such as regolith is challenging due to significant energy dissipation into the medium. The dynamics of sand flow during hopping are not yet well understood, especially for diagonal trajectories. This study investigates sand behavior during diagonal hopping using the Discrete Element Method (DEM) simulations. We focused on how the addedmass effect and the effective friction coefficient vary with the hopping angle. Our results revealed a fundamental trade-off: shallower hopping angles decrease the inertial resistance from added mass, yet simultaneously increase effective friction, which enhances propulsive force. In addition, we applied the observed friction behavior to hopping simulations and confirmed its effectiveness. A reduced-order simulation incorporating this variable friction behavior reproduced the experimental hopping efficiency (0.31 in simulation versus 0.35 in experiment) more accurately than the fixed-friction model. These insights provide a physical basis for the development of more efficient hopping mechanisms for future planetary rovers.

title: Integrating Gen-AI with RAG for Automating SDG Mapping from Sustainability Report of Organisations
authors: Shrey Arora, Anmol Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: This research focuses on automating the mapping of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) with sustainability reporting, specifically the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting (BRSR) framework, for IT service industries and manufacturing companies. Leveraging Generative AI (Gen AI) and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) integration with OpenAI's API, the study aims to quantify the extent to which companies are addressing SDGs by analysing their sustainability reports. The research evaluates the percentage of SDG goals being met and their completion status based on reported data. Additionally, the study compares the effectiveness of two methodologies: Gen AI + RAG integration and Contextual Similarity Aggregation using Transformer models. The goal is to determine which approach yields more accurate and contextually relevant SDG mappings. By automating this process, the research seeks to enhance the efficiency and precision of sustainability reporting analysis, providing actionable insights for industries to align better with global sustainability targets.

title: Wearable Gravity Compensation System for Muscular Load Reduction in Manual Handling of Heavy Objects
authors: Koki Ito, Ryoma Koizumi, Ryuji Tsuzuki, Riku Shibahara, Ken’Ichi Yano
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: As the working population shrinks due to a decreasing birthrate and an aging society, greater workforce participation by women and the elderly is expected. However, the manual handling of heavy objects imposes significant physical burdens, limiting their involvement and worsening labor shortages. Conventional assistive devices support only specific body parts, leading to residual strain. In this study, a wearable weight offloading system was developed to reduce muscular load on both the lower back and the arms. Its effectiveness was demonstrated through lifting and carrying experiments with a 10 kg object.

title: Adaptive Evaluation of Classical and PSO-Augmented Path Planning Algorithms Across Varying Obstacle Densities for AGVs
authors: Md Zeeshan, Shahida Khatoon, Md Danish Alam, Md Muzammil Sani
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In contemporary logistics and industrial automation, where reliable navigation in cluttered environments is essential, Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) play a critical role. This paper presents an evaluation of Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)-enhanced path planning algorithms—A*, Dijkstra, and Rapidly-Exploring Random Tree (RRT)—in comparison with their classical counterparts across obstacle densities of 10 %, 20 %, and 30 %. Each algorithm performed 20 trials in a 2D occupancy grid using 4 evaluation metrics: path length, sharp-turn ratio (STR), computation time, and success rate. The results obtained indicate even in dense environments, where traditional methods severely diminish, PSO-enhanced planners maintain success rates above 80 % while providing up to 10 % shorter and noticeably smoother paths. The enhancements in reliability, affordability, and path quality significantly exceed the minimal computational cost (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1-0.2 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>), which makes PSO-based hybrids a suitable option for AGV navigation in dense industrial environments.

title: Relationship Between the Benefit of Inconvenience and Emotions in a Sightseeing Experiment: An ANOVA and Correlation Analysis*
authors: Kotaro Itatsu, Hiroshi Kawakami
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This study examines the "benefit of inconvenience" in tourism through an experiment conducted in Nijo, Kyoto. Participants used two intentionally inconvenient tools: "Blur Navigation," which gradually obscures walked paths and lacks search functions, and an "Unfriendly Camera," which requires entering the reason and location for each photo and has a three-second delay in capturing. Emotional changes were measured before and after the tour using PANAS and POMS2, and Scenery memory was assessed with a post-tour quiz. Results showed that positive emotions such as "Enthusiastic," "Excited," and "Proud," as well as negative emotions like "Ashamed" and "Nervous," were significantly correlated with higher memory scores. Questionnaire responses indicated that the obscuring of paths and lack of search functions encouraged active route selection and attention to surroundings, while the camera delay promoted intentional photography but sometimes caused missed opportunities. These findings support that moderate inconvenience can enhance engagement, emotional richness, and memory retention in tourism experiences.
