title: A Flexible Field-Based Policy Learning Framework for Diverse Robotic Systems and Sensors
authors: José G. Buenaventura-Carreón, Floris Erich, Roman Mykhailyshyn, Tomohiro Motoda, Ryo Hanai, Yukiyasu Domae
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: We present a cross-robot visuomotor learning framework that integrates diffusion policy–based control with 3D semantic scene representations from D<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>Fields to enable category-level generalization in manipulation. Its modular design supports diverse robot–camera configurations, including UR5 arms with Microsoft Azure Kinect arrays and bimanual manipulators with Intel RealSense sensors, through a low-latency control stack and intuitive teleoperation. A unified configuration layer enables seamless switching between setups for flexible data collection, training, and evaluation. In a grasp-and-lift block task, the framework achieved an 80% success rate after only 100 demonstration episodes, demonstrating robust skill transfer between platforms and sensing modalities. This design paves the way for scalable real-world studies in cross-robotic generalization.

title: Communications for Low-Altitude Economy: A Precoding Transmission Method for RIS-Assisted UAV Communication
authors: Tong Cheng, Jie Tang, Shuaijun Li, Cui Yang, Youjun Xiang, Xiaokai Song
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: The low-altitude economy (LAE), as a novel and comprehensive economic model, presents vast development potential across various sectors. However, interference from the complex low-altitude environment and Doppler frequency shifts caused by relative motion between Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) pose challenges to the robustness of low-altitude UAV communication. Meanwhile, the coverage blind areas of base stations in low-altitude environments will also affect the quality of low-altitude communication. Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) provide a new approach to solving this problem. Specifically, we propose a precoding transmission method for RIS-assisted UAV communication systems to address the issue of base station coverage blind areas and enhance the robustness of low-altitude economy communication networks. By precoding the Chirp signal to enhance the communication system’s interference resistance, we transform the RIS-assisted UAV communication system into a Doppler-robust real-domain linear model through a differential detection algorithm. This approach simplifies the precoding process and improves both the system’s interference resistance and communication quality. Simulation results show that the proposed method effectively reduces channel estimation overhead and enhances both the reliability of information transmission and the interference resilience among multiple users.

title: RSV-LoRA: Enhancing Object Segmentation in Remote Sensing Videos via Fine-tuning with LoRA-based Mixture of Experts
authors: Chenyu Liu, Wei Ye, Pu Pang, Mingzhi Wang, Hao Ding, Guangzuo Li, Yirong Wu
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: With the widespread application of high-resolution remote sensing videos (RSV), object segmentation in RSV plays an irreplaceable role in various fields such as surface target recognition. However, existing methods struggle with this task due to the small object size, high object density, and significant scale variation among objects. To address these challenges, we propose RSV-LoRA, a novel approach that fine-tunes Segment Anything Model (SAM) to effectively capture and segment objects in remote sensing videos. First, RSV-LoRA integrates a Mixture of Experts into the LoRA framework, alleviating the limitations of fixed network structures in recognizing objects at varying scales. Subsequently, RSV-LoRA employs multi-scale dilated convolutions as experts while maintaining the inherent efficiency of low-rank adaptation. This introduces enhanced local feature extraction capability, thereby improving the recognition performance for objects in RSV. Moreover, RSV-LoRA is efficient in terms of training as only the RSV-LoRA layers are trained while the weights of SAM remain frozen, thereby reducing the number of trainable parameters. Owing to such training characteristics, RSV-LoRA is plug-and-play, allowing its layers to be adapted to various tasks and objects. Extensive experiments conducted on the SAT-MTB, iSAID, and VHR-10 datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of RSV-LoRA.

title: Tungsten Disulphide Modified Interdigitated Electrochemical Sensor for Nitrite Detection
authors: Ritesh Kumar Singh, Priyadharshini Govindan, Pavar Sai Kumar, Khairunnisa Amreen, Satish Kumar Dubey, Sanket Goel
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Nitrite is an industrial waste which causes lots of health-related issues when it comes in contact with humans. Thus, the detection of Nitrite is necessary. Herein, the detection of Nitrite was performed using a CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> laser ablated polyimide sensor. The sensor had an interdigitated array topology, with the working electrode being changed using the electrochemical deposition process to incorporate Tungsten Di-Sulphide (WS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>). The necessary material characterization has been performed, and the performance analysis of the sensor was accomplished using Cyclic Voltammetry (CV) and Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) using 5 mM K<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>[Fe (CN)<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub>] and 1 mM KCl. The linear range of the sensor was determined to be between 0.1 and 1000 μM. The sensitivity was 0.034 μA/ (μM cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) for concentrations ranging from 0.1 to 20 μM, and 0.004 μA/ (μM cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) for concentrations ranging from 50 to 1000 μM. The determined Limit of Detection (LoD) was 0.083 μM, and the Limit of Quantification (LoQ) was 0.252 μM. In addition, a selectivity study was conducted, and the sensor's performance was verified using real sample analysis. In future the on-chip readout circuit will be developed so that the point of care standalone system can be enabled.

title: Mesh-Based Deep Learning Surrogate Model for Rapid Wideband 3D Electromagnetic Scattering Computation_supp1-3661600.pdf
authors: Jimin Liang
publish: N/A
abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) wideband electromagnetic scattering underpins applications from radar cross section (RCS) analysis to stealth design, yet existing numerical methods often demand prohibitive computational resources. Drawing on recent advances in deep learning, we present GTC-3DEM, a mesh-based network that integrates graph neural networks, Transformers, and convolutional decoders to rapidly predict scattering fields from triangular surface meshes. By injecting global incident-wave parameters through a hierarchical conditioning control mechanism, GTC-3DEM captures both local and global scattering phenomena with high fidelity. Compared to conventional simulations, our approach reduces per-sample RCS computation time from tens of seconds to under 0.2 seconds, while preserving accuracy across diverse object geometries and frequencies. Further extended studies on frequency scalability, mesh sensitivity, polarization diversity, and total/complex electric-field prediction demonstrate the robustness and adaptability of the proposed framework. These results establish GTC-3DEM as a powerful surrogate model for fast, accurate, and scalable 3D electromagnetic scattering computations. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/cytosineXT/GTC-3DEM.

title: Explainable AI for Classifying Human Leg Movement Patterns in Load-Carrying Scenarios
authors: D. Nithyakalyani, T. Venkatesh, V.S. Balaji, M. Harini, K. Adalarasu
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: This work presents the importance of surface electromyography (sEMG) signals from lower limb muscles for classifying the loading and no loading gait conditions. The potential of machine learning classifiers for this muscle activity classification is also being explored in this study. Some of the machine learning classifiers used in this study viz. Ensemble learning, Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Naïve Bayes. The signals obtained from sEMG for both Left Calf Muscle (LCM) and Right Calf Muscle (RCM) are then provided as inputs to these classifier models, which then accurately classify the gait conditions on both the limbs. To enhance the model performance, simulations have been carried out to identify the most important features that presents significant information about the muscle loading in both the limbs. ANOVA based feature selection technique has been utilized to identify the important features in both LCM and RCM. The simulation results obtained using various machine learning models before and after feature selection are then compared to identify a classifier model that is superior in muscle fatigue identification and load assessment.

title: Smart Healthcare Evolution: Deep Learning Meets Cyber-Physical Systems in Brain CT Image Interpretation
authors: Lalit Kumar Behera, Sujata Chakravarty, Tumbanath Samantara
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The rapid and continuous change in artificial intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) along with cyber-physical systems (CPS) incorporating IOT devices have shown the path for transformative innovations in healthcare, particularly in medical imaging. This research work explores the convergence of DL techniques and CPS to enhance the analysis of brain computed tomography (CT) scans. By integrating DL models like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with CPS that involve data acquisition, processing and decision-making in real-time, this research aims to revolutionize the workflow of medical image analysis. Through the application of DL models like customized CNN, InceptionV3 and Xception, this work demonstrates the potential for automatic and highly accurate detection of brain anomalies, directly from CT scan images obtained from CPS. These models can learn complex patterns in image data, thus significantly reducing human error and improving diagnostic efficiency. Additionally, the integration of cyber-physical systems ensures seamless communication between imaging devices, computational resources and healthcare practitioners, enabling real-time diagnostics, remote monitoring and decision support. Furthermore, this research discusses the potential, limitation and future perspective of the current approach in advancing the intelligent healthcare ecosystem by making automated image analysis accessible, scalable and accurate, ultimately contributing to precision medicine.

title: MPC Parameter Tuning Based on Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm
authors: Shizhong Yang, Shicheng Liu, Hongchao Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: To address the issues of manual tuning in Model Predictive Control (MPC), such as the difficulty in balancing multiple objectives, reliance on experience, and insufficient robustness, a parameter tuning method for MPC based on the Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) is proposed. Through improvements in population initialization, adaptive nonlinear adjustments of control parameters, and modifications to the spiral update mechanism, the improved WOA algorithm is developed for MPC parameter tuning. Simulation results show that, compared to the standard WOA tuning method, the proposed IWOA-MPC tuning strategy can effectively balance system response speed and control energy consumption, reduce overshoot and steady-state error, and enhance the system's robustness to changes in operating conditions and constraint adaptation ability. This provides an efficient and feasible solution for parameter optimization of MPC in complex systems.

title:
Modeling of V
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Control for Three-Level Flying Capacitor Buck Converters
authors: Zhenting Wu, Sai Tang, Yuanbin He, Lijun Hang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper presents a discrete-time small-signal model of digital V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> control for three-level flying-capacitor buck converters (3L-FCBCs). Unlike first-order averages that smooth out the ripple exploited by V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, the proposed model preserves waveform slopes and explicitly accounts for discrete-time slope compensation (DSC) and output ripple. We focus on a trailing-edge improved digital predictive V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> (IDPV<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>–TE) algorithm: by using a mid-cycle voltage sample with one-step look-ahead, the duty is updated within the current period, eliminating the half-period sense/actuation latency of conventional DPV<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The resulting duty-to-output plant is approximately first order around the target bandwidth, enabling a transparent outer-loop design. A 48 V, 125 kHz prototype regulating v<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf> ≈9.6 V verifies the approach: under a 1.92 A→9.6 A load step, IDPV<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>–TE shortens the settling interval by ≈39% and reduces peak voltage droop by ≈28% relative to a peak current-mode control baseline.

title: Optimized YOLOV8N with Convolutional Block Attention Mechanism for Vision Based Systems
authors: B. Lahari, K. Varshitha, Ch. Pranav, M. Laxmi, G. Vallathan, K. Mamatha
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: This paper introduces a Mobile AIoT framework powered by deep learning for real-time visual guidance, targeting improved perception and decision-making in assistive and navigation tasks. Existing object detection models such as YOLOv4, YOLOv4_ResNet101, YOLOv5, MobileNetV2-SSD, and Faster R-CNN demonstrate strong detection performance but suffer from high computational cost, large parameter footprints, slower inference, and limited adaptability on resource-constrained mobile or IoT platforms, often degrading in dynamic environments. To address these challenges, an enhanced YOLOv8n integrated with an attention mechanism have been proposed to improve feature extraction and detection accuracy while preserving lightweight efficiency. The framework delivers robust detection, real-time responsiveness, and reduced resource utilization, making it practical for mobile deployment. Experimental evaluation confirms measurable gains, including a 0.052 % increase in accuracy and a 0.049 % improvement in precision, alongside consistent enhancements in recall, recognition reliability, and real-time performance. These results validate the superiority of the proposed method over existing approaches, ensuring adaptability, efficiency, and reliability in mobile AIoT applications.

title: Intermodal Journey Planning to Transportation Hubs in a Microscopic Environment: A Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Optimization Framework
authors: Dominik Wittenberg, Nick Schade, Jürgen Pannek
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Intermodal journey planning remains a challenge in intelligent transportation systems, particularly when accounting for heterogeneous passenger preferences and the integration into smart cities. Traditional planning approaches often fail to capture dynamic traffic conditions and the passenger-centric view required for future transportation systems. This study proposes a Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MOMARL) framework for individual intermodal journey planning across multiple modes. Two microscopic traffic models were developed in Simulation of Urban Mobility (SUMO), creating simulation environments in which passengers plan their journeys to arrive on time at transportation hubs. One simpler model for the verification of the framework and a calibrated model reflecting the dynamics of a real city. The transportation networks were modeled as multilayered graphs. Since each passenger has different preferences and access to transport modes, their individual cost-minimal paths are formulated as a multi-objective optimization (MOO) problem. From this, the scalarized reward signals used in the MOMARL framework are derived. Simulation results show that the proposed approach enables agents to generate feasible intermodal routes in a microscopic traffic environment, demonstrating the use of MOMARL for passenger-centric coordination in multimodal transport systems. Application to the calibrated model of Ingolstadt posed challenges regarding simulation complexity, highlighting the need to expand research in methods that allow the systematic reduction of model fidelity and granularity while retaining realistic dynamics.

title: Third-Harmonic Injection-Based Active Thermal Control for a Modular Multilevel Converter Considering Voltage Dip Depth
authors: Jingyuan Wen, Yi Long, Zhizhen Song, Heya Yang, Xin Xiang, Wuhua Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Modular multilevel converters (MMCs) is widely used in offshore wind power transmission due to their scalability and high capacity, with back-to-back MMCs being the typical solution. However, when an AC side fault occurs at the receiving end converter (REC) station, power transmission is constrained, causing a rise in DC voltage, an increase in arm current, and undesirable thermal stress on devices, which can lead to potential damage. In terms of this issue, an active thermal control method based on third harmonic injection injection is proposed, taking into account the voltage sag depth and making full use of redundant sub-modules (SMs) during the fault stage. With the proposed method, the thermal stress on the power device with the highest temperature is significantly reduced during both the fault and fault recovery stages. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulation experiments.

title: Research on Topology and Control Strategy of Single-stage Isolated Photovoltaic Micro Inverter Based on Triple-Active-Bridge Converter
authors: Mengfei Wu, Zheng Dong, Tianqu Hao, Jiazheng Huang, Tianlong Liu, Zhuoqi Zhang, Ikromjon Usmanovich Rakhmanov, Hongzheng Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: As a core power conversion unit in distributed grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) systems, micro inverters have garnered significant attention in the industry. Traditional two-stage microinverters suffer from multiple conversion stages and high energy losses, while single-stage micro inverters exhibit complex control strategies. Moreover, both topologies require additional ports to achieve power decoupling between the AC and DC sides. Drawing inspiration from the port anti-series configuration of three-port DC converters, this study proposes a single-stage PV micro inverter based on a triple active bridge (TAB) converter. The proposed topology features low energy loss, simplified control, and inherent suppression of double-line-frequency power ripple without the need for extra decoupling ports.

title: Clinical Summarization of Bradycardia and Desaturation Episodes in Preterm Neonates using LLM Models
authors: Nancy Jane Y, Pabitha P, Janani Priya R, Lakshmi V, Sumanth S Badhya
publish: 2025 Fourth International Conference on Smart Technologies and Systems for Next Generation Computing (ICSTSN)
abstract: Preterm neonates admitted to Neonatal Intensive Care Units (NICU) are at high risk of cardio-respiratory complications such as apnea, bradycardia, oxygen desaturation, hypoxemia, tachycardia. Monitoring these events generates large, time-stamped vital sign data, requiring effective temporal summarization to derive actionable insights, reduce data overload, and enable timely interventions. Hence, there is a growing need for intelligent computational frameworks that can provide interpretable insights to clinicians in real time. The objective of this work is to summarize the preterm neonate bradycardia and desaturation episodes using fine-tuned medical large language model (LLM), BioBART. The proposed work performs three key activities: Data preprocessing, LLM classification and Temporal summarization. In data preprocessing, the smoothened vital signals are annotated and segmented using threshold based sliding window segmentation approach. The LLM classification is carried out with the segmented signals using fine-tuned BioBART-Base model with Low- Rank Adaptation (LoRA). The obtained classification model achieves an accuracy of 90.50 %. The Temporal Summarization is performed by embedding classification results with BioBART-Large model which enables concise, clinically relevant insights from temporal vital signal data.

title: Detecting Deep Fake Audio: A Hybrid CNN-LSTM Approach Leveraging Spectrogram Analysis
authors: Kashika Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The rapid progress in audio synthesis technologies allows the generating of artificial audio deep-fakes that sound convincingly real, thus causing security, privacy, and authenticity verification difficulties. The current solution for recognizing if a deep-fake is used consists of extracting features using methods such as Mel-frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) used either traditionally with a classifier system accompanied with a Convolution Neural Network (CNN), Support Vector Machine(SVM) and similar models. Although these methods have some success, one of the most essential constraints is the complexity in audio signals, a form of technical innovation in deep-fake technology, which adds more and more realism to the manipulated sounds. In this paper, we explore deep-fake audio detection considering a different approach, involving conversion of audio signals to spectrogram representations and using a hybrid CNN-LSTM model. The CNN reveals spatial aspects in spectrograms, whereas the LSTM takes full advantage of the fact that audio files have inherent time dependencies, therefore allowing a greater span of analysis of a needed time frame for proper detection efficiencies. By adopting this hybrid architecture, the accuracy of the proposed model CNN-LSTM has increased to 92.56 % whereas only CNN model provides 85.38 % accuracy.

title: Spiral-Shaped Coupling Plate for Increased Coupling Capacitance in Capacitive WPT
authors: Waqar Khan, Yiming Zhang, Dukju Ahn
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: This paper proposes a spiral-shaped coupling plate to increase the coupling capacitance for capacitive wireless power transfer. The capacitor plate consists of a very thin but dense spiral pattern to increase the internal self-inductance of the capacitor. This self-inductance significantly compensates for the coupling capacitance, thereby increasing the effective net coupling capacitance value at the same physical outer dimensions. Compared to a conventional capacitive-inductive hybrid coupler, our proposed system does not need the litz-wire coupler and the heavy/brittle ferrite materials. The optimum matching inductance value which is required for the highest efficiency is reduced. Moreover, the circulating current through the inverter and TX-side matching network is also reduced, thereby reducing losses at TX-side as well. The measurement results achieve 88.46% efficiency with 200 W load power at a frequency of 2.8 MHz

title: A Thermal Stress Optimization Strategy Based on Variable Structure Converter
authors: Wenxu Wang, Yong Shi, Yuting Wang, Tuoyu Zhu, Yimeng Liu, Lihan Duan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: To reduce the junction temperature variations of the switching devices in the applications of the photovoltaic system, this paper proposes a new thermal stress control strategy based on a variable-structure DC-DC converter. The proposed method can effectively mitigate junction temperature ripple caused by high power cycle. Besides, the fixed-frequency sliding mode control is employed to decrease switching chattering. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy can effectively achieves a 60% reduction in junction temperature variation, which may enhance the reliability of power converter.

title: Battery-oriented Double-line-frequency Ripple Current Suppression Method Based on RDCX Converter
authors: Yuchen Wang, Xinke Wu, Wending Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Rapidly evolving residential energy storage systems (RESS) hold significant potential for future applications, with batteries serving as the primary energy storage units. The power conversion system (PCS) plays a critical role in energy management, largely determining the overall performance and competitiveness of the system. This paper proposes a novel isolated wide-range DC–DC converter, termed RDCX, which employs partial power processing specifically designed for RESS. To address key challenges like wide battery voltage variation and double-line-frequency (100Hz) ripple power—which induces corresponding ripple current on the battery side, the RDCX converter integrates a fixed-frequency series resonant converter (DCX) to achieve main voltage gain, along with a PWM regulator (R) for bus voltage regulation and ripple power processing. Furthermore, a dual-loop control strategy featuring outer voltage and inner current loops is implemented to enable simultaneous ripple suppression and high-efficiency power conversion.

title: Design, Modeling and Control of a Deformable Quadrotor Using McKibben Pneumatic Actuators
authors: Keiichiro Kan, Junichiro Sugihara, Jinjie Li, Masaki Kitagawa, Kotaro Kaneko, Moju Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Deformable aerial robots with articulated structures have attracted increasing attention for their ability to perform complex tasks through in-flight morphological adaptation. However, most existing implementations rely on mechanical actuators, which increase total weight and vulnerability to external impacts. To address these limitations, we propose a deformable quadrotor platform actuated by antagonistic McKibben Pneumatic Actuators (MPAs), which offer lightweight, flexible, and robust actuation. We develop a dynamic model of the quadrotor incorporating joint angles actuated by MPAs, and characterize the relationship between internal pneumatic pressure and joint angle. Based on this model, we construct a prototype platform and evaluate its performance through a series of experiments. We first identify the optimal actuator length by analyzing joint deformation under varying pressure conditions. Next, we demonstrate stable flight during in-air morphing transitions, such as X-type, T-type, and H-type configurations, with position and attitude errors remaining within acceptable ranges. The results confirm that the proposed system enables stable flight using soft pneumatic actuation and paves the way for future aerial manipulation and morphing applications.

title: A Multi-Factor Analysis of Gaming Habits and Their Impact on Mental Health, Learning Efficiency, and Behavioral Outcomes in Indian College Students
authors: Ankit Jangid, Anubhav Mishra, Ujjwal Sen, Radhakrishna Rambola
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Online gaming is an issue in which most students are involved in their day-to-day lives. As much as gaming relieves emotions and maintains social connection, it may as well lead to mental health and academic problems. The study investigates the gaming behavioral patterns among the college students in India regarding mental health, learning efficiency, and behavioral consequences. The research deals with the trends of gaming and the genre, frequency, as well as the self-rated gaming effects on cognition, emotion, and academics applying to survey data on students in one of the technical and engineering colleges. According to the survey results, there was a notable difference in the responses of the students since most of them were relieved of the stress and had a boost in mood, better social interaction, and better decision- making whereas, a handful of the students had the adverse effects of partial sleep, along with poor concentration, procrastination, and aggression. In this study, the correlation of the negative impact of gaming during studies and the feedback of the students is investigated. The study proposes to develop some awareness programs and self- regulation frameworks that are meant to change the way of gaming to the most positive in terms of utilizing the gaming with minimized adverse effects.

title: Matrix nearness problems, D -stability and μ-values in economic models
authors: Mutti-Ur Rehman, Saima Akram, Ali Algefary
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the interconnection between D-stability and the structured singular value (μ-values) to analyze linear time invariant dynamical systems subject to structured uncertainties. The new results on D-stability ensures that the spectrum remains enclosed into a specified domain of the complex plane subject to admissible perturbations which are critical for stability. The computation of structured singular values allow to measure the robustness, performance and stability under structured uncertainty. The results on relationship between D-stability and μ-analysis are developed by exploring how D-stable regions can be characterized within the μ-framework. Numerical testing demonstrate the the behavior of singular values and structured singular values under the proposed framework.

title: Video-Level Cross-Modal Temporal-Navigation for RGBT Tracking
authors: Jiaqi Deng, Si Chen, Da-Han Wang, Xu-Yao Zhang, Yan Yan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: RGBT tracking has recently garnered significant attention due to its all-weather tracking capability. Traditional RGBT tracking methods primarily concentrate on the fusion of cross-modal spatial information. However, these methods ignore contextual relationships between consecutive video frames and lack effective interactions between modalities, easily resulting in tracking drift gradually due to the accumulation of errors. To avoid this limitation, we propose a novel Video-Level Cross-Modal Temporal-Navigation method termed VCT for robust RGBT tracking, which fully leverages the complementary spatio-temporal information across modalities to improve cross-modal tracking accuracy. The VCT employs a simple, flexible, and effective video-level dual-stream architecture that accommodates video sequences of arbitrary length, enabling RGB and TIR streams to capture and synergize spatio-temporal features across frames. To achieve temporal consistency and adaptability, we design a Cross-Modal Temporal Prompt Navigator (CM-TPN) that dynamically aggregates and compresses the historical frame context to navigate predictions of subsequent frames through temporal prompts. In addition, we introduce a Modality-Specific Mixture of Adapters (MS-MoA) to promote the spatio-temporal interaction both within and between modalities, thereby dramatically adapting to appearance changes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the four popular RGBT tracking benchmarks.

title: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics

title: Det-Agent: Open-Vocabulary Object Localization and Detection with Reinforcement Learning Agent
authors: Ruisong Zhang, Xin-Jian Wu, Chuang Wang, Cheng-Lin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Object detection, which aims to locate and recognize objects in images, is evolving toward reduced reliance on manual annotations and enhanced adaptability to open-world scenarios. This shift has led to open-vocabulary object detection (OVD), which enables zero-shot detection of objects from novel categories beyond the base categories. In this work, we identify three key challenges in detecting unseen class instances: 1) locating the instances of new classes; 2) distinguishing new class instances from the background; 3) recognizing new class instances. We propose a detection framework that leverages vision-language pre-trained (VLPT) models, such as CLIP, as the backbone to jointly address these three challenges. Specifically, we treat localization as a box-deformation decision process, where the agent interacts with the image to learn a universal deformation strategy, enhancing generalization for unseen class objects. We further reformulate the foreground-background classification as an objectness ranking task to improve objectness evaluation, utilizing a specially designed AP loss. Additionally, a feature magnitude minimization constraint is introduced for the adapter during fine-tuning, boosting recognition performance for both base and novel classes. Experiments on COCO and LVIS datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms previous approaches in open-vocabulary object detection.

title: An Adaptive Sensor for Detecting Hybrid Defects on Steel Plate With Unknown Thickness
authors: Hang Xu, Jinhai Liu, Lin Jiang, Jiayue Sun, Huaguang Zhang, Qiangxin Li
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Hybrid defects of the oil and gas pipelines with unknown thickness occur frequently influenced by the complex working environment. Due to the mutual interference between hybrid-surface and hybrid-back defects and the lack of thickness information, it is difficult to accurately detect hybrid defects with unknown wall thickness. The integrated method has been widely developed for detecting hybrid defects due to its high detection efficiency and integrity. However, existing methods are unable to accurately detect hybrid defects with unknown wall thickness due to the difficulty in detecting completely covered hybrid-back defect and the nonadaptation to unknown thickness perfectly. To the above issues, an adaptive sensor is proposed, which does not require hybrid-surface and hybrid-back defects to be staggered or the known thickness in advance. First, an integrated structure is proposed by sharing magnetic source to synchronously collect multiple signals, which extends the information dimension without changing the excitation. Second, a circuitous strategy is proposed by energy detour to overcome the difficulty in detecting completely covered hybrid-back defect. Third, an adaptive strategy is proposed by matching the best frequency for unknown thicknesses. Finally, the experimental results indicate that the proposed sensor can accurately detect hybrid defects with unknown wall thickness.

title: Design of a High-Efficiency High-Power-Density Constant Current Source for Lasers
authors: Erqiang Zhou, Kangping Wang, Yuhang Ning, Ruochen Zhao, Daoxi Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Laser diode is a current-sensitive component, even a small change of the output current can significantly affect its output power. Also, it is vulnerable to the overvoltage and overcurrent. Therefore, the constant current source for the laser diodes has requirements of low ripple and high reliability. At the same time, to meet the requirements of the high-efficiency and high-power-density of the power supply in practical application scenarios, this paper presents a constant current source for lasers based on a two-phase interleaved parallel Buck converter. First, circuit parameter calculations and the current control loop design are completed. Next, the design is validated across multiple simulation platforms. Finally, the prototype undergoes experimental tests. Experimental results demonstrate that all the switches can achieve ZVS, with a peak efficiency greater than 98% and a power density of 2000 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, thereby meeting the requirements for practical applications.

title: Analysis and Improvement of Harmonic Transient Stability for Grid-Forming Active Power Filters
authors: Yang Liu, Wenjia Si, Yanxiang Yin, Chenming Liu, Zhida Shang, Jingyang Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Grid-forming converters have emerged as the enabler of modern power systems. Particularly, they can serve as active power filters for harmonic and reactive power compensation, known as grid-forming active power filters (GFM APFs). However, this paper reveals that excessive harmonic compensation will result in the harmonic transient instability of GFM APFs for the first time. Then, it derives the relevant stable regions to ensure the stable operation of GFM APFs. To achieve this, we establish a large-signal model of GFM APFs, which considers the coupling between active and reactive power control loops. Building on this model, we derive the active and reactive power transfer limitations of individual harmonics. Furthermore, we introduce a control method that is capable of improving the harmonic transient stability of GFM APFs. Finally, simulation and experiment results verify the proposed theoretical analysis and control method.

title: Differentially Private Wireless Federated Learning Using Orthogonal Sequences
authors: Xizixiang Wei, Tianhao Wang, Ruiquan Huang, Cong Shen, Jing Yang, H. Vincent Poor
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: We propose a privacy-preserving uplink over-the-air computation (AirComp) method, termed FLORAS, for single-input single-output (SISO) wireless federated learning (FL) systems. From the perspective of communication designs, FLORAS eliminates the requirement of channel state information at the transmitters (CSIT) by leveraging the properties of orthogonal sequences. From the privacy perspective, we prove that FLORAS offers both item-level and client-level differential privacy (DP) guarantees. Moreover, by properly adjusting the system parameters, FLORAS can flexibly achieve different DP levels at no additional cost. A new FL convergence bound is derived which, combined with the privacy guarantees, allows for a smooth tradeoff between the achieved convergence rate and differential privacy levels. Experimental results demonstrate the advantages of FLORAS compared with the baseline AirComp method, and validate that the analytical results can guide the design of privacy-preserving FL with different tradeoff requirements on the model convergence and privacy levels.

title: The Impact of Live Streaming E-Commerce on Impulsive Buying Behavior in the Fashion and Lifestyle Industry
authors: Dewa Brata Lintang Kencono, Firlii Zhavilla Rompis, Virgina Wirjadinata, Stefanus Rumangkit, Christopher Joshua Leksana
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: This study aims to analyze the impact of Impulsive Buying behavior on purchasing transactions conducted during live streaming sessions on the TikTok Shop ecommerce platform. This study employs a quantitative research method. The number of samples used was 100. The sampling technique used was purposive sampling. The variables examined in the study include social presence, arousal pleasure, and impulsive buying, all of which are measured using quantitative data collected through questionnaires. The data is then analyzed using Partial Least Squares. The findings of this study indicate that Streamer social presence on consumer arousal was found to be statistically insignificant, Streamer social presence on consumer enjoyment was also statistically insignificant, viewer social presence on consumer arousal was found to be statistically insignificant, viewer social presence on consumer enjoyment was statistically insignificant, live streaming platform social presence on consumer arousal was found to be statistically significant, social presence on consumer enjoyment was also statistically significant consumer arousal on impulsive buying behavior was found to be statistically significant, consumer enjoyment on impulsive buying behavior was also statistically significant.

title: Investigation on the effect of packaging on SiC MOSFETs under short-circuit conditions
authors: Xiaoliang She, Laili Wang, Fengtao Yang, Chao Shi, Yan Wang, Wei Liu, K.T.Chau, C.C.Chan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Under short-circuit (SC) conditions, silicon carbide (SiC) metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistors (MOSFETs) encounter significant reliability issues due to their small chip area and high heat flux density. Different packaging methods often introduce a large number of different parasitic parameters at each terminal, further compromising the SC reliability of the device. This paper first constructs the simulation model for 1.2kV SiC MOSFET. Secondly, the SC behavior and failure mechanism of SiC MOSFETs are investigated. Finally, technology computer aided design (TCAD) mixed-mode simulations are employed to examine the effects of packaging on the external circuit characteristics of the device under SC conditions, while also analyzing how packaging influences the internal carrier distribution, hole current, lattice temperature, and other microscopic parameters during the SC process.

title: A Hybrid Control Method for Flexible Single Inverter with Quantitative Grid-Following/Grid-Forming Power Apportion
authors: Yuxuan Li, Xiaochao Hou, Kehui Zhou, Haiguo Tang, Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The short-circuit ratio (SCR) is a key metric for evaluating grid strength and system stability. Single control mode of grid-following (GFL) or grid-forming (GFM) inverter has some limitations: GFL inverter lacks voltage support in low-SCR weak grids and islanded mode, while GFM inverter is prone to unstable in high-SCR strong grids. Nowadays, single inverter with hybrid GFL/GFM control provides a promising solution. But it fails to dynamically quantify GFL/GFM power allocation under varying SCR conditions. In this study, a hybrid GFL/GFM control method for flexible single inverter is proposed based on virtual dual-source circuit dynamic framework, which models hybrid control as parallel GFL/GFM circuits. Firstly, the output power characteristic of single inverter with hybrid control is intuitively derived. Then the SCR boundary for stable operation is characterized. Specially, the proposed method can achieve predesigned quantification of flexible GFL/GFM power allocation ratios. Simulation results demonstrate the stability boundary analysis of proposed method.

title: XGBoost-Based Energy Consumption Forecasting with Spatial Mapping: A Smart Building Approach Using IoT Smart Meter Data
authors: Ferdiansyach, I Ketut Agung Enriko, Andrianingsih, Panca Dewi Pamungkasari, Erina Rahma Zani, Mohammad Aldi
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Accurate energy consumption forecasting is critical for smart building management and sustainable operations. This study develops a machine learning-based forecasting framework using IoT smart meter data from a multi-floor office building, comprising over 2.5 million records from 30 distributed meter locations. We propose a three-stage methodology: spatial mapping for noise filtering, feature engineering with lag variables and meteorological data, and predictive modeling using XGBoost and LSTM architectures. The spatial filtering approach achieved an 85.5% improvement in RMSE by contextualizing meter data to registered building locations, reducing raw data noise from 320,000 Wh to 46,000 Wh. XGBoost outperformed LSTM with an RMSE of 46,211 Wh compared to 65,287 Wh, demonstrating 29.2% better accuracy. SHAP analysis revealed that the 1-hour lag feature contributed the highest importance score of 1,189, followed by hour-of-day (855) and 24-hour lag (794). Statistical validation using Wilcoxon test (p < 0.05) confirmed significant performance differences. Transfer learning evaluation showed external validity with RMSE of 57,397 Wh when trained on one building and tested on another. This framework offers practical implications for building energy management systems and demand response applications.

title: Multi-mode PLMPWM Transition Strategy Based on Stator Flux Linkage Deviation Vector
authors: Jingrun Du, Chaoqun Xiang, Ziyin Fan, Xinyu Fu, Hongsi Li, Shu Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Based on the principles of continuous fundamental voltage phase and minimum flux linkage deviation vector, a switching method for multi-mode power loss minimization PWM (PLMPWM) was designed. A SIMULINK model was established according to the duty cycle of a high-power heavy-haul locomotive traction converter. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method ensures continuity in both amplitude and phase of the stator flux linkage before and after switching, achieving smooth transitions between multi-mode modulation strategies during continuous voltage-frequency variation.

title: Research on a Single-UAV Follow Control Based on Enhanced Deterministic Supervised Learning
authors: Zijun Zhao, Shitao Chen, Xiangjun Cheng, Chaojia Jia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Control Systems and Automation Technology (ACSAT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of drone technology, intelligent follow control has become a core technology for formation flying and collaborative missions. Addressing the limited adaptability of traditional control methods in complex dynamic environments, this study constructs a comprehensive Markov decision process (MDP) modeling framework for drone follow control based on machine learning methodologies. An improved deterministic supervised policy learning control method is proposed. Experimental validation demonstrates that compared to PID controllers, this method exhibits significant advantages in reward function values and distance error, achieving a 45.3% increase in success rate and a 23.5% reduction in error. This indicates that neural networks can learn more refined nonlinear control strategies. The research validates the effectiveness of deterministic supervised policy learning in the field of intelligent UAV control, providing crucial technical support for autonomous flight in complex environments.

title: Critical Success Factors for the E-Business Software Projects in Indonesia and Its Prediction Model
authors: Rudi Setiawan, T.K. Abdul Rahman
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The rapid growth of internet users has encouraged many companies in Indonesia to develop B2C e-business software projects. However, these projects often face challenges that can hinder their success. This study aims to identify and analyze Critical Success Factors (CSFs) that influence the success of B2C e-business software projects in Indonesia. A systematic approach was applied, beginning with a literature review and validation by three experts to determine relevant CSFs. Subsequently, a survey was conducted with a technology company experienced in B2C e-business development. Pearson correlation analysis was used to measure the relationship between identified CSFs and project success. The results show that 28 CSFs significantly affect project outcomes, including project planning, requirement management, progress evaluation, realistic scheduling, human resource competence, and risk management. These findings highlight the importance of structured planning and effective management in reducing project failure risk. In conclusion, this study provides practical insights for companies to strengthen strategies in developing B2C e-business software projects, thereby improving performance and ensuring sustainable success in Indonesia’s digital business environment.

title: AI-Powered Student Recruitment Using Skilltract
authors: Atharv Mali, Sonali Bhoite, Sanket Patil, Shubham Mahalle, Soham Nikrad
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: SkillTract is a lively, full-stack platform that has been created to connect would-be students and potential re cruiters. It allows students to capture and showcase their aca demic journey, subject matter knowledge, skill set, certifications, and professional development in a stimulating, visually enhanced profile. Meanwhile, it provides recruiters advanced tools to sort, evaluate, and track talent aligned to their company needs. Skill Tract integrates frontend creativity in the form of React, Type Script, and Tailwind CSS with a highly capable backend powered by Supabase, enabling seamless authentication, data storage, and real-time updating. Some of the main functionalities are a resume builder, graphical display of academic performances, authenticity checking of certificates, and an applicant tracking system (ATS) for recruitment agents. By blending user-first design and scalable design, SkillTract not only optimizes student exposure but also simplifies recruiter workflows in choosing the ideal candidate.

title: Real-Time Joint-Torque Feedback in VR Pre-Training for Safe Lifting: A Comparative Study of Visual Encodings
authors: Kouichi Iwami, Tetsunari Inamura
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: We present a VR-based pre-training system that estimates user-specific joint torques in real time by coupling a VR interaction environment (SIGVerse) with a biomechanics simulator (DhaibaWorks). The system visualizes internal load together with postural information using four encodings (color map, bar graph, exemplar posture, and exemplar+self posture) and enables rehearsal of lifting posture without handling real weight. In a within-subject study (11 participants), a simulated box-lifting task was evaluated using (i) time-integrated lumbar torque normalized by body mass and (ii) two 7-point Likert ratings (perceived comprehension and perceived load reduction). Across conditions, we did not observe a reliable reduction in normalized torque after training. Perceived load reduction showed a significant overall condition effect, whereas perceived comprehension showed no clear between-condition differences. These findings indicate that visualizing internal load can influence users’ perception of effort, although the present short session did not yield measurable torque changes. The proposed platform provides a safe pre-training route for learning low-strain movement strategies and a foundation for adaptive human-agent interaction that can leverage real-time estimates of human physical state.

title: An Efficient Electrothermal Decoupling Method for Power MOSFETs in Three-Phase LLC Converters Based on Iterative Multi-Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Mingkai Chen, Yikang Wu, Jianing Wang, Zhicheng Gao, Shaolin Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Multi-physics electrothermal coupling can severely compromise device operational reliability, so it is essential to evaluate the electrothermal performance of MOSFETs in three-phase LLC converters. Existing evaluation methods suffer from limited computational efficiency and fail to fully account for how changes in circuit parameters and heat-sink geometries influence device electrothermal performance. To address these challenges, the article proposes an iterative multi-ANNs (IMA) framework prediction method, that decouples the complex electrothermal interactions of MOSFETs, enabling rapid generation of electrothermal performance data across a wide range of circuit parameters and heat-sink geometries. Compared with LTspice and COMSOL iterative simulations, the proposed method achieves a 93.8-fold acceleration in data collection and over a 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup>-fold speedup in evaluation. Experimental validation at multiple power ratings shows high accuracy, underscoring the method’s engineering applicability.

title: AI-Driven Ensemble Intrusion Detection System for Secure IoT Networks
authors: Priyanka Vashistha, Syed Zeeshan Hussain
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital infrastructure and connected devices has made modern networks increasingly vulnerable to cyberattacks. Traditional signature-based IDSs often fail to recognize new or evolving threats, making anomalybased approaches more critical than ever. This paper proposes a robust AI-driven IDS based on machine learning techniques to improve the detection and classification of malicious network activities. The proposed methodology incorporates class balancing using SMOTE, feature selection through a Random Forest classifier, intelligent label mapping, and comprehensive data preprocessing. Different ML classifiers, such as Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and AdaBoost, were implemented and compared. The ensemble model achieved the best performance, yielding a detection accuracy of 99.46% on the real-world network traffic dataset CICIoT2023. The model was able to identify minority attack classes like Brute-Force and Web-based intrusions. These findings indicate that AI-enhanced IDS frameworks can strengthen cybersecurity infrastructure and pave the way towards adaptive, intelligent, and scalable threat-detection systems.

title: State Estimation of a Shape-flexible Multi-fingered Robotic Hand Leveraging Multiple Proximity Sensors Measuring an Ambient Environment including the Self-body and a Constant Curvature Model
authors: Masato Morita, Hikaru Arita, Kazuto Nakashima, Kenji Tahara
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper studies state estimation for continuum robotic fingers during in-hand manipulation, where accurate pose estimation relative to the environment is required in feature-sparse scenes. To address this requirement, we adopt a SLAM-based formulation that estimates the robot pose and a local map from exteroceptive sensing. Continuum fingers lack encoder-based joint angle measurements, while conventional SLAM assumes feature-rich environments that are rarely available inside the hand. We propose a SLAM-based estimator that fuses exteroceptive proximity sensing with a constant-curvature kinematic prior by replacing encoder angles with virtual joint angles from the model. The key idea is to leverage designed in-hand self-body elements, namely the opposing fingers and the palm, as stable reference geometry to maintain observability in feature-spares environments. We evaluate our method through free motion and grasping simulations, and analyze the effect of presence and shape of the palm on estimation accuracy. The proposed estimator outperforms a kinematics-only baseline by suppressing bias, reducing a position error of an end effector, and improving map quality. We demonstrate that three-dimensional contoured palms enhance observability, and a composite wavy palm yields the smallest errors without temporal drift. These results indicate that designed in-hand geometry enables effective state estimation for continuum fingers in feature-sparse grasping scenarios, supporting reliable in-hand manipulation.

title: Causal Analysis and Mapping of Urban Traffic Congestion using Geosocial Analytics
authors: Zoheen Shahzad, Ramsha Arshad, Saif Ali
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: The annual cost of traffic congestion in the Delhi National Capital Region (Delhi NCR) was estimated to be USD 10 billion. The cost is attributed to the wastage of fuel due to idling of vehicles, economic productivity loss, air pollution, and road accidents. In this study, we demonstrate how trafficrelated volunteered geographic information (VGI) can be a valuable source for uncovering spatio-temporal traffic patterns. We use Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to conduct spatiotemporal mapping, causal analysis, and data visualization to arrive at policy-relevant insights. The widespread use of mobile phones and social media in urban spaces makes it possible to extract information about traffic congestion from VGI available through social media. Our analysis successfully reproduces well-established trends such as peak hour congestion, seasonal waterlogging, and the significant decline in traffic activity during the COVID-19 lockdown period. Our results suggest that substantial improvements in traffic congestion can be achieved by infrastructural interventions like strengthening the public bus system, heavy transport infrastructure, and drainage network, as bus breakdowns and waterlogging alone account for over half of the reported congestion causes. The use of VGI for traffic monitoring alleviates concerns around privacy and centralization that are intrinsic to expensive surveillance-based systems. Our study paves the way for integrating VGI in urban mobility management while promoting transparency and citizen participation.

title: A State Recognition Method of Industrial Robots Based on Adaptive Class Weight Gated Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Dejun Zheng, Jianzhong Hu, Qianfei Zhou, Feiyun Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Mechanical Automation and Electronic Information Engineering (MAEIE)
abstract: Industrial robot state recognition is critical for predictive maintenance and operational safety, yet faces data imbalance, limited samples, and label noise in transition regions. This paper proposes an adaptive class-weighted gated convolutional neural network integrating three innovations: (1) purity-based sliding window algorithm filtering label noise by extracting windows with homogeneous state labels (purity ≥0.75), reducing transition contamination; (2) gated convolutional architecture with channel attention adaptively suppressing noise while emphasizing discriminative time-frequency features from motor currents; (3) adaptive focal loss incorporating dynamic class weighting to address sample-level difficulty and class-level imbalance. Experimental validation on a SCARA robot executing three trajectories with 10 kHz current sampling achieved 96.08% test accuracy across four states—18% improvement over standard CNN. Ablation studies revealed gated convolution contributed the largest gain, while channel attention enhanced stability. All predictions exceeding 90% confidence were correct, demonstrating reliable uncertainty quantification. Results validate effectiveness for industrial robot condition monitoring, though future work should address generalization to diverse conditions, multi-modal sensor fusion, and continuous transition modeling for real-world deployment.

title: ParaMind: Parallelized Adaptive Memory Integration for Long-Context Large Language Models
authors: Sandesh Basrur
publish: 2025 13th International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) show impressive performance across a range of natural language processing tasks, but they struggle with long-context inputs due to high memory and computational costs. Existing solutions like sparse attention or retrieval-augmented generation either suffer from sequential bottlenecks or do not scale efficiently. In this work, we propose ParaMind, a novel framework for Parallelized Adaptive Memory Integration in long-context LLMs. ParaMind partitions memory into multiple independent shards, each processed in parallel, and introduces a lightweight routing mechanism to dynamically select relevant shards per input. A compression-aware aggregator fuses shard outputs into a unified representation used for final inference. Experiments on QA and reasoning tasks with up to 200 K -token contexts show that ParaMind reduces latency and memory usage by over $2 \times$ while maintaining or improving accuracy compared to Longformer, BigBird, RETRO, and Memorizing Transformer. We also analyze tradeoffs in shard activation, routing sparsity, and retrieval quality. ParaMind offers a scalable path forward for efficient and robust long-context language modeling.

title: Network-Layer Ransomware Protection for IoT in Resource-Constrained Environments Using Machine Learning
authors: Elang Prasakti Ghani, Adian Fatchur Rochim, Aghus Sofwan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Network-layer ransomware attacks are on the rise with the rapid deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) devices, particularly in environments with limited CPU, memory, and energy resources. Conventional defenses are often too complex, allowing stealthy ransomware traffic to bypass protection and compromise service reliability. This paper introduces a lightweight hybrid framework using Network Simulator version 3 (NS-3), which combines simulation-based rule enforcement with machine learning analysis. The rule-based mechanisms include token-bucket rate limiting, circuit-breaker quarantine, authentication validation, replay protection, and Fair Queuing Controlled Delay (FQ-CoDel). These mechanisms reduce excessive packet injection, quarantine malicious nodes, and sustain fairness across IoT flows. However, some ransomware traffic still evades filtering. To improve resilience, supervised learning classifiers, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and K-Nearest Neighbors, are trained on controller node logs. Results show that FQ-CoDel stabilizes throughput at approximately 45 kilobytes per second and maintains an average drop rate of 52 %. Random Forest provides the best detection accuracy of 84 % with an area under the curve of 94 %, while K-Nearest Neighbors records 79 %, and Support Vector Machine achieves 77 %. These findings confirm that integrating lightweight rule-based controls with machine learning enhances ransomware protection in resource-constrained IoT system.

title: 2D- Convolutional Neural Network for Non Surgical Breast Cancer Detection in Mammograms
authors: Varsha Gupta, Pawan Kumar Singh, Gagandeep Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: In today's scenario Breast Cancer is one of the most prominent causes for demise of a female universally, which increases the demand of technologies on war footings providing premature and accurate detection in order to be efficiently treated. Hence to satisfy the same the mammography has emerged as one of the most popular detecting methods, but because of the high inter-observer variability and understated irregularities, it found difficult to construe mammograms. In this quest, we hereby are proposing a framework for automated breast cancer detection in mammogram images that depends on 2D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). The suggested model makes use of CNNs' hierarchical feature of extraction capabilities to accurately diagnose malignant lesions. We achieved outmost results with respect to reliability and accuracy by training & evaluating model with publicly available data. We experimented these methods on Mammography Breast Cancer Detection (MBCD) data. The outcomes of the studies show that the 2D CNN architecture minimizes false positives and, maximizes diagnostic reliability by successfully capturing intricate patterns in mammograms. By providing a trustworthy alternative opinion, the above approach may assist clinicians in increasing early identification rates and improving the treatment of patients. The investigation focuses on how deep learning is transforming medical imaging and how easy it is to use these kinds of systems in healthcare settings.

title: Sequence Optimization in Multi-Camera Robotic Visual Inspection
authors: Miha Deniša, Tadej Petrič, Aleš Ude
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Low-volume, high-mix manufacturing presents unique challenges for visual inspection, where frequent product changes make automation difficult. Manual inspection remains common but is slow and error-prone, while fully automated systems are often too costly for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). We present a method to optimize the sequence of image acquisitions in robotic visual inspection. The problem is formulated as a unidirectional weighted graph and solved using Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) techniques. Unlike prior work focused on single-camera setups, we address the more complex case of two-camera inspection with larger numbers of inspection points, introducing a geometric grouping strategy that clusters inspection points by planar regions derived from object geometry. This enables efficient parallel use of cameras while maintaining low planning complexity. The proposed framework supports agile reconfiguration of inspection tasks, making it suitable for high-mix industrial environments. In simulation of a real-world scenario, our method reduces inspection cycle times by up to 2.35× while maintaining near-optimal sequencing, demonstrating its potential to make multi-camera robotic inspection more practical for agile manufacturing.

title: AIM-3D-Web: An Extensible Web-based Architecture for AI Integration in Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Anguo Hu, Haixiong Zhang, Shu Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: While numerous medical imaging tools exist, a significant gap remains in providing a system that seamlessly integrates advanced AI models, high-performance 3D web visualization, and collaborative workflows. To bridge this gap, this paper presents AIM-3D-Web, a novel B/S architecture-based system prototyped for abdominal CT segmentation. The system's core contribution is a modular, three-tier architecture featuring a high-performance front-end built with Cornerstone3D.js and a back-end designed with a model-agnostic, pluggable AI interface. We validate our architecture by successfully integrating a Swin UNETR model, achieving a reproduced mean Dice coefficient of 0.8055 on the public BTCV dataset. Furthermore, a quantitative user study demonstrates that the system's AI-assisted workflow reduces segmentation time by 85.3% compared to manual methods. These results confirm that our system provides a feasible and extensible solution that overcomes the inherent limitations of current standalone desktop and web-viewer solutions.

title: Semantic-Syntactic Feature Fusion via Mixture of Experts for Code Smell Detection
authors: Yutong Wang, Chunyang Ye, Yongji Sui
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on Computer Engineering and Intelligent Communications (ISCEIC)
abstract: Detecting code smells is critical for ensuring software maintainability and system trustworthiness. Existing methods either leverage pretrained models (e.g., CodeBERT) for semantics or Abstract Syntax Tree(AST)+Graph Neural Network(GNN) for syntax, but single-feature approaches underutilize complementary information and struggle with complex smells like Refused Bequest, God Class, and Feature Envy. We propose a semantic-syntactic fusion framework using cross-attention and Mixture of Experts (MoE): CodeBERT and AST+GNN extract semantic and syntactic vectors, which are fused and inferred via an MoE with seven experts. Experiments show an overall F1-score of 76.29%, outperforming CodeBERT (75.17%) and AST+GNN (73.58%). This framework bridges feature fusion and requirements design, offering a robust tool for reliable code quality assessment and supporting trustworthy software implementation.

title: Textile-Based Strain Sensor for Real-Time Bending Angle Monitoring of Soft Joints
authors: Claudia Sánchez, Jaime González, Jorge Muñoz, Santiago Martínez, Concepción A. Monje
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication, and evaluation of a textile-based strain sensor integrated into a soft robotic joint for real-time monitoring of bending angles. The proposed sensor consists of a conductive fabric encapsulated in a flexible silicone substrate, created using a custom co-casting process that enables seamless integration without compromising structural compliance. Electromechanical characterization under tensile and compressive loading demonstrated a strong piezoresistive response, with a gauge factor (GF) of 77.76 under tensile strain and 9.24 under compression, within working ranges of 6–12% and 3–14%, respectively. The sensor was embedded into a soft joint with asymmetric geometry to assess directional sensitivity and real-time performance. Experimental results confirmed reliable bidirectional strain detection and an over 500% increase in resistance under tensile bending. A second-order polynomial model accurately mapped the sensor’s resistance changes to joint bending angles (10°–25°), achieving R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values above 0.67 in both tensile and compressive configurations. These findings validate the sensor’s potential for real-time posture estimation in wearable and soft robotic systems.

title: Graph Neural Networks Enhance CTRCD Detection from 12-Lead ECG by Modeling Inter-Lead Relationships: A Preliminary Study
authors: Yifan Liang, Natsu Suyama, Yuki Ishizuka, Takio Kurita, Kazuko Tajiri, Akira Furui
publish: 2026 IEEE/SICE International Symposium on System Integration (SII)
abstract: Cancer therapy–related cardiac dysfunction (CTRCD) is a serious complication, posing significant risks to cancer survivors’ cardiovascular health. While early detection is crucial, current imaging-based methods are expensive and impractical for routine screening. This study proposes a deep learning framework that combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and graph convolutional networks (GCNs) to analyze 12-lead electrocardiography (ECG) data for CTRCD detection. By representing ECG leads as graph nodes and modeling their anatomical and physiological relationships through different adjacency matrices, our approach captures inter-lead dependencies overlooked by conventional methods. Experimental results demonstrate that the physiological function-based graph structure outperforms the conventional CNN approach, particularly in sensitivity and F1-score. Interpretability analysis reveals distinct lead-specific patterns, enhancing clinical understanding.

title: ICETISI 2025 Committees
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)

title: GaN Monolithic Over-Temperature Protection IC
authors: Zhaoheng Yan, Song Yuan, Xi Jiang, Xiangdong Li, Jincheng Zhang, Xiaowu Gong
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: A novel all-GaN monolithic integrated over-temperature protection (OTP) integrated circuit (IC) utilizing a p-GaN HEMT compatible process, is demonstrated for the first time in this work. The circuit is designed based on low voltage p-GaN enhancement-mode (E-mode) HEMT, lateral field-effect rectifiers, and partial p-GaN Schottky barrier diode, which are monolithically integrated with 650 V power GaN HEMT on a Silicon substrate. The OTP block provides a 2.53V temperature-independent reference voltage to compare with the amplified temperature signal. When the junction temperature exceeds the predefined protection threshold, the OTP block generates a shutdown signal to the gate driver, disabling the power device. The system autonomously re-enables the power device once the junction temperature returns to a predetermined safe operating level, ensuring robust thermal management and enhanced reliability.

title: Dynamic Power Coordination Control for PEM Startup Activation Voltage
authors: Lei Guo, Songwei Song, Boyang Zhao, Yankun Liu, Ning Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysis is important for green hydrogen production due to its high efficiency and fast response. However, during the start-up process, factors such as insufficient activation of the membrane electrode assembly (MEA) and low temperature often lead to increased activation voltage, causing issues such as voltage spikes, stack overheating, and energy loss. To solve this problem, a power-voltage coordination control strategy is proposed in this paper. By constructing a multi-parameter dynamic sensing and dual closed-loop regulation mechanism, the start-up performance and operational stability of the system are enhanced. The study first models the voltage composition and activation mechanism of PEM electrolyzers, revealing the coupling relationship between activation voltage, temperature, charge transfer coefficient, and exchange current density. And then, an improved control with dynamic performance and adaptability is constructed. Simulation results show that the proposed method improves the start-up speed, voltage stability, and power regulation accuracy, which is important for PEM hydrogen production system in case of fluctuating photovoltaic and wind power sources.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Sensors and Measurement Technologies: A New Paradigm for Precision and Sensitivity
authors: Surbhi Sharma, Rashmi Bhardwaj
publish: 2025 IEEE DELCON - International Conference on Recent Smart Technologies in Engineering for Sustainable Development
abstract: Advanced functionalities incorporated in the energy systems include the two-way communication feature, decentralized power generation, and the possibility to monitor operating conditions in real-time, which have already been achieved with the rapid deployment of smart grids. Nonetheless, current classical computing methods have reached severe constraints in facing the increase in complexity, massive volumes of data analysis, and intensive security demands of contemporary power grids. Quantum computation, based on the principles of superposition, entanglement, and quantum parallelism, has the transformative potential of surmounting these drawbacks. In this paper, how quantum computing can be applied to smart grid technologies in four significant areas including: (i) quantumenhanced optimization of dynamic energy distribution and unit commitment tasks, (ii) quantum machine learning to reliably forecast demand loads and renewable energy, (iii) quantum cryptography to secure communication and data verification, and (iv) quantum sensing to achieve high-fidelity fault detection and grid state monitoring is discussed. The findings point to the advantages of the use of quantum algorithms in solving complex computationally intractable optimization problems involved in grid management through near real-time solutions using quantum algorithms such as Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) and Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE). The issues of noise, decoherence, hardware scalability, and hybrid quantum-classical approaches toward near-term deployment are also examined. This paper demonstrates that quantum computing has the potential to substantially improve the reliability, security, and efficiency of next-generation smart grids.

title: Full- and Reduced-Order Models for Virtual Synchronous Generator Control in the Synchronous Reference Frame
authors: Lunbo Deng, Yang Shao, Yongheng Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: The multiple control loops in virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control for grid-forming inverters pose challenges for dynamic evaluation in the design phase. Although small-signal models for the VSG control have been extensively studied, they usually neglect the coupling between fast and slow dynamic loops, which simplifies the analysis but loses certain high-frequency dynamics. In this paper, a model that includes the slow loop and a reduced-order fast loop is thus proposed to support early-stage dynamic evaluation. The accuracy of the model is validated through extensive simulations.
