publish: N/A

title: Camera Motion-Conditioned Motion Estimation for Neural Video Coding for Cloud Gaming
authors: Yifan Wang, Fei Yang, Luka Murn, Juil Sock, Marc Gorriz Blanch, Shuai Wan, Wei Zhang, Fuzheng Yang, Luis Herranz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: The rising popularity of cloud gaming highlights the importance of effective video compression for this domain. Despite the potential of neural video codecs to surpass traditional codecs, their application to cloud gaming videos generally achieves limited performance primarily due to two reasons: (1) most advanced neural video codecs are designed for natural videos with only a few optimized for cloud gaming content, and (2) the distinctive characteristics of cloud gaming videos including abrupt and large camera movements, coupled with repetitive textures, make the direct application of existing codecs suboptimal. To this end, this paper introduces an enhanced neural video codec for cloud gaming with camera motion-conditioned motion estimation. By leveraging the unique feature of cloud gaming, we effectively utilize the camera motion information to condition motion estimation with an attention mechanism, overcoming the pronounced challenges of motion estimation and facilitating the accurate learning of optical flow. Furthermore, we optimize the loss function of the motion estimation network by employing a comprehensive loss across multiple feature levels, ensuring the learned optical flow is multi-faceted and well-suited to subsequent motion compensation. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed codec, providing improved motion estimation capabilities and superior rate-distortion performance. Moreover, the robustness of our codec to rapid camera movements is validated, which makes it highly suitable for cloud gaming scenarios.

title: The Blockchain Consortium Model to Enhance Information Transparency in Legal Services
authors: Inayatulloh
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Legal services include legal advice, documentation, representation, and dispute resolution. In legal practice, lawyers and law firms must verify clients' identities to prevent fraud, money laundering, and terrorism financing. However, transparency in digital identity management poses significant challenges and risks, particularly in terms of security, privacy, and legal compliance. In the legal services industry, limited transparency for clients is an important issue, as they often do not receive clear and accurate information about the services they are receiving. Therefore, this research aims to develop a consortium blockchain model to improve transparency in the digital identity system used in legal services. Blockchain technology uses peer-to-peer validation, where all nodes in the network verify each transaction. The choice of a consortium blockchain model is justified because legal services involve multiple institutions. The research employs a qualitative approach, utilizing a literature review. The study's findings, based on simulations with Hyperledger, show that all transactions related to legal services must be validated by institutions within the blockchain consortium. As a result, the information generated by legal services utilizing blockchain technology is both valid and transparent.

title: Implementation of a Deep Learning Model Based on CNN, CNN+LSTM and Ensemble Learning for MRI Image Analysis to Detect Brain Tumors
authors: Tasya Aurelia, Rima Tamara Aldisa, Mohammad Aldinugroho Abdullah
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: Early detection of brain tumors using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is crucial in clinical diagnosis. This study proposes an improved deep learning framework combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and ensemble learning to enhance the classification accuracy between tumor and non-tumor MRI images. To address reviewer concerns, methodological details have been clarified, including dataset description, preprocessing parameters, model architecture, and ensemble strategy. The dataset consists of two balanced classes subjected to normalization, contrast enhancement, and augmentation. CNN extracts spatial features, LSTM captures sequential correlations, and ensemble learning integrates multiple predictions using weighted voting. Updated experiments show CNN achieved 85% accuracy (AUC 0.581), CNN+LSTM reached 86% accuracy (AUC 0.684), while hybrid and ensemble models achieved 99.5–100% accuracy (AUC 1.000). Additional evaluation metrics such as ROC curves, confusion matrices, and misclassification analysis are included to ensure transparency. The corrected manuscript aligns with academic standards and addresses prior issues regarding clarity, overfitting concerns, and incomplete ensemble description.

title: Simulating Muscle-Level Energetic Cost When Humans Walk with a Passive Biarticular Thigh Exosuit
authors: Vahid Firouzi, Seungmoon Song, Oskar Von Stryk, Andre Seyfarth, Maziar Ahmad Sharbafi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Passive exosuits offer potential for reducing the metabolic cost of walking, but evaluating their effectiveness is challenging due to difficulties in measuring internal biomechanics and muscle-level energetics. This study investigates the metabolic effects of the BATEX, a passive biarticular thigh exosuit assisting hip and knee flexion/extension. The exosuit features a spring parallel to the rectus femoris (RF) and a spring parallel to the hamstring (HAM). We combined experimental data from ten healthy adults walking on a treadmill under various exosuit conditions (different stiffness and configurations) with EMG-informed musculoskeletal simulations to estimate muscle-level behavior. The simulations aimed to replicate experimental metabolic measures, analyze cost fluctuations across the gait cycle, and elucidate muscle-tendon energetic responses, comparing individuals experiencing reductions (positive responders, PR) versus increases (negative responders, NR) in metabolic cost. Simulation results successfully replicated experimental whole-body metabolic cost trends for most participants (8/10, r > 0.7, p < 0.05), showing comparable overall net reductions primarily driven by significant savings during the swing phase. Combining RF and HAM springs resulted in complex, non-additive interactions. Muscle-level analysis identified both targeted effects on hip/knee flexors/extensors and non-targeted effects on ankle and hip ab/adductor muscles, with responses differing markedly between PR and NR groups. Distinct energy-saving mechanisms were associated with each spring type (RF: reduced activation; HAM: reduced fiber velocity). The differing responses of the PR and NR groups to the same device setup motivate personalization, and our muscle-level insights offer a foundation for future device refinement.

title: Lightweight Data Compression Algorithm for Climate Monitoring in Internet of Things (IoT) Application
authors: Nor Asilah Khairi, Asral Bahari Jambek
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Information Systems and Informatics (ICETISI)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has become widespread in our society. It is expected that 48.6 billion IoT devices will be deployed in the field by 2034. However, this large deployment will increase network traffic and data storage requirements to store the monitored data. To solve this problem, data compression is required at the sensor node to reduce the measured data before it is transmitted to the base station. Since most IoT devices have limited computational power, memory, and battery life, lightweight data compression is needed for wireless sensor nodes. In this work, we propose a modified K-RLE algorithm that reduces data redundancy while maintaining the original data after decompression. The proposed algorithm can achieve up to 39% data reduction when tested with a climate dataset. The proposed algorithm achieves 19.8% improvement in compression efficiency compared to the existing RLE algorithm, while requiring only 45% of the computational time of the Huffman algorithm.

title: Research on Coordinated Control Strategy for Current Stress Optimization in Isolated AC-DC Matrix Converter
authors: Biying Ren, Yujun Song, Xiangdong Sun, Yuhao Shi, Yuan Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Power Electronics and Application Symposium (PEAS)
abstract: Due to its transformer leakage inductance, the isolated AC-DC matrix converter still suffer from the problem of high current stress under traditional single variable control method. In this paper, the mathematical model of energy balance is reestablished based on seven-stage bipolar current space vector modulation (B-C-SVM), and the functional relationships between transmission power and modulation coefficient and phase shift angle are deduced. The variable relationships among inductor current, modulation coefficient and phase shift angle are quantified by introducing Lagrange multiplier method, and then the strategy of switching current stress by optimizing combination between modulation coefficient and phase shift angle is put forward. The effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method in this paper are verified by experimental results.
